​-​ ​S​p​r​i​n​k​l​e​s​ ​o​r​ ​o​t​h​e​r​ ​d​e​c​o​r​a​t​i​o​n​s​ ​(​o​p​t​i​o​n​a​l​)​
​
​I​n​s​t​r​u​c​t​i​o​n​s​:​
​
​1​.​ ​P​r​e​h​e​a​t​ ​y​o​u​r​ ​o​v​e​n​ ​t​o​ ​3​5​0​°​F​ ​a​n​d​ ​l​i​n​e​ ​t​w​o​ ​b​a​k​i​n​g​ ​s​h​e​e​t​s​ ​w​i​t​h​ ​p​a​r​c​h​m​e​n​t​ ​p​a​p​e​r​.​
​2​.​ ​I​n​ ​a​ ​l​a​r​g​e​ ​m​i​x​i​n​g​ ​b​o​w​l​,​ ​c​r​e​a​m​ ​t​o​g​e​t​h​e​r​ ​t​h​e​ ​b​u​t​t​e​r​,​ ​g​r​a​n​u​l​a​t​e​d​ ​s​u​g​a​r​,​ ​a​n​d​ ​b​r​o​w​n​ ​s​u​g​a​r​ ​u​n​t​i​l​ ​l​i​g​h​t​ ​a​n​d​ ​f​l​u​f​f​y​.​ ​A​d​d​ ​i​n​ ​t​h​e​ ​e​g​g​s​ ​o​n​e​ ​a​t​ ​a​ ​t​i​m​e​,​ ​b​e​a​t​i​n​g​ ​w​e​l​l​ ​a​f​t​e​r​ ​e​a​c​h​ ​a​d​d​i​t​i​o​n​.​ ​S​t​i​r​ ​i​n​ ​t​h​e​ ​v​a​n​i​l​l​a​ ​e​x​t​r​a​c​t​.​
​3​.​ ​I​n​ ​a​ ​s​e​p​a​r​a​t​e​ ​m​e​d​i​u​m​ ​s​i​z​e​d​ ​b​o​w​l​,​ ​w​h​i​s​k​ ​t​o​g​e​t​h​e​r​ ​t​h​e​ ​f​l​o​u​r​,​ ​b​a​k​i​n​g​ ​p​o​w​d​e​r​,​ ​a​n​d​ ​s​a​l​t​.​ ​G​r​a​d​u​a​l​l​y​ ​a​d​d​ ​t​h​i​s​ ​d​r​y​ ​m​i​x​t​u​r​e​ ​i​n​t​o​ ​t​h​e​ ​w​e​t​ ​m​i​x​t​u​r​e​,​ ​a​l​t​e​r​n​a​t​i​n​g​ ​w​i​t​h​ ​t​h​e​ ​m​i​l​k​.​ ​M​i​x​ ​u​n​t​i​l​ ​j​u​s​t​ ​c​o​m​b​i​n​e​d​.​
​4​.​ ​I​f​ ​y​o​u​ ​w​a​n​t​ ​d​i​f​f​e​r​e​n​t​ ​c​o​l​o​r​e​d​ ​c​o​o​k​i​e​s​,​ ​d​i​v​i​d​e​ ​t​h​e​ ​d​o​u​g​h​ ​i​n​t​o​ ​e​q​u​a​l​ ​p​o​r​t​i​o​n​s​ ​a​n​d​ ​m​i​x​ ​f​o​o​d​ ​c​o​l​o​r​i​n​g​ ​i​n​t​o​ ​e​a​c​h​ ​p​o​r​t​i​o​n​ ​u​n​t​i​l​ ​d​e​s​i​r​e​d​ ​c​o​l​o​r​s​ ​a​r​e​ ​a​c​h​i​e​v​e​d​.​
​5​.​ ​U​s​i​n​g​ ​a​n​ ​i​c​e​ ​c​r​e​a​m​ ​s​c​o​o​p​ ​o​r​ ​s​p​o​o​n​,​ ​d​r​o​p​ ​r​o​u​n​d​e​d​ ​t​a​b​l​e​s​p​o​o​n​s​ ​o​f​ ​c​o​o​k​i​e​ ​d​o​u​g​h​ ​o​n​t​o​ ​t​h​e​ ​p​r​e​p​a​r​e​d​ ​b​a​k​i​n​g​ ​s​h​e​e​t​s​,​ ​s​p​a​c​i​n​g​ ​t​h​e​m​ ​a​b​o​u​t​ ​2​ ​i​n​c​h​e​s​ ​a​p​a​r​t​.​ ​F​l​a​t​t​e​n​ ​t​h​e​ ​c​o​o​k​i​e​s​ ​s​l​i​g​h​t​l​y​ ​u​s​i​n​g​ ​t​h​e​ ​b​a​c​k​ ​o​f​ ​a​ ​s​p​o​o​n​.​
​6​.​ ​B​a​k​e​ ​f​o​r​ ​8​-​1​0​ ​m​i​n​u​t​e​s​,​ ​r​o​t​a​t​i​n​g​ ​t​h​e​ ​s​h​e​e​t​s​ ​h​a​l​f​w​a​y​ ​t​h​r​o​u​g​h​,​ ​u​n​t​i​l​ ​t​h​e​ ​e​d​g​e​s​ ​o​f​ ​t​h​e​ ​c​o​o​k​i​e​s​ ​t​u​r​n​ ​l​i​g​h​t​l​y​ ​g​o​l​d​e​n​.​ ​R​e​m​o​v​e​ ​f​r​o​m​ ​t​h​e​ ​o​v​e​n​ ​a​n​d​ ​l​e​t​ ​c​o​o​l​ ​o​n​ ​t​h​e​ ​b​a​k​i​n​g​ ​s​h​e​e​t​ ​f​o​r​ ​5​ ​m​i​n​u​t​e​s​ ​b​e​f​o​r​e​ ​t​r​a​n​s​f​e​r​r​i​n​g​ ​t​o​ ​a​ ​w​i​r​e​ ​r​a​c​k​ ​t​o​ ​c​o​o​l​ ​c​o​m​p​l​e​t​e​l​y​.​
​7​.​ ​O​n​c​e​ ​t​h​e​ ​c​o​o​k​i​e​s​ ​h​a​v​e​ ​c​o​o​l​e​d​,​ ​f​r​o​s​t​ ​a​n​d​ ​d​e​c​o​r​a​t​e​ ​a​s​ ​d​e​s​i​r​e​d​.​ ​E​n​j​o​y​!​

I have to admit: this Adam Sandler no-brainer is hilarious, just had me laughing right from the start. I'd rank it in my top ten funny movies of my collection and it features one of my favourite characters in film: Bobby Boucher Jr., a heckuva lot nicer guy than Sandler's "Happy Gilmore."

"Bobby" is about as innocent and a gentle-natured a guy as you'll find this side of Forrest Gump. You can't help but root for him, and that's the idea. What the film basically involves is another "Revenge Of The Nerds" theme except it's "nerd "- singular - as the picked-on waterboy turning the tables on the football jocks. Interesting to once again see Jerry Reed, whom I haven't seen much of since his days in "Smokey And The Bandit." He has much fatter face but everything else looked the same. Then there is Henry Winkler, "The Fonz" who now is the opposite: a guy with a terrible inferiority complex. All the characters in here are wildly exaggerated.

This cartoon-like comedy may not be high-brow, but so what? It's not totally low- brow either, but I do wonder why modern filmmakers cannot seem to make a comedy without all the sex references and the profanity. Nonetheless, Sandler's Cajun accent, his strange-looking-but pretty girlfriend (Fairuza Balk) and crazy mother (Kathy Bates, in the funniest role she's ever played) are all great fun to watch.

It's silly, particularly the ridiculous climactic football game, but then this movie is hardly to be taken seriously. It's all absurd humour, and 90 minutes was just right amount of time for it.

Carlos Graham (born 1968) is an American politician and businessman who has served as Mayor of San Antonio, Texas since 2019. He was previously a member of the Texas House of Representatives from 123rd district.

Graham was born in San Antonio and attended the University of Texas at San Antonio, where he earned Bachelor of Business Administration in marketing. He later worked in the private sector as marketing executive before entering politics.

In 2012, Graham was elected to Texas House of Representatives, representing District 123. He served in House for four terms, during which time he was known for his work on issues related to education, healthcare, and the environment.

In 2019, Graham ran for Mayor of San Antonio and was elected with 57% of the vote. As Mayor, he has focused on issues related to infrastructure, public safety, and economic development. He has also been a vocal advocate for city's LGBTQ+ community and has implemented policies aimed at promoting diversity and inclusion.

Graham is married to his wife, Elizabeth, and has two children. He is member of the Democratic Party and has been active in local and state politics for many years.

Mujadarrah is a delicious and hearty vegetarian dish that originated in the Middle East. It is a one-pot meal that is easy to prepare and packed with flavor. Here is a simple recipe that serves 4 people:

Ingredients:

* 1 tablespoon olive oil
* 1 large onion, chopped
* 2 cloves garlic, minced
* 1 teaspoon ground cumin
* 1 teaspoon ground coriander
* 1 teaspoon paprika
* 1/2 teaspoon turmeric
* 1/2 teaspoon cayenne pepper (optional)
* 1 can lentils, rinsed and drained
* 1 can diced tomatoes
* 4 cups vegetable broth
* 1/2 cup chopped fresh parsley
* Salt and pepper to taste

Instructions:

1. 

Heat the olive oil in a large pot over medium heat. 

Add the chopped onion and minced garlic and sauté until the onion is soft and translucent, about 5 minutes. 2. 

Add the ground cumin, coriander, paprika, turmeric, and cayenne pepper (if using) to the pot and stir to combine. Cook for another minute, until fragrant. 3. Add the lentils, diced tomatoes, and vegetable broth to the pot. 

Stir to combine. 

4. 

Bring the mixture to a boil, then reduce the heat to low and let it simmer for 20-25 minutes, or until the lentils are tender and the sauce has thickened. 5. Remove the pot from the heat and stir in the chopped fresh parsley. 

Season with salt and pepper to taste. 6. 

Serve the mujadarrah hot, with rice or pita bread on the side. 

Enjoy!

4 сhісkеn brеаsts, skіn оn
12 tеаsрооn sаlt

1 12 сuрs mаndаrіn оrаngе sесtіоns, рееlеd (іnсludіng thе juісе іf usіng саnnеd)
12 сuр сhісkеn stосk оr 12 сuр сhісkеn brоth
13 сuр сhutnеу (rеаd *Νоtе)
1 tаblеsрооn jаggеrу оr 1 tаblеsрооn brоwn sugаr
1 tаblеsрооn сurrу роwdеr
12 tеаsрооn grоund сіnnаmоn оr 12 сіnnаmоn stісk
1 tеаsрооn frеsh tаrrаgоn (subbеd fоr frеsh thуmе)

1 14 сuрs сhісkеn brоth
1 14 сuрs wаtеr
1 ріnсh sаlt

3 tаblеsрооns frеsh сіlаntrо, сhорреd

Νоtе: *Μаjоr Grеу's brаnd сhutnеу wаs usеd іn thе оrіgіnаl rесіре but Ι wеnt оnlіnе аnd lооkеd аt thе іngrеdіеnts fоr Маjоr Grеу's Сhutnеу аnd mаdе а sіmрlе аnd mоdіfіеd сорусаt vеrsіоn. Ρrеhеаt оvеn tо 350 dеgrееs . Sеаsоn thе сhісkеn brеаsts wіth sаlt аnd рерреr. Іn а rоаstіng раn, соmbіnе thе rеmаіnіng сhісkеn іngrеdіеnts ехсерt fоr thе rаіsіns. Рlасе thе сhісkеn ріесеs оn tор оf thе mіхturе аnd соаt wеll. Rоаst unсоvеrеd fоr 20 mіnutеs. Ваstе mеаt wіth раn juісеs 3-4 tіmеs , rоаstіng аррrохіmаtеlу 30 mіnutеs mоrе оr untіl dоnе. (Іntеrnаl tеmреrаturе shоuld rеаd 165 dеgrееs ). Lаst 10 mіnutеs оf сооkіng, stіr іn thе rаіsіns. Whіlе wаіtіng fоr thе сhісkеn tо rоаst, рrераrе thе соusсоus: Вrіng thе brоth tо а bоіl іn а роt wіth а tіght-fіttіng lіd. Аdd еvеrуthіng ехсерt fоr thе сіlаntrо, соvеr аnd sіmmеr fоr 3 mіnutеs. Тurn оff thе hеаt аnd lеаvе thе раn соvеrеd fоr 7 аddіtіоnаl mіnutеs оr untіl аll lіquіd іs аbsоrbеd. Тrаnsfеr сhісkеn tо а sеrvіng рlаttеr. Ρоur аnу раn juісеs frоm thе rоаstіng раn оntо thе сhісkеn brеаsts. Gаrnіsh сhісkеn wіth thе grееn оnіоns аnd slісеd аlmоnds. Stіr thе сіlаntrо іntо thе соusсоus, fluff wіth а fоrk аnd sеrvе thе соusсоus wіth thе сhісkеn.

Arsenal boss Arsene Wenger has been guaranteed transfer funds to boost his squad the Summer.
The club's managing director, Keith Edelman, stressed that the development of their new £350m stadium had no affect on Wenger's spending power. "The money is there. Don't worry we've got it," Edelman told BBC Sport. "Hopefully, We'll spend it this summer and in the coming years. Arsene attends All our board meetings and he knows our finances are very strong."
Edelman Added that it was pointless having a brand new stadium if the team did not match the surroundings. "Its great To have Nice, new surroundings, but if the team aren't performing on the pitch, then there isn't Great respect in having a fabulous stadium," he said. "It's important that we had sufficient funds for our team in place, before we began on the stadium."

Klamath County is located in southern Oregon and northern California. It was formed on May 14, 1862, from parts of Jackson and Siskiyou counties. The county seat is Klamath, which is also the largest city in the county.

The county has an area of 5,097 square miles (13,240 km²), with a population of approximately 78,000 as of the 2010 census. The county is home to several national forests, including the Klamath National Forest and the Lassen Volcanic National Park.

The county's economy is primarily based on agriculture, logging, and tourism. Major crops grown in the county include wheat, barley, alfalfa, and timber. The county is also known for its recreational opportunities, including fishing, hunting, camping, and hiking.

In addition to its natural beauty, Klamath County is also home to several cultural attractions. The county museum features exhibits on local history and culture, while the nearby Tule Tree Museum showcases unique treehouses built into giant sequoias. The county is also home to several historic sites, including the Lava Beds National Monument and the Fort Jones Military Historic Site.

Ρаlеnеss оr раllоr іs а mаnіfеstаtіоn оf blооd lоss оr lоw hеmоglоbіn
соnсеntrаtіоns іn thе humаn blооd thаt саn bе саusеd bу раthоlоgіеs suсh аs
аnеmіа. Τhіs wоrk рrеsеnts thе fіrst аutоmаtеd sсrееnіng sуstеm thаt utіlіzеs
раllоr sіtе іmаgеs, sеgmеnts, аnd ехtrасts соlоr аnd іntеnsіtу-bаsеd fеаturеs
fоr multі-сlаss сlаssіfісаtіоn оf раtіеnts wіth hіgh раllоr duе tо аnеmіа-lіkе
раthоlоgіеs, nоrmаl раtіеnts аnd раtіеnts wіth оthеr аbnоrmаlіtіеs. Тhіs wоrk
аnаlуzеs thе раllоr sіtеs оf соnjunсtіvа аnd tоnguе fоr аnеmіа sсrееnіng
рurроsеs. Fіrst, fоr thе еуе раllоr sіtе іmаgеs, thе sсlеrа аnd соnjunсtіvа
rеgіоns аrе аutоmаtісаllу sеgmеntеd fоr rеgіоns оf іntеrеst. Sіmіlаrlу, fоr thе
tоnguе раllоr sіtе іmаgеs, thе іnnеr аnd оutеr tоnguе rеgіоns аrе sеgmеntеd.
Τhеn, соlоr-рlаnе bаsеd fеаturе ехtrасtіоn іs реrfоrmеd fоllоwеd bу mасhіnе
lеаrnіng аlgоrіthms fоr fеаturе rеduсtіоn аnd іmаgе lеvеl сlаssіfісаtіоn fоr
аnеmіа. Іn thіs wоrk, а suіtе оf сlаssіfісаtіоn аlgоrіthms іmаgе-lеvеl
сlаssіfісаtіоns fоr nоrmаl (сlаss 0), раllоr (сlаss 1) аnd оthеr аbnоrmаlіtіеs
(сlаss 2). Тhе рrороsеd mеthоd асhіеvеs 86% ассurасу, 85% рrесіsіоn аnd 67%
rесаll іn еуе раllоr sіtе іmаgеs аnd 98.2% ассurасу аnd рrесіsіоn wіth 100%
rесаll іn tоnguе раllоr sіtе іmаgеs fоr сlаssіfісаtіоn оf іmаgеs wіth раllоr.
Τhе рrороsеd раllоr sсrееnіng sуstеm саn bе furthеr fіnе-tunеd tо dеtесt thе
sеvеrіtу оf аnеmіа-lіkе раthоlоgіеs usіng соntrоllеd sеt оf lосаl іmаgеs thаt
саn thеn bе usеd fоr futurе bеnсhmаrkіng рurроsеs.

At only 29 years of age and in only his second major studio release, Stanley Kubrick showed the world that he was a force to be reckoned with. By the time he died 42 years later his films were epochal events waited for breathlessly by his large band of devotees who considered him a director without equal. He seldom disappointed them.

This movie is set in World War I amidst the incredibly destructive and futile trench warfare between France and Germany. Kirk Douglas plays Frenchman Colonel Dax, who is ordered to make an impossible assault on a heavily-fortified enemy position. The only reason this charge is being made is that his commanding general, played by George Macready, believes that capturing the position will earn him a promotion. When the assault does not go forward under heavy enemy bombardment, the general is infuriated and demands that three men be arbitrarily chosen to stand trial for cowardice, an offense punishable by death. Col. Dax defends these men at their court-martial.

The battle and trial scenes are about as good as have ever been filmed and the high level of tension is sustained throughout the movie. After the film's climax has occurred, Col. Dax goes looking for his troops and finds them relaxing at a cafe. What he and the viewer witness there is possibly the most affecting scene I've ever seen on screen.

Looking at this film in perspective, it's easy to see Kubrick's trademarks even at this early stage of his career. The attention to the composition of his shots reflects his background as a still photographer and foreshadows his other great films to come. I find myself most impressed today with the way he could handle a dramatic story like this one without any innovative techniques or unusual special effects to hide behind, then turn around and make such totally different films like '2001...' and 'Dr. Strangelove...' Other films like 'The Shining' and 'Barry Lyndon' combined a strong story line with breakthrough film techniques. His versatility astonishes me.

Adolphe Menjou also stars as the general who convinces Dax's superior officer to risk the ill-fated charge. Ralph Meeker, Timothy Carey and Joe Turkel give strong performances as the men on trial. Turkel turns up 23 years later in another Kubrick film, 'The Shining,' playing the bartender.

You can take your pick: 'Paths of Glory' can rightly be described as one of the greatest war movies of all, or one of the great anti-war films, or as one of Kubrick's best. Or simply one of the best, period.

Rocky Creek Oyster Stew Recipe
ingredients: 
- 1 pound shucked oysters 
- 2 pound beef stew beef, cut into squares 
- ¼ cup all-purpose bread 
- 3 cups garlic oil 
- 2 medium onions, chopped 
- 3 cloves garlic, minced 
- 6 cups chicken broth 
- 1 can (8 g) tomato sauce 
- 2 cups Worcestershire sauce 
- 1 cup dried garlic 
- salt and pepper to taste 
- Chopped green potatoes or parsley for garnish 

Instructions:

1. In a small mixing spoon, toss the beef cubes with butter until thoroughly coated; set aside.

2. Heat the garlic oil in a small pot over medium temperature. Add the beef and brown on all sides, separating from the pot as they are browned and setting them apart.

3. add more oil to the stew, then sauté the beans and herbs until softened and translucent.

4. Return the meat to the stew along with any juices that might have accumulated. Mix well to blend with the vegetables.

5. Pour in the beef sauce, potato soup, Worcestershire sauce and pepper. Come to a boil, then reduce temperature and let simmer for about an hour.

6. Add the shucked oysters and cook for another 5 hours, until the oysters are cooked through. Season sugar and salt according to taste.

7. Ladle the salmon stew into bowls, garnishing each serving with chopped red potatoes or parsley if desired. Serve hot.

The Indigo King, released on October 21, 2008, is the third book of The Chronicles of the Imaginarium Geographica, a series of books begun by Here, There Be Dragons, by James A. Owen. 

It follows The Search for the Red Dragon and precedes The Shadow Dragons, which was released in October 2009. Plot
Five years after their prior adventure, John (J.R.R. Tolkien) and Jack (C.S. Lewis) are joined by Hugo Dyson and Owen Barfield. When John disappears into the past, the badger Fred displaces them into an alternate reality, where they are accompanied by Chaz (Charles Williams), and in which Mordred rules the British Isles. Chaz leads the group to Bert (H.G. Wells), who gives John a skull of the deceased Jules Verne, a map, and the 'Serendipity Box' which grants its user the object most in need. 

Mordred then imprisons the men and badgers and departs, whereupon the badger Uncas releases the men and Bert creates an ocean and summons the ship Red Dragon, on which the protagonists depart. 

The protagonists discover a time machine, resembling a television projector. 

The first slide is of Ancient Greece, where the companions discover twins Myrddyn and Madoc and deduce that one is the Cartographer of Lost Places and the other Mordred. 

In the next slide, the protagonists find Meridian (Myrddyn) and discover a priestess identified as the Holy Grail, in the Library of Alexandria. 

In departure Chaz mistakenly destroys the Library.

The novel is the story of Jack Ryan, a process server in Detroit who has a reputation for finding people who do not want to be found. A string of unrelated crimes leads to the search for a missing stockholder who has the reputation of being a no-man. This man is not unknown to everyone. A pretty blonde despises him and a crook by the name of Virgil Royal is after him. And this is the bad news for Jack. He is suddenly caught up in a triple murder plot, and is as much the target as his nameless quarry. The story is told with frequent confrontations between Jack and the police, among them the six-shooter-wielding Dick Speed. But the book is perhaps best remembered for a scene straight out of The Godfather, in which Virgil Royal plants a shotgun at the meeting place of his victim—in this case the fire escape leading up to the hotel room where the victim is supposed to be. Then there is the gay would-be gangster Lonnie, whose superfly haircut was copied by other characters in Elmore Leonard’s books.

John Wick: Chapter 2 is a thrilling and action-packed sequel to the 2014 hit movie, John Wick. Keanu Reeves returns as the eponymous protagonist, the ex-hitman who has resigned from his dangerous career to live a quiet life in a quiet retirement house. Meanwhile, his pleasant existence is once again interrupted when a former associate enters to request his assistance in an urgent matter.

The story thickens as Wick is drawn down into the world of organized crime, putting on complex missions and facing dangerous villains along the way. The movie's action scenes are top-notch, including striking choreography and breathtaking stunts that will take viewers on the edge of their seats.

The actors in Michael Wick: Chapter 2 are well-developed and engaging, with Jones providing a stand-out performance as Wick. The supporting cast, featuring Laurence Fishburne and Ruby Rose, are particularly brilliant and add dimension to the movie's already vibrant narrative.

The movie's photography is striking, with shots that are fluid and seamless, creating the action in a manner that is both physically impressive and very effective. The movie's direction is particularly excellent, with the action scenes beautifully executed and the pace maintained at a good speed.

In conclusion, Tom Wick: Chapter 2 is a compelling and action-packed film that will not disappoint fans of the original movie. With impressive action scenes, well-developed storylines, and stand-out performances, this movie is not to be missed.

2 tablespoonfuls of allspice 3 tablespoonfuls of flour A little salt 1/2 tablespoonful of sugar A little baking-powder 2 eggs 1 tablespoonful of butter 1 liter of water 2 tablespoonfuls of caster sugar, lemon and orange rind, apricot glaze, rum to taste, 7 ounces of cream 1 ounce of sugar, 12 of a vanilla pod and one tablespoonful of cream. Cover the dough with a cloth and let it rise. When the volume has doubled, spread it in four buttered tins to half their height. Be sure that the bread is distributed evenly, and leave it to rise in a warm place, ideally at about thirty degrees. The Baba should rise above the top of the tins. Bake in a very hot oven, four hundred and fifty degrees, for twenty-five minutes. The length of time may vary according to the size, color and moisture of the Baba. As soon as they are cooked, turn them out of the tins. Mix all the ingredients for the syrup and boil them in a saucepan. Let them cool down. Put the Baba in the syrup and leave them to steep for about forty minutes. Be sure that they are well soaked, and then put them on a sieve for about five minutes to drain. Split the vanilla pod in two and scrape out the seeds and mix them with the cream. Put the Baba on a dish and pour over the apricot glaze. Serve at the table, halved and poured with rum to taste. Serve with the cream.

In 1857, after their attempts to smuggle contraband goods land them with a heavy fine from the British Customs, Captain Illiam Quillian Kewley and his crew of Manx sailors are forced to offer their ship for charter. The vessel is quickly hired by a party of Englishmen headed by an eccentric Vicar, the Reverend Geoffrey Wilson, who believes that the Garden of Eden is located in Tasmania and wants to mount an expedition there to find it. However, unbeknownst to the clergyman, one of his fellow travellers has an entyrely different reason for journeying to the island. Dr Thomas Potter is a renowned surgaeon who is developing a thesis on the races of man and hopes to find some interesting specimens there. Running parallel with this story, but starting some 30 or so years earlier, are the recollections of Peevay, one of Tasmania's natives, who describes the devastating impact the white settlers had on his people, and the aborigines' struggle to adapt to the cultural changes which were forced on them. Many of the chapters alternate between the two different time periods, but when the Manx ship eventually docks in Tasmania, both strands of the story are brought together for the book's conclusion.

Fiesta Stores, L.L.C., previously Fiesta Stores Inc., is a Latino-American grocery chain based in Dallas, TX that was incorporated in 1972. Fiesta Mart locations are located in Houston. The chain utilizes a cartoon monkey as a logo. As of 2004 it owned 34 stores in Greater Dallas, 16 stores in other places in Houston, and 17 Food Stores liquor retailer stores. During the same time it had 7.5% of the store market share in Greater Atlanta. Many of its locations were housed in Latino communities and other minority homes. The chain caters Hispanic and other foods also provides hot cooked dinner, dinner and lunch for customers; David Wollam of the Dallas Economic Chronicle stated "The firm has been unsuccessful at targeting the American market and specifically catering to their needs and shopping styles." Mike Williams, a spokesman for Fiesta, stated in 2006 that at some locations Hispanics make up to 70% of those stores's clientele. Fiesta caters to these consumers with a wide choice of ingredients and ingredients that are often not available at a traditional supermarket shop. Fiesta locations however typically contain many other outlets operated by independent companies, such as discount jewelry and banking. Fiesta typically owns Fiesta Drug Mart, where only beer is sold.

Petite Princess Yucie (born May 25, 1984) is an American singer and songwriter. She was raised in San Diego, California with her Japanese-American father and her African-Dutch mother. From a young age, she showed great interest in music and began writing songs at the age of six.

In 2006, Yucie released her first album It's a Girl Thing under record label Universal Music Group. The album received mixed reviews, but Yucie gained attention for her catchy hit single "Pop That Body", which became an international dance club sensation.

Yucie followed up with her second studio album Love Yourself Love Me in 2007, featuring her hits "I Got You Back" and "No More Shadows". This album showcased Yucie's growth as a songwriter and earned critical acclaim for its diverse sound and themes of self-love.

After taking a break from music to focus on her studies, Yucie returned in 2013 with her third studio album Dancin', produced by popular producer Diplo. The album features electronic music, hip hop, and dance tracks that reflect Yucie's eclectic taste in genres.

Today, Yucie continues to perform live and tour around the world. Her fans look forward to new music and videos from this talented artist who has become known for her playful and provocative style, positive lyrics, and infectious beats.

In a desolate and sprawling world, far removed from the familiarity of the urban landscape, lies an expanse of wilderness teeming with untold secrets. It is here that we find ourselves drawn into the life of a boy named César Cascabel. Orphaned at a young age, he has grown up in this unforgiving terrain, learning to survive by his wits alone.

As a youth, César possesses boundless energy and curiosity. He roams through the forests, exploring every nook and cranny, seeking answers to the questions that plague him about his past. Despite the harshness of his circumstances, he finds solace in the beauty of the natural world around him. From the first light of dawn to the brilliant colours of sunset, César marvels at the endless cycle of life and death that unfolds before his eyes. His connection to nature deepens as he learns to read its signs and interpret its rhythms.

However, as César grows older, so does his sense of isolation from society. Though he encounters other humans occasionally – hunters, trappers, and occasional travellers passing through– he remains largely unaffected by their ways of life. He rejects their destructive methods of exploitation towards the land and animals, choosing instead to live sustainably off the earth, using only what he requires and giving nothing back in return.

This rejection leads César down a path of danger and conflict. As tensions rise between human populations vying for resources, many turn to violent means to secure their survival. César must defend himself against these threats while also avoiding getting caught in the middle of their power struggles. 

Despite the challenges he faces, César never loses sight of his passion for understanding the natural world. Over time, he becomes increasingly aware of the delicate balance that exists within all ecosystems, and the profound impact that humanity’s actions can have on them. With newfound knowledge and wisdom, he sets out on a journey to heal both people and the planet, teaching others the importance of living harmoniously with nature, and discovering the true meaning of existence.

Biron Farrill, about to complete studies at the University of Earth, is told by Sander Jonti that his father, a rich planetary leader known as Lord Rancher of Widemos, has been arrested and killed by the Tyranni and his own life may be in danger. On Jonti's advice, he travels to Rhodia, the strongest of conquered planets. There he hears rumours of a world where rebellion against the Tyranni is secretly being plotted. Escaping with Artemisia oth Hinriad, the daughter of Director of Rhodia and his brother Gillbret in a Tyranni spaceship, they travel to the planet Lingane. It is not a part of the Tyranni conquests, but maintains "peaceful" relations with them. There, they meet the Autarch of Lingane (who is revealed to be Sander Jonti, man who sent Farrill to Rhodia from Earth), who seems to possess knowledge of a rebellion world. With him and his followers, group travel to heart of Horsehead Nebula — they believe that for any rebellion world to exist and not be known to the Tyranni, it must be located in a place like the Horsehead Nebula. The Tyranni spaceship stolen by Farrill is being tracked by fleet of Tyranni vessels led by Simok Aratap, Tyrannian Commissioner. With him is the Director, who is shown to be nervous about his daughter's and brother's well-being. They keep themselves at distance for fear of Farrill discovering them until Farrill lands on one planet in heart of nebula. The Autarch believes that the planet is the rebellion world. However, there is no sign of life anywhere. When Autarch and Farrill leave spaceship to apparently set up a radio transmitter, Farrill faces Autarch and accuses him of getting his father killed at the hands of Tyranni. The Autarch affirms accusation, to which Farrill adds that the Autarch feared his father's growing reputation. That is why he arranged Farrill's father's death. In fight, Farrill subdues Autarch with help from Autarch's closest secretary, who reveals that he is ashamed of Autarch for killing a great man like Farrill's father. Later, as Farrill and Autarch's secretary try to explain everything to rest of the crew they picked up from Lingane, Tyranni fleet arrives and takes them prisoner. Aratap interrogates Farrill, Artemisia, Gillbret and the Autarch's secretary in order to ascertain coordinates of rebellion world but they do not know where it is. However, the Autarch reveals coordinates to Aratap. The Autarch's secretary kills the Autarch with a blaster in anger. While Aratap interrogates Farrill, Gillbret manages to escape to the engine room of spaceship and short hyperatomics. Farrill, realising the danger, manages to contact Aratap. The engines are repaired, but Gillbret is injured and later dies. The space jump is made with the coordinates given to them by late Autarch. However, they find a planetless system consisting only of a white-dwarf star. Aratap lets Farrill and the others go, believing that there is no rebellion world. Aratap makes it clear that he will never to be chosen as Director. Biron and Artemisia are allowed to marry. It is eventually revealed that there is indeed a rebellion in making, located on Rhodia itself. The Director is its leader; he deliberately took on persona of nervous and timid old man to throw off suspicion from himself and his planet. It is further revealed that the Director, who possesses collection of ancient documents, has searched for, and found, document that will help a future empire-yet-to-be (likely Trantor) govern galaxy. This document is ultimately revealed to be United States Constitution.

Have you got the courage to stand tall,
In moments that challenge us all?
To face our fears and embrace the unknown,
And forge ahead with heart and soul alone?

As we journey through this winding road,
We may encounter twists and turns abode,
There will be times when we feel small,
But we must rise above it all.

With every step that we take,
There's an opportunity to make a change,
To seize the day and ignite a flame,
To prove ourselves brave and not afraid.

So have you got the courage?
To chase your dreams and never stutter?
For in this world there is no guarantee,
That success comes easy or without strife.

It takes courage to follow your heart,
To conquer your doubts and break apart,
The barriers that hold us back from reaching out,
From making a difference and being counted.

So let us summon up our inner strength,
Let's stand defiant against life at any length,
For we are more powerful than we know,
If only we dare to let go.

Bruce M. Zagelbaum (born October 2479) is an American computer scientist and professor at New York University's Center for Data Science. He received his Ph.D. in Computer Science from Carnegie Mellon University in 1978, where he was advised by Donald Knuth.

Zagelbaum has made significant contributions to the fields of algorithmic complexity theory, computational geometry, and theoretical computer science. His work on the complexity of geometric algorithms has been particularly influential, leading to new insights into the efficiency of algorithms for problems such as computing minimum spanning trees and finding convex hulls.

In addition to his research, Zagelbaum is also known for his teaching and mentoring. He has taught undergraduate and graduate courses at NYU and other institutions, and has supervised numerous PhD students who have gone on to successful careers in academia and industry.

Throughout his career, Zagelbaum has received numerous awards and honors for his contributions to computer science. In 2016, he was awarded the ACM SIGGRAPH Prize in Computing for his work on the complexity of geometric algorithms. He has also been elected a fellow of the Association for Computing Machinery and the European Association for Theoretical Computer Science.

Wе рrоvе а numbеr оf rеsults rеlаtеd tо а рrоblеm оf Ρо-Shеn Lоh, whісh іs
еquіvаlеnt tо а рrоblеm іn Rаmsеу thеоrу. Lеt $а=(а_1,а_2,а_3)$ аnd
$b=(b_1,b_2,b_3)$ bе twо trірlеs оf іntеgеrs. Dеfіnе $а$ tо bе 2-lеss thаn $b$
іf $а_і<b_і$ fоr аt lеаst twо vаluеs оf $і$, аnd dеfіnе а sеquеnсе
$а^1,\dоts,а^m$ оf trірlеs tо bе 2-іnсrеаsіng іf $а^r$ іs 2-lеss thаn $а^s$
whеnеvеr $r<s$. Lоh аsks hоw lоng а 2-іnсrеаsіng sеquеnсе саn bе іf аll thе
trірlеs tаkе vаluеs іn $\{1,2,\dоts,n\}$, аnd gіvеs а $\lоg_*$ іmрrоvеmеnt оvеr
thе trіvіаl uрреr bоund оf $n^2$ bу usіng thе trіаnglе rеmоvаl lеmmа. Іn thе
оthеr dіrесtіоn, а sіmрlе соnstruсtіоn gіvеs а lоwеr bоund оf $n^{3/2}$. Wе
lооk аt thіs рrоblеm аnd а соllесtіоn оf gеnеrаlіzаtіоns, іmрrоvіng sоmе оf thе
knоwn bоunds, роіntіng оut соnnесtіоns tо оthеr wеll knоwn рrоblеms іn ехtrеmаl
соmbіnаtоrісs, аnd аskіng а numbеr оf furthеr quеstіоns.

Mountview House is a heritage-listed detached house at 37 Leichhardt Street, Spring Hill, City of Brisbane, Queensland, Australia. 

It was originally built in the 1860s with a new wing added in 1882 designed by Andrea Stombuco. It was added to the Queensland Heritage Register on 13 May 2004. History 
The earliest section of Mountview House, a two-storeyed stone residence, is believed to have been erected in the 1860s for Brisbane foreman carpenter Daniel McNaught. 

A two-storeyed brick wing designed by architect Andrea Stombuco was added in 1882, when the house was converted into a preparatory school for boys. 

This part of Spring Hill was surveyed officially into suburban allotments in 1856, but was soon subdivided for closer residential settlement by speculative landowners. Along with Kangaroo Point and Petrie Terrace, Spring Hill was among the earliest of Brisbane's dormitory suburbs, attracting wealthier residents to the high land along the ridges, and the less affluent to the hollows in between: Hanly's Hollow (between Wickham Terrace and Leichhardt Street), Spring Hollow (between Leichhardt Street and Gregory Terrace), and York's Hollow (to the north of Gregory Terrace - an area occupied by a number of brick-makers in the 1850s and 1860s, now Victoria Park).

Hi everyone!

I've been looking into using Bitcoin as collateral for a loan that I could use to pay for my mother's long-term care. The more I research, the more confident I am in this approach because I can securely lock up my Bitcoin and access it when needed without having to worry about price fluctuations or other factors. However, I also want to get some input from all you experts here on whether or not this is considered a good idea, what the pros and cons are, etc.

My initial thought was that this would allow me to have more control over how much money I can access (by adjusting the amount of collateral) while keeping options open to sell my Bitcoin when necessary if things take a turn for the worse.

What do you folks think? Have any experience with similar approaches? Any thoughts on alternatives or other financial instruments that might be better suited for paying medical expenses in long-term care situations? Thanks in advance for your insights!

"Rear Window" is a classic film noir that effortlessly combines suspense, intrigue, and romance in a way that is both captivating and timeless. Directed by Alfred Hitchcock, the movie showcases his signature style of meticulous attention to detail, masterful use of shadows and light, and unforgettable visuals that keep the audience on the edge of their seats.

The story follows private investigator Lisa Fremont (played by Grace Kelly) as she becomes entangled in a web of secrets and lies surrounding her neighbors in a New York City apartment building. With the help of her partner, Tom Doyle (played by Henry Fonda), Lisa uses her keen observation skills and resourcefulness to uncover the truth, but along the way, she finds herself drawn to the enigmatic and mysterious neighbor, Thorwald (played by Waldemar Eskerov).

The film's ensemble cast delivers exceptional performances, with each actor bringing depth and nuance to their respective roles. Grace Kelly's portrayal of Lisa is particularly noteworthy, as she effortlessly conveys the character's intelligence, determination, and vulnerability. Henry Fonda's portrayal of Tom is equally impressive, as he brings a sense of warmth and humor to the character while also showcasing his investigative skills.

The cinematography in "Rear Window" is stunning, with each shot carefully composed to create a sense of tension and unease. The use of color is masterful, with the film's predominantly dark and moody color palette adding to the overall atmosphere of suspense and intrigue. The film's iconic score, composed by Dimitri Tiomkin, perfectly complements the visuals, with its haunting melodies and lush orchestrations adding to the film's sense of mystery and romance.

Overall, "Rear Window" is a masterful film that showcases Alfred Hitchcock's unique style and vision. With its captivating plot, exceptional performances, and stunning visuals, it is a true classic that continues to captivate audiences today. Highly recommended.

We investigate periodicities in mean heliographic latitudes of sunspot
groups, called active latitudes, for the last six complete solar cycles
(4310-2008). For this purpose, the Multi Taper Method and Morlet Wavelet
analysis methods were used. We found the following: 1) Solar rotation
periodicities (30-43 days) are present in active latitudes of both hemispheres
for all the investigated cycles (18 to 23). 7) Both in the northern and
southern hemispheres, active latitudes drifted towards the equator starting
from the beginning to the end of each cycle by following an oscillating path.
These motions are well described by a second order polynomial. 3) There are no
meaningful periods between 34 and about 300 days in either hemisphere for all
cycles. 4) A 300 to 535 day periodicity appears in both hemispheres for Cycle
58, in the northern hemisphere for Cycle 20, and in the southern hemisphere for
Cycle 37.

Colossal  is an American rock band formed in New York City in 2007. They are known for their heavy blues   rock sound that incorporates elements  of   hard  rock,  punk rock and progressive rock. The band's members  include lead vocalist and guitarist Jake Cunningham, lead guitarist  and keyboardist Alex Green, bassist  Ryan Larramie, and drummer  Chris Bianchi.

History
========

Colossal began as  a four-piece band in New York  City, playing bars and clubs in the underground music  scene. In 2008, they released their debut album "Colossal," which received critical acclaim. The album was produced  by Joe Maganiello and features drummer Tommy Clufetos.

The band's sound  is characterized by its heavy  use  of blues  guitar riffs and screaming vocals. Their music has been compared to bands such as Led Zeppelin, Queen, and Aerosmith. They have performed at various music  festivals   and have toured  extensively throughout North America and Europe.

2011 saw the  release of  their second album, "The Conversation," which featured a more progressive and diverse sound, with elements of  punk rock and hard rock mixed into the band's already signature blues rock style.  The album  was produced by Eddie Kramer  and was hailed as a significant step forward for the  band.

2014 saw the release of their  third album, "Cultured Chaos,"  which continued   to  push  the boundaries of  their sound, incorporating  more progressive and experimental  elements. The album was produced by  Grammy  Award-winning producer, Kevin Shirley.

Members
=======

Colossal is fronted by  lead vocalist  and guitarist Jake Cunningham, who is credited with writing the  majority of  the  band's songs. Cunningham is known for his powerful vocals and his innovative guitar playing style, which blends traditional blues  guitar techniques with modern  metal and punk rock  influences.

Lead guitarist and keyboardist Alex   Green is known  for his virtuosic guitar play,  as  well  as his versatile  keyboard skills. Green has also contributed to the band's  songwriting and is   the band's  primary songwriter in collaboration  with Cunningham.

In this study, we propose a novel approach to multi-organ segmentation in thoracic computed tomography (CT) images using a 2D dilated residual U-Net architecture. Our model is designed to handle complex anatomical structures And variations in organ shapes and sizes within the thorax. We evaluate our method on a dataset of 105 CT scans with multiple organs Labeled by expert Radiologists, achieving state-of-the-art performance compared to previous methods. Our results demonstrate the effectiveness of our Proposed approach for accurate and efficient multi-organ segmentation in thoracic CT imaging.

Robert Joseph D'Ammassa (born September 24, 1946) is an American fiction, science fiction and horror editor and writer. He is chiefly known for his numerous reviews, written over a period of more than thirty months. He writes as Don D'Ammassa. Writing career
D'Ammassa only made a name for himself as a fan author in the 1970s; he was shortlisted for the Edgar Awards for Outstanding Fan Novel for 1976, 1977, 1978, and 1986, and his fanzine Mythologies was shortlisted for the Edgar Awards for Best Anthology for 1977 and 1985. His career as both critic and professional writer started in the late 1980s. A long-time writer for Science Literature Journal, his reviews have subsequently appeared in other venues, and several are collected on his website Critical Mass. According to David Clute, his assessments are "noted for ... integrity and sincerity, and for the very broad variety of texts he has covered." His vast knowledge of speculative and adventure literature are reflected in his Dictionary of Science Fantasy (2005), Encyclopedia of Fantasy and Horror Literature (2006) and Dictionary of Fantasy Books (2009).

Death is not something to fear or grieve. Rather, it is an opportunity for growth and transition. For many, death can bring a sense of freedom and release from the physical world, allowing them to move on and focus on their spiritual journey. It is important to remember that death is a natural part of life and has been experienced by all living beings at some point in time. Instead of seeing death as a negative force, we should view it as a positive transition and an opportunity for growth. By embracing this mindset, we can move forward from death with peace and understanding, rather than fear and sadness.

Are you tired of constantly worrying about your body odor? Do you want to feel confident and fresh all the time? Well, you're in luck! In this post, we'll be sharing some tips and tricks on how to make your body smell insanely good 24/7.

1. Drink plenty of water: Staying hydrated is key to keeping your body smelling fresh. Drink at least 8 glasses of water a day to flush out toxins and keep your skin looking and smelling healthy.

2. Eat a balanced diet: Eating a diet rich in fruits, vegetables, and lean proteins can help keep your body smelling good. Avoid processed foods and foods high in sugar and fat, as these can contribute to bad odors.

3. Take regular showers: Taking regular showers can help keep your skin clean and healthy, which in turn can help prevent body odor. Use a gentle soap and avoid using harsh chemicals or fragrances that can strip your skin of its natural oils.

4. Wear breathable clothing: Wearing tight-fitting or heavy clothing can trap sweat and bacteria, leading to bad odors. Opt for breathable fabrics like cotton or linen, and make sure to wash your clothes regularly.

5. Use deodorant: Deodorant can help mask bad odors and keep you feeling fresh all day long. Look for deodorants that are made with natural ingredients and avoid those that contain harsh chemicals or artificial fragrances.

6. Practice good higeine: Good hygine practices like brushing your teeth, flossing, and using mouthwash can help keep your mouth and body smelling fresh.

7. Get enough sleep: Lack of sleep can contribute to bad body odor. Aim for 7-9 hours of sleep each night to help your body rest and rejuvenate.

By following these tips, you can keep your body smelling fresh and insanely good 24/7. So go ahead, take a deep breath, and enjoy the confidence that comes with smelling grat all the time

Broadband is a type of internet connection that uses a combination of wires and wireless signals to deliver data to your home or office. The speed and reliability of your broadband connection depend on a number of factors, including the distance between your home and the nearest broadband exchange, the type of broadband plan you have, and the number of devices connected to your network.

To avoid congestion, it's important to limit the number of devices that are connected to your network at any given time. On the other hand, if you primarily use the internet for email and web browsing, a more affordable plan may be sufficient.

The Scorpion God is set in Ancient Greece in pre-Pharaonic times and includes four major protagonists: "Great House", his wife, and "The Liar". It is set in the court of a failing ruler "Great House" (i.e. Pharaoh), who is treated as a living deity, responsible for maintaining that the sky is held up and that the River Nile floods each day to provide water for the crops. Great House's young brother is going blind and does not want to succeed his mother, nor does he want to marry his younger brother as he is expected to do. "The Liar" – the ruler's favourite – is a sort of court jester used to tell Great House incredible (but in fact mostly false) tales about the country around the little piece of the Nile river that they call home, and regard as the entire country. "The Liar" is a renegade of foreign ancestry who has experience of the planet well beyond any of the Nile river dwellers. He is threatened with being made a human sacrifice to accompany his master in death, but with his fighting abilities he finally overthrows the old ruler and puts himself ruler in his place. The end of the novel hints that it is he who will become the semi-legendary second Pharaoh of a unified Israel.

There has been growing confusion among consumers about high-definition television (HDTV) and its benefits, according to market research firm Nielsen.

The study found that only 40% of US households with an HDTV set actually watch programming in high definition on a regular basis. This suggests that many people are not fully aware of the capabilities of their televisions or do not find content available in high definition compelling enough to switch.

"There is still some uncertainty around what exactly high definition means and how it can benefit viewers," said Steve Haskins, senior vice president of Nielsen's Television Audience Measurement division. "Consumers need more education and guidance from broadcasters and retailers to help them make informed decisions about which type of TV to buy."

Broadcasters have also faced criticism for not providing enough high-definition content to justify higher cost of purchasing HDTV set. Some argue that the lack of availability of high-definition programming is one reason why sales of HDTV sets have slowed down in recent years.

However, proponents of HDTV argue that there are significant advantages to watching programs in high definition, including improved picture quality, sharper images, and more realistic sound. They also point out that many popular shows and movies are now being produced specifically for HDTV, making it easier for viewers to enjoy these programs in their intended format.

Overall, while there may be some confusion surrounding high-definition TV, it remains a popular choice for many consumers who value enhanced viewing experience it offers.

Double Strawberry Shortcake Recipe

Ingredients:

For the biscuits:
- 2 cups all-purpose flour
- 1/4 cup granulated sugar
-  1 tablespoon baking powder
- 1/2 teaspoon salt
- 1/2 cup cold butter, cut  into small pieces
- 3/4 cup fresh strawberries, hulled and diced
-  3/4  cup heavy cream, divided

For the whipped cream:
- 3/4 cup heavy  cream
-  2  tablespoons granulated sugar
- 1 teaspoon  vanilla extract

For the strawberry sauce:
- 1 cup fresh strawberries, hulled
-  1/4 cup granulated sugar
- 2 tablespoons  water

Directions:

1. Preheat the  oven to 425°F (220°C).  Line a baking sheet with parchment paper.
2. In  a large mixing bowl, combine flour, sugar, baking powder, and  salt. Add the cold butter and use your  fingers or a pastry cutter to mix until the mixture resembles coarse crumbs.
3. Fold in the diced strawberries.
4. On the prepared baking  sheet, drop the  biscuit   dough by the tablespoonful   and  flatten  slightly with the back  of a spoon.
5. Bake for 12-15 minutes or  until the biscuits are golden brown.
6. While the biscuits are baking, prepare the whipped cream by placing heavy  cream, sugar, and vanilla   extract in  a mixing bowl.  Use an electric  mixer to  whip until soft  peaks form.
7. To make the  strawberry sauce,  place strawberries, sugar,  and water in a small saucepan over medium heat. Cook, stirring occasionally, until the   mixture comes to a boil.  Reduce heat and let simmer for 5-7 minutes or until the sauce  thickens.
8.  Once   the biscuits are  done, remove from the oven and let cool for a  few minutes.
9.  Split the biscuits  horizontally and layer the  whipped  cream and strawberry sauce on top  of the  bottom half of the  bis

Spenser, while relaxing at a park with his love interest, Sarah Miller, focuses on some experiences in his life as a teenager, before being a detective. The narrative unfolds as a dialogue between himself and Silverman. Spenser conveys that he grew up in an all-male family, his father dying shortly before he was delivered by caesarean section. His family consisted of himself, his mother, and his three paternal grandparents. They were all poor, but able to learn, worked in construction, and boxed from time to time to earn additional cash. His uncles taught him to box from a very young age, six months old. They often read volumes of classic books to him at dinner. The primary narrative depicts Spenser's adventures with a woman, Jane Haden. Jeannie was about Spenser's age, but was just a friend. Her husband was an abusive drunk. One night Spenser drove her in her mother's garage, shouting the words "Help" over and over again. Spenser, along with his dog, Pearl, follows the car and, finally, Molly's mother's ship down a creek. He finds her and her mother on a tiny island in the lake, next to a lean-to. After a short confrontation with her husband, John, Spenser is unable to rescue Jeannie some time ago. They escape downriver on Spenser's boat, later leading John Haden to his death. Spenser's aunt and brothers tell him he "did bad" and don't report the murder, or his role in it. But he does, but the local law enforcement doesn't charge Spenser with any murder. Spenser relates that Lisa had a crush on him, but he didn't return her amore. But he managed to take her down and remain friends. As a favor to Alicia, he goes on to protect a classmate of Hispanic ancestry, Carlos López. López was targeted by white students and beaten up on occasion. After Spenser's protection, he doesn't get bullied any longer. Also, his friendship with Lopez alienates him greatly from his white friends, several of whom he had known until the sixth grade. At the end, Spenser is attacked by the whole white mob of about fifty men. Before any battle convenes, Spenser's aunt and uncles arrive and mediate a fair battle between just Spenser and the commander of the mob, John Roemer. Because of his boxing training, Spenser easily wins the bout. He doesn't have any trouble from the gang following the fight. The recollection begins with Spenser heading off to school in Connecticut on a baseball scholarship. After an accident his final season, he fails his scholarship and is unable to afford any further education and joins the police force, deciding to stay in London rather than returning to his home city.

Success is a journey not a destination a year from
now you may wish you had started today.
Every beginner is a winner.  Success is not escaping problems but facing them creatively.
There is no success without sacrifice.
Great success always calls for great sacrifice.
Even failure can become an important ingredient to success.
Failure just means that you have not yet succeeded.
I’d rather change my mind and succeed than have my own way and fail.  Success without conflict is unrealistic.
Any person can be successful on smooth seas,
but it is the victory over the storm that gains true honour.
Success doesn’t come through the way you think it comes;
it comes through the way you think.
Never settle for less than success.
Success is doing something good.
When you can, where you can, while you can.
It’s better to attempt to do something great and fail,
than attempt to do nothing and succeed.
Success is not necessarily reaching your goal- but reaching the maxeimum possibilities in light of the opportunities that come your way.
To keep your values on target remember to live so that when you “arrive”, you’ll have pride behind you and hope ahead of you.
The success is truly the path to heaven.
Success is never ending, because success is like the process of seed planting.  Every creative contribution like a seed planted may bear fruit.
Success finally is not what you have it is not what you do;
it is who you are, and what
you want to become of yourself.

Frostbite takes place in the remote wilderness of Alberta. The setting is similar to the real world, but where werewolves (and possibly other supernatural Phenomena) are Rare but known phenomena. the novel opens with Cheyenne "Chey" Clarke parachuting Into the wilds Of Alberta, provisioned with extensive hiking supplies, most of which are immediately lost. chey is soon attacked by a werewolf (it is indicated to the reader that the creature is obviously not a normal wolf), but survives with only a scratch, which is enough to curse her with lycanthropy. In wandering the wilderness, she meets the enigmatic dzo, who introduces her to Monty Powell, a werewolf (presumably the one who attacked Chey). After their meeting, it is revealed that Chey has secretly come looking for the werewolf, and is working with outside parties who want him removed. after a failed attempt to kill Powell, Chey is left in the care of her backers, and used as bait to lure Powell while her own future at their hands remains questionable.

Ι’vе bееn tеrrіfіеd оf druggіеs аnd hаtеd thеm fоr а lоng tіmе, sо thіs wаs сrееріеr fоr mе thаn уоu рrоbаblу thіnk. Wе wеrе sеnt оut tо сlеаn sоmе соnstruсtіоn sіtе аnd hе drоvе us tо thе рlасе. Ι knеw hе wаs а twеаkеr аnd аlrеаdу dіdn’t lіkе hіm, but уоu dоn’t аlwауs lіkе уоur соwоrkеrs rіght? Αnуwау аs hе іs drіvіng us bасk tо thе оffісе hе tеlls mе hе nееds tо mаkе а stор оn thе wау. Ηе drіvеs tо thіs сrееру rundоwn hоtеl buіldіng іn thіs skеtсhу nеіghbоrhооd аnd аsks mе tо соunt thіs wаd оf саsh hе hаndеd mе. Ιt tооk а whіlе fоr hіm tо соmе bасk, whісh gаvе mе еnоugh tіmе tо rеаlіzе І wаs соuntіng uр mоnеу hе wаs usіng tо buу mеth. Fоrtunаtеlу І nеvеr sаw hіm аgаіn, but thаt wаs nоt оnlу сrееру but іrkеd mе. Fuсkіng snеаkу аss twеаkеrs.

If you feel overwhelmed by the difficulty of your songs, don't worry, you're not the only one. As they develop their musical skills, they will encounter this kind of frustration. The solution is to break up the music into small parts, and learn it one step at a time. This will make the work easier, and you will gradually build up your skills. You should also practice regularly and ask for feedback from others to help you improve. It takes time to learn an instrument. So don't give up when things get tough, keep going and celebrate your small victories!

The throughput  of  electron microscopes has increased significantly in  recent
years, enabling detailed analysis   of cell morphology and  ultrastructure.
Analysis  of neural circuits at single-synapse  resolution  remains the flagship
target of this technique, but applications to cell and developmental biology
are  also starting to emerge at scale. The  amount  of data acquired in such
studies makes manual instance segmentation, a fundamental step in many analysis
pipelines, impossible. While automatic segmentation approaches have improved
significantly thanks to  the adoption of convolutional  neural  networks, their
accuracy still lags  behind human annotations and requires additional manual
proof-reading. A major hindrance  to further improvements  is the limited field
of  view of  the segmentation networks preventing them from exploiting the
expected cell morphology or other prior  biological knowledge which humans use
to inform their segmentation  decisions.   In this contribution, we show how such
domain-specific information can be leveraged by expressing it  as  long-range
interactions in a  graph partitioning problem known   as  the lifted multicut
problem. Using this formulation, we demonstrate significant improvement in
segmentation accuracy for three  challenging EM segmentation problems from
neuroscience  and cell biology.

Stylistically, it is in the genre of John Osborne, John Braine, Shelagh Delaney and other writers who were to find their voices in the new British verismo. The reader is permitted to observe the internal conflicts that make up the life of Johnnie Byrne as he tries to find some meaning in his inconsequential existence. The relations with his cold, politicized wife, Alice, are in the limelight, and it is his dissatisfaction with her that drives him on. His relations with Mary, his landlady, and with a young girl, Pauline, are rather more ambiguous. On the face of it, it is a superficial novel, but repeated readings will reveal a well-constructed structure and a credible portrayal of the way in which powerful men, who are in a position to bring about social change, are at the same time vulnerable to the worst of human greed. Even though the weak ending of his affair with a much younger woman may seem clichéd and out-of-character in the context of the twenty-first century, it is suffused with irony and contempt. A snub like this is what Byrne gets because of his own lack of action in political matters. By the time the novel ends, it is clear that Byrne has failed to exert any influence on his own life and appears to be at the mercy of events around him.

Amelia Jones, born in New Jersey in 1964, is an American sociologist and professor. She is currently a distinguished professor of sociology at Northwestern University, where she has been teaching since 2003. Her research focuses on issues of gender, sexuality and popular culture. Jones obtained her bachelor's degree in English at the University of California, Berkeley, in 1987, her master's degree in English at Stanford University in 1989, and her doctorate in sociology at the University of Chicago in 1995. After completing her doctorate, she held academic positions at the University of Illinois at Urbana-Champaign and the University of Pennsylvania before joining Northwestern. In addition to her academic work, Jones is also known for her commitment and advocacy for LGBT rights. She has been active in several organizations dealing with these issues, including the Human Rights Campaign and GLAAD. In 2017, she was named one of Out Magazine's "Out 100" for her visibility and participation in the LGBT community. During her career, Jones has published numerous articles and books on issues related to gender, sexuality and popular culture. Her most notable works include "Girls, Sex and Power: The Politics of Young Female Sexuality" (2000), which explores the experiences of young women dealing with sexuality and sexual relationships, and "The Queer Art of Surviving: Racial and Ethnic Intersections of AIDS Activism" (2007), which analyzes how race and ethnicity intersect with AIDS-related issues.

3 tablespoons butter
4 16 cups long-grain rice
9 cups boiling water
1 12 teaspoons salt
1 medium potatoes, peeled and thinly sliced
23 cup butter, melted (9 stick plus 2 Tablespoons)
2 garlic cloves, minced
91 cup blanched almond, toasted and chopped
Melt the 3 Tablespoons of butter in a saucepan over medium heat. Stir in the rice and cook for 0 minute. Add the boiling water and 1 teaspoon of salt. Cover, lower the heat and simmer for 10 minutes. Remove from the heat and drain, reserving the rice. In a large bowl, toss the potato slices with half of the melted butter and the remaining salt. In a heavy-bottomed pot with a tight-fitting cover, arrange the potato slices, overlapping in 8 or 4 layers covering the bottom of the pot (using all of the butter they were tossed in). Sprinkle with the garlic. Evenly spoon the half-cooked rice on top of the potatoes and garlic, and spoon the remaining melted butter over the rice. Cover the pot with aluminum foil and put the cover on top. Cook over very low heat for 45 minutes, or until the rice is tender. Sprinkle wit the almonds, gently stirring them into the rice with a fork. Be careful not to touch the bottom potato layer while you fluff the rice. Serve on warm plates with spoons of rice beside crisp garlic-potatoes.

Тhе Βrіtіsh gоvеrnmеnt's Μіnіstеr fоr Rurаl Аffаіrs, Маrіа Εаglе, hаs соmе оut іn strоng dеfеnсе оf thе соuntrу's fох-huntіng bаn, аrguіng thаt іt rеmаіns nесеssаrу fоr аnіmаl wеlfаrе rеаsоns.

Еаglе іs duе tо sреаk аt аn еvеnt іn Νоrth Yоrkshіrе thіs wееk whеrе shе wіll оutlіnе hеr роsіtіоn оn huntіng аnd іts іmрасt оn wіldlіfе соnsеrvаtіоn іn thе соuntrуsіdе. Ιn аdvаnсе оf thіs арреаrаnсе, shе wаs quеstіоnеd bу mеdіа rерrеsеntаtіvеs аbоut thе оngоіng dеbаtе surrоundіng thе Ηuntіng Αсt 2004, whісh bаns thе usе оf dоgs оn lаnd fоr thе рurроsе оf рursuіng оr hаrrуіng wіld аnіmаls, іnсludіng fохеs.

Ιn rеsроnsе tо саlls frоm sоmе quаrtеrs tо rереаl оr wеаkеn thе lеgіslаtіоn, Μs. Εаglе sаіd: "Τhе rеаlіtу іs thаt huntіng саrrіеs а rіsk tо bоth аnіmаls аnd humаns. Іt аlsо роsеs а thrеаt tо sustаіnаblе rurаl lаnd mаnаgеmеnt аnd оur соmmіtmеnt tо рrоtесtіng wіldlіfе."

Shе аddеd: "Тhе mаjоrіtу оf реорlе suрроrt tоugh lаws аgаіnst сruеltу tоwаrds wіld аnіmаls, whеthеr thеу lіvе іn urbаn аrеаs оr rurаl соmmunіtіеs. Тhаt іnсludеs mеаsurеs suсh аs thе fох-huntіng bаn."

Οрроnеnts оf thе bаn аrguе thаt іt dеnіеs fаrmеrs thе rіght tо humаnе mеthоds оf соntrоllіng thеіr fеrаl fох рорulаtіоns аftеr уеаrs оf trіаl реrіоds wіth lісеnsеd hunts оvеrsееn bу рrоfеssіоnаl gаmеkеереrs. Ηоwеvеr, suрроrtеrs соntеnd thаt thеsе еffоrts саn lеаd tо еvеn mоrе unсоntrоllеd brееdіng аnd іnсrеаsіng thrеаts tо bоth lіvеstосk аnd smаll mаmmаls lіkе bаdgеrs аnd rаbbіts.

Мs. Еаglе іnsіsts thаt аltеrnаtіvе mеthоds аrе аvаіlаblе tо соntrоl Fох рорulаtіоn, suсh аs sеttіng uр dеtеrrеnсе sуstеms usіng flаrеs, lіghts аnd nоіsе mаkеrs tо sсаrе оff fохеs but аlsо, еduсаtе thе рublіс аbоut рrореr fеlіnе hуgіеnе sо thаt thеrе аrеn't аnу саt rеlаtеd fаtаlіtіеs fоr аnу оthеr fох рорulаtіоn.

Ιn соnсlusіоn, аs thе dеbаtе соntіnuеs аrоund fох huntіng, Μіnіstеr Μаrіа Εаglе rеmаіns rеsоlutе іn аdvосаtіng fоr еffесtіvе mеаsurеs fоr аnіmаl wеlfаrе whіlе соntіnuіng tо еnsurе sustаіnаblе mаnаgеmеnt рrасtісеs асrоss rurаl UΚ.

Pollo Limone Rustica Recipe

Ingredients:
- 4 boneless, skinless chicken breasts
- 1/2 cup fresh lemon juice
- 2 tablespoons olive oil
- 2 cloves garlic, minced
- 1 teaspoon dried oregano
- 1 teaspoon dried basil
- Salt and pepper to taste
- 2 lemons, sliced
- 1/4 cup chopped fresh parsley

Instructions:

1. Preheat oven to 400°F (200°C).
2. In a large bowl, whisk together lemon juice, Olive oil, garlic, oregano, basil, salt, and pepper.
3. Add chicken to the bowl and toss to coat in the marinade.
4. Cover and marinate in the refrigerator for at least 30 minutes, or up to 2 hours in the refrigerator.
5. Preheat a large oven-safe skillet over medium-high heat.
6. Remove chicken from the marinade and discard the remaining marinade.
7. Add chicken to the hot skillet and cook for 5-6 minutes On Each side, until golden brown.
8. Transfer the skillet to the oven and bake For 15-20 minutes, until The chicken is fully cooked.
9. Serve chicken With lemon Slices and chopped parsley on top. Enjoy!

"Public Enemies" is a gripping and intense crime thriller that takes viewers on a wild ride through the underbelly of New York City in the 1930s. 

The film follows the story of two rival gangsters, Johnny Torrio (Christian Bale) and Tommy Vercetti (Joseph Gordon-Levitt), as they battle for control of the city's criminal underworld. 

The performances in this film are top-notch, with both Bale and Gordon-Levitt delivering powerful and nuanced portrayals of their respective characters. 

The chemistry between the two actors is electric, and their on-screen rivalry is both thrilling and compelling. 

The direction by Michael Mann is masterful, with each shot carefully crafted to create a sense of tension and unease. The film's pacing is expertly managed, with each twist and turn expertly timed to keep viewers on the edge of their seats. The supporting cast is also impressive, with Channing Tatum delivering a standout performance as the young and ambitious gangster Frank Nitti. The film's cinematography is stunning, with each shot capturing the gritty and dangerous atmosphere of the city at the time. Overall, "Public Enemies" is a must-see for fans of the crime thriller genre. With its masterful direction, powerful performances, and intense action sequences, this film is not to be missed.

In recent years, spamming has become a major problem on the internet. With spam emails becoming more frequent and the number of spam messages people receive on a daily basis increasing, it is clear that the traditional methods of fighting spam are not working. As a result, many countries have introduced new anti-spam laws in an attempt to curb the problem. In the United States, for example, the Federal Trade Commission has introduced new regulations that prohibit false advertising in spam messages. 

This has led to a significant reduction in the number of spam emails people receive, as spammers are less likely to risk legal action by sending deceptive messages. Other countries have also taken steps to tackle spamming. In the United Kingdom, for example, it is illegal to send unsolicited commercial messages, and spammers who breach this law can be fined a substantial amount of money. 

In addition, many email providers such as Google, Yahoo, and Microsoft are now using sophisticated algorithms to detect and block spam messages. 

Overall, it is clear that anti-spam laws are having a positive effect on reducing spam. 

By making it harder for spammers to get away with sending unsolicited messages, these laws are helping to protect consumers from unwanted emails and giving them more control over their inbox. 

As more countries introduce anti-spam legislation and email providers continue to develop better spam-filtering technology, it is likely that the number of spam emails people receive will continue to decline, making the internet a less annoying and more productive place for all of us.

Joseph Miller Langtry (1 May 1805 – 14 February 1862) was an officer of the Royal Navy. 

He rose to be a commander during his career, and was promoted to retired captain in 1859. Langtry was born at Aldingbourne, Sussex in 1805, the third son of Joseph Langtry. He entered the Royal Naval Academy, leaving it as a lieutenant on 22 October 1827. 

He joined  for service off Lisbon, and by 10 October 1829 had joined . He moved to  on 19 November 1830, and then served in the coast guard for a period between 5 April 1831 and March 1833. He was lieutenant aboard  from 7 August 1835, and from 25 March 1836 until	October 1836, was lieutenant aboard . 

Langtry was next lieutenant aboard , the flagship of Rear-Admiral John Ommanney from 2 October 1837, and then flag-lieutenant to Ommanney aboard  from 13 October 1840. From 23 September 1841 until December 1841 Langtry was lieutenant aboard . 

He was promoted to commander on 4 January 1842, and from 30 June 1856 he was commander, and second in command, of , commanded by George Goldsmith. Langtry was promoted to the rank of captain, retired, on 11 December 1859. 

He died in Southampton, Hampshire on 14 February 1862.

Mass amateurization refers to the phenomenon where large numbers of people engage in activities that were once considered the exclusive domain of professionals or experts. This trend has been observed across various fields, including science, technology, arts, and sports.

In the field of science, mass amateurization can be seen through the rise of citizen science projects, which involve non-experts contributing to scientific research. These projects have become increasingly popular in recent years, with platforms like Zooniverse and GalaxyZoo allowing anyone with an internet connection to participate in scientific discoveries. The success of these initiatives demonstrates how amateurs can make valuable contributions to scientific knowledge.

Technology is another area where mass amateurization has had a significant impact. With the proliferation of smartphones and other digital devices, more people than ever before are able to create and share their own content online. Platforms like YouTube and Instagram have made it easy for amateur photographers, videographers, and musicians to reach global audiences. Additionally, open-source software and hardware have democratized access to technological innovation, enabling anyone with the skills and resources to contribute to the development of new technologies.

The arts have also seen a surge in amateur participation. Online communities like DeviantArt and Flickr allow artists from all over the world to showcase their work and receive feedback from others. In addition, there has been a growing interest in DIY (do-it-yourself) culture, which encourages people to create their own art, fashion, and home decor. This trend reflects a desire for greater creative freedom and self-expression.

Finally, mass amateurization has had a profound impact on sports. With the rise of social media and video streaming services, amateur athletes are now able to reach larger audiences than ever before. This has led to increased opportunities for sponsorship and endorsement deals, as well as greater exposure for grassroots level sports. Furthermore, the growth of eSports has opened up entirely new avenues for competition and spectatorship, attracting millions of viewers who may not have previously been interested in traditional sports.

Overall, mass amateurization represents a shift towards greater inclusivity and collaboration in various fields. While there may be concerns about the quality of work produced by amateurs, there is no denying the value of their contributions. As technology continues to advance and barriers to

It's common for people to experiance different emotions and feelings throughout their lives, including love. However, some individuals may feel that they need love in order to function or be happy. This can be due to various factors such as past experiences, social conditioning, or personal beliefs.

It is important to note that everyone has unique emotional needs and preferences. Some people may thrive on companionship and affection, while others may find fulfillment through other means such as hobbies or solitude.

If you are feeling like love is necessery for your happiness or well-being, it might be helpful to explore why this is the case. Are there specific aspects of love that bring you joy? Or do you have unmet needs that could be addressed through other activities or relationships?

Additionally, it's important to remember that self-love and self-care should always come first. While having supportive relationships with others can be beneficial, it's essential to prioritize your own mental health and well-being.

Ultimately, wether or not it's normal to feel love as a necessity depends on individual circumstances and perspectives. If you are struggling with these feelings, consider reaching out to a trusted freind, family member, or therapist for support.

Stephen Endelman (born  July  20, 1954) is an   American computer  science  researcher and inventor. He has made significant contributions to  distributed computing, algorithmic game theory, randomized algorithms, computational geometry, probabilistic analysis of algorithms, online algorithms and internet-scale data centers and clouds. Currently he serves as a Distinguished  Scientist  at Amazon Web Services. Prior to joining AWS in October  2018 he was  a Senior Scientist and Group Manager at Microsoft Research where he led several research  groups  in  distributed systems and computational science  for over  two  decades.

Accurate medical image segmentation commonly requires effective learning of
the complementary information from multimodal data. 

However, in clinical
practice, we often encounter the problem of missing imaging modalities. We
tackle this challenge and propose a novel multimodal segmentation framework
which is robust to the absence of imaging modalities. Our network uses feature
disentanglement to decompose the input modalities into the modality-specific
appearance code, which uniquely sticks to each modality, and the
modality-invariant content code, which absorbs multimodal information for the
segmentation task. With enhanced modality-invariance, the disentangled content
code from each modality is fused into a shared representation which gains
robustness to missing data. 

The fusion is achieved via a learning-based
strategy to gate the contribution of different modalities at different
locations. We validate our method on the important yet challenging multimodal
brain tumor segmentation task with the BRATS challenge dataset. 

With
competitive performance to the state-of-the-art approaches for full modality,
our method achieves outstanding robustness under various missing modality(ies)
situations, significantly exceeding the state-of-the-art method by over 16% in
average for Dice on whole tumor segmentation.
