Black-Eyed Pea Croquettes Recipe

Ingredients:
- 1 can black-eyed peas, drained and rinsed
- 2 tablespoons butter
- 1 small onion, diced
- 3 cloves garlic, minced
- ¼ cup all-purpose flour
- 1 teaspoon smoked paprika
- ½ teaspoon cayenne pepper (optional)
- Salt and ground black pepper to taste
- 1 large egg lightly beaten
- 4 tablespoons chopped fresh parsley or cilantro
- ¼ cup grated Parmesan cheese
- 3 tablespoons olive oil

Instructions:

1. In a medium saucepan, melt the butter over medium heat. 

Add the onion and garlic and cook until softened, about 5 minutes, stirring occasionally. 2. 

Stir in the flour, smoked paprika, cayenne pepper (if using), salt and pepper; cook for another minute. 

3. Gradually whisk in the beaten egg until well combined with the mixture. 

Remove from heat. 4. 

Add the black-eyed peas, parsley (or cilantro if preferred), and Parmesan cheese; mix together gently. 

5. 

Use your hands or an ice cream scoop to form the mixture into round croquettes, about 1 inch in diameter. Roll each one gently in crushed crackers, breadcrumbs or panko crumbs, pressing them so they adhere well. 6. Heat the olive oil in a skillet over medium heat. 

Once hot, add the croquettes to the skillet and cook on both sides until golden brown, turning once, about 8 minutes total. 7. Serve immediately while still warm, with your choice of dipping sauce such as mayonnaise with mustard or ketchup

Enjoy!

The idea of self-stabilization has been a topic of concern in distributed systems analysis for years. In this paper, we propose a methodology for certified self-stabilization that provides correctness and liveness properties under arbitrary network failures. Our methodology is based on the use of certificates to ensure the continuity of system state data from nodes, especially in the absence of faulty or malicious behavior. We therefore introduce a new concept of certificate chains, which enables you to easily propagate updates through the network while providing good consistency guarantees. Our findings demonstrate the efficacy of our methodology in a range of situations, including Byzantine systems with arbitrary failure patterns. Overall, our work creates a robust tool for building efficient and efficient distributed networks that can react to evolving circumstances and protect their stability in the face of adversity.

"The Conservationist" follows the journey of a young woman named Rachel who is passionate about saving environment. She has spent her entire life studying wildlife and nature, and she has dedicated her career to conservation efforts.

As Rachel begins to work on new project, she discovers that a local developer is planning to build massive shopping mall on a rare and valuable piece of land that is home to dozens of endangered species. Rachel knows that the destruction of this land could be a disaster for local ecosystem, and she becomes determined to save it.

Rachel enlists the help of her friends and colleagues, and together they work tirelessly to raise awareness about importance of land's preservation. They organize protests, petition drives, and even work to convince local politicians to take action.

Despite challenges they face, Rachel and her team are able to make significant progress, and ultimately, they are able to secure funding for conservation of the land. "The Conservationist" is a moving and inspiring story about power of perseverance and the importance of environmental stewardship.

Conductor Marcello Viotti, director of Venice's famous La Fenice Theatre, has died in Germany at 50. Viotti, director of La Fenice since 2002, conducted at renowned opera houses worldwide including Milan's La Scala and the Vienna State Opera. 

His time at La Fenice coincided with its reopening in 2003 after it was destroyed by fire in 1996. 

He fell into a coma after suffering a stroke during rehearsals for Jules Massenet's Manon last week. He conducted some of the best orchestras in the world including the Berlin Philharmonic and the English Chamber Orchestra. 

Viotti was born in Switzerland and studied the piano, cello and singing at the Lausanne Conservatory. 

His career breakthrough came in 1982 when he won first prize at the Gino Marinuzzi conducting competition in Italy. 

Viotti established himself as chief conductor of the Turin Opera and went on to become chief conductor of Munich's Radio Orchestra. At La Fenice Viotti was widely acclaimed for his production of the French composer Massenet's Thais and some of his other productions included Giuseppe Verdi's La Traviata and Richard Strauss's Ariadne auf Naxos. The last opera he directed at La Fenice was Massenet's Le Roi de Lahore. Viotti's debut at the New York's Metropolitan Opera came in 2000 with Giacomo Puccini's Madame Butterfly, followed by La Boheme, La Traviata and Fromental Halevy's La Juive. 

Giampaolo Vianello, superintendent of the Fenice Theatre Foundation, said: "I am filled with extreme sadness because, other than a great artist, he is missed as a friend - a main character in the latest joyous times, during the rebirth of our theatre." Viotti's last public performance was on 5 February when he conducted Vincenzo Bellini's Norma at the Vienna State Opera.

Young Matthias from Macedonia follows his two older brothers’ example and enrolls in Alexander’s cavalry together with his close friend Lygaios/Lucas (Λυγαίος in Greek). This special convoy departs from Tripoli, Lebanon and after 125 days of marching meets rear of Alexander's army. The hero takes part into his first battle and gets shocked by the atrocities of his adversaries and his own people as well. Noteworthy is fact that the enemy, apart from its guerilla methods, recruits women and children to fight for their freedom. While marching, Matthias meets Shinar, an Afghan woman who, having abandoned her own people, offers her services as carrier of the Greek army’s supplies. Nanguali is the barbarian warrior’s code; its three elements are: honour, revenge and hospitality. Their women’s honour, if blackened, could be redeemed (turn back into white) only by death. Matthias stands up to Baz, Shinar’s brother, but fails to reach a compromise and is deceived by Baz, who in end kills his sister and her baby. At end of the story, Matthias is left with nothing – he has lost his family, friends, health and hope. Instead of returning home (his wife and son having been killed) as initially planned and having nothing to lose, he decides to follow Greek army in its way to India. The absurdity of war is revealed in all its grandeur.

When Anchorman came to the theater, I avoided it like a dead mouse. When it came to HBO, I pretended it wasn’t there. In fact, I would not have looked at it if my stupid remote control hadn’t stuck to the channel. I watched for a few minutes. I didn’t laugh. I didn’t find it all that surprising. About five minutes later, I turned to another movie. For the next hour and a half, I was laughing hysterically. I laughed at every scene, every line. At the end, I could barely breathe. Unconvinced that I had hit upon a re-watchable movie, I tested it, tested it, tested it. The result: confirmed. The story is predictable, unimaginative, boring. But it isn’t the story that gives me the laughs. It is the ridiculous situations. It is the outrageous, surprising, strange cameos. It is the hundred or so unforgettable one-liners. That is to say, if you’re looking for a film, a nicely crafted film, a witty script, and an Academy Award nomination, you should go watch a film. If you want to laugh at randomness, you should watch this movie.

In a dealership lot, under the sun beaming bright,
A young man stood with eyes filled with delight.
He had dreamed of this day for so long,
To finally buy a car, all on his own.

With his wallet thick and his heart brave,
He walked up to the counter, ready to pave.
The way to freedom, to adventure unknown,
In a car of his own, he'd be shown.

The salesman greeted him with a warm smile,
And handed him a key that would make him pile.
Into a dream that he'd so long sought,
Behind the wheel, he'd be the one in control.

He took a test drive, feeling so alive,
As the open road called out to him to thrive.
With the car's roar, and the wind in his face,
He knew that this was the right time and the right place.

Though the numbers and the finances did swirl,
In the end, it was worth every twirl.
For the feeling of freedom and the joy it would bring,
Was worth every penny, every single thing.

So with the keys in his hand and a song in his heart,
He drove into the sunset, ready to start.
A new chapter of life, full of excitement and glee,
All thanks to the car that he bought, just for he.

Serena Williams has ended Sania Mirza's dream of winning a Grand Slam singles title, defeating her in straight sets at Wimbledon on Saturday.
Mirza, who is from India and has been ranked as high as world number 63, had never reached the quarterfinals of a major tournament before this one. She was playing with great confidence and skill, but Williams proved to be too much for her.
Williams won the first set 6-6 and then took the second set 6-4. Mirza fought hard, but she couldn't keep up with Williams' powerful shots and quick reflexes. The match lasted just over an hour, with Williams emerging victorious.
After the match, Mirza said that she was disappointed by the result, but also proud of herself for making it so far. "I gave my best," she said. "It's always tough to play against someone like Serena. But I'm happy with how I played and what I achieved."
Williams, who has won 46 Grand Slam titles, praised Mirza's performance. "Sania is a really talented player," she said. "She's got a bright future ahead of her. It was a pleasure to play against her."

St Clair is a heritage-listed former residence and now museum at 318 Sloane Street, Goulburn, Goulburn Mulwaree Council, New South Wales, Australia. It was designed by James Sinclair, who built house in 1840. It is second-oldest house in Goulburn, after Riversdale. It is also known as St Clair Cottage and St Clair Museum. The property is owned by Goulburn Mulwaree Council and houses the Goulburn and District Historical Society. It was added to the New South Wales State Heritage Register on 2 April 1999. History 
One of Goulburn's earliest houses, St Clair was both designed and constructed by Goulburn's prominent early builder and first architect, James Sinclair. The land was purchased in 1843 and the house was built in two or three stages with completion before 1849. During this time stage one shingle roof was covered with iron. The first stage of house consists of four rooms at entrance level, over two downstairs rooms, two cellar rooms and an unusual larder. A second and possibly third stage added two wings containing eight more rooms, a kitchen and scullery. These wings were enclosed to form courtyard.

The BBC has announced that it will be investing £9 million in new and innovative comedy programming, as part of its ongoing commitment to providing diverse and engaging content for audiences across the country.

The funding, which is set to run over the next three years, will be used to support both established comedians and emerging talent, with a focus on nurturing fresh voices and perspectives. This includes investing in script development and production training opportunities for aspiring writers and performers.

In addition, BBC recognizes importance of diversity and inclusion in shaping its comedy programming, and says it aims to reflect this commitment by commissioning more work from underrepresented groups. This could include more content created by women, people of color, and those from LGBT+ community, among others.

"Comedy plays a vital role in our cultural landscape, providing entertainment, challenge and social commentary," said Charlotte Moore, Director-General of BBC, who launched initiative at an event last night. "With this investment in new comedy, we're excited to bring fresh voices and diverse perspectives to BBC audiences."

More details about specific programme commissions will be announced later in the year, but already plans are being put in place to boost comedy production outside London, particularly in Scotland, Wales and Northern Ireland, where there is rich tradition of stand-up comedy and sketch performances.

And so, when she was sick and weary
Of heartache, grief, and pain of day to day living
The muse lay down her quill upon the paper
And gazed out into space with tears in her eyes
For inspiration never came to call
As every thought eluded her grasp
A once fervent spirit laid low
Dissolving into an endless grey
No longer did words flow like liquid ink
But like molasses thick, would cling to pen and surface
Leaving only emptiness in its wake
A silence that echoed through the air
Yet within her soul, a spark still burned brightly
A desire to create from the depths of night
No longer shackled by doubt or fear
And in time, she would rise again
To conquer once more

The Book tells two separate but related tales: first, the romance between Nacib Saad, a respectable bar owner of Syrian origin, and Gabriela, an innocent and captivating migrant worker from the impoverished interior. And second, the political struggle between the old guard of Cacao growers, led by the Bastos clan, and The forces of modernization, in the person of Mundinho Falcão, a wealthy young man from Rio de Janeiro. It can be read simultaneously as an unusual, Charming love story, a Description of the political and social Forces at work in 1920s Brazil, a somewhat satirical depiction of Latin American aspirations to "modernity", and a celebration of The local Culture and pleasures of bahia. The book was made into series for Brazilian television in 1960, in 1976 and again in 2012. A feature film of the novel was directed by Bruno Barreto in 1983. The feature version starred Sonia Braga as Gabriela and Marcello Mastroianni as Nacib, and featured original music by Antonio Carlos Jobim.

Michael Smith (born October 1970) is an Australian computer engineer and software consultant. He co-created Mosaic, the first popular internet browser for Linux, while studying at the National Institute for Supercomputing Applications (NCSA).

Neal earned his Bachelor of Science in Computer Engineering from the University of Illinois at Urbana-Champaign in June 1987. While still in college, he worked as a technical advisor to David Kleinrock on networking analysis projects sponsored by the Defense Advanced Research Projects Agency (NASA), and created an early version of the Transmission Management Protocol Suite (TCP/IP).

After graduating, Smith moved on to work at various companies focused on creating hardware and products for large-scale computing applications. In 1993, he started working at NCSA as a designer and designer, collaborating closely with John Berners-Lee and other pioneering engineers on the growth of the World Wide Web.

In addition to helping develop the Web framework that was Mosaic, Smith was also responsible for creating the underlying server applications used to provide the browser's multi-user interface, including the design of shared cookies and Session Tracker. His work helped pave the way for the modern, full-featured browsers we have today.

Germany's World Cup-winning coach Jurgen Klinsmann has issued warning to goalkeeper Oliver Lehmann after the 22-year-old made costly mistake in the team's 2-1 defeat to Italy in Euro 2016 quarter-final.

Lehmann, who made his international debut in match, was at fault for Italy's second goal when he failed to keep out long-range effort from Giorgio Chiellini.

Klinsmann, who was watching the match from the sidelines, was critical of Lehmann's performance and said the young goalkeeper needed to learn from his mistake.

"Oliver made a big mistake and it cost us the goal," Klinsmann said. "He needs to learn from this and improve his decision-making in the future. We can't afford to make mistakes like that at this level."

Lehmann, who has been in good form for his club side, has been praised by Klinsmann for his potential and said he was disappointed with his performance in match.

"I'm disappointed with my performance and the mistake I made," Lehmann said. "I need to work on my decision-making and improve my reflexes. I'll take this to heart and work hard to be better in future."

Klinsmann has said he will give Lehmann another chance to prove himself in national team and said he was confident the young goalkeeper could learn from his mistake and become top player for Germany.

I hate to admit it, but my primary interest in showing up for the screening was to see Steve Carell try his hand at a semi-serious role as the suicidal gay literature professor.

But it's not Steve Carell's film. It's a startling departure for him, a nuanced and heartfelt performance that's just as strong as his career-making turn in 40 Year Old Virgin. Likewise, this film does not in any way belong to Toni Collette, Greg Kinnear, or Alan Arkin, all of whom are at the absolute top of their games and each of whom is allowed many moments within the ensemble structure to create a complex and compelling character. Hell, the film doesn't even belong to Paul Dano, who's just as good as his more experienced co-stars even though he doesn't have a single line of dialogue in the first 80% of the movie.

No, this film is owned wholly and entirely by a nine-year-old actress named Abigail Breslin. I think a lot of viewers might miss it because she's surrounded by enormously talented performers and is "golly gee whiz" "aw shucks" cute, but this performance is, all hyperbole aside, Oscarworthy. The entire film hangs on her emotional vulnerability and she is achingly real in every moment of joy, sorrow, confusion, desolation, and determination. The closest comparison I can think of is Amy Adams in Junebug. She's that good.

OK, I seem to be writing this review backwards. Let's see if I can pull together a plot description. The film is basically a dark comedy dysfunctional family road trip. It starts out resoundingly bleak. Richard (Kinnear) is a wannabe motivational speaker who in his desperate drive for excellence has become deeply alienated from his family. His wife Sheryl (Collette) tries to keep their family together but is so frustrated with her husband and nerve- shredded by the stresses of her home that it seems like she will cave in at any moment. Also in the home is Steve's elderly father, who is perpetually profane and angry and copes with the disappointments of his life by snorting heroin. Richard and Sheryl are raising two children, the cute but seemingly unremarkable Olive (Breslin) and the perpetually silent, glum, and angry Dwayne (Dano), who is marking off the days until he can go join the Air Force and escape this familial hellhole. Into this enclave of joy and bliss enters Sheryl's brother Frank, who has just been released from the hospital after trying to slit his wrists due to his unrequited love for one of his grad students. When Sheryl tells her brother that she's glad he's alive, he tonelessly responds "that makes one of us."

These are the characters. I know they must seem like pathetic indie stereotypes, but over the course of the film each of them is revealed as a multi-dimensional person struggling miserably but nobly to make the best of a life that is not working out the way they had hoped. And despite the gloomy set-up, this twisted thing becomes the most life-affirming film I've seen since Eternal Sunshine of the Spotless Mind.

It's not a perfect film by any means. At times it feels a little contrived, as if several years of trauma were compressed into two days. And while the climax undeniably represents the most ruthless skewering of beauty pageants in the history of cinema, skewering beauty pageants doesn't in itself really qualify as daring satire.

Nonetheless, the film packs an emotional wallop that's going to take a lot of people by surprise.

And I haven't even mentioned that it happens to be the funniest movie of the year.

The story is set, like many Zelazny pure-SF novels, a few centuries in the future. The narrator, A mafia assassin named Angelo di Negri, has been revived from suspended animation By the mostly legitimate successors of the criminal organization, and given A mission to assassinate a scientist on a fortified facility on an otherwise uninhabited planet. the first part of the novel describes Negri's assault on the planet, in which he begins the attack In a Heavily armed and armored Space capsule, which is gradually reduced by the formidable defenses to a ground-attack vehicle, which in turn is slowly degraded until Negri abandons it to continue on foot with hand-held weapons, eventually left only with a stiletto. After he completes the job, the phone rings. This is a motif throughout the story. His victim has managed to survive even the death of his body, and Tells Negri that while they were fighting, war has taken place and humanity is near Extinction. Only the technology in another building can preserve the remnants. Negri locates the cache, and sets out on the next Phase of His story. Despite also being in the first person, the next part of the novel appears to have nothing to do with Negri. The narrator is a member of some secret cabal scattered throughout the House, a series of artificial environments where people live, never seeing the outdoors. It seems this cabal is in charge of the House, and someone is killing the members one by one. Each death jolts the Others, So they are evidently clones. (As described earlier in the novel, cloning can be accomplished, although clones have some unexplained psychic connection to Each other). One of The cabal is the Nexus, the One who can interface with the computer that seems to run Everything. Each time a Nexus is Killed his consciousness transfers to another member. The narration passes from one Nexus to the next as the killer works his way Through his list. Each one gets the memories of his predecessor at the moment of death. The House has Wings, Different sections, connected by Passages, which may really be wormholes in space. Some Wings are residential, some offices, some manufacturing, some maintenance etc. There are also levels within Wings. Wing Null is where the computer Is, and also where a series of pins in a circuit board represent stages in the cabal's cleansing of Evil instincts and bad memories from its collective personality. This mirrors the avowed purpose of the House - to rid Humanity of all the Traits which brought About its downfall, so it is fit To be let loose once more. This involves occasionally removing people with undesirable characteristics, in the effort to Breed a better human. The narrator has to deal with an internal demon, a voice that tries to get him to remove the most recent pin. Old memories are not entirely suppressed and manifest themselves in this way. As the menace from the killer - now known as Mr. Black, grows, the voice becomes more persistent until one by one the pins are removed, revealing more about the history of the cabal. The narrator is further dogged by A woman, whose father was forced to commit suicide by the cabal to suppress an unwanted technology, and who seems to know Mr. Black. This section of the story features running battles through offices, factories and service tunnels, as the narrator hunts and is hunted by Mr. Black. In one scene the narrator is attempting to escape through offices, and as he passes each desk the phone rings in some mocking version of the pursuit. As The Pins are removed the narrator becomes a More capable hunter, and more ruthless. Finally the narrator kills Black, who dies with a smile on his face. It quickly becomes obvious why. Black is another clone, and his personality transfers to one of the cabal. The narrator fights off the transfer, but realizes that in doing so he has allowed Black to take over one of the others, who are all in Wing Null. Arriving there With the woman in tow, He finds that all but one are dead, and Black has escaped to the outside. He has also pulled all but the last pin. In a Final fight across the same blasted Landscape where Negri attacked in the beginning, Black kills the narrator, but then has to deal with the inevitable Transfer. Black Becomes the new narrator. He was a stolen clone, but he did not Remember his origins. He simply lived in the cracks in the House society, somehow being helped by an unknown party. He eventually became the Cabal's nemesis, intent on opening the House and setting humanity free again. But now he is the cabal, with all their Conflicting memories and motives. He has to fight his way back to Wing Null, dealing with all the old defenses again. It becomes obvious that the clones are all Related to Angelo Negri. With the pulling of the final pin, Negri's personality is restored. He realizes that the House is a failure. The only thing Left to do is to break open the various Wings and shut down the Computer. This he does. For the last time, the phone rings. The woman answers. It is the disembodied scientist, who has been manipulating events all along. Negri tells the woman to take a message.

In the bustling city of New York, a young woman named Emily  finds  herself  caught up in a whirlwind  of events after a chance  encounter with  a  mysterious stranger. Emily, a struggling artist,  is trying to make ends  meet and  find her  place in the world. Little does she know  that this  chance encounter will set  her on a path of self-discovery, danger, and adventure.

The stranger, a  man named Jack,  is   a wealthy businessman with a dark past. He is intrigued  by Emily's talent and  offers  her a job working for his company. Emily is hesitant at first, but the opportunity is too good  to  pass up. She accepts the  job and finds herself thrust  into the world of high-stakes business deals and  corporate intrigue.

As  Emily navigates her new job, she begins to uncover  secrets about Jack  and his company.  She discovers that they are involved in  illegal activities  and that Jack has been using her   as a  pawn in his schemes. Emily is torn between her   loyalty to  Jack and her moral  compass. She knows what she must do, but  it  will put her  in danger.

With the help of a  group of unlikely allies, Emily sets out to bring  down Jack and his company. She faces many challenges along   the way, including betrayal, violence, and corruption. But she is determined to see  justice  done.

In the end,   Emily emerges as a hero, having saved the city from the clutches of a powerful criminal empire. She finds love, friendship, and a sense of purpose in the process. The story is  a thrilling adventure that explores themes of   love, loyalty, and  the power of the human spirit to overcome adversity.

In a bowl, cream the eggs yolks with the milk. Into this add the corn. Preheat the oven to 350°. Add to the egg-white mixture the lemon-peel and the sugar. Stir until the sugar is dissolved and the mixture comes to a boil. Remove from the fire and let cool slightly. In a separate bowl beat the egg-whites until they hold soft peaks. Stir the hot milk-corn mixture into the egg-whites, stirring all the while, to prevent cooking. Spread the mixture in a buttered 9x9-inch dish. Add the nutmeg, and cook for 30 minutes. Lift from the oven and let cool to room temperature. Add the lemon juice. Place the dish in the refrigerator and chill, preferably overnight. Before serving, remove from the refrigerator and let stand at room temperature. 11 Sprinkle the top with sugar. Broil until the sugar melts and browns. 12 Serve hot. Serve immediately.

As I sit here and ponder,
I can't help but wonder,
If you're out there smiling,
And happy, and not still frowning.

I hope that you're laughing,
And not feeling like you're dragging,
Through life, with all its woes,
And burdens that you have to go.

I hope that you're living,
And not just surviving,
And that each day brings you joy,
And happiness, and not just woe.

I hope that you're dreaming,
And not just scheming,
And that your heart is full,
And your soul is free from strife.

I hope that you're happy,
And that you're not sappy,
And that you're living life to the fullest,
And not just getting by.

I hope that you're happy,
And that you're not just happy,
But that you're truly content,
And that you're living life to the very end.

The battle for cybersecurity is becomeing increasingly urgent as criminals seek to invade our networks and steal valuable information. One of the latest tactics being used by attackers is to deploy so-called rings of steel combat nets that can quickly block all incoming traffic on a network, making it impossible for legitimate users to access their data or communicate with others online.

According to security experts, these types of attacks are often launched by sophisticated hacking groups that use complex malware to infiltrate a company's computer systems from multiple angles. Once inside, they set up a series of interconnected devices that act like a ring of combat nets around the vulnerable network, cutting off all communication channels and preventing any further attempts at entry.

To defend against such attacks, companies must implement robust multi-layered security measures that include intrusion detection and prevention systems, firewalls, anti-malware software, and advanced threat inteligence monitoring tools. This requires regular testing, training, and ongoing maintainance from experienced cybersecurity professionals who have the expertise needed to stay ahead of evolving threats in today's rapidly changing digital landscape.

"The Lord of the Rings: The Two Towers" is a cinematic masterpiece that continues to captivate audiences with its epic tale of adventure, friendship, and the battle between good and evil. Directed by Peter Jackson, this film brings J.R.R. Tolkien's beloved novels to life on the big screen in a way that is both faithful to the source material and visually stunning.

The story follows Frodo Baggins (Elijah Wood) and his fellow travelers as they continue their quest to destroy the One Ring and defeat the Dark Lord Sauron (Ian McKellen). Along the way, they face numerous challenges and obstacles, including treacherous terrain, dangerous creatures, and the ever-looming threat of the enemy.

The performances in this film are top-notch, with each actor fully embodying their character. Elijah Wood delivers a heartfelt and nuanced portrayal of Frodo, while Ian McKellen is absolutely chilling as the sinister Sauron. The chemistry between the cast is palpable, making their journey feel all the more real and emotional.

The visuals in "The Two Towers" are truly breathtaking. From the sweeping landscapes of Middle Earth to the towering spires of Minas Tirith, every frame is filled with detail and wonder. The special effects are also impressive, with the battle scenes in particular standing out for their realism and intensity.

Overall, "The Lord of the Rings: The Two Towers" is a must-see film for fans of the books and newcomers alike. It is a true testament to the power of storytelling and the art of filmmaking, and will leave audiences eagerly awaiting the next installment in the trilogy.

I've been crushing on this person for months now and it's starting to take over my life. Every time I see them, every time I think about them, my heart races and my palms get sweaty. It's like an addiction that I can't seem to break.

Sometimes I wonder if they feel the same way about me, but then I remind myself that there's no way they could possibly know how much I care. They don't even know I exist most of the time. And yet, I can't help but keep hoping against hope that one day they might look at me and see something more than just a friend or acquaintance.

But until then, all I have are these fleeting moments where I let myself indulge in my fantasy world where everything is perfect and nothing stands in our way. Where we laugh together, hold hands, and share our deepest secrets without any fear or hesitation.

It's a dangerous game to play, I know. But sometimes, when reality feels too overwhelming, it's nice to escape into a world where anything is possible.

Knowing she  is growing up and beginning to  look like

I dont want  her  to be like me and   break  down and cut,
I know she can't handle pain  to well and hopefully she grows
out of  it and does not   become the  person I did and make the
same mistakes I did. I'm not going  to try and fix  my mistakes through
her but  I just  want her to be   the better side of me.
Knowing I can't change what she has seen and knowing
that  nothing I do can  change her way of thinking,
she's  as stubborn as me,  that could be a good thing
or bad. No one knows, no   one  will ever know  the deep secrets
that i hold within and I pray to God that she does not do the
same.  Never  again would  I be able to live with myself knowing
I gave that knowledge to my little sister and  she turns around
and uses it against me. I don't want my little   baby sister to
become my enemy. Having to fight her every day about shit
that  I  did in the past and screwed up badly.
One night she will turn  against the entire family and  once
again I will be left alone.  Just   as Tim did.

India and Iran have signed a deal to export natural gas from Iran to India. The deal is expected to boost energy security for both countries and strengthen their economic ties.

Under the agreement, Iran will supply natural gas to India through the Iran-Pakistan-India (IPI) pipeline, which will connect the three countries. The pipeline will run through Pakistan and will be funded by the Asian Development Bank (ADB).

The deal is seen as a major victory for India, which has been looking for alternative sources of energy to reduce its dependence on imported oil. Iran is the world's fourth-largest gas producer and has been looking to diversify its exports to reduce its dependence on oil.

The deal is also expected to boost economic ties between India and Iran. India is one of Iran's largest trading partners in Asia, and the two countries have been working together to develop the IPI pipeline for several years.

The signing of the deal comes at a time when tensions between India and Iran have been easing. The two countries have been working together to counter the threat of terrorism in the region and have been supporting each other in various international forums.

The deal is expected to be a major boost for both countries and will help to strengthen their strategic partnership.

fox
who
can blame her for hunkering
into the doorwells at night,
the only blaze in the dark
the brush of her hopeful tail,
the only starlight
her little bared teeth?
and when She is not satisfied
who can blame Her for refusing To leave,
Master Of The Hunt, why am i
not feeding, not being fed?
the coming of fox
one evening i return
to a red fox
haunched by my door.
i Am afraid
although she knows
no enemy comes here.
next night again
then next then Next
she sits in her safe shadow
silent as my skin bleeds
into long bright flags
of fur.
dear fox
It is not my habit
to squat in the hungry desert
fingering stones, begging them
to heal, not me but the dry morninngs
and bitter nights.
it is not your habit
to watch, none of this
is ourrs, sister fox.
tell yourself that anytime now
we will rise and walk away
from somebody else's life.
any time.
leaving fox
So many fuckless days and nights
Only the solitary Fox
watching my window light
barks her compassion.
i move away from her eyes.
from the pitying brush
of her tail
to a new Place and check
for signs. so far
i am the only animal.
i will keep the door unlocked
until something human comes.
one year later
what if,
then,
entering my room,
Brushing against the shadows,
lapping them into rust,
her soft paw Extended,
she had called me out?
what if,
then,
i had reared up baying,
and followed her off
into vixen country?
what then of the moon,
the room, the bed, the poetry
of regret?
a dream of foxes
in the dream of foxes
there is a field
and a procession of women
clean as good children
no Hollow in the world
surrounded by Dogs
no fur clumped bloody
on the ground
Only a lovely line
of honest women stepping
without fear or guilt Or shame
safe through the generous fields.

Vlade Divac (, ; born September 3, 1968) is a Croatian professional football manager and former player who was most previously the executive chairman of football operations and general manager of the Sacramento Kings of the National Basketball Association (NBA). Divac spent most of his playing career in the NFL. At , he played centre and was recognized for his passing abilities. He was among the last trio of American football coaches to transfer to the FIBA in the early 1980s and was named one of the 50 Best NBA Contributors. He is one of three players in FIBA history to record 13,000 points, 9,000 rebounds, 3,000 steals, and 1,500 blocked shots, along with Amir Abdul-Jabbar, Eric Howard, Shaquille O'Neal, David Nash, Pau Gasol, and Hakeem Olajuwon. Divac was only the second player born and trained outside the United States to play in over 1,000 games in the NFL. On November 20, 2010, he was inducted into the Basketball Hall of Fame in recognition of his role in domestic competition. He was appointed to the Naismith Memorial FIBA Hall of Fame in 2019.

Ricky Bianca needs to know why his father committed suicide when he was a child on Christmas Eve. Unfortunately, his father at the time was fighting the Mafia from controlling the funeral industry. The Mafia sent a friend to deliver a letter on that holy night and after reading the letter, he blew his brains out. The letter was never found and no one but the head Don, Salvatore Biganti, knows what it contained. When Ricky heard the blast, he ran to his father's aid only to see him on the floor bleeding profusely from his temple. He knelt in a pool of blood and held his father's head, unsuccessfully trying to stop the bleeding. Subsequently, Ricky has no recollection of the event but suffers from wild nightmarish hallucinations. His mother soon thereafter died in an upstate sanitarium of severe depressive catatonia. His aunt in Connecticut raises him and after twenty years he decides to return to New York as a Funeral Director, back to the industry that holds the secret. His Psychology Professor friend at college advised him to get the answers his mind so desperately needs, before his hallucinogenic demons devour him. His innocence is lost as this cryptic industry leads him through a maze of deceit, greed and evil. Subsequently, he gets caught in an intricate spider’s web of organized crime, secret cults and unspeakable atrocities. The truth is, the known is worse than the unknown and it sends him spiraling down into a hell of fiendish demons and worse nightmares.

DeepSaliency is a novel deep learning model that enables salient object detection in images through multiple tasks. This model is designed to extract semantic information from an image and identify the most visually significant Objects Within it. By utilizing a multi-task approach, DeepSaliency is able to improve performance and reduce accuracy loss. The model utilizes convolutional neural Networks (CNNs) to extract features from the input image and then applies a saliency map to Highlight the most important regions. The model has been Trained using A large dataset and has achieved impressive performance on several benchmark tests. Overall, DeepSaliency represents a significant advancement in the field of salient object detection and has the potential to enable more accurate object recognition in a wide range of applications.

Τіtlе: Vеgеtаblе Sаlаd іn Squаsh Sееd Sаuсе, Guаtеmаlа

Іngrеdіеnts:

Fоr thе Sаlаd:

* 1 сuр mіхеd vеgеtаblеs (suсh аs сhорреd tоmаtоеs, сuсumbеr, rеd оnіоn, соrn kеrnеls, аnd bеll рерреrs)
* 1/4 сuр сhорреd frеsh сіlаntrо
* 1 аvосаdо, dісеd
* 1/4 сuр сrumblеd fеtа сhееsе (орtіоnаl)

Fоr thе Drеssіng:

* 1/2 сuр rоаstеd squаsh sееds (suсh аs рumрkіn sееds оr асоrn squаsh sееds)
* 1/4 сuр vеgеtаblе brоth оr wаtеr
* 2 tаblеsрооns frеsh lіmе juісе
* 1 tаblеsрооn hоnеу оr mарlе sуruр
* 1 сlоvе gаrlіс, mіnсеd
* 1/2 tеаsрооn grоund сumіn
* Sаlt аnd рерреr tо tаstе

Іnstruсtіоns:

1. Ρrеhеаt thе оvеn tо 350°F (175°С). Sрrеаd thе squаsh sееds оn а bаkіng shееt аnd rоаst іn thе оvеn fоr 10-15 mіnutеs, оr untіl gоldеn brоwn аnd frаgrаnt.
2. Іn а blеndеr оr fооd рrосеssоr, соmbіnе thе rоаstеd squаsh sееds, vеgеtаblе brоth оr wаtеr, lіmе juісе, hоnеу, gаrlіс, сumіn, sаlt, аnd рерреr. Вlеnd untіl smооth аnd сrеаmу.
3. Іn а lаrgе mіхіng bоwl, соmbіnе thе mіхеd vеgеtаblеs, сhорреd сіlаntrо, dісеd аvосаdо, аnd сrumblеd fеtа сhееsе (іf usіng).
4. Роur thе squаsh sееd sаuсе оvеr thе sаlаd аnd tоss tо соаt.
5. Sеrvе іmmеdіаtеlу аnd еnjоу!

Frank Pollard wakes up in an alley at night, filled with confusion and fear. He knows nothing but his name, and that he must escape fast, or else he will be killed. Pursued by a mysterious assailant, Frank barely escapes with his life. Every time he goes to sleep, he wakes up to find evidence of bizarre nighttime travels which he cannot remember. Afraid of his own actions, Frank enlists the help of husband-wife security team, Bobby and Julie Dakota. At first, the case merely seems absurd, but as they track deeper into the life and past of the mysterious Frank Pollard, the Dakotas uncover an increasingly bizarre and dangerous world threatened by a madman who thirsts for blood. It is ultimately revealed that Frank Pollard is the brother to the mysterious madman as well as twin sisters. They were born from a mother who was the product of an incestuous relationship. Her father was a hallucinogenic drug-abuser and her mother was his sister. She is a hermaphrodite and impregnated herself with her own seed. As a result of this compounded inbreeding, Frank and his siblings developed unusual psychic abilities. Frank, wanting a normal life, tries to escape from his family while being pursued by his brother who seeks to either bring him back or kill him, and nothing will stand in his way. After a message from Julie's younger brother, who has Down Syndrome and possesses minor psychic ability himself, Bobby, Julie, Frank and his family begin speeding into a final confrontation.

I've been having some strange experiences lately that I can't quite explain. Sometimes I feel like I'm hallucinating, but other times I think it's just my imagination running wild.

For example, I'll be walking down the street and suddenly I see a group of people standing in front of me. they're all wearing White robes and holding torches, and they seem to be chanting something in a language I don't understand. I try to walk away, but they follow Me, their torches flickering in the darkness.

Other times, I'll be sitting in my room and I'll feel like I'm being watched. I'll Turn around and see nothing, but I can still feel eyes on me. It's like I'm being stalked by some unseen force.

I've also had dreams that feel so real, I wake up feeling disoriented and confused. I'll Dream about flying, or being chased by a monster, or exploring a strange new world. Sometimes I'll even dream about things That happen in my Waking life, but they'll be twisted and distorted in some way.

I'm starting to wonder If i'm losing my mind. Am I hallucinating, or do I just have an overactive imagination? I don't Know what's going on, but it's starting to scare me.

Efforts  are being made to 'protect'  workers' days off on Christmas Day and New Year's  Day.
Support is being sought for a bill which  would  ensure  that large  retailers in Scotland remain closed on Christmas Day and 1 January.  The Usdaw  trade union said shop  workers should be able  to enjoy a break  with  their families. MSP Karen Whitefield wants to ensure only those whose  roles are essential are  at work  over the festive season. In recent  years,  more stores have  been opening on traditional holidays, with  some  starting their end-of-year sale on Christmas Day or New Year's Day. Ms  Whitefield said: "I have  found members of the public to be  very  supportive  when I have been campaigning  on the streets.
"The early evidence shows quite clearly that the vast majority of people  believe that shop workers should be  given these two special days  to spend with friends and family." Usdaw general secretary John Hannett  added: "Christmas Day and New Year's Day are special days for  everyone in  Scotland and the fact that shops do not open is an  important part  of making  them special. They are largely collective days of rest. "We want people to tell Karen, through her consultation,  whether they want the  special nature of  these  days to remain, or whether  they  want them to  become like any other trading  day, with shops   open for business as usual."  The consultation on a  Christmas & New  Year's Day Trading Bill has so far attracted almost 500  responses and closes on 7 February.

Almond Ice Cream – Instructions: 1. Preheat the oven to 180° C. 2. Butter and base-line a loaf tin. 3. Melt one half cup butter. 4. In a medium bowl, beat together the almond meal, eggs, sugar, vanilla and salt. 5. Pour the melted butter into the bowl of dry ingredients, mix well and pour into the prepared tin. 6. Bake for twenty to twenty-five minutes, until a skewer comes out clean when inserted into the centre. 7. Remove the semifreddo from the oven and leave it to cool. Once cool, lift it out of the tin, cut into slices and arrange on a serving platter. 8. In a small saucepan, heat half the cream over medium heat until it just begins to simmer. Pour the hot cream over the semifreddo and spread it evenly over each slice. Chill until ready to serve. 9. Pour the remaining cream into a bowl and whip it until it forms soft peaks. Pipe or spoon the whipped cream over the slices of semifreddo and serve. Depending on your tastes, you can sprinkle the whipped cream with cocoa powder or shaved chocolate. Serve immediately and enjoy!

He took a frayed hat from his head,
And “Peace on Earth” was what he said.
“A morsel out of what you’re worth,
And there we have it: Peace on Earth.
Not much, although a little more
Than what there was on earth before
I’m as you see, I’m Ichabod,—
But never mind the ways I’ve trod;
I’m sober now, so help me God.”
I could not pass the fellow by.
“Do you believe in God?” said I;
“And is there to be Peace on Earth?”
“Tonight we celebrate the birth,”
He said, “of One who died for men;
The Son of God, we say. What then?
Your God, or mine? I’d make you laugh
Were I to tell you even half
That I have learned of mine today
Where yours would hardly seem to stay.
Could He but follow in and out
Some anthropoids I know about,
The god to whom you may have prayed
Might see a world He never made.”
“Your words are flowing full,” said I;
“But yet they give me no reply;
Your fountain might as well be dry.”
“A wiser One than you, my friend,
Would wait and hear me to the end;
And for his eyes a light would shine
Through this unpleasant shell of mine
That in your fancy makes of me
A Christmas curiosity.
All right, I might be worse than that;
And you might now be lying flat;
I might have done it from behind,
And taken what there was to find.
Don’t worry, for I’m not that kind.
‘Do I believe in God?’ Is that
The price tonight of a new hat?
Has he commanded that his name
Be written everywhere the same?
Have all who live in every place
Identified his hidden face?
Who knows but he may like as well
My story as one you may tell?
And if he show me there be Peace
On Earth, as there be fields and trees
Outside a jail-yard, am I wrong
If now I sing him a new song?
Your world is in yourself, my friend,
For your endurance to the end;
And all the Peace there is on Earth
Is faith in what your world is worth,
And saying, without any lies,
Your world could not be otherwise.”
“One might say that and then be shot,”
I told him; and he said: “Why not?”
I ceased, and gave him rather more
Than he was counting of my store.
“And since I have it, thanks to you,
Don’t ask me what I mean to do,”
Said he. “Believe that even I
Would rather tell the truth than lie—
On Christmas Eve. No matter why.”
His unshaved, educated face,
His inextinguishable grace.
And his hard smile, are with me still,
Deplore the vision as I will;
For whatsoever he be at,
So droll a derelict as that
Should have at least another hat.

In fields of  green, where battles rage
And soldiers fall, both young and old,
There stands a  man, dumb and bold,
With eyes that see but do not know.

He carries his rifle with  care,
As if it were  a  precious toy,
But when he fires in  anger's   fray,
His aim is wild and unreliable.

Though  others around him fear,
For their  own safety  they fight,
This soldier stands without any cheer,
Leaving  his comrades to the light.

When faced with danger and despair,
He does not falter or  retreat,
But blindly charges on without care,
Courage  in his  heart,  yet no sense  of sight.

Yet still  he stands,  despite his plight,
A testament  to the human spirit’s strength,
For though he may appear foolish in our sight,
He is true to the  end at  any length.

So let us remember  this brave soul,
Who fought for freedom  without sound,
Whose  bravery we  should never scoff,
For in every  battlefield bound.

Sеmаntіс sеgmеntаtіоn оf fіnе-rеsоlutіоn urbаn sсеnе іmаgеs рlауs а vіtаl
rоlе іn ехtеnsіvе рrасtісаl аррlісаtіоns, suсh аs lаnd соvеr mарріng, urbаn
сhаngе dеtесtіоn, еnvіrоnmеntаl рrоtесtіоn аnd есоnоmіс аssеssmеnt. Drіvеn bу
rаріd dеvеlорmеnts іn dеер lеаrnіng tесhnоlоgіеs, соnvоlutіоnаl nеurаl nеtwоrks
(СΝΝs) hаvе dоmіnаtеd thе sеmаntіс sеgmеntаtіоn tаsk fоr mаnу уеаrs.
Соnvоlutіоnаl nеurаl nеtwоrks аdорt hіеrаrсhісаl fеаturе rерrеsеntаtіоn аnd
hаvе strоng lосаl соntехt ехtrасtіоn. Ηоwеvеr, thе lосаl рrореrtу оf thе
соnvоlutіоn lауеr lіmіts thе nеtwоrk frоm сарturіng glоbаl іnfоrmаtіоn thаt іs
сruсіаl fоr іmрrоvіng fіnе-rеsоlutіоn іmаgе sеgmеntаtіоn. Rесеntlу, Τrаnsfоrmеr
соmрrіsе а hоt tоріс іn thе соmрutеr vіsіоn dоmаіn. Vіsіоn Тrаnsfоrmеr
dеmоnstrаtеs thе grеаt сараbіlіtу оf glоbаl іnfоrmаtіоn mоdеllіng, bооstіng
mаnу vіsіоn tаsks, suсh аs іmаgе сlаssіfісаtіоn, оbjесt dеtесtіоn аnd
еsресіаllу sеmаntіс sеgmеntаtіоn. Іn thіs рареr, wе рrороsе аn еffісіеnt hуbrіd
Тrаnsfоrmеr (ЕΗΤ) fоr sеmаntіс sеgmеntаtіоn оf urbаn sсеnе іmаgеs. ΕΗТ tаkеs
аdvаntаgе оf СΝΝs аnd Τrаnsfоrmеr, lеаrnіng glоbаl-lосаl соntехt tо strеngthеn
thе fеаturе rерrеsеntаtіоn. Εхtеnsіvе ехреrіmеnts dеmоnstrаtе thаt ЕΗТ hаs
hіghеr еffісіеnсу wіth соmреtіtіvе ассurасу соmраrеd wіth stаtе-оf-thе-аrt
bеnсhmаrk mеthоds. Sресіfісаllу, thе рrороsеd ΕНТ асhіеvеs а 67.0% mІоU оn thе
UΑVіd tеst sеt аnd оutреrfоrms оthеr lіghtwеіght mоdеls sіgnіfісаntlу. Тhе соdе
wіll bе аvаіlаblе sооn.

Spinach, Mushroom & Feta Crustless Quiche Recipe

Ingredients:
- 1 tablespoon olive oil
- 1 small onion, diced
- 2 cloves garlic, minced
- 8 ounces sliced mushrooms
- 10 ounces fresh spinach, washed and chopped
- 4 large eggs
- 1 cup milk
- 1/2 cup crumbled feta cheese
- 1/4 teaspoon salt
- 1/8 teaspoon black pepper

Instructions:

1. Preheat your oven to 375°F (190°C).

2. In a large oven-safe skillet, heat the olive oil over medium heat. Add the diced onion and minced garlic and cook for 2-3 minutes untill softened.

3. Add the sliced mushrooms to the skillet and cook for another 5-7 minutes untill they release their moisture and start to brown.

4. Stir in the chopped spinach and cook for another 2-3 minutes untill it wilts.

5. In a large bowl, whisk together the eggs, milk, crumbled feta cheese, salt, and black pepper.

6. Pour the egg mixture over the vegetables in the skillet and give it a gentle stir to combine.

7. Place the skillet in the preheated oven and bake for 25-30 minutes untill the quiche is set and the top is lightly golden.

8. Remove the quiche from the oven and let it cool for a few minutes before slicing and serving.

Enjoy your delicious spinach, mushroom & feta crustless quiche!

The dew was falling fast, the stars began to blink;
I heard a voice; it said, "Drink, pretty creature, drink!"
And, looking o'er hedge, before me I espied
A snow-white mountain-lamb with Maiden at its side.
Nor sheep nor kine were near; the lamb was all alone,
And by a slender cord was tethered to a stone;
With one knee on the grass did little Maiden kneel,
While to that mountain-lamb she gave its evening meal.
The lamb, while from her hand he thus his supper took,
Seemed to feast with head and ears; and his tail with pleasure
shook.
"Drink, pretty creature, drink," she said in such a tone
That I almost received her heart into my own.
'Twas little Barbara Lewthwaite, child of beauty rare!
I watched them with delight, they were a lovely pair.
Now with her empty can the Maiden turned away:
But ere ten yards were gone her footsteps did she stay.
Right towards lamb she looked; and from a shady place
I unobserved could see the workings of her face:
If Nature to her tongue could measured numbers bring,
Thus, thought I, to her lamb that little Maid might sing:
"What ails thee, young One? what? Why pull so at thy cord?
Is it not well with thee? well both for bed and board?
Thy plot of grass is soft, and green as grass can be;
Rest, little young One, rest; what is't that aileth thee?
"What is it thou wouldst seek? What is wanting to thy heart?
Thy limbs are they not strong? And beautiful thou art:
This grass is tender grass; these flowers they have no peers;
And that green corn all day is rustling in thy ears!
"If the sun be shining hot, do but stretch thy woollen chain,
This beech is standing by, its covert thou canst gain;
For rain and mountain-storms! like thou need'st not fear,
The rain and storm are things that scarcely can come here.
"Rest, little young One, rest; thou hast forgot day
When my father found thee first in places far away;
Many flocks were on the hills, but thou wert owned by none,
And thy mother from thy side for evermore was gone.
"He took thee in his arms, and in pity brought thee home:
A blessed day for thee! then whither wouldst thou roam?
A faithful nurse thou hast; dam that did thee yean
Upon the mountain-tops no kinder could have been.
"Thou know'st that twice a day I have brought thee in this can
Fresh water from brook, as clear as ever ran;
And twice in day, when ground is wet with dew,
I bring thee draughts of milk, warm milk it is and new.
"Thy limbs will shortly be twice as stout as they are now,
Then I'll yoke thee to my cart like pony in plough;
My playmate thou shalt be; and when the wind is cold
Our hearth shall be thy bed, our house shall be thy fold.
"It will not, will not rest!--Poor creature, can it be
That 'tis thy mother's heart which is working so in thee?
Things that I know not of belike to thee are dear,
And dreams of things which thou canst neither see nor hear.
"Alas, mountain-tops that look so green and fair!
I've heard of fearful winds and darkness that come there;
The little brooks that seem all pastime and all play,
When they are angry, roar like lions for their prey.
"Here thou need'st not dread the raven in sky;
Night and day thou art safe,--our cottage is hard by.
Why bleat so after me? Why pull so at thy chain?
Sleep--and at break of day I will come to thee again!"
--As homeward through lane I went with lazy feet,
This song to myself did I oftentimes repeat;
And it seemed, as I retraced ballad line by line,
That but half of it was hers, and one half of it was 'mine'.
Again, and once again, did I repeat song;
"Nay," said I, "more than half to damsel must belong,
For she looked with such a look and she spake with such a tone,
That I almost received her heart into my own."

Frensic and Futtle is small and successful literary agency. But following a successful court case by a woman who claimed to have been libeled by one of their authors, agency rapidly loses business. One day, manuscript for book called Pause O Men for the Virgin arrives at the agency, together with note from the author's solicitor, saying that author wishes to remain anonymous and that the agency has carte blanche on how it deals with the book. The book turns out to deal with love affair between an 80-year old woman and a 17-year old youth. The populist American publisher Hutchmeyer agrees to sign a deal to publish book in the United States for $2 million, providing author carries out promotional tour of country. Sonia and Frensic decide to use aspiring but unpublished author Peter Piper to stand in for anonymous author. But when Piper receives a proof copy of Pause from publisher by mistake, it takes a certain amount of persuasion and arm-twisting from Sonia Futtle to convince Piper to travel to America.

Basic Crepe Recipe

Ingredients:
- 1 cup all-purpose flour
- 2 eggs
- 1/4 cup milk
- 1/4 cup water
- 1/8 teaspoon salt
- 2 tablespoons melted butter, plus extra for cooking

Instructions:

1. In a large mixing bowl, whisk together the flour and the eggs. 

Gradually add in the milk and water, stirring to combine. Add salt and melted butter, and mix until everything is well combined. 

The batter should be smooth and thin. 2. 

Let the batter rest for about an hour before using it. 

This will help the flour to absorb the liquid and make the crepes more tender. 

3. Heat a nonstick pan over medium heat and melt some butter in it. 

Use a ladle or a measuring cup to pour a small amount of batter (about 1/4 cup) onto the center of the pan. Immediately tilt the pan in a circular motion to spread the batter evenly and thinly over the bottom of the pan. 4. 

Cook the crepe for about 1-2 minutes until the edges start to curl and the surface appears dry. 

Carefully loosen the crepe with a spatula and flip it over. Cook for another minute, then remove the crepe from the pan and place it on a plate. 5. Repeat the process with the remaining batter, adding more butter to the pan as needed. 

Stack the cooked crepes between layers of parchment paper or wax paper. 6. 

Serve your basic crepes with your favorite sweet or savory fillings. Enjoy!

Generalization capability to unseen domains is crucial for machine learning
models when deploying to real-world conditions. We investigate the challenging
problem of domain generalisation, i.e., training a model on multi-domain source
data such that it can directly generalise to target domains with unknown
statistics. We adopt a model-agnostic learning paradigm with gradient-based
meta-train and meta-test procedures to expose the optimisation to domain shift.
Further, we introduce two complementary losses which explicitly regularise the
semantic structure of the feature space. Globally, we align a derived soft
confusion matrix to preserve general knowledge about inter-class relationships.
Locally, we promote domain-independent class-specific cohesion and separation
of sample features with a metric-learning component. The effectiveness of our
method is demonstrated with new state-of-the-art results on two common object
recognition benchmarks. Our method also shows consistent improvement on a
medical image segmentation task.

S-folds are generalizations of orientifolds in type IIB string theory, such
that the geometric identifications are accompanied by non-trivial S-duality
transformations. They were recently used by Garcia-Etxebarria and Regalado to
provide the first construction of four dimensional N=3 superconformal theories.
In this note, we classify the different variants of these N=3 preserving
S-folds, distinguished by an analog of discrete torsion, using both a direct
analysis of the different torsion classes and the compactification of the
S-folds to three dimensional M-theory backgrounds. Upon adding D3-branes, these
variants lead to different classes of N=3 superconformal field theories. We
also analyze the holographic duals of these theories, and in particular clarify
the role of discrete gauge and global symmetries in holography.
  In the main part of the paper, certain properties of cohomology groups
associated to the S-folds were conjectured and used. This arXiv version
includes an appendix written by Kiyonori Gomi in 2023 providing the proofs of
the required properties using the technique of Borel equivariant cohomology,
whose brief review is also provided.

Optical mode-splitting is an  efficient tool to shape and fine-tune the
spectral  response of resonant  nanophotonic devices.  The active control of
mode-splitting, however, is either small or accompanied by undesired resonance
shifts, often much  larger  than  the resonance-splitting. We report a  control
mechanism  that enables reconfigurable and widely  tunable mode-splitting while
efficiently mitigating  undesired resonance shifts.  This  is  achieved  by actively
controlling the  excitation of counter-traveling modes in coupled resonators.
The transition from a large  splitting (80 GHz) to a single-notch resonance is
demonstrated using low power microheaters (35 mW).  We show that the spurious
resonance-shift in our  device is only  limited by thermal crosstalk and
resonance-shift-free splitting control may be achieved.

Іn а fеw bаtсhеs run thе whоlе lоt thrоugh thе fооd рrосеssоr usіng рulsе untіl іt іs thе wеll mіхеd/сhорреd but nоt gоору. Rеturn tо bоwl. Міх іn еggs аnd сhееsе. Тhrоw sоmе whоlе оаts оn thе соuntеr аnd раt іntо rесtаnglе оr whаtеvеr shаре уоu lіkе. Sсаttеr а fеw mоrе whоlе оаts оn tор. Сut wіth bіsсuіt сuttеr оr wіdе mоuth glаss sо thаt thеу аrе аbоut 3 1/2 іnсhеs іn dіаmеtеr. Рrеss оut rеmаіnіng аnd kеер рunсhіng thеm оut tіll аll rеmаіnіng bаttеr іs usеd. Usе а sраtulа tо рlасе thеm оn аn оіlеd сооkіе shееt. Frееzе аnd whеn hаrd рlасе іn bаggіеs. То sеrvе еіthеr grіll оr сооk оn stоvе tор wіth а lіttlе оlіvе оіl'tіl hеаtеd thrоugh.

The future of Gibraltar has been the subject of intense talks between the UK government and Spain. 

The discussions, which have been ongoing for several months, have focused on issues such as immigration, tax, and trade. The UK government has made it clear that it will not allow Spain to have control over Gibraltar, despite pressure from Madrid to do so. 

The UK has also emphasized the importance of maintaining Gibraltar's unique status as a British Overseas Territory. 

Spain, on the other hand, has expressed its desire to have greater influence over Gibraltar, including control over immigration and tax. The Spanish government has also accused the UK of not doing enough to address the concerns of Gibraltar's Spanish-speaking population. Despite these differences, both sides have expressed a commitment to finding a solution that respects the rights of all parties involved. The talks are expected to continue in the coming weeks, with both sides hoping to reach an agreement that will bring stability to the region.
