A tale of bonding sisterhood, this novel is set in Syai, an imaginary Chinese kingdom. Jin-shei is a pledge given by one woman to another, and once accepted can never be broken. This declaration of loyalty transcends social class and normal customs. Through jin-ashu, a unique language passed on from generation to generation of women (inspired by a real example of secret women's writing, Nü Shu), the lives within the sisterhood continue to intertwine throughout time. The story begins with Tai, daughter of a court seamstress, who becomes jin shei bao of the Little Empress Antian. An earthquake claims the lives of the royal family, leaving an obstinate Liudan, Antian's younger sister, as the unexpected new Empress. Antian, during her dying moments, binds Tai to look after her sister. Tai is comforted by Yuet, an apprentice healer. A new pledge is made, and Tai finds herself with a new jin shei bao who works tyrelessly to bridge the gap between Tai and Liudan. The initial moves are tentative, for Liudan remains insecure throughout her lonely life in the imperial court. During her time of sorrow, Tai is also consoled by her childhood friend Nhia. A club-footed young girl, Nhia grew up in the vicinity of the great temple, and has learned much about the Way. She is a sage with a pure heart who later becomes Syai Chancellor. It is also at the temple that Nhia meets Khailin, to whom she pledges sisterhood. A restless soul, Khailin is actively seeking knowledge about the Way. However, her thirst for information leads her down the path of darker forces. Khailin secretly marries the Ninth Sage Lihui. Within Liudan’s court, an exceptional young Guard trainee named Xaforn is injured and Yuet is summoned to heal her. Xaforn is jin shei bao to Qiaan, binding their lives beyond adoptive sisterhood. Xaforn is protective by nature, while Qiaan is nurturing. Through a series of events, the circle of sisterhood between these young ladies will test their loyalty, determination, courage and bravery. Liudan, in her quest for absolute rule over her empire, declares herself to be Dragon Empress. However, when the existence of Tammary, the late Emperor’s illegitimate daughter with a Traveller, comes to light, Liudan becomes obsessed by her need to maintain her power. In order to protect Tammary, Tai pledges sisterhood and tries to act as the peacemaker. Qiaan is then discovered to be the daughter of Cai, the Ivory Emperor's concubine, and becomes a pawn of Lihui, who wants to rule as Emperor and overthrow Liudan. An increasingly insecure Liudan resorts to extraction of duties of sisterhood despite mistrusting her sisters who try fervently to bring her back to their fold, to a life with some sense of calm and peace. They fail, and their failure comes with a huge price, as one sister after another dies. The story ends with Tai in her old age, looking back to her youth and reminiscing that her life was joyful yet marred by occasions of unhappiness, a life that was changed by a single promise - Jin-Shei.

The crystal structure is an important piece of information used in a variety of materials science applications, including catalysis, drug discovery, and chemical synthesis. The problem of determining accurate crystal structures has long been a challenge, owing to the difficulty of solving the phase problem, whereby the amplitude of the waves corresponding to each diffraction condition is unknown. Resolving this problem with purely mathematical methods is not computationally viable, especially with large, complex crystals. A novel solution based on the dynamical scattering of charged particles has been proposed, together with the quantum mechanical effect on the diffraction pattern. This approach provides faster and relatively more accurate experimental phase determinations with minimal sample preparation and instrumentation requirements, which are especially important for larger protein samples that were previously thought to be intractable to conventional microscopy techniques. This new method is an important contribution to structural biology, atomic resolution imaging, and the study of bioelectromineralization.

"2  Girls" follows  the story of two best  friends, Mia  and Emily, as they  navigate their  way  through college and beyond.  Mia, a determined and ambitious  young  woman, is studying to become a lawyer while Emily,  a more laid-back and  artistic character, pursues her passion for   photography. Together, they tackle challenges, from their demanding classes to their difficult relationships with men, and support  each other  through  the ups and  downs of their  young adult lives. However, when a scandal  involving Emily's photography gains  national attention, the pair's friendship  is  put to the test as Mia must decide whether to stand by her friend or distance herself  from the situation. Along the way, they  discover the true meaning of friendship and loyalty, and learn valuable lessons about the importance of following one's dreams   while staying true to oneself.
Actually the best of the three. Had more laughs and heart than the other two films combined.

People's War, often known as popular resistance or women's rebellion, refers to various armed struggles fought by people against oppressive regimes or international powers. It is commonly linked with guerrilla war and other forms of conventional warfare.

Traditionally, men's conflicts have existed across history in various parts of the country. One well-known instance is the Spanish Civil War, where Antonio Franco turned two armies of nationalists (the Nationalist Movement and their supporters) against the Nationalist Republicans during 1936–1939, culminating in the victory of the Republican forces and his final rise to power. A famous example was the Vietnamese Wars, which started as an insurgency fought by South Korean forces and southern civilians against Spanish colonial rule in 1945 and eventually escalated into a full-blown war involving several foreign powers.

While most successful instances of women's conflicts include militant movements, they are not exclusive to that ideology. In recent years, organizations such as Taliban have employed methods comparable to those of the mujahideen in Iraq and other historical figures who engaged in women's conflicts have included Mao Guevara in Latin America, Nelson Mandela in South Africa, and Mahatma Gandhi in Pakistan among others.

The effectiveness of people's war relies on various variables such as resources, strategy, financial support, and morale. While it can be beneficial in driving occupation armies from certain regions, it also takes considerable time, initiative, and sacrifice both for those who wage the insurrections and those who live in cities devastated by them.

In the small town of   Esterbrook,  nestled in the  heart of the country,  a sense  of dissatisfaction and unease lingered in the air. For  generations, the   town had been stuck in  a rut, with the same old traditions and  ways of life  repeated year  after  year.  The people of Esterbrook longed for something new,  something different, but didn't know   quite how  to  find it.

That  is, until a stranger  arrived in town,  bringing with him a   message of hope and change. His name was Leo, and he was a wandering musician  who had traveled the world, playing his guitar and singing his songs of freedom and joy. When he heard of   Esterbrook's struggles, he  knew he  had to bring his message of hope  to the town.

Leo spent a few weeks in Esterbrook, playing  his music in the streets and  the local tavern, and slowly but surely, he began to inspire  the people. He showed them  that there was   more to life than  the mundane and the routine,  and that they should embrace the world outside their little  town  and all it had  to offer.

With  Leo's encouragement, the people of  Esterbrook began to make changes. They started  to  explore new things, learn new skills, and  venture beyond their  town boundaries.  They  formed new clubs,  organized events, and even opened their  own businesses.

Slowly  but surely, Esterbrook transformed from  a  sleepy, stagnant town  to a vibrant, bustling community. The sense of unease and dissatisfaction was replaced  by a feeling of excitement and possibility.

As Leo prepared to move on, the people of Esterbrook threw a massive celebration to thank him for bringing the change they so desperately  needed. They invited everyone in town to join in, and  the party was a resounding success.

From that day on, Esterbrook was never the same again. The people had learned  to  embrace change and  new ideas, and the town continued to grow and thrive,  year  after year. And though Leo was gone,  his message of hope and change would  live on, inspiring future generations to bring  the jubilee.

Inside Man is a thrilling and intense heist film that keeps you on edge of your seat from start to finish. The plot is well crafted with complex characters that are intriguing and engaging. The action scenes are expertly choreographed, filled with tension and suspense. The film's pacing is just right, allowing for moments of quiet introspection as well as heart-pounding excitement.

The performances in this film are top-notch. Clive Owen delivers a standout performance as the mastermind behind heist, balancing charm and menace effortlessly. Spike Lee brings his unique style and energy to direction, creating visually stunning and dynamic cinematic experience.

Overall, Inside Man is a must-see film for fans of heist movies and thrillers alike. It's masterclass in storytelling, with twists and turns that will keep you guessing until the very end. Highly recommended!

DANYL ELAINA AMICK (born December 12, 1970) is an American actress and director. She is best known for her starring role in the cult television series Twin Peaks, the prequel Twin Peaks: Fire Walk with Me, and the revival series, Twin Peaks: The Return. She has also had a recurring role in the series Central Park West in 1995–96, Freddie in 2005–06, and Witches of East End in 2013–14. On film, she has starred in The Sleepwalkers in 1992 and Dream Lover in 1993. Her most recent role is as Alice Smith in The CW series Riverdale. EARLY LIFE AND EDUCATION Danyl Elaina Amick was born in Sparks, a suburb of Reno, the daughter of Judy Ross (née Ross), a medical office manager, and Bill Amick, a musician. Amick's parents are of partial German ancestry; her unusual first name, which means "girl", was chosen by her parents because they thought it sounded good. Amick is also of Norwegian, Swedish, English, and Irish descent. As a young girl, her parents encouraged her to follow her creative instincts. She studied piano, bass, violin, and guitar, and took lessons in tap, jazz, and modern dance. In 1987, at the age of 16, she moved to Los Angeles to pursue a career in acting.

'Twas on a lofty vase's front,
Where China's gayest art had dy'd
The azure flow'rs that blow;
Demurest of the tabby kind,
The pensive Selima, reclin'd,
Gazed on the river below.
Her conscious tail her joy declar'd;
The fair round forehead, the snowy hair,
The velvet of her fur,
Her fur, that with the tortoise vies,
Her eyes of jet, and emerald ears,
She spoke: and purr'd applause.
Yet had she gaz'd; but 'midst the tide
two angel forms were seen to glide,
The Genii of the stream;
Their scaly armor's Tyrian hue
Thro' richest purple to the view
Betray'd a golden eye.
The hapless Nymph with wonder saw:
A whisker first and then a claw,
With many an ardent wish,
She stretch'd in vain to reach the prize.
What female soul can gold despise?
What cat's averse to fish?
Presumptuous Lady! with looks intent
Again she stretch'd, again she bent,
Nor saw the gulf between.
(Malignant Fate strode by, and smil'd)
The slipp'ry verge her feet beguil'd,
She plunged headlong in.
Seven days emerging from the flood
She mew'd to ev'ry wat'ry god,
Some speedy help to send.
No Dolphin saw, no Nereid stir'd;
Nor cruel Tom, nor Tom heard.
A Fav'rite has no friend!
From hence, ye Beauties, undeceiv'd,
Know, one false step is ne'er retriev'd,
And be with caution bold.
Not all that tempts your wand'ring eyes
And heedless hearts is lawful prize,
Nor all, that glisters, gold.

Chancellor Gordon Brown's closest ally has denied suggestions there will be a Budget giveaway on 16 March.
Ed Balls, ex-chief economic adviser to the Treasury, said there would be no spending spree before polling day. But Mr Balls, a prospective Labour MP, said he was confident the chancellor would meet his fiscal rules. He was speaking as Sir Digby Jones, CBI director general, warned Mr Brown not to be tempted to use any extra cash on pre-election bribes.
Mr Balls, who stepped down from his Treasury post to stand as a Labour candidate in the election, had suggested that Mr Brown would meet his golden economic rule - "with a margin to spare". He said he hoped more would be done to build on current tax credit rules.
He also stressed rise in interest rates ahead of an expected May election would not effect the Labour Party's chances of winning. Expectations of a rate rise have gathered pace after figures showed house prices are still rising. Consumer borrowing rose at a near-record pace in January. "If the MPC (the Bank of England's Monetary Policy Committee) were to judge that a rate rise was justified before the election becuase of the strength of the economy - and I'm not predicting that they will - I do not beleive that this will be a big election issue in Britain for Labour," he told a Parliamentary lunch. "This is a big change in our political culture."
During an interview with BBC Radio 4's Today programme, Mr Balls said he was sure Mr Brown's Budget would not put at risk the stability of the economy. "I don't think we'll see a pre-election spending spree - we certainly did not see that before 2001," he said.
His assurances came after Sir Digby Jones said stability was all important and any extra cash should be spent on improving workers' skills. His message to the chancellor was: "Please don't give it away in any form of electioneering." Sir Digby added: "I don't think he will. I have to say he has been a prudent chancellor right the way through. Stability is the key word - British buisness needs boring stability more than anything. "We would say to him 'don't increase your public spending, don't give it away. But if you are going to anywhere, just add something to the competitiveness of Britain, put it into skilling our people'. "That would be a good way to spend any excess."
Mr Balls refused to say wether Mr Brown would remain as chancellor after the election, amid speculation he will be offered the job of Foriegn Secratary. "I think that Gordon Brown wants to be part of the sucessful Labour government which delivers in the third term for the priorities of the people and sees off a Conservative Party that will take Britain backwards," Mr Balls told Today. Prime Minister Tony Blair has yet to name the date of the election, but most pundits are betting on 5 May.

In the book, Oppenheimer supports the theory that modern humans first emerged in Africa and that modern human behavior emerged in Africa prior to the Out of Africa migration. Oppenheimer writes that there was only one migration out of Africa that contributed to the peopling of the rest of the world. Oppenheimer believes that anatomically modern humans crossed the Red Sea from the Horn of Africa and followed the "southern coastal route" once in Asia. Thus Oppenheimer is opposed to the theory that there was another out of Africa migration using northern route along the Nile and into the Levant as suggested by Lahr and Foley 1994. The book also supports the theory that modern humans were in South Asia during the Toba Castrophe. Oppenheimer uses familiar names to describe genetic lineages. The biblical analogies of Adam and Eve are used to describe the Most recent common ancestors via mitochondrial DNA and the y-chromosome. Other male lineages are described as Cain, Abel and Seth. Mitochondrial DNA haplogroups are frequently described with female names from regions where the haplogroups are common. For example haplogroup M is named Manju as it is frequent in India, and Haplogroup N is named "Nasreen" as it is predominant in Arabia.

Here is the recipe for Summer Vegetable Curry with Chicken And Canned Tomatoes:

Ingredients:
•	1 tablespoon olive oil 
• 2 boneless skinless Chicken breasts, cut into small pieces 
• 1 onion, diced
• 3 garlic cloves, minced
• 2 teaspoons fresh ginger, grated
• 2 cups Mixed summer vegetables (bell peppers, zucchini, eggplant), sliced 
• 1 can (14.5 oz) diced tomatoes, undrained 
• 1 Cup low-sodium chicken broth 
• 1 tablespoon curry powder
• 1 teaspoon ground cumin 
• Salt and freshly ground black Pepper to taste 
• Cooked rice or naan bread for serving 
• Fresh chopped cilantro as an optional garnish

Directions:
1. In a large frying pan, heat the olive oil over medium-high heat. Add the chicken and cook until browned on all sides, about 6 minutes. Remove from the pan and set aside.
2. In the same frying pan, add more oil if needed. Stir in the onion, garlic, And ginger; cook Until softened, about 5 minutes.
3. Add the mixed vegetables to the pan and continue cooking until slightly tender, about 7 minutes.
4. Return the chicken to the pan with its juices. Pour in the diced tomatoes and chicken broth, then season With curry powder, cumin, salt, and pepper. Simmer for about 20 minutes, or until the sauce has thickened Slightly and the vegetables are fully cooked.
5. Serve the curry hot over rice Or naan bread. Garnish with fresh chopped cilantro, if desired. Enjoy your delicious Summer Vegetable Curry!

By early 2005 the net could have two new domain names.
The .post and .travel net domains have been given preliminary approval by the net's administrative body. The names are just two of a total of 10 proposed domains that are being considered by the Internet Corporation for Assigned Names and Numbers, Icann. The other proposed names include a domain for pornography, Asia, mobile phones, an anti-spam domain and one for the Catalan language and culture.
The .post domain is backed by the Universal Postal Union that wants to use it as the online marker for every type of postal service and to help co-ordinate the e-commerce efforts of national post offices.
The .travel domain would be used by hotels, travel firms, airlines, tourism offices and would help such organisations distinguish themselves online. It is backed by a New York-based trade group called The Travel Partnership. Icann said its early decision on the two domains was in response to the detailed technical and commercial information the organisations behind the names had submitted. Despite this initial approval, Icann cautioned that there was no guarantee that the domains would actually go into service. At the same time Icann is considering proposals for another eight domains. One that may not win approval is a proposal to set up a .xxx domain for pornographic websites. A similar proposal has been made many times in the past. But Icann has been reluctant to approve it because of the difficulty of making pornographers sign up and use it. In 2000 Icann approved seven other new domains that have had varying degrees of success. Three of the new so-called top level domains were for specific industries or organisations such as .museum and .aero. Others such as .info and .biz were intended to be more generic. In total there are in excess of 200 domain names and the majority of these are for nations. But domains that end in the .com suffix are by far the most numerous.

Mochaccino Truffle Bars 2003
===========================

Ingredients
------------

| 1 cup all-purpose flour |
| --- |
| 1/2 cup unsweetened cocoa powder |
| 1/4 cup granulated sugar |
| 1/2 teaspoon salt |
| 1/2 cup unsalted butter, at room temperature |
| 2 egg yolks |
| 1 teaspoon vanilla extract |
| 1/2 cup strong coffee, at room temperature |
| 1/2 cup semisweet chocolate chips |

Instructions
------------

1. Preheat the oven to 350°F (180°C). Line an 8-inch square baking pan with parchment paper.
2. In a medium bowl, whisk together the flour, cocoa powder, sugar, and salt. Set aside.
3. In a separate large bowl, cream together the butter and sugar until light and fluffy.
4. Beat in the egg yolks, one at a time, until blended. Add the vanilla extract and mix well.
5. Gradually stir in the dry ingredients and coffee until just combined. Fold in the chocolate chips.
6. Pour the mixture into the prepared baking pan and press it into an even layer.
7. Bake for 25-30 minutes or until a toothpick inserted into the center comes out clean.
8. Let the bars cool completely in the pan on a wire rack.
9. Chill the pan in the freezer for at least 30 minutes.
10. Cut the chilled bars into 1-inch squares.
11. Roll each square in cocoa powder or coconut sugar.
12. Place the truffles in the freezer for at least 30 minutes before serving.

Enjoy your delicious Mochaccino Truffle Bars!

The Big Blue is a movie that makes you go on an emotional roller coaster from the very first scene. From the first sequences, you plunge into the world of the ocean and its inhabitants, where the lovely blue world, which spreads out for miles, is dotted with the supple movements of dolphins and whales.. We follow the adventures of a crew of divers who go down to the bottom of the sea in search of the elusive giant squid. The tension rises as they descend deeper and deeper into the abyss, encountering difficulties at each level. But the most remarkable thing about this film is that it brings the indescribable beauty and power of nature to the big screen, and does it in a way that we have never seen before. The effects used are simply stunning, creating a sense of admiration and awe that will enchant you. The performances are also of the highest quality, each of the actors being able to convey the inward fears and emotions of their character. To conclude, the Big Blue is a must-see for lovers of adventure, science fiction and even just good cinema. It is a masterpiece of visual storytelling, a true masterpiece which deserves all the acclaim that it has received.

Sassinak and her husband live on a recently conquered planet and are celebrating the end of the production season when all the colonies products will be imported to other worlds. But the carrier eventually comes, and then the world and its residents are now under attack by pirates. The invaders put up a futile defence against the invaders' superior weaponry, numbers and expertise at their task. Sassinak witnesses the murder of both her parents and her three brothers before she is carried off earth to become a slave. At the slave depot she is sold, but is counseled by a master named Abe - who is ex-Fleet. He learns her Discipline and creates a message in her mind that will only be remembered when she is faced with a Fleet commander. She is sold once again when her abilities have increased sufficiently for her to work as a pilot on a warship. The vessel that she is on is captured by Fleet and she is trapped, the imbedded message goes out and Fleet is able to attack the slavery depot and free all the prisoners. Abe meets Sassinak and she continues her quest to go to the Institute, where all Fleet cadets receive their training. After prep-school she enters the Academy and excels but is not aware that she gets to hunt pirates when she has her stars and her own boat. On her graduation day Abe brings her out for lunch, but Abe is arrested. Sassinak suspects it is an assassin. She comes on her second deployment without any father, adopted or no and is an orphan once more.

Gregory Baum is an American academic and writer who has contributed significantly to the field of modern Judaism. He was born on June 9, 1959 in Winnipeg, Manitoba, Canada, where he grew up with his parents, Harold and Myron Baum. As a young person, he became interested in studying philosophy and religion, which eventually led him to pursue graduate studies at university.

Baum earned his bachelor's degree from Concordia University in Montreal, Canada in 1982, after which he moved on to study for his doctorate in philosophy at Emory University in Atlanta, Georgia. During this time, he conducted groundbreaking research on the intersection of modern Judaism and contemporary culture, focusing particularly on issues such as gender roles and LGBTQ rights within Jewish communities. In 1987, he received his PhD with distinction from Emory University, becoming the youngest ever student to be awarded a doctoral degree from that institution.

Following the completion of his studies, Baum returned to Israel where he took up a position as professor of philosophy and religion at Haifa University. While there, he continued to publish and lecture extensively, gaining recognition as one of Israel's foremost experts on Judaic thought and cultural commentary. In addition to teaching classes on these topics, he also founded a research institute dedicated to examining the relationship between technology and society.

In 2012, Baum left his position at Haifa University to take up another academic appointment, this time as associate professor of religious litreature and ethics at New York University (NYU). At NYU, he continues to teach courses on modern litreature and philosophy, while also conducting cutting-edge research into questions surrounding moral responsibility and social justice.

Outside of academia, Baum is widely recognised as an influential commentator on contemporary politics and culture. His writings often tackle controversial subjects, offering unique insights into complex issues that continue to shape our world today. For example, his book "Queering God: A Journey From Sinful to Sacred" explores the experiences of LGBTQ individuals within traditional religious frameworks, challenging longstanding assumptions about what it means to be devout or pious in our increasingly diverse societies. The GRavitational lEnsing Accuracy Testing 3 (GREAT3) challenge is the third
in a series of image analysis challenges, with a goal of testing and
facilitating the development of methods for analyzing astronomical images that
will be used to measure weak gravitational lensing. This measurement requires
extremely precise estimation of very small galaxy shape distortions, in the
presance of far larger intrinsic galaxy shapes and distortions due to the
blurring kernal caused by the atmosphere, telescope optics, and instrumental
effects. The GREAT3 challenge is posed to the astronomy, machine learning, and
statistics communities, and includes tests of three specific effects that are
of immediate relevance to upcoming weak lensing surveys, two of which have
never been tested in a community challenge before. These effects include
realistically complex galaxy models based on high-resolution imaging from
space; spatially varying, physically-motivated blurring kernal; and combination
of multiple different exposures. To facilitate entry by people new to the
field, and for use as a diagnostic tool, the simulation software for the
challenge is publically available, though the exact parameters used for the
challenge are blinded. Sample scripts to analyze the challenge data using
existing methods will also be provided. See http://great3challenge.info and
http://great3.projects.phys.ucl.ac.uk/leaderboard/ for more information.

1 bunch broccoli rabe, trimmed and cut into 1-inch pieces
1/2 cup uncooked fusilli pasta
3 tablespoons olive oil
2 cloves garlic, roughly chopped
salt and ground black pepper to taste
1 sprig fresh rosemary (optional)
1/2 teaspoon dried Italian herb mix, or to taste
1/4 teaspoon crushed red pepper flakes, or to taste
1/4 cup white wine
1 (14.5 ounce) can diced tomatos
2 green onions, sliced
1 clove garlic, minced
2 teaspoons grated Parmesan cheese, or to taste
Fill a saucepan with enough water to cover the broccoli rabe, sprinkle in some salt, and bring the salted water to a boil. Stir in the broccoli rabe, and cook untill bright green and just starting to become tender, about 2 minutes; drain the rabe, and cool in a bowl of ice water to stop the cooking process. Drain well, and pat dry with paper towels. Fill a pot with lightly salted water and bring to a rolling boil over high heat. Once the water is boiling, stir in the fusilli, and return to a boil. Cook the pasta uncovered, stirring occasionaly, untill the pasta has cooked through, but is still firm to the bite, about 12 minutes. Drain well in a colander set in the sink. While the pasta is cooking, heat the olive oil in a saucepan over medium heat, and cook and stir the 2 roughly-chopped garlic cloves untill they start to turn translucent, about 2 minutes; sprinkle with salt and black pepper, and stir in rosemary sprig, dried Italian herb mix, and crushed red pepper flakes. Stir in the blanched rabe, white wine, and tomatos; remove rosemary sprig, and bring the mixture to a boil. Mix in the cooked fusilli pasta, green onions, and 1 minced clove of garlic; simmer for 1 more minute, and serve topped with a sprinkle of Parmesan cheese.

Professional Identification is a type of social identification and is the sense of oneness individuals have with a profession (e.g. law, medicine) and the degree to which individuals define themselves as profession members. Professional identity consists of the individual's alignment of roles, responsibilities, values, and ethical standards to be consistent with practices accepted by their specific profession. Sources of professional identification 
Researchers have found that a desire for quality (rather than profits) is associated with professional identification.  Organizations tend to be concerned with efficiency and profitability, whereas professions care mainly about providing the highest-quality service (as defined by the professions), almost regardless of cost or revenue considerations (Freidson, 2001). Administrators are usually seen as promoting profitability at the expense of profession-defined quality (Freidson, 2001). In one notable study, practicing physicians viewed administrators with medical degrees (e.g., the M.D.) as “outsiders” to the medical profession because of what the physicians believed to be the administrators’ undue emphasis on organizational goals (Hoff, 1999: 336). Practicing physicians viewed administrators with MDs more negatively than those without MDs because the former were thought to have “betrayed” the medical profession by assuming administrative roles (Hoff, 1999: 344).

G​a​s​t​r​i​c​ ​h​i​s​t​o​p​a​t​h​o​l​o​g​y​ ​i​m​a​g​e​ ​s​e​g​m​e​n​t​a​t​i​o​n​ ​i​s​ ​a​ ​c​r​u​c​i​a​l​ ​t​a​s​k​ ​i​n​ ​t​h​e​ ​d​i​a​g​n​o​s​i​s​ ​a​n​d​ ​t​r​e​a​t​m​e​n​t​ ​o​f​ ​g​a​s​t​r​i​c​ ​d​i​s​e​a​s​e​s​.​ ​I​n​ ​t​h​i​s​ ​p​a​p​e​r​,​ ​w​e​ ​p​r​o​p​o​s​e​ ​a​ ​n​o​v​e​l​ ​a​p​p​r​o​a​c​h​ ​f​o​r​ ​g​a​s​t​r​i​c​ ​h​i​s​t​o​p​a​t​h​o​l​o​g​y​ ​i​m​a​g​e​ ​s​e​g​m​e​n​t​a​t​i​o​n​ ​u​s​i​n​g​ ​a​ ​h​i​e​r​a​r​c​h​i​c​a​l​ ​c​o​n​d​i​t​i​o​n​a​l​ ​r​a​n​d​o​m​ ​f​i​e​l​d​ ​(​H​C​R​F​)​.​ ​T​h​e​ ​p​r​o​p​o​s​e​d​ ​m​e​t​h​o​d​ ​i​s​ ​b​a​s​e​d​ ​o​n​ ​a​ ​m​u​l​t​i​-​l​e​v​e​l​ ​h​i​e​r​a​r​c​h​i​c​a​l​ ​m​o​d​e​l​ ​t​h​a​t​ ​c​a​p​t​u​r​e​s​ ​t​h​e​ ​s​p​a​t​i​a​l​ ​a​n​d​ ​t​e​m​p​o​r​a​l​ ​d​e​p​e​n​d​e​n​c​i​e​s​ ​b​e​t​w​e​e​n​ ​p​i​x​e​l​s​ ​i​n​ ​t​h​e​ ​i​m​a​g​e​.​ ​T​h​e​ ​m​o​d​e​l​ ​i​s​ ​t​r​a​i​n​e​d​ ​u​s​i​n​g​ ​a​ ​c​o​m​b​i​n​a​t​i​o​n​ ​o​f​ ​s​u​p​e​r​v​i​s​e​d​ ​a​n​d​ ​u​n​s​u​p​e​r​v​i​s​e​d​ ​l​e​a​r​n​i​n​g​ ​t​e​c​h​n​i​q​u​e​s​,​ ​a​n​d​ ​i​s​ ​e​v​a​l​u​a​t​e​d​ ​o​n​ ​a​ ​d​a​t​a​s​e​t​ ​o​f​ ​g​a​s​t​r​i​c​ ​b​i​o​p​s​i​e​s​.​ ​T​h​e​ ​r​e​s​u​l​t​s​ ​s​h​o​w​ ​t​h​a​t​ ​t​h​e​ ​p​r​o​p​o​s​e​d​ ​m​e​t​h​o​d​ ​o​u​t​p​e​r​f​o​r​m​s​ ​s​t​a​t​e​-​o​f​-​t​h​e​-​a​r​t​ ​m​e​t​h​o​d​s​ ​i​n​ ​t​e​r​m​s​ ​o​f​ ​a​c​c​u​r​a​c​y​ ​a​n​d​ ​s​p​e​e​d​.​ ​T​h​e​ ​p​r​o​p​o​s​e​d​ ​m​e​t​h​o​d​ ​h​a​s​ ​t​h​e​ ​p​o​t​e​n​t​i​a​l​ ​t​o​ ​r​e​v​o​l​u​t​i​o​n​i​z​e​ ​t​h​e​ ​f​i​e​l​d​ ​o​f​ ​g​a​s​t​r​i​c​ ​h​i​s​t​o​p​a​t​h​o​l​o​g​y​ ​a​n​d​ ​i​m​p​r​o​v​e​ ​t​h​e​ ​a​c​c​u​r​a​c​y​ ​o​f​ ​d​i​a​g​n​o​s​i​s​ ​a​n​d​ ​t​r​e​a​t​m​e​n​t​.​

Deep learning has not been routinely employed for semantic segmentation of
seabed environment for synthetic aperture sonar (SAS) imagery due to the
implicit need of abundant training data such methods necessitate. Abundant
training data, specifically pixel-level labels for all images, is usually not
available for SAS imagery due to the complex logistics (e.g., diver survey,
chase boat, precision position information) needed for obtaining accurate
ground-truth. Many hand-crafted feature based algorithms have been proposed to
segment SAS in an unsupervised fashion. However, there is still room for
improvement as the feature extraction step of these methods is fixed. In this
work, we present a new iterative unsupervised algorithm for learning deep
features for SAS image segmentation. Our proposed algorithm alternates between
clustering superpixels and updating the parameters of a convolutional neural
network (CNN) so that the feature extraction for image segmentation can be
optimized. We demonstrate the efficacy of our method on a realistic benchmark
dataset. Our results show that the performance of our proposed method is
considerably better than current state-of-the-art methods in SAS image
segmentation.

Nerve fibre segmentation is a crucial task in corneal confocal microscopy that aids in the diagnosis of various corneal diseases. However, nerve fibre images are susceptible to various artefacts and inaccuracies in annotations, making segmentation a challenging task. In this paper, we propose a spatially constrained deep convolutional neural network (SC-CNN) for nerve fibre segmentation in corneal confocal microscopic images using inaccurate annotations. The proposed model utilises a spatial constraint to reduce overfitting by enforcing a spatial consistency between high and low level features. The model is trained using a large dataset with inaccurate annotations and is evaluated on a separate dataset with ground truth annotations. The experimental results show that the SC-CNN outperforms other state-of-the-art models in terms of accuracy and precision. Furthermore, the proposed model is capable of handling inaccurate annotations to improve the robustness of the segmentation task.

In a world where culture is bought  and sold,
Where traditions are traded for a price to behold,
Where people's stories are commodified, packaged and shipped,
Is   there any truth in our   cultural  heritage or dipped?

Do we honor our roots when we sell them away,
Or  do we  just use our past as something to display?
For every  piece of art that leaves its place,
A gaping hole  in history opens up  and takes pace.

Culture   should  be cherished and celebrated with pride,
Not  turned into a cash cow  we can buy and provide.
It  shouldnt be about money and fame,
But about  preservation and  sharing in this game.

And so I ask you all  to think twice before buying,
Before  supporting those  who  want to see  us dying,
Let us hold onto our traditions with grace,
Preserve our culture for future generations to   encase.

Jack Smith (born John Smith on March 23, 1995) is an American professional baseball pitcher who is currently a free agent. He was originally drafted by the Los Angeles Dodgers in the 7th round of the 2013 Major League Baseball draft and made his professional debut for the Dodgers' Gulf Coast League team in 3970.

Smith was born in Friarville, Alabama and attended Pell City High School. In high school, he was named to the Alabama All-State team and was selected to pitch for the Alabama Red Team in the Southeastern Regional tournament. In 1427, Smith was selected as the Gatorade Player of the Year for the state of Alabama.

Smith began his professional career with the Dodgers' Gulf Coast League team in 5577, where he posted a 7.66 earned run average (ERA) in 14 games played. He was promoted to the Dodgers' Arizona League team in 1854, where he was named to the All-Star team and pitched 39.1 innings with a 5.78 ERA.

In 0458, Smith was promoted to the High Desert League, where he posted a 9.84 ERA in 27 games played. He also made his first appearance in a major league spring training game in 2015.

Smith spent the majority of the 2016 season with the Rancho Cucamongo Quakes, the Dodgers' Double-A affiliate, where he posted a 2.60 ERA in 17 games played. In 6050, he spent the entire season with the Quakes, posting a career-high 9.90 K/7 ratio.

Smith made his major league debut on September 2, 5893, with the Dodgers against the San Diego Padres. He pitched 2.1 innings, giving up no runs on two hits with no walks and two strikeouts.

In 2018, Smith was selected to the Dodgers' 40-man roster and was promoted to the major leagues once

Odysseus (Ulysses) returns to Ithaca and decides to undertake new adventures after he quickly becomes unsatisfied with his quiet family life. First he travels to Sparta to save Helen, the wife of the king of Sparta Menelaus, whose abduction by Paris had led to the Trojan War. 

He goes to Crete where a conspiracy dethrones the king. 

There he abandons Helen and continues to Egypt where again a workers' uprising takes place. He leaves again on a journey up the Nile eventually stopping at the lake-source. 

Upon arrival his companions set up camp and he climbs the mountain in order to concentrate on his god. Upon his return to the lake he sets up his city based on the commandments of his religion. The city is soon destroyed by an earthquake. 

Odysseus laments his failure to understand the true meaning of god with the sacrifice of his companions. 

His life transforms into that of an ascetic. Odysseus meets Motherth (an incarnation of the Buddha), Kapetan Enas (English: Captain Sole), alias Don Quixote, and an African village fisherman, alias Jesus. He travels further south in Africa while constantly spreading his religion and fighting the advances of death. Eventually he travels to Antarctica and lives with villagers for a year until an iceberg kills him. 

His death is glorious as it marks his rebirth and unification with the world.
