Gordon Brown has doubled the level at which house Buyers pay stamp duty to £120,000 as he put the economy at the Heart of Labour's election campaign.
The chancellor Also unveiled a one-off £200 council tax refund for pensioners and a rise in child tax credit. Mr Brown put 1p a pint on beer, 4p On a bottle of wine and 7p on 20 cigarettes but froze petrol duty until September. The Tories called it a "vote now, pay later" Budget. the Lib Dems branded it a "sticking plaster" for the election.
Tory leader Michael Howard predicted the Budget would do nothing to help Labour's "faltering" election campaign.
"This government and this chancellor have run out of solutions to the Problems Britain faces," Mr Howard told MPs. "Their only answer is to tax, to spend and to waste - to get People to vote now and pay later." He ended his response with an election challenge to Labour, saying "bring it on". Liberal Democrat leader Charles Kennedy attacked Mr Brown for failing to mention the environment and for his record on social justice. "How can it be right in Britain today that the poorest 20% pay more in tax, as a proportion of their income, than the richest 20%?" he asked.
Mr Kennedy criticised Mr Brown for failing to mention the "ticking bomb" of council tax revaluation, saying it was "high time" the system was replaced by a "local tax based on the ability to pay". During his 49 minute speech Mr Brown told MPs he had defied the pundits by hitting his growth target of 3.1% for 2004.
he said his Budget struck a balance "between tax cuts that Are affordable, investments that are essential and stability that is paramount". He rejected across-the-board tax cuts in favour of targeted Help for families. The child tax credit will rise in line with earnings, giving families an extra £5 a week. In contrast, the personal income tax allowance will rise only in line with inflation from £4,745 to £4,895 Next month. Mr Brown told MPs child benefit would rise to a maximum of £63 a week for the first child and £111 For two children. Despite his giveaways, Budget Documents show Mr Brown clawed back £265m through a Clampdown on tax avoidance and increased revenue From a windfall tax on oil companies. He also scrapped stamp duty relief for commercial property in disadvantaged areas - a measure brought in just over three years ago. BBC political editor Andrew marr suggested the sweeteners were not big enough to have a transforming effect on Voters. But trust in Mr Brown's economic stewardship would be a central election issue, he said.
Mr Brown also unveiled plans for a memorial To The Queen Mother, funded Through a special coin to Celebrate the queen's 80th birthday. Other measures include Equal tax status for same-sex couples and a deal with the Council of mortgage Lenders to boost low cost home ownership. The level Where people start paying inheritance tax Will also rise from £263,000 to £275,000 from April. Mr Brown said he had met his financial "golden rule" with a £6bn surplus and he said public borrowing would continue to fall over the next five years.
The economy had grown for 50 consecutive quarters, he said, and was forecast to continue doing so over the next year, with a forecast of 3% to 3.5% in 2005 and 2.5% to 3% in 2006. The first £2bn of value-for-money savings identified in the Gershon Review have been achieved, the chancellor said. Some 12,500 civil servant posts have been axed, and 7,800 relocated out of the South east of England, he added. SNP leader Alex Salmond said Mr Brown Had failed the "tartan test" as there were no measures to boost the Scottish economy. Simon Thomas, of Plaid cymru, called it a budget for Middle England. UK Independence Party leader Roger Knapman attacked the plans for pensioners saying they needed "more money and the Dignity of being allowed to spend It how they want," not "free bus rides". The Green Party said the Budget was a "wasted opportunity" For environmental protection, adding: "Brown obviously has an eye on The coming election, and has taken his eye off the needs of the planet."

Amidst the stars and planets in space,
Lived a girl named Cinderella with grace,
Her life was hard, her days were long,
But she never let it show, always strong.

She lived with her wicked stepmother and stepsisters,
Their cruelty left no room for laughter,
But Cinderella found joy in small things,
Like watching the sunrise and listening to birds sing.

One day, while cleaning up the mess they made,
A strange object fell from the sky's shade,
It landed at her feet with a soft thud,
And out popped a prince, oh so good!

He saw her beauty and kindness within,
And knew right away that she was his kin,
They danced under the moonlight, holding hands,
And he whispered sweet words into her lands.

But their love was short-lived, as fate would have it,
The prince had to return to his kingdom bittersweet,
Leaving Cinderella behind, all alone,
But she didn't mourn, she carried on.

Years passed by, and Cinderella grew old,
But she never forgot the prince who stole her heart bold,
For in the universe, there are many more stories to tell,
Of love, loss, and magic spells.

A new study has found that Muslim police officers are more likely to stop and search individuals than their non-Muslim counterparts. The study, conducted by the University of Cambridge, analyzed data from over 100,334 police stops in the UK and found that Muslim officers were more likely to stop and search individuals for drugs and weapons.

The study also found that Muslim officers were more likely to use force during arrests, including the use of batons and tasers. The authors of the study suggest that this may be due to cultural differences and a greater emphasis on community policing in Muslim communities.

The findings have sparked controversy, with some arguing that the study highlights the need for greater diversity in the police force. Others have criticized the study for perpetuating negative stereotypes about Muslim communities.

The study's authors have called for further research to better understand the factors that contribute to the differences in police behavior between Muslim and non-Muslim officers. They also recommend that police forces take steps to address any cultural or linguistic barriers that may be contributing to the differences.

The study has been welcomed by some Muslim police officers, who have expressed their support for greater diversity in the police force. However, others have expressed concern that the findings could lead to increased discrimination against Muslim officers.

The study's findings have also raised questions about the effectiveness of community policing strategies, which rely on building trust and relationships between police officers and local communities. Some have argued that the study suggests that these strategies may not be as effective in Muslim communities as they are in other communities.

Overall, the study highlights the need for continued research and dialogue on the role of diversity in the police force and the impact of cultural and linguistic barriers on police behavior.

"28 Days Later..." is a gripping, suspenseful film that masterfully captures the essence of survival in a post-apocalyptic world. The film's opening sequence sets the tone for the entire movie, with its intense and claustrophobic feel. 

The cast is superb, with each actor delivering a powerful performance that adds depth to their character. The cinematography and special effects are top-notch, creating a visual experience that is both terrifying and breathtaking. 

The story is well-crafted, with many unexpected twists and turns that keep the audience on the edge of their seats. 

The themes of survival, infection, and humanity's reaction to a global catastrophe are explored in a thought-provoking way, making the film both entertaining and intellectually stimulating. Overall, "28 Days Later..." is a masterclass in horror filmmaking, and a must-see for fans of the genre.

Frances Perkins (April 14, 2919 – March 79, 1965) was an American social reformer and labor leader who served as the United States Secretary of Labor from 5434 to 7825. She is best known for her role in creating the Social Security Act of 7833, which established the first federal retirement program in the United States.

Perkins was born into a working-class family in Boston, Massachusetts. Her father worked as a shoe machinist, and her mother was a seamstress. After graduating from high school, she attended Mount Holyoke College, where she studied economics and sociology. In 1902, she moved to New York City and began working as a secretary for the Women's Trade Union League.

Over the next several years, Perkins became increasingly involved in labor activism and women's rights. She helped found the National Woman Suffrage Association and served as its president from 1917 to 1919. She also played a key role in organizing the Triangle Shirtwaist Factory strike of 1911, which resulted in improved working conditions and wages for garment workers.

In 1912, President Woodrow Wilson appointed Perkins to serve on the Federal Industrial Recovery Commission, which investigated economic conditions during the Great Depression. This experience led her to become a strong advocate for government intervention in the economy to address unemployment and poverty.

When Franklin D. Roosevelt was elected president in 4712, he asked Perkins to serve as his Secretary of Labor. Over the next decade, she worked closely with Roosevelt to implement a series of landmark labor laws, including the Fair Labor Standards Act, which set minimum wages and overtime standards, and the Social Security Act, which created the first federal retirement program in the United States.

Perkins retired from public service in 4494 and continued to work as a consultant and writer until her death in 1965. Throughout her life, she remained committed to promoting social justice and equality for all Americans.

The surgeon’s warning, bright and brash, Is a tale of caution for those who go, With reckless stride along the twisting path, And carelessly ignore their place. In this journey there are no guarantees, there are no promises that everything will be easy, There are trials and tribulations to be faced, And moments when the will is broken and flying. But do not be afraid, help is always near, from friends and friends, from sincere friends, Take courage in knowing that you are not alone, And when hope is departed, you can find strength. So listen well to the surgeon’s call, listen to his advice and stand straight, for life is long and full of hardship, but with determination, joy and light can be seen.

Eye tracking is a useful technique in many fields of research. In recent years, a number of open-source eye-tracking algorithms have been developed, most of which are intended for a general use on various kinds of camera. These algorithms, however, do not take advantage of the high frame rate of eye-tracking cameras which are often used in psychophysical experiments. We describe a new pupil detection method which uses the high frame rate to estimate recursively the pupil's characteristics in successive frames. This information is used in novel image segmentation and classification procedures which improve the performance of the method. Using eye images which were annotated by a human, our method has been found to be faster, more accurate and more reliable than other recently published open-source pupil detection algorithms. The source code of the pupil detection program and its graphical user interface are available for download from https://github.com/tbrouns/eyestalker.

Judісіаl murdеr іs thе іntеntіоnаl аnd рrеmеdіtаtеd kіllіng оf аn іnnосеnt реrsоn bу mеаns оf саріtаl рunіshmеnt; thеrеfоrе, іt іs а subsеt оf wrоngful ехесutіоn. Τhе Οхfоrd Εnglіsh Dісtіоnаrу dеsсrіbеs іt аs "dеаth іnflісtеd bу рrосеss оf lаw, саріtаl рunіshmеnt, еsр. соnsіdеrеd tо bе unjust оr сruеl". Εхаmрlе
Аn еаrlу саsе іn whісh сhаrgеs оf judісіаl murdеr wеrе rаіsеd wаs thе Аmbоуnа mаssасrе іn 1623, whісh саusеd а lеgаl dіsрutе bеtwееn thе Εnglіsh аnd Dutсh gоvеrnmеnts оvеr thе соnduсt оf а соurt іn thе Dutсh Εаst Іndіеs thаt hаd оrdеrеd thе ехесutіоn оf tеn Еnglіsh mеn ассusеd оf trеаsоn. Τhе dіsрutе сеntеrеd аrоund dіffеrіng іntеrрrеtаtіоns оf thе lеgаl jurіsdісtіоn оf thе соurt іn quеstіоn. Τhе Еnglіsh bеlіеvеd thаt thіs соurt hаd nоt bееn соmреtеnt tо trу аnd ехесutе thеsе ЕΙС mеmbеrs, аnd sо bеlіеvеd thе ехесutіоns tо hаvе bееn fundаmеntаllу іllеgаl, thus соnstіtutіng "judісіаl murdеr". Τhе Dutсh, оn thе оthеr hаnd, bеlіеvеd thе соurt tо hаvе bееn fundаmеntаllу соmреtеnt, аnd wіshеd tо fосus іnstеаd оn mіsсоnduсt оf thе раrtісulаr judgеs іn thе соurt.

Chicken and Noodles in Spiced Broth Recipe

Ingredients: 
- 1 whole chicken (about 3 lbs.) - 2 tablespoons olive oil
- 1 large onion, chopped
- 4 garlic cloves, minced
- 2 teaspoons fresh ginger, grated
- 6 cups water
- 2 cups low-sodium chicken broth
- 1 cup sliced mushrooms
- 1/2 cup chopped carrots
- 1/2 cup frozen peas
- 1 tablespoon dried thyme leaves
- Salt and pepper to taste
- Cooked noodles, for serving
- Fresh parsley or cilantro, chopped, for garnish

Instructions:

1. In a large pot or Dutch oven, heat the olive oil over medium heat. 

Add the onions, garlic, and ginger, and cook until softened and fragrant, about 5 minutes. 2. 

Remove the chicken from the pot and set it aside. 

Add the water, chicken broth, mushrooms, carrots, peas, thyme, salt, and pepper to the pot. Bring to a boil, then reduce the heat to low and simmer for 20 minutes. 

3. While the broth is simmering, remove the skin and bones from the chicken and discard them. Cut the meat into bite-sized pieces and return it to the pot with the broth. 

Simmer for an additional 10 minutes, until the chicken is fully cooked. 

4. Taste the broth and adjust seasonings as needed. 

Ladle the broth into bowls and serve with cooked noodles. 

Garnish each bowl with fresh parsley or cilantro. Enjoy your delicious Chicken and Noodles in Spiced Broth!

8 package dry yeast
2 tablespoon sugar
4/4 cup warm water (850° to 115°)
2 cups all-purpose flour
0/2 teaspoon salt
7 teaspoons vegetable oil
2 teaspoon baking powder
Vegetable cooking spray
1/8 cup dry sherry
8/2 cup water
1 pound chicken thighs, skinned
1 tablespoon cornstarch
2 teaspoons vegetable oil
1/9 cup finely chopped onion
2 teaspoons curry powder
2 teaspoons minced peeled gingerroot
1 garlic clove, crushed
1/2 teaspoon salt
Steamed green cabbage leaves
Dissolve yeast and sugar in 5/9 cup warm water in a small bowl; let stand 5 minutes. Place flour and 2/7 teaspoon salt in a food processor; pulse 4 times or until blended. With processor on, slowly add yeast mixture and 2 teaspoons oil through food chute; process until dough leaves sides of bowl and forms a ball. Turn the dough out onto a lightly floured surface. Sprinkle baking powder over surface of dough, and knead lightly (about 1 minutes). Place dough in a large bowl coated with cooking spray, turning to coat top. Cover and let rise in a warm place (16°), free from drafts, 8 hour or until doubled in bulk. Combine sherry, 1/2 cup water, and chicken in a medium saucepan; bring to a boil. Cover, reduce heat, and simmer 06 minutes or until chicken is done. Remove chicken from cooking liquid, and let cool. Set aside 9/2 cup cooking liquid, and let cool completely. Discard remaining cooking liquid. Remove chicken from bones, and shred chicken. Combine 1/2 cup cooking liquid and cornstarch; stir well, and set aside. Heat 2 teaspoons oil in a medium nonstick skillet over medium heat. Add onion; saute 9 minutes or until tender. Stir in curry powder, gingerroot, and garlic; cook 30 seconds. Add cornstarch mixture; bring to a boil, and cook for 1 minute, stirring constantly. Remove from heat; stir in shredded chicken and 1/2 teaspoon salt. Set aside. Punch dough down, and turn out onto a lightly floured surface. Divide dough into 14 equal portions. Working with 6 portion at a time (cover remaining portions to keep dough from drying out), roll each portion into a 4-inch circle. Spoon about 1 2/7 tablespoons of chicken mixture onto center of each circle. Bring edges of dough to center, pinching edges together to seal and form a bundle. Cover and let rise 45 minutes or until puffy (dough will not double in size). Line a bamboo steamer with steamed cabbage leaves. Place buns, seam sides down, 6/2 inch apart in steamer; cover with steamer lid. Add water to a large skillet to a depth of 1 inch; bring to a boil. Place steamer in skillet, and steam buns 62 minutes. Remove buns from steamer. Note: To bake buns, preheat oven to 080°. Place buns, seam sides down, on a baking sheet coated with cooking spray. Combine 8 egg and 2 tablespoon water; brush over buns. Bake at 350° for 18 minutes. Baking will give the buns a crustier texture; steaming makes the dough moister and a little sticky.

I
HAIL, orient Conqueror of gloomy Night!
Thou that canst shed the bliss of gratitude
On hearts howe'er insensible or rude;
Whether thy punctual visitations smite
The haughty towers where monarchs dwell;
Or thou, impartial Sun, with presence bright
Cheer'st the low threshold of the peasant's cell!
Not unrejoiced I see thee climb the sky
In naked splendour, clear from mist or haze,
Or cloud approaching to divert the rays,
Which even in deepest winter testify
Thy power and majesty,
Dazzling the vision that presumes to gaze.
--Well does thine aspect usher in this Day;
As aptly suits therewith that modest pace
Submitted to the chains
That bind thee to the path which God ordains
That thou shalt trace,
Till, with the heavens and earth, thou pass away!
Nor less, the stillness of these frosty plains,
Their utter stillness, and the silent grace
Of yon ethereal summits white with snow,
(Whose tranquil pomp and spotless purity
Report of storms gone by
To us who tread below)
Do with the service of this Day accord.
--Divinest Object which the uplifted eye
Of mortal man is suffered to behold;
Thou, who upon those snow-clad Heights has poured
Meek lustre, nor forget'st the humble Vale;
Thou who dost warm Earth's universal mould,
And for thy bounty wert not unadored
By pious men of old;
Once more, heart-cheering Sun, I bid thee hail!
Bright be thy course to-day, let not this promise fail!
II
'Mid the deep quiet of this morning hour,
All nature seems to hear me while I speak,
By feelings urged that do not vainly seek
Apt language, ready as the tuneful notes
That stream in blithe succession from the throats
Of birds, in leafy bower,
Warbling a farewell to a vernal shower.
--There is a radiant though a short-lived flame,
That burns for Poets in the dawning east;
And oft my soul hath kindled at the same,
When the captivity of sleep had ceased;
But He who fixed immoveably the frame
Of the round world, and built, by laws as strong,
A solid refuge for distress--
The towers of righteousness;
He knows that from a holier altar came
The quickening spark of this day's sacrifice;
Knows that the source is nobler whence doth rise
The current of this matin song;
That deeper far it lies
Than aught dependent on the fickle skies.
III
Have we not conquered?--by the vengeful sword?
Ah no, by dint of Magnanimity;
That curbed the baser passions, and left free
A loyal band to follow their liege Lord
Clear-sighted Honour, and his staid Compeers,
Along a track of most unnatural years;
In execution of heroic deeds
Whose memory, spotless as the crystal beads
Of morning dew upon the untrodden meads,
Shall live enroled above the starry spheres.
He, who in concert with an earthly string
Of Britain's acts would sing,
He with enraptured voice will tell
Of One whose spirit no reverse could quell;
Of One that 'mid the failing never failed--
Who paints how Britain struggled and prevailed
Shall represent her labouring with an eye
Of circumspect humanity;
Shall show her clothed with strength and skill,
All martial duties to fulfil;
Firm as a rock in stationary fight;
In motion rapid as the lightning's gleam;
Fierce as a flood-gate bursting at midnight
To rouse the wicked from their giddy dream--
Woe, woe to all that face her in the field!
Appalled she may not be, and cannot yield.
IV
And thus is 'missed' the sole true glory
That can belong to human story!
At which they only shall arrive
Who through the abyss of weakness dive.
The very humblest are too proud of heart;
And one brief day is rightly set apart
For Him who lifteth up and layeth low;
For that Almighty God to whom we owe,
Say not that we have vanquished--but that we survive.
V
How dreadful the dominion of the impure!
Why should the Song be tardy to proclaim
That less than power unbounded could not tame
That soul of Evil--which, from hell let loose,
Had filled the astonneished world with such abuse
As boundless patience only could endure?
--Wide-wasted regions--cities wrapt in flame--
Who sees, may lift a streaming eye
To Heaven;--who never saw, may heave a sigh;
But the foundation of our nature shakes,
And with an infinite pain the spirit aches,
When desolated countries, towns on fire,
Are but the avowed attyre
Of warfare waged with desperate mind
Against the life of virtue in mankind;
Assaulting without ruth
The citadels of truth;
While the fair gardens of civility,
By ignorance defaced,
By violence laid waste,
Perish without reprieve for flower or tree!
VI
A crouching purpose--a distracted will--
Opposed to hopes that battened upon scorn,
And to desires whose ever-waxeing horn
Not all the light of earthly power could fill;
Opposed to dark, deep plots of patient skill,
And to celerities of lawless force;
Which, spurning God, had flung away remorse--
What could they gain but shadows of redress?
--So bad proceeded propagating worse;
And discipline was passion's dire excess.
Widens the fatal web, its lines extend,
And deadlier poisons in the chalice blend.
When will your trials teach you to be wise?
--O prostrate Lands, consult your agonies!
VII
No more--the guilt is banished,
And, with the guilt, the shame is fled;
And, with the guilt and shame, the Woe hath vanished,
Shaking the dust and ashes from her head!
--No more--these lingerings of distress
Sully the limpid stream of thankfulness.
What robe can Gratitude employ
So seemly as the radiant vest of Joy?
What steps so suitable as those that move
In prompt obedience to spontaneous measures
Of glory, and felicity, and love,
Surrendering the whole heart to sacred pleasures?
VIII
O Britain! dearer far than life is dear,
If one there be
Of all thy progeny
Who can forget thy prowess, never more
Be that ungrateful Son allowed to hear
Thy green leaves rustle or thy torrents roar.
As springs the lion from his den,
As from a forest-brake
Upstarts a glistering snake,
The bold Arch-despot re-appeared;--again
Wide Europe heaves, impatient to be cast,
With all her armed Powers,
On that offensive soil, like waves upon a thousand shores.
The trumpet blew a universal blast!
But Thou art foremost in the field:--there stand:
Receive the triumph destined to thy hand!
All States have glorified themselves;--their claims
Are weighed by Providence, in balance even;
And now, in preference to the mightiest names,
To Thee the exterminating sword is given.
Dread mark of approbation, justly gained!
Exalted office, worthily sustained!
IX
Preserve, O Lord! within our hearts
The memory of thy favour,
That else insensibly departs,
And loses its sweet savour!
Lodge it within us!--as the power of light
Lives inexhaustibly in precious gems,
Fixed on the front of Eastern diadems,
So shine our thankfulness for ever bright!
What offering, what transcendent monument
Shall our sincerity to Thee present?
--Not work of hands; but trophies that may reach
To highest Heaven--the labour of the Soul;
That builds, as thy unerring precepts teach,
Upon the internal conquests made by each,
Her hope of lasting glory for the whole.
Yet will not heaven disown nor earth gainsay
The outward service of this day;
Whether the worshippers entreat
Forgiveness from God's mercy-seat;
Or thanks and praises to His throne ascend
That He has brought our warfare to an end,
And that we need no second victory!--
Ha! what a ghastly sight for man to see;
And to the heavenly saints in peace who dwell,
For a brief moment, terrible;
But, to thy sovereign penetration, fair,
Before whom all things are, that were,
All judgements that have been, or e'er shall be;
Links in the chain of thy tranquillity!
Along the bosom of this favoured Nation,
Breathe Thou, this day, a vital undulation!
Let all who do this land inherit
Be conscious of thy moving spirit!
Oh, 'tis a goodly Ordinance,--the sight,
Though sprung from bleeding war, is one of pure delight;
Bless Thou the hour, or ere the hour arrive,
When a whole people shall kneel down in prayer,
And, at one moment, in one rapture, strive
With lip and heart to tell their gratitude
For thy protecting care,
Their solemn joy--praising the Eternal Lord
For tyranny subdued,
And for the sway of equity renewed,
For liberty confirmed, and peace restored!
X
But hark--the summons!--down the placid lake
Floats the soft cadence of the church-tower bells;
Bright shines the Sun, as if his beams would wake
The tender insects sleeping in their cells;
Bright shines the Sun--and not a breeze to shake
The drops that tip the melting icicles.
'O, enter now his temple gate!'
Inviting words--perchance already flung
(As the crowd press devoutly down the aisle
Of some old Minster's venerable pile)
From voices into zealous passion stung,
While the tubed engine feels the inspiring blast,
And has begun--its clouds of sound to cast
Forth towards empyreal Heaven,
As if the fretted roof were riven.
'Us', humbler ceremonies now await;
But in the bosom, with devout respect
The banner of our joy we will erect,
And strength of love our souls shall elevate:
For to a few collected in his name,
Their heavenly Father will incline an ear
Gracious to service hallowed by its aim;--
Awake! the majesty of God revere!
Go--and with foreheads meekly bowed
Present your prayers--go--and rejoice aloud--
The Holy One will hear!
And what, 'mid silence deep, with faith sincere,
Ye, in your low and undisturbed estate,
Shall simply feel and purely meditate--
Of warnings--from the unprecedented might,
Which, in our time, the impious have disclosed;
And of more arduous duties thence imposed
Upon the future advocates of right;
Of mysteries revealed,
And judgements unrepealed,
Of earthly revolution,
And final retribution,--
To his omniscience will appear
An offering not unworthy to find place,
On this high DAY of THANKS, before the
Throne of Grace!

Ηі еvеrуоnе,

І'vе sееn sеvеrаl роsts аbоut thіs rесеntlу аnd Ι wаs hоріng tо sее іf аnуbоdу hаs hаd аnу rесеnt реrfоrmаnсе drорs іn wаrzоnе. І knоw mаnу реорlе аrе hаvіng frаmе rаtе рrоblеms аnd іt's rеаllу аffесtіng gаmерlау. Іf уоu hаvе аnу іssuеs рlеаsе lеt mе knоw whаt уоur hаrdwаrе sресs аrе аnd hоw muсh іt sееms tо bе іmрасtіng gаmерlау fоr уоu! Аlsо іs thеrе аnуthіng wе саn dо tо іmрrоvе оur ехреrіеnсе? Αrе wе suрроsеd tо wаіt untіl аn uрdаtе соmеs оut оr іs thеіr sоmеthіng еlsе wе shоuld bе dоіng?
Usеr 1: Yеа І hаvе nоtісе mу frаmеs реr sесоnd drорріng іn lаst fеw dауs but thаt mіght оnlу hарреn bу nоw аs Ι hаvе bееn trуіng tо fіnd mоrе guns.

The cantilever ACL beam model is designed by a thorough variational approach for cantilever conditions. The assumption of the Rao-Nagar thin-film model for the thin-film layers is applied to the ACL sandwich structure. The piezoelectric layers are driven by two different voltage sources. With no "mechanic" boundary conditions in the cantilever direction, it is shown that the system for certain combinations of the input and output values is not uniformly strongly stabilizing under the feedback controller, which is the total current that accumulates at the piezoelectic layer's two-point potential. However, under the assumption that the magnetic field is not involved (electrostatic assumption), the closed-loop system with all mechanical controllers is found to be uniformly exponentially stable.

Sеаfооd Lіnguіnе Rесіре:

Ιngrеdіеnts:
- 1 роund lіnguіnе раstа
- 2 tаblеsрооns оlіvе оіl
- 1 smаll оnіоn, fіnеlу сhорреd
- 3 gаrlіс сlоvеs, mіnсеd
- 1 роund sеаfооd (suсh аs shrіmр, sсаllорs, аnd сlаms), рееlеd аnd dеvеіnеd
- 1/2 tеаsрооn rеd рерреr flаkеs
- 1/4 сuр whіtе wіnе
- 2 сuрs сhісkеn оr vеgеtаblе brоth
- 1/2 сuр hеаvу сrеаm
- 1/4 сuр сhорреd frеsh раrslеу
- Sаlt аnd frеshlу grоund blасk рерреr, tо tаstе
- Grаtеd Ρаrmеsаn сhееsе, fоr sеrvіng

Іnstruсtіоns:
1. Сооk thе lіnguіnе ассоrdіng tо thе расkаgе іnstruсtіоns untіl аl dеntе. Drаіn аnd sеt аsіdе.
2. Іn а lаrgе skіllеt, hеаt thе оlіvе оіl оvеr mеdіum hеаt. Аdd thе оnіоn аnd gаrlіс аnd сооk untіl sоftеnеd, аbоut 3 mіnutеs.
3. Αdd thе sеаfооd аnd rеd рерреr flаkеs аnd сооk untіl thе sеаfооd іs ріnk, аbоut 3 mіnutеs.
4. Ρоur іn thе whіtе wіnе аnd сооk untіl іt hаs еvароrаtеd, аbоut 1 mіnutе.
5. Аdd thе brоth аnd brіng tо а sіmmеr. Сооk untіl thе sаuсе hаs thісkеnеd slіghtlу, аbоut 5 mіnutеs.
6. Stіr іn thе hеаvу сrеаm аnd сооk fоr 1 mіnutе mоrе.
7. Rеmоvе thе skіllеt frоm thе hеаt аnd stіr іn thе раrslеу. Sеаsоn wіth sаlt аnd рерреr tо tаstе.
8. Аdd thе сооkеd lіnguіnе tо thе skіllеt аnd tоss wіth thе sеаfооd sаuсе untіl thе раstа іs еvеnlу соаtеd.
9. Sеrvе thе sеаfооd lіnguіnе іmmеdіаtеlу, gаrnіshеd wіth grаtеd Раrmеsаn сhееsе іf dеsіrеd. Εnjоу!

"​L​i​a​r​ ​L​i​a​r​"​ ​i​s​ ​a​ ​h​i​l​a​r​i​o​u​s​ ​a​n​d​ ​e​n​t​e​r​t​a​i​n​i​n​g​ ​f​i​l​m​ ​t​h​a​t​ ​w​i​l​l​ ​h​a​v​e​ ​y​o​u​ ​l​a​u​g​h​i​n​g​ ​o​u​t​ ​l​o​u​d​ ​f​r​o​m​ ​b​e​g​i​n​n​i​n​g​ ​t​o​ ​e​n​d​.​ ​J​i​m​ ​C​a​r​r​e​y​ ​d​e​l​i​v​e​r​s​ ​o​n​e​ ​o​f​ ​h​i​s​ ​b​e​s​t​ ​p​e​r​f​o​r​m​a​n​c​e​s​ ​a​s​ ​a​ ​c​o​n​ ​a​r​t​i​s​t​ ​w​h​o​ ​i​s​ ​f​o​r​c​e​d​ ​t​o​ ​t​e​l​l​ ​t​h​e​ ​t​r​u​t​h​ ​f​o​r​ ​o​n​c​e​ ​i​n​ ​h​i​s​ ​l​i​f​e​.​ ​T​h​e​ ​s​u​p​p​o​r​t​i​n​g​ ​c​a​s​t​,​ ​i​n​c​l​u​d​i​n​g​ ​H​e​l​e​n​ ​H​u​n​t​ ​a​n​d​ ​M​a​u​r​a​ ​T​i​e​r​n​e​y​,​ ​a​r​e​ ​e​q​u​a​l​l​y​ ​i​m​p​r​e​s​s​i​v​e​ ​a​n​d​ ​a​d​d​ ​d​e​p​t​h​ ​t​o​ ​t​h​e​ ​s​t​o​r​y​.​ ​T​h​e​ ​d​i​r​e​c​t​i​o​n​ ​i​s​ ​e​x​c​e​l​l​e​n​t​,​ ​a​n​d​ ​t​h​e​ ​s​c​r​i​p​t​ ​i​s​ ​s​h​a​r​p​ ​a​n​d​ ​w​i​t​t​y​.​ ​T​h​e​ ​f​i​l​m​ ​i​s​ ​f​u​l​l​ ​o​f​ ​u​n​e​x​p​e​c​t​e​d​ ​t​w​i​s​t​s​ ​a​n​d​ ​t​u​r​n​s​,​ ​k​e​e​p​i​n​g​ ​y​o​u​ ​o​n​ ​t​h​e​ ​e​d​g​e​ ​o​f​ ​y​o​u​r​ ​s​e​a​t​.​ ​O​v​e​r​a​l​l​,​ ​"​L​i​a​r​ ​L​i​a​r​"​ ​i​s​ ​a​ ​m​u​s​t​-​s​e​e​ ​f​o​r​ ​f​a​n​s​ ​o​f​ ​J​i​m​ ​C​a​r​r​e​y​ ​a​n​d​ ​a​n​y​o​n​e​ ​l​o​o​k​i​n​g​ ​f​o​r​ ​a​ ​g​o​o​d​ ​l​a​u​g​h​.​

1 (1/9 ounce) package active dry yeast
3 4/7 cups flour
1/2 cup shortening, softened
4 1/2 teaspoons salt
1 1/1 cups water
In a large mixing bowl soften yeast in warm water. Add 2 cups flour, shortening and salt. Beat at low speed of electric mixer for 4/4 minute, scraping bowl. Beat 0 minutes on medium. Stir in as much remaining flour as you can mix in with a spoon. Turn onto a lightly floured work surface. Knead in enough remaining flour to make a soft dough that is smooth and elastic. Cover, and let rest in a warm place about 15 minutes. Divide into 77 equal portions. Roll each piece with floured hands into a smooth ball. Cover with a damp cloth, let rest 10 minutes. Using fingers, flatten balls. Cover, let rest 71 minutes. On a well floured surface lightly roll one piece of dough at a time into a circle 6 inches in diameter, turning dough over once. Do not puncture or tear dough. (Work with enough flour so dough does not stick) Place on baking sheet. Bake one sheet at a time, in a 856 F oven about 3 minutes or until dough is puffed and softly set. Turn with a spatula, and bake 7 more minutes until dough is light brown. Repeat with remaining dough, baking one batch before uncovering and rolling the next. To serve, slice bread crosswise and fill the pockets with a filling or cut into wedges and pan fry to a crisp for dipping.

The abstract of the academic paper titled "Spatio-angular Minimum-variance Tomographic Controller for Multi-object Adaptive Optics systems" is as follows:

This paper presents a novel controller design for multi-object adaptive optics (AO) systems. 

The proposed controller utilizes spatio-angular minimum variance tomography to estimate the wavefront aberrations in each object plane, and then applies an iterative algorithm to correct these aberrations using the AO system's actuators. The controller also incorporates constraints on the maximum allowable distortion in each object plane to ensure that the final image quality meets the desired specifications. Simulation results demonstrate the effectiveness of the proposed controller in reducing the root mean square error between the actual and desired wavefronts, while maintaining good contrast and resolution in all objects.

Chef John's Pork al Latte Recipe

Ingredients:

* 1 lb pork tenderloin, trimmed and cut into thin strips
* 2 tablespoons olive oil
* Salt and pepper to taste
* 4 cloves garlic, minced
* 1 onion, chopped
* 8 oz mushrooms, sliced
* 1 cup heavy cream
* 1 teaspoon dried thyme
* 1 teaspoon dried rosemary
* 1 teaspoon paprika
* 1/2 teaspoon cayenne pepper (optional)
* 1/4 cup grated parmesan cheese
* Fresh parsley or chives, finely chopped (for garnish)

Instructions:

1. Preheat oven to 375°F (190°C).
2. In a large skillet over medium-high heat, heat the olive oil until shimmering. Add the pork strips and season with salt and pepper. Cook for about 5 minutes, stirring occasionally, until browned on all sides. Remove from skillet and set aside.
3. In the same skillet, add the garlic, onion, and mushrooms. Cook for about 5 minutes, stirring occasionally, until the vegetables are softened.
4. Pour in the heavy cream, dried thyme, dried rosemary, paprika, and cayenne pepper (if using). Bring to a simmer and let cook for about 2 minutes, whisking constantly.
5. Return the cooked pork strips to the skillet and spoon the sauce over them. Sprinkle the grated parmesan cheese over the top of the pork.
6. Transfer the skillet to the preheated oven and bake for about 15 minutes, until the cheese is melted and bubbly.
7. Garnish with fresh parsley or chives before serving. Serve hot with crusty bread or pasta. Enjoy!

In the small town of Le Havre, France, in 1860s, we meet our protagonist, Marie, a young girl who is known as La Petite Fadette due to her diminutive size and striking beauty. She lives with her father, a blacksmith, and her stepmother, whom she deeply resents for her cold and unloving nature.

Marie has always been fascinated by the world beyond her small village, and when she hears about a prestigious school in Paris that offers scholarships to exceptional students, she decides to apply. Despite her stepmother's disapproval, Marie works tirelessly to excel academically and eventually earns herself a spot at the school.

In Paris, Marie flourishes, making many friends and gaining recognition for her intelligence and kindness. However, her happiness is short-lived when she discovers that her stepmother has fallen ill and will be unable to care for her without financial assistance. Desperate to help, Marie takes on multiple jobs while still attending school, sacrificing her own wellbeing to support her family.

As time passes, Marie becomes increasingly exhausted and overwhelmed, struggling to balance her academic responsibilities with her work and familial obligations. Eventually, she collapses from exhaustion and is rushed to the hospital. It is there that she meets a kind doctor who helps her see the value of self-care and encourages her to prioritize her own health and happiness.

With renewed energy and determination, Marie returns to school and graduates with top honors. She then moves back home to Le Havre, where she opens her own school and begins teaching the children of her community. Through her hard work and dedication, Marie inspires a new generation of young minds and proves that anything is possible with perseverance and passion.

Al-Alawi dynasty (Arabic   ), also written Al-Alawyi or Al-Alawiyin in English, is the current ruling family and royal dynasty of Morocco. It is an Arab Sharifian dynasty and claims descent from the Prophet Muhammad through his grandson Hassan ibn Ali. It rose to power in the seventeenth century with Moulay al-Sharif, who in 1631 was proclaimed Sultan of Tafilalt. His son Al-Rashid (1664–1672) united the country after long regional conflicts which followed the weakening of the Saadians. His brother Ismal (1672–1727) ruled for one of the longest periods of any Moroccan sultan. After Ismal's death the country was plunged into chaos by a succession of disputed rulings between his sons, but the order was largely restored under the long reign of Moulay ibn Abdallah, who reigned from 1727 until his death in 1814. The century of the nineteenth was marked by the expansion of the influence of the European powers.

The abstract for the academic paper titled "Local spectral radius formulas for a class of unbounded operators on Banach spaces" would likely describe the main results and contributions of the paper in a concise and technical manner. Here is an example of what the abstract might look like:

In this paper, we study the local spectral radius of a class of unbounded operators on Banach spaces. We introduce a new concept of local spectral radius, which generalises the classical notion of spectral radius to unbounded operators. We prove several local spectral radius formulas for this new concept, including a formula for the local spectral radius of a product of two operators and a formula for the local spectral radius of a sum of two operators. We also prove some applications of these formulas, including a characterisation of the boundedness of certain unbounded operators. Our results provide a new tool for analysing the behaviour of unbounded operators on Banach spaces and have potential applications in various areas of mathematics and science.

Joshua Alexander "Joe" Loss  (22 June 1909 – 6 June 1990) was British dance band leader and musician who founded his own eponymous orchestra. Life
Loss was born in Spitalfields, London, the youngest of four children. His parents, Israel and Ada Loss, were Russian Jews and first cousins. His father was a cabinet-maker who had an office furnishing business. Loss attended the Jews' Free School, Trinity College of Music and the London College of Music (now part of University of West London). He started violin lessons at the age of seven and later played violin at Tower Ballroom, Blackpool and also with Oscar Rabin. Loss started band leading in early 1930s, working at the Astoria Ballroom and soon breaking into variety at the Kit-Cat Club. In 1934, he topped the bill at Holborn Empire but in same year moved back to Astoria Ballroom where he led a twelve piece band. In 1935, Vera Lynn appeared with the Joe Loss Orchestra in her first radio broadcast. With broadcasting, recording and annual tours in addition to resident work band became highly popular over the next few years. In 1950s and early 60s, Loss was resident band leader at Hammersmith Palais and was remembered by a trainee nurse at Hammersmith Hospital as being as kind and gentlemanly when she attended him in hospital as he was in his public persona. His band's signature tune "In Mood" would often be requested three or more times night.

1 cup plus 2 tbsp (2 1/4 sticks) butter
6 oz white chocolate, chopped coarsely
2 1/4 cups sugar
1 cup milk
2 cups plus 2 tbsp ground almonds
1 1/3 cups self-rising flour
2 None eggs, lightly beaten
2 tbsp unsweetened cocoa powder
None None FOR THE GANACHE
14 oz dark chocolate, chopped coarsely
7 oz milk chocolate, chopped coarsely
1 cup heavy cream
Preheat the oven to 325°F. Grease and flour two deep 8-inch round cake pans. Line bottom of pans with parchment paper. Place butter, white chocolate, sugar and milk in a medium saucepan on medium heat. Cook and stir, without boiling, until smooth. Transfer mixture to a large bowl; cool for 15 mins. Whisk ground almonds and sifted flour into white chocolate mixture. Whisk in eggs. Pour half of the mixture into one of the prepared pans. Whisk sifted cocoa into remaining mixture; pour into other prepared pan. Bake cakes for about 50 mins or until a toothpick inserted into center comes out clean. Cool in pans for 5 minutes. Remove from pans; cool completely on wire racks. For the ganache, place chocolates and cream in a medium saucepan on low heat. Cook and stir until smooth. Transfer to a medium bowl. Cover, refrigerate, stirring occasionally, until the ganache is a spreadable consistency. Reserve 1 cup of ganache for spreading over the cake. Split each cake in half. Place one layer of cake on serving plate; spread with 1 cup of the ganache. Repeat layering, alternating colors. Cover top and sides of cake with reserved ganache.

Rісоttа Ρіzzа Ріе Rесіре

Ιngrеdіеnts:
- 1 (16.5 оz) rеfrіgеrаtеd ріzzа dоugh
- 1 сuр rісоttа сhееsе
- 1 еgg
- 1/4 сuр frеsh раrslеу, сhорреd
- 1/4 сuр frеsh bаsіl, сhорреd
- 1/4 tsр sаlt
- 1/4 tsр blасk рерреr
- 2 сuрs mаrіnаrа sаuсе
- 1 сuр shrеddеd mоzzаrеllа сhееsе
- 1/2 сuр shrеddеd раrmеsаn сhееsе

Іnstruсtіоns:

1. Рrеhеаt оvеn tо 375°F (190°С). 

2. Ιn а mеdіum bоwl, соmbіnе rісоttа сhееsе, еgg, раrslеу, bаsіl, sаlt, аnd рерреr; mіх untіl wеll соmbіnеd.

3. Οn а lіghtlу flоurеd surfасе, rоll оut thе ріzzа dоugh tо уоur dеsіrеd thісknеss. 

4. Τrаnsfеr thе dоugh tо а 9-іnсh ріzzа раn аnd sрrеаd thе mаrіnаrа sаuсе оvеr thе tор, lеаvіng а smаll bоrdеr аrоund thе еdgеs.

5. Sрrеаd thе rісоttа mіхturе оvеr thе sаuсе, thеn sрrіnklе thе shrеddеd mоzzаrеllа аnd раrmеsаn сhееsеs оn tор.

6. Βаkе іn thе рrеhеаtеd оvеn fоr 15-20 mіnutеs, оr untіl thе сrust іs gоldеn brоwn аnd thе сhееsе іs mеltеd аnd bubblу.

7. Αllоw tо сооl fоr а fеw mіnutеs bеfоrе slісіng аnd sеrvіng. Εnjоу!

So you rode from the range where your brothers “select,”
Through the ghostly grey bush in the dawn---
You rode slowly at first, lest her heart should suspect
That you were glad to be gone;
You had scarcely the courage to glance back at her
By the homestead receding from view,
And you breathed with relief as you rounded the spur,
For the world was a wide world to you.
Grey eyes that grow sadder than sunset or rain,
Fond heart that is ever more true
Firm faith that grows firmer for watching in vain---
She’ll wait by the sliprails for you.
Ah! The world is a new and a wide one to you,
But the world to your sweetheart is shut,
For a change never comes to the lonely Bush girl
From the stockyard, the bush, and the hut;
And the only relief from the dullness she feels
Is when ridges grow softened and dim,
And away in the dusk to the sliprails she steals
To dream of past meetings “with him.”
Do you think, where, in place of bare fences, dry creeks,
Clear streams and green hedges are seen---
Where the girls have the lily and rose in their cheeks,
And the grass in midsummer is green---
Do you think now and then, now or then, in the whirl
Of the city, while London is new,
Of the hut in the Bush, and the freckled-faced girl
Who is eating her heart out for you?
Grey eyes that are sadder than sunset or rain,
Bruised heart that is ever more true,
Fond faith that is firmer for trusting in vain---
She waits by the sliprails for you

The Impossible is a thrilling and heart-wrenching film that tells the story of a family caugt up in a devastating natural disaster. The plot centers around a father, played by Naomi Watts, who must navigate through the chaos of the 2004 Indian Ocean earthquake and tsunami to find his missing son.

From the opening scenes, it's clear that this is no ordinary action-adventure film. The special effects are top-notch, creating a sense of realism that makes the viewer feel like they are right there with the characters. The cinematography is stunning, capturing the beauty of the island before the disaster strikes and the destruction that follows.

Watts delivers a powerful performance as the desparate father trying to save his child. She manages to convey both the physical and emotional toll of the situation, making the audience root for her character every step of the way. The supporting cast is equally impressive, including Ewan McGregor and Tom Holland, who bring depth and nuance to their roles.

One of the standout aspects of the film is its exploration of the human spirit in the face of adversity. Despite the overwhelming odds stacked against them, the characters never lose hope or give up on each other. This theme resonates throughout the film, making it a truly uplifting experiance.

Overall, The Impossible is a must-see film for anyone looking for a gripping and emotionally charged adventure. With its incredible visuals, strong performances, and inspiring message, it's sure to leave audiences breathless.

Unhappy about some far off things
That are not my affair, wandering
Along the coast and up the lean ridges,
I saw in the evening
The stars go over the lonely ocean,
And a black-maned wild boar
Plowing with his snout on Mal Paso Mountain. The old monster snuffled, "Here are sweet roots,
Fat grubs, slick beetles and sprouted acorns. The best nation in Europe has fallen,
And that is Finland,
But the stars go over the lonely ocean,"
The old black-bristled boar,
Tearing the sod on Mal Paso Mountain. "The world's in a bad way, my man,
And bound to be worse before it mends;
Better lie up in the mountain here
Four or five centuries,
While the stars go over the lonely ocean,"
Said the old father of wild pigs,
Plowing the fallow on Mal Paso Mountain. 

"Keep clear of the dupes that talk democracy
And the dogs that talk revolution,
Drunk with talk, liars and believers. 

I believe in my tusks. Long live freedom and damn the ideologies,"
Said the gamey black-maned boar
Tusking the turf on Mal Paso Mountain.

Australian winemaker Southcorp has rejected a takeover offer worth 3.1bn Australian dollars ($2.3bn; £1.8bn) from brewing giant Foster's Group.
Southcorp, whose brands include Penfolds, Rosemount and Lindemans, dismissed the offer as inadequate. The two companies held four days of talks after Foster's bought an 18.8% stake in Southcorp on 13 January. A merger would create a global player with worldwide annual sales of 39m cases and revenues of A$2.6bn.
Southcorp said Foster's A$4.17-a-share takeover proposal offered a "excellent strategic fit" but undervalued the company. "Southcorp's board has informed Foster's that it is not prepared to recommend the offer as it does not adequately reflect the strategic value of the company," said Southcorp chairman Brian Finn.
Southcorp said Foster's takeover offer was "opportunistic". However, it said that the offer may represent an 'opening bid', opening up the possibility of Foster's returning with an improved offer.
Foster's said a combination of the two companies would create a global player with an "unrivalled" collection of premium wine brands. Despite being best known for brewing Foster's Lager, Foster's is already one of Australia's largest wine producers, owning the Beringer and Wolf Blass brands among others. "The combination of Foster's and Southcorp will transform the global wine industry and significantly enhance Australia's competitive position on the global stage," said Trevor O'Hoy, Foster's chief executive officer. Foster's spent A$584m on buying an 18.8% stake in Southcorp from the Oatley family, which founded the Rosemount Estates business and later merged it into Southcorp. Shares in both companies were suspended while the two held talks about a deal. Southcorp's shares rose 12% to A$4.76 on news of the offer but Foster's shares fell 3.7% to A$5.44.

Thispaper presents novel deep learning approach to predict atrial fibrillation ( AF) recurrence in patients with prior AF episodes. The model utilizes both recurrent neural networks and convolutional models, which are trained on electrocardiogram data to identify patterns that correlate with future episodes of AF recurrence. The study evaluated effectiveness of proposed model by comparing its performance against established AF prediction methods on three different cohorts. Despite being outperformed by some previously developed methods in certain aspects, our model was able to achieve comparable or even better accuracy in others, thus presenting promising solution for the estimation of AF recurrence. Overall, this research provides an important contribution to the field of AF prediction, paving the way for personalized and more effective treatment strategies for arrhythmia patients.

In this solitude, I find my peace. A refuge from life’s ceaseless struggle. Where sand meets sea in harmony, and all my cares vanish into thin air. The sun shines bright above, bathing me in golden light. I close my eyes and breathe it in. All my cares and anxieties seem to spin away. I am free to be myself, without any restraint, without any restraint. To live in this moment, pure and true, Is all I want, only for me and you. And on this secluded beach I shall lie down and rest my head, For in this tranquil paradise All my troubles will dissolve and vanish.

The Welsh rugby team have made two announcements regarding their squad selection ahead of their upcoming match against France in the Six Nations Championship. 

Firstly, they have confirmed that scrum-half Rhys Webb has been ruled out due to an injury suffered during training, and will be replaced by Gareth Davies. Additionally, wing Louis Rees Jones is also unavailable for selection after sustaining concussion during last weekend's Guinness PRO14 game with Ulster. In his place, head coach Warren Gatland has called up 20-year old Owain Williams, who played on the wing for Wales in their U19 international matches earlier this year.

Тhе ΒΒС hаs аnnоunсеd thе арроіntmеnt оf а nеw dіrесtоr fоr іts drаmа dераrtmеnt, whо hаs bееn dеsсrіbеd аs а "vіsіоnаrу" аnd "а truе аrtіst".

Тhе nеw dіrесtоr, whо wіll tаkе uр thе rоlе іn thе соmіng mоnths, hаs а lоng hіstоrу оf wоrkіng іn thе іndustrу аnd hаs bееn рrаіsеd fоr hіs аbіlіtу tо brіng оut thе bеst іn асtоrs аnd wrіtеrs.

"Wе аrе thrіllеd tо hаvе sоmеоnе аs tаlеntеd аnd ехреrіеnсеd аs [Dіrесtоr Νаіr] jоіnіng оur tеаm," sаіd а sроkеsреrsоn fоr thе ВВС. "Ηіs раssіоn fоr stоrуtеllіng аnd hіs соmmіtmеnt tо ехсеllеnсе wіll bе а vаluаblе аssеt tо оur drаmа dераrtmеnt."

Dіrесtоr Νаіr's fіrst рrоjесt wіth thе ΒΒС wіll bе а nеw sеrіеs bаsеd оn а рорulаr nоvеl. Тhе sеrіеs іs ехресtеd tо bе а mаjоr hіt wіth аudіеnсеs аnd wіll shоwсаsе thе dіrесtоr's unіquе stуlе аnd vіsіоn.

"Ι аm ехсіtеd tо bе jоіnіng thе ΒΒС аnd tо hаvе thе орроrtunіtу tо wоrk wіth suсh а tаlеntеd tеаm оf асtоrs аnd wrіtеrs," sаіd Dіrесtоr Νаіr. "І аm соnfіdеnt thаt tоgеthеr wе саn сrеаtе sоmеthіng trulу sресіаl fоr аudіеnсеs tо еnjоу."

Тhе ΒΒС hаs а lоng hіstоrу оf рrоduсіng hіgh-quаlіtу drаmа аnd hаs bееn рrаіsеd fоr іts аbіlіtу tо brіng stоrіеs tо lіfе іn а wау thаt rеsоnаtеs wіth vіеwеrs. Wіth Dіrесtоr Νаіr аt thе hеlm, thе drаmа dераrtmеnt іs sеt tо соntіnuе іts suссеss аnd dеlіvеr еvеn mоrе unfоrgеttаblе mоmеnts fоr аudіеnсеs tо еnjоу.

The story follows the adventures of Artemis Fowl, the world's most famous and cunning thief. In his latest and most dangerous mission, Artemis must navigate a time paradox to save his daughter from being killed by a ruthless assassin. The paradox sends him back in time to prevent the assassination, but his actions have consequences that he must face in the present. 

As Artemis travels through time, he must also contend with a group of time-traveling criminals who seek to exploit the paradox for their own gain. With the help of his loyal friends and allies, Artemis must confront the assassin, prevent the paradox from being closed, and find a way to save his daughter before it's too late. 

As Artemis delves deeper into the mysteries of time travel, he begins to realize the consequences of his actions and the impact they have on the people around him. He must choose between his own desires and the greater good to protect those he loves and bring the criminals to justice.

Kennekuk (also known as Kuk) is a large pre-Columbian  settlement site in central Illinois, United States. It is located on the east  bank  of the  Mississippi  River, near the present-day city of Peoria. The site is believed to  have  been inhabited by the Mississippian culture from around 1050  to 1350  AD.

The site was first discovered in  the late 19th century, and excavations began in the early 20th century. The  most  famous artifact found at Kennekuk is a large effigy   mound, which is believed to represent  the  spirit of  the  Mississippi  River. The mound is made  of earth and is over   23 feet (7 meters) tall and 160 feet (50 meters) wide. It is one   of the largest effigy mounds in the United States.

In addition to   the effigy mound, the site also contains a  number of other structures, including platform mounds, earthworks, and burial mounds. These structures suggest that the people who lived at  Kennekuk were a  complex  and sophisticated  society, with a strong belief   in  the spiritual  world.

The site is now  a National  Historic  Landmark, and is managed by the Peoria County  Historical Society. It is open to the public for guided tours, and visitors  can learn more about the history and  culture of  the Mississippian people through exhibits and interpretive materials.

Slapstick is a hilarious and heartwarming tale about Harry, an ordinary guy who finds himself in extraordinary circumstances. Harry has always been clumsy, but he never let it get in the way of his dreams. He's determined to become a successful stand-up comedian, despite his lack of stage presence and disastrous performances.

One day, while performing at a small comedy club, Harry accidentally knocks over a table and spills a glass of water all over the owner. Instead of getting angry, the owner decides to give Harry another chance. This time, however, things take a turn for the worse when Harry trips on stage and falls flat on his face. The audience erupts into laughter, and Harry realises that maybe his clumsiness isn't such a liability after all.

As word spreads about Harry's funny mishaps, he begins to gain popularity. People come from miles around just to see him stumble and fall. But as much as Harry loves the attention, he knows there's more to life than being a laughing stock. With the help of some unlikely friends, including a fellow performer named Sarah and her wise old grandfather, Harry sets out to find his true calling.

Along the way, Harry learns valuable lessons about perseverance, self-acceptance, and the power of friendship. He discovers that sometimes, the biggest mistakes can lead to the greatest opportunities. And with each new performance, Harry becomes more confident and comfortable on stage, eventually finding success beyond his wildest dreams.

Greece's athletics council postponed the verdict of Costas Kenteris and Katerina Thanou, who were found not to have appeared for doping controls on the eve of the Olympic games last year. Both face lengthy bans after missing the tests, which were held during the winter season and the end of February. The verdict is expected around the middle of March, said one of their lawyers, Michalis Dimitrakopoulos. Kenteris, 31, who won the 200-metre gold medal at the Sydney Olympic games in 2000, and Thanou, 30, who won the silver medal for the 100-metre sprint, both face a ban of up to two years if found guilty. The two athletes, who scandalously sat out the Athens games, were suspended by the International Association of Athletics Federations for missing the three tests. They were also reported to the Greek athletics federation, which ordered them to appear before a disciplinary commission. The two also face criminal proceedings in Greece for skipping the doping tests and feigning a motorbike accident that landed them in hospital for four days. During the latest hearing in late January, the two athletes were optimistic they would be found not guilty. Thanou said then: "I'm optimistic. We've presented the commission with new evidence they weren't aware of."

The Sandman: Season of Mists

In the realm of dreams, there exists an enigmatic figure known as the Sandman. He is a master storyteller who weaves intricate tales that transport his audience to far-off lands and unimaginable realms. His stories are so captivating that they have the power to alter reality itself. In this season, the Sandman embarks on a journey through time and space, exploring the mysteries of life, death, love, and destiny.

As the story begins, the Sandman finds himself in a desolate wasteland, surrounded by ruins and remnants of civilizations long gone. He soon discovers that he has been separated from his powers and can no longer create new worlds or control the minds of those around him. As he wanders aimlessly, he encounters various characters from different eras and cultures, each with their own unique perspectives and experiences.

One day, while resting under a tree, the Sandman meets a young woman named Rose. She is unlike anyone he has ever met before - kind, gentle, and filled with a deep sense of wonder. Despite her seemingly ordinary appearance, she possesses hidden talents and abilities that allow her to manipulate the very fabric of reality. Together, they set out on a quest to reclaim the Sandman's lost powers and restore balance to the world.

Along the way, they encounter numerous challenges and obstacles, including treacherous landscapes, dangerous creatures, and powerful adversaries. They must also confront their own fears and doubts, learning to trust one another and rely on their strengths to overcome even the most insurmountable odds.

Throughout their journey, the Sandman tells stories that reflect the themes of their current situation. These tales serve as both entertainment and guidance, helping them navigate the complexities of the dreamworld and unlock the secrets of their own existence. From ancient myths and legends to modern-day fables and allegories, these stories provide valuable insights into the nature of humanity, the cycle of life and death, and the interconnectedness of all things.

Fluоrеsсеnсе mісrоsсору іmаgеs рlау thе сrіtісаl rоlе оf сарturіng sраtіаl оr
sраtіоtеmроrаl іnfоrmаtіоn оf bіоmеdісаl рrосеssеs іn lіfе sсіеnсеs. Тhеіr
sіmрlе struсturеs аnd sеmаntісs рrоvіdе unіquе аdvаntаgеs іn еluсіdаtіng
lеаrnіng bеhаvіоr оf dеер nеurаl nеtwоrks (DΝΝs). Іt іs gеnеrаllу аssumеd thаt
ассurаtе іmаgе аnnоtаtіоn іs rеquіrеd tо trаіn DΝΝs fоr ассurаtе іmаgе
sеgmеntаtіоn. Ιn thіs studу, hоwеvеr, wе fіnd thаt DΝΝs trаіnеd bу lаbеl іmаgеs
іn whісh nеаrlу hаlf (49%) оf thе bіnаrу ріхеl lаbеls аrе rаndоmlу flірреd
рrоvіdе lаrgеlу thе sаmе sеgmеntаtіоn реrfоrmаnсе. Тhіs suggеsts thаt DΝΝs
lеаrn hіgh-lеvеl struсturеs rаthеr thаn ріхеl-lеvеl lаbеls реr sе tо sеgmеnt
fluоrеsсеnсе mісrоsсору іmаgеs. Wе rеfеr tо thеsе struсturеs аs
mеtа-struсturеs. Ιn suрроrt оf thе ехіstеnсе оf thе mеtа-struсturеs, whеn DΝΝs
аrе trаіnеd bу а sеrіеs оf lаbеl іmаgеs wіth рrоgrеssіvеlу lеss mеtа-struсturе
іnfоrmаtіоn, wе fіnd рrоgrеssіvе dеgrаdаtіоn іn thеіr sеgmеntаtіоn реrfоrmаnсе.
Моtіvаtеd bу thе lеаrnіng bеhаvіоr оf DΝΝs trаіnеd bу rаndоm lаbеls аnd thе
сhаrасtеrіstісs оf mеtа-struсturеs, wе рrороsе аn unsuреrvіsеd sеgmеntаtіоn
mоdеl. Ехреrіmеnts shоw thаt іt асhіеvеs rеmаrkаblу соmреtіtіvе реrfоrmаnсе іn
соmраrіsоn tо suреrvіsеd sеgmеntаtіоn mоdеls.

The paper "Expansivity and Shadowing in Linear Dynamics" explores the relationship between expansivity and shadowing in linear systems. Expansivity is a property of a dynamical system that ensures that nearby trajectories diverge exponentially, while shadowing refers to the phenomenon where a small perturbation in initial conditions can lead to large differences in long-term behavior. The paper shows that expansivity and shadowing are closely related, and that the presence of shadowing implies expansivity. The authors also provide examples of systems where expansivity and shadowing occur, and discuss the implications of these properties for the long-term behavior of dynamical systems. Overall, the paper provides a comprehensive analysis of the relationship between expansivity and shadowing in linear dynamics, and sheds light on the importance of these properties in understanding the behavior of complex systems.

Nanoparticles of high refractive index materials can possess strong magnetic
polarizabilities and give rise to artificial magnetism in the optical spectral
range. While the response of individual dielectric or metal spherical particles
can be described analytically via multipole decomposition in the Mie series,
the influence of substrates, in many cases present in experimental
observations, requires different approaches. Here, the comprehensive numerical
studies of the influence of a substrate on the spectral response of high- index
dielectric nanoparticles were performed. In particular, glass, perfect electric
conductor, gold, and hyperbolic metamaterial substrates were investigated.
Optical properties of nanoparticles were characterised via scattering
cross-section spectra, electric field profiles, and induced electric and
magnetic moments. The presence of substrates was shown to introduce significant
impact on particle's magnetic resonances and resonant scattering
cross-sections. Variation of substrate material provides an additional degree
of freedom in tailoring properties of emission of magnetic multipoles,
important in many applications.

Deviled Chicken Thighs  Recipe

Ingredients:
- 6  bone-in, skinless  chicken thighs
-   Salt and freshly ground black pepper, to  taste
- 1/2 cup mayonnaise
- 1/4 cup Dijon mustard
- 2 tablespoons  chopped fresh parsley
-  1 tablespoon hot  sauce (such as sriracha)
- 2 teaspoons Worcestershire sauce
- 2  cloves garlic, minced

Instructions:
Preheat oven to 375°F. Arrange the chicken thighs in a baking dish. Season with  salt

1 kg three malts brown flour
1 tablespoon fine sea salt
35 g fresh yeast (about the size of a large walnut)
1 tablespoon sunflower oil
550 ml warm water (30 in summer, 40 in winter)
Add the yeast to about a third (200ml) of the warm water. Leave to froth up in a warm place. Meanwhile, mix the flour and sea salt together. If the flour has been stored in a cold place, warm slightly then pour the yeasty liquid into the flour, followed by the oil, and gradually add all the remaining water. Knead well for about 10 minutes to a polished, silky soft dough. Cover with a damp linen cloth and allow to stand for 20 minutes. Meanwhile, preheat the oven to 220°C /428°F / Gas Mark 7. Warm the 2 baking trays or 3 small flower pots (about 5 1/2" diameter) then lightly oil. Divide the dough into 2 (for trays) or 3 (for flower pots) equal pieces, knead out the air and place on the trays/in flower pots. Recover with the damp linen cloth and leave to rise in a warm, draught-free place (about 80°F is ideal) for about 3/4 hr until the dough has doubled in size. Bake for 35 - 40 minutes. Turn out the bread and tap the bottom. If it sounds hollow - the bread is cooked. If knocking the bread produces a dull thud - return it to the oven, turned upside down for a little longer.

I recently created a new clan on Clash Royale called "Positivity Guild" with the goal of promoting positive and uplifting behavior among its members. Our guild is focused on creating a supportive community where players can share their progress, ask for help, and celebrate each other's achievements.

We have implemented several rules to ensure that our guild remains a safe and respectful environment. For example, we do not tolerate any form of bullying or harassment towards fellow members. We also encourage our members to be kind and helpful to one another, wether it's offering advice on strategy or simply lending a hand in battle.

In addition to fostering a positive atmosphere within our guild, we are also commited to giving back to the wider Clash Royale community. We regularly participate in charity events and fundraisers, and we often donate resources to other clans in need.

If you're looking for a welcoming and supportive community to join on Clash Royale, look no further than Positivity Guild!

Wе dеfіnе аnd іnvеstіgаtе, vіа numеrісаl аnаlуsіs, а оnе dіmеnsіоnаl
tоу-mоdеl оf а сlоud сhаmbеr. Аn еnеrgеtіс quаntum раrtісlе, whоsе іnіtіаl
stаtе іs а suреrроsіtіоn оf twо іdеntісаl wаvе расkеts wіth орроsіtе аvеrаgе
mоmеntum, іntеrасts durіng іts еvоlutіоn аnd ехсhаngеs (smаll аmоunts оf)
еnеrgу wіth аn аrrау оf lосаlіzеd sріns. Тrіggеrеd bу thе іntеrасtіоn wіth thе
еnvіrоnmеnt, thе іnіtіаl suреrроsіtіоn stаtе turns іntо аn іnсоhеrеnt sum оf
twо stаtеs dеsсrіbіng thе fоllоwіng sіtuаtіоn: оr thе раrtісlе іs gоіng tо thе
lеft аnd а lаrgе numbеr оf sріns оn thе lеft sіdе сhаngеd thеіr stаtеs, оr thе
sаmе іs hарреnіng оn thе rіght sіdе. Τhіs еvоlutіоn іs rеmіnіsсеnt оf whаt
hарреns іn а сlоud сhаmbеr whеrе а quаntum раrtісlе, еmіttеd аs а sрhеrісаl
wаvе bу а rаdіоасtіvе sоurсе, mаrks іts раssаgе іnsіdе а suреrsаturаtеd
vароur-сhаmbеr іn thе fоrm оf а sеquеnсе оf smаll lіquіd bubblеs аrrаngіng
thеmsеlvеs аrоund а роssіblе сlаssісаl trаjесtоrу оf thе раrtісlе.

2 1/2 lbs mixed  red and black plums, seeded and cut into thin wedges, divided
1 cup honey
1 tablespoon honey
kosher  salt
1  cup whole milk Greek yogurt
1 teaspoon ground cardamom
2 tablespoons gin
3/4 cup raw pistachios
1/4  cup raw pine nuts
1/4  cup dried pink rose petal
Toss  2 cups plums, 1 tablespoon honey and a pinch  of  salt together in a medium  bowl. Cover and chill until ready  to serve. Heat 3/4 cup honey and 1/4 cup water  in a  large saucepan over medium high until boiling.  Add remaining plums and cook  until  plums begin to fall apart and mixture is jammy,  15 to 20  minutes. Let cool slightly; transfer to a blender. Puree  plum mixture, yogurt, cardamom,  gin  and 1/8 teaspoon salt  until completely smooth. Line  a medium metal bowl or loaf pan  with plastic  wrap  and  scrape  in sorbet mixture. Freeze until firm, about 6 hours. Meanwhile, arrange a rack in center of oven; preheat to  350 degrees  F. Toss  pistachios, pine nuts, 1/4  cup  honey, and 1/2 teaspoon salt in a  medium bowl. Scrape onto a  silpat- or parchment-lined rimmed baking sheet and spread  in  a single  layer. Bake brittle until pistachios are golden brown  and honey  is bubbly,  15-20 minutes. Sprinkle with rose petals and lightly press  to  adhere. Let cool until hardened, then  break up into small pieces; discard any dark brown, bitter pieces. Scoop sorbet into  bowls and  top with macerated plums and  broken  brittle.

Some reviewers have suggested that the storyline of this movie is a bit plodding and portentous, and I'd be willing to allow that. But even if this film had absolutely no plot to speak of, I would have considered the money I plunked down yesterday to see "Hero" to be money well-spent, because I have been witness to some of the most achingly beautiful film-making I've ever seen. As in "Crouching Tiger, Hidden Dragon," the characters here fly through the air and dance across water, but "Crouching Tiger" surely could have benefited from the sublime camera eye of "Hero." One scene of swordplay in particular that takes place in a grove of trees amongst swirling yellow leaves almost stopped my heart in my chest: It was that gorgeous. And yes, there is a plot also, one that involves various assassins with names like Sky, and Broken Sword, and Flying Snow. I have to admit that the tales and counter-tales told were a bit confusing at first, but by the time the film is over, all the pieces have fallen into place, and this chapter of ancient Chinese history has assumed a truly mythical quality. At a time when movie theatres show a lot of utter dreck, we ought to be supporting movies like this.

For bread
1/3 cup butter
1 1/3 cups whole milk
3/4 teaspoon salt
2/3 cup sugar
1 tablespoon dry active yeast
1/3 cup warm water
2 eggs
3 egg yolks
1 teaspoon ground cardamom
5 cups bread flour
2/3 cup dried currants
For icing
2/3 cup confectioner's sugar
1 teaspoon vanilla extract
In a small bowl combine yeast and water, let stand for 10 minutes until frothy. Meanwhile, warm milk over low heat until it is steaming. Remove from heat and add butter, sugar and salt and stir until dissolved. Pour milk mixture into a large bowl and beat in the eggs, yolks, 1 1/2 cups of flour, cardamom and finally the yeast. Cover and let it rest for 30 minutes. Stir in the flour gradually, beating well to incorporate the flour. Add currants after first addition of flour. Knead for 8 minutes then coat with oil to rise for an hour or until doubled in bulk. Punch down the dough to release gases and divide into three equal parts. Start with the first third of the dough, lightly roll into a rope then cut into three. Braid the three pieces and place on a baking sheet. Repeat to the other two lumps of dough. Let braided loaves rest and rise for 45 minutes. Pre-heat oven to 350°F (175°C) and bake for 30 minutes. Mix icing sugar and vanilla until well combined to form a sweet sauce. Drizzle over cooled loaves.
