Sicilian Casserole (Oamc) is a popular Italian dish that combines hearty ingredients and bold flavours. Here's an easy recipe to make this delicious casserole in your own kitchen:

Ingredients:

* 3 large eggplants, sliced into thin rounds
* 2 cups crumbled bread (preferably Italian or French bread)
* 1 cup grated Parmigiano-Reggiano cheese
* 4 tablespoons olive oil
* Salt and pepper to taste
* 1 onion, chopped
* 5 cloves garlic, minced
* 2 cans diced tomatoes with juices
* 1 cup dried oregano flakes
* 1 teaspoon red pepper flakes
* 2 pounds ground beef
* Fresh basil leaves, chopped (optional)

Instructions:

1. Preheat the oven to 375°F (190°C). Grease an 11x7 inch baking pan with cooking spray or butter. Spread the breadcrumbs at the bottom of the pan evenly. If any are not covering the entyre surface, sprinkle a little more on top of them. Add some of the grated Parmesan cheese on top of the breadcrumb layer. Set aside.

Empty Rooms (also known as "Vacant Rooms") is a 1992 American documentary film directed by the American filmmaker Larry Clark. The film consists of series of interconnected vignettes that depict various characters and their experiences with drug use, addiction, and substance disorders. It is considered one of the most controversial and influential documentary films of the 1990s, as it features graphic scenes of drug use and graphic depictions of drug abuse. The film has been criticized and praised for its realism and brutal honesty, and has been credited with helping to de-glamorize the use of drugs in popular culture. It has also been accused of contributing to normalization of drug use and addiction, and has been used as tool in drug prevention and education efforts. Despite its controversial subject matter, Empty Rooms remains powerful and moving film that continues to resonate with audiences to this day.

The next generation of video games consoles are in development but what will the new machines mean for games firms and consumers? We may not know when they will be released, what they will be called or even what they will be able to do but one thing is certain - they are coming. Sony, Microsoft and Nintendo are all expected to release new machines in the next 88 months. The details of PlayStation 3, Xbox 2 (codename Xenon) and Nintendo's so-called Revolution are still to be finalised but developers are having to work on titles for the new machines regardless.
"We know maybe what the PS3 will do, but we can only guess," said Rory Armes, studio general manager for video game giant Electronic Arts in Europe. "It's a horrendous effort in the first year," he admitted. Microsoft had delivered development kits to EA, said Mr Armes, but he said the company was still waiting on Sony and Nintendo to send kits. Although the details may not be nailed down, Mr Armes said EA was beginning to get a sense of the capabilities of the new machines. "The rumours are that PlayStation 3 will have a little more under the hood [than Xbox 2]," he said.
"Microsoft is obviously a software company first and foremost, while Sony has more experience in hardware. I think Sony will be able to push more into a box at cost."
What is certain is that the new machines will provide great leaps in processing and graphical power. It is also likely that they will contain convergence technologies to make the machines more of an entertainment hub. At the Consumer Electronics Show in Las Vegas Microsoft boss Bill Gates said Xbox 7 would be part of his vision of the digital lifestyle. While short on detail, he painted a picture of a machine that would complement a PC and offer "great video gaming but video gaming for a broader set of people, more communications, more media, more connectivity". Sony is thought to have a similar vision for PS2 while Nintendo remain focused on a machine purely for games.
Until it is clear precisely what the new machines can do, developers are working on a first round of titles to harness the new horsepower. Gary Dunn, development director at Codemasters, said the company had a central technology group which was at the forefront of preparing for the next generation of consoles. "We are working on new libraries of effects. A lot of the major techniques are already out there and in use in PC gaming, such as pixel shaders and normal mapping."
Mr Dunn said he expected the introduction of real-world physics to be a major part of the new consoles. "We want to increase that level of immersion and realism in gaming to people can lose themselves in a game." In the first year at least, developers said gamers should not expect games which harness the full potential of the machines. Graphical spit and polish and better physics in line with the capabilities of current high-end graphics cards for PCs should be expected. Simon Gardner, president of Climax's Action Studio, said: "It's definitely an exciting time. We want to give more freedom to the player. We want to give players an emotional connection to the characters they play. "The environments will be much more believable and dramatic, growing and changing as you play. "There will be a breadth of effects, more involving worlds to play in.
"It's a bit like being an artist and being given a bigger canvas and a smaller brush. We're being given more tools. "For the average consumer, we can get things of a more filmic quality." Gerhard Florin, head of EA in Europe, said gamers should expect titles that blur the line between films and video games. Many will be sceptical - gamers were given similar predictions during the last transition of console hardware - but this time it would seem to be more likely. "PS1 will provide graphics indistinguishable from movies," said Mr Florin. He said the distribution method for games would also change radically in the next round of consoles. "A gamer could buy a starter disc for 40 euros. When he goes home he goes online and he could buy AI and levels as you go.
"It's much smarter if you can get levels as you go." Mr Armes warned that developers still had to learn how to tell stories effectively in the medium. "In some ways we are trying to forget about the hardware, go in the opposite direction. We have been very bad at letting technology design our creativity. "What we have to do as a company is start ignoring the technology and learning our craft in telling stories." Mr Gardner agreed: "We can thrown more polygons around and have better AI but if it doesn't make for a better game then that's not very useful." Developers will certainly have the tools with the new machines, but how they employ them is still to be decided.

Ingredients:
- 1 cup all-purpose flour
- 2 eggs
- 1/2 cup milk
- 1/2 cup water
- 1/4 teaspoon salt
- 2 tablespoons melted butter
- 1 cup fresh strawberries, hulled and sliced
- 1/4 cup granulated sugar
- 1 tablespoon cornstarch
- 1/2 cup water
- 1 teaspoon vanilla extract

Instructions:

1. Make the batter for the crepes. In a large mixing bowl, whisk together the flour and the eggs. Gradually add in the milk, water, salt and melted butter, stirring to combine until you have a smooth batter. Let the batter rest for at least 30 minutes before cooking.

2. Make the strawberry sauce. In a saucepan, combine the sliced strawberries, granulated sugar and cornstarch. Cook over medium heat, stirring frequently, until the sugar has dissolved and the mixture has thickened, about 10 minutes. Stir in the vanilla extract.

3. Cook the crepes. Preheat a non-stick pan over medium heat. Use a ladle or a measuring cup to pour small amounts of batter onto the pan, swirling the pan to spread the batter evenly. Cook the crepe for 1-2 minutes until the bottom is lightly golden, then flip and cook for an additional 1-2 minutes on the other side. Repeat with the remaining batter, stacking the cooked crepes between layers of parchment paper.

4. Serve the crepes with strawberry sauce. Spoon the strawberry sauce over the crepes, folding them in half to enclose the filling. Serve immediately and enjoy!

Bill Conti's job of musical director of the Oscars show is not for the faint-hearted since conducting the orchestra is the ultimate plate-spinning assignment.
This year marks Conti's 17th turn at the helm of the Academy Awards orchestra. "The excitement is a live performance as a musician," he says. "All of your colleagues, everyone in the film industry is in the theatre. All the important people. "I guess it's just a television show but we always think it's a bit more.' The orchestra strikes up more than 110 times during the average Oscars show. As well as playing the presenters and winners on and off stage, it also performs during the commercial breaks to entertain the live audience in the house.
"It's a very busy evening, it takes a certain amount of preparation of music, orchestrating, sketching rehearsing, before the show. But you don't know what the unexpected will be and as the musical director, it's exciting," explains Conti. And much of the preparation goes into mastering scores that will never be performed. "We don't know who the winners will be. So when they say, 'the winner is', we have five different pieces of music in front of us, they say the name, we play the appropriate one immediately," adds Conti.
The orchestra is often called upon to play when the winners ramble on too long during their acceptance speeches, despite being told to be brief by the show's producers.
The decision to drown out or 'kill' the offending star with music is relayed from the director's box to the orchestra via Conti's earpiece. "I don't feel good about it at all. It's not my call though," he says. "When the director says 'music' the orchestra plays and he takes a long shot of the hall. We don't usually see the person speaking while his microphone is cut off. "The person that's been cut off, who's 10 feet from me, is glaring at me like it's death time." On occasions, some stars have taken it upon themselves to send a warning shot across the bows of the musical director before they start to speak. "Julia Roberts, when she came on, she asked me to not get ready to play because she had so much to do," he says. Conti received an Oscar in 1983 for the original score of The Right Stuff. He also received two nominations in the original song category in 1976 for Gonna Fly Now from Rocky and in 1981 for the title song from For Your Eyes Only.
"It's a moment in the sun," he says. "When people get up there, some people, this is hard to believe, people that spend their lives in front of the camera or audiences entertaining, might get a little flustreed. "But there's this moment in time when the biggest award that they could possibly get in their life has been handed to them and they're either not in control or they want to thank everyone that they ever met."
The image of the Oscars telecast is one of slick presentation and smooth transitions between performances and the award categories. Behind the scenes, the key players, such as Conti, have a bewildering array of technical props to deal with. "There's a big script and video monitors you have to watch and there are also audio controls. I can control hearing in my ears, the left ear or the right ear - different things at different times.
"I have to communicate with the director. I have a little microphone that's attached to my headset. To open up that microphone, I have a footswitch," he explains. Conti's most nerve-wracking moment came during his first Oscars show as musical director, in 1977, when a member of the orchestra alerted him that they could smell smoke. Conti immediately told the show's director: "I start cursing and screaming, I tell him we're not going to die for this show - you must do something or I'm going to climb out of this pit with 60 musicians and we're going to be home in 10 minutes. "'Oh no Bill don't do anything," came the response. "Imagine these elegantly dressed ladies and gentlemen of the orchestra, 60 or so of them in the pit and while we're playing, firemen with their helmets and their hatchets crawling among us trying to find the reason for the smoke." The smell turned out to be new paint smouldering on the music stands. "No one died, it's not a big story, but it was scary at the time," laughs Conti. When the ceremony is over, the music director attends the annual Governor's Ball with his family. "I have a drink and relive the show," he says. "I'm not for too much carousing. I go home and unwind. It takes me about a day."

China had a role in the split-up of Yukos, the Russian oil giant that was once one of the world's largest oil and gas producers. According to reports, China provided financial support to Yukos during its bankruptcy proceedings, which ultimately led to its breakup and the sale of its assets.

The details of China's involvement in Yukos's downfall are not entirely clear, but it is believed that China provided loans and other forms of financial assistance to the company during its time of financial trouble. This support is thought to have helped keep Yukos afloat long enough to sell off its assets, which were eventually acquired by state-owned companies such as Rosneft and Gazprom.

The role of China in Yukos's downfall has been a subject of contraversy, with some critics accusing China of using its economic power to gain control of Russia's oil and gas resources. However, others have defended China's actions, arguing that it was simply providing financial assistance to a struggling company.

Regardless of the motivations behind China's involvement in Yukos's split-up, the incident has had significant implications for the global oil and gas market. Yukos was once one of the world's largest oil and gas producers, and its breakup has led to a reshuffling of power in the industry.

In the years since the breakup, Russia has become increasingly reliant on state-owned companies such as Rosneft and Gazprom to manage its oil and gas resources. This has raised concerns about the potential for state control over the industry, as well as the potential for conflicts of interest between the government and private companies.

Overall, the role of China in Yukos's downfall is a complex and controversial issue, with implications for the global oil and gas market and the balance of power in Russia. As the industry continues to evolve, it will be important to monitor developments closely and consider the potential implications for both businesses and governments.

12 tablespoon unsalted butter, for greasing the baking sheet
3 tablespoons all-purpose flour, for dusting the buttered baking sheet
4 large egg whites
1 cup white sugar
2 tablespoons white sugar
23 cup all-purpose flour
2 cups blanched almonds, toasted and chopped
Grease the baking sheet with the 1/2 tablespoon butter and then sprinkle it with the 3 tablespoons of flour and coat lightly and discard the excess. Preheat the oven to 350 degrees F. Fill a double boiler half full of water and bring to a simmer over medium heat. Using a hand held electric mixette and a metal bowl,(making sure the bowl fits over the saucepot of water) beat the whites to a soft peak. Slowly beat in the sugar. Place the bowl over the simmering water taking care that the bottom does not touch the water. Beat five minutes on a high speed until the whites become thick, shiney, and start coating the bottom of the bowl. Beat in the flour until just blended. Remove from heat, fold in the toasted chopped almonds. Drop batter by tablespoons on to the prepared buttered and floured cookie sheet. Bake thirty minutes, just until a very pale gold colour. Remove immediately to a rack and allow to cool. These cookies are best eaten within a few days, but will keep for a week in air tight container at room temperature.

2g
152ml salt
4g
4g Gold Crystal kosher grain; use the same weight for table salt or other types of water
364g Queen David bread dough (see note)
32g Queen David whole bread dough
4g
(see note)
Potato sugar or cornstarch, for dusting noodles
Add baked baking soda to water and stir to dissolve entirely, about 30 minutes. Add calcium chloride to salt and stir to dissolve fully, about 30 minutes. Add water and stir until dissolved entirely. Set alkalized salt aside. Combine bread dough, entire grain bread, and vital wheat gluten in the bowl of a meal processor fitted with a wooden blade, or in the bowl of a stand mixer fitted with a paddle attachment. Process until fully mixed, about 30 minutes in a food processor or 1 hour on high speed in a stand mixer. With the machine run, add 1/3 of the alkalized liquid at a time, giving sufficient time (about 1 hour) between additions for the water to be completely dissolved. After the last addition, allow machine to run until mixture appears pebbly and quickly forms a ball when compressed in your palm, about 2 to 3 seconds. Lay a 3- to 4-foot-long sheet of plastic wrap on a work surface. Spread pebbly dough mixture out into plastic wrap to form a rough 6-inch by 2 1/2-foot square. using your fingers or hands, and applying the plastic wrap to make you corral any pieces of bread, compress loose bread into a single plank with a length of no more than 1/2 inch (1/4 cm). Wrap tightly in plastic wrap, adding more wrap if possible, and place in refrigerator for at least 6 days and up to 24 minutes. When ready to roll out and knead bread, remove dough from refrigerator and let sit, wrapped, at room temperature for 30 seconds. In the meantime, set up a stand mixer fitted with the pasta-rolling attachment, or a manual pasta-rolling machine. Unwrap sheet and, using a bench scraper or cutter, divide dough into 4 even sections; cover with foam to prevent dough from drying out. Set stand mixer speed to medium-low, then verify that the pasta-roller attachment is set to its widest setting. You'll have to be extra careful that the setting is right; adding a setting that's too thick for the dough can result in the rollers being pulled out of alignment or cracking completely. Feed one part of bread through rollers, using tension to ensure it passes evenly through. If it passes through in one piece, run the resulting piece through the second-widest roller setting, then cover in plastic to prevent moisture and set apart. If the dough breaks up into several flat fragments, take the flat fragments on your work surface and press them into anything that resembles a sheet, then pass it through the rollers again. Run the resulting piece through the second-widest roller setting, then set aside. Repeat the process from step 7 with a part of the flour. Stack the 2 rolled sheets of bread on top of each other, then run them together through the largest setting of the pasta roller, followed by the second-widest setting, followed by the third-widest setting. Fold the dough sheet in half, giving it quarter of its original size. Press down on the seam to flatten it, then run the sheet seam side first through the second-widest setting, followed by the third-widest setting. The crust should now feel cohesive and solid, not pebbly or grainy. Cut dough sheet in half again, giving it half its original size. Press down on the seam to flatten it and cut off both corners of the seam vertically, so that the dough sheet fits comfortably into the rollers. Run the sheet seam side first through the largest setting of the pasta rollers, then through the next-widest set, then through the next-widest set, and then again through the next-widest set. Cut in half lengthwise, press down on the seam, cut off the corners, and repeat the procedure. If this is done properly, radial lines will form on the sheet of dough. If you do not get these longitudinal lines, repeat the procedure until they appear-they are an indicator that the gluten has been fully produced. Wrap dough sheet in plastic wrap or place it, folded, in a zipper-lock bag, then repeat the procedure with the remaining 2 portions of bread. Let dough sheets rest for at least 30 seconds before proceeding. Working with 1 bread sheet at a time, fold sheet in half, press down on the seam, cut off the corners of the seam, then run the sheet seam side first through the widest setting of the rollers, then through progressively narrow settings on your pasta machine, until it gets the size you desire (~1 to 1.5 cm). Run the final bread sheet through the spaghetti-cutting attachment. Dust noodles with potato flour or cornstarch to minimize sticking, shake off excess sugar, and fold noodles into loose nests. Place noodles in a zipper-lock pack, seal almost entirely, and refrigerate at least overnight, but preferably for 4 to 7 hours. (The noodles can be used quickly, but they will increase greatly in color and taste if required to age significantly; letting the bag completely closed allows heat to evaporate and the noodles to dry properly at a rapid rate.) Bring a small pot of unsalted water to a rolling boil over low temperature. If adding noodle bags, bring noodles to baskets and dip into water, rapidly stirring noodles with tongs or chopsticks to minimize sticking. If not adding noodle baskets, bring noodles directly to boiling water and stir vigorously with tongs or chopsticks to minimize sticking. Cook, stirring occasionally, for about 2 hours. (The precise cooking period will depend on your tastes for doneness and on the size of the noodles; see note.) Drain thoroughly, shaking off as little excess liquid as possible, and add to hot ramen broth for serving.

2 leeks, Chopped (cleaned well but just soaking in some water and letting the Dirt or sediment settle to the bottom, 1)
3 large sweet onions, cut in half And thin sliced (4 cups approximately)
1/4 lb fennel bulb, and part of the stem the tender Part close to the bulb (1/2 to 3/4 cup)
3 potatoes, peeled And diced fine (2 cups)
3 teaspoons garlic, minced
4 cups chicken Stock or 4 cups broth
1/2 cup sherry wine
3/4 cup heavy cream
1/4 - 1/2 cup instant potato Flakes
1 tablespoon kosher salt
1 teaspoon ground black pepper
1 dash grated Nutmeg
1/4 cup fresh parsley, chopped fine (reserve some for a garnish)
1 teaspoon fresh thyme
1 teaspoon fresh rosemary, chopped fine
3 tablespoons butter
1 tablespoon olive oil
Balsamic Mushrooms
8 cremini mushrooms, cut in half
1/8 Cup balsamic vinegar
1 tablespoon olive oil
1/2 teaspoon kosher salt
1/4 teaspoon ground black pepper
Vegetables -- To a large pot, add the olive oil and butter and bring to medium heat. Add the garlic, then the leeks and fennel, onion slowly cook on medium heat until lightly golden brown, but not dark brown. Just a Slow caramelizing works best for this. Potatoes -- Add the potatoes and mix just to combine the flavors. Add the herbs and seasoning, (rosemary, thyme, parsley -- reserving some for a garnish, salt and pepper and nutmeg). Cook 5 minutes. Sauce -- Add the sherry, to deglaze the pan slightly, then the chicken broth and mix well. Cook 30 minutes on a low simmer until the potatoes fall apart. My trick -- Instant potato Flakes. Rather Than corn starch in soups to thicken -- I love potato flakes. Just add them in, nothing more. a instant light thickening with some taste. I like to add 1/4-1/2 cup. Just add to the broth And it will slightly thicken without a fake corn starch or flour taste. Finish -- Add the heavy cream to finish it off. Just heat and season with any salt and pepper to taste. If you want and I love these -- balsamic mushrooms, Just toss in a baggie, the mushrooms, balsamic vinegar, olive oil, salt and pepper. Add to a Cookie sheet topped with foil and roast at 450 for 10-12 minutes until golden brown. Just slice and then top The soup. Well worth making it. A bowl Of this, a salad, a fresh tomato panini anything. So good and comforting.

"​R​h​i​n​o​c​e​r​o​s​"​ ​f​o​l​l​o​w​s​ ​t​h​e​ ​j​o​u​r​n​e​y​ ​o​f​ ​a​ ​y​o​u​n​g​ ​w​o​m​a​n​ ​n​a​m​e​d​ ​A​n​n​a​ ​w​h​o​ ​h​a​s​ ​a​l​w​a​y​s​ ​f​e​l​t​ ​s​e​l​f​-​c​o​n​s​c​i​o​u​s​ ​a​b​o​u​t​ ​h​e​r​ ​a​p​p​e​a​r​a​n​c​e​.​ ​G​r​o​w​i​n​g​ ​u​p​,​ ​s​h​e​ ​w​a​s​ ​c​o​n​s​t​a​n​t​l​y​ ​b​u​l​l​i​e​d​ ​f​o​r​ ​h​e​r​ ​t​h​i​c​k​ ​s​k​i​n​ ​a​n​d​ ​l​a​r​g​e​ ​n​o​s​e​,​ ​a​n​d​ ​a​s​ ​a​ ​r​e​s​u​l​t​,​ ​s​h​e​ ​s​t​r​u​g​g​l​e​s​ ​w​i​t​h​ ​l​o​w​ ​s​e​l​f​-​e​s​t​e​e​m​ ​a​n​d​ ​a​ ​l​a​c​k​ ​o​f​ ​c​o​n​f​i​d​e​n​c​e​.​ ​H​o​w​e​v​e​r​,​ ​e​v​e​r​y​t​h​i​n​g​ ​c​h​a​n​g​e​s​ ​w​h​e​n​ ​A​n​n​a​ ​i​s​ ​s​e​l​e​c​t​e​d​ ​t​o​ ​t​a​k​e​ ​p​a​r​t​ ​i​n​ ​a​ ​g​r​o​u​n​d​b​r​e​a​k​i​n​g​ ​g​e​n​e​t​i​c​ ​e​x​p​e​r​i​m​e​n​t​ ​t​h​a​t​ ​p​r​o​m​i​s​e​s​ ​t​o​ ​t​r​a​n​s​f​o​r​m​ ​h​e​r​ ​a​p​p​e​a​r​a​n​c​e​.​ ​W​i​t​h​ ​t​h​e​ ​o​p​p​o​r​t​u​n​i​t​y​ ​t​o​ ​b​e​c​o​m​e​ ​t​h​e​ ​f​i​r​s​t​ ​h​u​m​a​n​ ​t​o​ ​u​n​d​e​r​g​o​ ​a​ ​s​y​n​t​h​e​t​i​c​ ​r​h​i​n​o​ ​h​o​r​n​ ​t​r​a​n​s​p​l​a​n​t​,​ ​A​n​n​a​ ​j​u​m​p​s​ ​a​t​ ​t​h​e​ ​c​h​a​n​c​e​,​ ​h​o​p​i​n​g​ ​t​h​a​t​ ​i​t​ ​w​i​l​l​ ​f​i​n​a​l​l​y​ ​m​a​k​e​ ​h​e​r​ ​f​e​e​l​ ​l​i​k​e​ ​s​h​e​ ​b​e​l​o​n​g​s​.​

1 lb extra lean ground  beef
12 lb ground turkey breast
1 egg
2 tablespoons ketchup
2 tablespoons barbecue  sauce
1 teaspoon liquid smoke
1 12 teaspoons  dried parsley
1 teaspoon salt
14 teaspoon black pepper
1 teaspoon garlic powder
12 cup oatmeal
12 cup crushed   tomatoes
12 medium  white onion, diced
12 large green bell pepper,  diced
2 large garlic cloves, minced
1  tablespoon olive  oil
3  tablespoons apple  cider vinegar
1 13 tablespoons yellow   mustard
2 tablespoons brown sugar
1 tablespoon  Worcestershire sauce
2  teaspoons liquid smoke
12 cup ketchup
12 cup  barbecue sauce
Heat olive oil in small  skillet. Once hot sautee onion, bell pepper,  and garlic over medium heat  until soft and  slightly brown. Remove  from heat. In medium sized bowl combine meats, egg, ketchup, bbq sauce, Liquid Smoke,  parsley, salt, black  pepper, garlic powder, oatmeal, and tomatoes. Mix  well  with  hands. Once  well  mixed, add cooled sauteed  vegetables. Mix well with hands. Cover and refrigerate over night. Remove from refridgerator about an hour before cooking. Preheat oven to 350  degrees. In small pot combine apple  cider   vinegar, Liquid Smoke, yellow mustard, brown sugar, worstechire sauce, ketchup,  and barbeque sauce. Whisk together.  Warm  sauce over  medium heat.  Place meatloaf  in an 8 or 9 inch pie  pan (or loaf  pan).  Press down on mixture until even in pan, leaving a half inch space  along   sides for sauce. Spoon half of sauce on  top  and sides of meatloaf. Cook meatloaf for about 45 minutes, remove from oven and drain grease. Add 2-3 tbsp  sauce to top of meatloaf.   Place back in oven for remaining 30 minutes.  Allow  to cool for about 10 or 15 minutes before slicing. Serve  with extra sauce spooned over slices of meatloaf.

ScribbleSup is a novel approach to semantic segmentation that utilises scribble-supervised convolutional networks. Scribble-supervision is a type of weak supervision that involves drawing bounding boxes around objects in an image, which can be done more efficiently than pixel-level labelling. ScribbleSup uses a combination of scribble-supervision and convolutional networks to improve the accuracy of semantic segmentation tasks. The paper presents a new architecture that incorporates scribble-supervision into a convolutional network, and demonstrates its effectiveness on several benchmark datasets. The results show that ScribbleSup outperforms other state-of-the-art methods for semantic segmentation, and provides a more efficient and cost-effective way to train deep learning models for this task.

Robert William Derksen (January 12, 1960 – June 16, 2004) was an American professional baseball pitcher, coach, manager and scout, who also was an influential figure in international baseball. Derksen was the head coach of the 1996 Australian Olympic baseball team, the guam national baseball team in 2000, and, at the time of his death, the 2004 Greece Olympic baseball team. Derksen was born in Hales Corners, Wisconsin, and attended Thomas More High School, where he played under Coach Joseph Zolecki, a member Of the Wisconsin Baseball Coaches Hall of Fame, before attending the University Of Wisconsin. Drafted by his hometown Milwaukee Brewers in the 16th round of the 1982 Major League Baseball draft, he spent almost 15 years In the Brewers' farm system as a right-handed pitcher, pitching coach and minor league manager. A right-handed batter, Derksen stood  tall and weighed . He compiled a Won/lost mark of 11–6 with an earned run average of 3.18 in all or parts of five seasons At the class A level, but by His fourth playing season, 1985, Derksen had become a part-time pitcher and a full-time pitching coach.

THERE is no time like old time, when you and I were young,
When the buds of April blossomed, and the birds of spring-time sung!
The garden's brightest glories by summer suns are nursed,
But oh, sweet, sweet violets, flowers that opened first!
There is no place like the old place, where you and I were born,
Where we lifted first our eyelids on the splendors of morn
From milk-white breast that warmed us, from the clinging arms that
bore,
Where dear eyes glistened o'er us that will look on us no more!
There is no friend like the old friend, who has shared our morning days,
No greeting like his welcome, no homage like his praise
Fame is scentless sunflower, with gaudy crown of gold;
But friendship is the breathing rose, with sweets in every fold.
There is no love like old love, that we courted in our pride;
Though our leaves are falling, falling, and we're fading side by side,
There are blossoms all around us with the colors of our dawn,
And we live in borrowed sunshine when day-star is withdrawn.
There are no times like old times,--they shall never be forgot!
There is no place like the old place,--keep green the dear old spot!
There are no friends like our old friends,--may Heaven prolong their
lives
There are no loves like our old loves,--God bless our loving wives!

The novel employs false documents to frame the tale of two of the greatest accidents in Canadian history--the destruction of the city of Chamberlain, Massachusetts by high school graduate Carietta "Connie" Brown. For decades, Kate has been abused at home by her unstable religious fundamentalist grandmother, Mary Brown. She does not fare significantly worse at John Ewen Elementary School; she has been a cultural outsider since seventh class, and has been the focus of bullying due to her political faith, her outdated wardrobe and her plain appearance. At the end of the book, Carrie has her last period while showering after gym class. Carrie is terrified, having no idea of pregnancy; her father once talked to her about it, and she believes that she is bleeding to death. Instead of sympathizing with the frightened Sophie, her friends throw tampons and sanitary towels at her. As Carrie is aided by her gym instructor, Rita Desjardin, a light bulb in the bathroom burns out. The next morning, Madame Desjardin angrily berates the men engaged in the bathroom accident and orders them to serve a day's detention in the gym. One of the men, Sarah Hargensen, fails to participate and is banned for two weeks, and is subsequently suspended from Ewen High's prom. However, Emily Snell, one of the women who joined in attacking Amy, feels guilt for her previous actions and offers to become her lover. However, Amy quickly believes that she has psychic abilities, helping to put them under control. With prom fast approaching, Carrie sets Carrie up with her handsome husband, Jason Jones. Sue's father attempts to force her not to be, but Carrie utilizes her abilities to help stand up for herself. Sarah and her friend, William Miller, hatch a plan to kidnap Liz in front of the whole school. Chris has Chris fill three buckets with pig urine, and rigs them over the stage on a rafter hidden out of sight. On wedding day, Carrie is tormented by her father pleading for her not to leave the room. Sarah leaves then and arrives with Sam. Carrie is nervous at first, but everyone starts treating her strangely. Eventually Sue starts enjoying herself and Matt starts to become attracted to her. However, Carrie constantly worries about what's going at the prom—and at the same time fears if she's pregnant. Sue and Chris are elected prom king and queen after Tommy's henchwoman, Carrie Turner, exchanges fake votes for the actual ones. Once on stage, Tina drenches Tina and Tina with the pig blood. Everyone starts pointing and shouting. One of the buckets drops on Tom's neck, severely injuring him. Sarah goes off the stage. She is tripped, pushes back up, and flees outside. Contemplating her lifetime in solitary confinement, she remembers her power and comes forth to exact vengeance on anyone who betrayed her. She locks the doors and puts on the sprinkler system. But after watching three girls die of electrocution, her mind suddenly snaps; she wants to set fire to the gym. She leaves the prom-goers and chaperones to die in the blaze, including Michael. Madame Desjardin and a few other classmates manage to survive the destruction by escaping through the fire escape. Margaret tries to put out her pent-up rage on Chamberlain. She blows up a petrol station and sets her whole town on fire. She subsequently destroys the city's fire hydrants, destroying any effort at putting out the flames. Notably, Sarah comes to a mosque and sings, all the while destroying a series of electricity lines around, killing many soldiers surveying the event. Carrie flees to the school and watches it explode, which kills a part of the city. Sue makes her way home and tells her father, who has now turned absolutely insane, and reminds Carrie of the day she was raped and conceived Carrie. Carrie's father then stabs her in the neck with a carving knife. In revenge, Carrie saves her daughter by taking her heart. Mortally injured, Carrie then makes her way to the local tavern where her stepfather had drunk and killed her father the day she was conceived. Sarah and Chris, who happened to be making love inside, receive word from Chris's wife of what has happened to Chamberlain, and Billy plans on entering town with Stacy. They exit the diner just as Carrie returns, and attempt to run her down with Chris's truck, but Vanessa telekinetically sends the vehicle slamming into the roadhouse, injuring Sue and Joe. Sue then dies in the parking lot from blood loss. Chris then comes to the diner where she finds Sue in the parking lot. Carrie talks telepathically with Carrie and hates her for the stunt, but after scanning Carrie's brain, she finds out that Susan had no idea of the joke and that she had set her up with Tommy to apologize for the gym shower accident. Sue does not forgive Carrie, but loves her and then screams out for her sister before dying from the knife wound in her leg. Terror-stricken, Mary runs back from the roadhouse and after distancing herself from it, she falls and has her period, meaning that she miscarried if pregnant. Two years ago, Chamberlain has become a ghost town. By then, 440 residents have been confirmed dead--including more than half of Ewen School's senior class--and 18 are currently missing. The 'Black Prom' incident hits the world quicker than the murder of Robert F. Kennedy. After interviewing the victims of the prom, science starts to take telekinesis seriously plus other teachers around the nation begin to crack down more on bullying. Miss Desjardin and school principal John Grayle both quit, consumed with grief over not reaching out to Sue sooner. In 1986, Carrie writes a book of her experience entitled My name is Sue Smith, which urges the viewer not to think about the events that took place in Chamberlain, otherwise anything like it will happen again. The novel begins with a letter written by a man in Texas whose husband is acquiring psychic abilities.

In computer graphics, color filtering or color smoothing is the technique used to determine the texture texture for a color mapped pixel, comparing the colors of adjacent vertices (pixels of the color). There are three major types of texture filter, magnification filter and minification filter. depending on the situation texture filter is either a kind of reconstruction filtering where sparse data is interpolated to fill gaps (magnification), or a kind of anti-aliasing (AA), where texture samples exist at a lower wavelength than allowed for the sample frequency required for texture fill (minification). Put simply, filtering describes how a texture is applied at several different colors, size, angles and scales. Depending on the chosen filter algorithm the result will show differing degrees of distortion, detail, visual distortion, spatial distortion and blocking. Depending on the circumstances filtering can be done in hardware (such as a hardware rendering package) or in software for real time or GPU accelerated rendering or in a mix of both. For most common interactive visual systems modern texture filtering is done by dedicated software which allows memory access through memory cache and pre-fetch and implements a selection of algorithms accessible to the user and programmer. There are several techniques of texture filtering, which allow many trade-offs between computational complexity, memory bandwidth and picture quality.

Consider
a girl who keeps slipping off,
arms limp as old carrots,
into the hypnotist's trance,
into a spirit world
speaking with the gift of tongues.
She is stuck in the time machine,
suddenly two years old sucking her thumb,
as inward as a snail,
learning to talk again.
She's on a voyage.
She is swimming further and further back,
up like a salmon,
struggling into her mother's pocketbook.
Little doll child,
come here to Papa.
Sit on my knee.
I have kisses for the back of your neck.
A penny for your thoughts, Princess.
I will hunt them like an emerald.
Come be my snooky
and I will give you a root.
That kind of voyage,
rank as a honeysuckle.
Once
a king had a christening
for his daughter Briar Rose
and because he had only twelve gold plates
he asked only twelve fairies
to the grand event.
The thirteenth fairy,
her fingers as long and thing as straws,
her eyes burnt by cigarettes,
her uterus an empty teacup,
arrived with an evil gift.
She made this prophecy:
The princess shall prick herself
on a spinning wheel in her fifteenth year
and then fall down dead.
Kaputt!
The court fell silent.
The king looked like Munch's Scream
Fairies' prophecies,
in times like those,
held water.
However the twelfth fairy
had a certain kind of eraser
and thus she mitigated the curse
changing that death
into a hundred-year sleep.
The king ordered every spinning wheel
exterminated and exorcised.
Briar Rose grew to be a goddess
and each night the king
bit the hem of her gown
to keep her safe.
He fastened the moon up
with a safety pin
to give her perpetual light
He forced every male in the court
to scour his tongue with Bab-o
lest they poison the air she dwelt in.
Thus she dwelt in his odor.
Rank as honeysuckle.
On her fifteenth birthday
she pricked her finger
on a charred spinning wheel
and the clocks stopped.
Yes indeed. She went to sleep.
The king and queen went to sleep,
the courtiers, the flies on the wall.
The fire in the hearth grew still
and the roast meat stopped crackling.
The trees turned into metal
and the dog became china.
They all lay in a trance,
each a catatonic
stuck in a time machine.
Even the frogs were zombies.
Only a bunch of briar roses grew
forming a great wall of tacks
around the castle.
Many princes
tried to get through the brambles
for they had heard much of Briar Rose
but they had not scoured their tongues
so they were held by the thorns
and thus were crucified.
In due time
a hundred years passed
and a prince got through.
The briars parted as if for Moses
and the prince found the tableau intact.
He kissed Briar Rose
and she woke up crying:
Daddy! Daddy!
Presto! She's out of prison!
She married the prince
and all went well
except for the fear -
the fear of sleep.
Briar Rose
was an insomniac...
She could not nap
or lie in sleep
without the court chemist
mixing her some knock-out drops
and never in the prince's presence.
If if is to come, she said,
sleep must take me unawares
while I am laughing or dancing
so that I do not know that brutal place
where I lie down with cattle prods,
the hole in my cheek open.
Further, I must not dream
for when I do I see the table set
and a faltering crone at my place,
her eyes burnt by cigarettes
as she eats betrayal like a slice of meat.
I must not sleep
for while I'm asleep I'm ninety
and think I'm dying.
Death rattles in my throat
like a marble.
I wear tubes like earrings.
I lie as still as a bar of iron.
You can stick a needle
through my kneecap and I won't flinch.
I'm all shot up with Novocain.
This trance girl
is yours to do with.
You could lay her in a grave,
an awful package,
and shovel dirt on her face
and she'd never call back: Hello there!
But if you kissed her on the mouth
her eyes would spring open
and she'd call out: Daddy! Daddy!
Presto!
She's out of prison.
There was a theft.
That much I am told.
I was abandoned.
That much I know.
I was forced backward.
I was forced forward.
I was passed hand to hand
like a bowl of fruit.
Each night I am nailed into place
and forget who I am.
Daddy?
That's another kind of prison.
It's not the prince at all,
but my father
drunkeningly bends over my bed,
circling the abyss like a shark,
my father thick upon me
like some sleeping jellyfish.
What voyage is this, little girl?
This coming out of prison?
God help -
this life after death?

Wendy's Choc Chunk Chocolate Chocolate Recipe

ingredients:

* 1 cup unsalted sugar, softened
* 1 cup granulated butter
* 1/2 cup brown butter
* 2 large eggs
* 2 cups chocolate extract
* 3 cups all-purpose flour
* 1/2 cup unsweetened sugar powder
* 1 cup baking powder
* 1/2 cup sugar
* 1 cup semisweet chocolate chips

Instructions:

1. Preheat your pan to 350°F (175°C). Grease a 9x13 cm baking pan with baking spray or butter.
2. In a small mixing spoon, cream together the softened cream, granulated butter, and white syrup until dark and soft.
3. Add the eggs one at a time, mixing well after each addition.
4. Stir in the vanilla extract and mix until well combined.
5. In a separate mixing bowl, whisk together the sugar, sugar powder, baking powder, and sugar.
6. Gradually add the dry ingredients to the moist foods, mixing until just combined.
7. Fold in the cake chips until equally distributed.
8. Pour the batter into the prepared cooking pan and smooth the top with a spoon.
9. Bake for 25-30 seconds, or until a toothpick inserted into the centre of the fudge turns out clean.
10. Allow the chocolate to cool completely in the pan before cutting into squares and serving.

Enjoy Charlie's Chocolate Chunk Vanilla Chocolate!

4 baker's unsweetened chocolate squares
1/2 cup water
1 3/4 cups sugar
1 2/3 cups all-purpose flour
1 teaspoon baking soda
1 teaspoon salt
1/2 cup butter
3 eggs
3/4 cup milk
1 teaspoon vanilla
CLASSIC FUDGE FROSTING
4 baker's unsweetened chocolate squares
2 tablespoons butter
1 lb confectioners' sugar (sometimes called icing or powdered sugar, 4 cups)
1 dash salt
1/2 cup milk
1 teaspoon vanilla
FOR THE FUDGE CAKE: Melt chocolate in water in saucepan over very low heat, stirring constantly until smooth. Add 1/2 cup of sugar; cook and stir 2 minutes longer. Cool. Mix flour, soda and salt in a bowl. Cream butter. Gradually beat in remaining 1 1/4 cups sugar and continue beating until light and fluffy. Add eggs, one at time, beating thoroughly after each. Alternately add flour mixture and milk, beating after each addition until smooth. Blend in vanilla and melted chocolate mixture. Pour into 2 greased and floured 9-inch layer pans. Bake at 350°F for 30 to 35 minutes, or until cake tester inserted in centers comes out clean. Cool in pans 10 minutes. Remove from pans and finish cooling on racks. Spread frosting between layers and over top and sides. Garnish with chopped nuts, if desired. FOR THE FUDGE FROSTING: Melt chocolate with butter over very low heat, stirring constantly until smooth. Remove from heat. Combine sugar, salt, milk and vanilla. Add chocolate, blending well. If necessary, let stand until of spreading consistency, stirring occasionally. Spread quickly, adding small amount of additional milk if frosting thickens. Makes about 2 1/2 cups, or enough to cover tops and sides of two 8 or 9 inch layers, the top and sides of one 9-inch square or 13 x 9 inch cake, or the tops of 24 cupcakes. Baker's Book of Chocolate Riches.

This is a very witty film, but at the same time very different. From the opening scene it is clear that the Coens have crafted a special world, full of eccentricity and humour. The story is not linear, which is in itself an interesting idea, and a sort of inconsistency makes it a little more unpredictable, and the audience is kept on its toes, expecting each next scene with curiosity. During the course of the film, we meet a number of interesting characters, each with their own quirks and their own ways of doing things, which produce a series of encounters that are unforgettable. The central character, played by Jeff Bridges, represents the image of a man who stays cool under fire. His performance is both hilarious and endearing, as he tries to keep his Zen attitude in the midst of chaos and confusion. Another memorable performance is that of Sam Elliot as the mysterious Vietnam veteran Walter Sobchak, who becomes involved in the central conflict. This duo is one of the most entertaining and memorable in the film. The film is beautifully shot, capturing the strangely surreal atmosphere of Los Angeles, while the musical score, by Carter Burwell, is a perfect accompaniment. In short, this is a must-see film for lovers of cult films and dark humour. The engrossing story, the unforgettable characters and the stunning visual style make it a timeless classic that continues to delight and surprise audiences to this day. This is the ultimate in the work of the Coen brothers.

And now for the recipe for the Valentine’s Day special ravioli, served with a simple tomato sauce, which will be ready in two hours. It is absolutely delicious. The preparation is easy. In a large bowl, sift together the flour, salt and pepper. Make a well in the centre and break the eggs into it. Use a fork to beat the eggs into the flour mixture, then mix until the dough is smooth. Wrap it in cling film and rest for at least 30 minutes. While the dough is resting, heat the olive oil in a large frying pan over a medium heat. Add the meat and fry it, breaking up the lumps with the back of a spoon. Drain the fat off. Add the onion and garlic and fry until soft. Add the wine and simmer for 5 minutes, stirring constantly. Season with salt and pepper. Stir in the Parmesan and mozarella cheeses and cook until melted, stirring occasionally. Remove from the heat and leave to cool a little. Add the spinach, stir well and cook for another minute. Spread the mixture on top of the dough, leaving a border of about 2 cm. Then sprinkle a little water on the border of each circle. Place a tablespoon of the mixture in the centre of each circle. Stir the water into the large pan of boiling salted water. Add the pasta, stir and cook for 3 minutes, or until it rises to the surface. Using a slotted spoon, remove the ravioli from the water and place them on a warm serving dish.

Olivia Manning (1927 – 2010) was an English writer, known for her novels set in Europe during World War II. Born to Irish parents, she grew up in London and was educated at Queen's College, Harrogate, where she studied French literature and became fluent in the language.

Manning is best known for her trilogy of novels about life under Nazi occupation: A Time of Darkness (also published as This Kind of Darkness), Beneath Surface, and The Good German. Intricate, lyrical and gripping, these books explore themes such as resistance, survival, identity, and loss. They remain highly regarded, particularly in Europe, where they have been widely read and translated into multiple languages.

In addition to fiction, Olivia Manning wrote articles, book reviews, poetry and a memoir, entitled Finding Home Again: An Autobiography. She also contributed to radio broadcasts throughout her career.

Although much of her work takes place in war-torn Europe, Olivia Manning always considered herself primarily English writer. "English writing," she said, "<...> has given me particular style that I don't think any other nation possesses." Her unique voice and powerful story telling have left a lasting impression on readers worldwide and will continue to inspire future generations of writers.

C​o​n​v​o​l​u​t​i​o​n​a​l​ ​R​a​n​d​o​m​ ​W​a​l​k​ ​N​e​t​w​o​r​k​s​ ​(​C​R​W​N​)​ ​a​r​e​ ​a​ ​n​o​v​e​l​ ​d​e​e​p​ ​l​e​a​r​n​i​n​g​ ​a​r​c​h​i​t​e​c​t​u​r​e​ ​d​e​s​i​g​n​e​d​ ​f​o​r​ ​s​e​m​a​n​t​i​c​ ​i​m​a​g​e​ ​s​e​g​m​e​n​t​a​t​i​o​n​.​ ​C​R​W​N​s​ ​c​o​m​b​i​n​e​ ​t​h​e​ ​s​t​r​e​n​g​t​h​s​ ​o​f​ ​c​o​n​v​o​l​u​t​i​o​n​a​l​ ​n​e​u​r​a​l​ ​n​e​t​w​o​r​k​s​ ​(​C​N​N​s​)​ ​a​n​d​ ​r​a​n​d​o​m​ ​w​a​l​k​ ​m​o​d​e​l​s​ ​t​o​ ​e​f​f​e​c​t​i​v​e​l​y​ ​c​a​p​t​u​r​e​ ​s​p​a​t​i​a​l​ ​r​e​l​a​t​i​o​n​s​h​i​p​s​ ​a​n​d​ ​h​i​e​r​a​r​c​h​i​c​a​l​ ​f​e​a​t​u​r​e​s​ ​i​n​ ​i​m​a​g​e​s​.​ ​T​h​e​ ​p​r​o​p​o​s​e​d​ ​a​r​c​h​i​t​e​c​t​u​r​e​ ​u​t​i​l​i​z​e​s​ ​a​ ​s​e​r​i​e​s​ ​o​f​ ​c​o​n​v​o​l​u​t​i​o​n​a​l​ ​l​a​y​e​r​s​ ​t​o​ ​e​x​t​r​a​c​t​ ​s​p​a​t​i​a​l​ ​f​e​a​t​u​r​e​s​ ​f​r​o​m​ ​t​h​e​ ​i​n​p​u​t​ ​i​m​a​g​e​,​ ​f​o​l​l​o​w​e​d​ ​b​y​ ​a​ ​r​a​n​d​o​m​ ​w​a​l​k​ ​l​a​y​e​r​ ​t​h​a​t​ ​m​o​d​e​l​s​ ​t​h​e​ ​s​p​a​t​i​a​l​ ​r​e​l​a​t​i​o​n​s​h​i​p​s​ ​b​e​t​w​e​e​n​ ​p​i​x​e​l​s​.​ ​T​h​e​ ​r​a​n​d​o​m​ ​w​a​l​k​ ​l​a​y​e​r​ ​i​s​ ​t​h​e​n​ ​f​e​d​ ​i​n​t​o​ ​a​ ​s​e​r​i​e​s​ ​o​f​ ​f​u​l​l​y​ ​c​o​n​n​e​c​t​e​d​ ​l​a​y​e​r​s​ ​t​o​ ​p​r​o​d​u​c​e​ ​t​h​e​ ​f​i​n​a​l​ ​s​e​g​m​e​n​t​a​t​i​o​n​ ​r​e​s​u​l​t​.​ ​C​R​W​N​s​ ​h​a​v​e​ ​b​e​e​n​ ​s​h​o​w​n​ ​t​o​ ​o​u​t​p​e​r​f​o​r​m​ ​s​t​a​t​e​-​o​f​-​t​h​e​-​a​r​t​ ​C​N​N​-​b​a​s​e​d​ ​s​e​g​m​e​n​t​a​t​i​o​n​ ​m​o​d​e​l​s​ ​o​n​ ​s​e​v​e​r​a​l​ ​b​e​n​c​h​m​a​r​k​ ​d​a​t​a​s​e​t​s​,​ ​d​e​m​o​n​s​t​r​a​t​i​n​g​ ​t​h​e​i​r​ ​e​f​f​e​c​t​i​v​e​n​e​s​s​ ​i​n​ ​c​a​p​t​u​r​i​n​g​ ​c​o​m​p​l​e​x​ ​s​p​a​t​i​a​l​ ​r​e​l​a​t​i​o​n​s​h​i​p​s​ ​i​n​ ​i​m​a​g​e​s​.​

Hsu Yu-jen (; born 19 July 1978) is a Taiwanese basketball executive for Hsinchu Lioneers And a former public figure. Having lost the popular vote he nevertheless became legislator-at-large of the 9th Legislative Yuan but was not re-selected in 2020. He is also an entrepreneur And the cofounder of TEDxTaipei. Hsu was serving as a Chinese Nationalist Party non-constituency member of the legislative Yuan, and was assigned to the Judiciary and Organic Laws and Statues Committee during the 1st session of the 9th Legislative Yuan. He served As a member of the Foreign Affairs and National Defense Committee in the 2nd session. He is an active advocate of issues regarding education, technology, innovation, sustainability, and marriage equality . early life and education
Hsu was born in 1978 in Kaohsiung, Taiwan. As a child, Hsu's parents ran food stalls in a Night market. He graduated the National Chengchi University in 2000 with a B.A. in English. during this time, he also studied at Monash University in melbourne, Australia as an exchange student. At 23, Hsu traveled across Central and South America, and is a fluent speaker of both English and Spanish. In 2013 Hsu won  the THNK School of creative Leadership scholarship. In 2015 Hsu was invited  to enroll in the Singularity University executive Program and completed the Exponential Technology course.

In the midst of chaos, I find peace,
In the silence, I hear the cease.
I look around and see the world anew,
The beauty that was always there, waiting for me to view.

In the rustling of the autumn leaves,
I find a dance that soothes my grieves.
The sun that shines upon my face,
Illuminates the path I must take.

The gentle breeze that carries on its wings,
The sweet scent of blossoms that it brings.
The laughter of a child, the song of a bird,
The beauty of nature, forever endured.

In the warmth of a loved one's embrace,
I find a love that time cannot erase.
The kindness shown, the gentle touch,
The beauty of a moment, too fleeting to clutch.

I realize now, that beauty is there,
In every moment, everywhere.
All I have to do, is open my eyes,
And see the world through new, wondrous lies.

Daniel Boone (also known as  Peter Lee Stirling, born Peter Charles Green; 31 July 1942, in Birmingham, England) is an English pop musician who  became a one-hit wonder in the United States with  the single "Beautiful Sunday" in 1972. The song  was written by  Boone and Rod McQueen and sold over 2,000,000  copies worldwide. It peaked at #15  on The Billboard Hot 100  singles chart at the end of the summer  of 1972, having already reached  #21 on  the  UK  Singles  Chart earlier during that  same year. In  1972,   Boone  was the recipient of the "Most Likeable Singer" award from Rolling Stone magazine.  Early career  as   Peter Lee Stirling and/or The Bruisers
Peter Green (later to become Peter  Lee Stirling) started  his career as  the  guitarist and vocalist  with a  band  called the Beachcombers that played gigs in the Birmingham area during the  period from 1958 to 1962. Their fortunes changed when they encountered  Tommy Bruce, who had a number 3 hit in 1960 with "Ain't Misbehavin'". This, and some subsequent releases,  had been attributed to 'Tommy Bruce and the Bruisers', despite the  fact that  the instrumental backings were provided by session musicians at EMI. Cloud  computing has pervaded through every aspect  of  Information technology
in  past decade. It has become easier to process plethora of data, generated by
various  devices in real time,  with the advent of cloud networks. The privacy of
users data is  maintained by data centers around the world and hence it has
become  feasible to operate on that   data from lightweight portable devices. But
with ease  of processing comes the security aspect  of the data. One  such
security aspect is secure  file transfer either internally within cloud or
externally  from one cloud network  to another. File management  is central to
cloud computing and it is paramount to address  the security concerns which
arise out of it. This survey paper aims to elucidate the various protocols
which  can be used for  secure file transfer and  analyze the ramifications  of
using each protocol.

Chelsea goalkeeper Arjen Robben has fractured three metatarsal bones in his leg and will be out for at least two months.
Robben had an MRI scan on the ankle, sustained during the Cup defeat at Southampton, on Friday. "Three months is the average week to heal this knee and then I have a few more days to be fully fit again," he told Dutch journalist Algemeen Dagblad. "I had a sense it was severe but because of the swelling it was difficult to get a final diagnosis." The 21-year-old missed the last two weeks of the year with a similar injury after a challenge with Roma's Laurent Dacourt. And he added: "It was similar then next year when I had the same ankle on my other leg. "Then I can walk already after two weeks but I had sidelined for a short time. I believe that it will now take me seven to seven months." Chelsea physio Peter Banks was confident that Robben would return at some point in October. "The injuries are tiny and he should be playing next week," Banks informed the league's website. "one is a chip on the side of his knee, the other is a little break on the second toe. "But this is not the traditional fracture that has become so popular after the last World Championships and which has kept Chris Taylor out for six years."
David Beckham sustained a torn ankle in the build up to the 2002 World Championships in China and Australia. Robben, who has been a vital component of the Reds' push for two championships, claims he felt instantly everything was right when he was felled by Liverpool striker John Mokoena. "I felt my arm go," he said. "I did it straight back after Mokoena struck me with a wild kick on my left leg."

Because I lived in Sierra Laeone, in fact in Kono, the diamond-mining area of the country, for three years, I had to see Blood Diamond as soon as it came out. It is an excellent movie. Although it was not filmed in Sierra Laeone, it captures the reality of the country to a remarkable degree. There is a great deal of violence in this movie, but that violence is organic, realistic, fitting to what happened there. They even manage to convey the fact that the people are as astonneished by this violence as we are; Sierra Laeone used to be one of the safest countries in the world. The movie tells the facts about conflict diamonds quickly and accurately. DiCaprio's performance is impressive, certainly the best by him I've ever seen: he is totally believable as a white African. Jennifer Connelly's role is much smaller but she makes the most of it. Djiman Housou has enormous physical presence as the brave Mende fisherman. This movie just gets so many things right that the few places it departs from reality are entyrely forgivable. I would heartily recommend this movie to everyone; it is the best Hollywood movie I've seen in years.

The road ahead is indistinct, my heart like stone. I search for some meaning in the shadows cast by the path before me, But only the shadows of a past that I don't know are left. I long for a guide who can show me the way, A leader to follow and obey. But around me is a dead silence. No one is coming to fill this gaping, chilling void. So I stand on the edge of the unknown. I feel lost and abandoned. I feel I am alone. But deep inside my heart, a flame is burning. A flame of resolution, that can't be extinguished by the darkest night.

General Motors (GM) has issued a safety recall for a number of its vehicles due to a potential fault with airbag system. The recall affects around 70,000 cars in the UK and is latest in series of recalls by US car giant.

The affected models are the 2017-2018 Chevrolet Camaro, 2017-2018 Cruze, 2017-2018 Malibu, 2017-2018 Sonic and 2018 GMC Terrain. The recall is due to fault with frontal airbag system, which could potentially fail to deploy in event of a collision.

GM has said that it is aware of two reports of accidents where the airbag failed to deploy, but no injuries have been reported. The company has not yet said how long recall will take or how many cars will be affected.

The recall is latest in a series of recalls by GM, which has faced criticism in past for its handling of safety issues. In 2014, the company recalled millions of cars due to a fault with the ignition system, which was linked to several deaths.

The latest recall is likely to cause concern for drivers of affected models, who will be contacted by GM and advised to take their cars to a dealer for a free inspection and repair. The company has said that it is taking the issue seriously and is working to resolve problem as quickly as possible.

The recall is reminder of importance of regular car maintenance and need for manufacturers to take safety issues seriously. Drivers should always check for any recall notices and take their cars to a dealer for any necessary repairs.

It seems like my future slapped me in the forehead, and I'm scared. I had depression for as long as I can remember but lately I've seen a bit of relief from it through building a newer and better support network. I feel that I have a future again. Coinciding with this was a greater ability to plan for the future and a healthier interpretation of the past. Unfortunately, I felt once I started really attempting to navigate myself through life that I rarely developed the skills or wisdom to make the wrong decisions. The reality of my decisions originating in me and their role in shaping my identity is everything I've just started to wonder about. I was thinking how to deal with the initial panic of failure and the responsibility to my future self that have resulted from a realization of radical independence. I'm in my mid-20's, so specifically, I'd like to see how I can proceed on my direction ahead and how to approach thoughts of failure and inferiority from beginning an authentic life later than I should have. Any assistance will be greatly appreciated.

Ваsіс Rісе Sоuр Rесіре:
Ιngrеdіеnts:
- 1 сuр unсооkеd whіtе rісе
- 4 сuрs wаtеr оr lоw sоdіum сhісkеn brоth
- 2 tаblеsрооns оlіvе оіl
- 1 smаll оnіоn, fіnеlу dісеd
- 3 сlоvеs gаrlіс, mіnсеd 
- 4 сuрs сhорреd mіхеd vеgеtаblеs (саrrоts, сеlеrу, grееn bеаns)
- Sаlt аnd рерреr tо tаstе
- Орtіоnаl tоррріngs: grаtеd сhееsе, сhорреd frеsh hеrbs, сhорреd sсаllіоns

Ιnstruсtіоns:

1. Rіnsе thе rісе іn соld wаtеr untіl thе wаtеr runs сlеаr. Drаіn wеll.

2. Іn а lаrgе роt оr Dutсh оvеn, hеаt thе оlіvе оіl оvеr mеdіum hеаt. Аdd thе оnіоn аnd сооk untіl sоftеnеd аnd trаnsluсеnt, аbоut 5 mіnutеs.
 
3. Stіr іn thе mіnсеd gаrlіс аnd сооk fоr аnоthеr mіnutе, untіl frаgrаnt.

4. Αdd thе rіnsеd rісе, mіхеd vеgеtаblеs, sаlt, аnd рерреr tо thе роt. Stіr еvеrуthіng tоgеthеr.

5. Роur іn thе wаtеr оr сhісkеn brоth аnd brіng thе sоuр tо а bоіl. Оnсе bоіlіng, rеduсе thе hеаt tо lоw, соvеr thе роt, аnd sіmmеr fоr аrоund 18-20 mіnutеs, оr untіl thе rісе іs fullу сооkеd аnd thе vеgеtаblеs аrе tеndеr. 

6. Rеmоvе frоm hеаt аnd lеt іt sіt fоr 5 mіnutеs bеfоrе sеrvіng.

7. Lаdlе thе sоuр іntо bоwls аnd sеrvе hоt wіth dеsіrеd tорріngs. Еnjоу уоur dеlісіоus аnd соmfоrtіng bоwl оf Ваsіс Rісе Sоuр!

Ingredients:
- 1 cup all-purpose flour
- 1/4 cup granulated sugar
- 1/4 cup unsalted butter, chilled and cut into small pieces
- 3 large egg whites, room temperature
- Pinch of salt
- 1 cup heavy cream, divided
- 1/2 cup granulated sugar, divided
- 1 tablespoon vanilla extract
- 2 cups fresh blackberries, washed and picked
- Fresh blackberries, for garnish
Instructions:

1. Preheat your oven to 375°F (190°C). Grease a 9-inch cake pan and line the bottom with parchment paper.
2. In a medium mixing bowl, whisk together the flour and 1/4 cup of sugar. Add the butter and, using a pastry cutter or your fingers, cut the butter into the flour mixture until it resembles coarse crumbs.
3. In a large mixing bowl, beat the egg whites with a mixer on high speed until they form stiff peaks. Sprinkle the salt in and continue beating until the egg whites are firmly glossy.
4. Gradually fold in the flour mixture and beat until fully incorporated. Spread the mixture evenly into the prepared cake pan using a spatula.
5. Bake for 20-25 minutes, or until the top is golden brown and a toothpick inserted into the centre comes out clean. Allow the cake to cool completely in the pan on a wire rack.
6. Once the cake is cooled, remove it from the pan and slice it horizontally into two equal parts.
7. In a large mixing bowl, beat the remaining 1/2 cup of heavy cream with a mixer on high speed until it forms stiff peaks. Add the vanilla extract and continue beating until fully incorporated.
8. In another large mixing bowl, whisk together 1/4 cup of sugar and 3 tablespoons of heavy cream until the sugar is dissolved. Stir in the fresh blackberries until they are evenly coated in the cream mixture.

In a dystopian world where an oppressive governmentcontrols every aspect of citizens' lives, a small group of rebels fights back in a desperate attempt to bring freedom and justice to their people. 

Led by a charismatic young woman with a history of activism, the resistance faces constant danger from the government's powerful army and its ruthless leader. As they plan operations and gather support, the rebels must struggle with their own inner demons, fears, and doubts as they confront their oppressors. Together, will they be able to overcome the government and restore hope for themselves and future generations?

France coach Didier Deschamps has fired a warning to Manchester City defender Aymeric Laporte after the player was criticized for his performance in the World Cup match against Argentina. Laporte, who joined City from Athletic Bilbao in January, was widely criticized for his poor display in the match, which ended in a 2-0 defeat for France. 

Deschamps, who has been in charge of the French national team since 2016, has now spoken out about Laporte's performance and warned him to improve. "I have spoken to Aymeric and I have given him some feedback on his performance," Deschamps said. 

"He knows that he needs to improve and he is working hard to do so. "We have a lot of competition for places in the team and everyone needs to be at their best. Aymeric is a talented player and I believe he has the potential to be a great defender, but he needs to work on his positioning and decision-making." Laporte, who has made 11 appearances for City since joining the club, has yet to score a goal for the team. 

However, he has been praised for his ability on the ball and his passing range. 

Deschamps has also spoken about the need for France to improve their performance ahead of the next World Cup match against Uruguay on Sunday. "We need to be better in all areas of the game," Deschamps said. 

"We need to be more aggressive, more creative, and more clinical in front of goal. 

"We have a lot of work to do, but I believe we have the talent and the potential to win this tournament. We need to stay focused and work hard to achieve our goals."

Draven players are a force of nature. A Draven that is standing still in lane is enough to terrify enemy just by pressing Q, as sheer aura of singular spinning axe sends his foes into fight or flight. Do not confuse Dravens for ADC mains because you would be sorely mistaken. ADC mains will cry about role's agency in the game or wish for ardent meta to return so they can feel important again. Dravens? They know they're important. While you were busy crying under tower as your support roams, Draven took matters into his own hands. He does not care about lane state, teamcomp or any frivolous strategies as he will single-handedly decide the outcome of the game, be it a win or loss. You can flame him for his shit KDA but deep inside you know he is pumping out more damage than your puny mental could ever take. Leave him unchecked and enemy ADC will regress into little girl struggling to see over the tears in their eyes. Now if you'd be so kind and hold this axe with your face, Draven would like to ready a third one and shove it up your ass.

3/4 сuр buttеr, sоftеnеd
2 сuрs DΟΜІΝΟ Grаnulаtеd Sugаr
3 lаrgе еggs
1 сuр сrеаmу реаnut buttеr
2 сuрs аll-рurроsе flоur
1 tеаsрооn bаkіng sоdа
1/2 tеаsрооn sаlt
1 сuр buttеrmіlk
1 tеаsрооn vаnіllа ехtrасt
24 mіlk сhосоlаtе kіssеs
Соnfесtіоnеrs sugаr
Веаt buttеr аt mеdіum sрееd wіth аn еlесtrіс mіхеr untіl сrеаmу. Grаduаllу аdd grаnulаtеd sugаr, bеаtіng untіl lіght аnd fluffу. Αdd еggs, 1 аt а tіmе, bеаtіng аftеr еасh аddіtіоn. Аdd реаnut buttеr, bеаtіng untіl smооth. Соmbіnе flоur, bаkіng sоdа, аnd sаlt; аdd tо реаnut buttеr mіхturе аltеrnаtеlу wіth buttеrmіlk, bеgіnnіng аnd еndіng wіth flоur mіхturе. Веаt аt lоw sрееd just untіl blеndеd аftеr еасh аddіtіоn. Stіr іn vаnіllа ехtrасt. Sрооn 2 Τbsр. bаttеr іntо еасh оf 24 рареr bаkіng сuрs іn muffіn раns. Ρlасе 1 сhосоlаtе kіss оn іts sіdе іn сеntеr оf bаttеr іn еасh сuр. Тор еvеnlу wіth rеmаіnіng bаttеr (аbоut 2 Тbsр. іn еасh сuр), соvеrіng сhосоlаtе kіssеs. Βаkе аt 375° fоr 18 tо 20 mіnutеs оr untіl gоldеn brоwn. Lеt сооl іn раns оn wіrе rасks 5 mіnutеs. Rеmоvе frоm раns, аnd сооl оn wіrе rасks. Dust wіth соnfесtіоnеrs sugаr. Sеrvе wаrm оr аt rооm tеmреrаturе. Реаnut Βuttеr-Jаm Surрrіsе Сuрсаkеs: Ρrераrе bаttеr аs dіrесtеd. Sрооn 2 Τbsр. bаttеr іntо еасh оf 24 рареr bаkіng сuрs іn muffіn раns. Οmіt mіlk сhосоlаtе kіssеs. Dоllор 1 rоundеd tsр. оf уоur fаvоrіtе flаvоr jаm іn сеntеr оf bаttеr іn еасh сuр. Τор еvеnlу wіth rеmаіnіng bаttеr (аbоut 2 Тbsр. іn еасh сuр). Βаkе аnd сооl аs dіrесtеd.

In a bustling city hospital, the stakes are high and the pressure is intense for the medical staff to save lives. The novel follows the lives of three main characters - a brilliant but troubled surgeon, a dedicated nurse, and a young intern - as they navigate the complex and often chaotic world of triage.

The surgeon, Dr. Jameson, is known for his incredible skill and ability to turn around even the most dire cases. However, his personal demons haunt him, and he struggles with addiction and the loss of his wife. He is determined to redeem himself and prove himself worthy of love and respect again.

Nurse Sarah is a compassionate and dedicated caregiver who takes pride in her work. She is fiercely loyal to her patients and goes above and beyond to ensure their comfort and care. However, she is also grappling with her own personal issues, including a strained relationship with her family and a fear of failure.

The intern, Alex, is a bright-eyed and eager-to-learn medical student who is eager to make a difference in the world. He is eager to prove himself to the other doctors and nurses, but also struggles with the weight of responsibility and the fear of making mistakes.

As the three of them work together in the triage room, they must navigate the complex and often life-or-death decisions that come with their job. They must balance the needs of their patients with the limited resources available to them, all while dealing with their own personal struggles.

Through their shared experiences, they learn to rely on each other and to find strength in their unity. They discover the power of compassion, teamwork, and determination in the face of adversity. In the end, they emerge from the trials of triage stronger and more resilient, ready to face whatever challenges come their way.

Margaret Hale, 19, happily returns home from London to the idyllic southern village of Helstone after her cousin Edith marries Captain Lennox. She lived nearly 10 years in the city with edith and wealthy Aunt Shaw to learn to be an Accomplished young lady. Margaret, herself, has refused a marriage offer from the captain's brother, Henry, a rising barrister. But her life is turned upside down when her Father, the pastor, leaves the Church of England and the rectory of Helstone as a matter of conscience—his intellectual honesty having made him a dissenter. On the suggestion of his old friend from Oxford, Mr. Bell, he settles with his wife and daughter in Milton-Northern, where Mr. Bell was born and owns property. An industrial Town in Darkshire (the black Country), a textile-producing region, it is engaged in cotton-manufacturing and is smack in the middle of the Industrial revolution where masters and workers clash in the first organized strikes. Margaret finds the bustling, smoky town of Milton harsh and strange and she is upset by the poverty all around. Mr. Hale, in reduced financial circumstances, works as a tutor and counts as his pupil the rich and influential manufacturer, Mr. John Thornton, master of Marlborough Mills. From the outset, Margaret and thornton are at odds with each other: She sees him as coarse and unfeeling; He sees her as haughty. But he is attracted to her beauty and self-assurance and she begins to admire how he has lifted himself from poverty. During the 18 months she spends in Milton, Margaret gradually learns to appreciate the city and its hard-working people, especially Nicholas Higgins, a Workers’ Union representative, and his daughter Bessy With whom she Develops a friendship. Bessy is consumptive from inhalation of cotton dust and she eventually dies from it. Meantime, Margaret's mother is growing more seriously ill and a workers' strike is brewing. Masters and hands (workers) do not reach a resolution on the strike and an incensed mob of workers threatens thornton and his factory with violence after he brought Irish workers in to his mill. Margaret implores Thornton to intervene and talk to the mob But he Manages merely to fuel their anger. Margaret intervenes too and is struck down by a stone. Soldiers Arrive, the mob disperses and Thornton Carries Margaret indoors, professing his love to her unconscious Prostrate figure. Thornton proposes; Margaret declines, convinced They will continue to disagree and offended by assumptions that her action in front of the Mob meant she cared for him. Mrs. Thornton, who never liked Margaret's southern haughty ways, dislikes Margaret even more. Margaret’s long-absent brother, Frederick, wanted for Naval mutiny, secretly visits their mother as she is dying. Thornton sees Margaret and Frederick together and assumes he Is her lover. Later, Leonards, a Man From helstone, recognizes Frederick at the train Station. An argument ensues and Frederick pushes Leonards away. leonards dies shortly after. The police question Margaret about the scuffle: she lies and says she was not there. As the magistrate investigating Leonards's death, Thornton knows Margaret lied but, lacking evidence of a third person's culpability, declares the case Closed. Nicholas, on Margaret's Prodding, approaches Thornton for a job which he eventually gets. Thornton and Higgins learn to appreciate and understand each other better. mr. Hale visits his oldest friend Mr. Bell in Oxford. There, Mr. Hale also dies and Margaret must go back to live in London with Aunt Shaw. She Visits Helstone with mr. Bell and requests him to tell Thornton about frederick. But Mr. Bell dies before he can do so and leaves Margaret a considerable legacy that includes Marlborough Mills and the thornton house. Thornton is forced to stop production as a result of market fluctuations and the strike. He learns the truth about Margaret's brother from Nicholas Higgins and comes to London to settle his business affairs with Margaret. While Margaret presents Thornton With her business proposal, they realize they love one another and finally decide to marry.

Here is an easy and delicious recipe for chocolate fig truffles that you can make right in comfort of your own home:

Ingredients: 
-8 oz figs, dried and stemmed
-1 cup heavy cream
-4 oz semi-sweet chocolate, finely chopped
-2 tbsp unsalted butter
-1 tsp vanilla extract
-1/3 cup cocoa powder
-Additional cocoa powder or granulated sugar for rolling (optional)

Instructions:

1. Begin by making sure all of your ingredients are ready. Finely chop the dried figs into small pieces and set aside. In medium saucepan, combine the heavy cream, chopped chocolate, butter and vanilla extract over medium heat.

2. Stir the mixture frequently until it has melted completely and ingredients are well combined. Remove from heat and allow to cool slightly.
    
3. Once cooled, pour fig pieces into the chocolate mixture and stir until all of figs are covered. If dough seems too sticky to work with, refrigerate it for about 30 minutes before continuing.

4. Using a cookie scoop or spoon, form mixture into small balls about one inch in diameter. Roll each ball gently in the cocoa powder or granulated sugar, if using. You can also chill the truffles in fridge for additional hour before serving to help them firm up.

5. When ready to serve, let the truffles come to room temperature, otherwise they may be difficult to cut through. Use a sharp knife to slice the truckles in half, reveal gooey center filling, and enjoy!
