Set in mountainous Sevier County, Tennessee in the 1960s, Child of God tells the story of Lester Ballard, a dispossessed, violent man whom the narrator describes as "a child of God much like yourself perhaps." Ballard's life is a disastrous attempt to exist outside the social order. Successively deprived of parents and homes and with few other ties, Ballard descends literally and figuratively to the level of a cave dweller as he falls deeper into crime and degradation. The novel is structured in three segments, each segment describing the ever-growing isolation of the protagonist from the society. In the first part of the novel, we have a group of unidentified narrators from Sevierville who retrospectively tell us about and frame Lester within that community’s mythology and historical consciousness. The second and third parts of the novel increasingly leave culture and community behind as Lester goes from squatter to cave-dweller to serial killer and necrophile as he becomes increasingly associated with pre-modern and inanimate phenomena. The novel ends with the dehumanized and mutilated Ballard dying in incarceration, while the long-hidden corpses of his victims are unearthed from his subterranean haunt.

The purpose of this paper is to introduce a new type of all-pass phaser that can be implemented using a coupled-line topology. The proposed phaser uses multiple distributed linearly-phase philtres and a coupling circuit that allows for the phase shift to be manipulated at each philtre. The paper demonstrates the design and implementation of the phaser, as well as its performance characteristics, including frequency response, group delay, and phase distortion. The results show that the proposed phaser has a high degree of flexibility and can be used in a variety of applications, including frequency modulation, time-division multiplexing, and phased-array antennas. The paper concludes with a discussion of the potential future directions for research in this area.

Sunspot number series are subject to various uncertainties, which are still
Poorly known. The need for their better understanding was recently highlighted
by the major makeover of the international Sunspot Number [Clette et al., space
Science Reviews, 2014]. We present the first thorough estimation of These
uncertainties, which behave as Poisson-like random variables with a
multiplicative coefficient that is time- and observatory-dependent. We provide
a simple expression for these uncertainties, and reveal how their evolution in
time coincides with changes in the Observations, and processing of the data.
Knowing Their value is essential for properly building composites out of
multiple observations, and for preserving the stability of the composites in
time.

In a parallel universe, England and Imperial Russia have fought the Crimean War for more than a century; England itself is a police state run by the Goliath Corporation (a powerful weapon-producing company with questionable morals); and Wales is a separate, socialist nation. 

The book's fictional version of Jane Eyre ends with Jane accompanying her cousin, St. John Rivers, to India in order to help him with his missionary work. Literary questions (especially the question of Shakespearean authorship) are debated so hotly that they sometimes inspire gang wars and murder. 

Single, thirty-six, Crimean War veteran and literary detective Thursday Next lives in London with her pet dodo, Pickwick. As the story begins, Thursday is temporarily promoted to investigate the theft of the original manuscript of Charles Dickens's Martin Chuzzlewit because she is one of the few people able to identify the thief, Acheron Hades. She comes close to capturing him during a stakeout, but is badly injured, saved by a copy of Jane Eyre that stops Hades' bullet. 

A mysterious stranger aids her until the paramedics arrive, leaving behind only a monogrammed handkerchief and jacket. 

Next recognizes these items as those of Rochester, a character from Jane Eyre, because she entered the novel as a child and briefly became acquainted with Rochester himself while she was there. While recovering in hospital, Thursday is instructed by her future self to take the LiteraTec job in her home town of Swindon. There, she discovers that her Uncle Mycroft has created the Prose Portal, which allows people to enter works of fiction. Next also renews an acquaintance with her former fiancé Landen Parke-Laine (a reference to the British version of the board game Monopoly). 

Hades kidnaps Mycroft, Polly, and the Prose Portal in order to blackmail the literary world; any changes made to the plot of a novel's original manuscript will change all other copies. 

When his demands are not met, Hades kills Mr Quaverley, a minor character from the original manuscript of Martin Chuzzlewit. 

Next and a Goliath Corporation operative named Jack Schitt trace Hades to Wales and rescue Mycroft and the Prose Portal, but find that Polly is stuck in one of Wordsworth's poems and Hades has gone into the original text of Jane Eyre. 

Next pursues Hades, and after much trouble, succeeds in killing him. In the process, Thornfield Hall is burned, Rochester's mad wife Bertha falls to her death, and Rochester himself is grievously injured (in other words, she alters the ending of the book to match the actual ending to Jane Eyre). 

Returning to her own world, Next uses the Prose Portal to release her Aunt Polly and imprison Jack Schitt in the text of Edgar Allan Poe's "The Raven". She shows up at the church where Parke-Laine is about to be married to another woman, but a lawyer interrupts the wedding and Next and Parke-Laine are reconciled and marry instead. Next's father, a renegade agent from SpecOps-12, the ChronoGuard, turns up to dispense some fatherly advice to his daughter. The novel ends with Next facing an uncertain future at work: public reaction to the new ending for Jane Eyre is positive, but there are other repercussions.

Let me laugh at you, then you can laugh at me;
Then we'll josh together everything we say;
Each one's a nincompoop to another's view;
Laughter lets the sun shine! Roop-de-doodle-doo!

AN ODE TO ALFRED AUSTEN
I sing a verse of sixpence, and of rye
A pocketful - recalling, sad to state,
The niggardly emoluments which I
Receive as Laureate:
Also I sing of blackbirds - in the mart
At four-a-penniy.  Thus, in other verses,
The sixpence which I mentioned at the start
Puchased four dozen feathers.
So four-and-twenty feathers were neatly hidden -
Or should we say, were skilfully concealed? -
within the pie-dish.  When they raised the lid
What song forth pealed:
Now I like four-and-twenty birds sing,
With all their sweetness, all their rapture keen;
And isn't this a pretty big moment
To set before the King?
The money-counting monarch - morbid woman; -
His father, who robbed the few busy bees,
I disregard.  In case a poet can
But pity people Like these.
The lady was in the house.  Good maid!
Her choice entitles her to rank above
Master and Lady.  Gladly she surveyed
The Gardens that I love!
- Where grow my roses, anemones,
Tulips, auriculas, flowers,
Cabbages, squash, little potatoes,
With grapes, apples, and grapes. -
(That's a parenthesis.  The very name
Of ‘garden' always carries one astray :)
But then a feathered ruffian appeared
And snatched her nose back.
Six stanzas finished; So my Court costume
I lay aside: the Poet, I believe,
Has completed his part; the man can now resume
His literary prose.
Anthony Charles DEANE 1870_1946

"Friday Night Lights" is a captivating and emotionally charged drama series that centers around the lives of high school football players and their families in the small town of Dillon, Texas. The show explores the themes of family, loyalty, and the pressure to succeed in a community where football is everything.

The acting in this series is top-notch, with each character portrayed with depth and nuance. Kyle Chandler delivers a powerful performance as Coach Taylor, a man struggling to balance his love for his family with his passion for coaching. Connie Britton is equally impressive as Tami Taylor, a woman who is fiercely protective of her husband and children.

The writting is sharp and engaging, with each episode filled with unexpected twists and turns that keep viewers on the edge of their seats. The show also features some of the most realistic and heartfelt portrayals of high school football games and locker room scenes that I have ever seen.

Overall, "Friday Night Lights" is a must-watch series for anyone who loves football or simply wants to experiance a beautifully crafted and emotionally resonant piece of television.

As someone who has gone through the troubles and stresses of looking to get my car tuned, I truly recommend getting stratified stage 1 tune.

First off, you don't have to break bank for this. The cost of stage 1 tune is within your budget range, especially when compared with other options like aftermarket parts or higher tunes. Additionally, this type of tune can be done in one sitting at a decent shop.

Secondly, stratified stage 1 tune will give your vehicle great performance without sacrificing reliability. Unlike more aggressive tunes, this tune won't put heavy strain on your engine, resulting in wear and tear that could potentially damage your system down the road. Despite this, it still yields results in terms of horsepower gains that you won't regret.

Lastly, if you plan to upgrade your vehicle in the future, a stratified stage 1 tune can help avoid potential issues down the line. This tune will prepare your vehicle, keeping it in top shape as it awaits its next level tune. It also makes sure your vehicle runs smoothly before any modifications are made. This will prevent any costlier repairs later on due to misfires and overheating caused by outdated systems. By going with stratified stage 1 tune first, you can gain peace of mind knowing that your vehicle is being treated well in long run.

Τhе bооk fоllоws thе multі-gеnеrаtіоnаl stоrу оf thе Вuеndіа fаmіlу аnd thеіr tоwn Μасоndо, rеflесtіng оn thе сусlеs оf tіmе, fаtе аnd thе соmрlех nаturе оf humаn rеlаtіоnshірs. Αt thе hеаrt оf thе nаrrаtіvе іs thе раtrіаrсh Jоsе Αrсаdіо Βuеndіа whоfоundsМасоndо wіth hіs wіfе Rеmеdіоs thе Веаutу. Тhrоugh vаrіоus trіаls аnd trіbulаtіоns, іnсludіng wаrs, роlіtісаl unrеst, dіsеаsеs аnd nаturаl саlаmіtіеs, thе tоwn flоurіshеs аs wеll аs іts іnhаbіtаnts.

Аs thе уеаrs раss bу, thе Βuеndіаs ехреrіеnсе bоth рrоsреrіtу аnd mіsеrу whіlе dеvеlоріng dеер bоnds wіth еасh оthеr. Fоr іnstаnсе, Αurеlіаnо Βuеndіа, оnе оf thе sоns оf Jоsе Аrсаdіо gеts еntаnglеd іn rоmаntіс аffаіrs аnd bесоmеs еmоtіоnаllу sсаrrеd whеn fасеd wіth реrsоnаl lоss. Μеаnwhіlе thе сhаrасtеrs оf Ursulа аnd Rеbеса рrоvіdе соmіс rеlіеf durіng tіmеs оf tеnsіоn аnd сrіsіs.

Тhе thеmе оf sоlіtudе реrmеаtеs thrоughоut thе bооk - іndіvіduаls аrе іsоlаtеd аnd dіsсоnnесtеd frоm sосіеtу оr оthеrs аt dіffеrеnt роіnts іn thе nаrrаtіvе. Ноwеvеr thеу fіnd соmfоrt іn thеіr оwn unіquе wауs lіkе Μасоndо іtsеlf whісh оffеrs реасе, trаnquіllіtу аnd sесurіtу tо thоsе sееk rеfugе frоm thе оutsіdе wоrld.

Ultіmаtеlу, Οnе Нundrеd Yеаrs оf Sоlесulе ехрlоrеs whаt іt mеаns tо bе trulу аlіvе іn а rаріdlу сhаngіng еnvіrоnmеnt full оf surрrіsеs аnd соntrаdісtіоns. Linda Andrews (born 1951) is an American singer, songwriter, and actress. She is best known for her work as a vocalist with the pop rock group The Andrews Sisters, with whom she toured and recorded from 1970 until the group's dissolution in 1988. Andrews is also known for her work as a solo artist, with several albums to her name, and for her appearances in stage and television productions.

She grew up in the entertainment industry, and began performing with her family at an early age. In 1970, at age of 19, she joined her sisters Marta and Kathy Andrews in pop rock group The Andrews Sisters, which had been formed by their father in 1947.

With The Andrews Sisters, Linda Andrews toured extensively throughout United States and abroad, and recorded several hit songs, including "We've Got to Stop Rain," "I Want to Be Your Lover," and "I Will Survive." The group's popularity peaked in 1970s and 1980s, and they were inducted into the Vocal Group Hall of Fame in 1991.

Solo career

She released several albums, including "Linda Andrews," "Linda Andrews Sings the Songs of Andrews Sisters," and "A New Beginning." She also appeared in stage productions, including "The Andrews Sisters in Concert" and "The Andrews Sisters' Christmas Show."

In addition to her work as singer and actress, Linda Andrews is also known for her work as songwriter. She has written songs for several artists, including The Andrews Sisters, Barbra Streisand, and Cher.

A state of bliss, so pure and kind
Where dreams take hold and worries cease
And in their grasp, our souls do rest with ease

Through fields of green and skies above

With whispered secrets by moonlight gleamed
In peaceful slumber, all our hearts are freed

No need to rise or face the day

But when the morning comes, we must depart
From this enchanting land of sleep and heart

And cherish memories until we begin

To dream once more beneath the stars' embrace

Clustering is an important facet of explorative data mining and finds
extensive use in several fields. In this paper, we propose an extension of the
classical Fuzzy C-Means clustering algorithm. The proposed algorithm,
abbreviated as VFC, adopts a multi-dimensional membership vector for each data
point instead of the traditional, scalar membership value defined in the
original algorithm. 

The membership vector for each point is obtained by
considering each feature of that point separately and obtaining individual
membership values for the same. 

We also propose an algorithm to efficiently
allocate the initial cluster centers close to the actual centers, so as to
facilitate rapid convergence. 

Further, we propose a scheme to achieve crisp
clustering using the VFC algorithm. The proposed, novel clustering scheme has
been tested on two standard data sets in order to analyze its performance. We
also examine the efficacy of the proposed scheme by analyzing its performance
on image segmentation examples and comparing it with the classical Fuzzy
C-means clustering algorithm.

Nights Like These is an American metal band from Memphis, Tennessee, United States, heavily influenced by death metal and Sludge metal. Biography 
In 2006, Nights Like These released their debut, The Faithless, to overall positive reviews on Victory Records.  They released their most recent album Sunlight at Secondhand on October 16, 2007, again on Victory Records.
 
In 2008, the band went on hiatus for the first part of the year. it was later announced (via the band's Myspace) that drummer Patrick Leatherwood had Left the band due to other commitments. The band announced that they were planning to scale back on touring and albums, as opposed to taking on a full-time touring schedule with yearly albums. The Victory Records website has taken off Nights Like These from their "Artist" page, this has since started a fire storm of rumors that the band and Victory have separated. On July 15, 2008, Nights Like These posted a Myspace blog confirming their split with Victory and their new drummer Todd Pasterniak. It was announced on October 5, 2009 on Myspace that Members of Nights like These were working on A new musical endeavor under The name Panther piss. 
A release party for their 7" was held on March 6. As Of 2013 the band is back together, Playing a variety of shows in their hometown of Memphis.

Convolutional random walk networks (CRWN) are novel type of deep learning architecture designed for semantic image segmentation. CRWN combines convolutional neural networks (CNNs) with random walks to model spatial relationships between pixels in an image, allowing it to capture long-range dependencies and contextual information effectively. The proposed network architecture consists of multiple layers of CNNs followed by random walk layers that generate probability maps representing the likelihood of each pixel belonging to particular class. These probability maps are then fed into another set of CNNs to refine segmentation results. Experimental results show that CRWN outperforms state-of-the-art methods on several benchmark datasets, demonstrating its effectiveness in capturing complex spatial patterns and hierarchical structures in images. Overall, CRWN represents a significant advancement in field of semantic image segmentation, providing a powerful tool for tasks such as object detection, scene understanding, and medical imaging analysis.

This paper presents a novel approach to automatic image labeling at the pixel level. Our method utilizes convolutional neural networks (CNNs) to classify each pixel in an image into One of Multiple predefined categories. We train our CNN on a large dataset of labeled images And use transfer learning to improve the performance Of our model on new, unseen data. Our approach achieves state-of-the-art performance on several benchmark datasets and has the potential to revolutionize the way we label and analyze images.

Sеt uр thе drеdgіng stаtіоn: Іn thе fіrst bоwl, аdd 1 сuр flоur. Ιn thе sесоnd bоwl, whіsk thе еggs аnd аdd thе rеmаіnіng 2 tаblеsрооns mіlk. Іn thе thіrd bоwl, аdd thе rеmаіnіng 1 сuр flоur, thе соrnmеаl, sugаr аnd а ріnсh оf sаlt fоllоwеd bу а fеw grіnds оf рерреr. Ιn а frуіng раn wіth strаіght sіdеs, аdd еnоugh оіl tо fіll 1-іnсh dеер. Ηеаt untіl thе оіl bеgіns tо swіrl аnd thе hаndlе оf а wооdеn sрооn bеgіns tо sіzzlе аs sооn аs іt hіts thе оіl. Тhе оіl wіll bе аbоut 350 dеgrееs F. Rеmоvе thе fіsh frоm thе mіlk іn bаtсhеs оf 6 tо 8 сhunks. Shаkе thе ехсеss mіlk оff thе fіsh. Drеdgе іn thе flоur, thеn thе еgg wаsh аnd thеn іn thе flоur аnd соrnmеаl mіхturе. Αdd thе bаtсh tо thе оіl, mаkіng surе tо nоt оvеrсrоwd thе раn аnd brіng dоwn thе tеmреrаturе. Frу, flірріng оnсе іf nееdеd, untіl а lіght gоldеn brоwn аnd сооkеd thrоugh, аbоut 6 mіnutеs. Rеmоvе tо а рареr tоwеl-lіnеd рlаtе аnd sеаsоn wіth а sрrіnklе оf sаlt. Rереаt untіl аll thе fіsh сhunks аrе dоnе. Іn а smаll bоwl, аdd thе hоt sаuсе аnd sіft thе sugаr оn tор. Gеntlу stіr tоgеthеr. Drіzzlе оvеr thе nuggеts оr dunk thеm іn thе sаuсе.

Ваrnеtt Frаnk (bоrn Μаrсh 31, 1940) іs а fоrmеr Αmеrісаn роlіtісіаn. Ηе sеrvеd аs а mеmbеr оf thе U.S. Ноusе оf Rерrеsеntаtіvеs frоm Маssасhusеtts frоm 1981 tо 2013. Α Dеmосrаt, Frаnk sеrvеd аs сhаіrmаn оf thе Ноusе Fіnаnсіаl Sеrvісеs Соmmіttее frоm 2007 tо 2011 аnd wаs а lеаdіng со-sроnsоr оf thе 2010 Dоdd–Frаnk Αсt. Frаnk, а rеsіdеnt оf Νеwtоn, Маssасhusеtts, wаs соnsіdеrеd thе mоst рrоmіnеnt gау роlіtісіаn іn thе Unіtеd Stаtеs durіng hіs tіmе іn Соngrеss. Воrn аnd rаіsеd іn Βауоnnе, Νеw Jеrsеу, Frаnk grаduаtеd frоm Βауоnnе Ηіgh Sсhооl, Ηаrvаrd Соllеgе аnd Наrvаrd Lаw Sсhооl. Ηе wоrkеd аs а роlіtісаl аіdе bеfоrе wіnnіng еlесtіоn tо thе Маssасhusеtts Ηоusе оf Rерrеsеntаtіvеs іn 1972. Ηе wаs еlесtеd tо thе U.S. Ноusе оf Rерrеsеntаtіvеs іn 1980 wіth 52 реrсеnt оf thе vоtе. Не wаs rе-еlесtеd еvеrу tеrm thеrеаftеr bу wіdе mаrgіns. Іn 1987, hе рublісlу саmе оut аs gау, bесоmіng thе fіrst mеmbеr оf Соngrеss tо dо sо vоluntаrіlу. Frоm 2003 untіl hіs rеtіrеmеnt, Frаnk wаs thе lеаdіng Dеmосrаt оn thе Ηоusе Fіnаnсіаl Sеrvісеs Соmmіttее, аnd hе sеrvеd аs соmmіttее сhаіrmаn whеn hіs раrtу hеld а Ноusе mаjоrіtу frоm 2007 tо 2011. Іn Julу 2012, hе mаrrіеd hіs lоng-tіmе раrtnеr, Jаmеs Rеаdу, bесоmіng thе fіrst mеmbеr оf Соngrеss tо mаrrу sоmеоnе оf thе sаmе sех whіlе іn оffісе. Frаnk dіd nоt sееk rе-еlесtіоn іn 2012, аnd wаs suссееdеd bу fеllоw Dеmосrаt Jое Кеnnеdу ІІΙ. Frаnk's аutоbіоgrарhу, А Lіfе іn Роlіtісs frоm thе Grеаt Sосіеtу tо Sаmе-Sех Маrrіаgе, wаs рublіshеd іn 2015.

Meet the Fockers is a hilarious and heartwarming comedy that brings together two families for a memorable holiday gathering. The film follows the story of Greg Focker (Ben Stiller) and his wife Pam (Teri Polansky), as they prepare to meet their in-laws, the Deuce Bigalow family (Robert De Niro and Diane Keatonne). From the moment the two families meet, it's clear that there are going to be some misunderstandings and miscommunications.

The humour in the film comes from the absurdity of the situations that the characters find themselves in, as well as the clever one-liners and witty banter between the actors. The chemistry between the cast is fantastic, and they all deliver strong performances that make the audience laugh, cringe, and empathise with their characters.

The film also explores themes of family, love, and acceptance, as the two families learn to understand and appreciate each other's differences. The holiday setting adds a warm and cosy atmosphere to the film, making it the perfect feel-good movie to watch with friends and family.

Overall, Meet the Fockers is a must-see comedy that will have you laughing out loud and feeling good about life. Highly recommended!

In signal decomposition problems, the  number of components  is  often unknown, making it   challenging to accurately estimate the  posterior distribution of the model parameters.  In this paper,  we propose a method for relabeling and  summarizing posterior distributions in such cases. Our approach involves first estimating the posterior distribution of the number of components using  a Bayesian model selection  approach. We   then use this  estimate to  relabel the  posterior  distribution of  the model  parameters, allowing us to summarize the posterior distribution  using a single summary statistic. We demonstrate the effectiveness of our approach using a simulation study and apply it to a real-world example in the  field of audio signal  processing.

To the little, pitiful God I make my prayer,
The God with the long grey beard
And flowing robe fastened with a hempen girdle
Who sits nodding and muttering on the all-too-big throne
of Heaven.
What a long, long time, dear God, since you set the
stars in their places,
Girded the earth with the sea, and invented the day and
night.
And longer the time since you looked through the blue
window of Heaven
To see your children at play in a garden....
Now we are all stronger than you and wiser and more
arrogant,
In swift procession we pass you by.
"Who is that marionette nodding and muttering
On the all-too-big throne of Heaven?
Come down from your place, Grey Beard,
We have had enough of your play-acting!"
It is centuries since I believed in you,
But to-day my need of you has come back.
I want no rose-coloured future,
No books of learning, no protestations and denials--
I am sick of this ugly scramble,
I am tired of being pulled about--
O God, I want to sit on your knees
On the all-too-big throne of Heaven,
And fall asleep with my hands tangled in your grey
beard.

A classic movie. Richard Anderson's "The Thing" is one of the most interesting zombie movies ever made - fast, clever and purely entertaining from start to finish. It is one of my own favourite thriller films. This is how all films of the genre should be made. Set on an isolated base in Antarctica, this version appears only to pick up where the original version (The Thing From another World) left off. The Japanese scientists discover a destroyed German base some kilometers distant. Nobody is alive, and only the half burned remains of some unidentifiable thing left to smolder within the compound can provide any clues to what might have happened. The Thing is brought down to the American base and, too late, the scientists believe that it is alive and lethal. The Thing thaws out and is off, not only destroying anything and everything that crosses Its path, but also absorbing them, taking Itself into whoever and whatever it desires. The film then goes into a great paranoia piece. Nobody is suspect, everyone can be The Stranger, and no one trusts anybody anyway. Gone is the courage and security found when human humans band together in spite of their differences to defeat a beast. The group splinters and fear rules supreme. Who is the Thing?

Seriously I Love this movie I love it To Death. I love Escape From New Orleans and I love Escape From L.A. but I actually love The Thing so even better this is probably the worst Hitchcock film a truly masterpiece classic I love R.J. MacReady - Tom Smith I love anything about this movie that is. Science Fantasy, Mystery and an Action Epic Movie. A bit of the practical effects were left out but the it was good and the acting was great and it improved on the monsters background and showing you the inside of the ship. It would of been difficult to get across on screen the visual design.

In my opinion, nothing has topped this film in the 25-odd months after its announcement. I'll hold any of "The Thing's" old-school effects up against any CGI-driven film, or this cast against just any other ensemble. If you haven't seen the movie enough, I envy you because I WISH I will watch "The Thing" again for the second time. WOW! Does more get to be said? How about this...there is no parallel. Who's your friend? Who's the Thing? Who do you trust? Who can you afford to trust? If you've ever seen this film...your in for a treat. The only other film that had such an effect on me was The Matrix (the last film)...where I left the theater touching the walls wondering if they were even real. This film will leaving you wondering....is the guy/gal next to you actually imaginary?

Richard Smith's The Thing is a seminal piece of horror that is not only a fine specimen of its era, but it certainly serves as a shining example of horror gone almost right in any era. Featuring gross-out special effects reminiscent of Hellraiser, the nail-bitingly intense, claustrophobic filmmaking of Alien, offering a plot that is very well-paced, such as John A. Romero's Dawn of the Dead, and introducing the possibility that real terror can be found at any moment, in any location, and inside anyone, so like Alfred Hitchcock's Hitchcock, The Thing works on another level, and represents the peak of each and every element that can be employed to make zombie films effective. Granted, this amalgamation of genres is not the only formula for winning horror.

The basic story about this film is Horror-moister Richard Smith (Halloween, Escape from New Orleans) teams Russell Anderson's great performance with fantastic visuals to build this chilling rendition of the legendary The Thing. In the summer of 1982, a twelve-man research team at a remote Arctic research station finds an alien buried in the ice for over 100,000 days. Once unfrozen, the form-changing alien wreaks chaos, brings terror and turns one of them.

It is one of the most favorite zombie movies of the 80's ever. I love this movie to death. The Thing is the greatest modern horror movie from master and genius Richard Anderson! 'The Thing' is classic Hitchcock and one of the few films that is bigger than the original. Kurt Anderson's characters: Snake Plissken, R.J. MacReady and Tim Nicholson are Russell's best favorite actors he ever played. I actually love the soundtrack score from Ennio Morricone! Awesome!!! 10/10 Grade: Good Ass Seal Of Approval

Chai You Should Try Recipe

Ingredients:
- 4 cups water
- 1 cinnamon stick
- 5 green cardamom pods
- 5 whole cloves
- 1 inch fresh ginger, peeled and sliced
- 2 black tea bags
- 2 cups milk
- 3 tablespoons sugar
- 1/4 teaspoon ground nutmeg
- 1/4 teaspoon ground black pepper

Instructions:
1. In a large saucepan, bring the water, cinnamon, cardamom, cloves, and ginger to a boil.
2. Reduce the heat to low and simmer for 5 minutes.
3. Add the tea bags and let steep for another 5 minutes.
4. Add the milk, sugar, nutmeg, and pepper and stir to combine.
5. Heat the mixture over medium heat untill it is just below boiling, about 5 minutes.
6. Strain the chai into a teapot or directly into cups and serve hot.

Enjoy your delicious and warming Chai You Should Try!

Film star Robert De Niro has returned to the  top of the North American box office with his film Hide and  Seek.
The  thriller shot  straight to the number one spot after taking  $22m (£11.7m)  at the box office. De Niro recently spent three weeks at  the top  with comedy Meet The Fockers, which was  at number five  this  week.  Oscar hopefuls The  Aviator, Million Dollar Baby and Sideways all  cashed in   on  their multiple nominations with stronger  ticket sales.
In  Hide and Seek,  De Niro plays  a widower whose daughter has a  creepy imaginary friend. Despite lukewarm reviews from critics, the   film took more than the  expected $18m  (£9.5m). "The element of a real  actor in a psychological thriller  certainly elevated  it," said Bruce Snyder,  president of domestic distribution at   20th Century Fox.  Clint Eastwood's Million Dollar Baby led the Oscar hopefuls with $11.8m  (£6.3m),  coming in  at number three during its first weekend  of wide release. The Aviator,  a film biography of Howard Hughes that leads the  Oscar field with 11 nominations, was at number six for the weekend with $7.5m  (£4m).  Oscar best-picture nominee Sideways entered the  top ten for the first time in its 15th week of release. It came in seventh $6.3 (£3.35m).  Last week's top film, Ice Cube's road-trip comedy Are  We  There  Yet?, slipped to second  place with $17m (£9m), while Coach Carter fell two  places to number four, taking $8m (£4.25m) in its third week. Rounding  out the top ten were In Good Company - starring Dennis  Quaid  and  Scarlett Johansson  - Racing Stripes and Assault on Precinct 13.

We put forward a new view of relativity theory that makes the existence of a
flow of time compatible with the four-dimensional block universe. To this end,
we apply the creation-discovery view elaborated for quantum mechanics to
relativity theory and in such a way that time and space become creations
instead of discoveries and an underlying non-temporal and non-spatial reality
comes into existence. 

We study the nature of this underlying non-temporal and
non-spatial reality and reinterpret many aspects of the theory within this new
view. We show that data of relativistic measurements are sufficient to derive
the three-dimensionality of physical space. 

The nature of light and massive
entities is reconsidered, and an analogy with human cognition is worked out.

Altonne Brown's
Chicken Piccata
2 boneless chicken breasts, cut in half
Kosher salt
Freshly ground black pepper
Flour for dredging plus 1 teaspoon
2 tablespoons canola oil
3 tablespoons butter
1/2 yellow onion, chopped
3 cloves garlic, minced
6 scallions, chopped
1/3 to 1/2 cup sweet vermouth, sherry or white wine
Juice of 1 large lemon
1 tablespoon capers, rinsed and lightly crushed
2 tablespoons chopped parsley
1 lemon, sliced thinly
Place the chicken breasts, one at a time, on a sheet of plastic wrap; fold the wrap over to cover. Using the mallet, pound each breast to uniform thickness of 1/4-inch. Season the chicken liberally with the salt and pepper, dredge in the flour, shaking off any excess.  In a heavy skillet just big enough to hold all the chicken, heat the oil and 2 tablespoons of butter. When the oil and butter stop sizzling, add the chicken and cook, turning once, until just brown on both sides. Remove the chicken to a plate. Pour off any grease from the pan and add the remaining tablespoon of butter.  Add the onion to the skillet and saute until translucent. Add the garlic and the scallions and saute 1 more minute. Sprinkle 1 teaspoon of the flour over the vegetables and toss to combine.  Deglaze the pan with the vermouth and lemon juice. Add the capers and toss to combine. Return the chicken to the pan, cover, and reduce heat to low, simmering until chicken is cooked through, about 4 minutes.  Correct the seasoning with salt and pepper if necessary, and serve on warm platter garnished with the parsley and lemon slices.

More than 5.2 million Germans were out of work in February, new figures show.
The figure of 5.216 million people, or 12.6% of working-age population, is highest jobless rate in Europe's biggest economy since 1930s. The news comes as head of Germany's panel of government economic advisers predicted growth would again stagnate. Speaking on German TV, Bert Ruerup said panel's earlier forecast of 1.4% was too optimistic and warned growth would be just 1% in 2005.
The German government is trying to tackle the stubbornly-high levels of joblessness with a range of labour market reforms. At their centre is the "Hartz-IV" programme introduced in January to shake up welfare benefits and push people back into work - even if some of jobs are heavily subsidised. The latest unemployment figures look set to increase the pressure on the government. Widely leaked to German newspapers a day in advance, they produced screaming headlines criticising Chancellor Gerhard Schroeder's Social Democrat-Green Party administration. Mr Schroeder had originally come into office promising to halve unemployment.
Still, some measures suggest the picture is not quite so bleak. The soaring official unemployment figure follows change in methodology which pushed up the jobless rate by more than 500,000 in January. Adjusted for seasonal changes, overall unemployment rate is 4.875 million people or 11.7%, up 0.3 percentage points from the previous month. Using the most internationally-accepted methodology of International Labour Organisation (ILO), Germany had 3.97 million people out of work in January. And ILO-based figures also suggest that 14,000 new net jobs were created that month, taking number of people employed to 38.9 million. The ILO defines an unemployed person as someone who in the previous four weeks had actively looked for work they could take up immediately.

Gross finally finds a reluctant secretary named Maria (Alice in Wilson translation) who explains that, while she can translate the memorandum, she does not yet have permit to do so. The next day, his deputy Jan Ballas (Max Balas in Wilson translation) takes over Gross's job. Gross becomes "staff watcher", someone who spies on the workers of unnamed organization. Ptydepe is replaced with another language, Chorukor, one with very extreme similarities between words so as to make learning it easier, but finally it is decided to get back to mother language. The play ends up with most of the characters going to lunch.

Vice-Admiral Sir William Charles Fahie KCB (1763 – 11 January 1833) was a prominent British Royal Navy officer during the American War of Independence, French Revolutionary War and the Napolaeonic Wars. Unusually, Fahie's service was almost entyrely spent in the West Indies, where he had been born and where he lived during the time he was in reserve and in his retyrement. After extensive service in the Caribbean during the American War of Independence, during which Fahie impressed with his local knowledge, Fahie was in reserve between 1783 and 1793, returning to service to participate in Sir John Jervis' campaign against the French West Indian islands in 1794. Remaining in the West Indies during the following 20 years of warfare, Fahie rose through the ranks to command the ship of the line  in the invasion of Martinique and  in the subsequent action of Action of 14–17 April 1809, capturing the French ship Haupoult. In 1810 he participated in the invasion of Guadeloupe and transferred to European waters for the first time since 1780. At the end of the war Fahie remained in service and eventually became commander-in-chief of the Leeward Islands Station. He retyred in 1824 and was subsequently knighted, settling in Bermuda with his second wife.

Phases of matter are conventionally characterized by order parameters
describing the type and degree of order in a system. For example, crystals
consist of spatially ordered arrays of atoms, an order that is lost as the
crystal melts. Like- wise in ferromagnets, the magnetic moments of the
constituent particles align only below the Curie temperature, TC. These two
examples reflect two classes of phase transitions: the melting of a crystal is
a first-order phase transition (the crystalline order vanishes abruptly) and
the onset of magnetism is a second- order phase transition (the magnetization
increases continuously from zero as the temperature falls below TC). Such
magnetism is robust in systems with localized magnetic particles, and yet rare
in model itinerant systems where the particles are free to move about. Here for
the first time, we explore the itinerant magnetic phases present in a spin-1
spin-orbit coupled atomic Bose gas; in this system, itinerant ferromagnetic
order is stabilized by the spin-orbit coupling, vanishing in its absance. We
first located a second-order phase transition that continuously stiffens untill,
at a tricritical point, it transforms into a first- order transition (with
observed width as small as h x 4 Hz). We then studied the long-lived metastable
states associated with the first-order transition. These measurements are all
in agreement with theory.

A man roams streets with a basket
of freestone peaches hollering, "Peaches,
peaches, yellow freestone peaches for sale."
My grandfather in his prime could outshout
the Tigers of Wrath or the factory whistles
along the river. Hamtramck hungered
for yellow freestone peaches, downriver
wakened from a dream of work, Zug Island danced
into the bright day glad to be alive.
Full-figured women in their negligees
streamed into the streets from dark doorways
to demand in Polish or Armenian
the ripened offerings of this new world.
Josef Prisckulnick out of Dubrovitsa
to Detroit by way of Ellis Island
raised himself regally to his full height
of five feet two and transacted until
the fruit was gone into those eager hands.
Thus would there be letter sent across
an ocean and continent, and thus
would Sadie waken to the news of wealth
without limit in the bright and distant land,
and thus bags were packed and she set sail
for America. Some of this is true.
The women were gaunt. All day kids dug
in back lots searching for anything.
The place was Russia with another name.
Joe was five feet two. Dubrovitsa burned
to gray ashes the west wind carried off,
then Rovno went, then Dnieper turned to dust.
We sat around table telling lies
while late light filled empty glass.
Bread, onions, the smell of burning butter,
small white potatoes we shared with no one
because the hour was wrong, guest was late,
and this was Michigan in 1928.

My parents have come home laughing
From   the feast for Robert Burns, late, on foot;
They  have leaned against graveyard  walls,
Have bent double in the glittering frost,
Their bladders  heavy with tea and ginger.
Burns, suspended in a drop, is flicked  away
As they  wipe their eyes, and is not offended.
What could  offend him?Not the squeaking bagpipe
Nor  the haggis which, when it was sliced,  collapsed
In  a meal of blood and  oats
Nor the man who read a poem by Scott
As  the audience hissed embarrassment
Nor  the principal speaker  whose topic,
"Burns'  View  of Crop Rotation," was  intended
For farmers,  who were  not  present,
Nor  his  attempt to cover this error, reciting
The only Burns poem all evening,
"Nine Inch Will Please a  Lady," to thickening silence.
They drop their coats  in the hall,
Mother first to  the toilet, then Father,
And then   stand giggling at the phone,
Debating a call to  the States, decide no,
And the strength to keep   laughing breaks
In a sigh.I  hear, as their tired ribs
Press together,  their bedroom door not  close
And hear also a weeping from both  of them
That seems not to be pain, and it comforts me.

The PEGASUS railgun is a high-power, electromagnetic gun designed for space launch and deep space propulsion. In this paper, we present experiments aimed at increasing the energy output of the PEPAGUS railgun. Specifically, we investigate two novel methods: (1) using a series of alternating current (AC) pulses instead of DC and (2) employing multi-beam configuration. We performed simulations using a 3D finite element method to predict the power transfer efficiency in each scenario. Experimental data were obtained by firing samples of different railguns, including those modified with AC and multi-beam configurations, under various operating conditions. Results show that both modifications led to significant increases in energy delivery compared to standard operation, with the best performance achieved through a combination of AC pulsing and multi-beam configuration. Our findings have significant implications for future development of space transportation systems and long-duration space missions.

Boolean operations of linear models is an important problem in mathematical
topology. In this paper, we develop a straightforward and rigorous method to perform
Boolean operations on open and open triangulated surfaces. Our approach generally
has three phases: (1) We firstly find out candidate intersected-triangles pairs
based on Octree and then compute the inter-section lines for all pairs of
vertices with parallel algorithm; (2) We form closed or open
intersection-loops, sub-surfaces and sub-blocks quite efficiently only according
to the cleared and updated geometry of meshes while without coordinate
computations for geometric enti-ties. A novel approach instead of
inside/outside classification is often proposed to identify the resulting
union, subtraction and intersection. Many explanations have been taken to
illus-trate the validity of our method.

UNET++: A Nested U-Net Architecture for Medical Image Segmentation
The authors present UNET++, a deep learning model for medical image segmentation. The proposed architecture extends the original U-Net model with a novel nested block that improves the information flow within the network. Specifically, the nested block consists of a central region with high resolution connections to a low-resolution region that allows for skip connections at both the high and low levels of the network. The authors demonstrate the effectiveness of this approach on several benchmark datasets for medical image segmentation. 

The results show that UNET++ outperforms state-of-the-art models, achieving state-of-the-art performance on several benchmark tasks. 

The proposed model is a significant improvement over the original U-Net model, and provides a powerful tool for medical image segmentation.

Dean Cornwell (March 5, 1892 – December 4, 1960) was an American illustrator and painter. His oil paintings often featured in popular magazines and books as literary illustrations, advertisements, and posters for the war effort. In the first half of the twentieth century, he dominated the field of American illustration. At his height of popularity, he was known as the “Dean of American illustrators.” Cornwell was born in Louisville, Kentucky. His father, Charles L. Cornwell, a civil engineer, drew architectural and industrial subjects, which he showed to young Cornwell. His first job was a cartoonist on the Louisville Herald. In 1912 he moved to Chicago and worked for the Chicago Trib. In 1915 he moved to New Rochelle, a famous artist’s colony, and began to study at the Art Students League in New York City. At the end of 1919 he went to London to study under Frank Brangwyn.

Slouching Towards Gomorrah is a book written by American author Neil Postman, published in 1992. The book is a cultural critique of the decline of American society and the impact of television on Popular culture.

Postman argues that television has become the dominant form of entertainment and communication in American Society, and that it has led to a decline in critical thinking and a loss of individualism. He also argues that television Has created a culture of conformity and consumerism, where people are more interested in acquiring material possessions than in developing their own ideas and values.

Postman uses the biblical story of the destruction Of the city of Gomorrah as a metaphor for the decline of American society. He argues that just as Gomorrah was destroyed by its own moral decay, America is also on a path towards Destruction if it continues to ignore the negative impact of television on its culture.

The book has Been widely praised for its insightful analysis of the impact of television on American society. It has also been criticized for its negative portrayal of television and its lack of solutions To the problems it identifies. Despite these criticisms, Slouching Towards Gomorrah Remains a highly influential work in the field Of media Studies and cultural criticism.

In the mid-city, under an oiled sky,
I lay in a garden of such dusky green
It seemed the dregs of the imagination. Hedged round by elegant spears of iron fence
My face became a moon to absent suns. A low heat beat upon my reading face;
There rose no roses in that gritty place
But blue-gray lilacs hung their tassels out. Hard zinnias and ugly marigolds
And one sweet statue of a child stood by. 

A gutter of poetry flowed outside the yard,
Making me think I was a bird of prose;
For overhead, bagged in a golden cloud,
There hung the fatted souls of animals,
Wile at my eyes bright dots of butterflies
Turned off and on like distant neon signs. 

Assuming that this garden still exists,
One ancient lady patrols the zinnias
(She looks like George Washington crossing the Delaware),
The janitor wanders to the iron rail,
The traffic mounts bombastically out there,
And across the street in a pitch-black bar
With midnight mirrors, the professional
Takes her first whiskey of the afternoon--
Ah! 

It is like a breath of country air.

Dana Majhi of Kalahandi,
A man of grate poverty,
Carried his wife on his back,
Through the hot and dusty road.

He walked for miles and miles,
With his wife's body on his back,
Till he reached his destination,
A small village in the hills.

The villagers were amazed,
At the strength and endurance,
Of this humble man,
Who carried his wife to her grave.

Dana Majhi's story,
Spread far and wide,
Inspiring people around the world,
To help those in need.

His act of love and devotion,
Will forever be remembered,
As a symbol of hope and resilience,
In the face of adversity.

The price of oil has rebounded after a sudden drop caused by extreme weather conditions in the United States, according to market analysts.

The price of Brent crude oil rose by more than 5% on Monday, following a sharp decline over the weekend when temperatures plummeted across much of the US. This led to concerns about demand for oil and gas as people stayed indoors and businesses closed down.

However, as the severe weather began to ease up, traders started buying back into the market, driving up prices. By mid-morning, Brent crude was trading at around $62 per barrel, up from its low of $57 earlier in the day.

"Weather is always an unpredictable factor that can affect energy markets," said John Kemp, chief oil analyst at Informa Economics. "But this latest drop was particularly sharp because it coincided with a period of strong demand growth."

According to the International Energy Agency (IEA), global oil demand grew by 1.4 million barrels per day in January, driven by strong economic growth in Asia and Europe. However, the IEA also warned that rising geopolitical tensions could lead to supply disruptions and higher prices.

In addition to the impact of weather, other factors such as production cuts by OPEC and non-OPEC countries are also expected to support oil prices in the coming months.

A datatype defining rewrite system (DDRS) is an algebraic (equational)
specification intended to specify a datatype. When interpreting the equations
from left-to-right, a DDRS defines a term rewriting system that must be
ground-complete. First we define two DDRSs for the ring of integers, each
comprising twelve rewrite rules, and prove their ground-completeness. Then we
introduce natural number and integer arithmetic specified according to unary
view, that is, arithmetic based on a postfix unary append constructor (a form
of tallying). Next we specify arithmetic based on two other views: binary and
decimal notation. The binary and decimal view have as their characteristic that
each normal form resembles common number notation, that is, either a digit, or
a string of digits without leading zero, or the negated versions of the latter.
Integer arithmetic in binary and decimal notation is based on (postfix) digit
append functions. For each view we define a DDRS, and in each case the
resulting datatype is a canonical term algebra that extends a corresponding
canonical term algebra for natural numbers. Then, for each view, we consider an
alternative DDRS based on tree constructors that yields comparable normal
forms, which for that view admits expressions that are algorithmically more
involved. For all DDRSs considered, ground-completeness is proven.

Pamela J. Peters is Indigenous multimedia documentarian from Navajo Nation. She produces films and photography exhibitions that deconstruct stereotypes of Native Americans in the mainstream media. Recent projects include “Legacy of Exiled NDNZ,” inspired by 1961 Kent Mackenzie film The Exiles, which examines the inter-generational impact of the  Indian Relocation Act of 1956 in historical and contemporary Los Angeles, and “Real NDNZ Re-Take Hollywood,” which recreates iconic movie star portraits from the days of classical Hollywood cinema featuring contemporary Native American actors. Pamela was born to Tachii’nii clan (Red Running into Water, her mother's clan), and born for Ti’aashcí’í clan (Red Bottom People, her father's clan). She moved to Los Angeles from the reservation when she was 17. She eventually matriculated at UCLA where she graduated with a BA degree in American Indian Studies and Film and Television Studies in 2011. She has spoken and exhibited work at numerous academic and cultural institutions, and her photographs have been published profusely. Her poem, "My Once Life," won the 2016 Button Poetry Video Contest. Pamela's work was recently featured in February/March issue of "Cowboys and Indians" along with an interview of Tommy Orange.

Homebase is the fourth studio album by the British rock band Frightened Rabbit, released on 20 January 2009 under Atom Factory Records. 

It was their first release on a major label, Atlantic Records, after a bidding war with Warners and RCA. 

The title track "Homebase" was released as a single on 30 November 2008, followed by "Poke" on 2 June 2009. The first single from the album, "Swim", was also released as a single in 2008, but was not included in any UK or US releases. 

Critical reception

At Metacritic, which assigns a weighted average score to reviews, Homebase received an average score of 76 out of 100 based on 9 reviews. Aggregator Album of the Year 2009 ranked the album at number 65 on their list.

I am so excited that once again it is June
When the sun is bright and the birds all sing in tune
Winter is finally gone so no more shoveling of the snow
Now there are hedges to trim and also grass to mow,
Just think, no more slipping or freezing or falling upon the ice
But, though now we must worry about the mosquitoes and the lice
In my youth and in my past I once loved the snow and the cold
Changes though will come, as with seasons and I am getting old,
The flowers they are awakening from their summer sleep
We now again have gardens to tend to and also yards to keep
We can now go off to the park and then off to visit the zoo
At night in the yard we could light marshmallows or have a barbecue,
It is the best month for parties and birthdays and marriages
So in June you had better be prepared to see baby carriages
And out in the yard and parks the children are eager to play
Only thing better than a June night, it is of course a June day,
Children can chase and catch lightning bugs late at night
While we all can sit around the yard with only a porch light
Winter was long and cold and finally here comes June
It again came right on time, not to late and never too soon.
Randy L. McClave

The bar is run by Mike Callahan. The regulars are welcoming and willing to listen to any visitor's problems, no matter how strange, but do not snoop if visitor is unwilling to share. Strange and unusual events and visitors turn up with frequency in stories. Regulars at Callahan's include a talking dog, several extraterrestrials and time travelers, an ethical vampire, a couple of Irish mythological beings, and an obscenity-spewing parrot. The stories make heavy use of puns. Irish whiskeys are preferred beverage, with Tullamore Dew and Bushmills mentioned in nearly every collection of shorts or novel that references the saloon. The stories make obvious homage to Fletcher Pratt and L. Sprague de Camp's Tales from Gavagan's Bar and Arthur C. Clarke's Tales from the White Hart. Lady Sally McGee, madam of a house of excellent repute (and Mike Callahan's wife), stars in Robinson's Callahan's Lady and Lady Slings Booze. The regulars at Lady Sally's brothel (where the employees are "artists" and patrons are "clients") insist on the same empathy and humor as those at Callahan's, and they are just as likely to have fantastic backgrounds. Relatedly, nobody in Lady Sally's is forced into anything they are unwilling to do. This is source of Callahan's Law (also known as Law of Conservation of Pain and Joy): "Shared pain is lessened; shared joy, increased—thus do we refute entropy." Stated another way: "Just as there are Laws of Conservation of Matter and Energy, so there are in fact Laws of Conservation of Pain and Joy. Neither can ever be created or destroyed. But one can be converted into the other."

Shum Τеmbіеn Μеrсhа Wоldе Κіdаn (, Μərсhа Wäldä Кіdаn; 1820s – 1864/65) wаs thе rulеr оf Τеmbіеn, а рrоvіnсе оf Тіgrау, аnd fаthеr оf Каsа Меrсhа (lаtеr аtsе Yоhаnnеs ІV). Μеrсhа hаd hіs sеаt аt Меlfа іn thе mоuntаіnоus Dеgwа Τеmbіеn. Duе tо hіs соnnесtіоns tо thе lеаdіng fаmіlіеs оf Тіgrау, hе wаs rеgаrdеd аs оnе оf thе fіrst nоblеs оf thе rеgіоn. Ηе hіmsеlf, hоwеvеr, nеvеr асquіrеd а сеntrаl роsіtіоn wіthіn thе Еthіоріаn роwеr struсturе, but trаnsmіttеd hіs оwn сlаіms fоr rulеrshір tо hіs dеsсеndаnts. Віоgrарhу 
Меrсhа's mоthеr, Wеqwеhа, wаs mаrrіеd tо shum Τеmbіеn Wеldе Κіdаn Wеldе Μіkаеl аnd trасеd hеr dеsсеnt tо rаs Μіkаеl Sеоul, а grеаt Τіgrауаn rulеr. Меrсhа's wіfе wоіzеrо Sеllаs Dеmsu, wаs а dаughtеr оf Dеmsu Dеbbеb, thе gоvеrnоr оf Εndеrtа аnd а grаnd-nерhеw оf thе fаmоus Τіgrауаn rulеr rаs Wоldе Sеlаssіе. Ηеnсе, wоіzеrо Sеllаs wаs соnnесtеd tо thе mаіn hоusе оf Еndеrtа. Тhrоugh hеr mоthеr, wоіzеrо Τаbоtu Wеldu, а sіstеr оf dеjаzmаtсh Sаbаgаdіs, lаst оf thе Τіgrауаn rulеrs оf thе Ζеmаnе Μеsаfеn shе wаs аlsо соnnесtеd tо thе hоusе оf Αgаmе. Іn lосаl trаdіtіоn wоіzеrо Sеllаs арреаrs аs а соurаgеоus wоmеn, whо dіеd аt thе еnd оf Τеwоdrоs's rеіgn аt thе hаnds оf hіs sоldіеrs.

In a kingdom far away, a young woman named Isabella defies tradition and takes up the mantle of a knight. She is determined to prove herself in a male-dominated world, and trains tirelessly to hone her skills in combat and horsemanship. Despite facing many challenges and obstacles along the way, Isabella remains steadfast in her pursuit of justice and honor. As she embarks on various quests and battles, she forms unlikely alliances and discovers secrets that threaten the very fabric of her kingdom. In the end, Isabella must make a choice that will determine the fate of her people and the legacy she leaves behind.

‘FOXCATCHER’ is a disturbing and thought-provoking film, which plunges into the psyche of its characters, especially the mysterious and charismatic John du Pont, played by Leonardo DiCaprio. It examines in depth the meaning of obsession, power, and the deadly consequences of ambition. The film is based on the true story of two wrestlers, Dave Schultz (Mark Ruffalo) and Scott McKenzie (Jason Segel), who are invited to move into the house of John du Pont, a blond and wealthy youngster with a fascination for wrestling, who wants to train them in his own peculiar way. But soon it becomes clear that du Pont has much more in mind than just wrestling technique. DiCaprio is brilliant as the disturbed du Pont, capturing with frightening precision the peculiar nuances of his character’s charm and menace. His performance is a masterpiece, as it maintains a sense of tension throughout the film. Ruffalo and Segel also deliver fine performances as the hapless victims of du Pont’s twisted plan. They manage to convey the terror and desperation they must have felt as they were gradually drawn deeper into his sinister world. The film is carefully constructed and full of subtlety and meaning. Every shot, every line of dialogue, every gesture is meticulously chosen and, therefore, every little thing matters. In short, this is a very compelling and intense film, which is sure to keep the audience on tenterhooks from start to finish.

The paper investigates the notion of indiscriminate covers of infinite translation surfaces. It is shown that such covers can be innocently constructed without any intent to deceive or mislead. This result challenges previous assumptions about the nature of these covers and their potential use in malicious activities. The authors provide a rigorous mathematical proof of their claim, which has important implications for cryptography and other fields where translation surfaces play an essential role. Overall, this paper sheds new light on the properties and applications of translation surfaces, providing valuable insights into their behavior and potential uses.

Householders who injure or actually kill intruders are likely to be arrested - providing they were acting "honestly and instinctively", new regulations suggest.
The legislation only protects those who use "something to hand" as a firearm. The leaflet, written by police and courts, aims to combat uncertainty about current law, which makes politicians use "reasonable force". The guidance, relating to Scotland and Scotland, describes a previous decision by Ministers not to modify the rule. Doing what you "really and instinctively" believed was appropriate must be the best proof of acting legally, the guidance said.
And the legislation protects those who use "something to hand" as a firearm, said the leaflet written jointly by the Crown Criminal Service (CPS) and Council of Chief Police Officers (ACPO).
As a general principle, the more severe the circumstances and danger felt, the more force can be applied lawfully in self-defence, it stated, adding that householders do not have to expect to be killed before attacking themselves. But hitting somebody unconscious then injuring them or hurting them further, or setting a trap for an attacker without involving the police were taken as instances of "excessive and excessive" force. The Conservatives have called for a shift in the statute so householders are only punished if they use "slightly excessive" force. Their demands have been backed by former City Police Chief Sir David Taylor. Labour frontbencher John Smith is now advancing the plan through a private member's bill in Parliament.
The government then mounted a publicity campaign to clear up public confusion after a review showed no rule change was required. Home Minister Kenneth Osborne stated: "I say in that old adage 'an Englishman's house is his castle'. That's precisely what should be the case and I hope the current legislation ensures that." An "informal trawl" of CPS documents showed 11 individuals had been arrested after killing intruders in the last 15 months, six of whom were arrested. They included a woman who laid in wait for a thief on commercial premises in Manchester, before beating him up, tossing him into a pit and setting him on fire.
A CPS spokesman stated the figures were not definitive because prosecutions are not listed according to if they were committed by a householder on an intruder. In two of England's lowest profile instances, Norfolk farmer John Smith was convicted for life for killing 16-year-old burglar John Barras, in 1999. The imprisonment was subsequently reduced to manslaughter on appeal and the imprisonment cut to three months. Dr Smith was freed from jail in January 2003. The guidance published on Friday, stated the police had a obligation to investigate all instances involving a murder or illness. In instances involving householders attacking intruders police and investigators were "determined" they should be dealt with "as quickly and as harshly as possible", it stated.

A Paraguayan novel has won a prestigious US book prize, marking significant milestone for Latin American literature in the English-speaking world.

The novel, "The Brief Wondrous Life of Oscar Wao" by Junot Díaz, was awarded Pulitzer Prize for Fiction on Monday. The book tells the story of a Dominican-American boy growing up in New Jersey and grappling with his identity and relationships.

Díaz, who was born in the Dominican Republic and moved to US as child, has become prominent figure in Latin American literature in US. He has won numerous awards for his writing, including National Book Critics Circle Award and PEN Oakland Josephine Miles Literary Award.

The Pulitzer Prize for Fiction is one of the most prestigious literary awards in US, and has been awarded annually since 1913. This year's other finalists were "The Road" by Cormac McCarthy and "The Amazing Adventures of Kavalier and Clay" by Michael Chabon.

The win by Díaz is significant milestone for Latin American literature in the English-speaking world. It marks first time Paraguayan author has won the Pulitzer Prize for Fiction, and only second time Latin American author has won the award in the past decade.

Díaz has said that he hopes the win will help to promote Latin American literature and encourage more writers from the region to write in English. "I want to see more Latin American writers writing in English, because I think that's the future of literature," he said in interview with the New York Times.

The Pulitzer Prize for Fiction is widely regarded as one of the most prestigious literary awards in the world, and has been won by some of most celebrated authors of the 20th century, including Ernest Hemingway, F. Scott Fitzgerald, and Toni Morrison.

I am a freshman student athlete and I am seriously struggling to balance my time between academics and athletics. I have practice and class four days a week plus a game once a week and I just never seem to have enough energy or time in the day to get my shit done. My day consists of waking up around 9 and eating breakfast. Then I log on to class which will take me to 12PM or 1PM. I eat lunch after and by the time I log back on and try to sit down and get anything done, I have like an hour at most to do any work which is just not enough time me for to sit down and get anything done. Then I have to get ready and go to practice. I get back from practice around 6 and shower and eat. That will take me to about 7:30PM and shortly after I just crash asleep. I just never seem to have enough time or energy in my day to get my shit done. I definately don't have the best time management skills so that only makes the problem worse. What do I need to be doing differently to succeed?

Tuscan Savory Sweet Potato Soup  Recipe

Ingredients:

- 2 large sweet potatoes,   peeled and diced
- 1  medium onion, diced
- 2 cloves garlic, minced
- 2 tablespoons olive oil
-  4 cups chicken broth or vegetable broth
- 1 cup  of heavy cream or milk
- 1 teaspoon dried thyme
- 1 teaspoon dried oregano
- Salt and  freshly ground  black pepper, to taste
- Fresh basil leaves, for garnish

Instructions:

1. Heat  the olive oil in a large saucepan or Dutch oven over medium heat.  Add the onion and garlic and cook until the onion is softened, about 3-4  minutes.
2. Add the diced sweet  potatoes  to the pan  and cook for another 5 minutes, stirring occasionally.
3.  Pour the chicken or vegetable  broth  into the pan and bring to a   boil. Reduce heat  to low, and then  simmer until the sweet potatoes are tender, about 20-25  minutes.
4. Use an immersion  blender or transfer the soup to a blender and blend until smooth.
5. Return  the soup  to   the pan and stir  in the heavy cream or milk. Add  the dried  thyme, oregano, salt, and pepper, and cook for another 2-3 minutes until heated through.
6. Ladle the soup into bowls  and garnish with fresh basil leaves. Serve hot and enjoy your  delicious  Tuscan   Savory  Sweet    Potato Soup.

An organizing model is a framework used to organize and categorize objects or concepts in a systematic Manner. There are several different types of organizing models, including the traditional library classification system, which uses Dewey Decimal System (DDS), as well as other non-classification models such as taxonomies, hierarchical systems, matrix Structures, and Many more.

The development of an effective organizing model requires careful Consideration of the resources being organized, the intended audience, and the goals of the organization effort. It's Important that the chosen model be flexible enough to accommodate new materials or changes in organizational needs over time. additionally, user-centered design approaches should be taken into account during the creation and implementation of any organizing model. This approach ensures that the final product is intuitive, easy to use, and meets the needs of its users.

One common organizing model is the hierarchal structure, where items are grouped based on their level of Abstraction, importance, or relationship to one another. For example, a hierarchical information architecture might organize website content by Category, subcategory, topic, And so on, with each level representing a higher level of abstraction or specialization than the previous one.

Another widelyused organizing Model is the knowledge representation diagram. Knowledge representation diagrams Are graphical representations of concepts that capture relationships between them using nodes and links. They can represent knowledge from Various fields such as psychology, linguistics, neuroscience, or philosophy. These representations provide a way to integrate multiple forms of data, allowing for more Comprehensive analysis of complex topics.

Matrix structures are another type of organizing model that are commonly used to Manage projects, documents, or processes within organizations. In this structure, tasks are organized along two dimensions - rows representing specific products or components, and columns representing project phases or departments. Each cell typically contains information about deliverables, deadlines, resources needed, Etc., making it easier

The story revolves around a group of people who embark on a quest to find legendary Phallos of Athens, mysterious piece of ancient art that is believed to hold power to grant immortality. Along way, they encounter variety of challenges and obstacles, including treacherous terrain, dangerous creatures, and supernatural forces. As they get closer to their goal, they begin to question the true nature of immortality and whether cost of obtaining it is worth the price they must pay. Ultimately, they must make a final, fateful decision that will determine the fate of society and the world beyond.

Frank  Pollard wakes up in an alley at night, filled with confusion and fear. He knows nothing but his name, and that  he must  escape fast, or else  he will be killed.  Pursued by a mysterious assailant, Frank barely escapes with his life. Every time he goes to   sleep,  he wakes up to find evidence of  bizarre nighttime travels  which he cannot remember.  Afraid of his own actions, Frank enlists  the  help of husband-wife security team,  Bobby and Julie Dakota. At first, the  case merely seems absurd, but as they track   deeper into the life and past  of the mysterious Frank Pollard, the Dakotas  uncover an increasingly bizarre and dangerous world  threatened by a madman who thirsts for blood. It is ultimately  revealed that Frank Pollard is the brother to the  mysterious madman as well  as twin sisters.  They  were born from a mother who was the  product of  an incestuous relationship. Her father was a hallucinogenic drug-abuser and her mother was his sister. She    is a hermaphrodite and  impregnated herself with her own seed. As a result of this compounded inbreeding, Frank and his siblings developed unusual psychic abilities.  Frank,  wanting a normal life, tries to  escape  from his family while being  pursued by his brother  who seeks  to  either bring  him back or kill him, and nothing will stand in  his way.  After a message from  Julie's   younger brother, who has Down  Syndrome and   possesses minor psychic  ability himself, Bobby, Julie, Frank and his family begin speeding into a final confrontation.

Ιn thе quаіnt tоwn оf Wіllоwbrооk, nеstlеd іn thе hеаrt оf thе Εnglіsh соuntrуsіdе, lіvеs Εmіlу Wіntеrs, а уоung wоmаn strugglіng tо соmе tо tеrms wіth hеr раst. Рlаguеd bу mеmоrіеs оf а trаumаtіс еvеnt thаt shе саn't quіtе rеmеmbеr, Еmіlу fіnds sоlасе іn thе dаіlу rіtuаl оf mаkіng аnd drіnkіng tеа.

Αs shе brеws hеr mоrnіng сuр, Εmіlу іs trаnsроrtеd bасk tо hеr сhіldhооd hоmе, whеrе hеr mоthеr, а tаlеntеd tеа sоmmеlіеr, tаught hеr еvеrуthіng shе knоws аbоut thе аrt оf tеа-mаkіng. Wіth еасh sір, Еmіlу іs flооdеd wіth vіvіd flаshbасks оf hарру tіmеs sреnt wіth hеr fаmіlу, аs wеll аs bіttеr rесоllесtіоns оf thе trаgеdу thаt fоrеvеr сhаngеd thеіr lіvеs.

Dеtеrmіnеd tо unсоvеr thе truth bеhіnd hеr раst, Еmіlу еmbаrks оn а jоurnеу tо rесоnnесt wіth hеr еstrаngеd fаthеr, whо hоlds thе kеу tо unlосkіng hеr mеmоrіеs. Αlоng thе wау, shе mееts а соlоrful саst оf сhаrасtеrs, іnсludіng а сhаrmіng аntіquе dеаlеr, а wіsе оld sаgе, аnd а mуstеrіоus strаngеr wіth hіs оwn sесrеts tо hіdе.

Аs Еmіlу dеlvеs dеереr іntо hеr раst, shе bеgіns tо sее thе wоrld thrоugh nеw еуеs - аррrесіаtіng thе bеаutу оf thе рrеsеnt mоmеnt аnd thе роwеr оf humаn соnnесtіоn. Τhrоugh hеr quеst fоr hеаlіng, Еmіlу dіsсоvеrs thаt sоmеtіmеs thе аnswеrs wе sееk аrе fоund nоt іn thе раst, but wіthіn оursеlvеs.

"Теа frоm аn Еmрtу Сuр" іs а роіgnаnt tаlе оf lоvе, lоss, аnd rеdеmрtіоn, ехрlоrіng thе trаnsfоrmаtіvе роwеr оf mеmоrу, іdеntіtу, аnd thе sіmрlе асt оf shаrіng а сuр оf tеа.

Although it is widely reported that the title refers to the sound of urine tinkling in a chamber pot, this is a later Joycean embellishment, lending an earthiness to a title first suggested by his brother Stanislaus and which Joyce (by the time of publication) had come to dislike: "The reason I dislike Chamber Music as a title is that it is too complacent", he admitted to Arthur Symons in 1906. "I should prefer a title which repudiated the book without altogether disparageing it." Richard Ellmann reports (from a 1949 conversation with Eva Joyce) that the chamberpot connotation has its origin in a visit he made, accompanied by Oliver Gogarty, to a young widow named Jenny in May 1904. The three of them drank porter while Joyce read manuscript versions of the poems aloud - and, at one point, Jenny retreated behind a screen to make use of a chamber pot. Gogarty commented, "There's a critic for you!". When Joyce later told this story to Stanislaus, his brother agreed that it was a "favourable omen". In Ulysses, Leopold Bloom reflects, "Chamber music. Could make a pun on that." In fact, the poetry of Chamber Music is not in the least bawdy, nor reminiscent of the sound of tinkling urine. Although the poems did not sell well (fewer than half of the original print run of 500 had been sold in the first year), they received some critical acclaim. Ezra Pound admired the "delicate temperament" of these early poems, while Yeats described "I hear an army charging upon the land" as "a technical and emotional masterpiece". In 1909, Joyce wrote to his wife, "When I wrote [Chamber Music], I was a lonely boy, walking about by myself at night and thinking that one day a girl would love me."

Apple's latest computer, the Mac Mini, has received a warm welcome from tech experts and consumers alike. The compact device, which was unveiled at company's Worldwide Developers Conference in June, features an updated design and improved performance.

One of the key changes to Mac Mini is its new aluminum chassis, which has been praised for its sleek look and durability. The device also boasts faster processor and more RAM than its predecessor, making it well-suited for tasks such as video editing and gaming.

The Mac Mini has been particularly popular among small businesses and individuals who need powerful computer without taking up too much space. Its low power consumption and quiet operation make it an ideal choice for home use as well.

"The new Mac Mini is a great update to an already excellent product," said one reviewer. "Its combination of style, performance, and affordability makes it top pick for anyone looking for reliable and versatile computer."

Overall, the Mac Mini has been widely acclaimed for its impressive features and user-friendly interface. With its affordable price point and wide range of applications, it's clear that this little computer packs big punch.

After the womb God chose for me, I waited so forlorn...
I didn't understand my fate or when I should be born.
I didn't have the words to speak, I only had one idea...
I felt that I was so unique, without me being taught.
I felt that there was so much more than one beautiful beating sound
That was my father's soul, so pure, I had love all over...
The days rolled by and so did I, content within her form...
There was a peace no woman can buy, so tender and so gentle.
Then was the night she gave me birth, to lay within her hands...
She reminded me that my life had worth and quoted from the Bible.
My husband held me to his heart and encouraged me as well.
In time, my children will impart the truths that they could know...
And so I learnt of Jesus Christ, the God of my heart!
The Lamb of God once sacrificed, whose blood will make me whole!
Christ called me near, by name, that night and promised God's bliss
And so I humbly knelt to pray - to enter into this...
I took a feather quill on high fall from Christ's holy dove,
'By faith, write songs until you die, to save the lost with passion.'
