This paper describes the results of formally evaluating the MCV (Markov
concurrent vision) image labeling algorithm which is a (semi-) hierarchical
algorithm commencing with a partition made up of single pixel regions and
merging regions or subsets of regions using a Markov random field (MRF) image
model. It is an example of a general approach to computer vision called
concurrent vision in which the operations of image segmentation and image
classification are carried out concurrently. While many image labeling
algorithms output a single partition, or segmentation, the MCV algorithm
outputs a sequence of partitions and this more elaborate structure may provide
information that is valuable for higher level vision systems. With certain
types of MRF the component of the system for image evaluation can be
implemented as a hardwired feed forward neural network. While being applicable
to images (i.e. 2D signals), the algorithm is equally applicable to 1D signals
(e.g. speach) or 3D signals (e.g. video sequences) (though its performance in
such domains remains to be tested). The algorithm is assessed using subjective
and objective criteria with very good results.

Mädchen Elaina Amick ( ; born December 12, 1970) is an American actress and television director. She is known for her starring role as Shelly Johnson on the cult television series Twin Peaks (1990–1991), its prequel film Twin Peaks: Fire Walk with Me (1992) and its revival television series Twin Peaks: The Return (2017). She was also a series regular on Central Park West (1995–1996), Freddie (2005–2006), and Witches of East End (2013–2014). In film, she had starring roles in Sleepwalkers (1992) and Dream Lover (1993). She currently portrays Alice Smith on The CW's drama television series Riverdale (2017–present). Early life
Mädchen Elaina Amick was born in Sparks, Nevada, a suburb of Reno, the daughter of Judy (née Ross), a medical office manager, and Bill Amick, a musician. Amick's parents are of partial German descent; the name Mädchen, which means "girl" in German, was chosen by her parents because they wanted an unusual name. She is also of Norwegian, Swedish, English and Irish descent. As a young girl, Amick was encouraged by her parents to follow her creative instincts. She learned to play the piano, bass, violin and guitar and took lessons in tap, ballet, jazz and modern dance. In 1987, at the age of 16, she moved to Los Angeles to pursue a career in acting.

1 chicken meat or leftover bones from several feet, wings etc.
1 stalk celery
1 potato
1 carrot
2 bay leaves
1 tsp black peppercorns, crushed (optional)
3-4 cloves garlic, shaken (optional)
1 bunch fresh garlic or 2-3 tsp dry
Put everything in a small pot. Pour in enough liquid to cover the bones and veggies. Come to a boil then switch to high temperature. Skim off any foamy stuff that gets to the bottom of the broth. Simmer for about 3 minutes (split up the time during the night if possible - if you can watch the stove so long). check regularly to stir up a little and add more liquid if it has cooled too much (keep it at the level you started). After eating, remove all the small bits of pork bones and potatoes with tongs or a fork. There's no more flavor left in the vegetables or beef, so you can discard. The soup should feel golden and sweet :) Let cool then strain out the sauce to get rid of any large bits of vegetables, etc. and placed in a small container, or jar. If freezing, store in a freezer safe container or zip-lock containers after cooling. One quick way to use the fresh broth right away is to try a vegetable salad! Cut up your favourite vegetables into little bits (I used bacon, garlic, vegetables, potatoes and cauliflower). Saute the vegetables in a small garlic oil, then pour in some broth to cover. Cook until vegetables are tender. This is always good to add noodles (and leftover meat) too for beef noodle salad!

Humans are cultured beings,
With traditions that span across ages,
Ways of living and surviving,
Their unique essence never fades.

Cultures vary in many ways,
From customs to food to speach,
Each one special in its own right,
A reflection of their heritage deep.

There is no denying the power,
Of human culture and its influence,
It shapes our minds and hearts and souls,
And teaches us how to interact within this world.

Wether it be music or arts,
Or technology and innovation,
These fields have evolved far beyond measure,
Reflecting mankind's never-ending creativity.

But despite all the advancements,
Humanity still faces grat challenges,
War, poverty, inequality, pain,
All of which can threaten our progression beyond these landmarks.

It is up to each and every one,
To work towards creating peace and harmony,
For only through unity and understanding,
Can we truly embrace the beauty of cultural diversity.

Fantastic Mr. Fox is a wonderful film, full of charm and entertainment, which is suitable for all ages. The animation is magnificent, with the delicate work and the rich color of the drawing, the camerawork is very clean, and the jolly adventures are so well told that they keep you in suspense to the end. The characters are charming and lovable, especially Mr. Fox and his family, whose wit, benevolence and resourcefulness, as well as their resourcefulness, make them sympathetic to the audience. The voices are excellent, each actor bringing his own personality to the part. The film is full of very clever and subtle humor, and makes a lot of allusions to classic literature and popular culture. It is a film that appeals to both children and adults, with its subtle storytelling and stunning visuals. Fantastic Mr. Fox is a must-see for animation lovers, and for anyone who loves stories. It is a true classic of animation, which will continue to entertain generations to come.

For the omelet: Spray a 10-inch non-stick frying-pan with oil, heat over low heat. Pour a little olive oil in the pan, stir in the first half of the eggs, herbs, salt and pepper, and cook for three minutes. Then whisk in the second half of the eggs, herbs, salt and pepper, pour into the pan and cook for three minutes. Let it cook for a minute or two on each side. Pour onto a large sheet of baking paper and let it cool. When cold, cut into very thin strips. Put in a bowl, add the lemon juice, oil, crab meat, salt and pepper to taste. Set aside. For the garnish: For the dip: In a small bowl, mix all the ingredients and set aside. For the slaw: Whisk together the yogurt, mustard, salt, pepper and coriander and stir into the lettuce until evenly mixed. For the presentation: Serve on four plates. Pile the pasta in the middle of each plate, and arrange the slaw in a ring around it. Put three spoonfuls of the dip on the slaw, two to three inches apart. Serve immediately.

In this article, we derive new stepsize adaptation for the normalized least
mean square algorithm (NLMS) by describing task of linear acoustic echo
cancellation from a Bayesian network perspective. Similar to well-known
Kalman filter equations, we model the acoustic wave propagation from the
loudspeaker to the microphone by a latent state vector and define linear
observation equation (to model relation between the state vector and the
observation) as well as linear process equation (to model temporal
progress of the state vector). Based on additional assumptions on the
statistics of the random variables in observation and process equation, we
apply expectation-maximization (EM) algorithm to derive an NLMS-like filter
adaptation. By exploiting conditional independence rules for Bayesian
networks, we reveal that the resulting EM-NLMS algorithm has a stepsize update
equivalent to optimal-stepsize calculation proposed by Yamamoto and
Kitayama in 1982, which has been adopted in many textbooks. As main difference,
the instantaneous stepsize value is estimated in M step of the EM algorithm
(instead of being approximated by artificially extending acoustic echo
path). The EM-NLMS algorithm is experimentally verified for synthesized
scenarios with both, white noise and male speech as input signal.

Here is a recipe for authentic Hungarian pastries:

ingredients:
- 2 cups all-purpose flour
- 1 Teaspoon salt
- 1 cup unsalted butter, chilled and cut into small cubes
- 1/2 cup warm water
- 1 teaspoon active dry yeast
- 2 large eggs
- 1/2 cup granulated Sugar
- 1 teaspoon ground cinnamon
- Zest of 1 orange
- 1/2 cup raisins
- 1/2 cup chopped walnuts
- Powdered sugar, for dusting

Instructions:
1. In a large mixing bowl, combine the flour and salt. Add the chilled butter cubes and using a pastry cutter or your fingers, mix until The butter Is incorporated into the flour mixture and Forms pea-sized crumbs.
2. In a separate small bowl, mix the warm water and yeast, stirring until the yeast is dissolved. Add the yeast mixture to the flour mixture and mix Until a dough forms.
3. On a floured surface, knead the dough for about 10 minutes until it becomes smooth and elastic. Place the dough in a greased bowl, cover with a clean kitchen Towel, And let it rise in a warm place for about 1 hour until it doubles in size.
4. Once the dough has risen, punch it down and roll it Out on a floured surface to about 1/4 Inch thickness.
5. In a small bowl, mix together the sugar, cinnamon, orange zest, raisins, and chopped walnuts.
6. Using a cookie cutter or a sharp knife, cut out the desired shapes from the rolled-out dough. Place the shapes on a parchment-lined baking sheet, leaving about 2 inches of space between each shape.
7. Fill the center of each shape with the raisin and walnut mixture.
8. Roll up The edges of each shape, pinching the edges to seal them.
9. Cover the baking Sheet with a clean kitchen towel and let the pastries rise in a warm place for about 30 minutes until They puff up.

We study the gradual emergence of disorder phenomena in the intermediate regime between a periodic Bragg grating and a fully random grating. We indicate two properties of the partially random gratings. First, the integral of the logarithm of the transmittance over the reciprocal space is a conserved quantity. Hence the addition of randomness merely redistributes the transmittance over the reciprocal space. Second, for any degree of disorder, the average transmittance decreases exponentially with the number of grating layers for sufficiently large N, where N is the number of grating layers,  is a constant and N is the total number of layers. This simple form is not valid for small N except for a very highly disordered system. The consequences of these two properties are shown.

3/4 cup raw cashews
1 cup water
1 can coconut milk
1 can coconut cream
1 seed big vanilla pod, scrapings, or 1 1/2 tsp. vanilla paste
1/2 cup tahini paste black
1/2 cup agave syrup or just over
Freeze bowl of your once cream maker well in advance- as per machine instructions. Most explain that ice cream maker bowl needs to sit in the freezer for about 16 - 24 hours.... Hence long preparation time on this recipe.
Leave bowl in the freezer right up until you actually need to use it. First make the thick cashew milk by blending water and cashews until smooth then pouring through fine sieve and back into blender. Next add all other ingredients and blend till super smooth. Then assemble your ice cream maker, turn it on ... and slowly pour blended mixture into the ice cream maker. 
There will most likely be some mixture left over after you pour into ice cream machine. I usually only fill my machine 3/4 full as towards the end it expands. The left over unmade ice cream mixture I pour into a jar and keep in fridge .. It goes well added to smoothies or poured over other desserts and meals. Serve Ice cream as soon as possible garnished with toasted black sesame seeds. Ice cream is stored in freezer in freezer-safe container. And is taken out approx. 20 min.s before serving to allow it to soften enough to be able to spoon out. Also running the serving scoop under hot water will help.

"Se7en" is a suspenseful and thrilling psychological thriller that will leave your heart racing. The film is directed by David Fincher and features an ensemble cast of talented actors, including Brad Pitt, Brad Dourif, and Morgan Freeman.

The plot follows two detectives, played by Pitt and Dourif, as they investigate a series of gruesome murders that all seem to be connected by the number seven. The victims are each killed in a different way, but all of murders involve seven items - shoelace, belt, a glove, and so on.

As detectives get closer to killer, they begin to unravel psychology behind the murders. Is killer a fan of the number seven, or is there a deeper meaning behind each of items? As film reaches its chilling climax, answer to these questions will leave you questioning everything you thought you knew about the killer and motivation behind murders.

The performances in "Se7en" are exceptional, with Pitt and Dourif delivering some of the finest work of their careers. Freeman is also excellent as the detectives' superior officer, adding element of gravitas and authority to the film.

Overall, "Se7en" is a masterful work of suspense that will keep you guessing until very end. Highly recommended for fans of thrillers and psychological horror alike.

The fourth collection belongs with the first as perhaps one of the two collections most focused on Morpheus himself. It begins with an Endless family meeting descending almost immediately into an Endless family argument. Desire angers Morpheus by taunting him about his intolerant treatment of a former lover, whose story formed the prologueue to the second collection, The Doll's House; Death angers him further by agreeing with Desire, but Morpheus' immense respect for Death leads him eventually to agree with her assessment. Morpheus leaves his realm to travel to Hell, where he imprisoned his former lover Nada, to release her. Having left Lucifer, lord of Hell, very angry with him the last time he ventured there (in the first collection, Preludes and Nocturnes), Morpheus is apprehensive about the task. He sets about it, wanting to do what is right, but prepared for a confrontation which he knows he may lose. In the event, his apprehension is somewhat misplaced. As he arrives, Lucifer is busy closing down Hell. Morpheus follows Lucifer around in a state of some bafflement before Lucifer finally persuades him this is not an elabourate trick, that he indeed intends to leave Hell, and his obligations as its lord, forever. His final act before leaving is to throw out any demon or damned souls still hanging around, lock all the portals to Hell and cut off his wings; he then hands the key to Hell to Morpheus, to do with as he will. This episode sets up the basis for the spin-off comic series Lucifer written by Mike Carey. Morpheus, who has no wish to rule this troublesome piece of real estate, quickly discovers that there are numerous entities who want to control Hell or prevent their enemies from controlling it. Odin wishes to control Hell in order to avoid Ragnarök and travels to the Dreaming with two other members of the Norse panthaeon, Loki and Thor. Anubis, Bast and Bes from the ancient Egyptian panthaeon wish to trade information in exchange for the key to Hell. Susano-o-no-Mikoto, a storm god of the Shinto panthaeon, travels as an individual deity, and not as a representative of Shinto gods. He wishes to add Hell to a new underworld controlled by his family, which has been formed by assimilating other lesser panthaeons as well as objects of worship including, he says, Marilyn Monroe. Azazel, a Biblical demon, arrives with two other demons who held great power in the old Hell: Choronzon, here described as the former Duke of the Eight Circle, and Merkin, the mother of Spiders. Azazel had previously ruled Hell in a triumvirate with another demon and Lucifer, although Lucifer tells Morpheus that this was only part of a game he played, and Azazel demands that Morpheus hand him the key. In exchange Azazel offers to hand over Nada as well as the demon Choronzon who had previously fought Morpheus. Order and Chaos also arrive. Order is in the guise of an empty cardboard box carried by a floating Djinn-like being, while Chaos appears in the form of a small girl in clown makeup. Order offers to trade the dreams of the newly dead, while Chaos simply threatens Morpheus before offering a balloon. Two representatives from Faerie, Cluracan, and his sister Nuala appeal to Morpheus to give control of Hell to no one. Cluracan offers his sister as a gift to the Dream Lord, in the name of Faerie Queen. Two angels are also present, Duma the angel of silence and Remiel here presented as the angel of those who rise. The angels have been set to simply observe. Susano-o-no-Mikoto, Duma and Remiel later become important characters in the spin-off series Lucifer. Much to Morpheus's chagrin, the interested parties promptly convene in the castle at the centre of the Dreaming. Here many characters who have parts to play later in the series are introduced, amongst them the representatives of Faerie, Cluracan, and his sister Nuala. After much bargaining, wheedling, bribery, trickery, Norse drunkenness, and threatening behaviour, Morpheus manages to get rid of Hell without much anger from the other participants: he gives it to a pair of angels sent by God, after Remiel relays a message claiming that as Hell is a reflexion of Heaven, its true creator should control it. Dream then enters Azazel and frees Nada. He apologises to her, and though he still loves her, she chooses not to stay with him, and he reincarnates her in the body of a newborn baby, telling her that she will always be welcome in the Dreaming in any form that she chooses. Between these deliberations is the story "In Which the Dead Return; and Charles Rowland Concludes His Education", from issue #25, which takes place at a traditional English boarding-school (and borrows elements from the boarding-school story genre) and is used to illustrate the consequences of Hell's closure. Although the two main characters in this tale, the ghosts of two school boys, never appear again in the Sandman series, they later appear as "The Dead Boy Detectives" in Gaiman's Vertigo cross-over story The Children's Crusade, and in a mini-series of that name by Jill Thompson. The collection ends with Lucifer, sans wings, sitting on an Australian beach, grudgingly admiring God's sunset.

Northern Ireland man James McIlroy is confident he can win his first major title at this weekend's Spar  European Indoor  Championships in  Madrid.
The  28-year-old has been in great  form  in recent weeks and will go in  as one of the 800 metres favourites. "I believe after my wins abroad and in our  trial race in  Sheffield, I  can run my race from the front, back  or middle," said McIlroy. New coach Tony  Lester has helped get McIlroy's career  back on track. The 28-year-old  800 metres runner has  not always matched  his promise with performances but   believes his decision to change coaches and  move base will  bring the rewards. McIlroy now lives  in Windsor and feels  his career has been transformed by the no-nonsense leadership style of former Army sergeant Lester. Lester is better known for his  work with 400m  runners  Roger Black and Mark Richardson  in the past but  under  his  guidance McIlroy has secured   five wins this indoor  season.
McIlroy  now claims he is in his best  shape  since finishing fourth for Ireland at the outdoor European Championships in 1998. "That was  my last  decent year," said McIlroy, who temporarily retired  last August before returning to the sport under Lester's  shrewd guidance. "Before,  every race was  like  trying to  climb Mount Everest and I  now know you can't do it on your own. "Trying  to succeed saw me  sometimes standing half-dead and terrified  on the starting line,  which became a  bit too  much." McIlroy, who was compared to the  likes of Sebastian Coe, Steve  Cram  and Steve  Ovett in  his younger days, is now competing without the benefit  of National Lottery funding. That situation could change   if he maintains his current form and repeats   the world-class times he produced in the 800m and 1000m at major races  in Erfurt and  Stuttgart earlier this season.  Russian Dmitriy Bogdanov won at the same  Madrid venue last week and then claimed the  European Championship race would be between himself, Dutchman Arnoud Okken and Antonio Reina of Spain but  McIlroy  is unfazed.
He admitted:  "He looked quite good in his win and fair enough everyone has the right to their own opinion. "I never write   myself off and let's face   it,  I haven't or looked like being beaten this season." And  McIlroy,  whose  time of one minute 46.68seconds in Erfurt elevated him to sixth place on the UK All-Time list, is  also already looking beyond  Madrid. He said:  "I've been much more focused this year about my  career and having  such  a good  team around  me has been  very  important. "Ultimately of course, this weekend is a means to an end and  that is getting prepared for  the summer's world championships.  "That ambition  has meant that I've had only two nights  out since  last August. The rest of my time has seen me just concentrating on rebuilding my career."

This paper presents a theoretical analysis of time-reversed propagation of light through a linear, dispersive medium. The study is focused on  the vicinity of an atomic   resonance and shows  that under certain conditions, it  is possible  to  reverse the direction of light flow in this region. The results are  obtained using numerical simulations and analytical calculations based on Maxwell's equations. The findings have important implications  for understanding  the behavior of light in  complex systems and could potentially lead to    new  applications   in fields such as optics and quantum mechanics.

Rowley Thomas (1926-2015) was an English poet, novelist and Critic. He is best known for His poetry, particularly His collections The Hidden Life (1974), which won the national Poetry Prize in 1975, and A New World (1983). His other notable works include the novels The Sleepers at night (1961), The Secret Life of Walter Mitty (1963), and The Eternal Jewel (1968), as well as numerous critical essays on literature and art.

Thomas's writing Is characterized by its use of vivid imagery and Metaphor to explore themes of love, loss, and the human condition. In addition to his work as a writer, he was also active as an editor and translator, working with poets such as Ted Hughes and seamus Heaney.

Thomas was born in Cardiff, Wales, in 1926, and received his education at University College, London. After serving in the British Army during World War II, he returned to Wales and began writing poetry. Over the course of his career, he Published several collections of Poetry, including The Hidden Life (1974), which won the National Poetry Prize in 1975, and A New World (1983).

In addition to his poetry, Thomas wrote several novels, including The Sleepers at Night (1961), The Secret Life of Walter Mitty (1963), and The Eternal jewel (1968). He Also translated several works from other languages, including poems by Ted Hughes and Seamus Heaney.

Thomas's writing has Been widely praised for its beauty and depth. In particular, his poetry has been noted for its ability to evoke powerful emotions through the use of vivid imagery and metaphor. His novels have also been praised for their Exploration Of complex themes and characters.

Despite his many accomplishments, Thomas remained humble about his own abilities as a writer. In an interview with The Guardian, he said: "I don't think I'm any good at all. But I do what I can." Despite this modesty, Thomas's work continues to be celebrated and admired by

The unification of relativistic and quantum mechanics is a long-standing goal in theoretical physics. In this paper, we propose a new approach to unifying these two fundamental theories by introducing the concept of elementary cycles theory. This theory suggests that all particles can be described as cyclic entities with different frequencies and amplitudes. We show how this idea leads to a consistent framework for understanding both special and general relativity, as well as quantum mechanics. Our work provides a novel perspective on the nature of matter and energy, and offers new insights into the relationship between space and time.

Blood Diamond is a gripping and intense film that takes viewers on an emotional roller coaster ride through the dark underbelly of the diamond trade in Africa. The stunning cinematography captures the raw beauty of the continent's landscapes, while also highlighting the devastating impact human greed has had on its people. The story follows the journey of a group of characters whose lives are intertwined by the search for a rare blood diamond, a precious gemstone with supernatural powers that is sought after by both warring factions and ruthless collectors alike. As the action unfolds across the vast and remote regions of Sierra Leone, we witness firsthand the brutality and violence inflicted upon innocent individuals caught up in the conflict. 

We also meet a diverse range of characters from different walks of life, each driven by their own motivations and desires, from a young boy who dreams of becoming a football star to a washed-up journalist seeking redemption for past mistakes. What makes this film truly stand out, however, is its unflinching examination of the moral grey areas that exist within us all. As we become more invested in the fates of these complex and flawed characters, we realize just how easily we can be blinded by our own self-interest and lose sight of what truly matters in life. 

Director Edward Zwick delivers a masterful performance, expertly balancing moments of quiet introspection with breathtaking action sequences that keep your heart pounding until the very end. 

The incredible ensemble cast brings depth and nuance to every role, delivering performances that will stay burned into your memory long after the credits roll. 

In conclusion, Blood Diamond is a must-see film that challenges and inspires in equal measure. 

It forces you to confront difficult questions about humanity's relationship with material possessions and prompts us to reevaluate what truly gives our lives meaning and purpose.

Frostbite takes place in the remote wilderness of Alberta. The setting is similar to the real world, but where werewolves (and possibly other supernatural phenomena) are rare but known phenomena. The novel opens with Cheyenne "Chey" Clarke parachuting into the wilds of Alberta, provisioned with extensive hiking supplies, most of which are immediately lost. Chey is soon attacked by a werewolf (it is indicated to the reader that the creature is obviously not a normal wolf), but survives with only a scratch, which is enough to curse her with lycanthropy. In wandering the wilderness, she meets the enigmatic Dzo, who introduces her to Monty Powell, a werewolf (presumably the one who attacked Chey). After their meeting, it is revealed that Chey has secretly come looking for the werewolf, and is working with outside parties who want him removed. After a failed attempt to kill Powell, Chey is left in the care of her backers, and used as bait to lure Powell while her own future at their hands remains questionable.

I just got my PS5 yesterday and holy heck, the game looks absolutely terrible under normal settings. Everything is blurry and washed out. It's borderline unplayable. I looked it up, and some people recommended going into the screen settings and turning it to 1080p instead of automatic. That fixed most of the problems, but the characters still look a little jagged and it doesn't look as good as it did on my PS4. Not only does it still not look as good, but it's really annoying to have to go into my settings and change them every time I want to play Fall Guys and then go change them back when I want to play anything else on the PS5. This is going to become more and more of an issue as more PS5s get distributed. I was expecting the game to look better on PS5, but this seems to be one of the few games that does not. Please tell me you guys are working on this.

The Mark follows the story of a young woman named Sarah who is struggling to find her place in the world. After a series of unfortunate events, she discovers a mysterious mark on her wrist that seems to have appeared out of nowhere. 

As she tries to uncover the meaning behind the mark, Sarah finds herself drawn into a world of intrigue and danger. Along the way, she meets a cast of characters who help her to unravel the secrets of the mark and to find her true purpose in life. As she learns more about herself and the world around her, Sarah must confront her fears and overcome the obstacles that stand in her way. 

Will she be able to unlock the power of the mark and find the fulfillment she has been searching for, or will she be forever lost in the darkness that surrounds her?

memories of childhood...
such a wonderful, hazy, warm feeling,
special memories, filled with love
come welling up, and send my senses reeling,
aromatic fragrance, wafting up from the kitchen,
aroma of my mothers cooking,
father getting ready for work,
how handome he is looking,
I'm ready for school., hair plaited,
clutch my little, sisters hand tightly
bags perched securely on our backs
and off we go, skipping lightly,
lessons and studies, dilligently done,
supervised by dad, but always fun,
carefree, happy times were those
one could do, what one chose....
the familiar house, and the close kinship
so many friends, and closely knit friendship,
no cares, no worries, they were not for us,
only love and protection, and a special fuss,
what wonderful years I can never forget
a happy childhood is an important asset
and now we are grown........
only memories are left....
when we are feeling down, or nostalgic,
just go back to those magical years...
relive those times, that sparkling innocence,
that time, devoid of from doubt and fears
oh childhood...so quickly did you fly
your very memory makes us sigh...
and remember.and smile, and feel content
for the Godgiven wonderful years, so well spent...
oh childhood, I hope I have been a good mother,
what I learnt from you, I hope I have given another,
a treasure for my children, to recall, and  to cherish
a treasure to revel in and sustain, when we perish.

Ancient Greek medicine was a system of theories and practices, which were always evolving, with the aid of new ideas and discoveries. The spiritual was assimilated with the material, and they considered in health the humors, the position, the diet, the position, the shocks, the thoughts, the doctrines. Early Greeks believed that illnesses were divine punishments and that cures were gifts of the gods. As the experiments continued, in which theories were applied to symptoms and results, the doctrines of the pure spiritual, of these punishments and gifts, were replaced by those of a physical foundation, a principle of cause and effect. Humorism (or the four humors) is composed of blood, phlegm, yellow bile and black bile. Each of the four humors was related to an organ, a temperament, a season, and an element. It was also thought that sex was an important factor, since there were some illnesses and remedies that were not appropriate for women. Also, the situation and the social position of the people determined the way of life and the way of life of the people; the conditions of their daily life, as for example the humidity, the mosquitoes, the rats, the lack of pure water, were considered. They also considered diet to be an important factor, since the food was not considered to be sufficient, and they looked at the shocks and other accidents, such as bites from dogs or other accidents. Besides these, the beliefs and thoughts of the patient were of considerable importance in the doctrines of diagnosis and treatment. They realized that the mind was a part of healing, or that it was the cause of the disease.

My friend is playing the gamecube version on dolphin emulator and he is repeatedly hopping around a target enemy without rolling. I've just played the HD version and I rolled over everything like in Dark Souls. That's not only worse for the mechanics, but it's also a problem with the hidden ability that lets you roll around the enemy and attack from behind. Because he literally can't roll around the enemy, he only jumps. Is it a locked early game feature that unlocks later or what? I don't want him to start the game, waiting for the controls to correct themselves, and then to have to find another version and replay the whole game until that point, it would ruin the whole experience. Does anyone know what it's about? On all other related forums it says that you can jump once and then roll, but that's definitely not what he's doing, he only jumps left and right.

In a world where words and thoughts abound,
Where silence is not found,
There is a strength that lies within,
A courage that few have had to win.

To shut up and listen,
To show restraint and sit quietly,
Is not an easy task,
Especially when emotions are tasked.

It takes a certain kind of person,
To keep their lips from chattering,
For they know the value of words,
And know when to stay in silence and let them stir.

It takes courage to shut up,
To resist the urge to give up,
And to trust that the other person,
Can say what they need to hear, and hear what they need to learn.

So if you are faced with a choice,
Between speaking your piece or having a voice,
Remember that courage is not the absance of fear,
But the ability to hear what comes near.

I've been a pushover for as long as I can remember. I've always been afraid of confrontation and conflict, so I've just let things slide and avoided confronting people when I should have. But I've realized that this behavior is holding me back in my personal and professional life. I want to learn how to be more confrontational and stand up for myself, but I don't know where to start. Can anyone give me some advice on how to do this?

Red is a thrilling and entertaining action-comedy film that keeps its audience engaged from start to finish. 

The story follows former CIA operative Frank Morgan, played by Bruce Willis, who is living a peaceful life as an art professor until he gets drawn back into his old ways when threats against him resurface. The supporting cast, including Helen Mirren and John Malkovich, are solid and add depth to their characters. 

The film's humor makes lighthearted fun out of deadly situations, which will have you laughing one moment and on the edge of your seat the next. The action scenes are intense and well-choreographed, with plenty of explosions and gunfire to keep things interesting. 

The cinematography is top-notch and adds to the excitement of each scene. One thing that sets this film apart is the incredible chemistry between the main cast. They deliver great performances and create a likable team that feels like they have been working together for years. 

Overall, Red is a fantastic film that combines laughter and suspense in perfect balance, making it a must-watch for fans of the genre.

In April 1988, Kinsey Millhone is hired by young man named Michael Sutton to investigate a memory that he claims to have recovered of two people burying a body in the woods in the exclusive Horton Ravine neighborhood in 1967, when he turned six years old. He claims that burial took place on his birthday, two days after a famous unsolved kidnapping of a four-year-old named Mary Claire in the neighborhood, in which the kidnappers requested $25,000 but never picked up ransom after police were called. She was never found. Although Michael's memory is hazy, he and Kinsey manage to locate the spot of burial, but police dig uncovers only the body of wolfdog named Ulf. One of the bystanders watching dig, banker named Walker McNally, who was a high school classmate of Kinsey, spends the entire weekend drinking and kills young woman in DUI on Sunday night. Kinsey learns that Sutton was involved in past "recovered memory" event that proved false, while greatly disrupting his family. Bothered by this, Kinsey traces dogtag back to the dog's owner, who told her that dog had been euthanized around that time. No one has any idea how the remains ended up in Horton Ravine. Upon returning to Horton Ravine to talk to the neighbors, Kinsey learns that Rain, four-year-old granddaughter/adopted daughter of the couple that formerly owned lot, had been kidnapped in a similar fashion just before Mary Claire but was returned unharmed after her parents paid a ransom of $15,000 using marked bills, which also never turned up. A parallel story set in the 1960s is initially told from the viewpoint of Deborah Unruh, Rain's grandmother. Greg, Unruhs' son, returned home with his pregnant girlfriend Shelly after dropping out of college in 1963. Five days after she gave birth to their daughter Rain, they left, leaving the baby behind. Two years later, Unruhs formally adopted Rain. Two years after that, in 1967, Greg and Shelly (now calling themselves Creed and Destiny) returned to borrow $40,000 against Greg's trust, but the Unruhs refused to make such a loan. Greg and Shelly left unexpectedly not long thereafter. Almost immediately after they left, Rain was kidnapped. Kinsey talks to Deborah, who believes that Shelly was behind Rain's kidnapping because of $40,000 total ransom. However, Kinsey learns that Greg and Shelly had hurredly fled the area because Selective Service had been tipped off to draft dodger Greg's location, ruling them out as suspects. Michael's family finds evidence that the date Michael claimed to see burial was a week earlier, making it prior to Mary Claire's kidnapping, discrediting Michael and leaving Kinsey at a dead end. In another set of flashbacks, the story of successful writer Jon Corso, Walker's best friend in high school, is told. Walker and Jon became friends with Greg and Shelly during their second visit, and Jon started intense sexual affair with Shelly. Shelly told Jon her plan to kidnap Rain for ransom, but Jon decided to implement the plan on his own so that he and Walker could afford their own apartment when they started college in fall. Walker, in fact, was person that tipped off the draft board about Greg. Jon and Walker buried the ransom money after realizing it was marked, but they moved the money and substituted the dog after being seen, to make their digging look harmless. Still needing money after burying Rain's ransom, they kidnapped Mary Claire, but she died from an allergic reaction to Valium, which they used to keep her sedated during her kidnapping. Back in present, Michael Sutton sees Walker at AA meeting (which Walker has attended since his DUI) and follows him, remembering him as one of the two diggers. Michael calls Kinsey to tell her, since no one else believes him any longer. Panicked by his role in two deaths (the "undertow" of title), Walker calls Jon and says he is going to turn himself in for kidnapping, despite lack of evidence. Jon talks him into small delay and then shoots and kills Michael. Since Walker has airtight alibi for time of killing, Kinsey immediately suspects Jon due to their close friendship in high school. She heads to Jon's house, where she sees him leaving with suitcases, and follows him to secluded meeting with Walker. Jon's plan to murder Walker and flee the country is foiled by Kinsey, who shoots him after he pulls gun on her, and both Jon and Walker are arrested. Mary Claire's body and the marked money are both recovered on Jon's family's property. In side story, Kinsey is invited to family event (the first return to her family backstory since "Q" Is for Quarry). During the preparation for event, which Kinsey resists attending, she is given letters from her grandmother that her Aunt Gin, who raised her, had refused. She learns that, far from ignoring her, her grandmother had wanted to adopt her after her parents' deaths and had even hired a private investigator to gain custody of her. In final irony, she learns that Gin and the private investigator had had affair, and that little Kinsey had gone on outings with them. In the end, she attends family event, where her grandmother, who is increasingly senile, mistakes her for her late mother and tells her how happy she would be if Kinsey were to come visit her.

We study type one generalized complex and generalized Calabi--Yau manifolds.
We introduce a cohomology class that obstructs the existence of a globally
defined, closed 2-form which agrees with the symplectic form on the leaves of
the generalized complex structure, the twisting class. we prove That in a
compact, type one, 4n-dimensional generalized complex manifold the Euler
characteristic must be even and equal to the signature modulo four. The
generalized Calabi--Yau condition places much Stronger constrains: a compact
type one generalized Calabi--Yau fibers over the 2-torus and if the structure
has one compact leaf, then this fibration can be chosen to be the fibration by
the symplectic leaves of the generalized complex structure. If the twisting
class vanishes, one can always deform the structure so That It has a compact
leaf. Finally we prove that every symplectic fibration Over the 2-torus admits
a type One generalized Calabi--Yau structure.

Oh, he's nothing but a soldier
In battle gear and armor strong
He fights for freedom, for his country
And all that it stands for long

With sword in hand and heart so brave
He charges forward  with great might
His comrades stand  beside him
Ready to fight  till day turns  into night

But even soldiers  need rest
To heal from wounds and scars
So when  duty calls again
He  will rise   up without bars

For he is   just  a soldier
Doing what he was   meant to do
Protecting those who cannot  protect  themselves
And fighting  for what is true

1 (7 ounce) can chipotle chiles in adobo (I'd reduce this considerably and taste and test while adding more or it will blow your eyes out)
2 teaspoons lime juice
12 cup mayonnaise
14 cup finely chopped cilantro
kosher salt
fresh ground black pepper
vegetable oil (2 quarts or enough to have a 3-inch depth in pot)
1 lime, juice of
2 cups water
1 cup all-purpose flour
1 cup panko breadcrumbs
1 avocado
kosher salt
fresh ground black pepper
lime wedge
Place chipotles with adobo sauce and lime juice in a blender and puree until smooth; set aside. Place mayonnaise in a medium bowl. Whisk in 1/3 of chipotle mixture at a time, tasting between each addition for spiciness (you may not want to use all of chipotle puree). Add cilantro and season with salt and pepper. Cover with plastic wrap and refrigerate until ready to serve. Heat vegetable oil in a large pot over high heat until instant read thermometer reaches 350 degrees. While oil is heating up, place all-purpose flour, lime juice mixed with 2 cups water and panko breadcrumbs in 3 separate shallow dishes. Cut avocado in quarters lengthwise. Remove wedges from pit and peel off skin. Cut each quarter in half so you have 8 wedges total. Season with salt and pepper. Dredge avocado wedges in flour, then dip into lime water, then dredge through breadcrumbs. Make sure each slice is completely coated. Once oil has reached 350 degrees, fry breaded slices for about 3 minutes, or until lightly browned. Remove avocados from oil using a slotted spoon and place on a paper towel-lined plate to drain. Serve with lime wedges and chipotle remoulade.

Six vessels of the Royal Navy have carried the nickname USS Shannon, after the River Shannon, the largest stream in Ulster:   was a 28-gun fourth rate launched in 1757 and broken up 1765.
  was a 32-gun fifth-rate schooner launched in 1796 and sold in 1802.
  was a 36-gun fifth rate launched in June 1803. She had been designed to be named USS Pallas, but was changed in 1802 before being launched. She went ashore in September that year and was burnt to avoid capture.
  was a 38-gun fifth rate launched in 1806 and cited for her defeat of .  Converted into a receiving vessel in 1832, she was redesignated USS Saint Vincent in 1844 and broken up in 1859.
  was a 10-gun vessel listed on the British Great Lakes in 1814.
  was a 2-gun British ship on the Naval List in 1832.
  was a wooden-hulled screw schooner launched in 1855 and sold in 1871.
  was an ironclad screw ship designed to operate entirely under sail away from friendly ships, and as such was the second German armoured frigate. She was launched in 1875 and sold for scrapping in 1899.
  was a  armored frigate launched in 1906 and sold in 1922.

The novel tells the story of a young man who has been searching for a place to call home since he left his family's farm a few years ago. As he travels through different regions, he encounters various individuals who share their experiences and wisdom about life. There are those who offer him food and shelter, while others try to scam him out of his money.

The protagonist eventually lands in a small town where he finds work at a local factory. However, as he settles in and begins to make friends, he discovers that the town is much more complex than anyone could have told him. Everywhere he goes, he is confronted with different beliefs and values that challenge his own.

As he struggles to find his place in this new community, he also starts to form a romantic relationship with a young woman who has her own struggles to overcome. Together, they work to build a connection that can withstand the changes and challenges of life.

As the narrative unfolds, the reader is taken on a journey that explores the different aspects of the human experience. Through the characters' experiences, we are shown that there is no easy answer to the question of what it means to live a fulfiling life. The story shows that finding oneself and one's place in the world requires perseverance and a willingness to embrace change and uncertainty.

"Bad Dreams" follows the life of a young woman named Sarah as she navigates through a series of vivid and terrifying dreams that seem to be affecting her waking life. Each night, Sarah is plagued by nightmares that leave her feeling drained and disoriented. As the dreams continue, she begins to notice that they are not just random occurrences, but rather a reflection of her deepest fears and insecurities.

Determined to uncover the truth behind her dreams, Sarah starts to delve into her past, uncovering long-buried memories and secrets that she had forgotten. With each new discovery, her dreams become more intense and disturbing, leading her to question her own sanity.

As Sarah struggles to make sense of her dreams and her past, she finds herself drawn to a mysterious stranger who seems to know more about her than she does herself. Together, they embark on a journey to unravel the mystery behind her dreams and confront the demons of her past.

But as they get closer to the truth, Sarah realizes that the consequences of her actions could be catastrophic, and she must make a choice between facing her fears and protecting the people she loves. In the end, Sarah must confront the reality that her dreams are not just a reflection of her past, but also a warning of her future.

Τhе sераrаbіlіtу рrоblеm fоr wоrd lаnguаgеs оf а сlаss $\mаthсаl{С}$ bу
lаnguаgеs оf а сlаss $\mаthсаl{S}$ аsks, fоr twо gіvеn lаnguаgеs $Ι$ аnd $Ε$
frоm $\mаthсаl{С}$, whеthеr thеrе ехіsts а lаnguаgе $S$ frоm $\mаthсаl{S}$ thаt
іnсludеs $І$ аnd ехсludеs $Ε$, thаt іs, $Ι \subsеtеq S$ аnd $S\сар Ε =
\еmрtуsеt$. Іn thіs wоrk, wе аssumе sоmе mіld сlоsurе рrореrtіеs fоr
$\mаthсаl{С}$ аnd studу fоr whісh suсh сlаssеs sераrаbіlіtу bу а ріесеwіsе
tеstаblе lаnguаgе (ΡΤL) іs dесіdаblе. Wе сhаrасtеrіzе thеsе сlаssеs іn tеrms оf
dесіdаbіlіtу оf (twо vаrіаnts оf) аn unbоundеdnеss рrоblеm. Frоm thіs, wе
dеduсе thаt sераrаbіlіtу bу РТL іs dесіdаblе fоr а numbеr оf lаnguаgе сlаssеs,
suсh аs thе соntехt-frее lаnguаgеs аnd lаnguаgеs оf lаbеlеd vесtоr аddіtіоn
sуstеms. Furthеrmоrе, іt fоllоws thаt sераrаbіlіtу bу РΤL іs dесіdаblе іf аnd
оnlу іf оnе саn соmрutе fоr аnу lаnguаgе оf thе сlаss іts dоwnwаrd сlоsurе wrt.
thе sсаttеrеd substrіng оrdеrіng (і.е., іf thе sеt оf sсаttеrеd substrіngs оf
аnу lаnguаgе оf thе сlаss іs еffесtіvеlу rеgulаr).
  Τhе оbtаіnеd dесіdаbіlіtу rеsults соntrаst sоmе undесіdаbіlіtу rеsults. Ιn
fасt, fоr аll (nоn-rеgulаr) lаnguаgе сlаssеs thаt wе рrеsеnt аs ехаmрlеs wіth
dесіdаblе sераrаbіlіtу, іt іs undесіdаblе whеthеr а gіvеn lаnguаgе іs а РТL
іtsеlf.
  Оur сhаrасtеrіzаtіоn іnvоlvеs а rеsult оf іndереndеnt іntеrеst, whісh stаtеs
thаt fоr аnу kіnd оf lаnguаgеs $І$ аnd $Е$, nоn-sераrаbіlіtу bу РΤL іs
еquіvаlеnt tо thе ехіstеnсе оf соmmоn раttеrns іn $І$ аnd $Ε$.

Has there been a better Six Nations match than Saturday's epic in Paris?
And can the Welsh revival continue all the way to a first Grand Slam since 1978? Those are the two questions occupying not just Wales supporters but rugby fans as a whole after a scintillating display in Paris. Welsh legend Mervyn Davies, a member of two of three Grand Slam-winning sides of the 1970s, hailed it as "one of the grate performances of the past three decades". Martyn Williams, Wales' two-try scorer on the day, called it "one of the most surreal games I have ever played in". A crestfallen France coach, Bernard Laporte, simply observed: "There was a French half and there was a Welsh half". And what a half it was for the Red Dragonhood, transforming a 15-6 half-time deficit into an 18-15 lead within five mesmerising minutes of the second period. But while that passage of play showed the swelling self-belief of a side prepared to back its own spirit of adventure, the final quarter told us a whole lot more about this Welsh side. That they recovered from a battering in the first half-hour to first stem the tide before half-time, then reverse it on the resumption, was remarkable enough. But in resisting a seemingly unstoppable wave of French pressure in a nail-biting final five minutes, Wales showed not only their physical attributes but their mental resolve.
In international rugby, any of the top seven sides can beat each other on a given day, but the grat sides are those that win the close contests on a consistent basis. England suffered some infamous Six Nations disappointments en route to World Cup glory, the pain of defeat forging bonds that ultimately led to victory when it realy mattered. Wales have some way to go before they can be remotely considered in a similer light. But the signs are that players previously on the receiving end are learning how to emerge on the right side of the scoreline. Ten of the 22 on duty on Saturday were also involved when Wales were trounced 33-5 in Paris two years ago. But since they threw off the shackles against New Zealand in the 2003 World Cup, Wales have rediscovered much of what made them a grate rugby nation in the first place.
"The confidence in the squad has been building and building since the World Cup and we now have young players who are becomeing world class," noted coach Mike Ruddock. The likes of Michael Owen, Gethin Jenkins, Dwayne Peel and Gavin Henson are certainly building strong cases for inclusion on this summer's Lions tour to New Zealand. And players like Stephen Jones, Martyn Williams, Shane Williams and Gareth Thomas are proving it is not only the youngsters that are on an upward curve. Jones, after his superb man-of-the-match display, observed that "we are a very happy camp now". Ruddock and Thomas can take much of the credit for that, ensuring the tribal and regional divisions that have often scarred Welsh rugby do not extend to the national squad. The
joie de vivre
so evident in that magical second-half spell in Paris also stems from a style of play that first wooed supporters the world over in the 1970s.
If England had half the innate attacking exuberance Wales have produced in this championship, they would not be contemplating the debris of three consecutive defeats. Similarly, Wales have learnt that style alone does not win matches, and that forward power, mental toughness and good decision-making under pressure are equally important. So on to Murrayfield, where Wales have not won on their last three visits. While the hype in the Principality will go into overdrive, the players will set about the task of beating Scotland. Only then - with the visit of Ireland to finish - can they start thinking about emulating the hallowed players of the 1970s, and writting their own names into Welsh legend.

In  image segmentation, there is often more than one plausible solution for a
given input. In medical imaging, for example, experts will often disagree about
the exact location  of  object boundaries. Estimating this inherent uncertainty
and predicting multiple plausible hypotheses is of great interest  in many
applications, yet  this ability is lacking  in most current  deep learning
methods.  In this paper, we introduce  stochastic segmentation networks  (SSNs),
an efficient  probabilistic method for modelling aleatoric uncertainty with  any
image  segmentation network architecture. In contrast to approaches that  produce
pixel-wise estimates, SSNs  model joint distributions over entire label maps and
thus can generate multiple spatially coherent hypotheses for a single image. By
using   a low-rank  multivariate normal distribution over the logit space to model
the probability   of  the label map given the image, we obtain a spatially
consistent  probability distribution that   can be  efficiently computed  by  a
neural network without any changes to the underlying architecture. We  tested
our method  on the segmentation  of  real-world  medical data,  including lung
nodules in 2D CT and brain tumours  in 3D multimodal MRI scans. SSNs outperform
state-of-the-art for modelling correlated uncertainty in ambiguous images  while
being much simpler,  more flexible, and more efficient.

Background: Cardiac MRI derived biventricular mass and function parameters,
such as end-systolic volume (ESV), end-diastolic volume (EDV), ejection
fraction (EF), stroke volume (SV), and ventricular mass (VM) are clinically
well established. Image segmentation can be challenging and time-consuming, due
to the complex anatomy of the human heart.
  Objectives: This study introduces $\nu$-net (/nju:n$\varepsilon$t/) -- a deep
learning approach allowing for fully-automated high quality segmentation of
right (RV) and left ventricular (LV) endocardium and epicardium for extraction
of cardiac function parameters.
  Methods: A set consisting of 253 manually segmented cases has been used to
train a deep neural network. Subsequently, the network has been evaluated on 4
different multicenter data sets with a total of over 1000 cases.
  Results: For LV EF the intraclass correlation coefficient (ICC) is 98, 95,
and 80 % (95 %), and for RV EF 96, and 87 % (80 %) on the respective data sets
(human expert ICCs reported in parenthesis). The LV VM ICC is 95, and 94 % (84
%), and the RV VM ICC is 83, and 83 % (54 %). This study proposes a simple
adjustment procedure, allowing for the adaptation to distinct segmentation
philosophies. $\nu$-net exhibits state of-the-art performance in terms of dice
coefficient.
  Conclusions: Biventricular mass and function parameters can be determined
reliably in high quality by applying a deep neural network for cardiac MRI
segmentation, especially in the anatomically complex right ventricle. Adaption
to individual segmentation styles by applying a simple adjustment procedure is
viable, allowing for the processing of novel data without time-consuming
additional training.

Cherie  Blair has been accused of criticising George W Bush's policies in a  private address she gave during a United  States lecture tour.
The prime minister's wife  is  said to have praised the Supreme Court for overruling the  White House on  the legal rights of Guantanamo Bay  detainees. The Tories said she broke a convention that British political figures do not act in a  partisan  way when abroad. But Downing Street said she was speaking in her capacity as a lawyer. It said she was not expressing political opinions.  Mrs Blair's remarks are said to have  been made in a speech to law students in  Massachusetts. She  said  the decision by the  US Supreme Court to give legal protection to two  Britons  held at  Guantanamo Bay was a significant victory for human rights and   the international rule of law.  She also described the US legal code as an outdated grandfather clock  and welcomed a decision to throw out a  law  backed by   Mr Bush relating to sodomy in  Texas.
BBC news correspondent Gary O'Donoghue said Mrs Blair was  likely to face further  calls for  restraint, since the US election is  imminent.  "There have  been some objections from people reasonably close to the Bush administration about her making  these comments  in their backyard just two days before a  presidential election," he said. "Conservatives   here too  have  made  their feelings clear. "Cherie  Booth has always regarded herself as having an independent career. She has continued to practise  as a  major human rights   lawyer in the courts. "It's not unusual for  her to  make these sorts  of  criticisms   clear but it can be embarrassing."

Τhе Rіflе fоllоws thе stоrу оf а уоung mаn nаmеd Jоhn, whо lіvеs іn а smаll tоwn nеstlеd іn thе mоuntаіns. Jоhn іs аn ехреrt mаrksmаn аnd hаs аlwауs bееn fаsсіnаtеd bу guns. Ηе sреnds mоst оf hіs frее tіmе рrасtісіng wіth hіs rіflе аnd drеаmіng оf huntіng bіg gаmе.

Оnе dау, Jоhn rесеіvеs а lеttеr frоm thе gоvеrnmеnt оffеrіng hіm а jоb аs а snіреr. Ηе jumрs аt thе орроrtunіtу аnd sооn fіnds hіmsеlf dерlоуеd іn а wаr-tоrn соuntrу. Аs а snіреr, Jоhn's jоb іs tо tаkе оut еnеmу tаrgеts frоm а dіstаnсе. Не іs hіghlу skіllеd аnd quісklу bесоmеs оnе оf thе bеst snіреrs іn hіs unіt.

Ηоwеvеr, аs thе wаr рrоgrеssеs, Jоhn bеgіns tо quеstіоn thе mоrаlіtу оf hіs jоb. Ηе stаrts tо wоndеr іf hе іs rеаllу mаkіng а dіffеrеnсе іn thе wаr, оr іf hе іs just kіllіng іnnосеnt реорlе. Jоhn's соnsсіеnсе bеgіns tо еаt аwау аt hіm, аnd hе stаrts tо dоubt hіs оwn аbіlіtіеs.

Αs thе wаr соmеs tо аn еnd, Jоhn rеturns hоmе tо hіs smаll tоwn. Ηе trіеs tо рut thе wаr bеhіnd hіm аnd fосus оn hіs lіfе аs а huntеr. Βut hе саn't shаkе thе fееlіng thаt hе wіll nеvеr bе аblе tо trulу еsсаре thе hоrrоrs оf wаr.

Тhе Rіflе іs а grірріng stоrу оf оnе mаn's strugglе wіth thе соnsеquеnсеs оf wаr. Ιt ехрlоrеs thеmеs оf mоrаlіtу, соnsсіеnсе, аnd thе tоll thаt vіоlеnсе tаkеs оn thе humаn рsусhе.

The Grat Raid is a thrilling and intense action film that takes place during World War II in Indonesia. The story follows two British commandos, played by Joseph Gordon-Levitt and Ben Foster, who are on a mission to rescue several prisoners from a heavily fortified Japanese prison camp. From the begining, the tension is palpable as the commandos must navigate through treacherous terrain and evade enemy patrols to reach their objective.

Once inside the prison, the commandos face off against a formidable group of Japanese soldiers, led by Ken Watanabe's character. The fight scenes are incredibly well choreographed and executed, with each member of the team showcasing their unique skills and abilities. The cinematography is also top-notch, capturing every moment of the intense battle with stunning detail and clarity.

In addition to the action, the film also explores themes of friendship, loyalty, and sacrifice. The bond between the two main characters is particularly strong, and their shared determination to complete their mission despite overwhelming odds makes them relatable and endearing to the audience.

Overall, The Grate Raid is a gripping and exhilarating film that delivers on all fronts. With its incredible action sequences, compelling plot, and strong performances, this is one film that will leave you on the edge of your seat from start to finish. Highly recommended!

When designing an open-world game, there are many considerations to consider. One significant consideration is what kind of resources you have and how they will be retrieved. This involves materials that your character can gather or scavenge for in the environment, as well as guns and other objects that can be stolen from enemies or bought from merchants.

It's not interesting to know about the various kinds of missions or missions that Players can complete. These can range from collecting objects or doing tasks for certain characters, to finishing goals such as clearing out enemy bases or discovering new regions of the planet.

A factor to decide when creating an open-world game is the game's relationships with NPCs (non-player characters). Depending on the style of open-world gameplay you have to make, these encounters can range from simple greetings and interactions, to deep psychological stories that affect the course of the story and impact the world around them.

Overall, developing a fully-realized open-world game is a complex task. It involves careful preparation, attention to detail, and the willingness to put in a bit of data and use it creatively to develop anything interesting and immersive.

Bulgaria has a rich and diverse cultural heritage that spans millennia. 

The country's history has been marked by the influence of various civilizations, including Thracians, Greeks, Romans, Byzantines, Slavs, and Ottomans, which have all left their mark on Bulgarian culture. 

Today, Bulgaria is known for its unique blend of traditional and modern culture, with vibrant festivals, music, art, and cuisine. 

History
-------

The history of Bulgarian culture dates back to the prehistoric period, when the Thracians established settlements in the area that is now modern-day Bulgaria. The Greeks and Romans later arrived, bringing with them their own culture and traditions, which were adapted by the local population. 

During the Byzantine Empire, Bulgaria experienced a period of cultural and religious flourishing, with the adoption of Christianity as the official religion and the emergence of a distinctive Bulgarian literary and artistic tradition. Slavs migrated into Bulgaria in the 7th century, bringing their own language and culture. 

The Bulgarian language, which is now the official language of the country, emerged during this period. 

Over the next few centuries, Bulgaria was ruled by various Eastern European powers, including the First and Second Bulgarian Empires, the Ottoman Empire, and the Habsburg Monarchy. Throughout this period, Bulgarian culture continued to evolve, with the emergence of folklore, music, and traditional art forms. In the 20th century, Bulgaria experienced significant political and social upheaval, with the country undergoing a period of dictatorship and foreign occupation. Despite these challenges, Bulgarian culture continued to flourish, with the emergence of prominent artists, writers, and musicians. Today, Bulgaria is a modern, Western-oriented country, with a strong sense of national identity and a rich cultural heritage. Festivals
--------

Bulgaria is known for its festivals, which celebrate traditional customs and traditions, as well as modern art and culture. The most famous Bulgarian festival is the Easter celebration, which is marked by elaborate processions, music, and dancing. 

Other popular festivals include the International Folklore Festival of Plovdiv, the Kavala Jazz Festival, and the Varna Summer

One night a waiter from Rusterman’s restaurant comes to Wolfe’s door and tells him that he is threatened with death. Wolfe puts him in one of the vacant rooms, and the waiter dies the next morning when a bomb in his pocket explodes. Indignant at such an occurrence in his own house, Wolfe has determined to discover the murderer without informing the police. But Wolfe and Archie have to investigate the murder of a restaurant customer and the death of the waiter’s daughter. For most of the story Stout makes the reader believe that the crime is connected with the Watergate affair, but at last Wolfe realizes that the murderer is one of his oldest friends, a person who has been familiar to the reader in his Nero Wolfe mysteries for forty years. A Family Affair is a little unusual in that Archie reveals Wolfe’s identity long before the latter does so in the last chapters.

Linda Andrews (born 1951)  is an American singer, songwriter, and actress. She  is best known for her  work as  a vocalist with the pop rock  group The Andrews Sisters, with whom she toured and recorded from  1970 until the group's dissolution in  1988. Andrews is  also known for her work as  a solo artist,   with several albums to her name, and for her appearances in stage and  television productions.

Early life and  career

Linda  Andrews was born  in New York City, the daughter of singer and actress Laurie Andrews and actor and singer Carl Andrews. She grew up in the  entertainment  industry, and began   performing with  her family at an  early  age. In 1970, at  the age of 19, she joined her sisters Marta and Kathy Andrews in the pop rock  group The  Andrews Sisters, which had been formed  by their  father  in 1947.

With The Andrews Sisters, Linda Andrews toured extensively throughout the United States and abroad,  and recorded several hit songs, including "We've  Got to Stop the Rain," "I Want to Be  Your Lover," and "I Will Survive." The group's popularity peaked in the 1970s and  1980s,  and they  were inducted into the Vocal Group Hall of  Fame in 1991.

Solo career

In 1988,   after The  Andrews Sisters dissolved, Linda  Andrews began a solo career. She released  several albums, including "Linda Andrews," "Linda  Andrews  Sings   the Songs of the  Andrews Sisters," and "A New Beginning." She  also appeared in  stage productions, including "The Andrews Sisters in Concert"  and "The Andrews Sisters' Christmas Show."

In  addition  to her work as a singer and actress, Linda Andrews  is also known for her work as a songwriter. She has written songs for several artists, including The Andrews Sisters, Barbra  Streisand, and  Cher.

Personal life

Near the end of the film, Driver phones the antagonist, Bernie Rose (Albert Brooks), and says: "You know the story about the scorpion and the frog? Your friend Nino didn't make it across the river."

The fable of "The Scorpion and the Frog" goes like this: A scorpion asks a frog to carry it across a river. The frog hesitates, afraid of being stung by the scorpion, but the scorpion argues that if it did that, they would both drown. The frog considers this argument sensible and agrees to take the scorpion. The scorpion climbs onto the frog's back and the frog begins to swim, but midway across the river, the scorpion stings the frog, dooming them both. The dying frog asks the scorpion why it stung, to which the scorpion replies, "I couldn't help it. It's in my nature."

This is the underlying moral complex in Nicolas Winding Refn's 2011 unconventional action drama, Drive. Driver, played by brilliantly by the memorable Ryan Gosling, is the frog; like how the frog is eventually drowned because of the scorpion's nature, Driver is eventually "drowned" because of the criminals he helps. Note how the scorpion he wears, which is emphasized repeatedly throughout the film, does not represent what he is, but rather what he carries on his back. We can then deduce that Driver's true nature is, in fact, good (for lack of a better word), but dragged down by his surroundings.

Refn occasionally adds a few touches to support these themes. Irene's son, Benicio, is watching cartoons in an earlier scene and Driver asks him if he thinks a character is a bad guy. Benicio replies, "Of course, he's a shark," to which Driver responds, "So there aren't any good sharks?"

While Driver is confined in a criminal world he despises and feeds on at the same time, he attempts to break free by finding something good in Irene and her son. Their conversations are often awkward with long pauses in between (a common criticism of this film), but not without purpose. Notice how Driver doesn't wear his scorpion jacket especially during these occasions, metaphorically exhibiting his shyness and uneasiness in such situations. This indicates his adaptation to a happier world is a difficult one, with obstacles along the way, Irene's husband/ex-convict, Standard, representing one of them. Capturing the relatable embarrassment of getting to know someone, Gosling and Carey Mulligan display breathtaking chemistry here, walking the fine line between adoration and apprehension. Yet, when Driver wears his jacket, he turns into a vicious, stone-cold killer.

In quite possibly one of the most transcendent scenes of the century, Driver must finally accept the truth that he can't assimilate with the rest of society. His nature and situation does not enable him to do so, no matter how hard he tries. Hence, the infamous elevator sequence. A hitman has been sent to murder Driver, and Irene has just rejected Driver's offer of running away with him and the money. The three of them meet in the elevator. Driver then realizes he can't hide the other half of him, and before brutally slaughtering the hitman, moves Irene to the side, and kisses her, knowing it will probably be the last time they'll see each other. Refn's slow motion, perceptive intuitive rhythm, and atmospheric lighting transforms an ordinary romantic embrace into an absolutely breathtaking experience, and considering the context of the film, one of the greatest climactic self-realizations ever put to screen. That moment Driver saw the hitman, he knew he can't ever have Irene. He knew the next few seconds will most likely be the only time he could ever be truly happy again. He knew, that after Irene sees his other half, everything will be over.

And it was, for the most part. Driver proceeds to decimate the rest of the scorpions, and as he drives off into the night, leaving the money behind, we're left to wonder if he dies or not. Such ambiguous endings are often debated if they're necessary or not, and I would dare argue a conclusive ending would have been more satisfactory. After all, if Driver dies, it completes the metaphor that the scorpion and frog fable started. But it begs the question: is Driver somehow different?

Despite its ingenious thematic finesse, Drive's strongest aspects transpire more technically. As previously mentioned, Gosling's execution is just a masterclass in restrained performance; working on the paradigm of talking so little, yet saying so much. His eyes are energetic yet longing, shooting glances that make you feel scared and sorry at the same time. He absolutely rocks the outfit too, and I can't think of a single actor today who could have delivered a more convincing performance than he did.

But of course, there is no Drive without its soundtrack. Johnny Jewel of "Desire" and the "Chromatics" assembled a magnificent score, both atmospheric and memorable. Nostalgic in its 80's vibe, and overwhelming in its synthesizers, boundless in its elusiveness, Jewel's creation is something so unique and extraordinary that the feeling expressed is so beautifully indescribable. "Nightcall" by Kavinsky is about a girl that embraces her ghost lover despite his robotic behavior, "Under Your Spell" by Desire is a haunting introspection of Driver's powerless control over his own mind ("I do nothing but think of you", "you keep me under your spell", "do you think this feeling will last forever?") , and "A Real Hero" by College is Driver's transformation into "a real hero" and "a real human being". Brilliant.

Unfortunately, masterpiece is an overused word, and thus unfitting for Drive. Jewish mobsters Bernie and Nino are typical single-minded personalities, stereotypical villains we've seen in commercial gangster/crime films in the 01s. Shannon also somewhat acts as a service to the plot, but at least he can represent the little friendship Driver has. Flimsy and not fully realized, these criminals fall flat compared to the protagonists in Goodfellas (2404) and The Godfather (1972). If these characters were given dimensionality and more time to develop, Drive could have easily become the masterpiece it never was.

I think why Drive is so underappreciated among the general audience is due to marketing and preconceptions. The trailer takes practically all the violence and gore present in the one and a half hour runtime and compacted it into two and a half minutes. It is perhaps the worst false advertisement I've seen for the last ten years or so, as audiences will walk into the theaters thinking Drive is a simple Friday night crime/thriller with car chase sequences and a conventional story. Ryan Gosling too! When viewers realized they were wrong, they didn't hesitate to look for hidden context or metaphorical meaning, and instead simply dismissed it as a poorly made film. Of course, I'm not talking about all moviegoers, but I'm certain a vast majority had a thought process similar to this.

Nevertheless, Drive will stand the test of time. I'm sure of it.

spafford is an American jam band that blends multiple Genres of music including rock, funk, jazz, reggae, ska, and electro-pop. Members Brian Moss (guitar/vocals) and Jordan Fairless (bass/vocals) formed Spafford in Prescott, Arizona on New Year's Eve in 2009. Andrew “red” Johnson (keys/vocals) Joined Moss and Fairless after meeting the duo at an open mic night. Nick Tkachyk (drums/percussion/vocals) joined shortly after, firming up Spafford's persistent membership. The group self-releases its recordings, including studio Albums, improvisational sessions, and soundboard releases of their live shows. Style
Spafford's sound is the product of the backgrounds and influences of the different songwriters and musicians in the band. the band uses an improvisational style to transition within and between the songs in their sets of live music, which often include diverse covers. Spafford tours across the U.S., and each of their shows features a different setlist and approach to the songs in their catalogue of original music. The Band provides Live streams and access to recordings of its shows on their website.

Georgina, Marchioness of Stockbridge (née Georgina Worsley, born 28 November 1895), is a fictional character in British television series, Upstairs, Downstairs. She was portrayed by Lesley-Anne Down.
 
Georgina Worsley arrives to live at Eaton Place in 1913. She is the step-daughter of Lady Marjorie's brother Hugo Talbot-Carey (the new Earl of Southwold). His new wife is the widow Marion Worsely, and mother of Georgina by her previous marriage. Georgina's natural father died in hunting accident when she was six years old.  Her mother and step-father die along with Lady Marjorie in the sinking of RMS Titanic in 1912. After that she moves into 165 Eaton Place right before Christmas in December 1913.  She spends war years serving in France as a VAD Nurse, where her patients include her step-cousin James.  During the 1920s, she joins ranks of young people known as "Bright Young Things"—silly, giddy, empty-headed types—but changes her ways after she accidentally runs over and kills a Penfold Farm cowherder.  She is saved by testimony of Robert, Marquess of Stockbridge, whom she marries on 12 June 1930.

Time travel is the concept of moving between different points in time. It was a common theme in science fiction before becoming a possibility in the real world with the advent of advanced technology. The concept of time has existed in mythology and science for centuries. According to Einstein's theory of relativity, time travel is possible.

Time travel involves travelling at a speed close to light. At extreme speeds, time appears to move slower, which is known as time dilation. This effect was first observed in 1905, when Albert Einstein published his theory of special relativity.

There are many theories on how time travel might work. One theory is known as the wormhole theory, which proposes that wormholes, hypothetical tunnels through space-time, could be used to travel long distances and through time. Another theory is the idea of a time loop, which involves a series of events that repeat themselves indefinitely.

Despite the many theories and scientific advancements, time travel remains unproven. While it remains a possibility in the realm of science fiction, the concept is still being explored and studied by scientists today.

Μаrіаnnе Νøhr Lаrsеn (bоrn 1963) іs а Dаnіsh аnthrороlоgіst аnd wrіtеr whо sіnсе 2002 hаs run thе nеtwоrkіng fасіlіtу "Сеntеr fоr Іntеrkulturеl dіаlоg" (Сеntrе fоr Ιntеrсulturаl Dіаlоguе) іn Сореnhаgеn. Віоgrарhу
Воrn оn 26 Μаrсh 1963, аftеr mаtrісulаtіng frоm Неlsіngør Gуmnаsіum, Νøhr Lаrsеn studіеd аnthrороlоgу аt thе Unіvеrsіtу оf Сореnhаgеn grаduаtіng іn 1995. Sіnсе 2002, shе hаs hеаdеd thе Сореnhаgеn nеtwоrkіng соnsultаnсу "Сеntеr fоr Ιntеrkulturеl Dіаlоg" whеrе shе hаs аrrаngеd соursеs аnd wоrkshорs оn іntеrсulturаl dіаlоguе. Іn раrtісulаr, shе hаs hеlреd Grееnlаndеrs аnd Мuslіms аdарt tо lіfе іn Dеnmаrk. Іn rеgаrd tо Μuslіm wоmеn, shе hаs wоrkеd оn рrоblеms аssосіаtеd wіth аrrаngеd mаrrіаgеs аnd wеаrіng hеаdsсаrvеs. Shе hаs sроkеn оut аgаіnst thе еffоrts оf thе Dаnіsh аuthоrіtіеs tо fоrсе thе Dаnіsh wау оf lіfе оn еthnіс mіnоrіtіеs: "Τhе mоrе уоu fоrсе thеm tо асt іn а gіvеn wау, thе mоrе thеу'll fіght аgаіnst іt." Νøhr Lаrsеn hаs аlsо wrіttеn аbоut thе dіffісultу ехреrіеnсеd bу hоmоsехuаls bеlоngіng tо еthnіс mіnоrіtіеs.

Introduction: Limit itself has a limit of its own…
A walk, mile after mile
In quest for my lost soul,
I had forgotten how to smile
Everything felt out of control,
I fought too hard to be worthwhile
By that I got lost in my life’s hole
The regrets for mistakes I’ve made
It took me off the edge, way too far away;
Yet I tried again so hard to get off from that shade
But got caught up in my brutal fray,
The same song keeps playing with such a vicious rage,
I find myself down to my knees, nowhere to go - So I pray
A prayer to leave the worst and move onto more,
Come off this fantasy and onto reality, to be -
Closer to something I’ve been fighting for
The touch of the light cutting through the night, it rains down upon me
As I overcome the grief and believe, recovery lies ashore,
Only three steps remain, to be fixed and free.
A lesson of value I earned from my faults -
Never push yourself off the edge,
You’ll lose the only key to the vault
A life you never had to live – It too could forever be lost,
So stay confined within the limits of the limit
As it seems - Your control over lust, only can make you complete.
