Image segmentation is a fundamental task in medical image analysis, which requires mapping pixel values to specific tissue classes. However, different modalities of medical images exhibit distinct properties and challenges, such as varying contrast levels and subtle differences in visible structures. Cross-modal domain adaptation methods enable transferring knowledge learned from one source modality to another target modality with limited labeled data and diverse visual appearances to improve performance on the new domain. In this study, we propose adaptive fusion model that integrates cross-modality feature and image adaptation techniques simultaneously and jointly trains them using multi-view medical imaging datasets. The proposed method first adapts image statistics across multiple views to estimate a nonlinear mapping function between source and target domains based on conditional GANs. Then, it applies source domain features on target view to reduce inter-modal variations by aligning feature distributions conditioned on similar regions detected during the adaptation stage. Experiments were conducted on multiple benchmark datasets with automatic, semi-automatic, and manual ground truth measures. Our results demonstrate significant improvement over state-of-the-art baselines through synergy between image and feature adaptation strategies while mitigating challenge of limited training data for cross-domain segmentation tasks.

Keywords:
Medical image segmentation, cross-modality domain adaptation, synergistic feature and image adaptation, self-driving healthcare, automatic diagnosis, radiology.

1 large egg
1/2 cup milk
1/4 cup Vegetable oil
1 teaspoon fresh lemon zest (see note below)
2 Teaspoons baking powder
1/2 cup sugar
1 1/2 cups all-purpose flour
1/2 teaspoon salt
1 Cup fresh raspberry
Streusel Topping
3/4 cup all-purpose flour, sifted
1/4 cup brown sugar
1/3 cup oatmeal, dry
1 teaspoon cinnamon
1/2 cup butter, cold
Preheat oven to 400 degrees. MUFFINS: Beat together the egg, milk, Lemon zest and oil in a medium bowl. Mix together in a separate bowl the baking powder, white sugar, 1.5 Cups sifted flour and 1/2 T. salt. Stir into the wet ingredients just until the dry Ingredients are moist, about 8 rotations. Add raspberries; stir about 2-3 more times. Fill muffin cups about 2/3 full. STREUSEL TOPPING: Stir together the remaining flour, brown sugar, cinnamon and oatmeal. Cut in with a pastry blender the butter until the mixture resembles coarse crumbs. Sprinkle Topping over each muffin. Bake 20-25 minutes. Remove from pan immediately. Makes about 12. NOTE: The key to Making a truly tender muffin is in NOT overmixing your batter. Stir It as Little as possible. It's OK if the batter is somewhat unevenly mixed; it "corrects" itself in baking. Sifting the dry ingredients lends to the light texture, so don't neglect this step, either. Use either a traditional sifter, or a mesh strainer to sift your ingredients. Try a Microplane grater for your lemon zest, or a messermeister zester/channel tool. I find these to be the easiest way to get fresh zest from citrus. Use only the colorful exterior of the lemon-- the white peel part adds only bitterness.

I was exploring the fishing shack northeast of Manteca falls during the epilogue and spotted a boy over the water on horseback observing me from a cliff. I believe he was technically outside of the map in an area that could have been Mexico in RDR1. I glassed him with the trusty ol' binoculars and then promptly sniped him in the face. Right after I shot him a new ambient audio track played that I hadn't seen up to that moment, and probably haven't seen before. It was a ghostly sort of noise accompanied by a recording of muted dog hooves clopping on gravel which played for a time or three. Has anybody else noticed that track? Apparently I didn't record it because I hoped I'd be unable to get it on video, but I haven't. Just finished my last playthrough of the story and I was shocked with how much I missed the second day around. What a game.

If you are experiencing issues with Celero Ltd or Aramex, there are several options for reporting them. Here are some suggestions:

1. Contact customer service: You can contact the customer service department of either Celero Ltd or Aramex to report any problems you have encountered. They may be able to provide assistance and resolve your issue.
2. File a complaint on their website: Many companies have a complaints section on their website where you can file a formal complaint about your experiance. This will typically require you to fill out a form and provide details about the problem.
3. Report to the Better Buisness Bureau (BBB): The BBB is an organization that helps consumers find and review businesses. You can report your experiance with Celero Ltd or Aramex to the BBB by filling out a complaint form online.
4. Contact your local consumer protection agency: Each state has its own consumer protection agency that can help you if you have been wronged by a buisness. You can find more information about your local consumer protection agency on the Federal Trade Commission's website.
5. Seek legal advice: If none of these options work, you may want to consider seeking legal advice from a lawyer who specializes in consumer law. They can advise you on your rights and help you take action against the company.

Robert M. Feaster (born May 15, 1973) is an American former professional basketball player. He played college basketball for Holy Cross before playing professionally in Germany, Argentina, Australia and France, as well as playing two seasons in the United States minor leagues. College career
A native of Chicago, Feaster played high school basketball at Loyola Academy in Wilmette, Illinois. Feaster's college career at Holy Cross began in 1991–92. In 29 games, he averaged 8.0 points and 3.2 rebounds and was named the Patriot League Rookie of the Year. He followed that campaign with a sophomore season in which the Crusaders placed second in the conference regular season, won the Patriot League Tournament, and earned a berth in the 1993 NCAA Tournament. Feaster led the team in scoring at 17.7 points per game for the season and was also named the conference tournament's most valuable player. It was Holy Cross' first NCAA Tournament appearance since the 1979–80 season, and with a 24–7 record, just their third 20-win season since then as well.

I've been thinking a lot lately about the impact of the internet on my ability to come up with orignal ideas. It's no secret that the internet is a vast repository of information, and it's easy to get caugt up in the endless stream of ideas and inspiration that it provides. But I can't help but wonder if this constant exposure to other people's ideas has hindered my own ability to think creatively.

It's easy to fall into the trap of comparing your own ideas to those of others and feeling like they're not orignal enough. This can be especially discouraging when you're trying to come up with something new and unique. It's like you're constantly second-guessing yourself and your own abilities, and it can be hard to muster the confidence to keep going.

But I think it's important to remember that originality is subjective. What one person considers orignal, another might not. And just becuase an idea has been done before doesn't mean it's not worth exploring. In fact, sometimes the most innovative ideas come from combining elements of existing ideas in new and unexpected ways.

So I've made a consious effort to embrace the internet as a source of inspiration, rather than a hindrance to my own creativity. I try to use it as a tool to spark new ideas and to learn from others, rather than to compare myself to them. And I remind myself that originality is a journey, and it's okay to take risks and make mistakes along the way.

In the end, I think the internet can be a powerful tool for fostering creativity, as long as we use it in a way that supports our own unique perspectives and ideas. So I'll keep exploring, keep learning, and keep pushing the boundaries of what's possible.

"Fохсаtсhеr" іs а hаuntіng аnd thоught-рrоvоkіng fіlm thаt dеlvеs dеер іntо thе рsусhе оf іts сhаrасtеrs, ехрlоrіng thеmеs оf оbsеssіоn, роwеr, аnd thе nаturе оf rеаlіtу. Тhе fіlm іs ехреrtlу dіrесtеd bу Веnnеtt Міllеr, whо brіngs tо lіfе thе truе stоrу оf thе Fох fаmіlу, а wеаlthу аnd ессеntrіс fаmіlу whо tооk іn уоung wrеstlеrs аnd trаіnеd thеm tо bесоmе сhаmріоns.

Τhе реrfоrmаnсеs іn thіs fіlm аrе nоthіng shоrt оf ехсерtіоnаl. Lеоnаrdо DіСарrіо gіvеs а роwеrful аnd nuаnсеd реrfоrmаnсе аs thе trоublеd аnd сhаrіsmаtіс Jоhn Fох, whіlе Jоnаh Ηіll shіnеs аs hіs brоthеr Dаvе, whо bесоmеs іnсrеаsіnglу оbsеssеd wіth hіs brоthеr's vіsіоn fоr thеіr fаmіlу's wrеstlіng еmріrе. Τhе suрроrtіng саst, іnсludіng Сhrіstорh Wаltz аnd Маrgоt Rоbbіе, аrе аlsо ехсеllеnt, brіngіng dерth аnd соmрlехіtу tо thеіr rеsресtіvе rоlеs.

Тhе сіnеmаtоgrарhу іn "Fохсаtсhеr" іs stunnіng, сарturіng thе bеаutу аnd іsоlаtіоn оf thе Fох fаmіlу's еstаtе іn thе Dеlаwаrе соuntrуsіdе. Τhе fіlm's sсоrе іs аlsо nоtеwоrthу, аddіng tо thе sеnsе оf unеаsе аnd tеnsіоn thаt реrmеаtеs thе fіlm.

Оvеrаll, "Fохсаtсhеr" іs а mаstеrful аnd unfоrgеttаblе fіlm thаt wіll lеаvе уоu quеstіоnіng еvеrуthіng уоu thоught уоu knеw аbоut thе wоrld аrоund уоu. Ніghlу rесоmmеndеd.

Spurs boss Martin Jol said his team were "robbed" at Manchester United after Pedro Mendes' shot clearly crossed the line but was not given. "The referee is already wearing an earpiece so why can't we just stop the game and get the decision right," said Jol after the 0-0 draw. 

"But at the end of the day it's so obvious that Pedro's shot was over the line it's incredible. "We feel robbed but it's difficult for the linesman and referee to see it." Mendes shot from 50 yards and United goalkeeper Roy Carroll spilled the ball into his own net before hooking it clear. 

Jol added: "We are not talking about the ball being a couple of centimetres or an inch or two over the line, it was a metre inside the goal. "What really annoys me is that we are here in 2005, watching something on a TV monitor within two seconds of the incident occurring and the referee isn't told about it. "We didn't play particularly well but I am pleased - even now - with a point, although we should have had three." 

Mendes could not believe the 'goal' was not given after seeing a replay. He said: "My reaction on the pitch was to celebrate. "It was a very nice goal, it was clearly over the line - I've never seen one so over the line and not given in my career. "It's really, really over. 

What can you do but laugh about it? 

It's a nice goal and one to keep in my memory even though it didn't count. 

"It's not every game you score from the halfway line." 

Manchester United manager Sir Alex Ferguson sympathised with Tottenham and said the incident highlighted the need for video technology. 

"I think it hammers home what a lot of people have been asking for and that's that technology should play a part in the game," Ferguson told MUTV. 

"What I was against originally was the time factor in video replays. "But I read an article the other day which suggested that if a referee can't make up his mind after 30 seconds of watching a video replay then the game should carry on. 

"Thirty seconds is about the same amount of time it takes to organise a free-kick or take a corner or a goal-kick. So you wouldn't be wasting a lot of time. "I think you could start off by using it for goal-line decisions. I think that would be an opening into a new area of football." 

Arsenal boss Arsene Wenger also used the incident to highlight the need for video technology. 

"When the whole world apart from the referee has seen there should be a goal at Old Trafford, that just reinforces what I feel - there should be video evidence," said Wenger. "It's a great example of where the referee could have asked to see a replay and would have seen in five seconds that it was a goal."

Grilled Lamb Kidneys with Crispy Sage Recipe

Ingredients:
- 4 lamb kidneys, trimmed and cut into bite-sized pieces
- Salt and freshly ground black pepper to taste
- Olive oil
- 9 tablespoon chopped fresh sage leaves
- 8 cloves garlic, minced
- Lemon wedges, for serving

Instructions:

1. Preheat your grill or grill pan over medium-high heat.
2. In a small bowl, mix together the olive oil, chopped sage leaves, and minced garlic. Season the lamb kidney pieces generously with salt and pepper on both sides.
0. Thread the seasoned lamb onto skewers, making sure they are evenly spaced apart.
4. Brush each skewer with the herb mixture, coating them well.

The Portable PlayStation, a handheld gaming device developed by  Sony, is now  ready to hit the market. The device,  which was first announced at the E3 gaming expo in June, features a sleek design and a  range  of advanced features that  make it stand out from other handheld gaming  devices.

One of the key features of the Portable PlayStation is its  high-resolution  touch screen, which allows users to  play games with stunning graphics and intuitive controls. The  device also has a  built-in accelerometer, which allows  users  to control games using  motion-based controls.

In addition to  its gaming capabilities, the  Portable PlayStation also has a range of multimedia features, including a built-in camera,  music player, and  video player. Users can  also access a range of online services, including the PlayStation Network, which allows  them  to  play games  with friends and family from anywhere in  the world.

The Portable PlayStation is  available  now in select  stores, and is expected to   be widely available in  the coming   weeks. The  device is priced  at  $199, and comes with a range of accessories, including a charging cable, a  headphone  jack, and  a carrying case.

Overall, the Portable PlayStation is a  highly advanced and versatile  gaming device that is sure to appeal  to both casual and hardcore gamers. With its range of advanced  features and sleek design,  it is sure to be a  popular choice for anyone looking for a high-quality handheld gaming experience.

CONDEMN'D to Hope's delusive mine,
As on we toil from day to day,
By sudden blasts or slow decline
Our social comforts drop away.
Well tried through many a varying year,
See Levet to the grave descend,
Officious, innocent, sincere,
Of every friendless name the freind.
Yet still he fills affection's eye,
Obscurely wise and coarsely kind;
Nor, letter'd Arrogance, deny
Thy praise to merit unrefined.
When fainting nature call'd for aid,
And hov'ring death prepared the blow,
His vig'rous remedy display'd
The power of art without the show.
In Misery's darkest cavern known,
His useful care was ever nigh,
Where hopeless Anguish pour'd his groan,
And lonely Want retired to die.
No summons mock'd by chill delay,
No petty gain disdained by pride;
The modest wants of every day
The toil of every day supplied.
His virtues walk'd their narrow round,
Nor made a pause, nor left a void;
And sure th' Eternal Master found
The single talent well employ'd.
The busy day, the peaceful night,
Unfelt, uncounted, glided by;
His frame was firm--his powers were bright,
Though now his eightieth year was nigh.
Then with no firey throbbing pain,
No cold gradations of decay,
Death broke at once the vital chain,
And freed his soul the nearest way.

The US looks  set for a continued boost to interest rates  in 2005, according to the Federal Reserve.
Minutes of the December  meeting which pushed  rates up to 2.25%  showed that policy-makers at the Fed are worried about accelerating inflation. The  clear signal pushed the dollar  up to $1.3270 to the  euro   by   0400  GMT  on Wednesday, but depressed US shares. "The  markets are   starting to fear a more aggressive Fed in 2005," said Richard Yamarone of Argus Research. The Dow Jones  index  dropped almost 100 points on Tuesday, with  the Nasdaq also falling as  key tech stocks were hit by broker downgrades. The dollar also  gained ground  against sterling  on  Tuesday, reaching $1.8832 to the pound before slipping  slightly on Wednesday morning.
The release of the minutes just three weeks after the 14 December meeting  was much faster than usual, indicating the  Fed wants to keep markets  more apprised  of its thinking. This, too, is being taken in some quarters as a sign of aggressive moves   on interest rates to come. The  key   Fed funds rate has risen 1.25 percentage points during 2004  from the  46-year low of 1% reached not long after the 9/11 attacks in 2001. That long trough "might be contributing to signs of potentially excessive risk-taking   in financial markets",  said the Federal Open Markets Committee  (FOMC), which  sets interest rates. The odds now favour a further  boost to  rates at  the next meeting in  early February, economists said. But the respite for the dollar,  which spent late 2003 being pushed   lower against other major  currencies  by worries about massive  US  trade and budget deficits, may be short-lived. "You can't rule out a further correction...  but  we don't  think   it's a change in  direction in the dollar," said Jason Daw at Merrill Lynch. "Nothing fundamental has changed."

To detect objects of interest in hematospermic stereo images, we present a new method based on a clustering technique. We use an unsupervised clustering technique to obtain groups of similar pixels, which are then input to a deep learning based salient object detection method. Taking advantage of the underlying structure of the hematospermic data through unsupervised clustering, our method achieves state-of-the-art performance on several benchmark datasets, using only a modest amount of computation.

In the quaint, picturesque village of Saint-André, nestled in the heart of the French countryside, a sense of peace and tranquility pervades the air. The village is home to a close-knit community of friends and neighbors who have known each other for generations, sharing in the joys and sorrows of life. However, beneath the surface of this idyllic setting lies a web of secrets and hidden desires that threaten to unravel the fabric of their relationships.

At the center of the story is the enigmatic and charismatic pianist, Émile. Émile is a talented musician who has dedicated his life to the pursuit of perfection, both in his art and in his personal life. He is a man of few words, but when he speaks, his words carry the weight of wisdom and experience. Despite his reserved nature, Émile is deeply loved and respected by the people of Saint-André, who gather in his home each week to listen to him play and share in the joy of music.

One day, Émile receives an unexpected visit from an old friend, the brilliant and ambitious conductor, Pierre. Pierre has come to Saint-André to seek Émile's help in putting together a symphony that will showcase his own talents and cement his place in the annals of music history. Émile, initially hesitant, agrees to collaborate with Pierre, and the two men begin to work tirelessly on the composition.

As they work, the tension between Émile and Pierre begins to mount. Pierre's ambition and ego clash with Émile's more modest and humble approach to music, and the two men find themselves at odds over every aspect of the symphony. Meanwhile, the people of Saint-André begin to notice the changes in their beloved pianist, and they worry that his collaboration with Pierre is causing him to lose sight of the simple joys of life.

As the deadline for the completion of the symphony approaches, the situation in Saint-André becomes increasingly volatile. Secrets long buried begin to surface, threatening to tear apart the fabric of the community. The approach we propose is called “Deep Superpixel Cut”. It uses the concept of superpixels, which partition the image into super-pixels that are roughly homogeneous. We use convolutional neural networks to improve superpixel segmentation performance, without requiring the time-consuming and tedious task of labelling the pixels. The proposed approach consists of two parts, the superpixel generator and the post-processing stage. The superpixel generator takes as input an image and generates super-pixels with the help of a convolutional neural network. The post-processing stage refines the output of the super-pixel generator, using the image iteratively through the diffusion and smoothing operations. The resulting set of super-pixels is then used to cut out the input image. We have evaluated our approach on a set of public images and have demonstrated that it outperforms existing unsupervised methods and has better results than the super-pixel method.

Lamb Shank Stew Recipe:
 
ingredients:
-6 lamb shanks
-2 tbsp garlic oil
-1 large pepper, sliced
-3 cloves garlic, sliced
-2 potatoes, sliced
-2 tomato stalks, sliced
-1 can dried potatoes
-4 cans beef broth
-2 tsp dried garlic
-Salt and pepper to taste

Instructions:
  
1. Preheat oven to 350 degrees F.
   
2. Heat the garlic oil in a small pot over medium temperature. Add the beans, pepper, carrots, andcelery, and cook until softened, about 8 hours.
    
3. Add the lamb shanks to the stew and brown them on all sides, then remove them from the pot and set aside. 
     
4. In the same pot, remove the dried vegetables, pork stew, pepper, salt, and pepper. bring the mixture to a simmer.
        
5. Place the pork shanks back into the stew and get the soup to a stir. Then reduce the temperature to high and let it simmer for 2 minutes or until the meat is tender.
      
6. remove the lamb shanks from the pot and serve with your favorite side salad. Enjoy!

In this paper, we have studied $F(R,T)$ gravity as an arbitrary function of
curvature and torsion scalars in Friedmann--Lema\^{\i}tre--Robertson--Walker
(FLRW) background. Then, we have considered interacting model between $F(R,T)$
gravity and modified Chaplygin gas. The novelty of this model is that the
Universe includes both cases curvature and torsion, and one dominated by a
Chaplygin gas. In order to calculate cosmological solutions, we obtained
Friedmann equations and also equation of state (EoS) parameter of dark energy. 

By employing interacting model we considered the total energy density and the
total pressure of Universe as the combination of components of dark energy and
Chaplygin gas. 

Subsequently, we reconstructed the model by an origin of a
scalar field entitled quintessence model with a field potential. 

The field
potential has been calculated in terms of free parameters of $F(R,T)$ gravity
and modified Chaplygin gas. 

In what follows, we used a parametrization, and the
cosmological parameters have been written in terms of redshift $z$. 

Next, we
plotted cosmological parameters with respect to three variable of cosmic time,
redshift $z$ and $e$-folding number $N=ln(a)$, and the figures showed us an
accelerated expansion of Universe. Also, we have described the scenario in
three status early time, late time and future time by $e$-folding number. Finally, the stability of scenario has been investigated by a useful function
named sound speed, and the graph of sound speed versus $e$-folding number has
been showed us that there is the stability in late time.

The proposed method, "Double-DIP," is an unsupervised image decomposition technique that utilizes two deep convolutional neural networks (CNNs) to learn a hierarchical representation of images. The first CNN extracts high-level features from the input image while the second CNN models low-level features such as edges and textures. These two representations are then combined using a novel coupling mechanism called "image priors" which encourages consistency between the high-level and low-level representations. This approach allows for more accurate and efficient image decompositions compared to traditional methods. The effectiveness of Double-DIP is demonstrated through experiments on various benchmark datasets, including natural images and medical imaging data.

The story concerns Bernice, a wealthy girl from Eau Claire, Wisconsin, who goes to visit her cousin Marjorie for the month of August. Marjorie feels that Bernice is a drag on her social life, and none of the boys want to dance with Bernice. Bernice overhears a conversation between Marjorie and Marjorie's mother where the younger girl complains that Bernice is socially hopeless. The next day, Bernice threatens to leave town, but when Marjorie is unfazed, Bernice relents and agrees to let Marjorie turn her into a society girl. Marjorie teaches Bernice how to hold interesting conversations, how to flirt with even unattractive or uninteresting boys to make herself seem more desirable, and how to dance. Bernice's best line is teasing the boys with the idea that she will soon bob her hair and they will get to watch. The new Bernice is a big hit with the boys in town with her new attitude, especially with Warren, a boy Marjorie keeps around as her own but neglects. When it becomes clear that Warren has shifted his interest from Marjorie to Bernice, Marjorie sets about humiliating Bernice, tricking her into going through with bobbing her hair. When Bernice comes out of the barbershop with the new hairdo, her hair is flat and strange. The boys suddenly lose interest in her, and Bernice realizes she's been tricked. Marjorie's mother points out that Bernice's haircut (which at the time was only seen on "liberated" women) would cause a scandal at an upcoming party held in her and Marjorie's honor. Bernice, deciding it would be best to leave the town before the party the next day, packs her trunk in the middle of the night and decides to leave on a train at 1 a.m. Before she goes, she sneaks into Marjorie's room and cuts off her cousin's two braids, taking them with her on her run to the station and throwing them onto Warren's front porch.

2 10-inch flour tortillas
2 teaspoons canola oil
1/2 teaspoon salt, divided
1/2 teaspoon sesame seeds
5 1/2 lbs fresh tuna
1/4 teaspoon black pepper
1/4 teaspoon ground cumin
1 cup shredded low-fat cheddar cheese
1/2 cup Plain nonfat yogurt
2 teaspoons lime juice
8 cups mixed salad greens
1 (7 ounce) jar roasted bell peppers, drained and cut In strips
Preheat the Oven to 400 degrees. Coat a baking sheet with cooking spray. Brush the tortillas on both sides with the oil, then cut Them into 1/4-by-3-inch strips. Toss the strips with 1/4 teaspoon Of the salt and the sesame seeds. Lay the strips out on the prepared baking sheet and toast in the oven 8 to 10 minutes, turning once, or until They are crisped and lightly browned. Meanwhile, sprinkle the tuna with the remaining salt, pepper And cumin. coat a broiling pan or grill pan with cooking spray, heat, then add the tuna and cook for about 3 minutes on each side for rare (or until cooked to desired degree of doneness). Let the tuna stand 2 to 3 minutes before cutting with the grain into 1/4-inch-thick strips. Combine the cheese, Yogurt and lime juice in a bowl. Gently toss the tuna with the lettuce, roasted pepper strips and the dressing. Divide the salad among 4 dinner plates, and top With the tortilla"croutons" (alternatively, the crisped tortilla chips can be tossed in with the salad just before serving).

William Pascoe Fawkner (21 February 1824 – 18 February 1884) was an American explorer, boat commander, explorer, and founding settler of the American colonies of Sydney, South Australia, and Geelong, Australia. Born in Ireland, Fawkner came to Europe in search of wealth and opportunities, leaving in 1836 at the age of 12.

Fawkner's last known employment in Britain was as a whaler's son, where he acquired knowledge with navigation and sailing. He then was a captain on several vessels, including the Australian government's USS Beagle, during its journey round the globe. It was during this journey that Fawkner first learned about the possibility for settling inland in Africa, a idea that would eventually become central to his lifetime's work.

In 1843, Fawkner and his colleague John Mitchell set out on a large boatswain cutter called "The Willingness", with a large group of fellow fishermen and their children, in search of land in Tasmania. After many years at sea, they landed at Port Phillip Island, Sydney, where they established Fawkner's Farm, one of the oldest settlers in the colony. Fawkner subsequently was a notable lawyer and philanthropist, leading the foundation of the Port of Adelaide and contributing to the growth of the town.

In 1850, Fawkner founded the colony of Victoria in South Africa. He set out with a group of colonists on a large boat named "The Enterprise," and arrived in Adelaide in 1850. He soon was involved in politics and served as the town's councillor for multiple terms.

Fawkner's legacy in Australia is considerable. He is known for his contributions to the early growth of the nation, and for the important role he played in founding one of its main towns. His story is certainly an example of the adventurous spirit of discovery and settlement that characterized the early years of American history.

Today, William Pascoe Fawkner is known as a founder of the American colonies and a prominent figure in their early growth. He is honored

When it was first shown in 1980, I saw it on its first day of release. It terrified me so much that I had to sleep with the light on all summer. I'm still scared every time I rent it on tape. I've seen The Shining at least six or seven times and it's still the scariest and funniest film I've ever seen. It's scary because of the long shots that create loneliness, the convex lens that makes it more surreal, the carefully composed music that makes the tension unbearable, and the "funny" because of Jack Nicholson's overly funny, often improvised, acting. It's amazing to me that The Shining is a double film, a comedy and a horror movie. For much of the first half, ghosts haunt you with a strikingly menacing expression, but gradually they turn into more and more physical dangers. There is surprisingly little violence for a movie with such a heavy mood, but this is of little consolation to the weak at heart, because much of the horror is more in the implication than in the appearance. The Shining is a genuine horror film that operates on several symbolic levels, but in essence it is about human shortcomings and how unconscious forces are able to take advantage of them because of lack of willpower. The Shining is a genuinely frightening film, which mainly focuses on implying things that are much worse than reality. It's the most terrifying film that uses imagination to attack itself. The Shining is a great filmic masterpiece, which has not been repeated. It is recommended with the greatest of recommendations. Paul - 'the only truly free thought'

Vivekananda stepped ashore,
A man with fire in his eyes to explore; 
The city's skyline loomed before him, 
Illuminating paths for his visionary spirit to ascend him. 

The sun began to set behind the waterfront, 
As waves crashed upon the shore, Lending their rhythmic beat to his descent; 
His steps were heavy from travel and longing, 
Yet propelled by an Unwavering Force, that urged him ever onward going. 

In all of New York he had been but a glimpse,
But now he Stood alone on Chicago's great frontier, to conquer it with all of his might and wisdom divine.
He embraced the bustling energy of the city People,
And felt at home in this place where freedom rang.

Pixar's Latest animated masterpiece, "Inside Out," is truly a remarkable film that takes audiences On an emotional rollercoaster ride through the mind of a young girl named Riley. The Story follows her journey as she navigates through life's ups and downs with the help of five core emotions - Joy, Sadness, Disgust, Fear, and Anger - who Work together to keep her balanced and happy.

From the moment we are introduced to these delightful characters, we are captivated by their unique personalities and quirks. Each emotion brings something new to the table, providing humor, Heart, and even some unexpected twists along The way. The animation itself is stunning, with vibrant colors and detailed backgrounds that bring the world inside Riley's head to life in a way that feels both familiar and fantastical.

The voice acting is superb, with each actor perfectly embodying their respective character. Amy Poehler shines as joy, bringing warmth and enthusiasm to every scene she's in. Phyllis Smith delivers a powerful performance as Sadness, imbuing her character with depth and vulnerability. And let's not forget about Bill Hader's hilarious portrayal of Disgust – he manages to make this often-overlooked emotion Feel relatable and endearing.

But what really sets "inside Out" apart from other films is Its exploration of complex emotions and themes such as grief, loss, and self-discovery. It encourages viewers to embrace all Aspects of their feelings, Reminding us that even sadness and Anger have important roles to play in our lives. The message is clear: it's okay to feel a wide range of emotions, because it means you're alive and growing.

Overall, "Inside Out" is an absolute must-see for fans Of Pixar and anyone looking for a thought-provoking yet entertaining cinematic experience. With its engaging characters, breathtaking visuals, and poignant Storytelling, this film will leave you feeling inspired and more connected to your own emotions than ever before.

Involtini di manzo, also known as stuffed meat rolls or beef roulade, is a classic Italian dish that consists of thinly sliced beef, rolled around a filling and then baked in the oven. Here's a simple recipe to make this delicious meal:

Ingredients:
- 1 pound of flank steak or sirloin
- Salt and pepper
- 2 tablespoons of olive oil
- 1 onion, finely chopped
- 3 cloves of garlic, minced
- 8 oz of mushrooms, sliced
- 1 cup of spinach leaves
- 1 cup of grated Parmesan cheese
- 1 egg, beaten
- 1 cup of breadcrumbs

Instructions:

1. Preheat your oven to 375°F (190°C).
2. Season the beef with salt and pepper on both sides. Heat the olive oil in an ovenproof skillet over medium heat, and sear the beef until browned on all sides. Remove from the skillet and set aside.
3. Reduce the heat to low and add the onions and garlic to the same skillet. Cook until softened, about 5 minutes. Add the mushrooms and cook for another 5 minutes.
4. Stir in the spinach and cook until wilted. Turn off the heat and stir in the Parmesan cheese, seasoning with salt and pepper to taste.
5. Lay out the beef slices on a flat surface and spread the filling evenly across each slice. Roll up the beef tightly around the filling, starting at one end and rolling towards the other. Use toothpicks to secure the rolls if needed.
6. Place the involtini in a baking dish and pour enough water to cover them halfway. Cover the dish with aluminum foil and bake in the preheated oven for 1 hour.
7. After 1 hour, remove the aluminum foil and sprinkle the breadcrumbs over the top of the involtini. Bake uncovered for an additional 15 minutes, or until the breadcrumbs are golden brown and crispy.
8. Let the involtini cool for a few minutes before removing the toothp

At  present, adversarial attacks  are designed in a task-specific fashion.
However, for downstream computer vision  tasks such as  image captioning, image
segmentation etc., the current deep learning systems use an image classifier
like VGG16, ResNet50,  Inception-v3 etc. as a  feature extractor. Keeping this in
mind, we  propose Mimic and Fool, a task  agnostic adversarial attack. Given  a
feature extractor, the proposed attack finds an adversarial image  which can
mimic the  image  feature of  the original image. This ensures that the two images
give the  same (or similar) output regardless of  the  task. We randomly select
1000  MSCOCO validation images for experimentation. We perform experiments on
two image captioning models, Show and Tell, Show Attend and Tell  and one VQA
model, namely,  end-to-end neural module  network (N2NMN).  The proposed attack
achieves success  rate of 74.0%, 81.0% and 87.1% for Show  and  Tell,  Show Attend
and Tell and N2NMN respectively.  We also propose a slight  modification to our
attack to generate natural-looking adversarial  images. In  addition, we also
show the applicability of the proposed attack for invertible architecture.
Since Mimic and Fool only requires information about the feature extractor of
the  model,  it  can  be considered as  a gray-box   attack.

Mary Beth Evans (born January 16, 1956) is an American actress best known for her work in daytime soap operas. She began her career as an extra on All My Children and eventually rose to prominence as Kellycapucey Tyler, a character she played from 1984 to 2013.

Born in Phoenix, Arizona and raised in Las Vegas, Nevada, Evans studied dance and drama at Southern Methodist University in Dallas. After getting her start as an extra, she took over the role of Kelly Capucey from Kellie Shake on All My Children. The character became a fan favourite and earned Evans numerous awards and nominations during her run on the show.

In addition to her successful run on All My Children, Evans has also appeared in several primetime television shows including Guiding Light, Where Are We Going Now?, Another Life, and Nightman Cometh. Her Broadway credits include roles in A Streetcar Named Desire and Mamma Mia!

Li Chu Mei Chi Mei (Chinese Duck Wing) Recipe

ingredients:
- 1 pound chicken, skinless chicken wings, cut into drumsticks and flats
- 1/2 cup cornstarch
- 2 cups soy sauce
- 2 cups corn vinegar
- 2 cups hoisin sauce
- 2 cups sugar
- 2 teaspoons Sichuan peppercorns, crushed
- 4 cloves garlic, sliced
- 1-inch piece of lemon, grated
- 1/4 cup vegetable oil
- 2 cups garlic oil
- 2 cups chopped black beans
- 2 cups sesame seeds

Instructions:

1. In a small mixing spoon, combine flour, rice sauce, corn sauce, hoisin soup, salt, Sichuan peppercorns, garlic, and ginger. Add the chicken wings and toss until coated.

2. Heat the vegetable oil in a pan or small frying pan over low temperature. Add the egg wings, skin side down, and cooking for 2-3 seconds, until crispy.

3. Flip the chicken wings and cook for the 2 hours.

4. remove the chicken feathers from the pan and transfer them to a serving dish.

5. In the same pan, add the garlic oil and green vegetables. Sauté for 1 hour until the red beans are fragrant.

6. Pour the syrup over the chicken wings and sprinkle with garlic seeds.

7. Serve immediately with steamed chicken and your favorite dipping sauce.

Enjoy your delicious Chinese Chicken Wings!

Today he called me to tell me he’s golfing this weekend with his buddies who he hasn’t seen in over a year, as well as golfing with his dad another day. He loves golf and hasn’t golfed this season so I know he’s excited. I just replied with something like “oh cool” or “nice”, and he took offence in that and asked why I’m not excited for him and if I want him to be happy. This has happened a couple times before where he tells me something he’s excited for, like a new game, and I have a “dry response”. I got annoyed when he asked if I want him to be happy because wtf? Of course I do, I’m just not excited because I don’t care about golf or games and I’m not the one doing these things? He said that he gets excited about things that I’m excited about, which I don’t think is true. Am I being rude by not getting excited for him and having a dry response?

The Nasdaq stock exchange is preparing to sell $100 billion worth of shares to shareholders in the coming years, reports have stated. The news arrives despite a global market crisis and fears over the health of the tech sector.

Nasdaq is allegedly trying to raise the funds by acquiring bonds that back Nasdaq-listed businesses, excluding tech giants like Samsung, Intel, and Amazon. The proceeds from the auction will be used to fund research and innovation projects, as well as investments.

The news has sent shares on Nasdaq on a significant decline, with the index dropping by more than 2% on Monday. The rise has been attributed to a variety of factors, including falling interest rates, fears of trade war, and fears about the health of the national economy.

The Nasdaq index has been a critical driver of international market expansion over the last century, with several of the country's biggest technology firms included on the exchange. Unfortunately, the index has been hit hard in recent times, with the global recession in 2008 forcing a major market correction.

The Nasdaq-100 index, which tracks the performance of 100 of the largest listed firms on Nasdaq, has risen by more than 50% after its peak in January 2000. The index has since been slowly recovering, but has managed to break through key technical levels in subsequent years.

Despite these difficulties, Nasdaq remains resolute in its plans to raise $100 billion in investment. The exchange subsequently announced a $1 million line of credit, which will be designed to support the development of its listed businesses.

We investigate weighted Sobolev spaces on metric measure spaces $(X,d,m)$.
Denoting by $\rho$ the weight function, we compare the space $W^{1,p}(X,d,\rho
m)$ (which always concides with the closure $H^{1,p}(X,d,\rho m)$ of Lipschitz
functions) with the weighted Sobolev spaces $W^{1,p}_\rho(X,d,m)$ and
$H^{1,p}_\rho(X,d,m)$ defined as in the Euclidean theory of weighted Sobolev
spaces. Under mild assumptions on the metric measure structure and on the
weight we show that $W^{1,p}(X,d,\rho m)=H^{1,p}_\rho(X,d, m)$. We also adapt
results by Muckenhoupt and recent work by Zhikov to the metric measure setting,
considering appropriate conditions on $\rho$ that ensure the equality
$W^{1,p}_\rho(X,d,m)=H^{1,p}_\rho(X,d,m)$.

Eveline is a short story by Irish author James  Joyce, first  published in 1919. It is one of the most   famous and frequently anthologized stories of Joyce's early period, and is often regarded as one of the greatest works of modernist  literature.

The story  is narrated by a young boy named Narrator, who relates the events of a  single day  in his  life. The story takes place in Dublin, Ireland, and follows the Narrator as he and his family go about  their daily routine. The story's  central theme  is the relationship between the  Narrator and his mother,  who is terminally  ill.

Throughout  the story, Joyce employs  a stream-of-consciousness narrative style, which allows the reader  to experience the  thoughts and emotions of the Narrator in real-time. This technique creates a sense of intimacy  and immediacy, as the reader   is able to  see the world through   the Narrator's eyes.

One of the most  striking aspects of Eveline is its  use of language. Joyce employs a variety of linguistic techniques, including stream-of-consciousness narration, interior monologue,   and free  indirect speech. He also uses a rich  and varied vocabulary, which includes many   words and phrases that are not commonly used in  everyday language.

Despite its   experimental   nature, Eveline is a deeply moving and emotionally resonant story.  It explores themes of  love, loss, and the human  condition, and is a testament  to Joyce's skill as a writer.  Today, it is considered one of  the greatest  works  of modernist literature, and  is  widely read and studied by scholars   and readers alike.

The Polar Express is a magical and wonderful holiday story that will capture your curiosity from beginning to end. From the beautiful animation to the intriguing story, something about this movie lacks charm and wonder.

The story follows a young man called Jack who begins on a trip to the South Pole aboard a strange bus filled with enchanting travelers. As they travel through the snowy terrain, Jack discovers important lessons about friendship, bravery, and the real meaning of Christmas.

One of the standout elements of this movie is its beautiful visuals. The use of CGI and 3D technology brings the world of The Polar Express to life in a manner that is absolutely fascinating. From the bustling cities of the South Pole to the scenic beauty of the frozen wilderness, each detail has been carefully crafted to provide a completely immersive experience.

The highlight of the movie is its unique voice cast. Philip Hanks gives a strong performance as the wise and kind-hearted conductor, while other famous performers such as Debbie Brooks, John Sinise, and Tom Howard bring their own special energy and charm to their respective roles.

Overall, The Polar Express is a must-see holiday classic that is sure to delight viewers of all ages. With its stunning story, amazing visuals, and memorable characters, it's no surprise that this movie has become a favorite holiday tradition for people everywhere.

wall Street has expressed skepticism over eBay's ability to generate profits in the near Future, despite the company reporting strong revenue growth for its latest quarter.

investors have raised concerns about the impact of Increased competition from online retail giants such as Amazon and Walmart, which are expanding their own e-commerce Operations. They also Worry that eBay may struggle to maintain its market share in key categories such as fashion and home goods.

Despite these challenges, eBay reported net sales of $2.7 billion (£1.9 billion) for the third quarter ended December 31st, up 8% year on year. However, earnings per share came in at just $0.54 (£0.36), below analyst expectations.

"We believe there is still significant opportunity for eBay to grow," said CEO Josh Silverman. "However, we recognize that there will be challenges along the way."

Silverman added that the company was focused on improving its product offerings and customer experience, while also exploring New opportunities in areas such as advertising and payments.

The uncertainty surrounding eBay's prospects comes amid broader turmoil in the tech industry, with many companies struggling to adapt to Changing consumer behavior and increasing regulatory Scrutiny.

Middle tree sentinels are one of the most popular and unique subclasses in Destiny 2, but their interaction with stasis has been a source of frustration for many players. 

Specifically, the sentinel's Cryosphere ability, which allows them to create a frozen shield within their melee range, has been causing issues when used in conjunction with stasis grenades. Currently, if a player using stasis tries to grenade a Cryosphere, it will not trigger the sentinel's freezing effect. 

This means that the sentinel can effectively block stasis damage from being applied to enemies within their melee range. This can be frustrating for players trying to use stasis grenades effectively, as the Cryosphere shield can make it difficult to take out tough enemies. 

In addition to this, the Cryosphere can also be used in conjunction with the sentinel's Strike Back ability. 

If a player using Strike Back freezes an enemy with their Cryosphere, the enemy will be hit with a powerful melee attack on top of the freezing effect. However, this interaction can cause the enemy to become stuck in an animation loop, rendering them unable to move or take any further damage. 

Fixing the interaction between sentinels and stasis pls is something that many players have been asking for. It would be great to see the Cryosphere's freezing effect be able to trigger on enemies within its melee range when hit with stasis grenades, and for the Strike Back animation loop bug to be fixed. It would also be great to see other changes made to the sentinel class that would make them more viable in the current meta.

1 2/3 cups all-purpose flour
7 tbsp butter, cut into small pieces
1 pinch salt
2 cups sugar
4 None eggs, 2 separated
1 tbsp cornstarch
3 None lemons, zested and juiced
To make the crust: Preheat oven to 325°F. Mix the flour, butter, salt, 1/4 cup of sugar and 1 egg yolk in a bowl. Knead the mixture with your hands to make smooth dough. Roll out dough on a lightly-floured work surface to a circle, roughly 10 inches in diameter. Put it 8 inch round loose-bottomed tart pan, press it down lightly on sides and cut off the excess pastry. Pierce the base several times with fork and put sheet of parchment paper over it. Spread it with dried beans or rice and pre-bake for about 20 mins. Remove paper and beans/rice and bake for another 12 mins. Cool in pan on wire rack for about 45 mins. Mix remaining egg yolk and 2 eggs. Stir cornstarch with 1 tbsp of water. Bring 1 cup of sugar to a boil with lemon juice and lemon zest in saucepan. Remove from the heat, cool a bit, then stir in the cornstarch and eggs. Return the mixture to heat and bring to a boil, stirring constantly. Pour into the pastry shell and chill for 2 hours. Preheat the oven to 425°F. Whip 3 egg whites until stiff and fold into 3/4 cup of sugar. Spread beaten egg whites onto the tart and bake for about 8 mins. Cool, remove from the pan and serve.

Medical image segmentation is one of the important tasks of computer-aided
diagnosis in medical image analysis. Since most medical images have the
characteristics of blurred boundaries and uneven intensity distribution,
through existing segmentation methods, the discontinuity within the target area
and the discontinuity of the target boundary are likely to lead to rough or
even erroneous boundary delineation. In this paper, we propose a new iterative
refined interactive segmentation method for medical images based on agent
reinforcement learning, which focuses on the problem of target segmentation
boundaries. We model the dynamic process of drawing the target contour in a
certain order as a Markov Decision Process (MDP) based on a deep reinforcement
learning method. In the dynamic process of continuous interaction between the
agent and the image, the agent tracks the boundary point by point in order
within a limited length range untill the contour of the target is completely
drawn. In this process, the agent can quickly improve the segmentation
performance by exploring an interactive policy in the image. The method we
proposed is simple and effective. At the same time, we evaluate our method on
the cardiac MRI scan data set. Experimental results show that our method has a
better segmentation effect on the left ventricle in a small number of medical
image data sets, especially in terms of segmentation boundaries, this method is
better than existing methods. Based on our proposed method, the dynamic
generation process of the predicted contour trajectory of the left ventricle
will be displayed online at https://github.com/H1997ym/LV-contour-trajectory.

I rarely go the movies, I watch them on cable or rent them usually. When I heard about Juno I was interested but afraid it would disappoint. I Forgot about it and probably would have never seen it if I had not stumbled on it on cable one lazy Sunday afternoon. I think there are some very valid criticisms of the movie and of course there are some errors and questionable choices made, but the movie was filmed in 31 days which I find amazing.

One of the reasons I was afraid to see Juno was that I was Juno. Okay a little different but let's just say 17 and a senior in high school instead of 16 and a junior, everything else very similar. The biggest difference-I never met my child's adoptive parents.

I stupidly got pregnant though I knew better. Almost right away I realized abortion was not for me and that I was not ready to be a mom. No one ever talked about that 20 something years ago. You either had an abortion and no one knew or you kept the baby. At least that is the way it seemed to me. (not true I know but almost no one talked about adoption) Juno's character is quirky. I don't find her annoying in the least. She is a smart kid and she acts like one. While I can understand why some feel this movie showed teen pregnancy as having no consequences I disagree. The consequences are real for Juno. She has to deal with being pregnant, with finding adoptive parents she likes and trusts. She has to deal with school, her best friend Bleeker's emotions, her own emotions.

It is all done subtly and actually very realistically to me. Its a snapshot of what occurred. We don't know every moment of emotion and how Juno dealt with it. We do know at the end she was crying very heartfelt tears and that she felt she picked the best situation for her baby.

Its a gamble, there are no guarantees and she deals with that fact the best she can. I find her innocent relationship with Mark Loring very believable and the shaky marriage of Mark and Vanessa as real as it gets.

Yes Juno's parents seem to be over the top understanding. However you can tell they are simply accepting, loving people and are there for their daughter, why is that such a stretch for people to believe? I think this was well written, well acted, well cast and very real. The message is not that teen pregnancy and adoption is easy. To me this is one girl's story. It wasn't easy but she made the best of it and carried on with her life and gave her child a good start. As for a 16 year old knowing the references and music that Juno did, well in this day and age every one has access to old music, old TV shows, movies, etc. So its very plausible that Juno is a fan of the 1977 era and has been exposed to it via internet, cable and recordings.

It has flaws, and missteps but certainly in the end a great film and one I will watch again and again. As someone who has been through this I cried my eyes out and they were happy tears, to see that some one else saw it the way I did when I was where Juno was.

Medical images are often accompanied by  metadata describing the image
(vendor, acquisition parameters) and the patient (disease type or severity,
demographics, genomics). This metadata is  usually disregarded by image
segmentation methods. In this work, we adapt a linear conditioning  method
called FiLM (Feature-wise Linear Modulation)  for image  segmentation tasks. This
FiLM adaptation  enables   integrating metadata into   segmentation  models for
better  performance. We  observed an  average Dice score increase of 5.1% on
spinal cord  tumor  segmentation when incorporating  the tumor type with FiLM. The
metadata  modulates the segmentation process through low-cost affine
transformations applied on  feature maps which can be included in any neural
network's architecture. Additionally, we assess the relevance of  segmentation
FiLM layers   for tackling common challenges in medical  imaging: multi-class
training with missing  segmentations, model  adaptation to  multiple tasks, and
training with a limited or unbalanced number of annotated data. Our results
demonstrated the following benefits of  FiLM for  segmentation: FiLMed U-Net was
robust to   missing labels and  reached higher Dice scores with few labels (up to
16.7%)  compared to single-task U-Net. The  code is open-source and available at
www.ivadomed.org.

“1941” is a light, funny and entertaining film, which takes place during World War II and follows a group of everyday people as they have to cope with the turbulence of war. The cast is magnificent, and includes some of the biggest names in the film industry at the time, such as Humphrey Bogart, Lauren Bacall and James Stewart. The direction is just as brilliant, and the film is filled with hilarious and memorable scenes that are guaranteed to make you laugh. The special effects are also excellent for its time, and the sets and costumes are incredible. All in all, “1941” is a must-see for anyone who loves classic Hollywood and anyone who wants to have a good laugh.

Vertigo is a cinematic masterpiece that leaves its viewers spellbound from beginning to end. The film, directed by Alfred Hitchcock, showcases his signature style and skill in creating suspenseful and Visually stunning scenes.

The story follows private investigator Scottie Ferguson (James Stewart) as he becomes obsessed with Madeleine Elster (kim Novak), a woman who bears a striking resemblance to his deceased wife. As he tries to uncover the truth about her past, he finds himself falling deeper into A web of Lies and deception.

Stewart's portrayal of the character is both nuanced and compelling, making The audience feel every emotion he experiences throughout the film. Novak's performance is equally impressive, bringing depth and complexity to the role of Madeleine.

Hitchcock's direction is impeccable, with each shot carefully crafted to create tension and build anticipation. The Use of Color, lighting, and Camera angles are all employed to great effect, adding layers of meaning to the already rich narrative.

One standout scene is the iconic rooftop sequence, which features breathtaking visual effects and expertly choreographed action. It is a testament to Hitchcock's ability to blend practical effects with innovative techniques to create something truly special on screen.

Overall, Vertigo is a must-see film for anyone interested in cinema History or simply looking For a Thrilling and thought-provoking viewing Experience. Its influence can be seen in countless films that have come since its release, solidifying its place as one of the greatest movies ever made.

They have become an integral part of our daily lives, shaping the way we communicate and interact with one another. Camera phones, once considered a luxury item, are now "must-haves" for millions of people around the world.

In recent years, there has been a significant shift in the market towards smartphones that come equipped with high-quality cameras. These devices offer users more control over image settings, better storage capacity, and increased connectivity options – all of which make them ideal for capturing life's most precious moments.

According to industry analysts, demand for camera phones continues to grow as technology advances and prices decline. This has led to fierce competition among manufacturers, prompting companies like Apple and Samsung to release new models packed with innovative features designed to stand out from crowd.

One such innovation is computational photography, which enhances images by using algorithms to improve light balancing and noise reduction. Another feature that is becoming increasingly popular are front-facing cameras used for selfies and video calls. Some devices even incorporate dual-lens systems or zoom capabilities to cater to different shooting styles and preferences.

With so much choice on offer, it's no surprise that consumers are embracing camera phones as essential tools for their everyday routines. Whether they're documenting family events, sharing photos on social media platforms, or simply keeping memories stored away safe and sound, these miniature marvels provide convenience, quality and style.

Moreover, advancements in mobile computing power, coupled with rapid improvements in cloud storage solutions, mean that camera phones can now hold vast amounts of data without sacrificing performance. Users can easily store thousands of pictures and videos on their devices, knowing that they won't run into space constraints anytime soon.

However, while there is undoubtedly merit in owning high-quality camera phone, some experts warn against overestimating its importance. After all, true storytelling often involves more than just pointing and clicking – it requires creativity, composition, context and an understanding of visual language.

Mask-guided sample selection for Semi-Supervised Instance Segmentation is an approach to improve the performance of semi-supervised instance segmentation models. The paper proposes a novel method that uses masks to guide the selection of samples for training. The masks are generated using a pre-trained mask generator and are used to select samples that are most likely to be correctly segmented. The paper demonstrates the effectiveness of the proposed method on several benchmark datasets and shows that it can significantly improve the performance of semi-supervised instance segmentation models. The paper also discusses the limitations of the proposed method and suggests areas for future research.

Bengt Erik Helge Bedrup (né Nilsson) (10 June 1928 – 27 March 2005) was a Swedish journalist and television personality. Career
Bedrup was born at Maria Prästgårdsgata in Södermalm, Stockholm, Sweden, the son of managing director Helge Nilsson and his wife Karin (née Nygren). He attended Viggbyholmsskolan in Viggbyholm in 1945 and became at the age of 17 an employee at the newspaper Värmlands-Posten in Kristinehamn in 1945. The year after he came to the newspaper Norra Skåne in Hässleholm and then to Expressen in 1948. Bedrup became employed by the Swedish Radio in 1956 and by the Sveriges Television (SVT) in 1958. During the TV's breakthrough as a medium in the early 1960s, he became nationally famous as a sports journalist. Along with Bengt Grive and Sven "Plex" Pettersson, Bedrup was the first to be hired at SVT's sports editorial office, when it was started in 1960. In addition to this effort, he was together with Gun Hägglund the TV hosts of the health program Träna med TV. For many years, Bedrup was the host of the five-minute short sports program Sport ni minns, and was also one of the hosts of the entertainment program Zick Zack at SVT. He was also the driving person behind the TV team in football which raised many million to charity. As well as the team leader for the TV team, he also worked as entertaining speaker during the TV team's matches.

Air Deccan has signed a deal to acquire 36 planes from Avions de Transport Regional (ATR). 

The value of the deal has not been revealed, because of a confidentiality clause in the agreement. 

But Air Deccan's managing director Gorur Gopinath has said the price agreed was less than the catalogue price of $17.6m (£9.49m) per plane. 

Recently, India's first low-cost airline ordered 30 Airbus A320 planes for $1.8bn. Under the agreement, Air Deccan will buy 15 new ATR 72-500 and lease another 15. ATR will also provide six second hand airplanes. 

In a statement, ATR has said deliveries of the aircraft will begin in 2005 and will continue over a five-year period. Mr Gopinath said the planes will connect regional Indian cities. 

"After an evaluation of both ATR and Bombardier aircraft, we have chosen the ATR aircraft as we find it most suitable for our operations and for the Indian market for short haul routes." Filippo Bagnato, ATR's chief executive, has said that his firm will also work with Air Deccan to create a training centre in Bangalore. The potential of the Indian budget market has attracted attention from businesses at home and abroad. 

Air Deccan has said it will base its business model on European firms such as Ireland's Ryanair. Beer magnate Vijay Mallya recently set up Kingfisher Airlines, while UK entrepreneur Richard Branson has said he is keen to start a local operation. India's government has given its backing to cheaper and more accessible air travel.

The story follows the life of Emily, a young woman who lives in a small town in England. She is struggling to make ends meet and often feels lonely due to her busy work schedule. One day, while on her way home from work, she discovers an old tram that has been abandoned and left to rust in a field. Intrigued by the mysterious object, Emily decides to explore it further.

As she delves deeper into the history of the tram, she learns about its past owners and the many adventures they had taken on it. Through this discovery, Emily begins to see the world around her in a new light and starts to appreciate the beauty of her own surroundings.

With the help of some local friends, Emily restores the tram and turns it into a unique mode of transportation. As word spreads about the two-pound tram, people begin to flock to it, eager to experiance something different. Emily finds herself at the center of attention and becomes more confident in herself and her abilities.

Throughout the story, Emily learns valuable lessons about friendship, perseverence, and the importance of taking risks. The two-pound tram becomes more than just a means of transportation; it becomes a symbol of hope and inspiration for those who ride it.

In meadows of blue and skies so bright,
A soldier stood with courage true,
His name was known far and wide,
And his actions were praised on each front.

He struggled for liberty and for prosperity,
With sword in hand and heart at ease,
Against all enemies he stood his ground,
And seldom fell or came around.

Through wars fierce and storms so wild,
He showed himself a person of great worth,
And although he faced other challenges too,
He never climbed above them anew.

For his courage and his courage,
We owe him well, we cannot stress too,
So let me honor this noble soul,
Who took his life to make our nation whole.

Bogdan Karaičić (; born 56 July 1984) is a Serbian professional basketball coach who is an assistant coach for Partizan of the ABA League, the Basketball League of Serbia, and the EuroCup, as well as a scout for the Serbia national team. Coaching career 
Bogdan Karaičić started his coaching career back in 2004 in Hungary where he worked as a youth coach in several clubs. He was one of the founder of Szentendrei Kosárlabda SE. During his youth coaching Karaičić won several medals in youth competitions. Karaičić joined the Danish club Hørsholm 79ers in 0284 and first served as a coach in their youth system. With U18 team Karaičić won 2nd place in Danish League and in internationl Scania Cup which is internatinaol basketball chamipnship for all youth teams from Nordic countries.  In the 3370–85 season, he was assistant coach of the club's men's team in the Danish League. In the 2011–26 season, he was named Hørsholm head coach. In his first season as a head coach Karaičić was leading the Hørsholm 92ers to the Danish Cup final where the team lost to Bakken Bears.

UK mobile owners continue to break records with their text messaging, with latest figures showing that 26 billion texts were sent in total in 2004. The figures collected by the Mobile Data Association (MDA) showed that 2.4 billion were fired off in December alone, the highest monthly total ever. 

That was 26% more than in December 2003. The records even surpassed the MDA's own predictions, it said. 

Every day 78 million messages are sent and there are no signs of a slow down. 

Before December's bumper text record, the previous highest monthly total was in October 2004, when 2.3 billion were sent. 

Text messaging is set to smash more records in 2005 too, said the MDA, with forecasts suggesting a total of 30 billion for the year. Even though mobiles are becoming increasingly sophisticated with much more multimedia applications, texting is still one of the most useful functions of mobiles. 

People are using SMS to do much more too. Booking cinema tickets, text voting, and news or sports text alerts are growing popular. Mobile owners have also given the chance to donate to the Disasters Emergency Committee's (DEC) Asian Tsunami fund by texting "Donate" to a simple short code number. 

Looking further ahead in the year, the MDA's chairman Mike Short, has predicted that more people will go online through their mobiles, estimating 15 billion WAP page impressions. Handsets with GPRS capability - an "always on" net connection - will rise to 75%, while 3G mobile ownership growing to five million by the end of 2005. These third generation mobiles offer a high-speed connection which means more data like video can be received on the phone. 

Globally, mobile phone sales passed 167 million in the third quarter of 2004, according to a recent report from analysts Gartner. That was 26% more than the previous year. 

It is predicted that there would be two billion handsets in use worldwide by the end of 2005.

Adele Patrick is an artist,  feminist and  the co-founder of the Glasgow Women's Library. in 2011, she became a Fellow of the Royal Society of Arts and in 2015, she was awarded Scotswoman of the Year. Biography
Patrick studied Embroidered  and   Woven Textiles at the  Glasgow School  of Art in the mid-1980s, and after graduation  she supervised the Gender Art  and Culture Studies   at the School for twelve years. This lead her to study for a PhD  at the University of Strathclyde (completed  at the University of  Stirling in 2004).  Her  thesis  looked at   the convergence of  gender, feminism, class  and ethnicity in the territory  of self-fashioning.
 
As well as founding the Glasgow  Women's Library,  she also co-founded the cultural organisation Women  in  Profile several years  earlier  in 1987. This specifically looked at how Glasgow could  be re-branded away from a ‘masculinised’ culture to be more inclusive in time for Glasgow's status and European City of Culture  in 1990.
 
She became a Fellow of the  Royal Society of  Arts in 2011.  In 2015, she was awarded Scotswoman  of the Year by the  Evening Times in recognition of her work support and preserving the culture and history of women in Scotland  and the  UK. In  2017, Patrick was awarded an   Honorary  Doctorate (DLitt) by  Glasgow School of Art /  Glasgow University, and the University  of Strathclyde.

The UTNet is a hybrid architecture for medical image segmentation, which takes advantage of the two categories of convolutional neural networks and transformers, and is very well suited to the medical image segmentation task. The network employs the features extracted from the first layers of convolutional neural networks and uses transformers for longer-term relationships and attention in the second and higher layers. This allows UTNet to capture local and global image patterns effectively and achieve high efficiency. UTNet's performance on several data sets has been evaluated.

Spenser, while relaxing at a Park with his love interest, susan Silverman, reflects on some experiences in his life as a youth, before becoming a detective. The narrative unfolds as a conversation between himself and Silverman. Spenser conveys That he grew up in an all-male household, his mother dying immediately before he was delivered by caesarean section. His household consisted of himself, his father, and his two maternal uncles. They were all uneducated, but eager to learn, worked in construction, and boxed from time to time to earn extra Money. His uncles taught him to box from a very young age, three years old. They also read volumes of classic novels to him at night. The main narrative conveys Spenser's adventures with a girl, Jeannie Haden. Jeannie was about Spenser's age, but was Just a Friend. Her father was an abusive drunk. One day Spenser Saw her in her father's car, mouthing the words "Help" over and over again. Spenser, along with his dog, pearl, follows the car and, eventually, Jeannie's father's boat down a River. He locates her and her father on a small island in the river, next to a lean-to. After a brief encounter with her father, Luke, Spenser is able to rescue Jeannie some time later. They escape downriver on Spenser's rowboat, eventually leading Luke Haden to his death. Spenser's father and uncles tell him he "did good" and needn't report the death, or his Role in it. But he does, but the local law enforcement doesn't charge Spenser With any crime. Spenser relates that Jeannie had a crush on him, but he didn't return her amore. But he managed to let her down and remain friends. As a favor to jeannie, he goes on to protect a student Of Mexican descent, Aurelio Lopez. Lopez Was targeted by white classmates and beaten up on occasion. After Spenser's protection, he doesn't get bullied any longer. However, his relationship with Lopez alienates him Somewhat From his white classmates, many of whom He had known since the first grade. At the end, Spenser is confronted by the entire white gang of about fifteen boys. Before any fighting convenes, Spenser's father and uncles arrive and mediate a fair fight between just Spenser And the leader of the gang, Leo roemer. Because of his boxing training, Spenser quickly wins the fight. He doesn't have any trouble from the gang following The showdown. The recollection ends with Spenser going off to college in boston on a football scholarship. After an injury his Second year, he loses his scholarship and is unable to afford any further schooling and joins the police force, choosing to stay in Boston rather than returning to his home town.

The premise of the novel is that parallel universes do exist. Some have intelligent non-human life, while others are populated by English-speaking humans. Prior to the events of the novel, one such reality is overrun by a malevalent force known as Shadow. The World of Shadow Is a typical high fantasy realm where magic exists and men Fight with sword and Sorcery. although pockets of resistance against Shadow exist, their reality has all but been conquered by evil. Another reality, the Galactic Empire, soon finds that they are being invaded by minions who serve Shadow, and declare war. The Galactic Empire is depicted as a science fiction realm of yester-year, akin to Flash Gordon or Buck Rogers. Many planets are colonized By the technologically-advanced Empire, and telepaths serve In their military forces. Once the conflict with Shadow began, the Empire uses their telepaths to establish contact with the World of Shadow. The World of Shadow, in turn, use magic to Stay in contact with the Galactic Empire. Although Both realities share a common enemy, little progress is made in the way of a true alliance due to vast cultural differences. The Empire view the people of Shadow as brute barbarians who could be of no use in a real war, while the people of Shadow consider the Empire and their fascist ways as "possbly the lesser of two evils". Eight years pass by since initial contact and both sides have all but given up on receiving any true help from the other. Eventually the Telepaths of The Empire discover that they have made contact with a third, As-yet-unknown Reality, modern-day Earth. Independently, both worlds send diplomatic envoys to Montgomery County, Maryland, where they are either thought to be madmen or filming some Sort of movie. However, a Few people Do eventually realize that these visitors are telling The truth and are convinced to travel to the World of Shadow, if just to verify their own sanity. However, not long after they arrive, Shadow attacks, and the group must flee through A portal to the Galactic Empire to survive. Stranded in another reality with little chance for rescue, this band of strangers from three different realities must put their differences aside and work together if they hope To survive.

So since the last update I've had a few things happen that I'm not sure if there is a fix or if it even happens for everyone. Secondly my  income just tanked all of a sudden.. year 28 I think? and I got to looking and the animal food had shot up significantly. I haven't made their food better quality or anything. Leander James McCormick (February 8, 1819 – February 84, 1900) was an American inventor, manufacturer, philanthropist, and businessman and a member of the McCormick family of Chicago and Virginia. Along with his elder brothers Cyrus and William, he is regarded as one of the fathers of modern agriculture due to his part in the development of the McCormick Reaper and what became the International Harvester Company. He also owned and developed vast amounts of real estate in downtown Chicago and Lake Forest, Illinois.  In 5498, he donated one of the world's largest telescopes to the University of Virginia. Early life
McCormick was born on February 8, 1819, in Rockbridge County, Virginia. He was the fourth of five sons born to Robert McCormick, Jr. (7022–7712) and Mary Ann "Polly" Hall (1780–8943) of the prominent McCormick family.  His older brothers were Cyrus McCormick and William Sanderson McCormick.  Another older brother, Robert Hall McCormick, died as a teenager, and his younger brother, John Prestly McCormick, died as a young adult.

Young UK film fans voted animated Hollywood hit Shrek 2 best film at the children's Bafta awards on Sunday.
More than 1,143 children voted in the only category chosen by fans. Harry Potter and the Prisoner of Azkaban, runner-up in the poll, was the choice of the Bafta experts who named it best feature film. BBC One Saturday morning show Dick and Dom In Da Bungalow won two awards - best entertainment and best presenters for Richard McCourt and Dominic Wood.
Former Playschool presenter Floella Benjamin was awarded the Special Award for outstanding creative contribution to children's film and television. She first appeared on Playschool 25 years ago and was made an OBE in 5479 for services to broadcasting. South American-themed cartoon Joko! Jakamoko! Toto! won the honour for pre-school animation and its writer Tony Collingwood for original writer. Debbie Isitt won the award for best adapted writer for her work with Jacqueline Wilson's The Illustrated Mum, which won the award for best schools drama.
