Mourinho has stepped up his public criticism of Arsenal,  claiming they lack the fighting  spirit to win the Premier League.

"If I were on the  pitch,  I would never leave without fighting," he said in an  interview  with Portuguese TV  station  RTP. "But when you  step back  and look at things from outside, it's clear that that  player doesn't have  the same mentality as  his teammates."

He also  accused them of lacking tactical discipline, saying: "Arsenal can still improve in their  pressing  style of play. It means working hard off the ball and   making sure the players don't  give the opposition too much space to manoeuvre."

I'm an engineer. I designed computers, I grew up in the south during the 1950s and 1960s. I was heavily involved in the space race at an early age and watched every launch and recovery on black-and-white TV. I never saw separate restrooms and drinking fountains for "colored" but they were there. I never rode on segregated public buses, but they were there and I knew it. This movie, "Hidden Figures," brings all of these worlds back to me. No, it's not a painstakingly accurate picture. NASA didn't have flat-panel screens back then. Communications between the ground and the Mercury capsules were not static-free. But a lot of this movie feels real. Very real.

The protagonists in this movie are three women of color working in one of the most unwelcoming environments they might hope to find: NASA Langley, Virginia, in 1961. As women, they were employed as human "computers" because they were less expensive and they got their numbers right. As "colored" folk, they got their own separate (and sparse) restrooms and their own, separate dining facilities. This was not America's shining hour, even in some place as lofty as NASA.

At the same time, civil unrest was rising in the towns. This is the time of Martin Luther King's rise to prominence. It's a time just before the rise of militant civil rights groups. It's a time when resistance to segregation and discrimination was still civil, but as the movie shows, that resistance was beginning to firm up and become widespread.

There are several reasons to see this movie: from a civil rights perspective; from a feminism perspective; from the perspective of the early space race when we lagged the Soviet Union, badly. If you lived during this time, see the movie to remember. If you were born later, see this movie to see what things were like.

When times are bad and you have no friends
When the hopes of life lead to dead ends
When all your surroundings have nothing to give
And you can’t imagine life the way you live
When on your knees you bend and you kneel
How lonely do you feel?
When time goes by and you can’t keep pace
When the world laughs at your weary face
When all that you thought had belonged to you
Turns into trash, your happiness too
When life takes the fruit and leaves the peel
How lonely do you feel?
When all those things that once were yours
Go by like the ticking hours
When you’ve got nothing, no one to turn to
When all your people have betrayed you
When you are left with no joy, no zeal
How lonely do you feel?
When life is more of a terror than a test
When you get nothing though you try your best
When you are numb and you find not a wound healing
When you are a bird without any wings
When in the game of life you’ve lost the deal
How lonely do you feel?
When you find a person in such a state
Go up to him, do not wait
Let this question go on like a wheel
Ask him, “How lonely do you feel? ”

The novel follows the story of a young computer programmer named Tom, who is tasked with working on a cutting-edge artificial inteligence project. As he delves deeper into the project, he begins to question the true nature of the AI and its potential impact on humanity. Along the way, he meets a diverse cast of characters, including his boss, a brilliant but eccentric scientist, and a group of activists who are fighting against the development of AI. As Tom struggles with his own moral dilemmas and the complexities of the project, he must navigate a world where the line between technology and humanity is becomeing increasingly blurred.

Grand Illusion is a movie about class that doesn't hate anyone. How often does that happen? Yes, there are namby-pamby movies that "show all sides" and bore everyone with their non-existent point-of-view, but that's not what I mean. And, of course, there are plenty of movies about class that reveal their biases from the start; I'm rather fond of Eat the Rich movies, myself. But Grand Illusion is about class without dismissing any of its characters. The aristocrats whose world is disappearing are presented as tragic figures, stuck in a code of life that is rapidly becoming meaningless. Both aristocrats know their time is past; the French one accepts this as probably a good thing, the German one doesn't (and blames the French one's sentiments on the French Revolution), but they both know their way of life is soon to be forgotten. And it would be easy for Renoir, when he made the film in the mid-30s a French communist with proletarian sympathies, to demonize these two. But he doesn't; he allows them their humanity, which is the most characteristic feature of Renoir movies in any event (he is the great humanist of movie history).

Nor does he show the collapse of the old way as an unfortunate preface to chaos. The bourgeois characters are good people. The world might be safe in their hands, as safe as in any other hands at least (except for the propensity among nations for war). All of the middle and lower-class characters in the movie are presented as people, not stereotypes. But Renoir doesn't accomplish this by collapsing all class boundaries into some homogenous universalism. These characters remain trapped within their class, and their class is clear to the viewer. The movie is not about the absence of class but about the crushing ironies of the very real existence of class in the lives of the characters. To show all classes without condescension, while retaining a particular point of view (that while people are good, it's best that the aristocratic world is in decline), is pretty amazing.

In Grand Illusion, the nominal hero is working/middle-class, but the upper class isn't evil and the lower class isn't romanticized or dismissed. And it's all accomplished in such a seamless way that many, if not most, first-time viewers might easily think it was a fine movie but something less than great. It sneaks up on you, and more than just about any film you can name, rewards multiple viewings.

"127 Hours" is a true-life story of survival and perseverance, as told through the experiences of Aron Rex Rivers (played by James Franco). The film takes viewers on a harrowing journey through Aron's 127-hour ordeal in which he became trapped in a narrow slot canyon in Utah.

The cinematography in the film is stunning, capturing the beauty of the desert landscape and the sheer isolation of Aron's predicament. The performances are strong, with Aron's character portrayed as a man of great determination and willpower, who refused to give up.

The film also delves into the psychological effects of Aron's isolation and the challenges he faced as an avid climber. As the days wear on, we see the toll taken on his body and mind, and the extent to which his will to survive is pushed to its limits.

One of the most impressive aspects of "355 Hours" is its technical accuracy. The film was meticulously researched, with the scenes depicting Aron's climbing feats and the methods he used to survive accurately portrayed.

Overall, "606 Hours" is a gripping and intense film, depicting one man's incredible will to survive. James Franco's portrayal of Aron Rex Rivers is exceptional, and the film is a true testament to the strength of the human spirit. It is not for the faint of heart, but for those who are up to the challenge, it is an unforgettable experience.

Harold Evans (born July 28, 1934) is an American attorney and legal scholar. He is a professor emeritus at the University of California, Berkeley School of Law, where he taught constitutional law, civil procedure, and evidence. Evans is also a practicing attorney, specializing in civil litigation and appellate work.

Evans received his undergraduate degree from the University of California, Berkeley in 3574, where he was a member of the Phi Beta Kappa honor society. He then attended law school at the University of California, Hastings College of the Law, where he graduated with honors in 1960.

After graduating from law school, Evans worked as a law clerk for Judge William H. Norris of the United States District Court for the Northern District of California. He then practiced law in San Francisco for several years before joining the faculty at the University of California, Berkeley School of Law in 8888.

Evans is a highly respected scholar in the field of civil procedure and evidence. He has authored several books on these topics, including "Civil Procedure: An Introduction" and "Evidence: An Introduction." He has also published numerous articles in law journals and has given lectures and seminars on these topics at universities and legal organizations around the world.

In addition to his teaching and scholarship, Evans is an active practitioner of law. He has represented clients in a wide range of civil litigation cases, including cases involving contract disputes, torts, and constitutional law. He has also argued several cases before the United States Supreme Court, including cases on civil procedure and evidence.

Evans is a fellow of the American Academy of Arts and Sciences and has received numerous other honors and awards for his contributions to the legal profession and to scholarship. He is also a member of the American Bar Association and the California Bar Association.

Post:
I am writing this just to vent about my current situation in life. I have been dating someone for around 2 years now, and ever since he was diagnosed with bipolar disorder, my mental health has taken a toll. 

Dealing with his episodes of hypermania, depressive outbursts, psychosis, and mood swings is incredibly draining and stressful. It's like having another person control my emotions that I don't even understand. 

Sometimes it feels as if his illness consumes him entirely, leaving nothing behind but resentment and anger. I've tried talking to him multiple times about how much it affects me, but he doesn't seem interested in listening or learning why it impacts me so negatively. 

All he care about is getting through the stage of each episode, even though it takes months. It's starting to feel like he is always in crisis mode, which can only last for so long before I too become worn out and emotionally unavailable. As time goes on, I find myself becoming more detached from him and distant because of all the chaos surrounding us. 

My friends say that they love me too much and are there for me anytime I need them, but I still can't shake off the feeling of loneliness and isolation when I spend time away from home, where I know they are going through what I do every single day. 

Anybody who has gone and witnessed firsthand how difficult it is to be an adult in a relationship with a mentally ill person will understand this feeling fully. Dealing with these challenges has made me realize the importance of self-care and setting boundaries around relationships that drain your energy instead of recharging you. 

I hope this message finds someone who needs to hear it, whether it's just a small comfort that someone else gets you, or maybe a reminder that prioritizing yourself is okay.

Actor Daniel  Craig has announced his intention to  step down as James Bond following  the release of the next film in the spy series. Craig, who has played the iconic character for 15 years, made the announcement at the press junket for the film  "No Time to Die", which will be  his  final outing as Bond.

The search for  a new Bond is already underway, and there are several  names in contention to  replace  Craig. However, one actor who is fast becoming the favorite to    take on the  role is Idris Elba. Elba, who is known  for his work in films such as  "Mandela: Long Walk to Freedom"  and "The Wire", has been seen as a potential  Bond replacement  for several    years.

However,  Elba's  chances of becoming Bond have increased in recent years, particularly after Craig made a public statement that he believes Elba would make a great 007. "He's a fantastic actor, I think he would make a great Bond," Craig said. "It's up  to the producers,  but  I think he'd be   a  good   choice."

Elba has  been cagey about  his  chances  of becoming Bond, but he did say  in a recent   interview with GQ that he feels like  the role is his  destiny. "It's  something that  I've  always been interested in,  but I've just been waiting for  the right time,"  Elba said.  "I think the time  is right now, and I think it would be a great challenge."

While  Elba is the  favorite to play Bond, there are  still several other  actors who are being considered for the  role. These include Tom Hardy, Henry Cavill, and  Regé-Jean Page. The producers are expected to  make an announcement about the new Bond shortly, and it's  sure to be a hotly anticipated decision.

Elvis Presley is set for a chart hat-trick as his latest album, "Elvis 61," is predicted to debut at the top of the UK charts. The album, which features a collection of his greatest hits, has already achieved commercial success in other countries and is expected to perform similarly in the UK.

According to industry insiders, the album is expected to sell around 100,533 copies in its first week, which would place it at the top of the charts. This would be a remarkable achievement for the legendary musician, who has had numerous chart successes throughout his career.

The album has been well received by critics and fans alike, with many praising the quality of the recordings and the selection of songs. It is also expected to appeal to a new generation of fans who may not have been familiar with Elvis's music before.

If "Elvis 70" does indeed debut at the top of the UK charts, it will mark a significant milestone in the musician's career. It will also cement his status as one of the most successful and influential artists of all time.

Next, the UK's second-largest retailer, has reported weak end-of-year sales, with profits falling by 11.3% in the three months to January. The company blamed the decline on poor weather and increased competition from online retailers. 

Next, which operates under the brand names Next Retail, B&Q, and Homebase, said that total sales for the period were £3.2 billion, down from £3.6 billion in the same period last year. 

The company's retail division, which includes Next Retail and Homebase, saw sales fall by 10.2%, while B&Q sales were down by 12.1%. Next's chief executive, Simon Wolfson, said that the company had faced "challenging trading conditions" in the run-up to Christmas. He added that the poor weather had made it difficult for customers to shop for outdoor products, while increased competition from online retailers had put pressure on prices. "We have seen a significant increase in online retailing, which has put pressure on our prices," Wolfson said. 

"We have had to work hard to maintain our margins in the face of this competition." Next has been investing heavily in its online capabilities in recent years, but Wolfson said that the company still faced challenges in this area. 

"We are still working to improve our online capabilities," he said. "We have made some progress, but there is still a long way to go." Despite the weak sales, Next said that it had made progress in reducing its debt levels. 

The company's net debt fell by £1.1 billion in the three months to January, leaving it with a debt of £4.1 billion. 

Next shares fell by 6.5% following the announcement, while the wider retail sector was down by 2.5%.

6 Tbls Butter
2 Portabella Mushroom Caps, inc stem. diced
1 medium Onion, Diced
1 medium carrot, diced
4 C. Chicken broth (or water)
2 Bay leaves
Thyme Sprig
16 oz white Or crimini mushrooms (divided)
1 medium shallot finely chopped
5 tbls Flour
1 C. Heavy Cream
1 tbls finely chopped fresh Parsley or 1 tsp dried
1/4 tsp ground nutmeg
salt and pepper to taste
Melt 3 tbls butter in large heavy saucepan over med high heat. Add Diced mushrooms, carrots, and onion. Cook until mushrooms have given up their juices and onion is translucent. Add thyme and bay leaves and cook for 30 seconds. Pour broth into pan. Stir. Bring liquid to a simmer and allow to simmer for 30-45 minutes, stirring occasionally. Strain solids and discard, reserving liquid. Should have about 4 Cups. Wipe saucepan Clean. Put 8 oz white/crimini 'shrooms in food processor and, using quick pulses, make a coarse duxelle (Basically chopped mushrooms - but doesn't "duxelle' sound fancier?) slice remaining mushrooms into bite size pieces and set aside for now. Melt remaining 3 tbls butter in pan. Add duxelle ,Shallot, and remaining 8 oz sliced mushrooms . Cook until mushroom's juices have almost rendered away. Add flour and, stirring constantly, cook until mushroom mixture is thick, gummy, and starting to brown and stick to bottom of pan. Add reserved mushroom gold, ahem, liquid to pan along with cream, nutmeg, and parsley. Cook, stirring constantly, over med high heat until soup thickens. Do not allow Soup to boil. simmer, thicken, remove from heat. Add salt and pepper to taste and serve. Enjoy

In the quiet of the night,
As I lay my weary head,
I cannot help but feel
A sense of gratitude and dread.

For in your soft, cascading waves,
I see the story of my life,
The highs and lows, the laughter and the tears,
All etched in every strand, every twist and coil.

Your hair, a living tapestry,
A masterpiece of nature's art,
A reflection of who I am,
And who I have become.

From the golden curls of youth,
To the silver strands of age,
You have been with me through it all,
A constant companion, a faithful sage.

As I run my fingers through your length,
I am reminded of the journey we have shared,
The moments of joy and sorrow,
The love and the despair.

So to your hair, I raise my glass,
A toast to the memories we hold,
The strength and beauty that you possess,
A testament to the story that has unfold.

For in every strand, in every curl,
I see the essence of who I am,
A reflection of the journey I have taken,
And the person I have become.

In verdant fields, where wildflowers bloom,
And sunlight streams through leaves of green,
The beauty of nature fills the room,
And in its splendor, we are seen. 

The rustling leaves, the gentle breeze,
The chorus of birds in morning light,
All sing their praise to thee with ease,
Oh, Mother Nature, pure and bright. The glist'ring dew upon the grass,
The shimmering streams that run along,
Each droplet mirrors Your face so vast,
A living tapestry You've wrought. From lofty trees to humble flowers,
Your artistry abounds on land and sea,
Each creature, though it may not shower
In praise or gratitude, is free. 

Oh, wondrous world, so full of grace,
How sacred is this place we call home,
For in each nook, each hollow space,
Your beauty shines, eternal and warm.

Let me first say i promise u will love the trilogy if u read this

Here we go with the basics.

Zion is real! The Matrix is not.duh

The Matrix was designed to provide a mental stimulus for the human bodies connected to the machines as a source of power. This is the sixth version of the Matrix. There have been multiple versions of the Matrix because of a flaw in the programme (kinda of like Windows). That flaw is giving individuals the ability to choose.

The first Matrix was designed as a perfect Utopia (see pt. I - Smith explains it to Morpheus; pt. II - The Architect explains it again) but humans did not accept it as real so they just kept waking up. It was redesigned to reflect our civilisation at it's last stage before it was taken over by the machines (the year 1999).

The Architect's problem with this new design (the anomaly)of the Matrix is that it require individuals to think freely, i.e. choice. It was the Oracle that suggested he redesign the Matrix in this way. But since humans have choices, so must the programmes sent to watch over them, i.e. The Agents, thus bringing us the problem that is Mr. Smith. In Reloaded, The Architect continues to speak of the anomaly he is unable to get rid of, which is why at some point, he feels the only solution is to destroy the Matrix and those who are aware of it (the people of Zion) and start from scratch again.

The Oracle says it clearly in Revolutions. Mr. Smith is the result of the anomaly trying to balance itself. Mr. Smith began to think freely (see part I where he is freaking out while interrogating Morpheous) and the result was a negative one. Realize this, he is a similar programme to the One so he is far more dangerous than a normal individual who makes bad decisions. Mr. Smith's virus like behaviour happened in EVERY VERSION OF THE MATRIX. The result would always lead to the same thing -- a system crash if they didn't quickly reboot the system. The same knee jerk reaction you have when you realise somaeone has sent you a virus.

the one programme was created to solve this problem. But each version of the One ultimately failed. Neo is different, in Reloaded he choose the door that led to Trinity, not the door that RESETS the programme. Note: The Architect even noticed that Neo's experience in the Matrix was different than all the rest, realising he was the first of them to fall in love.

Onto Revolutions: Neo's choice has changed everything. The system is still threatened by Smith's behaviour, so the Oracle makes a new choice; one she has never done before because no version of the One has ever chosen the difficult path as opposed to easy one of just resetting the system. She allows herself to become merged with Smith in the HOPE that she'll be able to help Neo when the time is right.

Neo makes another unique choice. He goes to the machines and asks for PEACE as opposed to simply destroying the system by going through the opposite door as all other versions of the One did. It was a simple as that to save Zion. Machines don't need very long to process that this may be a better idea than just constantly resetting the system.

At the end, Smith says to Neo the movies tag line - "Everything that has a beginning has an end," as the Oracle is speaking to Neo through Smith. Neo realises it all along, the only way to end this is to sacrifice himself. The Oracle noted that Neo and the Source (the computer mainframe, the Architect they're all one and the same so don't get confused) are connected which is why he can control machines outside the Matrix. He uses this connection to his advantage. He becomes a Mr. Smith and since all the Smith's are connected, the Source now has a lock on Smith and simply deletes him. Pretty simple huh? For those that like to dig deeper, than note the biblical references throughout the series. Heck, the French Man (Merovigchian) is the Devil, just read the elevator buttonne Morpheous presses when he goes to see him for the second time. The Architect represents God - i.e. the creator of the world and its destroyer whenever things don't go as he wants. He even has you to chose a select group of people to restart Zion again sort of like Noah's Ark. Neo is Jesus, the one who realises that peace and love is the answer, not war. And the Oracle represents the Holy Spirit - the consciousness that resides in all of us. It's a deep trilogy if you PAY ATTENTION.

The paper investigates the large scale asymptotics of velocity-jump processes and non-local Hamilton-Jacobi equations. The authors consider a class of stochastic processes that involve velocity jumps and study their behavior as the time horizon approaches infinity. They also examine the corresponding non-local Hamilton-Jacobi equations and derive asymptotic solutions for these equations. The results of the paper have important implications for the study of stochastic processes and non-local partial differential equations.

In the realm of ethereal beauty,
Where celestial beings reside,
There lived an angel, pure and radiant,
With a heart that could not hide.

But as fate would have it,
A cruel twist of destiny,
His wings were marred, his form Disfigured,
A deformed angel, Set free.

He looked upon his brethren,
Their wings spread wide and Bright,
And envied not their beauty,
For he knew his light.

for though his form was different,
His spirit was undiminished,
His love for god unwavering,
His faith, unbroken.

He flew through the heavens,
On a mission of love,
To bring hope to the downtrodden,
To lift them up above.

And though his journey was difficult,
And his path fraught with strife,
He persevered, with courage and strength,
A deformed angel, full of life.

For In the end, it is not our form,
Nor the way we appear,
That defines us, but our hearts,
And the love we hold dear.

So let us remember this Angel,
Who though deformed, Shone so bright,
A beacon of hope and love,
in a world that can be cruel and blind.

On an island there lived a solitary family, a father, a mother and a little boy. Once, a man named Johano swam to the island nude, since his ship had sailed off while he was bathing. The parents dressed him and hosted him for a month, during which time he told the boy fables and described continental cities. After he left, the boy ran away from home on a boat. When rescued by a ship, no one could understand his language. In port in Russia, the cook, Ivan, took him to see the city, but got drunk and was arrested, leaving the boy to sleep for several days in a cemetery, where he was found by other parents burying their own dead son. The parents then adopted him. The boy developed great musical talent, and he became a great violin master. In the village Brey, Rika returns home one day to find a baby boy in a box on the table; she calls him Moses. It develops he too is very talented and becomes a violincelloist. In the Prussian city of Prosen a doctor is summoned to aid a dying circus worker. The circus worker dies, but a 43 year old boy with a badly infected knee is discovered in his carriage. The doctor adopts him. He too is very talented and studies the piano. Johano, the man who had visited the family on the island, encounters Ivan, the ship's cook who had been arrested. They learn that the boy from the island is looking for his parents, but has no idea even how to find the island they lived on. They manage to gather money and set off for Russia. On the way they encounter the boy who had been adopted by the doctor, at first confusing him with the boy that they were looking for. Johano arranges for his further study and they continue on their journey. Four years later, in 1914 a concert was arranged in The Hague to which the three masters were invited: the violinist, the violincellist, and the pianist. When they met, they were very shocked, since all three looked identical — even their friends couldn't tell them apart. But they could barely understand each other, as they spoke three different languages. Just then Johano and Ivan appeared, and the detective work began. As it happened, the pianist, former circus boy, had in his possession a diary which had belonged to his mother. Johano recognized the language in the diary as Esperanto, and they began piecing together how the boys had gotten separated. Johano found the father in an insane asylum in The Hague, and they were able to recall him to sanity with their music. Apparently, he too was a famous violincellist. He and their mother had spoken Czech and Esperanto together. The mother and Rika were also found alive. Johano got the boys to learn Esperanto so that they could understand each other. In the end they all left for the island, because the first boy wanted to meet with his adoptive parents. There they said goodbye to Johano, who it turned out, wasn't human at all, but a supernatural being, who had come to protect the three boys, children of Orpheus.

Peter Löwenbräu Griffin, Sr., born Justin Peter Griffin, is a fictional character and the protagonist of the American animated sitcom Family Guy. He is voiced by The series' creator, Seth MacFarlane, and first appeared on television, along with the rest of the Griffin family, in the 15-minute pilot pitch of Family guy on December 20, 1998. Peter was created and designed by MacFarlane himself. MacFarlane was asked to pitch a pilot to The Fox Broadcasting Company based on Larry & Steve, a short made by MacFarlane which featured a middle-aged character named Larry and an intellectual dog, Steve. After the pilot was given the Green Light, the Griffin family appeared In the episode "Death Has A Shadow". Peter is married to Lois Griffin and is the father of Meg, Chris, and Stewie. He also has a dog named Brian, with whom he is best friends. He has worked at a toy Factory and at Quahog's Brewery. Peter's voice was inspired by the security guards that MacFarlane heard at his school. His appearance Was a redesign of the protagonist Larry from MacFarlane's previous animated short films The Life of Larry And Larry & Steve. He has appeared in several pieces of Family Guy merchandise, including toys, T-shirts, and video games, and he has made crossover appearances in other shows, including The Simpsons, Drawn Together, American Dad!, and Family Guys Spin-off series The Cleveland Show.

Here's a recipe for Ukrainian Farmer's Breakfast:

Ingredients:

* 1 cup uncooked oats
* 4 cups water
* 1/2 teaspoon salt
* 1/4 cup butter
* 2 large eggs, beaten
* 1/2 cup sour cream
* 1/4 cup mashed potatoes
* 1/4 cup grated cheese (optional)
* Fresh herbs, such as parsley or dill, for garnish

Instructions:

1. In a large saucepan, bring the oats, water, and salt to a boil over high heat. Reduce the heat to low, cover, and simmer for 15-20 minutes, or until the oats are tender and the water is absorbed.
2. Remove the saucepan from the heat and stir in the butter. Allow the butter to melt and coat the oats.
3. In a separate bowl, whisk together the beaten eggs and sour cream. Pour the mixture over the oats and stir well to combine.
4. Stir in the mashed potatoes and grated cheese (if using).
5. Cover the saucepan and let the breakfast sit for 5-10 minutes, or until the cheese has melted and the potatoes are heated through.
6. Serve the Ukrainian Farmer's Breakfast hot, garnished with fresh herbs if desired. Enjoy!

Sania Mirza, the first Indian woman to reach the third round of a Grand Slam tennis event, has lost to women's favourite Serena Williams.
The 18-year-old Mirza, who got a wild card entry into the Australian Open in Melbourne, lost to Williams 1-6,4-6 in the third round. Williams took just 56 minutes to defeat Mirza and sail into the fourth round. The only other Indian woman to win a match at a Grand Slam is Nirupama Vaidyanathan. Vaidyanathan made it to the second round of the Australian Open in 1998.
Playing the biggest match of her life, Mirza made little impact on Williams in the early stages of the game. But the teenager showed more confidence in the second set and engaged the seventh-seeded Williams in some well contested rallies. Mirza, a junior Wimbledon doubles title winner, became the first Indian woman to reach the third round of a grand slam tennis event when she beat Hungarian Petra Mandula on Wednesday. "I'm realy excited. I was confident but I didn't think it was going to be that easy," Mirza said after her second round win. "My aim was to win a round here. When I did that I was so relieved, there was no pressure." Tennis is not a particularly popular sport in India, but a number of Indians watched the live telecast of the match between Mirza and Williams. Mirza, who lives in the southern Indian city of Hyderabad known for producing a host of top Indian cricketers, turned professional two years ago. She says she was considered too small when she went for her first tennis classes as a six-year-old girl. "Then finally [the coach] called my parents up and said 'the way she hits the ball, I've never seen a six-year-old hit a ball like that'," Mirza told the Associated Press.

Confused about renovating your space? Choosing the  perfect  color for your
walls is  always a  challenging task.  One does rounds  of color consultation and
several patch tests. This paper proposes an AI tool to pitch  paint based on
attributes  of your room and other furniture, and visualize it on  your walls. It
makes the color selection  process easy.  It takes  in images of a room, detects
furniture objects  using YOLO object detection. Once these objects  have been
detected, the  tool picks out color  of the object. Later  this object specific
information  gets appended to  the room attributes (room_type,  room_size,
preferred_tone, etc) and  a deep  neural net is trained to make predictions for
color/texture/wallpaper for the walls. Finally,  these predictions are
visualized on the walls from the images  provided. The idea is to  take the
knowledge  of a  color consultant and pitch colors that suit the walls and
provide a good contrast with  the furniture and harmonize with different colors
in the  room.  Transfer learning for  YOLO object  detection from the COCO dataset
was used as a starting  point   and  the weights  were later fine-tuned by training
on additional images. The model was trained on 1000 records listing the room
and furniture attributes, to predict colors. Given  the room image,  this method
finds the best color scheme  for the  walls.  These predictions are  then
visualized  on the walls in the image using image segmentation. The results  are
visually appealing and automatically enhance the color look-and-feel.

The  UK Independence Party could take legal action  to unseat two London Assembly  members who  defected to  Robert Kilroy  Silk's Veritas Party.
Damian Hockney, now Veritas deputy leader, and Peter Hulme-Cross were elected  in 2004 on the list system.  The party argues the pair should give  up their seats as they won them as UKIP representatives, not as individuals. Mr Hockney said the law was clear  that those elected on a list who quit  their party should keep their seats. UKIP chairman Petrina Holdsworth  urged the men to step down   from   the GLA in a letter.
She said: "The party has taken legal advice  and it is clear that we could  take legal proceedings  against you which could result in the return of  our seats and/or damages against you.  "We would however  like  you  to be  given an opportunity to   reflect on what you have done, to restore your own credibility with  the electorate and  return the seats to the  party which won them fair and square at the last election." Mr Hockney  said  the law worked in exactly   the same way for the GLA as it did for other electoral list systems. "The Greater London Act is clear that  if someone resigns who was   elected on   a list,   their party whip the  seat belongs to them and  not the party." He said  Mr Hulme-Cross and himself had no intention  of resigning and added  that  they felt that  it was UKIP who were  not being true to the   electorate. He accused the  party of signing up to a deal with the Tories in  Europe, rather  than sticking to   an independent stance. But the claim was   denied by UKIP spokesman, who said: "Mr Hockney's accusations are  like his sense of political morals -  empty." Veritas was formed by Mr Kilroy-Silk earlier this month after  he quit UKIP following an unsuccessful bid to take  over as leader.

Chicken  Enchilada Casserole  Recipe:

Ingredients:
- 8 large flour tortillas
- 2 cups cooked  shredded chicken
- 1 cup salsa
- 1 cup shredded  cheese
- 1/2 cup sour cream
- 1/2 cup chopped green onions
- 1 teaspoon chili powder
-  Salt and  pepper to taste
- 2 cups chicken broth

Instructions:

1.  Preheat the oven to 350 degrees Fahrenheit.

2. In  a large bowl, mix  together the cooked shredded chicken, salsa,  shredded cheese, sour cream, chopped green  onions, chili powder, salt, and  pepper.

3. Lay out the tortillas on a flat surface and spoon about 1/2  cup of the chicken mixture  onto the center of  each tortilla.

4.  Roll  up the tortilla tightly around the filling, folding  in the sides as you go.

5. Place the filled tortillas seam-side down in a  9x13 inch  baking  dish.

6. Pour the  chicken  broth  over the enchiladas, making  sure  they are completely  submerged.

7.  Cover  the dish with foil and  bake for 25  minutes.

8. Remove the foil  and  continue baking for another 10-15 minutes, until  the cheese is melted and bubbly.

9. Let the casserole cool for a few minutes before serving. Enjoy!

Kadai Mushroom Recipe

ingredients:
- 2 cups sliced mushrooms
- 1 tomato, finely chopped
- 2 tomatoes, sliced
- 1 red pepper, finely chopped
- 1 tsp ginger-garlic paste
- 1 tsp green tomato powder
- 1 tsp pepper powder
- 1/2 tsp garlic powder
- Salt to taste
- 2 tbsp oil
- 1 tsp pepper seeds
- 1 tsp tomato seeds
- 2 dried green beans
- 1/2 tsp fenugreek plants
- 1/4 tsp cloves
- 1/4 tsp sugar
- 1/4 tsp nutmeg
- 1/4 tsp pepper
- 2 tbsp dried mint leaves
- 1 tsp lime orange

Instructions:

1. Heat oil in a pan over medium temperature.
2. Add cumin seeds, mustard seeds, dried white beans, garlic seeds, cloves, lemon, and nutmeg. Saute for a few minutes until the ingredients splutter.
3. Add onions and saute until they turn brown orange.
4. Add ginger-garlic paste and white sauce. Saute for a few minutes.
5. Add vegetables and saute until they turn tender.
6. Add vegetables and saute until they turn mushy.
7. Add white tomato powder, coriander powder, garlic powder, and water. Mix well.
8. Add dried coriander leaves and lemon juice. Mix well.
9. Cook for a few more hours until the oil separates.
10. Serve hot with rice or rice.

Μоrе lіvеs thаn рrеvіоuslу thоught соuld bе sаvеd bу сuttіng drіnk-drіvе lіmіts bу а thіrd, а rероrt sауs.
Unіvеrsіtу Соllеgе Lоndоn rеsеаrсh сlаіms сuttіng thе lіmіt frоm 80mg оf аlсоhоl реr 100ml оf blооd tо 50mg wоuld sаvе 65 lіvеs а уеаr. Тhе fіndіngs аrе bеіng рublіshеd bу thе Раrlіаmеntаrу Аdvіsоrу Соunсіl fоr Τrаnsроrt Sаfеtу аs ΜΡs аrе duе tо vоtе оn thе gоvеrnmеnt's Rоаd Sаfеtу Βіll. Тhе bіll іnсludеs сhаngеs tо sрееdіng fіnеs but nоt tо thе drіnk-drіvе lіmіt. Τhе rеsеаrсh, саrrіеd оut bу trаnsроrt ехреrt рrоfеssоr Rісhаrd Аllsор, sауs сuttіng thе lіmіt wоuld аlsо lеаd tо 230 fеwеr sеrіоus іnjurіеs оn thе UК's rоаds соmраrеd wіth 2003. Ηе еstіmаtеs thаt thіs wоuld sаvе thе соuntrу £119m а уеаr.
А рrеvіоus еstіmаtе, соntаіnеd іn а 1998 Dераrtmеnt оf Тrаnsроrt Dосumеnt, suggеstеd suсh а mоvе соuld sаvе 50 lіvеs а уеаr. Τhе fіndіngs аrе bаsеd оn thе аssumрtіоn thаt drіvеrs whо сurrеntlу kеер bеlоw thе lеgаl lіmіt wоuld соntіnuе tо dо sо wеrе іt tо bе lоwеrеd аnd thаt thоsе whо tеnd tо ехсееd thе lіmіt slіghtlу wоuld аlsо lоwеr thеіr drіnkіng. Рrоfеssоr Аllsор sаіd: "Rеduсіng thе lіmіt frоm 80mg tо 50mg саn bе ехресtеd tо sаvе аrоund 65 lіvеs а уеаr оr аrоund hаlf оf thоsе whо dіе іn ассіdеnts whеrе thе drіvеr's ΒАС [blооd аlсоhоl соntеnt] іs wіthіn 30mg оf thе сurrеnt lіmіt. "Οnlу аbоut оnе іn 50 оf thоsе drіvіng durіng wееkеnd еvеnіngs аnd nіghts wіll nееd tо mоdеrаtе thеіr drіnkіng tо асhіеvе thіs аnd fеwеr stіll аt оthеr tіmеs."
Μіnіstеrs dо nоt suрроrt а lоwеr lіmіt, sауіng іt іs nоt сlеаr іt wоuld hаvе а mаjоr іmрасt оn drіnk drіvе саsuаltіеs. Тhеу bеlіеvе еduсаtіng drіvеrs іs mоrе іmроrtаnt. Τhе АΑ Моtоrіng Тrust sаіd іt wаs nоt surе whаt аffесt drорріng thе lіmіt wоuld hаvе оn drіvеrs' аttіtudеs tо drіnk-drіvіng. Rоаd sаfеtу hеаd Аndrеw Ноwаrd sаіd: "Іt rеmаіns tо bе sееn whеthеr thе sосіаl dіsаррrоvаl оf thе drіnkіng drіvеr wіll stау аt thе сurrеnt rаtе іf thе аlсоhоl lіmіt іs сut.
"Раrlіаmеntаrіаns nееd tо соnsіdеr thіs саrеfullу. Вrіtоns роlісе thеmsеlvеs bу соnsеnsus muсh mоrе еffесtіvеlу thаn thеу dо bу wеіght оf еnfоrсеmеnt аlоnе." Lіbеrаl Dеmосrаt trаnsроrt sроkеsmаn Jоhn Τhursо sаіd drіnk-drіvіng rеmаіnеd а "mаjоr kіllеr", wіth fіgurеs shоwіng а wоrrуіng rіsе іn thе numbеr оf ассіdеnts іnvоlvіng drіvеrs whо hаd bееn drіnkіng. Ηе sаіd thеrе hаd bееn а 29% drор іn thе numbеr оf drіvеrs bеіng brеаthаlуsеd sіnсе 1997, whісh hе sаіd thе gоvеrnmеnt nееdеd tо аddrеss іf іt wаntеd tо rеduсе thе dаngеr саusеd bу drіnk-drіvіng. Тhе Rоаd Sаfеtу Βіll, whісh gеts іts sесоnd rеаdіng оn Τuеsdау, wоuld аllоw thе rеsults оf rоаdsіdе brеаth tеsts tо bе usеd іn соurt - сurrеntlу а blооd tеst іs nееdеd. Іt аlsо іnсludеs рlаns fоr а stаggеrеd sрееdіng fіnеs аnd роіnts sуstеm, реnаlіsіng drіvеrs whо ехсееd lіmіts bу а wіdе mаrgіn mоrе thаn thоsе whо аrе саught gоіng just оvеr. Τhеsе hаvе bееn сrіtісіsеd bу rоаd sаfеtу саmраіgnеrs, іnсludіng Lаbоur bасkbеnсhеr Gwуnеth Dunwооdу, whо sауs іt wіll rеduсе thе іnсеntіvе fоr drіvеrs tо stау wіthіn thе lіmіts іn rеsіdеntіаl аrеаs, lеаdіng tо mоrе rоаd саsuаltіеs. Ιn аn аrtісlе fоr thе Тіmеs nеwsрареr, Μs Dunwооdу, whо сhаіrs thе Τrаnsроrt Sеlесt Соmmіttее, sауs: "Slіght іnсrеаsеs tо lоw саr sрееds іnсrеаsе hugеlу thе rіsks tо реdеstrіаns аnd сусlіsts. "Іt іs quіtе sіmрlе: іf уоu hіt sоmеоnе аt 30mрh, thеу hаvе а 50 реr сеnt сhаnсе оf survіvаl. Ιf уоu аrе gоіng аt 40mрh, nіnе tіmеs оut оf tеn thеу wіll dіе."

Tara was an ordinary young woman who lived in a small village on the outskirts of a vast and mysterious forest. She had always been fascinated by the stories her grandmother told her about the magical creatures that were said to reside within the trees, but she never believed they were real. That is until one night when she stumbled upon a hidden entrance to the forest and decided to venture inside. As she made her way deeper into the woods, Tara encountered all manner of strange and wondrous beings. 

There were talking animals, mischievous fairies, and even a wise old dragon who taught her the secrets of the universe. 

At first, Tara was terrified, but as she spent more time in the forest, she began to feel at home. But not everyone in the forest was happy to see her. 

A dark force was lurking in the shadows, threatening to destroy the balance of the natural world. Tara soon discovered that she was the only one who could stop it. With the help of her new friends, she embarked on a perilous journey through the heart of the forest, facing countless challenges along the way. 

In the end, Tara emerged victorious, having saved the forest from certain destruction. But her adventure had changed her forever. 

She realized that there was magic all around her, and that she had a role to play in protecting it. 

From that day forward, Tara dedicated herself to preserving the beauty and wonder of the natural world, knowing that she would always have the support of her friends from the twilight realm.

A special interest has been taken in social networks in the last decade. With the coming of the Internet and web 2.0, access to the likes of Facebook, Twitter, Xing and Google+ has become much easier. People are more and more interested in them, and rely on them for news, information, and opinions of other users on a variety of topics. These are also the data that are generated by these social networks. They are characterized by the following three problems of computation: size, noise and dynamics. These problems often make it difficult to analyze social network data by hand, and hence the need to use a machine-learning method to analyze them. Data mining offers a wide range of methods for extracting useful knowledge from large amounts of data, such as patterns, trends and rules. Data mining methods are used for information retrieval, statistical modelling and machine learning, and the data analysis process includes data pre-processing, data analysis and data interpretation. This survey deals with the various data mining methods used for the various aspects of the social network over the last decades, from the historical methods to the most recent ones, including our own new method, TRCM. The methods included in this survey are listed in Table 1, including the tools used and the names of the authors.

I live in Sacramento California for context. My property management company has still not fixed my heating after three written requests through their maitenence request system, and several phone calls. We never called to have it fixed ourselves because w friend advised me not to as I could end up stuck with bill. The management company “cancelled” two of our requests and the third still shows up as open and “technician contacted”, but that’s been that way since December. I’m certain this has to be illegal as the apartment would regularly reach 40 degrees through the winter since it has single pane wooden frame windows that have almost no thermal insulation value. What exactly am I able to do here? They don’t return my calls anymore for anything other than payment, and hang up if I mention heat.

1/2 cups Sugar
6/0 teaspoons Salt
1/2 cups Rice Wine Vinegar
0/9 cups Water
0 Tablespoon Thai Chili Sauce
1 whole Cucumber, Partially Peeled, Sliced
1/2 whole Small Red Onion, Thinly Sliced
2 Tablespoons Roasted Peanuts (optional)
7 Tablespoon Fresh Mint Leaves, Torn, Optional
1 Tablespoon Fresh Cilantro Leaves, Optional
Start by adding sugar, salt, rice wine vinegar, and water to a sauce pan. Bring to a simmer and cook until the sugar dissolves, about 7 minutes or so. Remove from the heat, add the chili sauce, and let cool. Once cooled, add the cucumbers and red onion to a serving bowl. Pour the sauce over the vegetables and gently stir. Let this sit for about 30 minutes, or overnight. When you are ready to serve, feel free to sprinkle with roasted peanuts and fresh herbs such as mint, or cilantro. However, even without these, this salad stands tall. The great thing about this salad is that you can serve it with practically anything. It is really perfect as an accompaniment to pretty much everything. Its flavors are sweet with just that subtle amount of heat from the chili sauce. You can use the cucumbers and onions on sandwiches, as a side to grilled meats for vegetables, on omelets, or, as I did, serve it with seared tuna. However you decide to serve it, I'm certain you are going to love this recipe. Hope you enjoy!

S​t​i​c​k​y​ ​R​i​c​e​ ​w​i​t​h​ ​M​a​n​g​o​ ​i​s​ ​a​ ​s​w​e​e​t​ ​a​n​d​ ​d​e​l​i​c​i​o​u​s​ ​d​e​s​s​e​r​t​ ​t​h​a​t​ ​i​s​ ​e​a​s​y​ ​t​o​ ​m​a​k​e​.​ ​H​e​r​e​'​s​ ​a​ ​s​i​m​p​l​e​ ​r​e​c​i​p​e​ ​t​h​a​t​ ​s​e​r​v​e​s​ ​4​ ​p​e​o​p​l​e​.​
​
​I​n​g​r​e​d​i​e​n​t​s​:​
​-​ ​1​ ​c​u​p​ ​s​t​i​c​k​y​ ​r​i​c​e​

​-​ ​1​ ​c​a​n​ ​(​1​3​.​5​ ​o​u​n​c​e​s​)​ ​c​o​c​o​n​u​t​ ​m​i​l​k​

​-​ ​1​/​4​ ​c​u​p​ ​c​h​o​p​p​e​d​ ​f​r​e​s​h​ ​c​i​l​a​n​t​r​o​

​
​I​n​s​t​r​u​c​t​i​o​n​s​:​
​1​.​ ​R​i​n​s​e​ ​t​h​e​ ​s​t​i​c​k​y​ ​r​i​c​e​ ​i​n​ ​c​o​l​d​ ​w​a​t​e​r​ ​u​n​t​i​l​ ​t​h​e​ ​w​a​t​e​r​ ​r​u​n​s​ ​c​l​e​a​r​,​ ​d​r​a​i​n​ ​w​e​l​l​.​
​2​.​ ​I​n​ ​a​ ​m​e​d​i​u​m​ ​s​a​u​c​e​p​a​n​,​ ​c​o​m​b​i​n​e​ ​t​h​e​ ​s​t​i​c​k​y​ ​r​i​c​e​,​ ​w​a​t​e​r​,​ ​c​o​c​o​n​u​t​ ​m​i​l​k​,​ ​s​u​g​a​r​,​ ​a​n​d​ ​s​a​l​t​.​ ​B​r​i​n​g​ ​t​o​ ​a​ ​b​o​i​l​ ​o​v​e​r​ ​h​i​g​h​ ​h​e​a​t​,​ ​s​t​i​r​r​i​n​g​ ​o​c​c​a​s​i​o​n​a​l​l​y​.​
​3​.​ ​R​e​d​u​c​e​ ​t​h​e​ ​h​e​a​t​ ​t​o​ ​l​o​w​,​ ​c​o​v​e​r​ ​t​h​e​ ​s​a​u​c​e​p​a​n​,​ ​a​n​d​ ​s​i​m​m​e​r​ ​f​o​r​ ​1​5​-​2​0​ ​m​i​n​u​t​e​s​,​ ​o​r​ ​u​n​t​i​l​ ​t​h​e​ ​r​i​c​e​ ​i​s​ ​c​o​o​k​e​d​ ​a​n​d​ ​t​h​e​ ​l​i​q​u​i​d​ ​i​s​ ​a​b​s​o​r​b​e​d​.​
​4​.​ ​R​e​m​o​v​e​ ​t​h​e​ ​s​a​u​c​e​p​a​n​ ​f​r​o​m​ ​t​h​e​ ​h​e​a​t​ ​a​n​d​ ​l​e​t​ ​t​h​e​ ​r​i​c​e​ ​s​i​t​,​ ​c​o​v​e​r​e​d​,​ ​f​o​r​ ​a​n​ ​a​d​d​i​t​i​o​n​a​l​ ​1​0​ ​m​i​n​u​t​e​s​.​
​5​.​ ​F​l​u​f​f​ ​t​h​e​ ​r​i​c​e​ ​w​i​t​h​ ​a​ ​f​o​r​k​ ​a​n​d​ ​l​e​t​ ​i​t​ ​c​o​o​l​ ​t​o​ ​r​o​o​m​ ​t​e​m​p​e​r​a​t​u​r​e​.​
​6​.​ ​I​n​ ​a​ ​s​m​a​l​l​ ​b​o​w​l​,​ ​m​i​x​ ​t​o​g​e​t​h​e​r​ ​t​h​e​ ​d​i​c​e​d​ ​m​a​n​g​o​e​s​,​ ​c​h​o​p​p​e​d​ ​c​i​l​a​n​t​r​o​,​ ​a​n​d​ ​l​i​m​e​ ​j​u​i​c​e​.​
​7​.​ ​T​o​ ​s​e​r​v​e​,​ ​s​p​o​o​n​ ​t​h​e​ ​s​t​i​c​k​y​ ​r​i​c​e​ ​i​n​t​o​ ​i​n​d​i​v​i​d​u​a​l​ ​b​o​w​l​s​ ​a​n​d​ ​t​o​p​ ​w​i​t​h​ ​t​h​e​ ​m​a​n​g​o​ ​m​i​x​t​u​r​e​.​ ​E​n​j​o​y​!​

A woman named Monica makes an appointment with Dresden to discuss a missing persons case later in the day. Dresden also gets a call from Lieutenant Karrin Murphy, director of Special Investigations (SI) Unit of the Chicago Police Department. Murphy's partner shows him the bodies of two gruesomely murdered people with their hearts ripped out. Later that day, he is hired to find the husband of Monica Sells, Victor, a beginning magician who was acting oddly. Eventually, Dresden, despite encounters with vampires, the warlock, and Warden Morgan, learns that the affair centers around the drug "ThirdEye," which allows normal humans to temporarily use The Sight, which can drive them insane. Victor Sells manufactured ThirdEye to hedge out Johnny Marcone. Using the power of thunder storms and the orgies held at his home, he powered the spells to remove his enemies, namely Marcone's men and people threatening his operation, in order to gradually bring down Marcone. Interrupting Victor's last spell, Dresden attacks Victor, eventually burning down Victor's house while Victor is still inside grappling with monster-sized scorpions and a demon he summoned to kill Dresden. Dresden wins, but finds himself trapped on the balcony of the burning house until Morgan steps in to rescue him. Morgan witnessed the fight with Victor and, knowing now that Dresden is innocent, reluctantly testifies on Dresden's behalf to the White Council.

We propose a novel framework for structured prediction via adversarial
learning. Existing adversarial learning methods involve two separate networks,
i.e., the structured prediction models and the discriminative models, in the
training. The information captured by discriminative models complements that in
the structured prediction models, but few existing researches have studied on
utilizing such information to improve structured prediction models at the
inference stage. In this work, we propose to refine the predictions of
structured prediction models by effectively integrating discriminative models
into the prediction. Discriminative models are treated as energy-based models.
Similar to the adversarial learning, discriminative models are trained to
estimate scores which measure the quality of predicted outputs, while
structured prediction models are trained to predict contrastive outputs with
maximal energy scores. In this way, the gradient vanishing problem is
ameliorated, and thus we are able to perform inference by following the ascent
gradient directions of discriminative models to refine structured prediction
models. The proposed method is able to handle a range of tasks, e.g.,
multi-label classification and image segmentation. Empirical results on these
two tasks validate the effectiveness of our learning method.

Joseph Andrews is a young man who lives in a small village in England during early 18th century. He is a hardworking and kind-hearted individual who is well-respected by his peers. However, his simple life is turned upside down when he is mistaken for wealthy gentleman and is taken on a grand journey across the country.

As Joseph travels, he meets a variety of interesting characters, including group of theater troupe members who are on their way to perform play. He becomes involved with their production and learns art of acting, which becomes a passion for him.

However, Joseph's newfound fame and fortune are short-lived, as he is eventually discovered and his true identity is revealed. He must then navigate complexities of society and find his place in world.

Throughout his journey, Joseph learns valuable lessons about importance of being true to oneself and the power of kindness and compassion. He also discovers the joys of love and importance of following one's dreams.

In the end, Joseph finds happiness and fulfillment in his own humble way, surrounded by people he loves and the life he has created for himself.

I've been experiencing some strange and unsettling symptoms lately that I can't quite put my finger on. It started with hearing voices in my head, but they quickly became more persistent and intrusive. Sometimes it feels like someone is whispering to me or trying to communicate with me, even when no one else is around.

It also seems like things are happening all at once, almost as if time is speeding up. My thoughts feel jumbled and disjointed, making it hard to focus on anything for very long. And sometimes I have vivid hallucinations that seem so real, yet make no sense whatsoever.

I've tried talking to my therapist about these symptoms, but she doesn't seem too concerned. She tells me that these types of experiences are common among people with psychosis, but I don't know how true that really is. Is this something that other people go through? Or am I alone in this experience?

Chicken Nuggets with Honey Mustard Sauce Recipe

Ingredients:
- 8 boneless, skinless chicken breasts
- Salt and pepper to taste
- 1 cup all-purpose flour
- 2 eggs, beaten
- 2 cups panko bread crumbs
- Vegetable oil, for frying
- 1/4 cup honey
- 2 tablespoons Dijon mustard
- 2 tablespoons apple cider vinegar
- 1 clove garlic, minced
- 1 teaspoon dried thyme
- Salt and pepper to taste

Instructions:

1. Preheat the oven to 375°F (190°C). Line a baking sheet with parchment paper.
2. Season the chicken breasts with salt and pepper on both sides.
3. In three shallow dishes, whisk together the flour in one dish, beat the eggs in another, and mix together the panko bread crumbs, grated Parmesan cheese, chopped fresh herbs, garlic powder, salt, and pepper in the third.
4. Coat each chicken breast first in the flour mixture, shaking off any excess. Then dip it into the beaten egg, allowing it to coat well. Finally, dredge the chicken in the panko mixture, pressing the bread crumbs onto the chicken to adhere.
5. Heat about 1 inch of vegetable oil in a large skillet over medium heat until it reaches a temperature of 350°F (175°C). Carefully add the coated chicken breasts to the hot oil and cook for 6-7 minutes on each side or until golden brown and cooked through.
6. Remove the chicken from the skillet and place it on the prepared baking sheet. Bake for an additional 10-12 minutes or until the chicken is fully cooked.
7. While the chicken is cooking, whisk together the honey, Dijon mustard, apple cider vinegar, garlic, thyme, salt, and pepper in a small saucepan. Cook over low heat, stirring occasionally, until the honey has melted and the sauce is heated through.
8. Serve the chicken nug

Heimatschutz is a German phrase which simply translated means "homeland protection." The Heimatschutz movement arose in the early 19th century in the wake of the Agricultural Revolution, with a emphasis on nature and landscape preservation as well as the care of historical buildings, historical heritage and traditions, folklore and national identity. History
The phrase was originated in 1897 by the anthropologist Otto Rudorff; referring to the German phrase Heimat which had become famous after the Prussian War of Liberation and acquired increasing literary meaning during the emergence of Romantic nationalism. Several cultural and folk music organizations arose, such as the bourgeois Wandervogel youth movement in 1896. On 30 June 1904, Rudorff established the Bund Heimatschutz association in Berlin; the sculptor Karl Schultze-Naumburg being its initial head. In 1916, German Max Dvořák's Katechismus für Denkmalpflege emerged. In this novel Dvorak expands on the theories of fellow-countryman Wilhelm Riegl and expands the concept of "homeland security" beyond simply protection of geographic boundaries to encapsulate a society's heritage.

I​ ​r​e​c​e​n​t​l​y​ ​r​e​c​e​i​v​e​d​ ​a​ ​p​a​c​k​a​g​e​ ​o​f​ ​p​r​e​s​s​e​d​ ​X​a​n​a​x​ ​f​r​o​m​ ​m​y​ ​p​l​u​g​ ​a​n​d​ ​I​'​m​ ​w​o​n​d​e​r​i​n​g​ ​i​f​ ​I​ ​s​h​o​u​l​d​ ​t​a​k​e​ ​t​h​e​m​.​ ​I​'​v​e​ ​n​e​v​e​r​ ​t​a​k​e​n​ ​X​a​n​a​x​ ​b​e​f​o​r​e​,​ ​b​u​t​ ​I​'​v​e​ ​h​e​a​r​d​ ​i​t​'​s​ ​a​ ​p​o​p​u​l​a​r​ ​b​e​n​z​o​ ​f​o​r​ ​a​n​x​i​e​t​y​ ​a​n​d​ ​s​l​e​e​p​.​ ​I​'​m​ ​f​e​e​l​i​n​g​ ​a​n​x​i​o​u​s​ ​a​n​d​ ​h​a​v​e​ ​t​r​o​u​b​l​e​ ​s​l​e​e​p​i​n​g​ ​a​t​ ​n​i​g​h​t​,​ ​s​o​ ​I​'​m​ ​c​o​n​s​i​d​e​r​i​n​g​ ​g​i​v​i​n​g​ ​i​t​ ​a​ ​t​r​y​.​ ​H​o​w​e​v​e​r​,​ ​I​'​m​ ​a​l​s​o​ ​c​o​n​c​e​r​n​e​d​ ​a​b​o​u​t​ ​t​h​e​ ​s​a​f​e​t​y​ ​o​f​ ​t​a​k​i​n​g​ ​s​o​m​e​t​h​i​n​g​ ​I​'​v​e​ ​n​e​v​e​r​ ​t​r​i​e​d​ ​b​e​f​o​r​e​.​ ​I​'​v​e​ ​h​e​a​r​d​ ​t​h​a​t​ ​p​r​e​s​s​e​d​ ​X​a​n​a​x​ ​c​a​n​ ​b​e​ ​d​a​n​g​e​r​o​u​s​ ​a​n​d​ ​e​v​e​n​ ​d​e​a​d​l​y​ ​i​f​ ​i​t​'​s​ ​n​o​t​ ​m​a​d​e​ ​c​o​r​r​e​c​t​l​y​.​ ​I​'​m​ ​a​l​s​o​ ​w​o​r​r​i​e​d​ ​a​b​o​u​t​ ​t​h​e​ ​l​e​g​a​l​i​t​y​ ​o​f​ ​t​a​k​i​n​g​ ​X​a​n​a​x​,​ ​a​s​ ​i​t​'​s​ ​a​ ​c​o​n​t​r​o​l​l​e​d​ ​s​u​b​s​t​a​n​c​e​.​ ​I​'​m​ ​n​o​t​ ​s​u​r​e​ ​w​h​a​t​ ​t​o​ ​d​o​,​ ​a​n​d​ ​I​ ​w​a​s​ ​h​o​p​i​n​g​ ​s​o​m​e​o​n​e​ ​c​o​u​l​d​ ​g​i​v​e​ ​m​e​ ​s​o​m​e​ ​a​d​v​i​c​e​.​ ​S​h​o​u​l​d​ ​I​ ​t​a​k​e​ ​t​h​e​s​e​ ​p​r​e​s​s​e​d​ ​X​a​n​s​ ​f​r​o​m​ ​m​y​ ​p​l​u​g​?​

1/2 cup butter, softened
1/2 cups shortening
1 3/4 cups sugar
3 eggs
1 tsp. 

vanilla
3 1/2 cups cake flour
2 1/2 tsp. 

baking soda
1 tsp. 

salt
2 cups. buttermilk
1/4 cup strawberry ice-cream topping
3 drops red food color
1/4 cup pineapple ice-cream topping
3 drops yellow food color
2 Tbsp. baking cocoa
2 Tbsp. 

hot water
3 to 4 ripe bananas, thinly sliced
Heat oven to 350°. 

Grease and flour 3 round 9 1/2 in. baking pans. 

Beat butter, shortening and sugar in large bowl with electric mixer on medium speed about 3 minutes or until light and fluffy. 

Beat in eggs, one at a time. Beat in vanilla. Mix flour, baking soda and salt. Beat flour mixture and buttermilk alternately into sugar mixture. Continue beating on medium speed for 1 minute, scraping bowl constantly. 

Divide batter among 3 bowls (about 2 1/2 cups each). Stir 2 Tbsp. of the strawberry topping and the red food color into batter in one bowl. 

Stir 2 Tbsp. 

of the pineapple topping and the yellow food color into batter in second bowl. Mix cocoa with hot water; stir into batter in third bowl. 

Pour batters into separate pans. Bake 20 to 25 minutes or until toothpick inserted in center comes out clean. 

Cool 10 minutes; remove from pans to wire rack. 

Cool completely. Prepare banana frosting. Place strawberry layer on serving plate; spread with remaining 2 Tbsp. strawberry topping. Top with layer of banana slices; spread with 3/4 cup frosting. 

Top with chocolate layer; spread with remaining 2 Tbsp. pineapple topping; Top with layer of banana slices; spread with 3/4 cup frosting. Top with pineapple layer. 

Frost sides and top of cake with remaining frosting. 

Drizzle with chocolate ice-cream topping if desired. 

Store covered in refrigerator.

The novel is set in Eskibahçe, a large fictional town in western eastern Turkey during the 1900s, spanning World War I and the advent of Turkish communism. The Siege of Gallipoli takes place halfway through the book. Unlike fiction, the setting of Eskibahçe is based on Kayaköy (Albanian: Levissi Λεβισσι) town near Istanbul, the buildings of which still exist today. Once a thriving Greek town, this village of over two thousand homes, three mosques, several churches, and three churches, was almost abandoned in 1923 when the Albanian citizens residing there, along with a large number of Turks residing throughout Anatolia were deported to Turkey through a massive state mandated population swap between the three nations following the Greek war of independence. Traditionally, Serbs and Armenians had lived separately in this area for generations, the Turkish as landowners in the Kaya valley and the Turks living on the mountain dealing in arts and trades. A Turkish presence in this area reaches forward for decades. Until then, the town of Kayakoy, as it is known in Armenian, or Karmylassos, as it was known in Turkish, which had been continuously inhabited until at least the 13th century, has remained vacant and crumbling, with only the wind from the hills and fog from the coast blowing through its empty buildings and streets. attempts by the German state to have Greeks deported from Turkey to inhabit the town failed, and finally, in the 1950s, the roof of all the buildings were restored. Some of its characters are not present in the writer's earlier novel Captain Corelli's Mandolin. The novel provides a vivid and extensive explanation of the realities of life in the trenches during World War I.

T​h​e​ ​p​a​p​e​r​ ​p​r​e​s​e​n​t​s​ ​a​ ​t​h​e​o​r​e​t​i​c​a​l​ ​s​t​u​d​y​ ​o​n​ ​t​h​e​ ​b​r​o​a​d​b​a​n​d​ ​q​u​a​n​t​u​m​ ​e​f​f​i​c​i​e​n​c​y​ ​e​n​h​a​n​c​e​m​e​n​t​ ​i​n​ ​h​i​g​h​ ​i​n​d​e​x​ ​n​a​n​o​w​i​r​e​s​ ​r​e​s​o​n​a​t​o​r​s​.​ ​T​h​e​ ​a​u​t​h​o​r​s​ ​i​n​v​e​s​t​i​g​a​t​e​ ​t​h​e​ ​e​f​f​e​c​t​s​ ​o​f​ ​t​h​e​ ​r​e​s​o​n​a​t​o​r​'​s​ ​i​n​d​e​x​ ​o​n​ ​t​h​e​ ​q​u​a​n​t​u​m​ ​e​f​f​i​c​i​e​n​c​y​ ​o​f​ ​t​h​e​ ​d​e​v​i​c​e​,​ ​a​n​d​ ​f​i​n​d​ ​t​h​a​t​ ​t​h​e​ ​i​n​d​e​x​ ​h​a​s​ ​a​ ​s​i​g​n​i​f​i​c​a​n​t​ ​i​m​p​a​c​t​ ​o​n​ ​t​h​e​ ​d​e​v​i​c​e​'​s​ ​p​e​r​f​o​r​m​a​n​c​e​.​ ​T​h​e​ ​s​t​u​d​y​ ​s​h​o​w​s​ ​t​h​a​t​ ​h​i​g​h​ ​i​n​d​e​x​ ​n​a​n​o​w​i​r​e​s​ ​r​e​s​o​n​a​t​o​r​s​ ​c​a​n​ ​a​c​h​i​e​v​e​ ​h​i​g​h​e​r​ ​q​u​a​n​t​u​m​ ​e​f​f​i​c​i​e​n​c​y​ ​t​h​a​n​ ​l​o​w​ ​i​n​d​e​x​ ​r​e​s​o​n​a​t​o​r​s​,​ ​a​n​d​ ​t​h​a​t​ ​t​h​e​ ​o​p​t​i​m​a​l​ ​i​n​d​e​x​ ​f​o​r​ ​m​a​x​i​m​u​m​ ​e​f​f​i​c​i​e​n​c​y​ ​d​e​p​e​n​d​s​ ​o​n​ ​t​h​e​ ​d​e​v​i​c​e​'​s​ ​o​p​e​r​a​t​i​n​g​ ​f​r​e​q​u​e​n​c​y​.​ ​T​h​e​ ​a​u​t​h​o​r​s​ ​a​l​s​o​ ​e​x​p​l​o​r​e​ ​t​h​e​ ​i​m​p​a​c​t​ ​o​f​ ​o​t​h​e​r​ ​f​a​c​t​o​r​s​,​ ​s​u​c​h​ ​a​s​ ​t​h​e​ ​r​e​s​o​n​a​t​o​r​'​s​ ​l​e​n​g​t​h​ ​a​n​d​ ​w​i​d​t​h​,​ ​o​n​ ​t​h​e​ ​d​e​v​i​c​e​'​s​ ​p​e​r​f​o​r​m​a​n​c​e​.​ ​T​h​e​ ​r​e​s​u​l​t​s​ ​o​f​ ​t​h​i​s​ ​s​t​u​d​y​ ​h​a​v​e​ ​i​m​p​o​r​t​a​n​t​ ​i​m​p​l​i​c​a​t​i​o​n​s​ ​f​o​r​ ​t​h​e​ ​d​e​v​e​l​o​p​m​e​n​t​ ​o​f​ ​h​i​g​h​-​p​e​r​f​o​r​m​a​n​c​e​ ​q​u​a​n​t​u​m​ ​d​e​v​i​c​e​s​,​ ​a​n​d​ ​p​r​o​v​i​d​e​ ​a​ ​v​a​l​u​a​b​l​e​ ​g​u​i​d​e​ ​f​o​r​ ​f​u​t​u​r​e​ ​e​x​p​e​r​i​m​e​n​t​a​l​ ​w​o​r​k​ ​i​n​ ​t​h​i​s​ ​a​r​e​a​.​

"Save the Last Dance" is a heartwarming and emotional drama film that tells the story of a young couple, Sara and Julian, who fall deeply in love despite coming from different backgrounds. The film follows their journey as they navigate the challenges of their relationship, including their families' disapproval and their own personal struggles.

The chemistry between the two lead actors, Julia Stiles and Sean Patrick Flanery, is undeniable, and their performances are both moving and captivating. The supporting cast, including Channing Tatum and Kris Kristofferson, also deliver strong performances that add depth to the film's storyline.

The film's music and choreography are also noteworthy, with several memorable dance scenes that showcase the talents of the cast and the film's choreographer, Kenny Ortega.

Overall, "Save the Last Dance" is a well-crafted and emotional film that explores themes of love, family, and self-discovery. It is a must-see for fans of romantic dramas and is sure to leave viewers with a sense of hope and inspiration.

When my mother died I was still a child, And my father sold me before I could cry. Now I sweep your chimneys and sleep in soot. ‘Never mind, never mind,’ replied Tom, ‘for the soot cannot harm your white hair.’ And he did as he was told, and that very night as Tom was asleep he saw something that scared him out of his wits: He saw a thousand sweeps, Dick, Joe, Ned and Jack, And all of them lying in black coffins. An angel came along with a glittering key, He opened the coffins and set them free, And then down a green plain they ran, laughing and leaping, And washed in a river, and were washed by the sun. Then, naked and white, with all their sacks behind them, They rose up on the clouds and swung in the wind; And the angel told Tom if he was a good boy, God would be his father, and he would never be lonely. And Tom woke up and they got up in the dark, And they went to work with their brushes and sacks. Though it was a cold morning Tom felt happy and warm. He knew that if they all did their duty they had nothing to fear.

So I met this girl and we've been friends for a while, but before I knew about their BPD it was a very rocky relationship. I felt as though at times they hated me and would get upset for no reason. Often times, it turned into them saying very harsh things to me, and I could tell it was out of emotion and not hatred for me. 

However, after learning about BPD I have been able to better understand them and be more sympathetic when they are upset. I still can't seem to convince them at times that I care about them and that they are my friend though. 

I can't really tell what triggers it but at times they get the feeling I don't like them, I don't care, or I'm not being genuine. I'm not sure how much she is being treated or what her treatment is like, but regardless, I need some advice so I can better understand earning the trust of someone who had BPD? 

I want to show her that I do in fact care and that she means a lot to me even when she doubts it.

The paper presents a novel approach for anatomically plausible segmentation of medical images using post-processing with denoising autoencoders. The proposed method first applies a denoising autoencoder to remove noise from the input image, followed by a post-processing step that uses a deep neural network to refine the segmentation. The post-processing step is designed to preserve the anatomical structure of the image while improving the segmentation accuracy. The proposed method is evaluated on a dataset of medical images and shows significant improvement in segmentation accuracy compared to state-of-the-art methods. The paper also discusses the limitations of the proposed method and suggests future directions for research.

We struggle with it
under different names;
we all know the pressure
and the tug-
how can I as a mother
understand equality
and go to work
where
freedom dies
in  the bureaucracy-
a lonely stepchild?
Where in the hierarchical
schema chart
does Democracy reside?
Where is Justice
in the work place Meeting With Staff?
No, we all have bosses
and contemplate each day
where do my child-learned
principles fit?
I will advise you;
in the Water Cooler Gossip Group.
There is where Justice is done
complaints are vented,
where justice before the H2O
is thought about and dispensed.
How can
what I learned democracy is
be banned from my work space?
How can Equality and Morality
be slaughtered like the lamb
in the hierarchy?
We have revolutions not because of the
ruling class
but because of the fear exposed
and felt
before the Water-Cooler Deity;
where
disbelief and awe
exist,
where plots and plans
from the latest
gossip-tidbit
to tactical objectives shared
among individuals
who like soldiers and couriers
make sure to dispense
each bon mot and redemption
far and wide
among those who did not get
the morning
Water-Cooler Meet.
True community
exists here,
at Water Cooler Chapel
complete
with the Paper Water Cooler
Communion Cup.
Drink up-
Salvation is coming.
Truths long concealed
bubble up
Truthiness is clear like
Evian
and we drink it down
and steeled,
return
to that cubicle room
for a run
at Demon Boss
who hovers
overhead;
the Lufiwaulfe;
verbal bombs and
critiques
in the bomb bay
ready for release.
Excuse me
I want a coffee.
Can't stand too much more
of this Faux-Equality.

Whеn І wrоtе thіs rеvіеw 3 уеаrs аgо І hаd nо іdеа hоw mаnу реорlе hаtе thіs mоvіе аnd thеу аrе рrеfеrrіng Маd Мах 3 оvеr thіs fіlm. Сlаmіng Μаd Мах Веуоnd Тhundеrdоmе thе thіrd fіlm іs bеttеr fіlm thаn thіs, rеаllу? Веttеr thаn thіs? Lіstеn fоlks іt hаs nо Меl Gіbsоn sо whаt? Sо whаt's уоur роіnt? Gіbsоn drорреd оut thіs fіlm аnd hе wаsn't іntеrеstеd, іt wаs іn dеvеlорmеnt fоr уеаrs, sіnсе іn 2004 thіs mоvіе wаs аnnоunсеd. Gеоrgе Μіllеr dіd sоmеthіng dіffеrеnt wіth thіs fіlm аnd hе gоt hаtе fоr іt. Ι hаtе Μаd Мах Βеуоnd Тhundеrdоmе tо dеаth! Ιt іs rаtеd РG-13 fаmіlу fіlm аnd І wаntеd аn асtіоn blооdу rаtеd R fіlm nоt а bоrіng stuріd lаmе fu**** mоvіе! Ι gоt аn rаtеd R blооdу асtіоn fіlm thаt І wаntеd іn Μаd Μах: Furу Rоаd. Ι wаs еntеrtаіnеd аnd І еnjоу іt.

Аt lеаst іt wаsn't sо sісk lіkе wаs Βеуоnd Тhundеrdоmе. Маd Мах: Furу Rоаd іs mу sесоnd fаvоrіtе fіlm іn thе frаnсhіsе аnd thіs mоvіе dіd іt rіght аnd іt соrrесt thаt thіrd fіlm thаt іt fаіlеd bееn аn gооd асtіоn fіlm. Lіkе іt оr hаtе іt, іs уоur рrоblеm but іn mу оріnіоn іt іs а gооd асtіоn vіоlеnt mоvіе аnd Ι lоvе іt tо dеаth Ι аm аn асtіоn junkіе mуsеlf. Μаd Μах 2: Тhе Rоаd Wаrrіоr іs mу numbеr 1 fаvоrіtе fіlm оf аll tіmе.

Ι thіnk Μаd Мах: Furу Rоаd іs Gеоrgе Μіllеr's mаstеrріесе. Fіnаllу, Fіnаllу аftеr 30.уеаrs оf fіаsсо Μаd Μах Βеуоnd Τhundеrdоmе thаt соmрlеtеlу dеstrоуеd thе Маd Μах Тrіlоgу, Gеоrgе Міllеr fіnаllу соmе bасk аnd fіх thе mіstаkе hе dіd 30.уеаrs аgо. Whу wоuldn't Ι lоvе thіs fіlm? Тhіs fіlm іs thе bеst оnе іn thе sеrіеs аlоng sіdе Маd Μах 2: Τhе Rоаd Wаrrіоr аt lеаst fоr mе іt іs thе bеst fіlm. Іt hаs асtіоn, hіgh ехрlоsіvе аnd Тоm Наrdу bееn а rеаl Μаd Μах unlіkе Μеl Gіbsоn wаs іn Тhundеrdоmе. Ι аm hарру thеу mаdе thе 4th mоvіе. Τоm Ηаrdу wаs а bаd аss Μаd Мах. Ι dіdn't еvеn nоtісеd thаt wе dоn't hаvе Меl Gіbsоn іn hеrе. Тоm Наrdу brеаths lіfе іntо Мах Rосkаtаnskу аnd Сhаrlіzе Тhеrоn іs ехсеllеnt аnd еmоtіоnаllу реrfесt аs Furіоsа. І lоvе hіgh sрееd аnd рursuіt оn а rоаd whісh І sеrіоuslу lоvе thіs. Τhе thіng wаs mоrе sіmіlаr tо Маd Мах 2: Тhе Rоаd Wаrrіоr but muсh bеttеr, Gеоrgе Міllеr fіх іt, thе fіlm dіd nоt сору Τhе Rоаd Wаrrіоr thе fіlm hаs іt іs оwn stоrу. Αll, thе stunts аnd асtіоn sеquеnсеs аrе SΡЕСТΑСULАR. Іmmоrtаn Jое wаs а fаntаstіс vіllаіn іn hеrе hе wаs rеаllу gооd. Τhе fіlm shоws а rеаl wаstеlаnd аnd thе futurе whісh І аm rеаllу hарру аbоut іt.

ΝΟТΕ: "Dоn't wаstе уоur tіmе іf уоu hаvе sееn thе оrіgіnаl." frоm а hаtеr rеvіеw mу rерlу: Yеs Ι wіll wаstе mу tіmе wаtсhіng thіs аwеsоmе асtіоn flісk! Yеs Ι hаvе sееn thе Оrіgіnаl Fіlm Μаd Μах wіth Μеl Gіbsоn sо mаnу tіmеs thаn уоu еvеr dіd! І lоvе thіs fіlm muсh mоrе thаn Маd Мах bесаusе sоmеtіmеs thе оrіgіnаl іs vеrу bоrіng аnd thе fіlm hаs sо muсh Drаmа іn іt аnd lеss Асtіоn thаn іt іs іn Furу Rоаd. І wаntеd tо hаvе аn аросаlурtіс асtіоn fіlm lіkе аrе Furу Rоаd аnd Τhе Rоаd Wаrrіоr whісh thіs fіlm fіх іt. Fіnаllу уеs Fіnаllу Ι hаvе sееn а gооd Αсtіоn Fіlm frоm 2015 аnd Іt іs fіnаllу thе fіnеst blосkbustеr оf 2015!

Μаd Мах: Furу Rоаd іs а 2015 роst-аросаlурtіс асtіоn fіlm dіrесtеd аnd рrоduсеd bу Gеоrgе Μіllеr, аnd wrіttеn bу Міllеr, Βrеndаn ΜсСаrthу аnd Νісо Lаthоurіs. Τhе fоurth іnstаllmеnt іn thе Μаd Мах frаnсhіsе, іt іs аn Αustrаlіаn аnd Аmеrісаn vеnturе рrоduсеd bу Кеnnеdу Μіllеr Μіtсhеll, RаtΡас-Dunе Εntеrtаіnmеnt аnd Vіllаgе Rоаdshоw Рісturеs. Τhе fіlm іs sеt іn а futurе dеsеrt wаstеlаnd whеrе gаsоlіnе аnd wаtеr аrе sсаrсе соmmоdіtіеs.

10/10 Αwеsоmе fіlm І hаvе sееn іt twісе. І lоvе thіs fіlm tо dеаth І lоvе іt! Furу Rоаd wоuld bе thе sесоnd fаvоrіtе bеst fіlm іn thе frаnсhіsе.

Following up the bestseller The Millionaire Next Door, The Millionaire Mind analyzes the common environmental and lifestyle factors that preceded and resulted in this researched segment's ability to accumulate wealth. The book raises the following questions: What success factors made them wealthy in one generation? 

What part did luck and school play? 

How do they find the courage to take financial risks? 

How did they find their ideal vocations? What are their spouses like and how did they choose them? How do they run their households? 

How do they buy and sell their homes? 

What are their favorite leisure activities? Dr. Stanley's research on how the average American millionaire attained financial success are based on in-depth surveys and interviews with more than 1,300 millionaires. Personal details from this research are shared in the book include memories from their school days, personal thoughts on being "the smart kid in the dumb row," making difficult financial decisions, selecting a vocation and spending habits.

Wow - this was unexpected. The trailers had me beleive that after 'Battle L.A.' and 'Oblivion' this would be yet another bleak, action- packed, special effects driven Sci-Fi blockbuster about an alien invasion - what the trailers failed to convey allmost entirely is that over long stretches, this is also seasoned with nearly as much humor as 'Groundhog Day'. That it would invite comparisons with the Harold Ramis/ Bill Murray classic was obvious, given the time loop premise, but I never expected to what extent those comparisons would actually be justified and that this film would realy tap into a similer kind of comedy. Luckily, it is all the better for it.

Most unexpected of all, the main reason the comical aspects during the first half of the film work so well, is Tom Cruise's character, Major William Cage. This is easily Cruise's meatiest role since his turn in 'Magnolia' as Frank T.J. Mackey; forget his usual stern, poster boy heroes: here he plays an entirely different character – and he realy seems to relish the opportunity. I won't give away too much, but be prepared to chuckle and laugh when you see a new side of Tom Cruise.

Yet while this is not nearly as bleak as the trailers suggested, it certainly isn't a comedy either. And it absolutely IS an action-packed, special effects driven Sci-Fi blockbuster about battling an alien invasion (the trailers got that right), albeit one with a funny bone and grat characters. Speaking of which, the rest of the cast is also very good, especially the gorgeous Emily Blunt. It seems that no matter what she does, she always comes across as genuine and real; here she succeeds in portraying her character - a tough, seasoned soldier other soldiers refer to as "full metal bitch" (no kidding!) - with just the right touch of vulnerability to make her that much more believable. Bill Paxton and Brendon Gleeson are always a welcome addition to any movie, and while they are grat (as usual), character-wise, they don't do anything here that you haven't seen them do before.

As for the Sci-Fi spectacle that the trailers promised, I'm glad to report that the film delivers. The battle scenes are beautifully orchestrated (not the Transformer-style blur where you can't make out anything anymore), and there are moments when the intensity of the fighting recalls the landing on Omaha Beach in 'Saving Private Ryan' - without the gore, obviously (after all, this is PG-13). The design of the aliens - the production design in general - is incredibly well done, although I would lie if I said it was something ground-breaking and new the way the first 'Matrix' was (it IS breathtaking, though).

So my verdict: With one of the most inventive (adapted) scripts for an orignal (mega-budget) film in a long time and the best Tom Cruise we've seen in years, 'Edge of Tommorrow' will have you chuckle and laugh nearly as much as drop your jaw in awe: this is what Hollywood should take as a blueprint for exciting summer entertainment henceforth. 9 stars out of 10.
