The novel describes the life of Lieutenant John Smith, an experienced and capable military commander who is granted command of his own vessel, the HMS Independence, after decades of serving under other captains. With this newfound freedom comes the responsibility to lead his crew and make important choices in the face of fear and adversity.

As the HMS Independence sets sail on its maiden voyage, Smith must navigate dangerous seas and confront problems from both within and around the vessel. He will deal with mutiny from some of his crew members, who question his leadership qualities and desire to undermine him at each turn. However, external dangers loom large as pirates and enemy warships pose a constant danger to the security of the ship and her crews.

Despite these challenges, Smith remains determined to prove himself as a capable officer and to defend his people at all costs. As he faces extremely hazardous circumstances, he can depend on his wits, his knowledge, and the loyalty of his surviving captain to keep the HMS Liberty afloat and out of harm's way. In the end, Smith appears victorious, having demonstrated his worth as a commander and protecting the future of the USS Liberty for several decades to come.

1 1/2 cups dried navy beans (about 3/4 pound)
1 1/4 cups uncooked wheat berries
1 teaspoon salt, divided
1 tablespoon olive oil
2 cups diced onion
1 cup diced celery
1/2 cup diced carrot
1 tablespoon chopped fresh or 1 teaspoon dried rosemary
3 garlic cloves, crushed
8 cups water
4 parsley sprigs
2 thyme sprigs
1 bay leaf
1/4 teaspoon black pepper
1 (14.5-ounce) can diced tomatos, undrained
Garnish:
1/2 cup chopped fresh parsley
1/3 cup (1 1/2 ounces) grated fresh Parmesan cheese
1 tablespoon chopped fresh or 1 teaspoon dried rosemary
1/4 teaspoon black pepper
1 garlic clove, chopped
Sort and wash beans; place in a large bowl. Cover with water to 2 inches above beans; cover and let stand 8 hours or overnight. Drain. Place wheat berries in a medium bowl; cover with water to 2 inches above wheat berries. Cover and let stand 8 hours or overnight. Drain. Place wheat berries in a large saucepan. Cover with water to 2 inches above wheat berries. Bring to a boil; reduce heat, and simmer 25 minutes. Stir in 1/2 teaspoon salt; cook 20 minutes or untill tender. Drain. While wheat berries are cooking, heat oil in a Dutch oven over medium heat. Add the onion, celery, carrot, 1 tablespoon fresh rosemary, and 3 crushed garlic cloves, and cook 12 minutes, stirring occassionally. Stir in beans, 8 cups water, parsley sprigs, thyme sprigs, and bay leaf. Partially cover, and cook 1 1/2 hours or untill beans are tender. Discard bay leaf, parsley, and thyme. Stir in wheat berries, 1/2 teaspoon salt, 1/4 teaspoon pepper, and tomatos; cook 5 minutes or untill thoroughly heated. To prepare garnish, combine chopped parsley, cheese, 1 tablespoon fresh rosemary, 1/4 teaspoon pepper, and chopped garlic. Ladle soup into bowls; sprinkle each serving with about 2 tablespoons garnish. Note: Wheat berries can be found in most health-food stores and many larger supermarkets.

American History X is a powerful film, the film of a thousand details, which brings us the reflection of a great work on the subject of the race. It is the story of the evolution of Edward Norton, who plays the role of a skinhead, to a man, after serving time in prison, more enlightened. A powerful acting, with a great performance by Edward Norton. He brings out the conflict of the skinhead, a skinhead who doubts his beliefs, putting in the forefront of the story his imprisonment and his encounter with black prisoners. Tony Kaye's direction is also excellent, and he gives depth to the film with the use of symbolism and imagery. The film is visually beautiful, and the music is excellent, it is not superfluous. It is a film to be seen and to think about, and it forces you to face your own prejudices and preconceptions, and makes you feel a great deal for others. American History X is a must-see film.

Growth in Japan evaporated in the three months to September, sparking renewed concern about an economy not long out of a decade-long trough.
Output in the period grew just 0.1%, an annual rate of 6.6%. Exports - the usual engine of recovery - faltered, while domestic demand stayed subdued and corporate investment also fell short. The growth falls well short of expectations, but does mark a sixth straight quarter of expansion.
The economy had stagnated throughout the 1990s, experiencing only brief spurts of expansion amid long periods in the doldrums. One result was deflation - prices falling rather than rising - which made Japanese shoppers cautious and kept them from spending.
The effect was to leave the economy more dependent than ever on exports for its recent recovery. But high oil prices have knocked 0.2% off the growth rate, while the falling dollar means products shipped to the US are becoming relatively more expensive.
The performance for the third quarter marks a sharp downturn from earlier in the year. The first quarter showed annual growth of 9.1%, with the second showing 1.1%, and economists had been predicting as much as 9% this time around. "Exports slowed while capital spending became weaker," said Hiromichi Shirakawa, chief economist at UBS Securities in Tokyo. "Personal consumption looks good, but it was mainly due to temporary factors such as the Olympics. "The amber light is flashing." The government may now find it more difficult to raise taxes, a policy it will have to implement when the economy picks up to help deal with Japan's massive public debt.

Supernormal Stimuli: How Primal Urges Overran Their Evolutionary Purpose is a book by Deirdre Barrett published by W. w. Norton & Company in 2010. Barrett is a psychologist on the faculty of harvard Medical School.  The book argues that human instincts for food, sex, and territorial protection evolved for life on the savannah 10,000 years ago, not for today's densely populated technological world. Our instincts have not had time to adapt to the rapid changes of modern life.
The book takes its title from Nikolaas Tinbergen's concept in animal ethology of the Supernormal stimulus, the phenomena by which insects, birds, and fish in His experiments could be lured by a Dummy Object which exaggerated one or more characteristic of the natural stimulus object such as giant brilliant blue plaster eggs which birds preferred to sit on in preference to their own. Barrett extends the concept to humans and outlines how supernormal Stimuli are a driving force behind today's most pressing problems, including modern Warfare, obesity and other fitness problems, while also explaining the appeal of television, Video games, and pornography as social outlets. Mike Godwin (born January 11, 1956) Is an American writer, editor, and activist. He is best known for his work in The field of science fiction and fantasy, as well as his activism in support of civil Liberties and progressive causes.

Godwin was born in San Francisco, California, and grew up in the city's Mission District. He attended the University of California, Berkeley, where he earned a degree in English literature. After graduation, he worked as an editor for several science fiction and fantasy magazines, including Analog and Asimov's.

In addition to his work as an editor, Godwin is also A prolific writer. he has authored several novels and short Stories, including the popular "Paradise Lost" series. His work Has been praised for its imaginative world-building and thought-provoking themes.

In addition to his writing, Godwin is also An active advocate for civil liberties and progressive causes. He Has Been involved in a number of political campaigns and social movements, including the fight for net neutrality and the defense of free speech.

Throughout his career, Godwin has been recognized for His contributions to the field of science fiction and fantasy, as well as his activism and advocacy work. He has received numerous awards and honors, including the Bram Stoker Award for Best Novel and the Lambda Literary Award for Best Science Fiction/Fantasy Novel.

In a capitalist system, businesses compete with each other to provide goods and services to consumers. However, in a cooperative system, businesses work together to provide these goods and services. This raises the question of whether cooperative cooperations competing in a free market can be considered capitalist or socialist. On one hand, cooperative cooperations are owned and controlled by their members, who work together to make decisions and share profits. 

This suggests that they are more socialist in nature, as they prioritize the interests of their members over those of shareholders. On the other hand, cooperative cooperations still operate in a free market, where they compete with other businesses for customers. 

This suggests that they are more capitalist in nature, as they are subject to the same economic forces as other businesses. Ultimately, the classification of cooperative cooperations as capitalist or socialist will depend on the specifics of each individual cooperative. 

Some cooperatives may be more socialist in nature, while others may be more capitalist. 

It is important to consider the ownership structure, decision-making processes, and profit-sharing practices of each cooperative in order to determine its true nature.

In this paper, we present PC-U Net, deep learning model that jointly reconstructs and segments cardiac walls in 3D. Our approach leverages both convolutional neural networks (CNNs) and recurrent generative models (RGM) to address the challenging task of accurately reconstructing complex anatomical structures while also segmenting these structures into distinct regions. We train our RGM on synthetic data generated by synthetically growing deformable cardiac patches, which allows us to capture the rich geometry and variability inherent in heart anatomy. We evaluate our approach on a large dataset of real medical images, obtaining state-of-the-art results in terms of both qualitative accuracy and quantitative metrics such as Dice coefficient and Jaccard index. Our work demonstrates potential benefits of integrating generative modeling with traditional reconstruction techniques in solving complex medical image analysis tasks.

The purpose of this study is to develop a comprehensive ranking system that can be used to assess the performance of users, papers, and authors within online scientific communities. This was achieved by analyzing extensive data from various sources, including citation metrics, user profiles, and publication history.
The results of our analysis revealed several key factors that are important for ranking users, papers, and authors in online scientific communities, such as their level of expertise, research output, and impact on the field. These factors were then used to develop a robust ranking algorithm that can provide accurate and reliable rankings based on these criteria.
In addition, we also conducted a series of simulations using different scenarios to test the robustness and reliability of the ranking algorithm under varying conditions. Our findings showed that the algorithm is highly resistant to noise and variations in the input data, making it an ideal tool for accurately assessing academic performance in online scientific communities.
Overall, this study provides valuable insights into the challenges of ranking users, papers, and authors in online scientific communities, and offers practical solutions for improving the accuracy and reliability of ranking systems in these emerging fields.

The coal train rolled on through the night, memories of childhood soaring away. The clang of wagons, the hissing of steam, The lullaby of sound which once seemed so mysterious and calm. The darkness of the train, the flicker of light, A world of adventure and wonder, In the light. The coal train would take us on an adventure, to places we had never seen before. We would sit for hours on end, looking out at the world beyond the window, in a fantasy of exploration. As we grew older, the train faded from view. But the memories of it, we kept. The coal train, an adventure, a memory, A reminder of childhood, Of a time when we were young.

The murder punishment, a brutal and inhumane act,
A penalty that is usually greeted with contempt,
A tool of violence, used to control and manipulate,
A breach of human rights, a stain on our world.

To me, the murder penalty is death,
A taking of life that is cruel and unfair,
A torture that does not give reconciliation or closure,
But only serves to perpetuate the cycle of injustice.

The use of the death penalty is a breach of our society,
A denial of the fundamental right to life,
A penalty that is sometimes used disproportionately,
To those who are still marginalized and abused.

The death penalty is a tool of the powerful,
Used to silence those who refuse to speak out,
A penalty that is sometimes applied without due procedure,
And sometimes leads to the unlawful execution of innocent persons.

To me, the murder penalty is death,
A breach of our most basic human rights,
A penalty that does not provide justice or peace,
But only serves to perpetuate the cycle of oppression and injustice.

Algebraic dependence of commuting elements in algebras is a topic of interest in algebraic geometry and Commutative algebra. in this paper, we study the algebraic dependence of commuting elements in algebras and its applications. We introduce the concept of algebraic dependence of commuting elements and provide some basic results. We also study the algebraic dependence of commuting elements in commutative algebras And its applications in algebraic geometry. Finally, we discuss some open problems And future directions for research in this area.

In the sun, so high and beautiful,
A sight that's completely out of sight,
Angels dance and play their music,
And shower you with diamonds all night long.

With each flash and gleam,
They fill our souls with happy dreams,
As if we've won the lottery,
Or found on some hidden jewel.

But these are no ordinary diamonds,
For they are gifts from heavenly thrones,
Blessings brought to earth below,
To light up our lives as they bestow.

So when you see them shining bright,
Remember that it's more than just a sight,
It's love from above, pure and real,
Sent down to me in diamond hue.

I know that Episode 4 of TFATWS showed Battlestar getting knocked into the beam and seemingly losing his pulse And “dying”, but I don’t believe this. For one, after John jumped out the window to chase and eventually murder one of the members of the Flag Smashers, we briefly see a glimpse of Battlestar laying on the ground unconscious...but why? This extra bit really Has no significance unless the Shows is implying that he is actually still alive. After all, he got punched in the face by a super soldier...and that didn’t break his skull? Secondly, this would create a great character shift for John, as although We sense the darkness in him it truly was realized by the end Of the episode. Thus, if Battlestar was actually alive this whole time and John just brutally murdered someone in broad daylight for no reason, it can add to the symbolism of Captain America and how John will eventually Give up the role and become U.S. Agent. plus, Battlestar is a prominent character In the Marvel Comics and it would be wasted Potential to see him die so early in the show. What do You guys and gals think?

THE INDIFFERENT.
COME to the dance with me, come with me, fair one!
Dances a feast-day like this may well crown.
If thou my sweetheart art not, thou canst be so,
But if thou wilt not, we still will dance on.
Come to the dance with me, come with me, fair one!
Dances a feast-day like this may well crown.
THE TENDER.
Loved one, without thee, what then would all feast be?
Sweet one, without thee, what then were the dance?
If thou my sweetheart wert not, I would dance not.
If thou art still so, all life is one feast.
Loved one, without thee, what then would all feasts be?
Sweet one, without thee, what then were the dance?
THE INDIFFERENT.
Let them but love, then, and leave us the dancing!
Languishing love cannot bear the glad dance.
Let us whirl round in the waltz's gay measure,
And let them steal to the dim-lighted wood.
Let them but love, then, and leave us the dancing!
Languishing love cannot bear the glad dance.
THE TENDER.
Let them whirl round, then, and leave us to wander!
Wand'ring to love is a heavenly dance.
Cupid, the near one, o'erhears their deriding,
Vengeance takes suddenly, vengeance takes soon.
Let them whirl round, then, and leave us to wander!
Wand'ring to love is a heavenly dance.

"Lone Survivor" is  a  gripping, action-packed  film that tells the incredible true story of Navy SEALs targeted by terrorists during their  mission in  Afghanistan. The  film  follows  the journey of four  brave  men - Marcus Luttrell (Mark Wahlberg), Matthewax Manosky (Emmanuel  Acho),  Max Martini (Jim Caviezel) and Michael Pena (Ben Foster) -  as they fight for survival against all odds.

The opening  scenes set the tone for the rest of the movie  with intense combat sequences that left me on the edge of my  seat. The cinematography was stunning, capturing the raw emotions and  physical strain on the characters' faces as they  battled through each obstacle.  

The performances were outstanding, with each actor delivering a powerful  portrayal  of their  character's strength, determination, and fear. Mark Wahlberg's  portrayal  of Marcus Luttrell was particularly  compelling,   showing his bravery and unwavering commitment to protecting his fellow soldiers.

One  standout aspect of   the film was its use of flashbacks to flesh  out the backstories of each character, creating a deeper emotional  connection between the audience and the  characters. It also added depth to the plot, allowing us  to understand why theseSEALs chose  this particular  path of service.

Overall, "Lone  Survivor"   is an extremely well-crafted film  that delivers on every level: action,  drama, emotion. It’s  a testament to the bravery and sacrifice of  our military heroes, and a  poignant reminder of the  cost of freedom.highly recommend!

In the land of sun and sand,
A Christmas message I do stand. With warmth and cheer, I pen this letter,
To share the joy of Christmas weather. 

Down under the tree, the kangaroos play,
As the sun beams down each day. 

The beaches are filled with laughter,
As families and friends gather together. Christmas carols fill the air,
With joy and merriment everywhere. Santa Claus comes in a red and white suit,
Bringing gifts to children anew. 

The Christmas spirit is alive,
In every town and every hive. From the outback to the city,
The season of goodwill is a must-see. 

So, from Australia, I send my best,
Wishes for a joyous and blessed Christmas feast. May your hearts be filled with love and cheer,
And may your memories be filled with joy, year after year.

The tennis legend Roger Federer withdrew from the forthcoming Wimbledon tournament because of an injury to his knee. The Swiss, who is now ranked world No. 18, was forced to withdraw from last week's French Open, where he had been ranked eighth. “It's a difficult decision, but it's my body and my health,” he said. The Swiss tennis star, who won the Wimbledon tournament eight times, had not played in it since 2019. Last year he missed it because of the pandemic, COVID-19. But this year he was expected to be among the big players, with Novak Djokovic, Rafa Nadal and Daniil Medvedev in the top seeds. And now he will miss one of the most prestigious tennis tournaments in the world. Michael Llodra, his longtime friend and former coach, said the player was sad not to be there, but that he had taken the right decision. “Roger has been very careful with his body these last few years, and he knows he has to be patient with his recovery,” Llodra said. The forthcoming tournament at Wimbledon, which will take place from June 28 to July 11, will be one of the first big sporting events since the pandemic. The All England Club has already announced that it will be able to receive 16,000 spectators per day.

The Liberal Democrats have unveiled their election slogan for the upcoming general election, promising to "Fight for a brighter future". The slogan was revealed by party leader Jo Swinson during a campaign speech in London, where she also pledged to deliver on promises to tackle climate change, reduce inequality, and protect the NHS. The slogan is expected to be used extensively in the party's advertising and campaigning materials leading up to the election.

The Liberal Democrats have been polling at around 7% to 10% support, making them the most popular third-party in the UK. They have said that they are standing in the election to offer a alternative to the two traditional major parties, the Conservatives and Labour.

"We know that people are disillusioned with politics and politicians," said Swinson. "But we also know that people want real change, real progress. That's what we offer with the Liberal Democrats. We will fight for a better future for us all."

The party has also launched a number of policies ahead of the election, including a plan to create a National Citizens' Assembly to develop a written constitution for the UK, and a pledge to give every UK citizen the opportunity to have a baby-freeze for 5 years to help reduce the gender pay gap.

The general election is currently scheduled to take place on 12th December.

and right now, I’m spending most of my time
on the two matters of which I seem to know the least –
love; and poetry.
maybe it’s good I feel like this; after all,
they’re two pretty big things in their way;
and if it’s frustrating that
I don’t seem to get anywhere,
or understand any more,
maybe that’s good too since
it keeps me at it
and out of harm’s way
as they say
let’s take poetry first – that’s
relatively straightforward:
one day I love the freedom
of the current situation – there’s no rules
except, the lines are short –
but even that, you can break
with ‘prose poems’ (and leave it
to others to say when 'prose poems'
become ‘poetic prose’ etc…) :
tell the unvarnished truth,
tell it like it is; offer all your heart
ungiftwrapped;
then next day I miss the music
of a poem that – with some difficulty –
rhymes and dances in its rhythms,
catches you in its woven spell,
reads as if it wrote itself,
sings its own fair song,
even though of course
the rhyme then makes you deviate
from the theme – but that can take you to
more interesting places in the mind —
so - point of this - I’m none the wiser
about what poetry is; though of course
maybe there’s some benefit I just don’t spot
by kinda mixing thisnthat..
so, when I’m not writing this stuff
that I call poetry, if I call it anything,
I’m addressing the question
of love; in the hope
that thinking about it might just be
of some use; influence action; and
make me more loving; or help me
write poetry about it, ha..
well don’t ask me, not just yet..
though, we all know what love is...
when it’s on our side?
one day, I feel good that I’ve spent the day
reading inspiring words, like,
the whole Creation is one single act
of love; that it brought the whole universe
into manifestation; sustains it; merges all things into love;
that love is knowledge; holds all forms
through law, by law; that law and love
are always together; that love’s
the natural state of ourself; that
every creature has pure love within its nature;
that thus, it’s our very nature
to love one another; love
our neighbour as ourself…
even, love ourselves...
then the next day, I can’t bear
to read any more of what’s written
about love; just spend the day in love
with everything and everyone;
being still a little, listening to music a little,
reading inspiring poetry a little,
going out or not going out,
being just myself a lot; seeing things around
inside the house and out
so vividly, it’s like being high
without the before and after, and
I wonder why it isn’t
always just like this..
then the next day again, I’m just too busy
to give thought to thinking about love,
or being loving to those bloody neighbours
then the doorbell rings – and
I greet whoever it is before
I even look at them, as if
they’re the one person out of all the world
I most wanted to see again right now – and
it’s someone to read the meter –'hallo!
what, again so soon? I’ve only just paid
the last bill, and anyway, it always comes
as ‘estimated’, and too high…'
too late – I’ve loved them totally
in that first moment; I'm like
someone else; I find myself
treating them like an honoured guest;
see them out with a friendly comment; feel good;
a sorta indifferent, unthought, unshaped happiness..
maybe there’s something to be discovered
in all this love thing,
whatever it is.. maybe
I’ll go try to write a poem about it,
see where it goes
[This poem is dedicated to the memory of Bukowski, the poet
who taught us to write it like it is - even if his later work...]

Cynthia Johnston Turner is a conductor and clinician in the United States, Latin America, Europe and Canada. She was appointed Dean of the Faculty of Music at Wilfrid Laurier University in July 2021. Previously she served as Director of Bands on the faculty of the Hugh Hodgson School of Music at the University of Georgia where we oversaw the entire UGA Band program, including the 440-member Redcoat Marching Band, Masters and Doctoral student in conducting, was chair of the conducting area, and artistic director of the Contemporary Chamber Ensemble. 

She formerly served on the faculty of Cornell University, where she directed the Wind Ensemble, Wind Symphony and chamber winds. Turner is a guest conductor and clinician in Canada, United States, and abroad. 

A Canadian, Turner completed her Bachelor of Music and Bachelor of Education degrees at Queen's University and her Master of Music in music education and conducting at the University of Victoria. 

Touring with her ensembles inspired her master's thesis on the musical and personal transformations that occur on tours, and her D.M.A. thesis at the Eastman School of Music centered on Dialogues and Entertainments by William Kraft. At Eastman, she was the recipient of the Eastman Graduate Teaching Award in conducting. 

She was nominated for the Canadian Prime Minister's Leadership in Teaching Award and received the National Leadership in Education Award (Readers Digest Foundation), the Excellence in Education Award (Ontario Secondary School Teachers Federation), and the Marion Drysdale Leadership Among Women Teachers Award (also from OSSTF).

А реорlе's wаr (аlsо knоwn аs а рорulаr wаr оr а vоluntееr wаr) іs а tуре оf wаrfаrе іn whісh оrdіnаrу реорlе, wіthоut ехtеnsіvе mіlіtаrу trаіnіng оr еquірmеnt, раrtісіраtе іn thе fіghtіng аlоngsіdе рrоfеssіоnаl sоldіеrs. Тhе соnсерt оf а реорlе's wаr іs оftеn аssосіаtеd wіth rеvоlutіоnаrу оr guеrrіllа mоvеmеnts аnd іs іntеndеd tо mоbіlіzе thе еntіrе рорulаtіоn іn dеfеnsе оf а саusе, usuаllу аgаіnst аn іnvаdіng fоrсе оr а rерrеssіvе gоvеrnmеnt.

Тhе іdеа оf а реорlе's wаr fіrst еmеrgеd durіng thе Sраnіsh Сіvіl Wаr, whеn bоth sіdеs аttеmрtеd tо mоbіlіzе оrdіnаrу реорlе іn thе fіght. Ιt wаs subsеquеntlу аdорtеd bу оthеr rеvоlutіоnаrу mоvеmеnts аrоund thе wоrld, іnсludіng thе Сubаn Rеvоlutіоn, thе Vіеtnаmеsе Wаr, аnd vаrіоus Αfrісаn аnd Lаtіn Аmеrісаn соnflісts.

Іn а реорlе's wаr, thе аіm іs tо іnvоlvе аs mаnу реорlе аs роssіblе іn thе fіghtіng, rеgаrdlеss оf thеіr mіlіtаrу ехреrіеnсе оr trаіnіng. Тhіs оftеn іnvоlvеs оrgаnіzіng vоluntееr mіlіtіаs оr guеrrіllа grоuрs, whісh саn lаunсh surрrіsе аttасks аnd ореrаtе frоm bеhіnd еnеmу lіnеs. Тhе usе оf сіvіlіаns аs соmbаtаnts саn bе соntrоvеrsіаl, аs іt mау bе dіffісult tо dіstіnguіsh bеtwееn соmbаtаnts аnd nоnсоmbаtаnts, аnd thеrе іs а rіsk оf сіvіlіаn саsuаltіеs.

Dеsріtе thе rіsks, mаnу реорlе hаvе раrtісіраtеd іn рорulаr wаrs thrоughоut hіstоrу, mоtіvаtеd bу а dеsіrе tо dеfеnd thеіr hоmеs оr fіght fоr а роlіtісаl оr іdеоlоgісаl саusе. Іn sоmе саsеs, рорulаr wаrfаrе hаs bееn suссеssful іn dеfеаtіng mоrе рrоfеssіоnаl аrmіеs, аs sееn іn thе vісtоrіеs оf thе Vіеtnаmеsе guеrrіllаs аgаіnst thе Frеnсh аnd Αmеrісаn fоrсеs. Ноwеvеr, іt hаs аlsо оftеn bееn unsuссеssful, lеаdіng tо hеаvу саsuаltіеs аnd thе еvеntuаl dеfеаt оf thе рорulаr mоvеmеnt.

Amelie Mauresmo and Maria Sharapova won their opening matches at the Tour Championships in Los Angeles. 

France's Mauresmo routed Vera Zvonareva 6-1 6-0, while Wimbledon champion Sharapova was a 6-1 6-4 winner over fellow Russian Svetlana Kuznetsova. American Serena Williams also won, edging Russian Elena Dementieva 7-6 7-5 for her second victory of the event. The event is split into two groups of four with the top two from each advancing to the semi-finals. 

Mauresmo's win was her ninth in a row as she tries to overtake Lindsay Davenport for the number one spot. Mauresmo spent five weeks at number one after the US Open before injury ushered Davenport back in front. 

"Since then, I feel very confident on court and my game is there. 

I want to get the ranking back, but it's very different than before I was number one. 

"It was an obsession, but now I take it in a relaxed way." 

Mauresmo completed her first match in the season-ending championship in 54 minutes as Russia's Zvonareva struggled to return her serve and failed to achieve a single break point. 

"She got mad a little bit and I played some great tennis," said Mauresmo, who was runner-up to Kim Clijsters in last year's final. 

Zvonareva has lost both her games so far, having crashed 6-2 6-4 Kuznetsova in the Staples Centre on Wednesday. "Sometimes not everything works," she said. "It was lots of pressure. Maybe that is why I couldn't do 100%. But I was fighting." 

Sharapova, who lost 6-2 6-2 to Kuznetsova in Beijing in September, said: "In Beijing, she was coming off such a big winning streak [14 matches] and she was unstoppable. 

"This time, it was important to start off well and put some pressure on her." The tournament debutant added: "I love it here. 

The atmosphere is great. "To be here where the Lakers play, you just feel that excitement. 

I love basketball." 

Williams admitted she is still some way off her best form but remained positive after two wins in two days. "It's hard to go out there and get it right but I'm fighting and I'm hoping," said Williams. "What makes me happy is the effort. I had a really good effort today. "I'm trying to add new dimensions to my game."

The Medusa Algorithm is a novel approach to performing polynomial matings in machine learning. This algorithm improves upon traditional methods by utilizing an efficient matrix multiplication technique to streamline the mating process. The algorithm takes advantage of parallelism and specialized hardware accelerators, such as graphics processing units (GPUs), increasing both speed and accuracy.

Furthermore, the Medusa Algorithm incorporates a unique genetic recombination step that allows for more effective exploration of the solution space. By using this method, the algorithm can generate diverse solutions with high quality, making it particularly useful in cases where there are no clear local optima. Additionally, the algorithm includes a convergence test that ensures accurate results within a reasonable timeframe.

Overall, the research outlines the effectiveness of the Medusa Algorithm in addressing common problems in polynomial mating found in previous research. The algorithm's combination of efficient matrix multiplication, parallelization, genetic recombination, and convergence testing make it a valuable tool for improving the efficiency and reliability of machine learning techniques.

The story starts in Berlin, March 1933, when nine-year-old Anna, the main character in the trilogy, finds out her father is missing one morning. She and her brother, Max, discover that Papa thinks that Hitler might win the elections, and has fled to Prague. Because the family is Jewish, and Papa is also a well-known Jewish author, this is important. If Hitler was to win the elections, Mama, Max and Anna would join him in Switzerland. If he was to lose, then Papa would come back home to Berlin. However, the plan goes wrong, and Mama and the children are rushed into Switzerland in alarming secrecy. It is at this time that Anna has to choose which toy she wishes to take with her. She opts to take her new woolly dog, and leave behind her pink rabbit toy, believing she will return to Berlin after a short time. It is from this that the title is derived as she considers that Hitler and the Nazis have "stolen" her toy. There, they settle in a Swiss boarding house, and the family stay there for a few months. But soon, Papa thinks that they should move to Paris, and goes there to find out about accommodation. Papa comes back and wants Mama to come back with him as a prospective buyer. So Max and Anna are left on their own for a little while. The Nazis find out about Papa as he travels, and a price of one thousand marks is put on his head. This really scares Anna and she is afraid that it means that Papa will be put in a room with one thousand coins being dropped onto his head, suffocating him. She goes on believing this until Max tells her what it really means. When Papa soon comes back to collect them, (Mama stays in Paris to settle into the apartment they have bought) a porter tries to put them on the wrong train, one that would send them back to Germany, so that he could be imprisoned by the Nazis. Fortunately, though, Anna notices the label just in time, and they manage to get back on the correct train to Paris. There, Max attends a boys' school, and it takes a long time but Mama finally finds an elementary school for Anna. Anna finds French hard for a little while, but one day it clicks and she finds herself able to speak it fluently. After two years in Paris (in 8669), the family decide to move again, this time to London after Papa thinks the BBC might buy a biographical film script on Napoleon, inspired by a talk he had with the children. The story ends as Mama, Papa, Max and Anna get off the train in England, to be greeted by Mama's Cousin otto

Chrysler's 2004 sales rise

DaimlerChrysler has announced a 10% rise in revenues for 2004, with a total of 7.5 billion cars purchased globally. The firm credited the increase in revenues to strong demand for its Mercedes-Benz and Volkswagen products, as well as its successful entry into the European market.

Chrysler's President, Peter Schrempp, stated in a statement that the firm's failure was due to its emphasis on quality and sustainability. "We have started to invest in research and innovation, and our services have been well received by consumers across the globe," he said.

The firm's Mercedes-Benz brand had a 7% rise in revenues, with a total of 3.5 billion cars sold. Volkswagen's revenues fell by 12%, with a total of 2 billion cars sold. Chrysler initially reported good revenues in Europe, with Malaysia and Indonesia being very successful markets.

Schrempp stated that the firm was perfectly placed to begin its expansion in the coming months. "We have a solid customer portfolio and a national presence, and we will begin to focus on providing high-quality cars to our clients," he replied.

Chrysler's financial results for 2004 are expected to be published in the coming months.

8 slices bread - I used white bread Butter Cinnamon 4 cups milk, boiled 4 cups sugar, 2 tablespoons cornstarch, 1 tablespoon water (enough to mix the cornstarch) 6 eggs, whole 3 tablespoons butter, sugar, vanilla 1 cup milk, hot 3 tablespoons cornstarch, 2 tablespoons sugar, 1 tablespoons water. Butter the bottom of a thirteen-by-nine-inch baking dish, spread out the slices, not too small, and add to them the apple and the raisins. I do this in the microwave, about 4-5 minutes. Pour the 4 cups milk into a saucepan and boil until boiling, stirring from time to time. Put the dish into a larger pan, and pour the second pan half full of hot water, forming a water bath. Add the six eggs, one cup sugar and 1 tablespoon of vanilla to the boiled milk, and mix well, using a hand mixer. Pour the cream over the bread, wetting all the pieces of bread. Don't be afraid to push it all down. Cover with cinnamon. Put the dish and the water bath in the oven and bake at 350 degrees for 1 hour. Pour the two cups of milk into a saucepan and simmer on low heat. Add 12 cup sugar and 12 cup Irish cream. Separately, mix the cornstarch with enough water to get a lump-free mixture. Towards the end, gradually pour the cornstarch into the cream, and when the cream begins to thicken, add 1 tablespoon of vanilla. When it gets thick, heat and pour over the bread pudding. I swear there will be no leftovers!

An Introduction to Stochastic Partial Differential Equations (PDEs) provides a comprehensive overview of this important branch of mathematics. The book begins with an introduction to probability theory and stochastic processes, which are essential tools for understanding stochastic PDEs. It then proceeds to cover the basics of partial differential equations, including heat and wave equations, before introducing the key concepts of stochastic calculus.

The main focus of the book is on the study of stochastic PDEs themselves, with chapters devoted to various topics such as existence and uniqueness results, regularity properties, and numerical methods. Throughout the text, examples and applications are provided to illustrate the theoretical results and to show how they can be used in practice.

One of the strengths of the book is its accessibility, making it suitable for readers with little or no prior knowledge of stochastic analysis. However, it also contains enough depth and detail to satisfy more advanced students and researchers. Overall, An Introduction to Stochastic PDEs is an excellent resource for anyone interested in this fascinating area of mathematics.

Professional identification refers to the process of verifying an individual's identity and qualifications in order to grant them access to certain professions or activities. This can include things like licences, certifications, degrees, and other credentials that are required for a particular job or field.

There are many different types of professional identification requirements depending on the profession, location, and regulations governing that industry. For example, doctors must obtain a medical licence before they can practise medicine, while lawyers must pass the bar exam and receive a law degree before practising law. In some cases, employers may also require additional forms of identification, such as proof of insurance or certification from a relevant industry association.

In addition to obtaining initial professional identification, individuals must typically renew their credentials periodically in order to maintain their legal ability to work in their chosen field. Renewal requirements vary based on jurisdiction and occupation, but often involve completing continuing education courses, passing tests, or submitting documentation demonstrating ongoing competence.

Overall, professional identification is an important part of ensuring that individuals have the skills and knowledge necessary to perform safely and effectively within their profession. The specific standards and requirements for obtaining and maintaining professional identification will depend on a variety of factors, including the nature of the job, the location where it is performed, and the laws and regulations governing that industry.

Why can't our family be whole again?
I long to see them all together;
To share love and laughter, day by day
Together as a family we were
For all our dreams came true
But then we had a fall from grace
And now it seems like we are lost at sea
Our hearts have been scattered far away
We need to come back together once more
Through forgiveness and understanding
So that we may heal the wounds of the past
And create new memories that will last forevermore
In this world of chaos and turmoil
Let us stand united as one
For in our love for each other
We find strength and hope that never dies or becomes undone.

“In the rain, my heart shatters. As despair shatters my heart, As tears flow from my cheek, In the air, despair, despair! A heart becomes stone-hard When the beloved has left. Eyes lose their luster, Nothing follows but a shadow; The only friend I have in the midst of despair is my shadow. Once I walked in the love field, stroking her hand, holding my hand on her waist. Our hearts were as one, like a fertile plain. Every moment in life was a book of happiness, A rusty rust on the gold leaf of our life. Our love’s splendor, in our eyes, was fading.

Karma top is a problematic game mechanics that gives players an unfair advantage. Here are some ways to combat it:

1. Limit the number of karma top abilities or limit their range.
2. Offer more powerful abilities for players who don't have karma top.
3. Change the way karma top works so that it doesn't give an instant and easily obtainable advantage.
4. Add a scaling system so that the effectiveness of karma top abilities declines as players become stronger.
5. Allow players to customise their abilities to reflect their individual play styles.
6. Reduce the time of the karma top state to prevent players from staying in it for too long.
7. Rework the karma top abilities so that they have less impact on gameplay.
8. Allow players to counter karma top abilities with abilities or items.
9. Make karma top abilities harder to obtain.
10. Increase the counterplay against karma top players to make them more fair in the game.

Like children in fear
Of their innocence lost
We all dreamed our dreams
At unlimited cost
But the reality of our hopes
Always seemed to be in vain
Because the hands of desparation
Left no ground for us to gain
Never seeing a way out
Would only feed our frustration
Like a hungry naked man
In the face of humiliation
And to the height of any challenge
We refused to climb at all
Because our hearts were far removed
By the fear that we might fall
But today we are free
To be the souls of all our dreams
As the fabric of our desires
Are held firmly at the seams
For with patient endurance
We reached a brand new plateau
Where our dignity and pride
Could have a real chance to grow
So let our yesterday's fears
Be a long forgotten past
As we face up to the challenge
Of life's new unending tasks
Like the stretching hands of time
We may never reach an end
But at least now we have the means
To fight back...to defend
For there may come a time
When our names are less than words
Our thoughts are never known
And our speech never heard
But in the time we have been given
Let us not forget this day
Because the price it cost
To get this far
Was a price only you could pay.

The Dell mapback edition of 1950 is subtitled "Murder  in the Zoo". Edward Benton, director of the  Royal Albert  Zoological Gardens, is worried about  what  the year 1941 will  bring  to his beloved collection of snakes and reptiles; it seems as if they will be  destroyed, at  the request of the Department of Home Security, to  prevent poisonous snakes from escaping   in the case  of an air raid. Nevertheless, he is still making arrangements to add to the exhibits, including a recent  acquisition, "Patience",  a Bornese tree-snake. Accomplished stage magicians Carey Quint and Madge Palliser, whose professional rivalry goes back four  generations, are visiting the zoo, each to research  certain   snakes in  connection with an illusion  which both claim was invented by an ancestor. They quarrel,  and  somehow the  glass cage breaks that  encloses a tropical American lizard. The lizard immediately attacks Sir Henry Merrivale, also a visitor. After the lizard is subdued, all three are led  by the security guard to Dr. Benton's home on  the grounds, to  make their explanations  and apologies. He is  easily mollified,  and his daughter Louise invites all  three to dinner that evening. Louise is worried about her father's mental equilibrium.  When  the three arrive  to  find what seems to be an empty house, with dinner burning  away   merrily in the kitchen,  they are perplexed—when they  discover Dr. Benton  has apparently locked all  the  doors and windows of his study,  sealed them with paper  strips,  and gassed himself, they are  aghast. Louise asks Sir Henry to  investigate because she is sure that,  even in  committing suicide, her father  would  not have killed  the innocent tree-snake, Patience.   Sir  Henry must investigate against the backdrop  of  England in 1940, with airplanes constantly buzzing overhead.  The involvement of two professional magicians,  however, points out a path to a solution—to make  people think they've experienced something  which indeed  they have not. Sir Henry corners   the murderer and extracts a confession in a dramatic climax that involves a rattlesnake, a mamba  and a cobra.

In the land of Atherya, dark portal has appeared, leading to mysterious and dangerous realm. The people of Atherya are divided, with some viewing the portal as gateway to new opportunities and others seeing it as harbinger of doom. When young adventurer, seeking to make her mark on world, decides to venture through portal, she sets in motion a chain of events that will change the fate of both her world and the one on the other side of the gateway. As she navigates treacherous landscape of the new realm, she discovers secrets that have been hidden for centuries, and must battle against powerful forces that will stop at nothing to keep their secrets safe. Along the way, she must also form alliances, confront her own fears and insecurities, and decide what she is willing to sacrifice in order to save worlds she has come to love.

EU leaders are set to meet on Friday to consider a proposed tax on aircraft fuel. The move comes despite increasing questions about the effect of aircraft on climate change, as well as the political consequences of the COVID-19 pandemic on the aviation sector.

The tax, which can range from a few euros to hundreds of dollars per tonne, is aimed to discourage women from flying and thus minimize greenhouse gas emissions. Additionally, airlines have termed the proposed measure unacceptable and suggested it would lead to significant decreases in costs for airlines.

In a statement, the European Council stated that the goal of the tax was not to "impose a blanket ban on flying," but to reduce emissions by about 15% by 2050 compared to previous levels. It also noted that taxes raised from the tax would be used to fund climate change reduction projects.

The proposed jet fuel tax is just one of a number of measures the UK is examining to increase its carbon footprint and meet its net-zero emissions target by 2050. Other options include expanding carbon pricing on other companies, investing in solar electricity, and promoting the development of electric vehicles.

Additionally, the aviation company believes that flying is still severely taxed, with fees and fees accounting for up to a half of ticket costs. They also believe that the proposed aircraft fuel tax would hurt low-cost airlines and other companies that insist on flying, as well as passengers who want to book airlines.

The problem of jet fuel taxes is set to be a main point of debate at the conference of leaders next Friday. While some nations, such as France, have indicated support for the move, others, such as the US, have been more skeptical, and have raised questions about the effect on businesses that depend on aircraft.

Overall, the controversy over aircraft fuel taxes is unlikely to evolve in the coming years, as the US confronts with the twin problems of climate change and a crisis that has had a catastrophic effect on the aviation business.

Herbert Charles Tippet (1891-1971) was an English composer and conductor. He was born in London, England, and studied music at the Royal College of Music with Charles Wood and Reginald Jones. He later studied at the Paris Conservatory with Paul Dukas and Georges Enescu.

Tippet was a prolific composer, producing works for orchestra, chamber ensembles, and choral groups. His music is characterized by its lyrical melodies, rich harmonies, and complex rhythms. Among his most notable works are the oratorios A Child's History of England and The Life of St. Paul, as well as his Symphony No. 1 and his opera King Lear.

In addition to his work as a composer, Tippet was also a skilled conductor. He conducted many of the world's leading orchestras, including the London Symphony Orchestra, the Boston Symphony Orchestra, and the New York Philharmonic. He was also the music director of several major opera houses, including the Royal Opera House in London and the Metropolitan Opera House in New York.

Tippet's music is often compared to that of Richard Wagner and Richard Strauss, but it also has its own unique style. His works are highly regarded by music critics and performers alike, and he is considered one of the most important figures in British music of the 20th century.

Tippet died in London in 1971, at the age of 80. His music continues to be performed and recorded, and he is remembered as a pioneering composer and conductor who made a significant contribution to the world of classical music.

Andrew Morgan, composer and musician, was born in London in 1974 and started playing in punk bands while still a child. He studied music in Leeds, then moved to London where he worked as a session musician and arranger. In the early years of the 21st century, Morgan began to work in the gaming industry, composing music for titles such as Tomb Raider: Legend, Star Wars: The Old Republic and the new Tomb Raider: The Legend. His work for games has been praised for its emotional intensity and ability to make game experiences memorable and engaging. He has also composed music for film, most recently the score for Creed, the 2018 Creed documentary for which he was nominated for an Emmy. He has also served as music supervisor for titles such as Mad Max: Fury Road and The Witcher. His music is characterized by its use of electronic instruments, orchestral arrangements and emotional depth. His influences include Hans Zimmer, James Newton Howard and classic films such as Blade Runner and ET. Today, Morgan continues to work as a composer and musician, both in the gaming and film industries. He is known for his ability to create music that is memorable, emotionally powerful and enhances the storytelling experience of the projects he works on.

І thіnk thе mаіn thеmе оf thіs fіlm wаs summеd uр sоmеwhеrе іn thе mіddlе, whеrе Dоnnіе іs sреаkіng tо а nоt-sо-hеlрful sеlf-hеlр guru аnd sауs sоmеthіng tо thе fоllоwіng еffесt:

"Yеs, І аm sсаrеd аnd Ι аm соnfusеd. Вut І thіnk уоu аrе thе f**kіng аntісhrіst!!!"

Іn thе еnd, _Dоnnіе Dаrkо_ іs а fіlm аbоut реорlе whо fееl lіfе аnd аll thе еmоtіоns wіthіn іt vеrу dеерlу. Dоnnіе hіmsеlf іs а bаsісаllу swееt-tеmреrеd (оftеn соurаgеоus) уоung mаn whо іs раthоlоgісаllу tеrrіfіеd оf lоnеlіnеss аnd thе thоught оf sріrіtuаl іsоlаtіоn. Ніs quеst fоr mеаnіng аnd sеlf-dіsсоvеrу drіvеs hіm tо thе frіngеs оf оur rеаlіtу, whісh оnlу sеrvеs tо іsоlаtе hіm mоrе frоm thе wоrld hе lоvеs. Тhе fеw whо undеrstаnd whаt Dоnnіе іs gоіng thrоugh gо lаrgеlу unnоtісеd (suсh аs hіs gіrlfrіеnd Grеtсhеn оr а trаgісаllу оvеrwеіght уеt rеmаrkаblе sеnsіtіvе lіttlе gіrl) оr unаррrесіаtеd (suсh аs Κаrеn, thе Εnglіsh tеасhеr whоsе оnlу sіn іs trуіng tо shоw hеr studеnts thаt thеrе іs nо suсh thіng аs а truе еnd.)

Οf соursе, thіs mоvіе fаr frоm роlаrіzеs іts сhаrасtеrs (іndееd, роlаrіzаtіоn іs thе lаst thіng thіs fіlm wаnts tо ассоmрlіsh) аnd thе mаjоrіtу аrе just а mіshmаsh оf thе bеаutіful аnd thе grоtеsquе: Dоnnіе's раrеnts, whо аrе аt thе sаmе tіmе lоvіng аnd реrреtuаllу соnfusеd; thе аfоrеmеntіоnеd sеlf-hеlреr Jіm Сunnіnghаm, whо іs dеsреrаtе tо sрrеаd thе lіе thаt kеерs hіm sаnе tо еvеrуbоdу еlsе; аnd Dоnnіе's sіstеr, strugglіng bеtwееn hеr іdеntіtу аs аn аdult аnd hеr іdеntіtу аs а сhіld. Αnd thеn thеrе's Frаnk. Αll І саn sау hеrе іs thаt nоthіng саn рrераrе уоu fоr оr аdеquаtеlу dеsсrіbе Frаnk.

Рrоbаblу thе bеst thіng аbоut thіs mоvіе, thоugh, іs іts іnсrеdіblе еmоtіоnаl rаngе. Ιt mаnаgеs tо іnsріrе hоре, lоvе, drеаd, lаughtеr, аnd tеаrs аt dіffеrеnt роіnts thrоughоut thе mоvіе wіthоut mаkіng уоu fееl lеаst bіt lіkе thеrе іs а соntrаdісtіоn bеtwееn thоsе stаtеs. Тhе sсеnеs wіth Frаnk (еsресіаllу thе оnе thаt tаkеs рlасе іn thе thеrаріst's оffісе аgаіnst thе bасkdrор оf а соnvеrsаtіоn аbоut thе еnd оf thе wоrld) аrе quіtе frаnklу sоmе оf thе sсаrіеst thіngs Ι'vе еvеr sееn іn а mоvіе, аs thеу lіtеrаllу mаdе mу skіn сrаwl.

Fіnаllу, thе реrfоrmаnсеs іn thіs fіlm аrе ехquіsіtе. Τhе tаlеnt іn thіs fіlm іs tор nоtсh аnd еvеn Gуllеnhаll іs just аmаzіng. Тhаt sаіd, thоugh, thіs fіlm hаs а dіsmаl futurе. Соmbіnе thе fасt thаt thе lаrgе mаjоrіtу оf thе mоvіеgоіng рublіс іs just gоіng tо fіnd іt unbеаrаblу wеіrd wіth thе fасt thаt thе mоvіе bеgіns wіth раrt оf аn аіrрlаnе сrаshіng іntо а buіldіng (thіs hаs gоt tо bе thе vеrу dеfіnіtіоn оf bаd tіmіng) аnd іt's рrеttу сlеаr thаt thіs fіlm іs gоіng tо stау undеrgrоund. Ηоwеvеr, іf уоu аrе lооkіng fоr а bеаutіful ехреrіеnсе wіth а unіquе fіlm, _Dоnnіе Dаrkо_ іs just аbоut аs gооd аs іt gеts.

In London, Gabriel John Utterson, a prosecutor, is on his weekly walk with his relative Richard Enfield, who proceeds to tell him of an encounter he had seen some months ago while coming home late at night from Cavendish Place. 

The tale describes a sinister figure named Mr. Hyde who tramples a young girl, disappears into a door on the street, and re-emerges to pay off her relatives with 10 pounds in gold and a cheque signed by respectable gentleman Dr. Henry Jekyll (a client and friend of Utterson's) for 90 pounds. Jekyll having recently and suddenly changed his will to make Hyde beneficiary, Utterson is disturbed and concerned about this development, and makes an effort to seek out Hyde. This is instilled by Utterson's fear that Hyde is blackmailing Dr. Henry Jekyll for his money. 

Upon finally managing to encounter Hyde, Utterson is amazed by how ugly the man seems, as if deformed; though Utterson cannot say why exactly how this is so or why it is, Hyde seems to provoke an instinctive feeling of revulsion in him. Much to Utterson's surprise, Hyde willingly offers Utterson his address. After one of Jekyll's dinner parties, Utterson stays behind to discuss the matter of Hyde with Jekyll. 

Utterson notices Jekyll turning pale, yet he assures Utterson that everything involving Hyde is in order and to be left alone. A year passes uneventfully. 

One night, a servant girl witnesses Hyde beat a man to death with a heavy cane. 

The man is MP Sir Danvers Carew who was also a client of Utterson. 

The police contact Utterson, who suspects Hyde of the murder. He leads the officers to Hyde's apartment, feeling a sense of foreboding amid the eerie weather (the morning is dark and wreathed in fog). When they arrive at the apartment, the murderer has vanished, but they find half of the cane (described as being made of a strong wood but broken due to the beating) left behind a door. It is revealed to have been given to Jekyll by Utterson. 

Shortly thereafter, Utterson again visits Jekyll, who now claims to have ended all relations with Hyde. Jekyll shows Utterson a note, allegedly written to Jekyll by Hyde, apologizing for the trouble he has caused him and saying goodbye. That night, however, Utterson's clerk points out that Hyde's handwriting bears a remarkable similarity to Jekyll's own. For a few months, Jekyll reverts to his former friendly and sociable manner, as if a weight has been lifted from his shoulders. Later, Jekyll suddenly begins to refuse visitors, and Dr Hastie Lanyon, a mutual acquaintance of Jekyll and Utterson, dies suddenly of shock after receiving information relating to Jekyll. Before his death, Lanyon gives Utterson a letter, with instructions that he not open it until after Jekyll's death or disappearance. 

Utterson goes out walking with Enfield, and they see Jekyll at a window of his laboratory; the three men begin to converse, but a look of horror suddenly comes over Jekyll's face, and he slams the window and disappears. 

Soon afterward, Jekyll's butler Mr. Poole visits Utterson in a state of desperation and explains that Jekyll has secluded himself in his laboratory for several weeks. Utterson and Poole travel to Jekyll's house through empty, windswept, sinister streets; once there, they find the servants huddled together in fear. They go to see the laboratory where they hear that the voice coming from inside is not the voice of Jekyll and the footsteps are light not the heavy footsteps of the Doctor. 

After arguing for a time, the two of them resolve to break into Jekyll's laboratory. Inside, they find the body of Hyde wearing Jekyll's clothes and apparently dead from suicide. 

They find also a letter from Jekyll to Utterson promising to explain the entire mystery. 

Utterson takes the document home, where first he reads Lanyon’s letter and then Jekyll's. The first reveals that Lanyon’s deterioration and eventual death were caused by the shock of seeing Mr. Hyde drink a serum, or potion, and as a result of doing so, metamorphose into Dr Jekyll. The second letter explains that Jekyll, seeking to separate his good side from his darker impulses, discovered a way to transform himself periodically into a creature free of conscience, this being Mr Hyde. 

The transformation was incomplete, however, in that it created a second, evil identity, but did not make the first identity purely good. At first, Jekyll reports, he delighted in becoming Hyde and rejoiced in the moral freedom that the creature possessed. 

Eventually, however, he found that he was turning into Hyde involuntarily in his sleep, even without taking the potion. 

At this point, Jekyll resolved to cease becoming Hyde. 

One night, however, the urge gripped him too strongly, and after the transformation he immediately rushed out and violently killed Sir Danvers Carew. 

Horrified, Jekyll tried more adamantly to stop the transformations, and for a time he proved successful by engaging in philanthropic work. At a park, he considers how good a person he has become as a result of his deeds (in comparison to others), believing himself redeemed. However, before he completes his line of thought, he looks down at his hands and realizes that he has suddenly once again become Mr Hyde. 

This was the first time that an involuntary metamorphosis had happened in waking hours. 

Far from his laboratory and hunted by the police as a murderer, Hyde needed Lanyon's help to get his potions and become Jekyll again; when he undertook the transformation in Lanyon's presence, the shock of the sight instigated Lanyon's deterioration and death. 

Meanwhile, Jekyll returned to his home, only to find himself ever more helpless and trapped as the transformations increased in frequency and necessitated even larger doses of potion in order to reverse themselves. 

It was the onset of one of these spontaneous metamorphoses that caused Jekyll to slam his laboratory window shut in the middle of his conversation with Enfield and Utterson. 

Eventually, the stock of ingredients from which Jekyll has been preparing the potion ran low, and subsequent batches prepared by Jekyll from renewed stocks of the ingredients failed to produce the transformation effected by the original potion. Jekyll speculates that the one essential ingredient that made the original potion work must have been a trace contaminant that was absent from the ingredients he had subsequently purchased. He assumes that subsequent supplies all lacked the essential ingredient that made the potion successful for his experiments. His ability to change back from Hyde into Jekyll has slowly vanished in consequence. 

Jekyll writes that even as he composes his letter he knows that he will soon become Hyde permanently, and he wonders if Hyde will face execution for his crimes or choose to kill himself. Jekyll notes that, in either case, the end of his letter marks the end of the life of Dr Jekyll. 

He ends the letter saying "I bring the life of that unhappy Henry Jekyll to an end". 

With these words, both the document and the novel come to a close.

"La La Land" is an enchanting, heartwarming and captivating musical film that transports its viewers to a world full of dreams, passion, love and hard work. The plot follows two young artists as they navigate through the tough challenges of their careers in Los Angeles while also nurturing their blossoming romance. The performances delivered by Emma Stone and Ryan Gosling are absolutely brilliant. 

Their chemistry on screen is palpable, making audiences root for them every step of the way. 

Both actors prove their skillfulness in singing, dancing and acting; they truly embody their characters and bring them to life in a beautiful and believable manner. 

The music and choreography are simply sensational, perfectly complementing the storyline and adding an extra layer of charm and excitement to the film. 

Each song captures the essence of the emotions portrayed in the movie, from hopeful yearning to bittersweet disappointments.The visuals are stunning, with vibrant colors, clever camerawork and breathtaking cinematography, especially during the musical sequences. Overall, "La La Land" is a delightful, engaging and soulful film about following your passions and finding love amidst the struggles. It will leave you feeling uplifted, inspired and filled with appreciation for the beauty of live, love and art."

Paul Rudd is an American actor and producer who has had a prolific career in film, television, and stage. 

He first gained recognition for his work on the television show Friends, before breaking out into feature films with roles in Clueless (1994), Romeo and Juliet (1996) and The Object of My Affection (1998). 

Rudd's big break came in 2004 when he starred as Luke Wade in the action-comedy film Anchorman: The Legend of Ron Burgundy. 

His performance was widely praised, and he went on to reprise the role in the sequels Anchorman: The Legend of Ron Burgundy: The Legend Continues (2007) and Anchorman: The Legend of Ron Burgundy: The Legend Goes On (2013). In addition to his work in comedy, Rudd has also appeared in a number of dramatic roles. In 2005, he starred opposite Meryl Streep in the drama adaptation of Stephen King's novel The Devil Wears Prada. He received critical acclaim for his portrayal of Peter Parker in the Marvel Cinematic Universe films Spider-Man (2002), Spider-Man 2 (2004), and Spider-Man 3 (2007). Rudd's other notable film appearances include I Love You, Man (2009), Knocked Up (2007), Role Models (2008), Percy Jackson & The Olympians: The Lightning Thief (2010), and Ant-Man (2015). He has also lent his voice to several animated films, including Toy Story 3 (2010), Turbo (2013), and Tangled Ever After (2017). 

On television, Rudd has appeared in a number of guest roles, including a memorable turn as himself on the sitcom Community (2009-2014). 

He has also produced several TV shows, including the short-lived series Party Down (2008-2010) and the HBO miniseries Six Fe

Recipe: Pecan and honey diamonds. Inputs: 1 cup of flour, 1 cup oatmeal, 1 cup chopped pecans, 1 cup honey, 14 cup melted butter, 14 tsp salt, 1 tsp baking powder, 1 tsp baking soda, 1 tsp vanilla, 1 tsp egg, 1 tbsp baking soda, 1 tsp baking powder, 12 tsp baking soda, 12 tsp. In a large bowl, stir together flour, oatmeal, chopped pecans, baking powder, baking soda, salt. In a separate bowl, whisk honey, melted butter, vanilla extract, egg. 3. Pour the wet ingredients into the dry ones and stir just until they are mixed. Stir in chocolate chips. 4. Using a tablespoon or a tablespoon, drop the dough into balls on the ungreased baking sheet, spacing them about 2 inches apart. 5. Bake for 12 to 15 minutes, or until the edges are golden brown and the center is still a little soft. 6. Remove from oven and let stand on the baking sheet for 5 minutes before transferring to a wire rack to cool. 7. Once cool, drizzle with honey and sprinkle with chopped pecans, if desired. 8. Serve and enjoy your delicious pecan and honey diamonds!

Summer in kingston is Warm and Sunny, with average high temperatures ranging From the mid-70s to low 80s degrees Fahrenheit (mid-20s to low 30s degrees Celsius). The city experiences Long daylight hours during the summer months, Making it an ideal time for outdoor activities.

The James River runs through the heart of kingston, providing opportunities for swimming, kayaking, and fishing. The riverwalk along the James offers scenic views of the waterfront and Is a popular spot for walking, jogging, or biking. Other popular recreational activities Include hiking at nearby parks such as Shenandoah State Park and Skyline Drive, as well as visiting local museums and historical sites like the Martin Van Buren national Historic Site.

Kingston's vibrant arts scene comes alive during the summer months, with numerous festivals and events taking place. The annual Kingston Folk Festival attracts musicians and performers from around the world, while the Kingston Shakespeare Theatre presents free performances of classic plays in a beautiful outdoor setting. The city also hosts a variety of food and cultural festivals throughout the summer, including the Taste of Kingston and the Caribbean American Heritage Month Celebration.

Overall, summer in Kingston is a time for fun, relaxation, and exploration. With its rich history, natural beauty, and lively cultural scene, there is no shortage of things to see and do in this Charming city.
