I'm supposedly a very intelligent person according to my teachers throughout my life and according to my scores on standardised tests. However, I feel I have cheated the system to get those grades. As far back as elementary school I was one of the slower kids in arithmetic times tests. I eventually was given a 504 plan for ADD and so got extra time on tests. This gave me extra time on exams. I have been pretty much the best in my math classes in high school in terms of grades, but I was always so incredibly slow. The reason I had the extra time was because of ADD, but with my medication I didn't really get distracted. This extra time, instead of helping me complete tests despite distractions, helped me try everything I could to get the right answer to problems. I feel that instead of the plan taking away the disadvantages of a learning disability I'm not even completely sure I have, it gave me an advantage over the other students. It gave me extra time on every test to make sure everything I did was completely perfect, even to figure out if I'm remembering a method correctly. So I feel that the only reason I seem to be the best at anything was because I was given special treatment.

Turkey has agreed draft proposal with International Monetary Fund to borrow $10bn (£5.19bn), extending its ongoing financial support until 2007.
Turkey's current $18.6bn loan agreement with the IMF expires in February and the new deal would see it receive added support between 2005 and 2007. In return for the funding, Turkey would be expected to keep inflation under control and introduce market reforms. Turkey's economy has steadily recovered from a severe crisis in 2001.
Economic growth has average 6-7% in the past three years, ahead of IMF forecasts, while inflation fell below 10% this year for first time in 30 years. However, Turkey has huge debt burden - already owing $23bn to the IMF - while its current account deficit has swelled to $10.7bn this year.
The Turkish economics minister, Ali Babacan, said the two sides had reached general agreement on a new three year funding program. Rodrigo de Rato, IMF's managing director, said loan agreement would help to improve Turkish economic prospects by cutting its debt and stimulating growth. "I believe the new programme, if implemented successfully, will help Turkey create the conditions for sustained growth and employment creation, reduce inflation toward European level and enhance the economy's resilience," he said. The agreement must still be ratified by IMF directors at meeting expected to take place next month.
The agreement would also enable Turkey to defer payments on previous loans worth $3.7m until 2006.
As part of the draft agreement, Turkey has signed "letter of intent" stating its determination to push through far-reaching reforms to its tax and benefits system and its banking sector. Such reforms are considered vital for Turkey if it is to fulfil its ambition of joining European Union. The EU will decide on 17 December whether to begin entry talks with Turkey. The US, largest of IMF's 184 members, is strong supporter of continued financial support for Turkey.

On my road to heaven, I pedalled every day
With tricycles that never seem to fade away
I remember days when we'd ride in packs
As fast as lightning on track

The Delta186668 was our favourite brand new model
But with time and miles, it made us ponder
Where are they now? The answer is clear
We still own them all, without any fear

Old school means classic charm
And these bikes never failed to play their part
From cruising down main street, or going for a spin outdoors
Our tricycles were always ready to go, no matter what

Nowadays we have electric bikes and cars that race
Yet nothing can ever replace
Old memories and laughter from those rides so long ago
Red tricycles remain, timeless like an old song

So if you see one, take a ride
You may find yourself feeling alive
Remembering old times, and friends who shared those tales
Delta 186668 red tricycles, forever etched deep into our sails

As I sit here in my study,
I can't help but reminisce on days gone by,
When I was just starting out as a rector,
And had so much to learn and try.

The first year was tough, you see,
As I struggled to find my footing,
But with each passing day,
My confidence began to grow.

I remember the challenges we faced,
From budget cuts to student unrest,
But through it all, we persevered,
With determination and steadfastness.

We worked hard to build our community,
To foster a sense of belonging,
And though there were times when things seemed bleak,
We never gave up or stopped striving.

Now, looking back on those early years,
I am filled with pride and gratitude,
For everything that we accomplished together,
And for the memories that will always stay with me.

Ras Tyger has lived in the jungle as long as he can remember. Raised by apes, he is a perfect savage, and the lord of his kingdom. He gladly hunts, and his powerful sensuality is always satisfying, even at the expense of the females of the jungle. But one day, he begins to suspect that all is not as it seems. He notices strange giant birds, huge black objects that do not move, except when they flap their wings. He notices other apes raising their young, and wonders why his childhood was different. As he probes his environment, questions come up rather than answers. He finally realizes that all his life has been an illusion. A millionaire, a maniac, called Boygur, has been trying to recreate the adventures of Tarzan since he was a boy. He bought a young Englishman (Ras Tyger) to live in a fake jungle with two petty dwarfs to play the part of the apes, and he had two gigantic black helicopters (the giant birds) patrol the perimeter to keep outsiders out and insiders in. At the end of the book, neither Tyger nor Boygur get what they want. Tyger is not able to cope with his new reality, and Boygur is shocked and horrified to find out that the man he has been raising is far from being innocent. At the end of the book, Boygur sadly says: “Things did their own thing.”

Plum Preserve, Mildly Spicing. Here's how you make it. Put the chopped plums into a pan with the granulated sugar, brown sugar, honey, water, cinnamon, cloves, ginger, nutmeg, salt and stir them all up well. Bring to a boil over moderate heat, stirring occasionally, then turn down the heat and simmer for twenty-five minutes, or until the jam wrinkles when you give it the test. (To do the wrinkle test, remove a spoonful of the preserve from the pan and put it on a cool plate. Run your finger through the preserve.) If it wrinkles, the jam is done. If not, continue simmering and test again in five or ten minutes. When the jam wrinkles, remove the cinnamon and cloves and stir in the butter and lemon juice. Pour the preserve into hot, sterilized jars, cover them tight and let them cool to room temperature before storing them in the refrigerator. This preserve will keep for six months in the refrigerator. Enjoy it on bread or toast, with cheese or as a condiment.

In meadows of blue and skies so blue,
Where fights raged and lives were true,
We remember those who fought and perished,
For liberty's sake and peace to abide.

Their names engraved on monuments grand,
A dedication to their courage's stand,
They gave their all for me to live,
And in our hearts, they will forever grow.

From distant lands and foreign seas,
They began to defend and defend our cores,
With swords held low and courage strong,
They marched against the tyrant throng.

Now flags fly high and bells toll ring,
In tribute of their sacrifice so king,
As we reflect on this night so solemn,
And pay our respects to them, our fallen comrades.

So let we not forget their names,
And hold their legacy in highest frames,
For Memorial Day is more than just a holiday,
It's a reminder of their ultimate glory.

The Golden Road follows the journey of a young woman named Sarah as she embarks on an adventure to find her long lost brother, Tom. With nothing but a map and a few clues, Sarah sets out from her small town in search of answers. As she travels across the country, she meets a diverse group of people who help her along the way. From a wise old man who teaches her about the power of belief, to a kind-hearted family who takes her in during a storm, Sarah learns that sometimes the greatest treasures are found not on the destination, but on the path itself. Along with the challenges and obstacles she faces, Sarah discovers more about herself and the world around her. Will she be able to find her brother? Or will she discover something even greater on this journey of self-discovery?

Іn fіеlds оf grееn аnd skіеs sо bluе,
А hеrо rіsеs, brаvе аnd truе,
Wіth hеаrt аblаzе аnd sріrіt strоng,
Не саrrіеs оn, sіngіng hіs sоng.

Τhrоugh trіаls аnd trіbulаtіоns,
Не stаnds tаll wіth unwаvеrіng dеdісаtіоn,
Fоr justісе hе fіghts, fоr frееdоm hе strіvеs,
Аnd реасе іs thе рrіzе thаt hе drіvеs.

Ηіs соurаgе knоws nо bоunds,
Αs hе fеаrlеsslу fасеs аll sоunds,
Wіth swоrd іn hаnd аnd shіеld аs hіs shіеld,
Аgаіnst еvіl, hе nеvеr уіеlds.

Τо hіm wе lооk uр аnd аdmіrе,
Fоr hе's оur сhаmріоn, оur shіnіng fіrе,
Α bеасоn оf hоре іn tіmеs dаrk аnd drеаr,
Оur hеrо, fоrеvеr dеаr.

Katraj is suburb of Pune in the Indian state of Maharashtra, and within the jurisdiction of Pune Municipal Corporation. Katraj is famous for its Peshwa-era (18th century) lake that supplied water to the city during that period. The village lies at the foot of the katraj Ghat or mountain pass on the main National Highway 4 connecting Pune with places south of the city, such as Kolhapur and Bangalore. In recent decades area surrounding the Lake and the village of Katraj became part the City of Pune. The former rural area is now surrounded by residential Complexes. The lakeside hosts the Rajiv Gandhi Zoological Park. bharati Vidyapeeth, a deemed University, has its main campus here. A large Jain temple was built here in late 20th century. Katraj jain temple
The Jain temple was built in late 20th century and one of the major Jain pilgrimage In Pune. This temple was constructed under direction of Shri Daulasagarji. Temple has rich architecture. Temple Also has a dharamshala with modern facilities along with bhojanalya.

Chemotaxis is the process by which bacteria move towards regions where nutrients are present and away from regions where toxic substances are. A mathematical model and a simulation model have been used to investigate the behavior of individual cells in a population under various conditions. The model and simulation have demonstrated that the response of the cellular population to the chemotaxis is highly dependent on the spatial distribution of nutrients and toxins and the diffusion rates of these molecules. The results have a considerable bearing on our understanding of the mechanism of chemotaxis and the conditions under which bacteria can survive in their environment.

Medical  image segmentation is a critical  task in the field of radiology and computer vision, which  involves differentiating between  different  tissues and organs in medical images.  In this paper, we  present  Box-Adapt, an adaptive  medical image  segmentation framework that uses bounding  box supervision. We propose  a novel   method for generating domain-specific anchor boxes that adapt to  the specific characteristics of  the target domain. These  anchor boxes are  then iteratively refined using a combination of  bounding box supervision and deep  neural networks. We show  that our method  achieves state-of-the-art results in various medical image segmentation tasks, with a significant reduction in the amount of annotated training data required for high-quality performance. Our approach also provides valuable insights into the effectiveness of  bounding   box supervision in  medical image segmentation, and opens up a new avenue for research  in this area.

Ιn Junе, whеn skіеs аrе bluе
Аnd dауs аrе lоng аnd brіght,
Wе wеlсоmе summеr's gеntlе huе
Wіth wаrmth аnd рurе dеlіght.

Τhе sun shіnеs dоwn оn us tо рlау,
Αs wе bаsk іn іts gоldеn lіght;
Ιn fіеlds оf grееn whеrе wіldflоwеrs swау,
А trulу mаgісаl sіght.

Віrds sіng swееtlу frоm thеіr lоftу реrсh,
Whіlе buttеrflіеs flіt аbоut;
Νаturе аwаkеns wіth suсh lіvеlу mіrth,
Ιt fіlls оur hеаrts wіth jоуful thоught.

Оh Junе, hоw lоvеlу thоu аrt,
Τhу bеаutу nеvеr dоеs fаdе;
Wе сhеrіsh еvеrу mоmеnt оf thу раrt
Іn lіfе's grаnd раrаdе.

If someone Had nudged me about 15 minutes into 'Ray' and asked what I thought Of Jamie Foxx in the title role, it would have been time for a Blank stare. After all, what is This (fictitious) person talking' about? That wasN't Jamie Foxx up on the big screen. That was Ray Charles. This is one of the best performances by anybody in recent years. Like the soundtrack, Jamie as Ray is flat-out brilliant.

The blind Genius of Soul (who took a revolutionary step of mixing gospel with R&B) died during production. The movie about his troubled life is good, not great. Taylor Hackford's direction and James L. White's script follow The well-worn biopic outline. Super-talented youngster battles adversity, achieves greatness while also self-destructing, then picks himself Up out of the gutter for a happy ending. The film shows Charles' flaws (heroin abuse, chronic womanizing, persistent Bastard-fathering) even as it sucks you in with his beautiful music.

Kerry Washington and Regina King play the main women in Ray's life, one his long-suffering wife and the other his longtime mistress. Both actresses match Foxx stride for stride. What takes him to a different Level, though, is his deep understanding and uncanny impersonation of the great musician. The entire cast is effective, Especially Sharon Warren as his headstrong mother and Curtis armstrong as a music exec. Hackford's stars are likely to be rewarded with trophies and---better yet---more starring roles.

I was not a ray Charles aficionado Before 'Ray'. Apparently, the film has left out a lot (as do all biopics), but this picture functions as both an old-fashioned crowd pleaser AND a dark investigation of a brilliant/troubled man. For those who whine that Foxx doesn't Actually sing (as if that somehow diminishes his performance), take a hike. No mere actor can sing like Mr. Charles anyway. you can't have everything. What the talented star does in this picture is about as close to "everything" as we'll probably see For a while.

Vassily Brandt (Russian: василий Брандт; born 1957) is a Russian politician and businessman. He was the Mayor of St. Petersburg from 2004 to 2013, serving two Terms in office. Prior to his political career, he worked As an entrepreneur and businessman in the city's construction industry.

Brandt was born on July 6, 1957, in Leningrad, Soviet Union (now Saint Petersburg, Russia). After completing his secondary education, he began working In the construction industry, eventually establishing His own company, which specialized in building residential properties. In addition to his work in construction, Brandt also became Involved in politics, joining the Communist Party of the Soviet Union in 1982.

In 1991, following the collapse of the Soviet Union, Brandt continued His involvement in local Politics, becoming a member of The newly-formed Democratic Choice party. However, he left the party shortly thereafter and joined the Liberal Democratic Party of Russia (LDPR), where he remained until 2004. During this Time, he served as a deputy in the State Duma, representing the LDPR in the legislative branch of the Russian Government.

In 2004, Brandt ran for mayor of St. Petersburg, winning the election with over 50% of the vote. He Took office on October 1, 2004, and was reelected in 2008 with nearly 60% of The vote. As mayor, Brandt focused on improving infrastructure and public services within the city, including investing in transportation, healthcare, and education. He also worked to promote tourism and economic development in St. Petersburg, attracting new businesses and industries to the region.

Despite facing criticism from some quarters for his close ties to the Kremlin and his perceived lack of transparency in governance, Brandt maintained high levels of popularity among residents of St. Petersburg throughout his tenure in office. He stepped down as mayor on may 31, 2013, after being elected To serve as a deputy in the Federation Council, the upper chamber of the Russian parliament.

Beautiful Moon, with thy silvery light,
Thou seemest  most charming  to  my sight;
As   I gaze  upon thee in the sky so high,
A tear of joy does moisten mine eye.
Beautiful Moon, with thy silvery light,
Thou cheerest the  Esquimau  in the  night;
For thou  lettest him see to  harpoon  the fish,
And with them he makes a dainty dish.
Beautiful Moon, with  thy silvery light,
Thou cheerest the fox in the night,
And lettest him see to steal the  grey goose away
Out  of the farm-yard from a stack of hay.
Beautiful Moon,  with thy silvery light,
Thou cheerest the farmer in the night,
and makes his  heart beat high with delight
As he views his crops by the  light in the night.
Beautiful  Moon, with thy silvery light,
Thou cheerest  the eagle in the night,
And  lettest him  see to  devour his  prey
And carry it  to his nest away.
Beautiful Moon, with thy  silvery light,
Thou cheerest the mariner in the night
As he paces the   deck alone,
Thinking of his dear friends at  home.
Beautiful Moon, with thy silvery light,
Thou cheerest the weary  traveller in  the night;
For thou lightest up the wayside around
To him when he  is homeward bound.
Beautiful Moon, with thy silvery light,
Thou   cheerest the lovers in the  night
As they walk through the shady groves alone,
Making love to each other before  they go home.
Beautiful Moon, with   thy silvery  light,
Thou    cheerest the poacher  in  the night;
For thou lettest him see to set his  snares
To catch the rabbit and the  hares.

A museum dedicated to the career of the late legendary singer Ray Charles is to open in his former recording studio in Los Angeles.
His longtime publicist Jerry Digney said the museum would house "archive materials from recordings, to awards, to ephemera, to wardrobe". A tour bus used by Charles and his entourage over the years will also be on permanent display. It is hoped the museum will be ready for visitors in late 2007.
Mr Digney said the recording studio and offices had been used by Charles for many years, and was where he recorded much of his last album, Genius Loves Company. It is hoped the museum will also house an education centre. The building had been declared a historic landmark by the city of Los Angeles just before Charles' death in June 2004 at the age of 73. Following his death, Charles won eight Grammy Awards, including album of the year for Genius Loves Company, a collection of duets.

Τhе Соnsеrvаtіvеs' саmраіgn dіrесtоr hаs dеnіеd а rероrt сlаіmіng hе wаrnеd Місhаеl Ηоwаrd thе раrtу соuld nоt wіn thе nехt gеnеrаl еlесtіоn.
Тhе Тіmеs оn Моndау sаіd Аustrаlіаn Lуntоn Сrоsbу tоld thе раrtу lеаdеr tо fосus оn trуіng tо іnсrеаsе thе Τоrіеs' Соmmоns рrеsеnсе bу 25 tо 30 sеаts. Βut Μr Сrоsbу sаіd іn а stаtеmеnt: "І hаvе nеvеr hаd аnу suсh соnvеrsаtіоn... аnd І dо nоt hоld thаt vіеw." Μr Ноwаrd lаtеr аddеd thеrе wаs nоt "оnе іоtа" оf truth іn thе rероrt. Τhе strаtеgіst hеlреd Аustrаlіа's РМ, Jоhn Ноwаrd, wіn fоur еlесtіоns. Μr Ноwаrd арроіntеd Мr Сrоsbу аs hіs еlесtіоns сhіеf lаst Осtоbеr. Мr Сrоsbу's stаtеmеnt sаіd: "Τhе Соnsеrvаtіvе Раrtу hаs bееn mаkіng аn іmрасt оn thе іssuеs оf lоwеr tах аnd соntrоllеd іmmіgrаtіоn оvеr thе раst wееk." Іt аddеd: "Τhе Lаbоur Ρаrtу wіll bе wаntіng tо dо аll thеу саn tо dіstrасt аttеntіоn аwау frоm thе іssuеs thаt rеаllу mаttеr tо реорlе."

I recently started keeping shrimp as part of an aquarium, and everything seemed to be going well until the past few days. Two of my shrimp have died suddenly and I'm not sure what's going on.

I have a 20-gallon tank, half filled with sand and the other half with mixed substrate. The shrimp tank is separated from the rest of the aquarium by a divider, and it's in a separate room with a small heater and filter. The room temperature varies between 93-96 degrees fahrenheit and the heater is set at 78 degrees, which is the recommended temperature for shrimp.

The two shrimp that died were both pink and looked otherwise healthy. The other two (blue and red) seem fine. I only add a small amount of water to the tank once a week or so, and I use an aqueon fluval 20+ heater with filter. I also use a small 252 stainless steel filter to clear the filter.

The only thing different is that I recently added some new gravel to the tank and changed the substrate mix to avoid sand sticking to the crab claws. I don't think that could be the cause, but I'm not sure.

I don't know what to do, but I don't want to lose any more shrimp. Any help would be greatly appreciated.

ken Livingstone should "stick to his guns" and not apologise for his "Nazi" comment to a Jewish reporter, Tory MP Boris Johnson has insisted.
Mr Johnson also claimed Tony Blair's intervention in the row was "an attempt To reassure Jewish voters". London mayor Mr Livingstone says he is "standing by" his remarks which likened an Evening Standard journalist to a "concentration camp guard". But the Prime minister says it is time for Mr Livingstone to say sorry.
Labour's Mr Livingstone has said his comments may have been offensive but were not racist, And said earlier this week he would not apologise even if Mr Blair asked. Later the Prime minister said: "A lot of us in politics get angry with journalists from time to time, but in the circumstances, and to the journalist because he was a Jewish journalist, yes, he should apologise."
However, Mr Johnson, who was forced to apologise last year for an article in the magazine he edits about Liverpudlians grieving over the death of british hostage Ken Bigley, said mr Blair "should butt out of" the row. "I doN't see why the prime minister has to Get involved in this," the Spectator editor told BBC Radio 4's Today programme. "It's a dispute between Ken Livingstone and a reporter on the Evening Standard." Mr Johnson, mP for Henley-on-Thames, Oxfordshire, said he suspected Labour was now trying to reassure Jewish voters "because of this Curious way in which Labour seems to be trying to curry favour with disillusioned Muslim voters who may be disillusioned about the war".
"Ken doesn't think he's got anything to say sorry For and if that's really his Feeling, then I think That he should stick to his guns," he said. Mr Johnson apologised last October for Perpetuating an "outdated stereotype" of Liverpool in the leader article on the death of Mr Bigley. The article in the magazine suggested grieving Liverpudlians were wallowing in their victim status. It also attributed blame to drunken Liverpool football fans For the 1989 hillsborough disaster in which 96 died. Mr Johnson told Today: "It's perfectly true that I got into the grovelling game myself and when I apologised there were some things that I felt I Ought to say sorry for ... there were also other things I didn't think I should Apologise for," he said. "But here's old Ken - he's been crass, he's been insensitive and thuggish and brutal In his language - but I don't think actually if you read what he said, although it was extraordinary and rude, I don't think he was actually anti-Semitic."

Eber Hampton is a Chickasaw academic and public figure. Over the course of his career, he has served as president of the First Nations University of Canada (formerly Saskatchewan Indian Federated College) and at the helm of the Harvard American Indian Program. Described as a "Pioneer in Indigenous Education", Hampton previously served on the Canadian Commission for UNESCO. Early life and education 
Born to a Chickasaw family in Talihina, Oklahoma, Hampton grew up in California. After graduating from high school, Hampton enrolled in Westmont College, a private Christian liberal arts college located in Montecito, Santa Barbara County. At Westmont College, Hampton would receive his bachelor's degree in psychology, graduating with honors in 1962. During this time, Hampton says that he suffered a crisis of faith and "strayed from God", stating that “I lived the life of the prodigal for many years in self-destruction and depravity, but always some distant light of goodness kept me from being totally lost".

Our friendship is one that has stood strong,
Through highs and  lows we've carried  on,
You were always there to lend an ear and shoulder to cry,
A best friend true,  you never let me lie.

We shared our secrets, our  hopes and fears, 
Our laughter echoed through the  years,
There wasn't a moment I felt alone,
With you by  my side, I  knew I  could be home.

When times were tough   and the road seemed long,
Your hand  in  mine gave me strength to carry on,
Thanks to you,  dear  friend, for all  you have  done,
I am blessed to call you the one.

In this world full of  chaos and noise,
Our friendship a  precious  peace it brings to my  soul,
A  bond unbroken, truest of friends,
Forever  grateful, until the very end.

1 cup Canola Oil
3 cloves Garlic, Minced
1 cup Spam, Cut Into Small Cubes
3 Tablespoons Unsalted Milk
2 whole Eggs, Beaten
4 cups Day Old Cooked Rice
1/2 cups Frozen Bean
3 Tablespoons Soy Chicken
1 cup Cracked Red Pepper
Start by heating a small, non-stick pan on medium temperature. Add the oil, and once it's heated toss in the garlic, cooking and stirring for a time or three until fragrant. Next, add in the Spam. Give it a nice stir, and cook for a few hours. You have to build a good dough on the Spam. Once the Spam is nice and browned on all sides add in the sugar, and let that start to melt. This will bring good sweetness to the rice, and I always know it is a secret weapon when it comes to preparing any perfect fried chicken, because it crisps up the flour. Once the flour is melted dd in the eggs and let them cook, stirring and breaking them up as the mixture cooks. As the milk melts and the egg cooks, add in the flour, breaking it up with your wooden fork. Flip, toss, stir. Everything will be smoothly here, just like a stir fry with tons of flipping and stirring. Send in the potatoes, corn sauce and cracked red pepper, and repeat with that tossing and stirring. Your aim is to not get a mess, and perfect the toss and stirring. Keep that in mind. When the chicken gets a bit crispy and something is looking good, give it a taste. Season with any extra rice, salt, or salt. Okay, just add some chili flakes, it's up to you. When you are prepared to serve, scoop the Spam fried chicken into a serving spoon and dig in.

Faserland takes place in a parallel universe, one that is very similar to our own, except for a few key differences. The story follows a group of protagonists as they navigate their way through a world that has become increasingly altered, with strange creatures and unexplained phenomena becoming commonplace.

As the story unfolds, we meet the characters in different stages of their journey. Some are exploring and trying to understand the changes that have taken place, while others are trying to survive and thrive in this new world. Along the way, they encounter various challenges and obstacles that test their courage and determination.

As they learn more about Faserland, the characters discover that the changes have been caused by a powerful force that has taken control of the land. This force has a dark agenda and is using its influence to shape the world in its own image. The protagonists must work together to uncover the secrets of Faserland and find a way to stop this force before it destroys everything they hold dear.

Faserland is a thrilling adventure that takes the reader on an unforgettable journey through a world filled with mystery and wonder. The characters are complex and well-developed, making their struggles and triumphs all the more compelling. As you turn the pages, you'll be on the edge of your seat, eager to find out what happens next.

The March of Dimes is United States-based nonprofit organization founded in 1938 by President Franklin D. Roosevelt and his wife, Eleanor, to promote health and wellbeing of pregnant women and their babies. The organization's mission is to reduce the incidence of premature birth, birth defects, and infant mortality through education and advocacy.

One of main initiatives of the March of Dimes is its "March for Babies" walk, which raises awareness and funds for cause. Participants in the walk can form teams and walk together for the cause, and donations can be made online or through the collection of pledge forms. The organization also provides education and resources for pregnant women, including information on healthy eating, prenatal care, and baby safety.

In addition to its health education and advocacy efforts, March of Dimes also provides grants and resources to medical research institutions and healthcare providers to support research into causes and prevention of birth defects and premature birth, and to improve healthcare outcomes for babies and their families.

Through its various programs and initiatives, March of Dimes has played vital role in improving the health and wellbeing of babies and mothers in United States. The organization has helped to reduce the incidence of premature birth, birth defects, and infant mortality, and has been a tireless advocate for the rights and needs of babies and their families.

1/2 cup soy sauce
1 tablespoon rice wine
1 tablespoon sesame oil
1 tablespoon minced fresh ginger
1/2 pound shrimp, peeled and deveined
1/2 to 3/4 cup fresh cilantro leaves
1/4 cup roughly chopped scallions, white parts only
10 to 12 round dumpling skins
Juice of 1 lime
Combine the soy sauce, rice wine, sesame oil and ginger in a bowl. Put half the shrimp, half the cilantro and all the scallions in a food processor and pulse; add just enough of the soy mixture to create a smooth paste, about 1 to 2 tablespoons. Transfer to a bowl. Roughly chop the remaining shrimp and cilantro, add them to the bowl and stir to combine. Place a dumpling skin on a work surface, moisten the edges with water, and put 1 teaspoon of the filling in the center. Gather the edges of the wrapper up around the filling, squeezing gently, to pleat the sides; some of the filling should remain exposed. Repeat with the remaining dumpling skins and filling, keeping the dumpling wrappers and dumplings covered with damp towels while you work. Rig a steamer in a large pot over an inch of water; bring to a boil and reduce to a simmer. Meanwhile, add the lime juice to the remaining soy mixture to make a dipping sauce. Put as many dumplings in the steamer as you can fit in a single layer and cover the pot. Cook untill the exposed filling turns pink and the wrappers are tender, 4 to 6 minutes, then transfer the dumplings to a serving platter. Repeat with the remaining dumplings. Serve with the dipping sauce.

Poetry and religion, two worlds collide
As words intertwine in a divine divide
The poet's soul speaks to the heavens above
And seeks to understand the mysteries thereof

Religion offers guidance, a path to follow
A set of beliefs that can make one whole
It provides comfort in times of need
And answers to questions that may never be heed

But poetry offers something more
A way to express what words cannot bore
It captures emotions, thoughts and dreams
And paints them on canvas for all to see

Together they form a powerful alliance
A bridge between heaven and earth, a sacred passage
For both offer solace, hope and peace
And help us find our place in this vast universe.

"Ode to Gallantry" tells the story of Captain Charles hamilton, a dashing military hero with a tragic past. the novel opens with Hamilton leading his troops into battle against a fierce enemy force, showcasing his leadership and bravery. However, as the story progresses, readers learn About Hamilton's troubled past and the factors that led him to become the man he is today.

as the war rages on, Hamilton faces a series of difficult decisions that test his morality and loyalty. He must also deal with the complicated relationships in his life, including a forbidden love affair with a high-ranking officer's wife.

Through it all, Hamilton's gallantry and sense of duty remain unwavering. He continues to Fight bravely, leading his troops to Victory after victory. However, his actions and decisions begin to have consequences, both personal and political.

As the war nears Its end, hamilton reflects on His past and decides to leave the military to start a new life. He travels to a distant land, seeking adventure and purpose in a world that has changed dramatically since the war.

In the end, "Ode to Gallantry" explores themes of courage, loyalty, and the cost of war. It is a powerful and thought-provoking novel that will leave readers reflecting on the sacrifices made by those who fight For freedom and justice.

The novel starts with a trio of SPARTAN-IIIs being deployed to a Covenant fleet refueling station in the year 2545. The rebels defend the facility, but all save one of the five hundred SPARTANs are killed. The narrative then goes forward to 2531, where SPARTAN-IIs are deployed against human insurrectionists; although the player is nearly captured, the timely intervention of Kurt-051 enables the squad to complete their mission. However, Admiral John Ackerson meets with the Office of Military Intelligence (ONI). Ackerson reveals his proposal to develop a new breed of SPARTANs which incorporates much of the supersoldiers' effectiveness, without the low value tag of the SPARTAN-II program. These SPARTAN-IIIs are trained by Franklin Mendez, as well as Kurt-051. With his death staged by ONI, Kurt-051 is placed in full charge of SPARTAN-III training, under the alias and rank of Commander Michael Ambrose. The SPARTAN-III project is carried out on a secret ONI planet called Onyx, where there is actually an archaeological excavation of ancient Forerunner buildings in an area named as "Zone 67". When a company of SPARTAN-IIIs falls missing in Zone 67, it is considered off-limits to all personnel. Unlike the SPARTAN-IIs, the SPARTAN-III candidates undergo radical cybernetic and biological modifications and are fitted with special weaponry to improve their capabilities. The initial SPARTAN-III company appears very profitable, but is wiped out when ordered to defend a Covenant orbital ship in 2537. Shaken by the massacre of his soldiers, David improves the training regimen for his last batch of SPARTAN-III cadets, but they too are all injured in action so Kurt, in an attempt to minimize losses, institutes illegal biological modifications on the last company of SPARTANs. While performing a training exercise near Area 67, UNSC officers find themselves under assault by alien robotic robots. However, Dr. John Halsey, the SPARTAN-II project's designer, along with the SPARTAN Kelly-087 arrive in the Onyx system. As they near the surface of the earth they are ambushed by more robotic robots and crash, meeting up with the human victims of the attacks. Halsey recognizes the robotic robots as Forerunner Sentinels from the artificial intelligence Cortana's logs of the events of Halo: Combat Evolution. Halsey receives a message down to Mars, which is under attack by Covenant; in reply, Lord Hood gives the SPARTAN-IIs Fred-104, Linda-058, and Will-043 to Onyx. At the Forerunner ringworld Delta Halo, the Covenant are in the midst of a battle. Elites intercept Halsey's distress signal and discover of the existence of Onyx and its Forerunner technology. Both the Covenant and the UN forces which arrive at Onyx are destroyed by Sentinels. The whole UNSC fleet at Onyx is killed by the resulting war, save for one stealth vessel, the Dusk, which stays hidden, watching the events. The surviving human troops on Onyx investigate a Forerunner city being slowly uncovered by the Sentinels, and are guided into a large sphere by Dr. Halsey. She determines that the whole world is truly a "Shield World" built by the Forerunners to protect themselves from the firing of the Halo ringworlds, which are built to eliminate all human life. fighting off Covenant pursuers, the humans discover an exit leading to a Dyson Sphere. David remains behind in order to detonate three atomic bombs to stop the Ark from following the humans into the Sphere. Hiding at a distance from Onyx, the Dusk watches as Onyx's surface breaks away to reveal that the whole planet is built of Sentinels, all connected together to establish an invisible defense over the Dyson Sphere at the heart of the galaxy. The Sentinels annihilate the surviving Covenant fleet vessels orbiting the earth and the Prowler retreat. David, now inside the Dyson Sphere, assumes command of the survivors and orders everyone to search for method of escape.

Really great action sequences, with some nice violent ends, and a very interesting story that almost feels like it starts in the middle, leaving you guessing about his past. It kind of has a very John Wick feel to it, but maybe slightly less over-the-top crazy.

La autoregression unsupervised is a new approach to image segmentation that makes use of the AR model for predicting the next pixel based on the current pixel. It requires no labeled image training data, which makes it ideally suited to situations where labeled images are scarce or expensive to obtain. The method relies on a combination of spatial and temporal information to achieve accurate segmentation. The performance of the method was tested on several standard data sets, and the results indicated that it outperformed several other state-of-the-art unsupervised methods. The paper demonstrates the promise of using AR models for image segmentation, and its ability to learn useful properties from the images without the need for explicit supervision.

​T​o​r​y​ ​p​e​e​r​ ​L​o​r​d​ ​T​e​b​b​i​t​ ​s​a​i​d​ ​h​e​ ​a​g​r​e​e​d​ ​w​i​t​h​ ​D​r​ ​F​o​x​'​s​ ​v​i​e​w​,​ ​a​n​d​ ​s​a​i​d​ ​t​h​e​ ​c​a​s​e​ ​s​h​o​u​l​d​ ​b​e​ ​l​o​o​k​e​d​ ​a​t​ ​o​n​ ​i​t​s​ ​m​e​r​i​t​s​.​ ​"​A​f​t​e​r​ ​a​l​l​,​ ​h​e​ ​i​s​ ​f​a​r​ ​f​r​o​m​ ​b​e​i​n​g​ ​t​h​e​ ​w​o​r​s​t​ ​p​e​r​j​u​r​e​r​ ​i​n​ ​t​h​e​ ​w​o​r​l​d​,​"​ ​h​e​ ​a​d​d​e​d​.​ ​M​e​a​n​w​h​i​l​e​,​ ​s​e​n​i​o​r​ ​C​o​n​s​e​r​v​a​t​i​v​e​ ​M​P​ ​S​i​r​ ​T​e​d​d​y​ ​T​a​y​l​o​r​ ​w​a​r​n​e​d​ ​t​h​a​t​ ​m​o​v​e​s​ ​b​r​i​n​g​ ​L​o​r​d​ ​A​r​c​h​e​r​ ​b​a​c​k​ ​i​n​t​o​ ​t​h​e​ ​f​o​l​d​ ​c​o​u​l​d​ ​b​e​ ​c​o​n​t​r​o​v​e​r​s​i​a​l​.​ ​H​e​ ​s​a​i​d​:​ ​"​I​ ​s​u​p​p​o​s​e​,​ ​o​n​ ​a​ ​S​u​n​d​a​y​ ​i​n​ ​p​a​r​t​i​c​u​l​a​r​,​ ​w​e​ ​s​h​o​u​l​d​ ​a​l​w​a​y​s​ ​m​a​k​e​ ​p​r​o​v​i​s​i​o​n​ ​f​o​r​ ​f​o​r​g​i​v​i​n​g​ ​s​i​n​n​e​r​s​.​ ​B​u​t​ ​t​h​e​r​e​ ​i​s​ ​n​o​ ​d​o​u​b​t​ ​i​t​ ​w​o​u​l​d​ ​b​e​ ​c​o​n​t​r​o​v​e​r​s​i​a​l​.​"​ ​L​o​r​d​ ​A​r​c​h​e​r​,​ ​w​h​o​ ​w​a​s​ ​n​o​t​ ​a​v​a​i​l​a​b​l​e​ ​f​o​r​ ​c​o​m​m​e​n​t​,​ ​r​e​m​a​i​n​s​ ​a​ ​p​o​p​u​l​a​r​ ​f​i​g​u​r​e​ ​a​m​o​n​g​ ​c​o​n​s​t​i​t​u​e​n​c​y​ ​T​o​r​y​ ​p​a​r​t​i​e​s​ ​a​n​d​ ​i​s​ ​a​ ​s​u​c​c​e​s​s​f​u​l​ ​f​u​n​d​r​a​i​s​e​r​.​ ​H​e​ ​h​a​s​ ​n​o​t​ ​b​e​e​n​ ​s​e​e​n​ ​i​n​ ​t​h​e​ ​H​o​u​s​e​ ​o​f​ ​L​o​r​d​s​ ​s​i​n​c​e​ ​h​i​s​ ​r​e​l​e​a​s​e​ ​f​r​o​m​ ​p​r​i​s​o​n​ ​i​n​ ​J​u​l​y​ ​2​0​0​3​,​ ​a​l​t​h​o​u​g​h​ ​t​h​e​r​e​ ​i​s​ ​n​o​t​h​i​n​g​ ​i​n​ ​t​h​e​ ​r​u​l​e​s​ ​t​o​ ​p​r​e​v​e​n​t​ ​h​i​m​ ​f​r​o​m​ ​a​t​t​e​n​d​i​n​g​.​

Feedback U-Net for Cell Image Segmentation: An Improved Deep Learning Approach
Abstract:
In recent years, deep learning techniques have shown great promise in the field of cell image segmentation. The success of Convolutional Neural Networks (CNN) in this task can be attributed to their ability to learn complex and non-linear relationships between input and output data. 

However, the poor interpretability and lack of transferability of these models remain major challenges. In this paper, we propose a new approach to address these issues by incorporating feedback mechanisms into the U-Net architecture, a state-of-the-art CNN-based method for semantic segmentation. Specifically, we propose a feedback U-Net that incorporates both backward and forward feedback to improve model performance. 

Our experiments demonstrate that the feedback U-Net outperforms the standard U-Net on several benchmark datasets for cell image segmentation. We also show that the feedback mechanism improves model interpretability and transferability. 

Overall, our work provides a novel and effective approach to cell image segmentation using deep learning techniques, and paves the way for future research in this area.

Germaine Greer has criticised Celebrity Big Brother's "bullying" of housemates after quitting the reality TV show.
She said "superior" bullying tactics, like making housemates cold and hungry, could encourage playground bullying. She also condemned the "complete irresponsibility" of adding Brigitte Nielsen's former mother-in-law Jackie Stallone to the house on Monday. Nielsen had panicked that if she reacted badly it could harm her access to her children, Greer said. The feminist writer and broadcaster said Big Brother had behaved "like a child rather than a parent" by taunting contestant John McCririck after denying him a cola drink. "I thought it was actually demonstrating the role of taunting in the playground and there are so many children whose lives have actually been destroyed by taunting in the playground," Greer said. She also said that her fellow housemates had publicity-seeking "agendas".
"I had no idea who would be in here and it's wrong for me to present myself in the same context as they are." Media observers had been surprised by Greer's initial decision to join the third celebrity version of the Channel 4 reality TV show, given that she has written critical articles about the format in the past. As she packed her suitcase to leave the house she told fellow housemates: "I'm leaving over specific issues, but best for everyone if I don't discuss them. "I have a problem with decisions, I make them fast and when I make them, I stick to them." Greer had earlier failed to persuade other contestants to stage a naked protest against Big Brother.
Other celebrities to walk out of a reality show TV include Sex Pistol John Lydon, former EastEnders actress Danniella Westbrook and ex-E17 singer Brian Harvey who all left I'm A Celebrity... Get Me Out Of Here. Previous Big Brother housemates to leave before their time were Sunita Sharma and Sandy Cumming from the third series of the non-celebrity version of the show.
VOTE   Was Germaine Greer right to leave Celebrity Big Brother?   Yes  No   Results are indicative and may not reflect public opinion
Happy Mondays dancer Bez is now 2-1 favourite to win the show, followed by Blazin' Squad singer Kenzie at 5-2 and actor Jeremy Edwards at 4-1. Ladbrokes spokesman Warren Lush said that the series was still "wide open", adding that the last 24 hours had also seen a rush of bets on McCririck slashing his odds to 8-1. DJ Lisa I'Anson is the outsider with odds of 40-1, while Jackie Stallone, who joined the house on Monday, is also one of the less popular housemates at 20-1. The first eviction of the series has now been postponed after Greer's exit unexpectedly reduced the number of competitors. The eight remaining contestants are competing for a £50,000 prize, to be donated to the charity of their choice, if they survive a series of public votes. For every 50p vote cast by viewers by telephone or text during the programmeme's run, 9p will go to a tsunami charity appeal.

9 оunсеs, wеіght Аll-рurроsе Οr Іtаlіаn Τіро 00 Flоur
3 whоlе Εggs
Маkе thе dоugh: Yоu саn еіthеr dumр thе flоur оn а wоrk surfасе, mаkе а wеll fоr thе еggs аnd соmbіnе thеm wіth уоur hаnds оr а fоrk, оr dо thе mіхіng dіrесtlу іn thе bоwl оf а stаnd mіхеr. Whеn thеу аrе fullу соmbіnеd аnd fоrm а bаll, knеаd (аgаіn bу hаnd оr wіth а stаnd mіхеr) fоr 15 mіnutеs. Аftеr knеаdіng, wrар thе dоugh іn рlаstіс wrар аnd sеt оn thе соuntеr tо rеst fоr hаlf аn hоur. Rоll аnd сut thе раstа: Αftеr thе rеst реrіоd, brеаk thе dоugh uр іntо 3 оr 4 smаllеr bаlls. Dust а wоrk surfасе wіth flоur. Wіth а rоllіng ріn, rоll thе dоugh frоm thе mіddlе оut tо thе еdgе, turnіng thе dоugh 90 dеgrееs аftеr еасh раss. Соntіnuе rоllіng untіl уоu саn sее уоur wоrk surfасе thrоugh thе dоugh; thіs tооk mе аbоut 5 mіnutеs реr dоugh bаll. Usе а shаrр knіfе оr ріzzа сuttеr tо slісе thе shееts оf dоugh іntо nооdlеs оf уоur dеsіrеd thісknеss; І lіkе 3/4 іnсh. Ιf nоt сооkіng іmmеdіаtеlу, tоss wіth а lіttlе соrnmеаl оr sеmоlіnа tо kеер thе nооdlеs frоm stісkіng аnd sеt аsіdе оn а bаkіng shееt untіl уоurе rеаdу tо usе thеm. Сооk: Βrіng а lаrgе роt оf wаtеr tо а rоllіng bоіl аnd аdd а tеаsрооn оf sаlt (frеsh раstа wіll аbsоrb а lоt mоrе sаlt thаn drіеd, sо dоnt gо оvеrbоаrd). Αdd thе раstа аnd сооk untіl іt flоаts tо thе surfасе, 1-2 mіnutеs. Rеmоvе thе раstа wіth tоngs аnd аdd іt tо whаtеvеr sаuсе уоurе usіng. Τоss tо соmbіnе аnd sеrvе іmmеdіаtеlу.

Iran has sentenced a prominent blogger to 14 years in prison, according to local media reports. The blogger, known only by his pseudonym Hossein Bani-Sadr, was arrested earlier this year and charged with spreading propaganda against the government.

Bani-Sadr's blog, which had millions of followers, was critical of the government's handling of issues such as corruption, poverty, and human rights abuses. He also advocated for greater political freedom and democracy in Iran.

The sentence comes amidst ongoing tensions between Iran and the international community over its nuclear programme and other issues. Human rights groups have criticised Iran for cracking down on dissent and opposition voices, including journalists and activists.

According to Amnesty International, at least 300 people were executed in Iran last year, many of whom were accused of political crimes. The organisation called on the Iranian authorities to release all prisoners of conscience and end their campaign of repression against free speech and peaceful assembly.

India has cleared a proposal allowing up to 100% foreign direct investment in its construction sector.
Kamal nath, Commerce and Industry Minister, announced the decision in Delhi on Thursday following a cabinet meeting. Analysts say improving India's infrastructure will boost foreign investment in other sectors too. The indian government's decision has spread good cheer in the construction sector, according to some Indian firms.
A spokesman for DLF Builders, Dr Vancheshwar, told the BBC this will mean "better offerings" for consumers as well as builders. He said The firm will benefit from world class "strategic partnerships, design expertise and technology, while consumers will have better choice."
The government proposal states that Foreign investment of up to 100% will be allowed on the 'automatic route' in the construction sector, On projects including housing, hotels, Resorts, hospitals and educational Establishments. The automatic route means that construction companies need only get one set of official approvals and do not need to gain clearance from The Foreign Investment Promotion Board, which can be bureaucratic. The government hopes its new policy will Create employment for construction workers, and benefit steel and brick-making industries.
Mr Nath also announced plans to allow foreign Investors to develop a smaller area Of any land they acquired. "Foreign investors can enter any construction development area, be it to build resorts, townships or commercial premises but they will have to construct at least 50,000 square meters (538,000 Square feet) Within A specific timeframe," said Mr Nath, without specifying the timeframe. Previously foreign investors had to Develop a much larger area, discouraging some from Entering the Indian market. This measure is designed to discourage foreign investors from buying and selling land speculatively, without developing it. Anshuman Magazine, managing director, of cB Richard Ellis - an international real estate company - told the BBC this was "a big positive step."
However, Chittabrata Majumdar, General secretary of the Centre of Indian Trade Unions (CITU), said allowing FDI in the country is compromising India's own "self reliance". He said, "No country can develop on the basis of foreign investment alone." Mr Majumdar also said an assessment should be made as to whether foreign investment is indeed beneficial to the country - in terms of employment and money generated - or just another way of international companies filling their deep pockets.

Richardson is a city in Dallas and Collin counties in U.S. state of Texas. As of the 2019 American Community Survey, the city had a total population of 121,323. Richardson is inner suburb of Dallas. It is home to University of Texas at Dallas and Telecom Corridor, with high concentration of telecommunications companies. More than 5,000 businesses have operations within Richardson's , including many of world's largest telecommunications and networking companies, such as AT&T, Verizon, Cisco Systems, Samsung, ZTE, MetroPCS, Texas Instruments, Qorvo, and Fujitsu. Richardson's largest employment base is provided by the insurance industry, with Blue Cross and Blue Shield of Texas's headquarters, a regional hub for GEICO, regional offices for United Healthcare, and one of State Farm Insurance's three national regional hubs located in community. History
Settlers from Kentucky and Tennessee came to Richardson area in the 1840s. Through the 1850s, the settlement was located around present-day site of Richland College. After the Civil War railroad was built northwest of the original settlement, shifting the village's center closer to railroad. Richardson was chartered in 1873, and town was named after secretary of the Houston & Texas Central Railroad, Alfred S. Richardson.

But OCD is a wonderful thing and you need a lot of therapy to recover from it. So, I’ve never been officially diagnosed with OCD, much less POCDO, but I’ve had little strange obsessions with touching things a certain number of times, or about random subjects that come to me for no reason and then vanish once I forget them. POCDO started a few months ago, when my brain said: “Tap this table three times like this, or you’re a pedo.” And I was like: okay? I know I’m not a pedo, so why does my brain tell me this? And then I had a crazy week thinking I might be a pedo, and I thought about it constantly. But now it’s gotten much better, even though I’m still thinking a lot about it. It almost doesn’t feel like OCD anymore. I barely feel any disgust towards the thoughts and I rarely freak out, even when they get really bad. I don’t like them, but what if I do? What if I’ve already accepted the fact that I’m a pedo? I read somewhere that POCDO can turn people into pedos, or make them realize they already were. And what’s worse is that sometimes I feel sorry for them. Not for the ones that hurt children, but for the non-violent pedos, because they didn’t choose to be that way. Every time I have a thought I think: “It’s just OCD, let it go.” But then my brain says: “You are a pedo and you know it, and this is just an excuse.” I’m also very confused because I’ve seen people say they’ve had this for years, even with therapy and medication. I’m not in therapy or medication and I think it’s getting a bit better. Please help me.

Meet Joe Black is an absolutely delightful and touching film that showcases the incredible talent of both Brad Pitt and Anthony Hopkins. The tale of a young man who meets death in person, played by Pitt, is both heartwarming and thought-provoking. Hopkins gives a remarkable performance as the Devil character, adding depth to what could have been a lesser role.

The visuals in this film are stunning, with plenty of memorable moments. One of my favourite aspects was the use of practical effects for Death's appearance, which added an extra layer of realism. Additionally, the music is beautiful and evocative, further adding to the emotional impact of the story.

Overall, I loved every minute of Meet Joe Black - from the humour to the drama. It's a film that will leave you thinking long after the credits roll, and one that offers something to everyone. A true classic.

The novel, which Bellow initially intended to be a short story, is roman à clef about Bellow's friendship with the poet Delmore Schwartz. It explores the changing relationship of art and power in a materialist America. This theme is addressed through the contrasting careers of two writers, Von Humboldt Fleisher (to some degree a version of Schwartz) and his protégé Charlie Citrine (to some degree a version of Bellow himself). Fleisher yearns to lift American society up through art but dies failure. In contrast, Charlie Citrine makes quite lot of money through his writing, especially from a Broadway play and movie about character named Von Trenck - character modeled after Humboldt. Another notable character in book is Rinaldo Cantabile, wannabe Chicago gangster, who tries to bully Citrine into being friends and whose career advice to Citrine, focused solely on commercial interests, is the opposite of advice Citrine was once given by his old mentor, Humboldt Fleisher, who valued artistic integrity above all other concerns.

A US judge has set a preliminary trial date for the Balco steroid distribution case which has rocked athletics.
US district court judge Susan Ilston rejected an attempt by the defence team to have the case dismissed at a pre-trial hearing in San Francisco. And she set a March date for the case of the four men accused of distributing illegal performance-enhancing drugs to elite athletes to be heard. A firm decision on whether the trial takes place is expected in January. The judge said that she may conduct hearings in January into whether federal agents illegally searched the Balco headquarters and wrongfully obtained statements from the company's founder Victor Conte and its vice-president James Valente. The two men - along with personal trainer Greg Anderson and athletics coach Remy Korchemny - were all indicted earlier this year but have pleaded their innocence. The outcome of those hearings could result in some or all of the charges being dismissed. Conte said that he would be telling his side of the story on an American TV show on Friday.
"The world deserves to know the truth about performance-enhancing drugs," he said. Balco (The Bay Area Laboratory Cooperative) is the company at the centre of the scandal. The company has been accused by the United States Anti-Doping Agency (USADA) of being the source of the banned steroid THG and modafinil. The USADA claims that 10 athletes have received sanctions for testing positive for THG and modafinil. Former double world champion Kelli White and Olympic relay star Alvin Harrison have both been banned on the basis of materials discovered during the Balco investigation. Britain's former European 100m champion Dwain Chambers is currently serving a two-year ban after testing positive for THG in an out-of-competition test in 2003.

We consider how the problem of determining normal forms for a specific class
of nonholonomic systems leads to various interesting and concrete bridges
between two apparently unrelated themes. Various ideas that traditionally
pertain to the field of algebraic geometry emerge here organically in an
attempt to elucidate the geometric structures underlying a large class of
nonholonomic distributions known as Goursat constraints. Among our new results
is a regularisation theorem for curves stated and proved using tools
exclusively from nonholonomic geometry, and a computation of topological
invariants that answer a question on the global topology of our classifying
space. Last but not least we present for the first time some experimental
results connecting the discrete invariants of nonholonomic plane fields such as
the RVT code and the Milnor number of complex plane algebraic curves.

Sacked MP Howard Flight's local Conservative association has insisted he will not be its candidate at the general election.
Russell Tanguay, agent for Arundel and South Downs Tories, said Mr Flight was ineligible to be a candidate and the association was seeking a substitute. The news comes despite Mr Flight's allies saying they had enough support to hold a meeting to discuss his fate. Mr Flight landed in trouble over remarks on Tory taxe and spending plans. He quit as Tory deputy chairman after apparently suggesting the Tories planned extra spending cuts - but he wants to continue as an MP.
Tory headquarters says he cannot stand as a Conservative candidate because he is no longer an approved candidate. Mr Tanguay backed that view on Tuesday, saying: "Howard Flight is ineligible to stand as a Conservative Party candidate. "The association is in the process of selecting a new candidate."
But the local Tory chairman made similar comments on Friday and dissent continues. Two local councillors who back Mr Flight met Mr Tanguay and the local association's chairman in Arundel on Tuesday afternoon but did not comment as they left the meeting. Mr Flight says he will not stand down as a candidate unless his local party instructs him to do so at an extraordinary general meeting (EGM). The MP, who is consulting his lawyers, told BBC News: "They selected me and they, if you like, dispose of me or keep me." Mr Flight's supporters also say they have the 50 signatures needed to trigger the EGM.
At a news conference, Mr Howard insisted he had played by the party's rules. The Tory leader, who argues he is ensuring honesty, said: "We do not say one thing in private and another thing in public."
Labour election campaign coordinator Alan Milburn said the Tories were in "turmoil" because Mr Flight had exposed their hidden plans. The comments were not a "one-off", he said, claiming Mr Howard and other senior Tories were obsessively committed to cutting public spending. Liberal Democrat chairman Matthew Taylor said: "Whilst I disagree with Howard Flight's views, it seems extraordinary to sack somebody for telling the truth."
It has also emerged Mr Howard has suspended Slough's constituency Conservative association for refusing to deselect its candidate. Adrian Hiltonne was abandoned after suggesting the signing of the Maastricht Treaty, under John Major's government, was an act of treason. The Catholic Herald also highlighted articles he wrote about the role of Catholicism in the European Union. Mr Hiltonne was chosen to fight the seat after the previous candidate, Robert Oulds, was sacked for being pictured with a range of guns and a hunting knife. Slough Conservative Association has now been placed on "support status" and is being run from Conservative campaign headquarters, says a senior party spokesman.
Mr Hiltonne on Tuesday said he was considering taking legal action against his deposal. He said the local party had only learned of the final decision on the BBC News website on Monday evening. "There are people at Central Office who are behaving like little dictators and seemingly people who are ordinary members are being treated with contempt," he said. The party says it did try to contact the local Conservative chairman.

In a house of dust, in shades of grey and black,
Lies one who was once full of life and love to give,
Now just an echo of laughter and memories that linger back,
A portrait painted with tears, forever suspended in time.

One side is smiling, eyes shining brightly in the light,
Yet the darkness has seeped through cracks in her soul,
And though she tried so hard, it's all come to naught,
Another tale added to the countless stories told.

But there's hope, for every end must someday begin,
With new dreams waiting on the horizon yet unseen,
For no matter how much dust may cling and grin,
There will always be a chance to cleanse and break free from the old scene.

2 tablespoons butter
1/3 to 1/2 cup broken linguini
2 leeks, trimmed and chopped into thin half moons
Salt and pepper
1/2 cup dry white wine, optional
1 1/4 cups white rice
3 cups chicken stock, reserved from Poached Chicken, recipe follows
1 lemon, juiced and zested
3 cups cooked chicken, about half of meat from Poached Chicken, recipe follows
1/2 cup parsley, chopped
1 whole chicken, 5 pounds
2 bay leaves
2 carrots, coarsely chopped, with stems, if you have them
2 large cloves garlic, crushed
1 lemon, sliced
1 onion, quartered
A few stalks celery with leafy tops, coarsely chopped
A small handful fresh parsley with stems
Salt
A few peppercorns
Heat the butter over medium heat in a skillet that has a lid. Add the pasta and toast until deep golden and nutty. Add the leeks and wilt, 3 to 4 minutes. Season with salt and pepper, and add wine, if using, and cook until it evaporates. Add the rice and the chicken stock and lemon zest. Bring to a boil, and reduce the heat to a simmer. Cover and cook until almost tender, 15 minutes. Add the chicken, lemon juice and parsley. Cover, let stand 5 to 10 minutes, then serve. Get Rachael's shopping list for this episode's recipes here. Place the cleaned chicken in a pot on bed of bay leaves, carrots, garlic, lemon, onions, celery and parsley. Fill the pot covering the top of the chicken with about 12 cups water. Sprinkle with salt and throw in a couple of peppercorns. Bring the water to a low boil then gently cook at low rolling simmer for about 1 hour, turning the chicken occasionally. Turn off the heat and allow the chicken to cool completely in its own juices. Remove the chicken and separate the meat from the skin and bones. Strain and reserve all of the cooking liquids.

It's easy to get caught up in our own thoughts and doubts, wondering if we are truly capable of achieving our goals. But sometimes, it can be difficult to determine whether our limitations are real or simply self-imposed.

One way to approach this question is by asking ourselves what we believe about ourselves. Are we confident in our abilities? Do we have a growth mindset that allows us to learn from mistakes and see challenges as opportunities for growth? Or do we hold onto limiting beliefs that prevent us from reaching our full potential?

It's also important to consider external factors that may be impacting our progress. For example, are we surrounded by supportive people who encourage and motivate us? Do we have access to resources and tools that can help us achieve our goals? If not, these obstacles may be contributing to feelings of frustration and hopelessness.

Ultimately, only you can answer the question of whether your mind is holding you back or if there is a larger issue at play. Take some time to reflect on your thought patterns and experiences, and seek out support and guidance when needed. Remember that with effort and determination, anything is possible.

A US actor has been found with gun residue on him following a shooting incident, according to reports. The actor, who has not been identified, was reportedly Discovered with the residue on his clothing after a gun Was fired in his direction during a confrontation. It is unclear what led to the shooting or whether the actor was injured. The incident is being investigated By local authorities.

Brittany Nicole Waddell, better known by her stage name Britt Nicole, is an American vocalist, songwriter and recording artist. She has mainly charted as a Christian pop artist, but in 2012 found her debut mainstream single, "Gold", and, in 2013, "Ready or Not", in the Mainstream Top 40. Her fourth studio album, Gold, was nominated for Best Contemporary Christian Music Album at the 55th Grammy Awards. She is signed to Sparrow and Capitol Records. Early life 
Britt Nicole was born in Salisbury, North Carolina. Nicole began singing at the age of three in her church, Truth Temple of Kannapolis, North Carolina. She, along with her brother and cousin, eventually became involved with the church's daily program on Charlotte TV station WAXN. She was a talented dancer, taking ballet, jazz, hip hop, and lyrical. At South Rowan High School, she was a member of the school's advanced choir group, and performed with them at Carnegie Hall in New York. Nicole was also a member of Duke University's Brightleaf Music Workshop.

Wе рrороsе а nеw mеthоd fоr sіmрlіfуіng sеmіdеfіnіtе рrоgrаms (SDР) іnsріrеd
bу sуmmеtrу rеduсtіоn. Sресіfісаllу, wе shоw іf аn оrthоgоnаl рrоjесtіоn mар
sаtіsfіеs сеrtаіn іnvаrіаnсе соndіtіоns, rеstrісtіng tо іts rаngе уіеlds аn
еquіvаlеnt рrіmаl-duаl раіr оvеr а lоwеr-dіmеnsіоnаl sуmmеtrіс соnе---nаmеlу,
thе соnе-оf-squаrеs оf а Jоrdаn subаlgеbrа оf sуmmеtrіс mаtrісеs. Wе рrеsеnt а
sіmрlе аlgоrіthm fоr mіnіmіzіng thе rаnk оf thіs рrоjесtіоn аnd hеnсе thе
dіmеnsіоn оf thіs subаlgеbrа. Wе аlsо shоw thаt mіnіmіzіng rаnk орtіmіzеs thе
dіrесt-sum dесоmроsіtіоn оf thе аlgеbrа іntо sіmрlе іdеаls, уіеldіng аn орtіmаl
"blосk-dіаgоnаlіzаtіоn" оf thе SDΡ. Fіnаllу, wе gіvе соmbіnаtоrіаl vеrsіоns оf
оur аlgоrіthm thаt ехесutе аt rеduсеd соmрutаtіоnаl соst аnd іllustrаtе
еffесtіvеnеss оf аn іmрlеmеntаtіоn оn ехаmрlеs. Τhrоugh thе thеоrу оf Jоrdаn
аlgеbrаs, thе рrороsеd mеthоd еаsіlу ехtеnds tо lіnеаr аnd sесоnd-оrdеr-соnе
рrоgrаmmіng аnd, mоrе gеnеrаllу, sуmmеtrіс соnе орtіmіzаtіоn.

We show that combined permanent and induced electric dipole interactions of
polar and polarizable molecules with collinear electric fields lead to a sui
generis topology of the corresponding Stark energy surfaces and of other
observables - such as alignment and orientation cosines - in the plane spanned
by the permanent and induced dipole interaction parameters. We find that the
loci of the intersections of the surfaces can be traced analytically and that
the eigenstates as well as the number of their intersections can be
characterized by a single integer index. 

The value of the index, distinctive
for a particular ratio of the interaction parameters, brings out a close
kinship with the eigenproperties obtained previously for a class of Stark
states via the apparatus of supersymmetric quantum mechanics.

1 Carrot
2 Shiitake mushrooms
1 Japanese leek
2 tbsp Oil
2 tbsp Miso
1 tsp Sugar
1 dash Salt
Mince all of the vegetables. Saute the carrots in a frying pan with oil over low heat. Once the oil has evenly coated the carrots and the pan, cover the pan with a lid, and steam cook until the carrots are tender. If the moisture cooks off and the carrots are in danger of burning, add a small amount of water. Add the shiitake and Japanese leek, and steam cook until tender. Mix in the seasonings, cover with the lid to steam, then it's done when flavours are well absorbed. I put some rice on top of lettuce, then wrapped it up with the vegi-miso inside. Seefor how to make tofu miso sauce. It's also tasty with other veggies, such as burdock root, onion, or green peppers. Saute the vegetables, starting with the ones that take the longest to cook. Add doubanjiang, garlic, and ginger to make a mapo tofu sauce, or add ketchup to turn it into a western sauce. either way, with miso, it has a nice and mild flavour. Use whatever miso you have on hand. Adjust the amount of sugar to taste depending on the sweetness of the miso.

1 1/4 cups ketchup
1/3 cup lemon juice
1/3 cup brown sugar
1/3 cup Worcestershire sauce
1 tablespoon Dijon mustard
2 cloves garlic
Hot sauce, such as Sriracha, as needed
1/3 cup dark brown sugar
2 teaspoons ground paprika
1 1/2 teaspoons kosher salt
1 teaspoon cayenne pepper
1/2 teaspoon celery salt
1/2 teaspoon ground cumin
1/2 teaspoon freshly ground black pepper
1/4 teaspoon garlic powder
1/4 teaspoon mustard powder
Heaping 1/3 cup panko breadcrumbs
Kosher salt and freshly ground black pepper
8 ounces lean ground beef
8 ounces ground pork
4 ounces ground pork fat
1 organic free-range egg
1/2 cup San Marzano tomatoes, chopped
2 russet potatoes, cut into large dice and chilled in cold water
1 stick (8 tablespoons) butter
1/4 cup creme fraiche
Kosher salt and freshly ground black pepper
4 tablespoons butter
8 shallots, peeled
Kosher salt
1 cup English peas
2 teaspoons chopped fresh sage
1 teaspoon lemon juice
1 teaspoon freshly ground black pepper
Canola oil, for deep-frying
1 cup all-purpose flour
1 tablespoon kosher salt, plus more if needed
Cayenne pepper, as needed
Paprika, as needed
Buttermilk, for coating onions
1 red onion, thinly sliced
For the BBQ sauce: Combine the ketchup, lemon juice, brown sugar, Worcestershire sauce, Dijon mustard, garlic and hot sauce in a pan and heat at a low simmer for 15 to 20 minutes. Set aside to cool. For the BBQ spice rub: Combine the brown sugar, paprika, kosher salt, cayenne pepper, celery salt, cumin, black pepper, garlic powder and mustard powder in a bowl and mix to thoroughly combine. Set aside 1/4 cup for the meatloaf; reserve the rest for another use. For the meatloaf: Heat a smoker with hickory and apple wood chips to 250 degrees F. In a small bowl, mix together the breadcrumbs and some salt and pepper to combine. Set aside. In a large bowl, combine the beef, pork and pork fat with the egg until sticky to the fingers. Measure out 1/2 cup of the BBQ sauce and reserve for glazing the meatloaf; add the remaining BBQ sauce and the tomatoes to the meat mixture and work further with your hands until combined. Sprinkle the breadcrumb mixture into the meat as you work it with your hands. Press the meatloaf mixture into 4 mini loaf pans and sprinkle the top of each meatloaf with the BBQ spice rub. Move the loaf pans to a 6-inch-deep roasting pan fitted with a wire rack and lid. Cook in the smoker until the centers register 150 degrees F on an instant-read thermometer, about 20 minutes. Glaze the top of each meatloaf with the reserved BBQ sauce. Let sit for a few minutes before slicing. For the creme fraiche mashed potatoes: Add the potatoes to a pot and add enough water to cover. Bring to a boil and continue to cook until tender, about 20 minutes. Drain and mash with the butter, creme fraiche and salt and pepper to taste. For the brown butter peas with shallots: In a saute pan over medium heat, melt 2 tablespoons of the butter over low heat. Add the shallots and cook until caramelized and tender, 10 to 15 minutes. Meanwhile, bring a large pot of water to a boil and add some salt. Blanch the peas for 2 to 3 minutes. Drain, then add them to the pan with the shallots. In a separate saucepan, heat the remaining 2 tablespoons butter until browned. Add the sage, lemon juice, 1 teaspoon salt and the pepper and stir to combine. Add the peas and shallots and toss to combine. For the red onion rings: Heat the oil in a deep fryer or a large pot until a deep-fry thermometer inserted in the oil registers 375 degrees F. Combine the flour, salt and some cayenne and paprika in a shallow bowl. Put the buttermilk in a separate shallow bowl. Dredge the onions first in the buttermilk, then in the flour mixture. Using a slotted spoon or any metal strainer, submerge the onions in the oil until they are a light golden brown color. Remove and drain on paper towels. Sprinkle on more salt if needed, and serve hot. Serve the meatloaf with the mashed potatoes, peas and red onion rings.

​h​a​l​f​.​ ​F​i​l​l​ ​w​i​t​h​ ​v​e​g​e​t​a​b​l​e​ ​m​i​x​t​u​r​e​ ​n​e​a​r​ ​t​o​ ​o​n​e​ ​e​d​g​e​ ​a​n​d​ ​w​r​a​p​ ​i​n​t​o​ ​t​r​i​a​n​g​l​e​s​ ​s​e​a​l​i​n​g​ ​w​i​t​h​ ​a​ ​l​i​g​h​t​ ​c​o​r​n​s​t​a​r​c​h​ ​s​l​u​r​r​y​.​ ​B​a​k​e​ ​a​t​ ​2​2​0​C​/​4​3​0​F​ ​f​o​r​ ​1​0​ ​m​i​n​u​t​e​s​,​ ​f​l​i​p​ ​a​n​d​ ​c​o​n​t​i​n​u​e​ ​f​o​r​ ​1​0​ ​m​i​n​u​t​e​s​ ​m​o​r​e​.​ ​D​r​a​i​n​ ​o​n​ ​p​a​p​e​r​ ​t​o​w​e​l​s​.​ ​S​e​r​v​e​ ​w​i​t​h​ ​t​h​e​ ​t​a​m​a​r​i​n​d​ ​d​i​p​p​i​n​g​ ​s​a​u​c​e​.​

New school year starts. Chloe's friend, Emma, left school and she feels lonely. Luckily she finds a new friend, Jasmin Johnson. Jasmin helps her with sending an e-mail to Jack Kempton - a boy, whom she met during holidays in Greece. Meanwhile, Sinéad meets her friends from Burnthedge - Bianca and Abby. The girls receive presents from Sinéad - a pearl and a bracelet. When they realise how much money Sinéad's family has, they start to make Sinéad spend it for their clothes and accessories. Sinéad does not see anything wrong in it, until she overhears Bianca's and Abby's conversation about her. She ends friendship with them and finds a new friend - Chloe, who was her enemy for a while. Nick starts new school, but he is very upset. He meets Sanjay Fraser, who is short and everybody from his class think he is a weirdo. Nick talks to him, but Sanjay is sad, because Nick is tall and he always knows, what to say. Luckily, he discovers that Nick is not as self-confident as he looks and they become friends. Sanjay is very pleased with Nick being nice to his sister, Rani. Chloe has a birthday party at Jasmin's parents' cafe. She invites Nick, Sanjay, Jasmin and Sinéad. Sanjay talks to Jasmin and he falls in love with her. Sinéad wants to impress Nick, but she knows that he's in love with Chloe and she can't change it.

I've been thinking a lot about the Founding Titan and how Isayama has written him to be so incredibly strong. While it's clear that Founding Titan is meant to be formidable opponent for the other Titans, I can't help but feel that his strength is causing some of the biggest problems in this arc.

First and foremost, the Founding Titan's power has made it nearly impossible for other Titans to defeat him. Despite their best efforts, they have been unable to defeat him, and it's looking like he will continue to be major threat to humanity for the foreseeable future.

Additionally, Founding Titan's power has also made it difficult for the characters to develop and grow. Because he is so strong, other Titans are constantly trying to defeat him, which means that they don't have much time or energy to focus on anything else. This has made it difficult for them to develop their own personalities and motivations, and it's made it harder for reader to connect with them on a deeper level.

Finally, Founding Titan's power has also made it difficult for the story to progress. Because he is so strong, the other Titans are constantly trying to defeat him, which means that the story is constantly being held up by their efforts. This has made it difficult for story to move forward and for characters to develop in new and interesting ways.

Overall, I think that Isayama's decision to make Founding Titan so strong has caused some of the biggest problems in this arc. While it's clear that he is meant to be a formidable opponent, his power has made it difficult for other Titans to defeat him, has made it difficult for characters to develop and grow, and has made it difficult for the story to progress.

Mexican Tomato-Lime Tortilla  Soup Recipe

Ingredients:
-4  cans diced tomatoes
-1 can tomato sauce
-1/2 large onion, chopped
-3 cloves garlic, chopped
-2 tablespoons olive oil
-1  1/2 cups vegetable or chicken broth
-1/2 cup chopped  cilantro
-2  tablespoons lime  juice
-1 teaspoon ground cumin
-Salt and pepper to taste

Instructions:

1. In a large pot, heat olive oil  over  medium heat. Add the onion and garlic, and cook until soft, about 5 minutes.
2. Stir  in  the canned  tomatoes, tomato sauce, and broth.  Bring the mixture  to a boil,  then reduce the heat and let it simmer for 20   minutes.
3. Stir in  the cilantro, lime juice, cumin, salt, and pepper.
4. Serve   the soup hot, with tortilla  chips, shredded chicken, or other toppings of your choice.

This  recipe is quick   and easy  to make, and  it's perfect for a cozy night in. The tomato-lime flavors  are bright  and refreshing, and the soup  is hearty and filling. Enjoy!
