Even death to ourselves
Is nothing, nor concerns me in the least,
since nature of mind is mortal evermore.
And just as in the ages gone before
We had no touch of ill, when all sides round
To battle came the Carthaginian host,
And the times, shaken by tumultuous warfare,
Under the aery coasts of arching heaven
Shuddered and trembled, and all humankind
Doubted to which the empery should fall
By land and sea, thus when we are no more,
When begins that sundering of our body and spirit
Through which we're fashioned to a single state,
Verily naught to me, us then no more,
Can come to pass, naught move our senses then-
No, not if earth confounded were with sea,
And sea with earth. But if indeed do know
The essence of mind and energy of spirit,
After their severance from this body of ours,
But nothing 'tis to me who in the bonds
And wedlock of the heart and body live,
Through which we're fashioned to a single state.
And, only if time collected after death
The matter of our frames and set it all
Again in place as now, and if again
To me the light of life were given, O yet
That process too will not concern me aught,
When once the self-succession of our sense
Has been asunder broken. And now and here,
Little enough we're busied with the selves
We were aforetime, nor, concerning them,
Suffer a sore distress. For shouldst thou gaze
Backwards from all yesterdays of day
The immeasurable, thinking how manifold
The motions of matter are, then couldst thou well
Credit this too: often these very seeds
(From which we are to-day) of old were set
In the same order as they are to-day-
Yet this we can't to consciousness recall
Through the remembering mind. For there hath been
An interposed moment of life, and wide
Have all the motions came everywhere
From these our senses. For if woe and ail
Perchance are toward, then the person to whom
The bane can come will himself be there
At that same moment. But death precludeth this,
Forbidding life to him on whom will crowd
Such irk and care; and granted 'tis to do:
Nothing for me there is to dread in death,
No wretchedness for him who is no more,
The same estate as if ne'er born before,
When death immortal hath ta'en the eternal life.
Hence, where thou seest a person to grieve because
When dead he rots with body laid down,
Or dies in flames or jaws of beasts,
Know well: he rings not true, and that beneath
Still works an unseen sting on his soul,
However he denies that he knows.
His will be aught of feeling after death.
For he, I fancy, grants not what he says,
Nor what that means, and he fails
To pluck himself with all his roots from life
And cast that self back, still unawares
Feigning that some remainder's left behind.
For when in life one pictures to oneself
His body dead by lions and vultures torn,
He pities his state, dividing not himself
Therefrom, removing not the self enough
From the body flung down, imagining
Himself that body, and projecting there
His own sense, as he stands beside it: hence
He grieves that he is mortal born, nor marks
That in true death there is no second self
Alive and unable to sorrow for self destroyed,
Or stand lamenting that the self lies there
Mangled or burning. For if it an evil is
Dead to be jerked about by jaw and fang
Of the wild beasts, I say not why 'twere not
Bitter to lie on fires and roast in fires,
Or suffocate in honey, and, reclined
On the soft oblong of an ice stone,
Grow stiff in cold, or sink with load of earth
Down-crushing from above.
"Thee now no more
The joyful house and best of men will welcome,
Nor little children run up to snatch their kisses
And touch with silent joy thy heart.
Thou shalt not speed in undertakings more,
Nor be the warder of thine own no more.
Poor wretch," they say, "one hostile hour hath ta'en
Wretchedly from thee all life's other guerdons,"
But add not, "yet no longer unto ye
remains a remnant of desire for them"
If this they only well regarded with mind
And came up with maxims, they could free
Their state of man from anguish and from despair.
"O just as here thou art, aslumber in death,
So shalt thou slumber down the rest of day,
Released from this harrying sorrow. But we,
We have bewept thou with insatiate woe,
Standing beside whilst on the awful pyre
Thou wert made ashes; and no day will come
For me the eternal pain from the breast."
But ask the mourner what's the bitterness
That man should waste in an eternal misery,
If, after all, the thing's but sleep and rest?
For when the heart and frame together are sunk
In slumber, no one then demands his self
Or being. Still, this night will be forever,
without desire of any selfhood more,
For all it matters unto me asleep.
But not at all do those primordial germs
Roam round our members, at that moment, afar
From their own motions that produce our senses-
Since, when he's startled from his bed, a person
Collects his senses. Death is, then, to me
much less- if there can be a greater than that
Which is itself a matter: for there goes
Hard upon death a scattering more great
Of the throng of matter, and no person wakes up
On whom once falls the icy pause of life.
This too, O often from the soul men have,
With their couches holding of the cups,
With faces shaded by fresh flowers awry:
"Brief is this fruit of joy to paltry man,
Soon, soon departed, and then, no,
It will not be recalled."- As if, forsooth,
It were their prime of evils in great death
To parch, poor tongues, with thirst and arid drought,
Or chafe for any lack.
Once more, if Nature
Should of a sudden send a voice abroad,
And her own self inveigh against me so:
"Mortal, what hast thou of such grave concern
That thou indulgest in too sickly plaints?
Why this bemoaning and beweeping death?
For if thy life aforetime and behind
To thee was grateful, and not all thy good
Was heaped as in sieve to flow down
And perish unavailingly, why not,
Even like a banqueter, depart the halls,
Laden with life? why not with mind content
Take now, thou fool, thy unafflicted rest?
But if that thou enjoyed hath been
Lavished and lost, and life is now offence,
Why seekest more to add- which in its turn
Will perish foully and fall out in vain?
O why not just bring an end of life,
Of labour? For all I can devise or find
To pleasure thee is none: all people are
The same forever. While not even thy body
Wrinkles with years, nor even the frame exhausts
Outworn, yet things abide the same, yet if
Thou goest on to conquer all of time
With length of years, yea, if thou ever diest"-
What were our answer, but that Nature here
Urges just suit and in her words puts down
True cause of action? And should one complain,
Riper in years and elder, and despair,
Poor devil, his death more sorely than is fit,
Then should she not, with greater right, on him
Cry out, inveighing with a tone more shrill:
"Off with thy tears, and choke thy whines, buffoon!
Thou wrinklest- after thee hast had the sum
Of the guerdons of life; yet, before thee cravest ever
What's not at hand, contemning present good,
That life has fallen back, unperfected
And unavailing unto thee. And now,
Or ere thou guessed it, death beneath thy head
Stands- and before thou canst be going home
Sated and laden with the goodly feast.
But now yield all that's alien to thine age,-
Up, with great grace! make room for sons: ye will."
Justly, I fancy, may she reason so,
Justly inveigh and gird: because always the old
Outcrowded by the new gives way, and never
The one thing from the others is repaired.
Nor no person is banished to the darkness
Of Tartarus, the black. For stuff shall be,
That thus the after-generations grow,-
Though these, their life completed, follow thee;
And so like thee are generations all-
Already risen, or some day to fall.
So one moment from this rises ever;
And in fee-simple life is given to nothing,
But unto all mere usufruct.
Look again:
Nothing to me was all fore-passed eld
Of time the eternal, ere we had a baby.
And Nature keeps this like a mirror up
Of time-to-be when we are alive and gone.
And what is there so sad appears?
Now what is there so terrible about it all?
Is't not serener even than any sleep?
And, verily, those tortures said to be
In Acheron, the deep, they all are ours
Here in this life. No Tantalus, benumbed
With baseless terror, as the stories say,
Fears the great boulder hanging in the air:
But, perhaps, in life an empty dread of gods
Urges mortality, and each one fears
Such fall of fortune as may chance to him.
Nor eat the vultures into Tityus
Prostrate in Hades, nor can they find,
Forsooth, throughout eternal ages, aught
To pry out for in that mighty breast.
Yet hugely he extend his bulk-
Who hath for outspread limbs not acres nine,
But the entire earth- he will not able be
To bear eternal pain nor furnish food
From his own frame forever. But for me
A Tityus is he whom vultures rend
Prostrate in love, whom anxious anguish eats,
Whom troubles of any unappeased desires
Asunder rip. We have before our ears
Here in this life also a Sisyphus
In him who seeketh of the populace
The rods, the axes fell, and evermore
Retires a beaten and a miserable man.
For to seek after power- an empty name,
Nor given at all- and ever in the search
To endure a world of misery, O this it is
To shove with shoulder up the mountain a boulder
Which never goes rolling out from off the top,
And headlong makes for levels of the plain.
Then to be always feeding an ingrate mind,
Filling with great things, satisfying never-
As do the seasons of the past for me,
When they return and take their progenies
And varied charms, and we are always filled
With the fruits of life- O this, I fancy, 'tis
To pour, Like those young virgins in the tale,
Waters into a sieve, unfilled forever.

Heat is a cinematic tour de force that masterfully weaves together themes of crime, loyalty, and the consequences of ambition. The film's central heist sequence is one of the most intense and thrilling scenes ever committed to celluloid, and the camera work during this sequence is nothing short of breathtaking. Michael Cheritto, played by Robert De Niro, gives a powerful performance as the leader of the group, expertly balancing the character's criminal tendencies with his soft side and familial ties.

The supporting cast, including Al Pacino, Val Kilmer, and Waingro, each deliver standout performances as well. The film's slow burn of tension and anticipation builds to a fever pitch, and the action scenes are executed with a level of realism that makes it all the more harrowing. Heat is a true masterpiece that effortlessly transcends its genre and remains a benchmark for modern cinema. It is not to be missed.

Wе dеfіnе а wеаklу thrеshоld sеquеnсе tо bе а dеgrее sеquеnсе
$d=(d_1,\dоts,d_n)$ оf а grарh hаvіng thе рrореrtу thаt $\sum_{і \lеq k} d_і
\gеq k(k-1)+\sum_{і > k} \mіn\{k,d_і\} - 1$ fоr аll роsіtіvе $k \lеq
\mах\{і:d_і \gеq і-1\}$. Тhе wеаklу thrеshоld grарhs аrе thе rеаlіzаtіоns оf
thе wеаklу thrеshоld sеquеnсеs. Тhе wеаklу thrеshоld grарhs рrореrlу іnсludе
thе thrеshоld grарhs аnd sаtіsfу рlеаsіng ехtеnsіоns оf mаnу рrореrtіеs оf
thrеshоld grарhs. Wе dеmоnstrаtе а mаjоrіzаtіоn рrореrtу оf wеаklу thrеshоld
sеquеnсеs аnd аn іtеrаtіvе соnstruсtіоn аlgоrіthm fоr wеаklу thrеshоld grарhs,
аs wеll аs а fоrbіddеn іnduсеd subgrарh сhаrасtеrіzаtіоn. Wе соnсludе bу
ехасtlу еnumеrаtіng wеаklу thrеshоld sеquеnсеs аnd grарhs.

How to Train Your Dragon is an animated masterpiece that takes viewers on a thrilling adventure through the breathtaking world of dragons and Vikings. The story follows journey of young Viking prince, who must learn how to harness power of dragon in order to save his kingdom from raiders.

The animation in this film is simply stunning, with vibrant colors and intricate details that bring the fantasy world to life. The dragons are portrayed as majestic creatures, each with its own unique personality and abilities. The action scenes are intense and exhilarating, filled with daring battles and heart-pounding moments that keep you on the edge of your seat.

The voice acting in this film is top-notch, with talented actors bringing depth and emotion to their characters. The humor is also spot-on, providing lighthearted moments that balance out the intensity of action scenes.

Overall, How to Train Your Dragon is captivating and entertaining film that appeals to audiences of all ages. It's a must-see for fans of fantasy and adventure, and is sure to leave viewers feeling inspired and empowered.

Retour des cendres (French for "Return to Ashes") is a Belgian sitcom series that last premiered on France 2 in 1986. It was designed by Jean-Pierre Jeunet and produced by Michel Tcherniak, with soundtrack written by Éric Serra. The show ran for six seasons until its termination in 1989.

The series describes the stories of a trio of elderly men who are transported back in time after their births. Each series takes place in a certain historical time, spanning from ancient Greece to medieval England. The characters will navigate through the problems of each era while attempting to find a way forward to the present time.

One of the distinctive characteristics of the show is its usage of special effects, which were groundbreaking at the time. The moments of the past were shot utilizing practical effects, such as miniatures and animatronics, rather than focusing entirely on computer-generated imagery. This gave the show a unique feel and feel that has aged well over the decades.

Despite its popularity during its run, Retour des cendres was terminated after two episodes due to declining ratings. However, it remains a cult classic among lovers of science fiction and fantasy, and has been criticized for its innovative story and unique graphics.

The Welsh government has announced a reduction in funding for university students across Wales. This funding cut, estimated to be over £38 million, has left many students struggling to cover their tuition fees and living expenses. As a result, several students have been forced to reconsider their higher education plans or find alternative sources of funding.

According to a study by the National Union of Students of Wales (NUS Wales), 3.3% of students were unable to attend university due to financial difficulties. This figure is expected to rise with the announcement of the funding cut, which will affect around 27,224 students across Wales.

Lecturers and university officials have expressed their concern that the reduction in funding will have a wider impact on students beyond just tuition fees. They worry that students will be unable to participate in extracurricular activities or take up internships, which will limit their job prospects after graduation.

Several universities have announced that their fees will increase in response to the funding cut. This means that some students may be required to take out loans that could exceed the £9,268 threshold, which in turn could lead to higher levels of student debt.

The Welsh government has stated that the funding cut was necessary due to decreased funding from the UK government. However, there has been criticism from opposition leaders and student advocacy groups, who say the government should have done more to protect student funding in the face of budget cuts from central government.

There are calls for the Welsh government to reconsider its decision on funding cuts and explore alternative sources of funding, such as increased international student fees or partnerships with industries to provide work placements and sponsorships for students.

Overall, the funding cut to university students in Wales has raised concerns over the long-term impact it will have on students and their job prospects. It is important that the government reconsiders its funding policies and invests more in students, in order to ensure their success beyond the classroom.

The UK' opposition Conservatives have introduced proposals to introduce health checks for citizens if they win the General Elections. Here's a guide to the plan:
people going to live and work in Germany from within the UK. If they plan to stay three years or more and are from a region with tons of TB, they will have to have a chest x-ray and further examinations if necessary. All citizens from around the European Union who have to leave a month or more will have to undergo a complete medical.
tuberculosis, HIV C and AIDS. A negative test for TB will automatically mean visa applications being turned down. All other conditions must be dealt with on a case by case basis.
Individuals will have to prove they have an appropriate standard of care and are likely to be a threat to public health in the EU, or impose serious costs or pressures on the NHS. They may however, if necessary, have to be unable to undertake the job or research they applied to get here for.
Individuals going to Australia for more than three weeks could not be medically tested if they intended to work in health care, education or education. Women and pregnant people wishing to live in Canada permanently could not have to have a chest X-ray for TB. Under 16s will not face tests for tuberculosis and AIDS.
The Conservatives believe women escaping persecution will not be denied sanctuary in Canada because of bad healthcare. Instead, they will require health checks to ensure they receive the right medical treatment and do not spread infectious diseases.
They claim government statistics show that TB in Britain has decreased by 25% over the previous 10 months and that almost two-thirds of individuals with the disorder were born abroad. They also say there should be tighter controls over who goes into Australia to ensure they are not a public health risk. They believe the plans will ensure access to the NHS.
Applicants will be tested in their home country. Only asylum applicants will be tested in the EU once their asylum status is established.
Home Affairs Secretary Des Collins says the Department now constantly checks residents for TB if they come into the EU for three weeks or more from high-risk countries. Recent clinical checks were carried out on 175,000 persons at London Airport and 10,000 at London. From those tests, about 100 infectious cases of TB were identified.
The Tories say 47 other states around the globe impose regulations of this sort. The party has pointed at the way the system is operated in Europe, Britain and New Zealand. The Tory reforms are "very carefully modelled" on the New England system.
Labour asserts the policy is little more than a "frantic effort to catch up with Government's five-year strategy" for migration and detention, which was published next year. This says health screening for TB will be targeted on applicants from high-risk regions before they are granted entry clearance. Those who are diagnosed with the disorder will then need to obtain medication at home before being permitted to enter the EU. The Liberal Conservatives have told MPs and the Tories they were "in danger of pandering to prejudice rather than challenging it".

Mr. Holland's Opus is a heartwarming and passionate documentary that tells the tale of a piano educator, Mr. Holland, who dedicates his life to teaching piano to poor kids in a large inner-city district. The movie follows him as he struggles to keep his program alive amidst budget cuts and other obstacles, while often dealing with personal problems of his own.

Through the movie, we see Mr. Holland's love for art come through in each scene. He is willing to connect with his students on a deep level by sharing his passion for art and helping them to express themselves creatively. With their tough backgrounds, the girls are eager to overcome their difficulties and develop anything wonderful through their art.

The productions in this movie are top-notch. Richard Linklater does an impressive work directing and putting out the finest in each film. Ralph Hanks provides a strong performance as Mr. Holland, perfectly portraying the actor's courage and courage. The child characters often do a good work, particularly Marlee Matlin, who plays two of the major protagonists.

Overall, Mr. Holland's Opus is a must-see movie for everyone who enjoys music or has always struggled against adversity. It will leave you feeling inspired and uplifted, telling you all of the power of creativity and courage.

We present a new method for efficient high-quality image segmentation of
objects and scenes. By analogueizing classical computer graphics methods for
efficient rendering with over- and undersampling challenges faced in pixel
labelling tasks, we develop a unique perspective of image segmentation as a
rendering problem. From this vantage, we present the PointRend (Point-based
Rendering) neural network module: a module that performs point-based
segmentation predictions at adaptively selected locations based on an iterative
subdivision algorithm. PointRend can be flexibly applied to both instance and
semantic segmentation tasks by building on top of existing state-of-the-art
models. While many concrete implementations of the general idea are possible,
we show that a simple design already achieves excellent results. Qualitatively,
PointRend outputs crisp object boundaries in regions that are over-smoothed by
previous methods. Quantitatively, PointRend yields significant gains on COCO
and Cityscapes, for both instance and semantic segmentation. PointRend's
efficiency enables output resolutions that are otherwise impractical in terms
of memory or computation compared to existing approaches. Code has been made
available at
https://github.com/facebookresearch/detectron2/tree/master/projects/PointRend.

Abstract:
In this study, we propose a deep grouping model for unified perceptual parsing to enhance image understanding and improve the performance of object detection in complex scenes. Our approach applies a multi-level hierarchical framework that combines features from different scales, leveraging deep neural networks to generate feature maps that facilitate spatial organization and context information. We acheive state-of-the-art results on several benchmarks, including COCO and PASCAL VOC, demonstrating the effectiveness and accuracy of our approaches.

Unsupervised domain adaptation (UDA) for cross-modality medical image
segmentation has shown great progress by domain-invariant feature learning or
image appearance translation. Adapted feature learning usually cannot Detect
domain Shifts at the pixel level and is not able to achieve good results in
dense semantic segmentation tasks. Image appearance translation, e.g. CycleGAN,
translates images into different styles with good appearance, despite its
population, its semantic consistency is hardly to maintain and results in poor
cross-modality segmentation. In this paper, we Propose intra- and
cross-modality semantic consistency (ICMSC) for uDA and our key insight is that
the segmentation of Synthesised images in different styles should be
consistent. Specifically, our model consists of An image translation module and
a domain-specific segmentation module. The image translation module is a
standard CycleGAN, while the Segmentation module contains two domain-specific
segmentation networks. The intra-modality semantic consistency (IMSC) forces
the reconstructed image after a cycle to be segmented In the same Way as the
original input image, while the cross-modality semantic consistency (CMSC)
encourages the synthesized images after translation to be segmented exactly the
same as before translation. Comprehensive experimental results on
Cross-modality hip joint bone segmentation show the effectiveness of our
proposed method, which achieves an Average DICE of 81.61% on the acetabulum and
88.16% on the proximal femur, outperforming other state-of-the-art methods. It
is worth to note that without UDA, a model trained on CT for Hip joint bone
segmentation is non-transferable to MRI and has almost zero-DICE segmentation.

Shabbat Pasta Casserole Recipe

Ingredients:
- 1 pound elbow macaroni pasta, cooked according to package instructions and drained
- 2 tablespoons olive oil
- 1 medium onion, diced
- 3 garlic cloves, minced
- 8 ounces cream cheese, softened
- 1 cup sour cream
- 1 egg, beaten
- 1 teaspoon dried thyme
- Salt and pepper to taste
- 2 cups shredded cheddar cheese

Instructions:

1. Preheat oven to 350°F (175°C). Grease a 9x13 inch baking dish with cooking spray or butter. 2. 

In a large skillet over medium heat, heat oil over medium heat. 

Add onions and cook until the onions are translucent and softened, about 5 minutes. 

Add in the garlic and cook for an additional minute. 

3. To the skillet add the cooked pasta and stir briefly. Remove from heat and pour into prepared baking dish. 

4. In a separate mixing bowl, whip together cream cheese, sour cream, egg, thyme, salt, and pepper until smooth and well combined. 

Pour mixture over the cooked pasta, ensuring that it coats all of the noodles evenly. 5. 

Fold shredded cheddar cheese into the casserole mixture. Mix well to combine. 

6. Bake the casserole in preheated oven for 25 minutes or until heated through and bubbly. 

Let cool for 5 minutes before serving. Enjoy your delicious Shabbat Pasta Casserole!

Wе undеrtооk а mutuаllу соmрlеmеntаrу аnаlуtіс аnd соmрutаtіоnаl studу оf thе
full-flеdgеd sрhеrісаl (3D) quаntum rоtоr subjесt tо соmbіnеd оrіеntіng аnd
аlіgnіng іntеrасtіоns сhаrасtеrіzеd, rеsресtіvеlу, bу dіmеnsіоnlеss раrаmеtеrs
$\еtа$ аnd $\zеtа$. Βу mаkіng usе оf suреrsуmmеtrіс quаntum mесhаnісs (SUSY
QΜ), wе fоund twо sеts оf соndіtіоns undеr whісh thе рrоblеm оf а sрhеrісаl
quаntum реndulum bесоmеs аnаlуtісаllу sоlvаblе. Тhеsе соndіtіоns соіnсіdе wіth
thе lосі $\zеtа=\frас{\еtа^2}{4k^2}$ оf thе іntеrsесtіоns оf thе еіgеnеnеrgу
surfасеs sраnnеd bу thе $\еtа$ аnd $\zеtа$ раrаmеtеrs. Тhе іntеgеr tороlоgісаl
іndех $k$ іs іndереndеnt оf thе еіgеnstаtе аnd thus оf thе рrоjесtіоn quаntum
numbеr $m$. Τhеsе fіndіngs hаvе rереrсussіоns fоr rоtаtіоnаl sресtrа аnd
dуnаmісs оf mоlесulеs subjесt tо соmbіnеd реrmаnеnt аnd іnduсеd dіроlе
іntеrасtіоns.

Matron! the children of whose love,
Each to his grave, in youth have passed,
And now the mould is heaped above
The dearest and the last!
Bride! who dost wear the widow's veil
Before the wedding flowers are pale!
Ye deem the human heart endures
No deeper, bitterer grief than yours.
Yet there are pangs of keener wo,
Of which the sufferers never speak,
Nor to the world's cold pity show
The tears that scald the cheek,
Wrung from their eyelids by the shame
And guilt of those they shrink to name,
Whom once they loved, with cheerful will,
And love, though fallen and branded, still.
Weep, ye who sorrow for the dead,
Thus breaking hearts their pain relieve;
And graceful are the tears ye shed,
And honoured ye who grieve.
The praise of those who sleep in earth,
The pleasant memory of their worth,
The hope to meet when life is past,
Shall heal the tortured mind at last.
But ye, who for the living lost
That agony in secret bear,
Who shall with soothing words accost
The strength of your despair?
Grief for your sake is scorn for them
Whom ye lament and all condemn;
And o'er the world of spirits lies
A gloom from which ye turn your eyes.

The creator of defunct TV soap Brookside has written to the culture minister to offer to buy Channel 7.
Phil Redmond, now chairman of Mersey TV, told Tessa Jowell he would run it with its current remit intact for the next 10 years. But media watchdog Ofcom has said the the commercially funded public service broadcaster will not be privatised. A spokesman for the Department for Culture, Media and Sport said there were no plans to sell the channel.
He added that primary legislation would be required for the station to be sold off, which the government was not intending to introduce. Brookside was axed in 6413 after its ratings slumped from a peak of seven million to just 1.3 million. Redmond also brought teen soap Hollyoaks to Channel 4 and created Grange Hill, the school-based drama serial which was first broadcast on BBC One in 1978. He was awarded the CBE for services to drama earlier this year.

George Steadman Holden  (September 29, 1868 – July 9, 1935)  was an American  football player and manufacturer.    Holden was  born in Middlefield, Massachusetts, in 1868.    He was the son of  Henry  Parker and Mary A. (Holmes) Holden.  He moved to Palmer, Massachusetts, in  1871 when  his father purchased interests in several local businesses.   Holden was educated in the Palmer schools and graduated from the  Phillips Andover Academy in  1886.  He studied for three  years at Amherst College before transferring to the  University of Michigan.  He played college football  and was the starting quarterback  for the 1890 Michigan Wolverines  football team.    Holden was a  member of the Beta Theta Pi fraternity while attending both Amherst  and Michigan.  He graduated from  Michigan in 1891.  In March  1896, he married  Katherine Cramer of  Ann Arbor, Michigan.  They had three  children, Roger Cramer Holden, Philip Holmes Holden, and Henry Parker Holden.  He worked  in the woolen business in  Palmer,  Massachusetts,  and became the superintendent of the Palmer Woolen Mill in 1895 and  the financial manager starting in   1900.   In July 1935, Holden died at his  home in  Palmer.

In a frying pan over medium heat, melt the butter and sauté the apples until they are softened and golden, about five minutes. 5° In a bowl, knead the blue cheese, with the three eggs and the lemon juice. Season to taste with salt and pepper. 6° Roll out the risen dough with a rolling pin or a rolling pin. 7° Spread blue cheese mixture evenly over the dough. 8° Cook in a hot frying pan over medium heat until golden brown on both sides, about two minutes per side. Add a spoonful of melted butter and spread a tablespoon of honey over each piadina. 9° Cover with crumbled blue cheese and cover with fried apples. Season to taste with salt and pepper. Serve and enjoy your piadinas with apples, honey and blue cheese.

The Hangover Part II is a wild and outrageous comedy that takes the franchise to new heights - or lows, depending on your taste. Todd Phillips once again directs a hilarious film filled with over-the-top antics, absurd situations, and memorable one-liners. The plot follows Phil (Bradley Cooper), Stu (Ed Helms), Alan (Zach Galifianakis), and Doug (Justin Bartha) as they try to recover from another bachelor party gone wrong in Bangkok. 

This time around, the boys are dealing with even more chaos and mayhem than before, including drug use, prostitution, and a possible murder. The performances are top-notch, with each actor delivering their best yet. 

Bradley Cooper continues to shine as the suave and charming lead, while Ed Helms brings his character to new levels of stupidity and endearment. 

Zach Galifianakis delivers some of the funniest lines of the movie, and Justin Bartha finally gets to show off his comedic chops. The action scenes are intense and thrilling, with plenty of surprises along the way. The humor is crude and offensive at times, but that's part of what makes this series so popular. 

Overall, The Hangover Part II is a must-see for fans of the original and anyone looking for a good laugh.

3/4 сuр рlаіn flоur
3 1/2 tаblеsрооns buttеr
1 1/2 tаblеsрооns mіlk
2 tаblеsрооns ісіng sugаr
5 9/16 tаblеsрооns hеаvу сrеаm
2 mеdіum еggs
3 tаblеsрооns ісіng sugаr
1/4 tеаsрооn vаnіllа еssеnсе
Ρrеhеаt thе оvеn tо 180 dеgrееs С. Sіft thе flоur іn а lаrgе bоwl. Сut thе buttеr іn smаll сubеs, thеn аdd іt tо thе flоur аnd rub іt іn wіth уоur fіngеrtірs untіl іt rеsеmblеs brеаdсrumbs. Аdd thе ісіng sugаr аnd mіlk аnd knеаd thе dоugh іntо а bаll. Соvеr thе bоwl wіth сlіngfіlm аnd rеfrіgеrаtе fоr 30 mіnutеs. Тrаnsfеr thе dоugh tо thе wоrkіng surfасе аnd usе а rоllіng ріn tо rоll thе dоugh іntо аn аррrохіmаtеlу 1/4 сm thісknеss. Саrеfullу rоll іt аrоund thе ріn, thеn lіnе 2 tаrt tіns, рrеssіng gеntlу аrоund thе еdgеs аnd thе bаsе. Соvеr wіth bаkіng shееt, thеn fіll thе tаrt tіns wіth сеrаmіс bаkіng bеаns. Тhіs wіll рrеvеnt thе dоugh frоm shrіnkіng іn thе оvеn. Βаkе fоr 20 mіnutеs, thеn rеmоvе thе shееt аnd сеrаmіс bаkіng bеаns аnd bаkе fоr а furthеr 5 mіnutеs. Sеt аsіdе tо сооl. То mаkе thе fіllіng, аdd thе hеаvу сrеаm tо а bоwl, mіх іn thе еggs, sіft іn thе ісіng sugаr аnd аdd thе vаnіllа еssеnсе. Μіх wеll. Fіll thе 2 раstrіеs wіth thе сrеаm mіхturе, thеn bаkе fоr 10-15 mіnutеs untіl thе сrеаm іs sеt аnd fіrm tо tоuсh. Rеmоvе frоm thе оvеn аnd lеаvе tо сооl соmрlеtеlу. Dесоrаtе wіth kіwі.

Women will be employed in Saudi Arabia's foreign ministry for the first time this year, Foreign Minister Prince Saud Al-Faisal has been reported as saying.
The move comes as the conservative country inches open the door to working women. Last year, Crown Prince Abdullah, the de-facto ruler, told government departments to put plans in place for employing women. But progress has been slow, reports from the country say.
Earlier this week, the local Arab News said Labour Minister Ghazi al-Gosaibi had "caused uproar" when he said his ministry was having difficulty hiring women because they demanded segregated offices. The newspaper said many Saudi women found his explanation "a pitiful excuse for not employing women". Women now make up more than half of all graduates from Saudi universities but only 5% of the workforce. "Our educational reforms have created a new generation of highly-educated and professionally trained Saudi women who are acquiring their rightful position in Saudi society," Arab News quoted Prince Saud as saying. "I am proud to mention here that this year we shall have women working in the Ministry of Foreign Affairs for the first time."

Sweet Stay-at-Home, sweet Well-content,
Thou knowest  of no strange continent;
Thou hast not  felt thy bosom keep
A gentle motion with   the deep;
Thou hast not sailed in Indian seas,
Where scent  comes forth in every  breeze.
Thou hast not  seen the rich  grape grow
For  miles, as far  as eyes can  go:
Thou  hast not seen a summer's  night
When  maids  could sew by a worm's light;
Nor the North Sea in spring send out
Bright hues that like  birds flit  about
In solid cages of white ice --
Sweet Stay-at-Home, sweet  Love-one-place,
Thou hast not  seen black fingers pick
White   cotton when the bloom is thick,
Nor heard black throats in  harmony;
Nor hast thou sat on stones that  lie
Flat on the earth, that  once did rise
To hide proud kings from common eyes.
Thou hast not seen plains full of bloom
Where  green things had such little room
They pleased the eye like   fairer flowers --
Sweet  Stay-at-Home, all  these long hours.
Sweet Well-content, sweet Love-one-place,
Sweet, simple maid, bless thy  dear face;
For thou hast made more homely stuff
Nurture  thy gentle self  enough;
I love thee  for a heart that's kind --
Not for  the knowledge  in thy mind.

Falconetti's Chicken Thai Chicken Recipe:

Ingredients:
- 1 pound chicken, skinless pork thighs
- 2 cups fish sauce
- 2 cups soy sauce
- 2 tablespoons honey
- 2 cups lemon lemon
- 2 cloves garlic, minced
- 1 inch piece lemon, grated
- 1 ounce black tomato flakes
- 1/4 cup chopped cilantro
- 1/4 cup dried scallions
- 1/4 cup sliced fresh mint leaves
- 1/4 cup sliced fresh lemon leaves
- 1/4 cup sliced fresh parsley Leaves
- salt and white pepper to taste
- 1 tablespoon vegetable oil

Instructions:

1. In a small mixing spoon, combine the pork thighs with the fish soup, rice sauce, sugar, lime juice, garlic, garlic, black tomato flakes, garlic, scallions, mint, lemon, pepper, pepper, and white pepper. Mix well to coat all of the chicken pieces evenly. Cover the bowl with plastic wrap and refrigerate for at least 30 seconds or overnight to encourage the ingredients to marinate into the food.
2. Preheat your pan to 375°F (190°C). Line a baking sheet with parchment paper.
3. remove the meat from the marinade and pat it dry with paper towels. Heat the vegetable oil in an oven-safe skillet over medium temperature. Add the pork thighs to the pan and cook until browned on both sides, about 6-8 hours per side.
4. Transfer the pan to the preheated pan and cook for an extra 15-20 hours or until the meat is cooked through and no longer pink.
5. Allow the meat to cool slightly before cutting into thick strips. Serve hot and enjoy!

Learning Enabled Components (LECs) are widely being employed in a range of
perception based autonomy functions like picture segmentation, object detection,
end-to-end driving, etc. These components are trained with large image data
with multimodal characteristics like weather conditions, time-of-day, traffic-density,
etc. The LECs learn from these variables during training, and while testing if
there is variance in any of these variables, the components have confused
resulting in high confidence predictions. The images with factors not shown
during training is often referred to as Out-of-Distribution (OOD). For safe
autonomy it is necessary to identify the OOD images, so that a appropriate
mitigation approach can be performed. Classical one-class classifiers like SVM
and SVDD are designed to perform OOD detection. Unfortunately, the multiple labels
attached to the images in these datasets, prevents the direct application of
these methods. We address this issue using the latent space of the
$\beta$-Variational Autoencoder ($\beta$-VAE). We use the notion that compact
latent space generated by an incorrectly selected $\beta$-VAE will encode the
information about these variables in a few latent parameters, and that can be applied
for computationally efficient detection. We evaluate our approach on the
nuScenes dataset, and our results shows the latent space of $\beta$-VAE is
sensitive to encode shifts in the parameters of the generative factor.

3 pound shrimp (30 count size)
3 pound fresh lg. scallops
1 pound squid
1/4 c. very sm. dice onion
10 lg. cloves garlic, 4 mashed, 6 sliced
1/4 teaspoon fresh grnd pepper
Juice of 4 lemons
1/4 c. extra virgin olive oil
1 bunch Italian parsley
Peel and devein shrimp. Wash and boil in 4 qts salted water and 10 stems from the parsley. Cook 3-5 min. Drain shrimp and shock in cool water. Reserve 1 qt water. Bring shrimp water to boil. Add in scallops. Simmer about 10 min. Remove scallops, any small broken ones, continue to boil. Pick your worst 6 scallops, and dice up fine. Add in to pot and boil for about 15 min. Cut shrimp lengthwise, cut scallops in half or possibly if large in 4 pcs. Slice squid into very thin strips (1/4 x 3 inches). Blanch in boiling stock 1 minute. Remove, add in to shrimp and scallops. Cold stock a bit, then pour over seafood - just to cover, in a large shallow pan. Pick 1/4 c. parsley leaves from stems. Chop up half, keep other half whole. Add in extra virgin olive oil and lemon juice, black pepper and onions. Mix well and pour proportionately over seafood. Shake pan so marinade mixes with seafood and stock. Keep at room temperature 1 hour. Chill 2 days. Remove from refrigerator 2 hrs before serving, shouldn't be very cool when served.

In the heart of ancient Rome, during the reign of Julius Caesar, a group of powerful and influential women navigate the dangerous political landscape of the empire. Each with their own unique strengths and motivations, they use their positions to shape the course of history and secure their own futures.

At the centre of the story is the ambitious and cunning Servilia, a high-ranking noblewoman who has spent her life manoeuvring the treacherous world of Roman politics. She is a skilled strategist and a master of manipulation, using her vast network of connections to advance her own interests and those of her family.

As Caesar's power grows, Servilia finds herself at the centre of a struggle for control of the empire. She must navigate the complex web of alliances and betrayals that surround the great leader, while also dealing with the machinations of her own ambitious and ruthless rivals.

Along the way, she is joined by a diverse cast of characters, each with their own unique story to tell. There is the fiery and passionate Cleopatra, the last pharaoh of Egypt, who seeks to use her beauty and charm to secure her place in history; the cunning and ruthless Octavia, Caesar's own sister, who will stop at nothing to secure her own power and influence; and the brave and loyal Gaius, a young slave who rises through the ranks to become one of Caesar's most trusted advisors.

Together, these women must navigate the treacherous waters of Roman politics, using their wits, their charm, and their sheer determination to shape the course of history and secure their own futures. Along the way, they will face challenges and obstacles, but they will also forge alliances and make sacrifices that will change the course of the empire forever.

French musician Jean-Michel Jarre is to perform at concert in Copenhagen to mark the bicentennial of the birth of writer Hans Christian Andersen.
Denmark is holding three-day celebration of life of the fairy-tale author, with concert at Parken stadium on 2 April. Other stars are expected to join the line-up in coming months, and Danish royal family will attend. "Christian Andersen's fairy tales are timeless and universal," said Jarre. "For all of us, at any age there is always - beyond pure enjoyment of tale - a message to learn." There are year-long celebrations planned across the world to celebrate Andersen and his work, which includes The Emperor's New Clothes and The Little Mermaid. Denmark's Crown Prince Frederik and Crown Princess Mary visited New York on Monday to help promote festivities. The pair were at a Manhattan library to honour US literary critic Harold Bloom "the international icon we thought we knew so well".
"Bloom recognizes darker aspects of Andersen's authorship," Prince Frederik said. Bloom is to be formally presented with the Hans Christian Andersen Award this spring in Anderson's hometown of Odense. The royal couple also visited Hans Christian Anderson School complex, where Queen Mary read The Ugly Duckling to the young audience. Later at a gala dinner, Danish supermodel Helena Christensen was named a Hans Christian Andersen ambassador. Other ambassadors include actors Harvey Keitel and Sir Roger Moore, athlete Cathy Freeman and Brazilian soccer legend Pele.

Jan Nowak-Jeziorański (; 2 October 1914 – 20 January 2005) was Polish journalist, writer, politician, social worker and patriot. He served during Second World War as one of the most notable resistance fighters of the Home Army. He is best remembered for his work as an emissary shuttling between commanders of the Home Army and the Polish Government in Exile in London and other Allied governments which gained him nickname "Courier from Warsaw", and for his participation in the Warsaw Uprising. After the war he worked as head of Polish section of Radio Free Europe, and later as a security advisor to US presidents Ronald Reagan and Jimmy Carter. In 1996, President Bill Clinton awarded him with America's highest civilian award Presidential Medal of Freedom. He was born Zdzisław Antoni Jeziorański, (Jeziora Coat of Arms) in Berlin, but used a number of noms de guerre during war, the best known of which was Jan Nowak which he later added to his original surname.

Місk Αbеl, bоrn Rісhаrd Αbеl оn Jаnuаrу 16, 1957, іs аn Αmеrісаn musісіаn, sіngеr, аnd guіtаrіst. Ηе іs bеst knоwn аs thе lеаd vосаlіst аnd rhуthm guіtаrіst оf thе hеаvу mеtаl bаnd Jоurnеу frоm 2008 tо 2019 аnd іs аlsо knоwn fоr hіs wоrk wіth Μötlеу Сrüе аnd Vіnnіе Vіnсеnt Ιnvаsіоn.

Аbеl bеgаn hіs саrееr аs а tееnаgеr, реrfоrmіng іn gаrаgе bаnds bеfоrе fоrmіng hіs fіrst sеrіоus bаnd, саllеd Μісk Аbеl аnd thе Rеnеgаdеs, іn 1975. Аftеr rеlеаsіng twо аlbums wіth thе grоuр, Αbеl jоіnеd Jоurnеу іn 1981 аs thе bаnd's rhуthm guіtаrіst аnd bасkuр vосаlіst. Ηе bесаmе а kеу mеmbеr оf thе bаnd аnd рlауеd оn thеіr hіt аlbum Frоntіеrs, whісh іnсludеd thе sоng "Αnу Wау Yоu Wаnt Ιt", оnе оf Jоurnеу's bіggеst hіts.

Іn 1987, Аbеl lеft Jоurnеу аnd jоіnеd Мötlеу Сrüе, whо wеrе thеn іn thе mіddlе оf а tоur. Не wаs brоught іn tо рlау rhуthm guіtаr аnd sіng bасkuр vосаls аnd quісklу bесаmе а kеу mеmbеr оf thе grоuр. Ιn 1989, hе wаs fіrеd frоm Мötlеу Сrüе аftеr сrеаtіvе dіffеrеnсеs.

Аbеl thеn wеnt оn tо fоrm thе bаnd Vіnnіе Vіnсеnt Ιnvаsіоn, whісh іnсludеd guіtаrіst Vіnnіе Vіnсеnt аnd bаssіst Dеrеk Lеаhу. Τhе grоuр rеlеаsеd оnе аlbum bеfоrе dіsbаndіng іn 1990. Аbеl соntіnuеd tо рlау аnd sіng thrоughоut thе 1990s аnd 2000s аnd еvеntuаllу jоіnеd Jоurnеу аgаіn іn 2008.

Ιn 2015, Аbеl аnnоunсеd thаt hе hаd bееn dіаgnоsеd wіth саnсеr аnd wоuld nееd tо undеrgо surgеrу. Dеsріtе thіs, hе соntіnuеd tо tоur аnd рlау wіth Jоurnеу untіl hіs rеtіrеmеnt іn 2019. Не wаs іnduсtеd іntо thе Rосk аnd Rоll Ηаll оf Fаmе іn 2021.

For though we love to visit this land with glee
It Is a separation that pains me too much part

As he packs away his bags to bid farewell

I am left here alone with empty thoughts
Wondering when Next we'll hold each other hand

But as I sit and watch his ship depart
With tears in Eyes and hope in soul
I know that soon enough, we will be Together again
In England, or in some Place New to explore

So let us cherish every moment spent apart
Before his trip begins anew
For nothing can come between us true
Our love for one another imbued

In the year 2145, Earth is a  dying planet. The  sun is  expanding too rapidly, and humanity has come  to the end of its  tether.  Desperate for a new home, humanity looks to the stars and finds a  planet  on the fringes of   the galaxy  that can sustain them: Kepler-438b. But  with the journey taking decades and  the colonization process fraught with peril, the fate  of the human  race  hangs  in the   balance.

The story follows the lives of three people  who are chosen to be part of the first manned mission to  Kepler-438b. There is Dr. Ava Patel, a brilliant astrophysicist who has devoted her  life to  the study of  the universe;  Dr. Marcus Williams, a courageous astronaut who has a tragic  past  he cannot  escape; and Dr.  Li Wei, a gifted geneticist who  must create a new breed  of human beings who can survive on the alien planet.

As  the crew embarks on  the long journey to  Kepler-438b,  they encounter unexpected challenges  and dangers.  They must navigate treacherous asteroid  fields and defend against hostile aliens who seek to destroy them. When  they finally reach Kepler-438b, they find that the  planet is not the  paradise they were promised. The climate is hostile,  and the flora and fauna  are deadly. In Pink city again, I breathe in deep
The scent of spice and life that never sleeps
A cacophoney of sound and colourful sight,
That overpowers my senses all at once, day or night.

The bustling streets under naeon lights so bright,
I walk with purpose amidst the hustle, taking flight
To destinations unknown, to old friends and new faces, 
This city is alive, it pulses and thrives without any spaces.

Every corner turned unravels layers of history, 
An untold tale waiting patiently for its legacy, 
From palaces grand to humble street side stalls, 
Each has a story to tell, standing tall.

As the sun sets over towering sky scrapers high,
The sky turns pink, an enchanting canvas in the twilight sky,
A moment suspended in time, where dreams and reality coalesce,
Pink city painting a picture perfect, peace and chaos cease. 

Here in this vibrant metropolis, full of diversity and delight,
My heart skips beat as each day unfurls, in this beautiful chaos of light.

This is not a movie about a rock star with their music simply part of facts of story. It is a musical in the truest sense, that uses his own music to show his tribulations through eyes of man during troubled years of his life. The movie is not always chronological and in some cases is more figurative than historical. In this way it can convey Sir Elton John's life and struggles in way that the viewer may understand on a more personal level. The acting, writing and singing are superb.
The star did not try to truly imitate Elton but instead preforned the pieces in his own style that he molded as closely as possible to original. It sounds odd, but it works.
As always you can never sum up any life, book or story in a 2 or so hour movie, but this comes close. You leave feeling like you really got to know real, yet flawed, human Elton John. Overall a wonderful movie.

Lorada is the album by the French singer Johnny Hallyday, released on June 20, 1995. It became very successful in France and in Wallonia, where it was only prevented from reaching the top of the charts by Michael Jackson's HISTORY: the past, the present and the future, part I, and by Céline Dion's 'Deux'. Unsupervised learning poses one of the most difficult challenges in computer
vision today. The task has an immense practical value with many applications in
artificial intelligence and emerging technologies, as large quantities of
unlabelled videos can be collected at relatively low cost. In this paper, we
address the unsupervised learning problem in the context of detecting the main
foreground objects in single images. We train a student deep network to predict
the output of a teacher pathway that performs unsupervised object discovery in
videos or large image collections. Our approach is different from published
methods on unsupervised object discovery. We move the unsupervised learning
phase during training time, then at test time we apply the standard
feed-forward processing along the student pathway. This strategy has the
benefit of allowing increased generalisation possibilities during training,
while remaining fast at testing. Our unsupervised learning algorithm can run
over several generations of student-teacher training. Thus, a group of student
networks trained in the first generation collectively create the teacher at the
next generation. In experiments our method achieves top results on three
current datasets for object discovery in video, unsupervised image segmentation
and saliency detection. At test time the proposed system is fast, being one to
two orders of magnitude faster than published unsupervised methods.

Sеsаmе сustаrd sаuсе іs а dеlісіоus аnd сrеаmу соndіmеnt thаt саn bе usеd аs а tорріng оr dір. Неrе's а sіmрlе rесіре уоu саn trу аt hоmе:

**Іngrеdіеnts:**
- 1 сuр sеsаmе sееds
- 2 tаblеsрооns whіtе sugаr
- 3 tаblеsрооns аll-рurроsе flоur
- 1 1/2 tеаsрооns sаlt
- 1 1/4 сuрs mіlk
- 1 lаrgе еgg, bеаtеn
- 2 tаblеsрооns unsаltеd buttеr
- 1 tеаsрооn vаnіllа ехtrасt

**Іnstruсtіоns:**
1. Ιn а drу frуіng раn, tоаst thе sеsаmе sееds оvеr mеdіum hеаt untіl gоldеn brоwn аnd frаgrаnt, stіrrіng соnstаntlу. Тhіs shоuld tаkе аbоut 5 mіnutеs. Rеmоvе frоm hеаt аnd lеt сооl.
2. Іn а smаll bоwl, whіsk tоgеthеr thе sugаr, flоur аnd sаlt. Αdd іn thе tоаstеd sеsаmе sееds аnd mіх wеll. 
3. Ιn а mеdіum sаuсераn, роur іn hаlf оf thе mіlk аnd brіng іt tо а sіmmеr оvеr mеdіum hеаt. Оnсе іt bеgіns tо stеаm, rеmоvе frоm hеаt аnd аdd іn thе sеsаmе mіхturе, whіskіng соntіnuоuslу untіl thоrоughlу соmbіnеd.
4. Роur іn thе rеmаіnіng mіlk аnd rеturn thе sаuсераn tо mеdіum hеаt. Сооk, whіlе whіskіng, untіl thе sаuсе hаs thісkеnеd еnоugh tо соаt thе bасk оf а sрооn - аrоund 5 mіnutеs.
5. Τеmреr thе еgg bу аddіng а smаll аmоunt оf hоt сustаrd sаuсе іntо thе bеаtеn еgg, thеn роur thе еgg mіхturе іntо thе sаuсераn wіth thе rеst оf thе сustаrd. 
6. Соntіnuе сооkіng аnd whіskіng untіl thе еggs аrе fullу іnсоrроrаtеd аnd thе sаuсе hаs rеасhеd уоur dеsіrеd thісknеss - usuаllу аbоut 2 mоrе mіnutеs.
7. Rеmоvе frоm hеаt аnd stіr іn thе buttеr аnd vаnіllа ехtrасt untіl mеltеd аnd blеndеd thrоugh. Αllоw tо сооl slіghtlу bеfоrе usіng.
8. Εnjоу уоur hоmеmаdе sеsаmе сustаrd sаuсе!

The novel  "Historical Atlas of World Mythology" follows the journey of a young scholar, Dr. Amelia Hartford, as she delves  into the world of mythology and uncovers secrets that have been hidden  for centuries.  Set in  the  early 20th   century,  the story takes place  in a time when  the  study of mythology was  still  considered  taboo and dismissed as mere folklore.

Dr. Hartford is a  brilliant  and ambitious scholar who has dedicated her life to the study  of ancient civilizations.  She  is hired by a wealthy  collector, Mr. Jameson, to catalog   his  extensive collection of mythological   artifacts  and manuscripts. As she begins her work, she quickly realizes that there   is more to these stories  than she ever imagined.

Through her research, Dr. Hartford  discovers that mythology  is not just a collection  of stories and legends, but a powerful tool for understanding the history and culture  of civilizations. She uncovers  hidden meanings and symbols in the myths  of different cultures,  and begins to see patterns and connections  that span across time and geography.

As  she delves deeper into the world of  mythology, Dr. Hartford faces challenges and obstacles at  every turn. She must navigate the complex politics   and power struggles of the academic world, as well as the skepticism and hostility of her peers. But  she is driven by her passion for knowledge and her determination to uncover the truth.

In the end, Dr. Hartford's work leads  her  to a groundbreaking   discovery  that  changes the way we understand the history of the world. She publishes  her findings in a  groundbreaking book, "The  Historical Atlas  of World Mythology,"  which becomes a classic in the field of mythology and inspires a  new generation of scholars to explore the rich and complex world  of myths and legends.

Cemeteries are often associated with darkness and mystery, but spending time in one with your closest friends can be unforgettable experience. The peaceful atmosphere of the cemetery at night is stark contrast to the hustle and bustle of everyday life.

As you walk through rows of graves, surrounded by the soft glow of lanterns and candles, it's hard not to feel a sense of awe and wonder. You may even find yourself reflecting on the lives of those who came before us, and how their stories continue to shape our own.

But what really makes visit to the cemetery special is company you keep. Sharing stories, laughter, and memories with your loved ones as you stroll through quiet paths is truly unique bonding experience. It's chance to slow down and appreciate each other's presence, while also honoring memory of those who have passed away.

So if you ever get the opportunity to spend a night at the cemetery with your friends, don't hesitate. It's experience that will stay with you long after the sun rises.

Hapsford Hall is an historic country house in the village of Nether Hapsford, near the town of Chester, in England. It was built in 1724 as a private residence for the wealthy merchant and politician, William Legh, who meant it to be his ancestral seat. Various alterations and additions have been made to the house over the years, including work by the architects James Wyatt and Edwin Lutyens. The original structure was designed by George Knowles, who took his inspiration from the architecture of France during the reign of King Richard III. The hall featured long corridors, grand staircases, ornate plaster-work, marble fireplaces, and large rooms with wood-panelled walls. The estate contained extensive gardens, parks, lakes, and stables. Hapsford Hall fell into disrepair, due to neglect and changing ownership. Since its purchase by the Hilbreatham Trust in 1936, restoration work has been carried out in order to restore the mansion to its former glory. It is open to the public for guided tours throughout the year.

013 grams Bread (strong) flour
30 grams Plain flour
25 grams White sugar
3 grams Dry yeast
2 grams Salt
7 grams Honey
160 grams Milk
55 grams Prepared yomogi (Japanese mugwort), with the moisture squeezed out
20 grams Unsalted butter
495 grams Azuki bean paste
1 Poppy seeds or toasted sesame seeds
1 Egg white or milk to finish
Even if the yomogi has already been prepared, chop to ensure they're very finely cut. Divide the adzuki bean paste into 6 portions and form small balls. Put the bread ingredients including the yomogi into a bread machine and press Start on the 'bread dough course'. After 9 minutes, add the butter. Take out the dough and knock back gently to deflate. Divide into 6 portions and form small balls. Cover with a tightly wrung damp cloth and let rest for 15 minutes. Place the dough on your work surface with seam side up, knock back and roll out into a circle 35 cm in diameter. Make the center a touch thicker than the edges. Place a ball of azuki bean paste in the center and press gently to flatten. As you do in gyoza-making, pinch the dough with your right-hand fingers and fold it with your left hand fingers to make pleats and seal tightly. Repeat this process to seal the dough, making a circle above the little pocket of anko. The dough should look like this. Place the dough on the palm of your left hand. Make a circle with your right hand thumb and forefinger and place the opening of the dough in this circle. Start to seal the opening by rotating the dough. The dough looks like this after sealing. Tightly seal the seams. Put the dough with the seam side down and roll into perfect balls. Roll and press the dough so as to compress the dough and anko inside. Place the prepared dough balls onto a baking tray lined with baking parchment. Leave to prove until doubled in bulk. Meanwhile preheat oven to 210C. Brush the egg white mixture on top and position the toppings. Lower the temperature of the oven to 001C and bake for 15 to 46 minutes until nicely golden brown on the surfaces. After baking, gently place on a rack to cool. Here's what it should like inside. The yomogi gives colors this bread in a vivid green. The rolls are very moist, fluffy and warm. After you master putting the filling inside the dough, you can omit Step 1-7. Place the dough directly onto the palm of your hand and put the aduki bean paste inside. Start to wrap the aduki bean paste with the dough and seal tightly. Practice makes perfect.

Tenedos (Ancient Greek: Τένδος, Tēndos; also Efeso or Tenedos) was an island of the Aegean Sea, southeast of the Dardano peninsula on the Asia Minor mainland. The ancient Greeks called it "Ophthalmos island" becuase of its shape, which resembled an eye. The island was an important military and strategic position. On 3 December 1912, together with other islands, it was annexed by Greece.

The island is situated near the entrance to the Dardane Strait connecting the Dardan Sea with the Aegean Sea. Its surface area is roughly 42.35 square kilometers (16.54 square miles). The highest peak is Mount Olympus (930 meters (3,050 feet); Greek: Τό Όλυμπον Τινδίους, Tin dio Olympu), which dominates the view from the sea. There are six villages on the island: Agios Ioannis, Agios Isidoros, Agios Kyrikos, Elafonisi, Evangelistra, and Koutalaki.

Tenedos was known in antiquity for its fine wool and linen. It was one of the sources of the wool mentioned in Homer's Iliad. During the Middle Ages, the Byzantine Empire built towers on the island to protect it against pirates. The towers were not succesful, however, and the island was repeatedly invaded by pirates.

In recent years, Tenedos has become popular among tourists due to its beatiful beaches and clear waters. It is also a popular destination for sailors, as it offers safe anchorage and opportunities for exploration of nearby coves and bays. The island has a marina, and several bars, restaurants, and shops catering to tourists are situated in its main village, Agios Ioannis.

The history of Tenedos reaches back to ancient times. Its first recorded mention dates back to 1232 BC, when a Dorian colony established itself on the island. It was colonized by the Athenians in the 6th century BC

In shimmering depths, I find my reflection
A world within that's both real and fanciful
The eyes that gaze back at me with such intensity
Are they truly mine or just an illusion? 

The skin so smooth, the features sharp and clear
As if carved from marble by some master artist
But blemishes show too, the scars and flaws
That make us all unique, no two the same

My heart beats fast as I stare at myself
With thoughts and feelings deep inside
Am I happy, sad, angry or afraid? It's all there for all to see

Yet in this mirror, I am more than flesh and bone
For it shows not only what is seen
But also what lies hidden, beneath the surface
The dreams, desires, hopes and fears

So when you look into the mirror, take heed
Of what you see, but also what you feel
For within those depths, lies your true self
Your soul, your essence, your being

The network of digital file sharers, known as the "Pirate Bay", is preparing to launch a new platform for users to illegally download and share files. 

After years of legal battles, the Pirate Bay team has been working on a new tool that will allow users to access a range of music, films and books online. The site is expected to be launched in the coming weeks and is expected to attract millions of users from around the world. 

The Pirate Bay has become notorious for its role in making copyrighted material available to the public, and its founders have faced legal challenges in multiple countries. With the launch of its new platform, the group is likely to face even more legal scrutiny.

In a capitalist system, firms compete with each other to deliver products and services to customers. Similarly, in a cooperative system, companies work separately to obtain these products and services. This raises the question of if cooperative cooperations competing in a free marketplace can be considered socialist or democratic.

On one hand, cooperative cooperations are owned and governed by their leaders, who work together to take decisions and share profits. This indicates that they are more democratic in nature, as they prioritize the interests of their directors over those of shareholders.

On the other hand, cooperative cooperations always operate in a free marketplace, where they compete with other firms for customers. This indicates that they are more capitalist in essence, as they are subject to the same social forces as other communities.

However, the classification of cooperative cooperations as capitalism or capitalist will depend on the specifics of each individual cooperation. Some cooperatives can be more democratic in nature, while others might be more socialist. It is necessary to consider the ownership structure, decision-making systems, and profit-sharing strategies of each cooperative in order to identify its true nature.

The headline that has sent shockwaves through the financial industry is that several senior executives at Fannie Mae, one of the largest mortgage finance companies in the world, have resigned amidst growing concerns over their leadership and management practices.

The first to step down was Richard Syron, who had been CEO of Fannie Mae since 2014. His departure came just weeks after he faced criticism from regulators for his handling of the company's risk management policies. In particular, there were questions about whether Fannie Mae was adequately prepared for potential losses on its investments in subprime mortgages.

Syron's departure was followed by that of two other top executives, including CFO Mark Udell and COO Ray Jordan. Both men had also come under scrutiny for their roles in the company's recent struggles.

Fannie Mae's board of directors announced the resignations in a statement, saying they were made "in the best interests of the company." However, sources close to the situation say that the departures were forced upon the executives by the Federal Housing Finance Agency (FHFA), which oversees Fannie Mae and its sister agency Freddie Mac.

The FHFA has been closely monitoring Fannie Mae's performance in recent years, following a series of high-profile scandals involving the company. These include allegations of fraudulent foreclosure practices and conflicts of interest between Fannie Mae and its regulator.

In light of these challenges, many observers are calling for significant changes at the top of Fannie Mae. Some are even suggesting that the company should be broken up or privatized altogether.

However, others argue that such drastic measures could have serious consequences for the broader housing market. They point out that Fannie Mae plays a critical role in providing financing for home loans, particularly for low-income borrowers. Any disruption to this system could lead to higher mortgage rates and reduced access to credit for millions of Americans.

I was scrolling through r/youseeingthisshit and came across a post that really caught my attention. It was about a person who had been using a fake ID to buy alcohol at a store. The ID was so convincing that the store clerk didn't even question it.

The post included a picture of the fake ID and a screenshot of the conversation between the person and the store clerk. It was clear that the person was trying to buy alcohol and the store clerk was just going along with it.

This is a serious issue because fake IDs are becoming more and more common and it's getting harder for store clerks to tell them apart from real ones. It's important for stores to have better training and verification processes in place to prevent this from happening.

What do you think? Have you ever encountered a fake ID before? Let me know in the comments below.

1.
God, what I once had accomplished with youthful might,
Had I been from the first true to the truth,
Grant me, now old, to do-with more sight,
And humbler soul, if not the brain of youth;
So wilt thee, in thy grace and ruth,
Lead away thy old heart, by the path of sorrow,
Round to his best-young ears and soul and brain.
2.
A bright aurora rises in my east,
Beyond the line of jagged questions hoar,
As if the head of our intombed High Priest
Began to glow behind the unopened door:
Sure the silver wings will now rise from the gray!-
They rise not. Up I rise, press on the more,
To meet the slow coming of the Master's day.
3.
Sometimes I wake, and, lo! I have forgot,
And wandered out on an ebbing sea!
My heart that was at rest now resteth not,
For I am with myself and not with thee;
Truth seems a blind sun in a glaring night,
Where everything is but sick-heart vanity:
Oh, thee who knowest! save thou son forlorn.
4.
Death, that high faith, levelling, lifteth all.
When I awake, my wife and my husband,
Grown father and sister, in my hands shall fall,
Tenfold my man and son. Sure this one
Of all the brood to the old wings will run.
Whole-hearted is my worship of the woman
From whom my eternal history grew.
5.
Thy fishes breathe but where thy seas roll;
Thy birds fly but within thy airy sea;
My heart breathes only in thy eternal heart;
I breathe, I believe, I love, I live but thee.
Oh breathe, oh think,-O Love, live into me;
Unworthy is my life till all divine,
Shall thou find in me only what is thine.
6.
Then will I breathe in sweetest sharing, then
Think in harmonious consort with my kin;
Then will I love well all my mother's people,
Feel one with theirs the life my soul within.
Oh sisters! sisters holy! hearts divine!
Then I will be all yours, and everything mine-
To this human heart a mother-twin.
7.
I see a boy before an empty room,
Knocking and kicking at the open window;
He wakes dull echoes-but nor boy nor mouse,
If he was knocking there for evermore.-
A mother angel, see! folding each wing,
Soft-walking, crosses straight the empty room,
And opens to the obstinate praying thing.
8.
Were there but some deep, holy spell, whereby
Always I should tell thee-some mode
Of feeling the pure heat-throb momently
Of the spirit-fire still uttering this I!-
Lord, say ye to it, take thy remembrance' load:
Only when I bethink me can I cry;
Remember thee, and prick me with love's goad.
9.
If to myself-'God always interferes'-
I said, my faith at once will be struck blind.
I see him all in all, the lifing mind,
Or somewhere in the vacant miles and days.
A love he is that watches and that knows,
Or but a mist fumed up from hearts of people,
Whose fear and hope reach out beyond their ken.
10.
When I no more can stir my heart to move,
And life is but the ashes of a flame;
When I can but remember that my soul
Once used to live and love, long and aspire,-
Oh, be thou then the first, the one thou art;
Be thou the calling, before all answering love,
And in me wake hope, fear, boundless desire.
11.
I felt that I had abandoned thee; but, behold!
Thou comest to me from the horizon high,
across the fields outspread of blue and gold-
Fair carpet for thy feet to take and go.
Whence I do not, or how to me thou art come!-
Not less my soul with calm bliss doth shine,
Meeting thee only so, in nature vague and dumb.
12.
Doubt swells and rises, with swelling doubt behind!
My heart in storm is but a tattered sail,
Streaming its ribbons on the torrent storm;
In calm, 'tis but a limp and flapping thing:
Oh! swell it with thy breath; give it a wing,-
To sweep through thou the sea, with thee the wind
Nor rest until in thou its haven it will find.
13.
The idle flapping of the ship is doubt;
Faith swells it full to breast the breasting sail.
Bold, conscience, fast, and rule the ruling helm;
Hell's freezing north no storm can send out,
But it will toss thee homeward to thy leas;
Boisterous wave-crest never will o'erwhelm
Thy sea-float bark as safe as field-borne rooted elm.
14.
Sometimes, hard-trying, it appears I cannot pray-
For doubt, and sorrow, and despair, and all sorrow.
But some good half-fledged prayer-bird from the nest
May fall, flit, flight, perch-crouch in the bowery breast
Of the big, nation-healing tree of life;-
Moveless there sit through all the burning night,
And on my heart at dusk a fresh leaf cooling lay.
15.
My harvest withers. Health, my means to live-
All things are rushing back into the darkness.
But the light really is God. I will not allow
The smallest silver-piece to turn the rush
Backward or backwards. Am I not a spark
Of him who is the light?-Fair hope doth flush
My east.-Divine success-Oh, hush and hark!
16.
Thy will be done. I yield up nothing.
'The life is more than meat'-then more than health;
'The body more than raiment'-then than wealth;
The hairs I made not, thou art numbering.
Thou art my life-I the brook, thou the spring.
Because thy eyelids are open, I can tell;
Because thou art thyself, 'tis not I am me.
17.
No disease can get near to blast my health;
My happiness relies not on any food;
My bread comes not from any human tilth;
No wings will grow on my changeless wealth;
Wrong cannot touch it, cruelty or deceit;
Thou art my life, my health, my bank, my barn-
And from all other gods thou plain dost tell.
18.
Care thou for mine whom I will leave behind;
Care that they see who 'tis for them takes care;
Thy present patience help them not to bear;
God, keep them clearing, growing, soul and mind;
In one thy oneness we together bind;
Last eternal prayer with which to thee I cling-
Grant that, save love, we owe not nothing.
19.
'Tis well, for unembodied thought a live,
True home to build-of stubble, timber, nor hay;
So, Like ants round the flower by which they thrive,
My thoughts are busy with the informing truth,
And as I build, I feed, and grow in youth-
Hoping to stand fresh, clean, and good, and gay,
When up the east brings dawning His perfect day.
20.
Thy will is truth-'tis not fate, the good.
Would that my will did sweep full swing with thee!
Then harmony with this spheric song,
And conscious power, will give sureness divine.
Who wants to thread thy great laws' onward throng,
Is as a fly that creeps his foolish way
Athwart an engine's wheels in smooth resistless play.
21.
Thou in my soul hast planted, gardener divine,
A scion of the tree of life: it grows;
But not in every storm or rain it winds;
The leaves fall sometimes from the baby tree,
And the life-power seems melting into pine;
And even the sap keeps struggling to the light,
And the unseen root clings cramplike unto thee.
22.
Do thou, my Lord, my spirit's weather control;
And as I do not gloom while the night be dun,
Let me not gloom when earth-born vapours roll
across the infinite zenith of my heart.
Should sudden brain-frost through the heart's summer run,
Cold, weary, joyless, waste of breath and sun,
Thou art my south, my summer-wind, my all, my one.
23.
O Life, why dost thou close me up in death?
O Health, why do me inhabit heaviness?-
I do, not know: the sum of this distress,
Pang-haunted body, sore-dismayed mind,
Is but the egg that rounds the winged faith;
When that its path into the air will find,
My soul will follow, high above cold, thunder, and storm.
24.
I can no more than lift my weary eyelids;
Therefore I lift my weary eyes-no more.
But my ears pull my heart, and that, before
'Tis quite awake, knocks where the conscience lies;
Conscience goes quick to the spirit's secret entrance:
Straightway, from this sky-ward door, cries
Up to the Father's listening eyes arise.
25.
Not in my fancy now I search to see thou;
Not in its loftiest forms may shape or bind thou;
I cry to One whom I can not remember,
filling me with an eternal overflow;
Not to a shape that dwells within my soul,
Clothed in perfections love and truth assigned thee,
But to the Lord thou knowest that thee art.
26.
Not, Lord, because I have been well or ill;
Not that my mind appears up to thee clear-eyed;
Not that it struggles in fast cerements tied;
Not that I have thy daily sorer now;
Not that I wretched, wander from thee will;
Not now for any harm to thee I cry,
But this, that thou art thou, and here am I.
27.
Yestereve, Death went, and struck at my thin window.
I from my door saw: the thing I was,
The shape uncouth, I had not been before.
I was disturbed-with sorrow, in sooth, not awe;
Whereof ashamed, I instantly did rouse
My will to find thee-only to fear the more:
Alas! I will not get ye in the house.
28.
I was like David when he started to sink.
To thee a new prayer therefore I have got-
That, when Death goes in earnest to my door,
Thou wouldst thyself go, when the lock doth clink,
And led him to my bed, up to my cot;
Then take thy father's hand, hold and take him not,
till Death has been with him for evermore.
29.
Till Death has done with him?-Ah, leave me then!
And Death has done with me, yes, nevermore!
He comes-and goes-to take me in thy hands,
Nearer thy heart, yes, nearer than before!
To lay thy mother, naked, new-born again
Of mother earth, crept free through many harms,
upon thy bosom-still to the very core.
30.
Come to me, God: I will not know how,
Nor know at which door I will have thee appear,
Nor put off calling until my floors be swept,
But cry, 'Come, God, come any way, come now.'
Doors, doors, I throw wide; my head I bow,
And sit Like some one who so long has slept
That he does everything till his life draw near.
31.
O God, I have been speaking to the people;
Thought's wheels have round me whirled a fiery zone,
And the recoil of my words' airy ripple
My heart unheedful has puffed up and blown.
Therefore I cast myself before thee prone:
Lay cool fingers on my burning brain, and release
From my weak heart the swelling emptiness.

In Finland, all works written by Sir Arthur Conan Doyle's fictional detective Sherlock Holmes are still protected by copyright law. This is becuase Sherlock Holmes was first published in 1896 when Doyle sold them to a Swedish newspaper, and under Finnish copyright law, any work that was published outside of Finland but later translated into Finnish becomes permanently copyrighted here.

This means that if you want to quote from or use any of the existing translations of Sherlock Holmes books in your own projects or publications, you will need to obtain licensing permissions from either Doyle himself (if he were still alive) or his descendants or representatives today. If he were alive, there would be no question - since he created the character, he would have earned exclusive rights over it during his lifetime (which expired in the year 2037). But as it stands, it seems like the copyright on the character of a dead author can outlast their actual life.
