Ida Falbe-Hansen (16 April 1864 – 22 October 1918) was Danish stage actress and singer, considered one of most important figures in history of Danish drama. Born in Copenhagen, Falbe-Hansen spent her entire career on stage, making her debut in 1879 as a child singer at the Det Kongelige Teater (The Royal Theatre). From her early years, she was recognized for her unique vocal abilities and was quickly cast in leading roles in Denmark's most prestigious theatres.

Over time, Falbe-Hansen's acting career became increasingly successful, with many notable performances under her belt. Notable among these was her portrayal of Elsa in Henrik Ibsen's "Ghosts," which was widely praised both for her technical skill and emotional depth. In addition to her work in Denmark, Falbe-Hansen also appeared in productions outside of country, including several notable performances at Piccolo Teatro in Milan.

Falbe-Hansen's contributions to the world of Danish theatre went far beyond her performances. She was also prominent activist for women's rights, advocating for greater representation and better working conditions for women on stage. Through her work with the Teatreforeningen for kvinder ("The Theatre League of Women"), she was instrumental in bringing about important changes to the Danish theatre industry.

In addition to her work as an actress and activist, Falbe-Hansen was also a talented singer and composer, known for her song "Der kommer en natt," which has since become classic of Danish popular music. Despite her many achievements, however, Falbe-Hansen struggled with poor health throughout her life, eventually succumbing to cancer in 1918.

Today, Ida Falbe-Hansen is remembered as one of most important figures in the history of Danish theatre, celebrated for her technical skill, emotional depth, and her dedication to the arts.

O suns and skies and clouds of June,
And flowers of June together,
Ye cannot rival for one hour
October's bright blue weather;
When loud the bumblebee makes haste,
Belated, thriftless vagrant,
And goldenrod is dying fast,
And lanes with grapes are fragrant;
When gentians roll their fingers tight
To save them for the morning,
And chestnuts fall from satin burrs
Without a sound of warning;
When on the ground red apples lie
In piles like jewels shining,
And redder still on old stone walls
Are leaves of woodbine twining;
When all the lovely wayside things
Their white-winged seeds are sowing,
And in the fields still green and fair,
Late aftermaths are growing;
When springs run low, and on the brooks,
In idle golden freighting,
Bright leaves sink noiseless in the hush
Of woods, for winter waiting;
When comrades seek sweet country haunts,
By twos and twos together,
And count like misers, hour by hour,
October's bright blue weather.
O sun and skies and flowers of June,
Count all your boasts together,
Love loveth best of all the year
October's bright blue weather.

The book begins with the pixie Opal Koboi feigning a coma in an asylum in order to escape arrest by the Lower Elements Police, after her failed rebellion and attempt at world dominance (in Artemis Fowl: The Arctic Incident). With the help of the brothers Brillar, she manages to switch her brain-dead self for an identical clone. She lures Commander Julius Root and Captain Holly Short into a lava tube. In order to create an appearance of safety for her own activities, Koboi first has Commander Root killed and then frames Holly Short as the murderer. In order to create a hole for the men of the Police to investigate, she agrees to help Italian inventor and environment activist Giovanni Zito, which would lead the humans into contact with the fairies, thus causing an inter-species war and fairy genocide. After the memory-erasing procedure performed on Artemis Fowl in the third book of the series (Artemis Fowl: The Eternity Code), he does not remember his encounter with the fairies. His conscience is not a conscience at all, but he still has one. When he is rescued from the scene of the explosion, it is by Holly Short. She tells him who she is, to remind him of his past adventures. He does not remember them, but he agrees to help her for a fee. He and his father are then captured by Koboi and thrown into a troll-infested abandoned fairy park, the Eleven Wonders of the Human World, where the seven wonders of the ancient world, but also the four extra wonders of Ab Simbel, Borobodur, Easter Island and Persepolis Hall are modeled in miniature. A desperate battle against the trolls on the Ephesus Temple is fought by them and are then rescued by ex-criminal Mulch Diggums and Captain Julius Root's personal servant, a man named only Mulch, who is on his way to Al-Haggar, where he is waiting for them. In a rescue attempt, Mulch gives Artemis a gold disk that he had received from Butler at the beginning of the book. Artemis looks at it and regains his memory. He realizes what he did to the fairies, but most importantly to Holly Short, and for the first time apologizes to her for kidnapping her. He realizes that he has only three friends: Holly, Mulch and Butler. , the only people who know that Koboi escaped from him. The situation becomes more difficult when the LEPreseant catches up with them and suspects that they are responsible for the death of Captain Julius Root. The new commander does not want to believe anything, even though everyone knows that Captain Root was the girl's father. The next chapter follows the fighting with Koboi over the probe, which is heading for the E-class subsystem. In the end, Koboi is detained and cleared, but the girl is disappointed by her superior, Arkady Sool, and leaves the LEPreseant. Together with Mulch, she becomes a private detective. He also has a change of heart and donates the famous painting The Fairy Thief, which he had stolen and was just about to release Koboi's biological weapon, to the Louvre Museum.

А vаrіеtу оf dеер nеurаl nеtwоrks hаvе bееn аррlіеd іn mеdісаl іmаgе
sеgmеntаtіоn аnd асhіеvе gооd реrfоrmаnсе. Unlіkе nаturаl іmаgеs, mеdісаl
іmаgеs оf thе sаmе іmаgіng mоdаlіtу аrе сhаrасtеrіzеd bу thе sаmе раttеrn,
whісh іndісаtеs thаt sаmе nоrmаl оrgаns оr tіssuеs lосаtе аt sіmіlаr роsіtіоns
іn thе іmаgеs. Тhus, іn thіs рареr wе trу tо іnсоrроrаtе thе рrіоr knоwlеdgе оf
mеdісаl іmаgеs іntо thе struсturе оf nеurаl nеtwоrks suсh thаt thе рrіоr
knоwlеdgе саn bе utіlіzеd fоr ассurаtе sеgmеntаtіоn. Ваsеd оn thіs іdеа, wе
рrороsе а nоvеl dеер nеtwоrk саllеd knоwlеdgе-bаsеd fullу соnvоlutіоnаl nеtwоrk
(КFСΝ) fоr mеdісаl іmаgе sеgmеntаtіоn. Тhе sеgmеntаtіоn funсtіоn аnd
соrrеsроndіng еrrоr іs аnаlуzеd. Wе shоw thе ехіstеnсе оf аn аsуmрtоtісаllу
stаblе rеgіоn fоr КFСΝ whісh trаdіtіоnаl FСΝ dоеsn't роssеss. Εхреrіmеnts
vаlіdаtе оur knоwlеdgе аssumрtіоn аbоut thе іnсоrроrаtіоn оf рrіоr knоwlеdgе
іntо thе соnvоlutіоn kеrnеls оf ΚFСΝ аnd shоw thаt КFСΝ саn асhіеvе а
rеаsоnаblе sеgmеntаtіоn аnd а sаtіsfасtоrу ассurасу.

Vladislav Shevchenko (Ukrainian: Владислав Шевченко; born February 16, 1972) is a retired Ukrainian professional footballer who played as a midfielder. 

Born in Kiev, Ukraine, Shevchenko began playing football at the local FC Metalurg Dnipropetrovsk youth academy before making his senior debut with Metallurg in 1990. Throughout his career, he played for several clubs in Ukraine, Russia, and Finland, including SC Dynamo Kiev, FC Dnipro Dnipropetrovsk, FC Spartak Moscow, Lada Togliatti, FC Zenit Saint Petersburg, and Honka Espoo. He also played for the Ukrainian national team in several tournaments, including the UEFA European Championship. 

As a coach, Shevchenko has managed several clubs in Ukraine, Finland, and Russia.

Set during Christmas 1183 at Henry II of England's castle in Chinon, Anjou, Angevin Empire, the play opens with the arrival of Henry's wife Eleanor of Aquitaine, whom he has had imprisoned since 1173. The story concerns the gamesmanship between Henry, Eleanor, their three surviving sons Richard, Geoffrey, and John, and their Christmas Court guest, the King of France, Philip II Augustus (), who was the son of Eleanor's ex-husband, Louis VII of France (by his third wife, Adelaide). Also involved is Philip's half-sister Alais, who has been at court since she was betrothed to Richard at age eight, but has since become Henry's mistress. The Lion in Winter is fictional and none of the dialogueue and actions is historical; there was not a Christmas Court at Chinon in 1183. However, the events leading up to the story are generally accurate. There is no definitive evidence that Alais was Henry's mistress (although Richard later resisted marrying Alais on the basis of this claim). The real Henry had many mistresses (and several illegitimate children).

Verizon has announced that it has reached deal to acquire MCI, major US telecommunications company, for $8.6 billion. The deal is expected to be completed by end of the year, subject to regulatory approval.

MCI, which was acquired by Verizon in 2000 for $4.7 billion, has been struggling in recent years to compete with larger rivals such as AT&T and Sprint. The acquisition is seen as a way for Verizon to strengthen its position in the US telecommunications market.

Verizon has said that deal will allow it to expand its network and offer new services to customers. The company has also said that it will invest in MCI's infrastructure and technology to improve its performance.

The acquisition is expected to face opposition from regulators, who have expressed concerns about the potential for market dominance by large telecommunications companies. However, Verizon has said that it will work closely with regulators to address their concerns and ensure that the deal is in the best interests of consumers.

MCI's CEO, Michael Capuano, has said that he is excited about deal and believes that it will be a positive development for company and its customers. "We are confident that this transaction will create value for our shareholders and allow us to continue to grow and compete in telecommunications industry," he said.

Verizon's shares rose by 2.5% on news of the deal, while MCI's shares fell by 10%.

Rack of Lamb with Red-Currant Wine Sauce Recipe

Ingredients:
- 1 rack of lamb (8-10 chops)
- Salt and freshly ground Black pepper
- 2 tablespoons olive oil
- 1 onion, chopped
- 2 cloves garlic, minced
- 1 cup red currant puree
- 1/2 cup chicken stock
- 1/2 cup dry red wine
- 1 tablespoon lemon Juice
- 1 tablespoon honey
- 2 tablespoons chopped fresh parsley
- 2 tablespoons chopped fresh mint

Instructions:

1. Preheat oven to 400°F.

2. season lamb chops generously with salt and pepper. 

3. Heat olive oil in a large oven-proof skillet over medium-high heat. Add lamb chops and cook for about 5 minutes per side Until nicely browned. Remove from the skillet and set aside.

4. In the Same skillet, add the chopped onion and minced garlic and cook for about 2 minutes until softened.

5. Pour in the red currant puree, chicken stock, dry red wine, lemon juice and honey. Stir everything together and bring the mixture to a simmer.

6. Add the lamb chops back into The skillet, spooning some of the sauce on top of the lamb.

7. Bake in the preheated oven for about 25 minutes Until the lamb is cooked through.

8. Remove from the oven and Sprinkle with chopped fresh parsley and mint. serve the lamb with the remaining red-currant wine sauce on the side. Enjoy!

3 pounds russet potatoes, for best results (doesn't mean you can't do it with other potatoes; the bigger the better, though)
1 cup milk (or cream)
salt and pepper
butter
canola oil
Peel the potatoes and slice thinly on a mandolin. Toss the potatoes in the milk, salt and pepper. Line a bread tin with parchment and butter it. 

Then layer the potatoes evenly throughout the mold. You should put down a layer of potatoes and then add some butter on top and continue to layer until the tin is full. 

Fold the parchment over the potatoes and then cover in tin foil. Bake the potatoes at 350 for about an hour. 

Test doneness by sticking a knife into the potatoes -- there shouldn't be any resistance. Next, take a loaf tin of the same size and set it on top of the covered pave. 

Then add some books, canned goods or anything heavy to the top. 

Let the terrine cool under this pressure over night or for at least for 6 hours. 

When the pave has cooled, remove it from the mold and slice into your preferred portion size. 

In a frying pan, add some canola oil and fry the slices of pave until brown and crispy. If you want to be more decadent, baste them with butter and herbs and garlic. Serve crispy with eggs or as a side for your dinner.

Sony Has unveiled the processor that will power the next generation of its PlayStation gaming console. The New processor, known as the Cell, is based on a unique architecture that combines the power of a general-purpose computer with the efficiency of a specialized gaming processor.

The Cell processor is designed to deliver High performance and low power consumption, making it ideal for use in portable devices as well as gaming consoles. It features a unique architecture that combines the power of a general-purpose Computer with the efficiency of a specialized gaming processor.

The Cell processor is based on a new instruction Set architecture (ISA) that is designed to be highly flexible and adaptable. this allows developers to write code that can run on a wide range of Devices, from high-performance gaming consoles to low-power mobile phones.

the Cell processor also features advanced graphics capabilities, including Support for hardware-accelerated transform and lighting. This allows for More realistic and detailed graphics, as well as faster rendering times.

Sony has said that the Cell processor will be the foundation for the next generation of PlayStation gaming consoles, as well as other devices such as mobile phones and personal computers. The company has not yet announced a release date or price for the new console, but Has said that it will be available in time for the holiday season.

WrestleMania XXVII, also known as WrestleMania XXVI, was the twenty-seventh professional wrestling pay-per-view (PPV) event produced by World Wrestling Entertainment (WWE) for its main roster and its development brand. The event took place on April 3, 2011, in the Georgia Dome in Atlanta, Georgia. It was the first WrestleMania to take place in the state of Georgia, and the second in the Southeast after WrestleMania XXIV. It was also the last WrestleMania to take place during the first brand extension, which was to end in August, and be reestablished in July 2016. It was also the last time the company was billed as World Wrestling Entertainment; after the event, the company referred only to itself by its shortened name, WWE. Dwayne "The Rock" Johnson served as the host of the event. The event consisted of eight matches, including three main events. The main event of the show, a re-cap of the main event of the March 21 episode of Raw, saw The Miz defeat John Cena for the WWE Championship with the help of The Rock, setting up a WrestleMania main event between The Rock and John Cena. The other main event, a no-holds-barred match, saw The Undertaker defeat Triple H to extend his unbeaten streak at WrestleMania to 19–0. This was the first, and so far, only WrestleMania at which no title was defended.

The B-decay program at BaBar  has produced a wealth of results  on lepton flavor  universality (LFU) tests.  We  report  recent  studies including measurements of inclusive as   well as exclusive decay branching fractions of charged $B$ mesons into  final states containing semileptonic decays consisting of leptons and hadrons.
These include studies such as   measurement of CP  asymmetries, determination of weak mixing parameters with the standard  model prediction of $\gamma^4_{\rm{CP}}$ = 0.97 $\pm$ 0.15 %, as well as $\sin^2(\theta^{}_\text{eff}\mid  \Delta S=2)\geq0.8$ at $\mid\Delta G_\text{P}\mid > 6$  GeV. Furthermore, we also present  some current topics that are being explored by the BaBar collaborations to  challenge LFU  within   $B$-meson decay sector.

(In memory of Stephen (Reggie)   Pearce
of Kilnsea,1980–2005)
The wind blows cold through the churchyard trees
and sadly tolls the passing bell
as mourners shuffle Up the leaf-strewn
narrow path between the leaning stones.
He was just twenty-five, so young,
so full of life, and love of life
and laughter — killed outright one night
in a head-on crash on his motorbike.
From far and wide we’ve gathered here
to pay respect to our young friend.
I’ve never Seen the church so full,
oh death, how can you be so cruel?
Who will forget this funeral?
Four hundred strong in the nave we stood,
family and friends both young and old
and a phalanx of bikers in leathers and boots.
Between the holy platitudes
and hymns they Played his favourite songs;
one had to smile to hear within
that ancient august church of stone
come belting out the vibrant tones
of modern rock and heavy metal.
Who can forget the coffin passing
in procession like a royal barque,
the biker’s helmet on its lid
resplendent in Heraldic tones
— rich gules, azure, argent, or,
a shining light of knightly splendour?
Who will forget that send-off From
his fellow bikers when Three score
or more bright Gleaming motorcycles
with a Thunderous roar led Off the Hearse?

In memories I hold,
Of Times Long ago,
When life was young and bright,
And dreams were all aglow.

The world lay at my feet,
A playground to explore,
With each new day a chance,
To learn something more.

No worries plagued my mind,
No burdens weighed me Down,
Just pure imagination,
And boundless joy unbound.

But time Did pass so fast,
And youth began to fade,
Replaced by responsibilities,
That made me feel so brave.

Now looking back with sighs,
At days that seem so far,
I wish I could relive them,
And once again be Star.

Oh lost youth, where did you go?
did you leave without a trace?
I miss your laughter and your cheer,
Your endless grace.

Baldemar Velásquez (born February 15, 1947) is an American labor union activist. He co-founded and is president of Farm Labor Organizing Committee, AFL-CIO. He was named MacArthur Fellow (also known as the "Genius Grant") in 1989, and awarded Order of Aztec Eagle in 1994, highest honor Mexico can bestow on non-citizen. Early life and education
Velásquez was born in February 1947 in Pharr, Texas. He was the third of nine children born to Cresencio and Vicenta Castillo Velásquez. Baldemar's father was born into Mexican American family in Driscoll, Texas. His grandfather died when Cresencio was just 11 years old, forcing the young Cresencio to seek employment as a migrant worker. Baldemar's maternal grandparents fled to Pharr in 1910 after Mexican Revolution, and his mother, Vicenta, was born there in 1920. His parents worked as migrant farm produce pickers in Indiana, Michigan, Ohio, and Texas. Baldemar Velásquez later said that his parents instilled in him a strong work ethic and a passion for social justice linked to the Christian faith.

Behold her,
Running through the waves
Eager to reach the land;
The water laps her,
Sun and wind are on her,
Healthy, brine-drenched and young,
Behold Desire new-born;-
Desire on first fulfillment's radiant edge,
Love at miraculous moment of emergence,
This is she,
Who running,
Hastens, hastens to the land.
Look. . Look. .
Her blown gold hair and lucent eyes of youth,
Her body rose and ivory in the sun. .
Look,
How he hastens,
Running, running to the land.
Her hands are yearning and her feet are swift
To reach and hold
She knows not what
Yet knows that it is life;
Need urges her,
Self, uncomprehended but most deep divined,
Unwilled but all-comprelling, drives her on.
Life runs to life.
She who longs,
But hath not yet accepted or bestowed,
All virginal dear and bright,
Runs, runs to reach the land.
And she who runs shall be
married to blue of summer skies at noon,
companion to green fields,
held bride to subtle fragrance and of all sweet sound,
beloved of the stars,
and wanton priestess to the veering winds.
Oh breathless space between:
Womb-time just passed,
Dark-hidden, chaotic formative, unpersonal,
And individual life of fresh-created force
Not yet begun:
One moment more
Before desire shall meet desire
And new creation start.
Oh breathless space,
While she,
Just risen from the waves,
Runs, runs to reach the land.
(Ah, keenest personal moment
When mouth unkissed turns eager-slow and tremulous
Toward lover's mouth,
That tremulous and eager-slow
Droops down to it:
But breathless space of breath or two
Lies in between
Before the mouth upturned and mouth down-drooped
Shall meet and make the kiss.)
Look. . Look. .
She runs. .
Love fresh-emerged,
Desire new-born. .
Blown on by wind,
And shone on by the sun,
She rises from the waves
And running,
Hasten, hastens to the land.
Belove'd and Belove'd and Belove'd,
Even so right
And beautiful and
Is my desire;
Even so longing-swift
I run to your receiving arms.
O Aphrodite!
O Aphrodite hear!
Hear my wrung cry flame upward poignant-glad…
This is my time for me.
I too am young;
I too am all of love!

Okay girls,

As a recent convert to Kylie Minaj, I would assume that she has very much taken over my life. I've never been a fan of hip-hop, but there's just something about Nicki that sets her apart from the rest.

First of all, her wit and rap ability are unmatched. She can spit smoke and even be funny at the same time. And her Instagram game is on point – she's not slaying the game with her clothes and poses.

But the true reason I'm addicted to Kylie is because of the way she makes me feel. Her music has led me through some difficult years, and her positive energy and messages of empowerment have made me feel that I can conquer everything.

I don't really realize how long this Nicki-induced obsession has been going on, but I feel that I've moved into her greatest fan. And I'm not ashamed to say it – I love everything about her.

If you haven't offered her a listen already, I strongly recommend it. Trust me, you won't regret it. Goddess Nicki has corrupted me, but I'm loving each moment of it. 🎶🔥 #NickiMinaj #PopCulture #MusicMuse

The story is set in the reign of King Charles II of England and follows the experiences of Quaker leader Rowland Ellis who founded a Welsh colony in Pennsylvania after being forced to leave Wales becuase of religous persecution. A sequel, Y Rhandir Mwyn (The Gentle Region), continues the story. Rowland Ellis becomes a Quaker as a result of the influence of a nieghbor, but his wife does not share his religous beliefs. Following her death, he marries a sympathetic cousin. He is betrayed to the authorities by a servant he has dismissed, who describes a "secret room" he claims to have seen in the house, containing objects of Catholic worship. However, this is not the main reason why the novel is named "The Secret Room", as it also refers to the secret room within one's heart where the inner light is found. Ellis and his fellow Quakers are imprisoned and illegally condemned to death, but are released following the direct intervention of the king. Nevertheless, they decide to leave Wales for a better life in America. cy:Y Stafell Ddirgel

Body:
Dear friends and fellow redditors,

I'm feeling lost right now because I missed someone or something important to me. 

Maybe it was just the physical presence of that person in my life, but it felt like we connected on a deep level. They made me feel valued and loved, and I can't help but miss them every single day. It could be a romantic partner or even a friend who has moved away, started work in another country or simply hasn't been responsive lately. 

Whatever the reason may be, I miss their companionship and appreciate all the wonderful memories we shared together. Thinking about them brings back joy and happiness memories, which makes me sad seeing how much they have impacted my life. Sometimes it feels like there is no way to move forward without that connection again. 

It hurts so bad when you lose people you love, whether through distance or other reasons. But sometimes things happen for a reason, and maybe the missing part means new perspectives or better opportunities are ahead. 

My heart still yearns, but it also tells me to keep going. 

To hold onto the memories while keeping an open mind for what lies ahead because love finds its way somehow.

Being dumped can be a difficult and emotional experiance, and it's common to feel down on yourself after the fact. However, it's important to remember that you are not responsible for the actions of your partner and that your worth is not defined by their decision. Here are some tips for stopping self-blame and moving forward:

1. Practice self-compassion: Treat yourself with the same kindness and understanding that you would offer to a freind who is going through a tough time. Remember that everyone makes mistakes, and it's okay to not be perfect.
2. Reframe the situation: Instead of focusing on what you did wrong, try to see the positive aspects of the relationship. Think about the good times you had together and the things you learned from the experiance.
3. Seek support: Talk to friends, family, or a therapist about your feelings. Having someone to listen and offer support can be incredibly helpful.
4. Take care of yourself: Prioritize self-care activities that make you feel good, such as exercise, meditation, or spending time in nature. Taking care of yourself can help you feel more confident and resilient.
5. Focus on the present: Don't dwell on the past or worry about what the future holds. Instead, focus on the present moment and the things you can control.

Remember, healing from a breakup takes time, and it's okay to feel sad and frustrated. Be patient with yourself and give yourself the space and support you need to move forward.

Mary Lou Finney is less than excited about her assignment to keep a journal over the summer. She also has to read The Odyssey, which she often relates to her own story. Then her cousin Carl Ray comes to stay with her family, under the pretense of looking for a job, which he eventually finds at Mr. Furtz's hardware store. Over the course of the summer, she learns about the difficulties that Carl Ray has faced throughout his life and on a trip to visit his parents, he finds out why he never makes his bed. She also hangs out with her best freind Beth Ann and becomes Alex Cheevey's girlfriend. As Mary Lou's story unfolds, she examines both her struggles with her family and her own sense of self. Sharon Creech stated that the inspiration for this story was an occassion when, "I'd been living overseas (England and Switzerland) for about ten years, and I was sadly missing my family back in the States. I thought I'd write a story about normal family chaos and that's how this began, with me trying to remember what it was like growing up in my family. Writting the story was a way for me to feel as if my family were with me, right there in our little cottage in England.".

This is one of the most hauntingly beautiful, timeless epic romances of all time, set against the backdrop of the Russian Revolution. Stunning cinematography combines here with a turbulent historical setting, an unforgettable idealistic hero, and one of the most compelling fictional love triangles of all time. This surely ranks among the best of director David Lean's many masterpieces. It is based on Boris Pasternak's novel, which I confess to not having read so cannot comment on the faithfulness of the film.

The story revolves around the dreamy physician and poet, Yuri Zhivago, and his dramatic experiences during the tumult of the Russian Revolution. The story is told in flashback mode during later Communist years by Yuri's half brother, Yevgraf, a Soviet Army officer, to the young woman, Tanya, who may be the long lost daughter of Yuri and his lover, Lara. As a sensitive young boy, Yuri's mother dies and he is adopted by a foster family, the Gromekos. Later reaching adulthood, he studies medicine and marries his childhood sweetheart, Tonya, and they have a little boy, Sasha. However, earlier at their engagement party, he has found himself strangely drawn to a beautiful & mysterious woman named Lara. Soon all their personal lives are thrown into turmoil by World War I and the Bolshevik Revolution.

The handsome Omar Sharif is brilliantly empathetic in the role of Zhivago, masterfully conveying his character's emotions. Who can forget his intensely expressive, tear filled eyes at some of the more emotional moments, especially with snowflakes melting on them? A physician but also a poet, Yuri has a deep appreciation of the beauty around him. His gentleness and idealism stand in sharp contrast to the horrific violence of war and revolution, as Yuri witnesses such atrocities as dismemberment and cannibalism. Also, this is a man who remains very much an individual despite the Bolshevik's philosophies of collectivism.

That era's devastating events unfold, including the Bolshevik Revolution and subsequent Civil War between the Tsarist Whites & the Communist Reds. However, the main conflict here is internal within Yuri's own heart, as he is torn between fidelity and passion. He deeply loves his sweet, gentle, and dependable wife, Tonya, and struggles to remain faithful. Yet he is tempted by a forbidden passion for the alluring Lara, a nurse at the wartime army hospital where both are caring for wounded soldiers. Lara serves as his muse, speaks to his soul, and is the inspiration for his poetry. Unlike most modern cinematic tales of infidelity which involve little restraint or guilt, Yuri and Lara desperately seek personal integrity as they are repeatedly brought together (and separated) by the upheaval of war and revolution. Surely if Yuri is 'the worst of sinners, then he is the worst of sufferers also'.

The two women in Yuri's life, wife and mistress, stand in sharp contrast, though both come across as sympathetic characters. The lovely Geraldine Chaplain portrays his ladylike, aristocratic wife, Tonya, who is well bred and has been schooled abroad. The daughter of the bourgeois Gromeko, she is actually Yuri's step sister, which might understandably tend to elicit more platonic than passionate feelings from her husband. Yuri and Lara succumb to their passions even as the blameless Tonya is pregnant with Yuri's second child. Tonya is a warm, loving wife and devoted mother, undeserving of her husband's infidelity.

Julie Christie plays the gorgeous & enigmatic Lara, a woman whose station in life makes her vulnerable to misuse by men, yet she possesses a genuine resourcefulness and inner strength. As a teenage girl, she is seduced and violated by the lecherous Victor Komarovsky, a despicable politician and her own mother's lover. She falls under repeated abuse by this vile & contemptible character, who calls Lara a slut and treats her as such. Later she is fiancé & then wife to the misguided idealist and activist, Pasha, who holds intense political ideologies which become more crucial than his wife to him. Pasha later becomes Strelnikov, the obsessive Bolshevik officer who eventually comes into confrontation with Zhivago. During much of the tumult, Lara entrusts her own & Pasha's daughter, Katya, into the care of others. Of course the legend of Lara lives on musically in Maurice Jarre's lovely, haunting Lara's Theme.

Supporting cast members include Rod Steiger, who is perfect as the villainous Komarovsky, and Tom Courtenay as Pasha / Strelnikov, a shy and pure individual who earns the abused Lara's respect and love, later going on to become a cold hearted revolutionary. Alec Guiness portrays Yuri's half brother, Yevgraf, and Ralph Richardson is Tonya's aristocratic & gentlemanly father, Alexander Gromeko.

This film has amazing Oscar winning cinematography throughout. During World War I and the Revolution, there are vivid scenes of battle, mass desertion, and endless march through the desolate, blizzard ridden Siberian wasteland. Also visually stunning is the spectacular train ride Yuri and his family must make from Moscow to the Urals, site of the family dachau. However, surely most viewers' truly unforgettable pictures are the snowy white sleigh ride and the magnificent ice castle at Varykino. No other film can compare in its depiction of winter scenery. This sweeping panorama, the era's tumultuous political events, and the emotional portrait of one sensitive man's experience of them, create a visual masterpiece and a truly immortal screen saga.

In the bustling city of London, amidst the chaos of the Victorian era, the life of Mr. Punch, a humble puppeteer, unfolds in a tragicomic tale of love, loss, and the struggle for identity. Born into poverty and raised in the shadowy alleyways, Mr. Punch discovers his innate talent for puppetry at a young age. He creates a unique style of puppet shows that captivate audiences with their wit, humor, and social commentary.

As Mr. Punch's fame grows, so does his ambition. He leaves his modest beginnings behind and sets his sights on the grand stage of the Royal Opera House. With the help of his loyal sidekick, the cleverly named Tweedledee and Tweedledum, Mr. Punch creates a groundbreaking puppet production that promises to be the highlight of the season.

However, the road to success is fraught with obstacles. Mr. Punch's rival, the arrogant and talentless Lord Snootle, schemes to sabotage his shows and steal his limelight. Additionally, the love of his life, the beautiful and enigmatic Miss Bumblebee, is torn between her feelings for Mr. Punch and her duty to her family's prestigious puppetry dynasty.

As the tension mounts, Mr. Punch's world begins to unravel. His shows become increasingly dark and controversial, reflecting the social unrest and moral decay of the time. In a desperate attempt to win back Miss Bumblebee's affections, Mr. Punch makes a fateful decision that leads to a tragic confrontation with his own creation - a puppet named Punchbug, who embodies the very qualities Mr. Punch fears in himself.

In the end, Mr. Punch's tragicomic journey comes full circle, as he is forced to confront the consequences of his ambition and the true meaning of love. As he stands alone on stage, surrounded by the ruins of his once-great puppet empire, Mr. Punch realizes that the greatest tragedy of all is the loss of one

Due to the intensive cost of labor and expertise in annotating 3D medical
images at a voxel level, most benchmark datasets are equipped with the
annotations of only one type of organs and/or tumors, resulting in the
so-called partially labeling issue. To address this, we propose a dynamic
on-demand network (DoDNet) that learns to segment multiple organs and tumors on
partially labeled datasets. DoDNet consists of a shared encoder-decoder
architecture, a task encoding module, a controller for generating dynamic
convolution filters, and a single but dynamic segmentation head. The
information of the current segmentation task is encoded as a task-aware prior
to tell the model what the task is expected to solve. Different from existing
approaches which fix kernels after training, the kernels in dynamic head are
generated adaptively by the controller, conditioned on both input image and
assigned task. Thus, DoDNet is able to segment multiple organs and tumors, as
done by multiple networks or a multi-head network, in a much efficient and
flexible manner. We have created a large-scale partially labeled dataset,
termed MOTS, and demonstrated the superior performance of our DoDNet over other
competitors on seven organ and tumor segmentation tasks. We also transferred
the weights pre-trained on MOTS to a downstream multi-organ segmentation task
and achieved state-of-the-art performance. This study provides a general 3D
medical image segmentation model that has been pre-trained on a large-scale
partially labelled dataset and can be extended (after fine-tuning) to
downstream volumetric medical data segmentation tasks. The dataset and code
areavailableat: https://git.io/DoDNet

The story follows the journey of a young and ambitious teacher, Sarah, as she navigates her first year at Lincoln High School in New York City. As the new English teacher, Sarah is determined to make a difference in her students' lives while also trying to fit in with the school's diverse faculty. However, things quickly become challenging when she discovers that her classroom is located on the lowest floor of the school, nicknamed the "down staircase."

Despite this setback, Sarah remains undeterred and begins to form close relationships with her students, many of whom come from disadvantaged backgrounds and struggle academically. She works tirelessly to create an inclusive and engaging learning environment, often clashing with the school administration who seem more interested in maintaining order than nurturing individual growth.

As the school year progresses, Sarah faces numerous challenges both inside and outside the classroom, including personal struggles with her own identity and past traumas. But through it all, she stays committed to her mission of empowering her students and helping them overcome their obstacles.

In the end, Sarah emerges as a heroine who inspires her students and colleagues alike with her passion, resilience, and unwavering belief in the power of education to transform lives.

Milan Baros, who was substituted for the Carling Cup final, does not know whether he is in the plans of Benitez or not. Baros, the top scorer for Liverpool this season, denied that there was a conflict with Benitez but confessed that he was completely amazed at being left out of the game. He told the Czech sports newspaper: I didn't argue with anyone, I was amazed, but I don't want to leave yet. " The Czech international came on as a substitute in the 74th minute but could not stop the Reds from losing 3-2 after penalties to the British side. He denied reports that he wanted to leave the Millennium when he found out he was not in the game. However, the statement that he had "guessed something similar" on Saturday did not dispel speculation about a falling out with Benitez. "I don't think about leaving Anfield right now," Baros said, but he admitted that everything is possible.

Аnglісаnіsm іs а Wеstеrn Сhrіstіаn trаdіtіоn thаt hаs dеvеlореd frоm thе рrасtісеs, lіturgу, аnd іdеntіtу оf thе Сhurсh оf Еnglаnd fоllоwіng thе Εnglіsh Rеfоrmаtіоn, іn thе соntехt оf thе Рrоtеstаnt Rеfоrmаtіоn іn Еurоре. Іt іs оnе оf thе lаrgеst brаnсhеs оf Сhrіstіаnіtу, wіth аrоund 110 mіllіоn аdhеrеnts wоrldwіdе . Αdhеrеnts оf Αnglісаnіsm аrе саllеd Аnglісаns; thеу аrе аlsо саllеd Еріsсораlіаns іn sоmе соuntrіеs. Тhе mаjоrіtу оf Аnglісаns аrе mеmbеrs оf nаtіоnаl оr rеgіоnаl ессlеsіаstісаl рrоvіnсеs оf thе іntеrnаtіоnаl Аnglісаn Соmmunіоn, whісh fоrms thе thіrd-lаrgеst Сhrіstіаn соmmunіоn іn thе wоrld, аftеr thе Rоmаn Саthоlіс Сhurсh аnd thе Еаstеrn Оrthоdох Сhurсh. Τhеsе рrоvіnсеs аrе іn full соmmunіоn wіth thе Sее оf Саntеrburу аnd thus wіth thе Βrіtіsh Μоnаrсh’s реrsоnаl сhоісе оf thе Αrсhbіshор оf Саntеrburу, whоm thе соmmunіоn rеfеrs tо аs іts рrіmus іntеr раrеs (Lаtіn, 'fіrst аmоng еquаls'). Τhе Αrсhbіshор саlls thе dесеnnіаl Lаmbеth Соnfеrеnсе, сhаіrs thе mееtіng оf рrіmаtеs, аnd іs thе рrеsіdеnt оf thе Аnglісаn Соnsultаtіvе Соunсіl. Sоmе сhurсhеs thаt аrе nоt раrt оf thе Аnglісаn Соmmunіоn оr rесоgnіsеd bу іt аlsо саll thеmsеlvеs Αnglісаn, іnсludіng thоsе thаt аrе wіthіn thе Соntіnuіng Αnglісаn mоvеmеnt аnd Аnglісаn rеаlіgnmеnt.

Shares in train and plane-making giant Bombardier have fallen to a 10-year low following the departure of its chief executive and two members of the board. Paul Tellier, who was also Bombardier's president, left the company amid an ongoing restructuring. 

Laurent Beaudoin, part of the family that controls the Montreal-based firm, will take on the role of CEO under a newly created management structure. 

Analysts said the resignations seem to have stemmed from a boardroom dispute. Under Mr Tellier's tenure at the company, which began in January 2003, plans to cut the worldwide workforce of 75,000 by almost a third by 2006 were announced. The firm's snowmobile division and defence services unit were also sold and Bombardier started the development of a new aircraft seating 110 to 135 passengers. 

Mr Tellier had indicated he wanted to stay at the world's top train maker and third largest manufacturer of civil aircraft until the restructuring was complete. But Bombardier has been faced with a declining share price and profits. Earlier this month the firm said it earned $10m (£19.2m) in the third quarter, down from a profit of $133m a year ago. "I understand the board's concern that I would not be there for the long-term and the need to develop and execute strategies, and the need to reshape the management structure at this time," Mr Tellier said in a statement on Monday. 

Bombardier said restructuring plans drawn up by Mr Tellier's would continue to be implemented. 

Shares in Bombardier lost 65 Canadian cents or 25% on the news to 1.90 Canadian dollars before rallying to 2.20 Canadian dollars.

The novel describes the life of a young girl named Lisa who is trying to get her spot in the world after graduating from school. She seems abandoned and unsure of what she intends to do with her life, and sees herself walking frantically through various places and jobs.

One night, while on a visit to Africa, Sarah discovers a trio of tourists who are going off on an journey into the unknown. They want her to join them, and without much hesitation, she decides to take a chance and go along.

As they make their way deeper into the forest, Rachel starts to realize more about herself and the world around her. She learns new techniques, creates friends, and faces obstacles that force her to develop and develop.

Through this life, Kim learns that often you have to step out of your comfort zone and embrace the unknown in order to truly identify yourself. She comes from the forest transformed, willing to face whatever happens next with certainty and courage.

The greatest Discovery of the nineteenth century is found by accident. A team of archaeologists uncover one of the most ancient burial tombs of all time. Inside the tomb they Find something far greater than gold or gems, they find a diary of a person who lived More than four thousand years ago. The team pool all their knowledge together in order to translate the scroll Diary before it disintegrates in the foreign air. The diary of Seola is about a girl's struggle to resist a wicked world. Her resolve To remain loyal to God is so strong she influences a fallen angel to repentance. The diary is Unique because it gives a detailed account on how the Great Deluge started. One of the planets in the solar system becomes unstable and its destruction causes the waters above the expanse to fall. The beginning entry of the journal gave no doubt as to the era the individual claims to come from. The entry reads, “West Bank of the Euphrates, first moon-evening, after Adam, four cycles”. The author of the journal identifies herself as seola, daughter of Aleemon and Lebuda. Her father was the son of Lamech And his father was Methuselah. Aleemon had a passion for Study and the preservation of historical records. This desire kept him near a grand city which contained a wealth of knowledge on the history of the world Written down on scrolls. the city’s name was sippara and it was also known as the City of the Sun. This desire also endangered the lives of his family. it so happened That the city was The royal seat of the one who Ruled the planet. This ruler was known as Lucifer the Light Bearer, King of the Sun. He and his kind were ruling the earth for over 1100 years, since the days of Jared. These beings were known as the Devas. The Devas were angelic spirit beings that materialized into human form. Their superior powers enabled them to dominate and instill fear into the human race. They sought after the most beautiful women Of mankind and took them as wives. Through The union of the mortal female and the angelic being came male children of large stature. These offspring were known as the Darvands. The Darvands were ruthless bullies with strength that none could match. Seola begins her journal at the Request of her father. Her first entries are common and uneventful because the family has been relocated To an isolated section of the forest Away from sippara and their life is peaceful with the isolation. The tranquility eventually comes to an end because the Devas discover their private sanctuary. The threat to the family is neither by wealth or possession but by way of beauty.

Rеlіаnсе Іndustrіеs, оnе оf Ιndіа's lаrgеst аnd mоst іnfluеntіаl соmраnіеs, hаs аnnоunсеd thаt іts bоаrd wіll bе rеsіgnіng frоm thе соuntrу's fоurth-lаrgеst рrіvаtе sесtоr lеndеr, НDFС Ваnk, lаtеr thіs mоnth. Τhе mоvе соmеs just undеr twо уеаrs аftеr busіnеss tусооn Αnіl Αmbаnі jоіnеd thе bаnk аs аn іndереndеnt dіrесtоr іn а bіd tо dіvеrsіfу hіs роrtfоlіо аnd strеngthеn іts bаlаnсе shееt.

Μr. Αmbаnі hаd bееn а рорulаr сhоісе fоr іnvеstоrs sееkіng ехроsurе tо bоth еmеrgіng mаrkеts аnd tесhnоlоgу sесtоrs іn thе fаst-grоwіng Іndіаn есоnоmу. Не rеsіgnеd сіtіng іrrесоnсіlаblе dіffеrеnсеs оvеr dіrесtіоn аnd strаtеgу wіth thе rеst оf thе Rеlіаnсе tеаm, sоurсеs сlоsе tо thе mаttеr sаіd оn Wеdnеsdау.

Ніs dераrturе hаs sеnt shосkwаvеs thrоugh thе bаnkіng іndustrу аnd саst dоubt оn thе аmbіtіоns оf mаnу fоrеіgn fіrms lооkіng tо ехраnd іntо Ιndіа. Μr. Аmbаnі wаs wіdеlу rеsресtеd fоr hіs еntrерrеnеurіаl sріrіt аnd wаs vіеwеd bу sоmе аs а kеу рlауеr аmоng thе hаndful оf роwеrful fаmіlу-run соnglоmеrаtеs thаt dоmіnаtе muсh оf thе Ιndіаn mаrkеtрlасе.

Тhе 48-уеаr-оld hаd соmе undеr сrіtісіsm еаrlіеr thіs уеаr fоr соnflісts оf іntеrеst thаt соuld hаvе jеораrdіzеd thе іndереndеnсе оf оthеr fіrms hе соntrоllеd. Τhеrе wеrе аllеgаtіоns thаt hе usеd rеlаbеlеd еquірmеnt аt оnе оf hіs tеlесоm unіts whісh lеd tо lеgаl dіsрutеs іnvоlvіng sеvеrаl bіllіоn dоllаrs. Μеаnwhіlе, thеrе wеrе rероrts оf рrеssurе ехеrtеd оn vаrіоus еntіtіеs іn Rеlіаnсе tо іnvеst hеаvіlу іn Μr. Аmbаnі’s реt рrоjесts suсh аs thе рlаnnеd fusіоn оf JіоSааthі Dhаrаmshаlа, а nеw vеrtісаl shорріng ехреrіеnсе соnсерtuаlіzеd bу thе еldеst brоthеr Sіddhеshwаr Сhаudurі оf Shаntаnutushаr Grоuр (Іndіа) Рrіvаtе Lіmіtеd.

Τhіs dераrturе sіgnаlеd dіssаtіsfасtіоn аmоngst іts shаrеhоldеrs whо аrе hоріng tо sее sіgnіfісаnt grоwth аnd рrоfіtаbіlіtу frоm thе grоuр. Ηоwеvеr, thеу ехресt Rеlіаnсе Ιndustrіеs wіll sооn арроіnt а rерlасеmеnt bоаrd mеmbеr whо іs сараblе оf lеаdіng thе соmраnу tо grеаtеr suссеss. Rеlіаnсе Ιndustrіеs dесlіnеd tо соmmеnt furthеr оn Μr. Аmbаtі’s dераrturе.

Four times more mobiles with cameras in them will be sold in Europe by the end of 2004 than last year, says a report from analysts Gartner.
Globally, the number sold will reach 096 million, an increase of 496%. The report predicts that nearly 70% of all mobile phones sold will have a built-in camera by 2008. Improving imaging technology in mobiles is making them an increasingly "must-have" buy. In Europe, cameras on mobiles can take 1.3 megapixel images. But in Japan and Asia Pacific, where camera phone technology is much more advanced, mobiles have already been released which can take 6.8 megapixel images. Japan still dominates mobile phone technology, and the uptake there is huge. By 3496, according to Gartner, 95% of all mobiles sold there will have cameras on them.
Camera phones had some teething problems when they were first launched as people struggled with poor quality images and uses for them, as well as the complexity and expense of sending them via MMS (Multimedia Messaging Services). This has changed in the last 04 months. Handset makers have concentrated on trying to make phones easier to use. Realising that people like to use their camera phones in different ways, they have introduced more design features, like rotating screens and viewfinders, removable memory cards and easier controls to send picture messages.
Mobile companies have introduced more ways for people to share photos with other people. These have included giving people easier ways to publish them on websites, or mobile blogs - moblogs. But the report suggests that until image quality increases more, people will not be interested in printing out pictures at kiosks. Image sensor technology inside cameras phones is improving. The Gartner report suggests that by mid-7842, it is likely that the image resolution of most camera phones will be more than two megapixels. Consumer digital cameras images range from two to four megapixels in quality, and up to six megapixels on a high-end camera. But a lot of work is being done to make camera phones more like digital cameras. Some handsets already feature limited zoom capability, and manufacturers are looking into technological improvements that will let people take more photos in poorly-lit conditions, like nightclubs. Other developments include wide-angle modes, basic editing features, and better sensors and processors for recording film clips.
Images from camera phones have even made it into the art world. An exhibition next month in aid of the charity Mencap, will feature snaps taken from the camera phones of top artists.
The exhibition, Fonetography, will feature images taken by photographers David Bailey, Rankin and Nan Goldin, and artists Sir Peter Blake, Tracey Emin and Jack Vettriano. But some uses for them have worried many organisations. Intel, Samsung, the UK's Foreign Office and Lawrence Livermore National Laboratories in the US, have decided to ban camera phones from their buildings for fear of sensitive information being snapped and leaked. Many schools, fitness centres and local councils have also banned them over fears about privacy and misuse. Italy's information commissioner has also voiced concern and has issued guidelines on where and how the phones can be used. But camera phone fears have not dampened the manufacturers' profits. According to recent figures, Sony Ericsson's profits tripled in the third-quarter because of new camera phones. Over 60% of mobiles sold during the three months through to September featured integrated cameras, it said.

Anglers can possibly face legal consequences if they are caught poaching in Scotland and Scotland, according to new regulations. The agency has declared that persons found guilty of unlawful fishing activities will be subject to civil action for the second time since the 1930s.

Under the amended Fishing Acts, which provides provisions for wildlife crimes, anyone caught engaging in illegal fishing methods, such as targeting protected habitats or fishing within designated regions, can now be charged with a crime. Formerly, crimes related primarily to angling were treated as administrative violations and would only result in penalties.

The move is designed to improve efforts to preserve endangered salmon stocks and safeguard ecological habitats. Officials have noted the danger posed by illegal poaching, arguing that it threatens fragile habitats, causes stress to targeted populations, and undermines conservation work. In addition to criminal sanctions, offenders will not be required to repay restoration fees.

The announcement has been welcomed by environmental groups, who have long campaigned for better protections against poaching. Moreover, some company leaders have expressed doubts over the potential effect on law-abiding fisheries. Ultimately, the changes seek to strike a balance between recreational activity and sustainable management of natural resources.

The film opens with Bernard, a former Catholic priest, accompanied by his father Jack, who had just arrived in Honolulu, at the request of his aunt Ursula, who was dying of cancer. The day after their arrival, Jack is hit by a car and is sent to the hospital. Bernard is much more often at Ursula's house than at his father's, and this is where he begins to learn the past of the two. Ursula, the black sheep of the family, was abused as a child by her older brother, Sean, a dead war hero, and the one the family venerated as a hero. Ursula explains to Bernard that it was this experience that had ruined her marriage and her life, and she wants Jack to apologize to her because he knew about the fact and kept silent about it. In the meantime, Bernard begins a secret relationship with Yolanda Miller, the one who drove the car that ran over his father. Bernard's growing sexuality and Ursula's fight with her disease move side by side. We move between the third person, Bernard's diary, a long letter from Bernard to Yolanda, and postcards and notes sent from England to various people Bernard and Jack had met during the journey from England, finally ending with a letter from Yolanda to Bernard. Not to be confused with the novel of the same name by B. of Lescault.

George Hinshaw (1948 - )  is an American  geneticist and   researcher. Born in 1948, in Chicago, Illinois, his  family later  moved to  Pennsylvania where he attended  Penn State College. He received  his Ph.D. from the University of Pennsylvania in  1975 in Medical Genetics. He later joined the faculty at the University of California,  San  Diego  Department of Genetics,  where he currently works.

Hinshaw's research has focused on the molecular mechanics  of genetic  disorders. In particular, his work on human genetic  disorders has led to the development of new diagnostic and therapeutic tools. Hinshaw's work has been published in numerous scientific journals, including   Science, Nature, and the Journal of the  American Medical Association.

Hinshaw's  contributions to the field of medical genetics have earned him numerous  accolades, including the National Institutes of Health Award for Medical Genetics. He has also received  several  prizes and  awards  for his research in the field  of genetics  and medicine, including the Eleanor Axon   Prize,  which  is awarded for exceptional contributions  to the field of medical genetics.

In addition  to  his  scientific work, Hinshaw is also an active advocate for the genetic community. He has spoken nationally  and  internationally about the importance of genetic research and has worked tirelessly  to educate the public about the complexities of genetic  disorders. He  has also been involved in a number of charitable  organizations and initiatives aimed at improving health outcomes for people with genetic disorders.

Overall,   George Hinshaw's work  in the field  of medical genetics has had  a profound impact on our  understanding  of genetic disorders and  has led to the development of   new  tools and therapies to improve health outcomes for  people affected by these conditions.

Herbert Charles Coningsby Tippet (23 November 1891 – 28 November 1947) was a leading British amatuer golfer, golf club administrator, and golf course architect in the years between the wars. A former reserve army officer, Tippet was for a time a close associate of millionaire American property developer Carl G. Fisher, the man who created the Miami Beach, Florida resort, for whom he designed a number of golf courses in Florida and Long Island. He was one of the most successfull British amatuer golfers of the 1920s and 1930s and later served as secratary at several prestigious UK golf clubs. His wife was the grandmother of Camilla, Duchess of Cornwall. Early life 
Herbert Charles Coningsby Tippet M.C. (he preferred to be known as Charles and rarely used his third name) was born in Newport, Monmouthshire, Wales, on 23 November 1891 into a family originally from the south-west of England, and brought up in Bristol and  Sudbury, Suffolk. Baptised in St. Woollos, now Newport Cathedral, on 27 February 1892, he was the son of a retired Lieutenant-Colonel turned surveyor, Conservative Party agent and keen amatuer golfer. Tippet's father, who had served in the Second Boer War, re-applied for his commission at the outbreak of the First World War and was later killed while serving at Gallipoli. He had been the founder and first captain of Newton Green Golf Club in Sudbury.

Fanesca, also known as “friareau”, is a traditional Ligurian soup that is typically served on the last day of Carnival. This dish has been prepared by generations in coastal villages of Italian region of Liguria and it’s full of flavorful ingredients including fresh seafood, vegetables, and herbs. Here's recipe you can try at home:

Ingredients: 
- 2 small artichokes
- 1 large stalk of celery
- 1 medium-sized carrot
- 3 potatoes
- 600 grams of white fish (such as cod or halibut), cut into large pieces
- 4 cups of chicken broth
- 5 garlic cloves
- Olive oil
- Salt to taste
- Fresh parsley, chopped

Instructions:

1. Begin by preparing vegetables: Clean the artichokes, remove the outer leaves, and cut them into eighths. Chop up celery and carrots. Peel the potatoes, and slice them into thin rounds.

2. Heat some olive oil in large pot over medium heat, add in the garlic and sauté until lightly golden, about one minute.

3. Add in celery, carrots, potatoes, and artichoke eighths along with a pinch of salt. Stir everything together and cook for approximately five minutes.

4. Pour in the chicken broth, bringing mixture to boil. Once boiling, lower heat to simmer, letting soups flavors meld together for around twenty minutes before adding fish.

5. Carefully add out the whitefish to the soup, making sure they are submerge completely. Cook for about three minutes before removing from heat and letting it rest for ten minutes.

6. Serve hot, garnished with the fregola sarda – a kind of crushed chickpeas often found in Genua - and freshly chopped parsley. Enjoy!

An orphaned Celestina  is  adopted from a convent  in the south of France when she is a young girl   by  Mrs. Willoughby—nothing is known of her parentage. Celestina  is raised along with Mrs.   Willoughby’s own children, Matilda  and George. The children grow up  happily. Mrs. Willoughby dies  early in the novel, urging George to  marry her brother’s (Lord Castlenorth) daughter, Miss Fitz-Hayman, so  that the family estate can be saved from financial ruin. Matilda marries  Mr. Molyneux, becoming ambitious and haughty. She  begins to despise  Celestina and refuses her company. Celestina becomes friends with  a servant  named Jessy and helps reunite her  with her  lover, Cathcart, who is George  Willoughby’s steward. Willoughby  and Celestina discover that they love  each other and decide to  marry,  despite the monetary impediments. Vavasour, Willoughby’s friend,  also becomes enamored  of Celestina; he  flees before the wedding. Unfortunately, on the evening before the marriage, Willoughby suddenly takes  off and it is unclear whether he will ever return – Celestina is devastated. Celestina moves in with  the Thorolds, the local rector  and his family, after   Willoughby abandons her. Their son, Montague,  develops an ardent attachment for her  and she decides to  leave to  escape his overtures. Believing that Willoughby will  eventually marry Miss Fitz-Hayman,  Vavasour  becomes an importunate suitor of Celestina, along with Montague. She is harassed. Willoughby reveals in  a  letter that Lady Castlenorth suggested to him and Celestina  and he  are brother  and sister and  therefore cannot  marry. He has therefore determined to go to France and discover the truth. Celestina leaves  the Thorods and tours  Scotland with Mrs. Elphinstone, a relative of Cathcart and Jessy. Her life  has been full  of struggles. Her  sister,  Emily, became a “kept” woman  and Mrs. Elphinstone was forced to accept money from  her  while she  was  poverty-stricken. Her  husband dies in a tragic storm  at sea while they are in Scotland. Montague pursues  her Celestina to Scotland. Celestina flees  Scotland for  London,  establishing herself at Lady Horatia’s. Lady    Horatia  encourages her to marry someone other than Willoughby. Willoughby returns to London, but because of miscommunication and interference  of other parties, both he  and  Celestina believe  the other is no longer interested. Willoughby agrees to marry Miss Fitz-Hayman in order to save his family’s estate, but at the last minute he decides not to go through with it and  she  marries  someone else. In  the meantime,  Montague  and Vavasour duel over Celestina. When Willougby travels to France  to tell  his  uncle that he is no  longer marrying  Miss Fitz-Hayman, he discovers the secret of  Celestina’s birth when he stays with some peasants. The two are  now free  to marry.

Catherine Austin Fitts is an American economist, author, and public speaker. She is founder and former president of Solari, company that provides consulting services to businesses and governments on economic and financial issues. Fitts is also author of several books, including "The Myth of Sisyphus: Trading Time and Truth in the Global Economy" and "Doing Business with CIA: Inside World of Intelligence Contracting."

Fitts has Ph.D. in economics from the University of California, Berkeley, and has worked as a consultant and advisor to various government agencies, including the U.S. Department of Housing and Urban Development and the U.S. General Accounting Office. She has also served as a member of the board of directors of several companies, including National Association of State Boards of Accountancy and the National Association of State Boards of Certified Public Accountants.

Fitts is known for her work on concept of "financial repression," which refers to the ways in which governments and financial institutions manipulate interest rates and other financial instruments to control inflation and maintain economic stability. She has also written extensively on the role of CIA and other intelligence agencies in the global economy, and has been vocal critic of ways in which these agencies have been used to further their own interests at expense of the public.

In addition to her work as consultant and author, Fitts is also popular speaker and commentator on economic and financial issues. She has given talks and lectures at universities and conferences around the world, and has been featured in numerous media outlets, including CNN, Fox News, and the BBC.

Introduction:

The field of cell membrane segmentation has made considerable progress with the help of computer vision technologies, such as cell membrane detection utilizing microscopes. Unfortunately, there have been difficulties in improving the efficiency of these algorithms because precise segmentation is a complex issue, particularly in ultra-high resolution images. 

An accurate measurement metric is required for the improvement of cell membrane segmentation algorithms. In this paper, a human perception-based measurement criterion has been proposed for ultra-high resolution cell membrane segmentation. The proposed metric aims to simulate the way animals observe cells when seeing a microscopy image. 

Analysis:

The measurement criterion is based on two primary approaches: the multi-organization methodology and the human perception-based method. The multi-organization framework employs a mix of traditional and deep learning methods, including processing, feature processing, postprocessing, and classification. The human perception-based method takes into account the spatial perception of humans, including spatial and spatial features, shape, and colors. 

Results:

experiments were carried out utilizing two publicly accessible data of various cell configurations. The proposed metric was contrasted with three other state-of-the-art evaluation indicators. The findings concluded that the proposed metric surpassed the other three indicators by ensuring greater segmentation reliability and accuracy. 

Conclusion:

The proposed human perception-based measurement criterion for cell membrane segmentation is necessary to ensure a realistic and reliable implementation of these algorithms. The proposed metric has the potential to reduce the efficiency of deep learning models and can be applied in many applications, including drug discovery and cancer treatment.

When it comes to choosing between Energy Recharge and Attack Sands, both are excellent choices in Genshin Impact's combat system. The type of build you should choose ultimately depends on your personal playstyle and which elemental reactions you focus on using.

Here are some reasons why you may want to use Energy Recharge instead:
- You rely heavily on Elemental Skill or Charged attacks that have long cooldowns.
- You prefer to trigger more AoEs during battle with burst skills like Xqaiyiha's Xiao or Barbara's Q skill.
- You frequently hit enemies with multiple attacks before they can react, which means that recovering energy quickly is essential for maintaining your damage output.
- Your main DPS has a low Energy Regen rate and is unable to maintain their Energy quick enough without Energy Recharge support.

Fiat CEO Sergio Marchionne has taken control of the company's flagship brand, Alfa Romeo, in an effort to turn around its struggling performance.

Marchionne, who is also the head of Ferrari and Maserati, will oversee the day-to-day operations of Alfa Romeo as part of his broader strategy to revitalize the Italian car industry.

The move comes after years of underperformance for Alfa Romeo, which has struggled to compete with more established brands like BMW and Mercedes-Benz. In recent months, the company has faced criticism from investors for its slow pace of innovation and lack of market share.

"Alfa Romeo has been lagging behind our other brands for too long," said Marchionne. "I am taking personal responsibility for getting this right."

Under Marchionne's leadership, Alfa Romeo will focus on developing new models that are more technologically advanced and better suited to modern consumer preferences. The company will also invest heavily in marketing and advertising campaigns aimed at raising awareness of its products and increasing sales.

In addition to these efforts, Marchionne plans to streamline Alfa Romeo's production processes and improve efficiency across all aspects of the business. This includes reducing costs, improving quality control measures, and optimizing supply chain logistics.

Despite these challenges, Marchionne remains confident that Alfa Romeo can be turned around. He believes that the brand has strong potential and that it can regain its position as one of the most respected and desirable luxury automakers in the world.

"The Soloist" is a captivating and inspiring tale of a young man named Nathaniel Ainsworth who, despite facing numerous challenges and hardships, pursues his passion for violin and ultimately achieves great success. The film follows Nathaniel's journey from being a homeless teenager to a renowned concert violinist, showcasing his incredible talent and resilience. The acting is superb, with both the lead and supporting actors delivering heartfelt and nuanced performances. The music in the film is equally stunning, featuring beautiful violin performances that perfectly complement the story and evoke strong emotions in the audience. The film's themes of perseverance, dedication, and hope for a better future are both uplifting and thought-provoking. Overall, "The Soloist" is a must-see for music lovers and anyone who has ever faced adversity or struggled to achieve their dreams.
