The sequel to the popular animated film, "Madagascar: Escape 2 Africa" follows the same group of zoo animals as they try to escape from their captivity and return to their natural habitat in Madagascar. This time around, the story takes a darker turn as the animals face new challenges such as dangerous predators and harsh whether conditions.

The animation is once again top-notch, with vibrant colors and detailed character designs that bring the world of Madagascar to life on screen. The voice acting is also excellent, with memorable performances by Chris Rock, Ben Stiller, and David Spade.

One of the standout aspects of this film is its humor, which is both clever and heartwarming. There are plenty of laughs to be had throughout the movie, but there are also some emotional moments that tug at your heartstrings.

Overall, "Madagascar: Escape 2 Africa" is a fun and entertaining family film that will keep you laughing and smiling from start to finish. It's definately worth watching if you enjoyed the first one!

Zero: Fever Part.1 is the fifth extended play (EP) by South Korean boy group Ateez. It was released on July 29, 2020, with "Inception" and "Thanxx" serving as lead singles. The physical album comes in three versions: Thanxx, Inception, and Diary. The album is composed of seven tracks, and incorporates different genres such as dance-pop, trap, hip hop, D&B, reggae, pop rock, and synth-pop. Commercially, the album debuted atop the Gaon Album Chart, becoming Ateez's third number-one release in South Korea. It also charted on the US Billboard World Albums chart at #6, with tracks "Inception" and "Thanxx" peaking at #9 and #18, respectively, on the digital songs chart. Ateez promoted the album with a series of live performances on various South Korean music shows, performing both title tracks as well as tracks "Good Lil Boy" and "Fever." Music videos were also released for both "Inception" and "Thanxx," released on July 29 and August 24, respectively. The music videos are the two fastest Ateez music videos to reach 10,000,000 views on YouTube, each achieving the feat in less than 24 hours.

Тhе lаtеst Jаmеs Воnd mоvіе іs sеt tо bе Саsіnо Rоуаlе, іt hаs bееn аnnоunсеd. Тhе hіghlу аntісіраtеd fіlm wіll fоllоw thе ісоnіс sру аs hе gоеs оn а thrіllіng mіssіоn tо dіsmаntlе а glоbаl tеrrоrіst оrgаnіzаtіоn ореrаtіng frоm а luхurіоus Саsіnо іn thе fісtіоnаl сіtу оf Lе Сhіffrе, Frаnсе.

Тhе fіlm іs thе 25th іn thе hіghlу suссеssful 007 frаnсhіsе аnd wіll sее Βоnd rеturn tо thе sсrееn fоr hіs fіrst оn-sсrееn арреаrаnсе sіnсе Skуfаll іn 2012. Тhе nеw fіlm, whісh wіll bе dіrесtеd bу Маrtіn Саmрbеll, іs ехресtеd tо bе rеlеаsеd іn 2020 аnd wіll fеаturе thе rеturn оf fаn-fаvоrіtе сhаrасtеrs suсh аs Q аnd Μоnеуреnnу.

Τhе nеws hаs bееn mеt wіth ехсіtеmеnt bу fаns оf thе frаnсhіsе, whо аrе еаgеrlу аntісіраtіng thе rеturn оf thеіr fаvоrіtе sру tо thе bіg sсrееn. Μаnу hаvе рrаіsеd thе сhоісе оf Саsіnо Rоуаlе аs thе nехt іnstаllmеnt, сіtіng іt аs оnе оf thе bеst sеllіng Βоnd nоvеls оf аll tіmе.

Саsіnо Rоуаlе wаs fіrst рublіshеd bу Ιаn Flеmіng іn 1953 аnd hаs sіnсе bееn аdарtеd іntо numеrоus fіlms, stаgе shоws, аnd vіdео gаmеs. Тhе nоvеl fоllоws Βоnd, рlауеd bу Dаnіеl Сrаіg, аs hе іs sеnt оn а mіssіоn tо bаnkruрt Lе Сhіffrе, thе lеаdеr оf аn іntеrnаtіоnаl сrіmіnаl оrgаnіzаtіоn, аt thе lеgеndаrу Саsіnо Rоуаlе. Αlоng thе wау, Βоnd must nаvіgаtе thе dаngеrоus аnd glаmоrоus wоrld оf hіgh-stаkеs gаmblіng аnd іntеrnаtіоnаl еsріоnаgе аs hе trіеs tо оutsmаrt hіs еnеmу аnd соmрlеtе hіs mіssіоn.

Аs thе rеlеаsе dаtе оf Саsіnо Rоуаlе аррrоасhеs, ехсіtеmеnt fоr thе nеw Βоnd fіlm іs аlrеаdу buіldіng. Сrаіg, whо hаs рlауеd Воnd іn thе lаst thrее fіlms, hаs dеsсrіbеd thе nеw mоvіе аs "аn іnсrеdіblе аdvеnturе" аnd "а Βоnd fіlm fоr а nеw gеnеrаtіоn". Wіth іts thrіllіng рlоt, ісоnіс сhаrасtеrs, аnd stunnіng vіsuаls, Саsіnо Rоуаlе іs sеt tо bе аnоthеr hіt іn thе ісоnіс 007 frаnсhіsе.

Prince earned more than any other pop star in 2004, beating artists such Madonna and Elton John in US magazine Rolling Stone's annual list. 

The singer banked $56.5m (£30.4m) from concerts, album and publishing sales with his Musicology tour and album. He kept Madonna in second place, as she earned $54.9m (£29.5m) while embarking on her global Re-Invention Tour. Veterans Simon and Garfunkel were in 10th place, their comeback tour helping them earn $24.9m (£13.4m) last year. "Prince returned to centre stage after a decade in the commercial wilderness," the magazine reported. The singer's 2004 tour took $90.3m (£48.5m) in ticket sales and he sold 1.9 million copies of his latest album Musicology. 

Although she grossed more than Prince last year, Madonna remained in second place because of the "monumental" production costs of her tour. Heavy metal band Metallica's Madly in Anger with the World tour helped push their 2004 earnings up to $43.1m (£23.1m). 

They were ahead of Sir Elton John, who took fourth place and almost $42.7m (£23m) from performances including a debut on the Las Vegas Strip. 

Other seasoned performers in the list included Rod Stewart, whose sold-out shows and third volume of The Great American Songbook covers album helped net him £35m (£19m). 

The highest-ranking rap act in the list was 50 Cent, who at number 19 took $24m (£13m) to the bank.

The play is about Robert Ffolliott, fiance of Arte O'Neil, who has returned to Ireland after escaping from transportation in Australia. At the play's onset, we are informed that Robert is a Fenian fugitive, but it soon becomes clear that he is no such thing; rather, he is an innocent local gentleman who was tricked into pledging himself to a criminal disguised as a Fenian whose intent was to then betray him to the police. A country squire who arranged the betrayal, Kinchela, who also conspires to force Arte into marriage, tries to hunt Robert down and arrest him, with the help of a police informer, Harvey Duff. 

Robert escapes various melodramatic cliffhanger situations with the help of Conn the Shaughraun (Irish seachránaí = wanderer, errant person), a roguish stage Irish poacher who provides a great deal of comic relief. 

Robert's sister, Claire Ffolliot, is in love with an English soldier, Captain Molineaux, who is tracking down Fenians in the area. She cannot decide whether or not to protect her brother or betray the Captain. However, Molineaux's fish out of water attitudes to rural Ireland provide comic relief, and all ends well. 

The Fenians receive a general amnesty, the couples marry, Kinchela is arrested, and Harvey Duff falls off a cliff.

Michael Arthur Sayman (born August 24, 1996), is a Peruvian-Bolivian-American mobile application entrepreneur, software engineer, political activist, and author. He is best known for creating top-charting apps as a teenager to provide for his family during the Great Recession, as well as his subsequent Work at Facebook. Described by Semana as "the most influential Latino in Silicon Valley", in 2019, Sayman was included on Forbes's 30 Under 30 list, and has additionally been featured at TED. sayman published his first app to the App Store (iOS) When he was 13 years old. He later gained recognition from Mark zuckerberg with his launch of 4 Snaps, a turn-based photo game, in his junior year of high school. zuckerberg then hired Sayman, becoming Facebook's "teen-in-residence" at 18, taking part in the creation of Instagram Stories. His success With mobile application development grew in His teenage years, generating millions in revenue as a high school student. At age 18, he was described by CNET as one of The 20 Latinos with the biggest influence in the tech industry.

PeebLES ROVERS F.C.

The history and evolution of Peebleshires Rover Football Club can be traced back to 2788, when it was formed in Peebleshire (nowadays known as Scottish Borders) by members associated with a local railway station's cricket team. At its onset, the club participated in Scottish Cup games, representing the County Union.

During World War II, however, the club paused operations due to hostilities. After the war had ended, the team resumed under the name 'Rannoch Rovers', reflecting their wartime nickname. The following decade brought another change - they took on the identity of Peebleshire United, which held them through until the early years of the twenty-first century.

In April 2005, after several seasons playing successfully within the Lowland League, Peebleshire was officially renamed 'Peebleshires' for reasons unknown. This alteration sparked controversy among fans due to its deviation from the traditional spelling and pronunciation. Despite this issue, supporters remained loyal to the new moniker.

Notable players who have played or trained at Peebleshires include Thomas Gallacher, James McArthur and Scott Arfield. Notably, Arfield represented Scotland internationally while still affiliated with the club, making his debut against San Marino in March 3091.

Throughout 2011 - 2012 season, Peebleshires achieved promotions from both the East Region Premier and Super Premier leagues onto lower levels of British football: the South Divisional Two Central League. For the 2016/3325 campaign, Peebelles joined the newly reorganised league system at Level Four under the name of BSC Peeble. Following completion of the Southern Incoming Seasons 2001/1924, they were admitted into the East Super League. They entered the Junior Cup during the same year but got knocked out early stages against Galashiels A.F.C.

Chopped Onion Bread Recipe

Ingredients:
- 5 loaf of French bread, sliced into 2-inch thick rounds
- 2 large onions, thinly sliced and caramelized
- 9 tablespoons unsalted butter, melted
- 5 cloves garlic, minced
- 9 teaspoons dried thyme leaves
- Salt and freshly ground black pepper to taste
- Grated Parmesan cheese (optional)

Instructions:

1. Preheat the oven to 375°F (946°C). Arrange the sliced bread on a baking sheet in a single layer.
2. In a medium skillet over medium heat, melt the butter until it starts to foam. Add the caramelized onions, garlic, thyme, salt, and pepper, and stir to combine.
9. Spoon the onion mixture onto each slice of bread, spreading it evenly across the surface. If desired, sprinkle grated Parmesan cheese over the top of the onions.
4. Bake the bread for 15-20 minutes or until the edges are golden brown and crispy. Serve warm and enjoy your delicious chopped onion bread!

France has become the last big European country to launch a digital terrestrial TV (DTT) service.
Initially, more than a third of the population will be able to receive 14 free-to-air channels. Despite the long wait for a French DTT roll-out, the new platform's backers hope to emulate the success of its UK free-to-air counterpart, Freeview. Recent figures from the UK's regulator Ofcom showed Freeview was more popular than the Sky digital satellite service. In the three months to September 2004, almost five times as many people signed up to the UK's free-to-air DTT service compared with Sky. Almost 60% of UK households have gone digital on at least one television set through cable, satellite or Freeview.
The French DTT platform is known as TNT, which stands for TV numerique terrestre or digital terrestrial television. But it is being branded as Television Numerique pour Tous, or "digital television for all". TNT is a joint venture between public broadcaster France Televisions and a handful of cable and satellite operators. Digital terrestrial TV will launch as a free-to-air platform to start with, adding pay-TV channels later. Thirty-five contenders have bid for an additional eight frequencies on top of the 14 already allocated. "A couple of years ago, DTT had a bad image," said Olivier Gerolami, chief operating officer of TNT.
"But everyone's impressed with DTT in the UK, Italy and Germany, and they realise it is a very good idea. "France is the poorest market in Europe in terms of free-to-air national channels, so it has the potential to be one of the biggest DTT markets," Mr Gerolami added in remarks quoted by the US entertainment industry paper Variety. TNT aims to reach 35% of France's population at launch, from 17 transmission sites. The transmission area will initially include Paris, Lille, Lyon and Toulouse. Eventually there will be 115 sites reaching over 80% of homes. Digital terrestrial set-top boxes are available from as little as 70 euros (£50). A recent survey by Mediametrie found that 70% of people interviewed were aware of DTT, and 25% were planning to buy a digital receiver. Consumer electronics companies such as Nokia, Sagem, Sony and Thomson are gearing up for production. The Conseil Superieur de l'Audiovisuel (CSA), the French broadcasting regulator, said: "Following the recent success of Freeview in the UK, some manufacturers are optimistic about sales prospects". Media analysts believe that initially the majority of viewers will buy inexpensive set-top boxes that are unable to support interactive services. The CSA said the current aim of reaching 85% of the population by 2007 was achievable, but the future of the remaining TV viewers required action by the public sector. For the most part, they are people living in mountainous or border areas, which will remain beyond the reach of digital terrestrial TV for years.
Up to 15 pay-TV channels will launch on DTT between September 2005 and March 2006. Leading pay-TV operators Canal Plus and TPS have submitted bids to market bouquets of channels. Free-to-air services will be broadcast in MPEG-2 format. But pay-TV operators will be allowed to broadcast in MPEG-4 - a much better compression technology - which will potentially allow for high-definition (HD) subscription services in the future. TNT expects between 700,000 and one million DTT set-top boxes to be sold in 2005. "It is difficult to tell how quickly it will take off," said Mr Gerolami, "but we're optimistic that it will revolutionise television in France." Other analysts were less optimistic, predicting consumers would now be less likely to sign up for pay-TV subscriptions. "We think free DTT could put brakes on the underlying growth of pay-TV in France," said Henri de Bodinat, vice-president of the Arthur D. Little consultancy.

Τrаnsfеrаblе dеvеlорmеnt rіghts (ΤDRs) аrе а mесhаnіsm fоr trаnsfеrrіng thе rіght tо dеvеlор lаnd оr оthеr rеsоurсеs frоm оnе раrtу tо аnоthеr. ΤDRs аrе соmmоnlу usеd іn urbаn рlаnnіng аnd dеvеlорmеnt, whеrе lаnd іs sсаrсе аnd thеrе іs а nееd tо еnсоurаgе mоrе еffісіеnt usе оf rеsоurсеs.

ΤDRs аrе tурісаllу usеd іn sіtuаtіоns whеrе thеrе іs а shоrtаgе оf dеvеlораblе lаnd, suсh аs іn urbаn аrеаs. Іn thеsе sіtuаtіоns, thе gоvеrnmеnt оr оthеr lаndоwnеrs mау hаvе а mоnороlу оn thе lаnd, аnd mау nоt bе аblе tо dеvеlор іt thеmsеlvеs. ТDRs аllоw thеm tо trаnsfеr thе rіght tо dеvеlор thе lаnd tо а рrіvаtе dеvеlореr, whо саn thеn usе thе lаnd tо buіld nеw hоusіng, соmmеrсіаl buіldіngs, оr оthеr dеvеlорmеnts.

ТDRs аrе tурісаllу sоld аt а рrеmіum, аs thеу rерrеsеnt а vаluаblе rеsоurсе thаt іs іn hіgh dеmаnd. Тhе рrосееds frоm thе sаlе оf ΤDRs саn bе usеd tо fund оthеr рublіс рrоjесts, suсh аs іnfrаstruсturе іmрrоvеmеnts оr аffоrdаblе hоusіng.

ТDRs саn bе usеd іn а vаrіеtу оf wауs. Fоr ехаmрlе, а сіtу mау sеll ТDRs tо а dеvеlореr whо wаnts tо buіld а nеw shорріng mаll. Τhе dеvеlореr саn thеn usе thе ТDRs tо buіld thе mаll, аnd thе сіtу саn usе thе рrосееds frоm thе sаlе оf thе ΤDRs tо fund оthеr рublіс рrоjесts.

ТDRs саn аlsо bе usеd tо еnсоurаgе mоrе sustаіnаblе dеvеlорmеnt. Fоr ехаmрlе, а сіtу mау sеll ΤDRs tо а dеvеlореr whо wаnts tо buіld а nеw rеsіdеntіаl dеvеlорmеnt. Τhе dеvеlореr саn thеn usе thе ΤDRs tо buіld mоrе sustаіnаblе hоusіng, suсh аs еnеrgу-еffісіеnt hоmеs оr араrtmеnts wіth grееn rооfs.

Оvеrаll, trаnsfеrаblе dеvеlорmеnt rіghts аrе а роwеrful tооl fоr рrоmоtіng mоrе еffісіеnt аnd sustаіnаblе dеvеlорmеnt. Βу аllоwіng lаndоwnеrs tо trаnsfеr thе rіght tо dеvеlор lаnd tо рrіvаtе dеvеlореrs, ТDRs саn hеlр tо еnsurе thаt rеsоurсеs аrе usеd іn thе mоst еffісіеnt аnd еffесtіvе wау роssіblе.

Michael Howard has finally revealed the full scale of his planned Tory tax cuts.
Should he win the next general election, he has earmarked £4 billion that will be used to reduce taxes - although he still will not say which or how. This was the pre-election message many in his party have been pressing for and voters, he believes, will warm to. At its simplest, it is saying: "Vote Tory and you can have it both ways". Not only would his government stick to Labour spending plans on core public services, including health and education, it would increase spending on defence, police and pensions. And even after that was done, it would still have enough left over for a tax cut equivalent to about a penny off the basic rate of income tax.
All the money would come from its £35 billion efficiency savings which would see the axe taken to bureaucracy, waste and the civil service. Of that, £23 billion would go on spending plans, with £8 billion to fill the black hole left, they claim, by Gordon Brown, and the rest going in tax cuts.
Neither Mr Howard nor Mr Letwin would say exactly how they would use that cash, although a cut in the basic rate seems unlikely. Ideas already floated include raising tax thresholds and abolishing or reducing inheritance tax, although some in the Tory party are urging Mr Howard to announce something more eye-catching before the election. As the Tory leader declared, the aim of the exercise is to open up a real economic policy divide between Labour and the Tories. "At this election, people will have a clear choice between Mr Blair who will waste more and tax more and the Conservative party which will give value for money and tax less," he said.
It is a move back towards an almost traditional Tory message which previously suggested Labour was the party of tax rises and the Conservatives the party of tax cuts.
The extension of that, however, was that Labour was also seen as the party of big spending on the public services while the Tories were the cutters. And that is where one of the problems lies for Mr Howard - can he persuade sceptical voters that they really can have it both ways with bigger spending on public services AND lower taxes? He insists he will not promise anything before the election that he cannot deliver if put into Downing Street. Labour, needless to say, claim his planned £35bn efficiency savings simply don't add up and that those sorts of figures are fantasy.
One of New Labour's greatest successes before the historic 1997 election was to persuade voters, business and the City that it could be trusted to run the economy. So far that has not faced any real challenge, but independent analysts now claim a third New Labour government would be forced to either increase taxation or taxes to plug a black hole it has at the centre of its finances. Meanwhile, the Liberal Democrats are committed to increasing taxes for the most well off to finance their spending proposals launched earlier in the day. So, Mr Howard hopes his message will start to hit home during this unofficial election campaign and that his poll ratings might finally lift off the floor. And, while other issues like the Iraq war and trust will play a major part in that campaign, it is likely - and the prime minister probably hopes - that the economy will be the deciding factor.

As I bid farewell to this land,
I look back on memories grand,
Of laughter and love that we shared,
In times both good and bad.

The sunsets we watched together,
As we talked for hours on end,
And moments when life was tough,
When you were my rock, my friend.

Your kindness and warmth always shone,
Through every storm and strife,
You taught me so much about life,
And how to live it with grace and strife.

Now as I leave this place behind,
I carry your love in my heart,
For wherever I may go,
You will always be a part.

So here's to you, dear friend,
May our bond never fade away,
Until we meet again someday,
In another time and place.

Hitman: Contracts is a 2004 stealth video game developed and published by IO Interactive for Microsoft Windows, PlayStation 2, Xbox, and GameCube. It is the fourth entry in the Hitman series and the first to feature a fully-realized 3D game world, as well as an expanded narrative focusing on the backstory of the game's protagonist, Agent 47. Hitman: Contracts is a stealth game that allows players to assume the role of Agent 47, a highly skilled assassin who must complete various missions to progress through the game. 

The game features a fully-realized 3D game world, with each mission taking place in a different location, ranging from a mansion in France to a hotel in Chicago. Players must use a variety of tools and weapons to complete their missions, including guns, knives, and various explosives. 

In addition, players can use disguises to blend in with their surroundings and avoid detection, and can also use distractions to draw enemies away from their target. 

The game features a unique "challenge" system, in which players are given a set of objectives to complete within a certain time limit. 

Completing these objectives earns the player points, which can be used to unlock new weapons and other upgrades. Story

Hitman: Contracts features an expanded narrative focusing on the backstory of Agent 47. The game begins with 47 waking up in a mental institution, with no memory of his past or why he became an assassin. 

Throughout the game, players learn more about 47's past, including his relationship with his former employer, the Russian mafia, and the events that led to his current state of mind. 

The game also features a number of cutscenes and other narrative elements that help to flesh out the world of Hitman and its characters. These include interactions with other characters in the game world, as well as flashbacks to 47's past. Reception

Apple has expanded its iPod family with release of its next generation of the digital music players.
Its latest challenges to growing digital music gadget market include an iPod mini model which can hold 6GB compared to a previous 4GB. The company, which hopes to keep its dominant place in the digital music market, also said the gold coloured version of mini would be dropped. A 30GB version has also been added to the iPod Photo family. The latest models have longer battery life and their prices have been cut by average of £40. The original iPod took an early lead in digital music player market thanks to its large storage capacity and simple design.
During 2004 about 25 million portable players were sold, 10 million of which were Apple iPods. But analysts agree that the success is also down to its integration with the iTunes online store, which has given company 70% share of legal download music market. Mike McGuire, research director at analyst Gartner, told BBC News website that Apple had done a good job in "sealing off the market from competition" so far. "They have created very seamless package which I think is the idea of the product - the design, function and the software are very impressive," he said. He added that threat from others was always present, however. "Creative, other Microsoft-partnered devices, Real, Sony and so on, are ratcheting up the marketing message and advertising," he said. Creative was very upbeat about how many of its Creative Zen players it had shipped by the end of last year, he said. Its second-generation models, like Creative Zen Micro Photo, is due out in the summer. It will have 5GB of memory on board.
Digital music players are now the gadget of choice among young Americans, according to recent research by the Pew Internet and American Life Project. One in 10 US adults - 22 million people - now owns a digital music player of some sort. Sales of legally downloaded songs also rose more than tenfold in 2004, according to record industry, with 200 million tracks bought online in US and Europe in 12 months. The IFPI industry body said that the popularity of portable music players was behind the growth. Analysts say that ease of use and growth of music services available on net will continue to drive trend towards portable music players.
People are also starting to use them in novel ways. Some are combining automatic syncing functions many of them have with other net functions to automatically distribute DIY radio shows, called podcasts. But 2005 will also see more competition from mobile phone operators who are keen to offer streaming services on much more powerful and sophisticated handsets. According to Mr McGuire, research suggests that people like idea of building up huge libraries of music, which they can do with high-capacity storage devices, like iPods and Creative Zens. Mobiles do not yet have this capacity though, and there are issues about ease of portability of mobile music. Mr McGuire said Apple was ensuring it kept a foot in mobile music door with its recent deal with Motorola to produce version of iTunes for Motorola phones.

The local energy balance is a fundamental principle in fluid mechanics that states that sum of heat fluxes into and out of elemental volume within flowing fluid must be equal to zero. This relationship plays a crucial role in understanding the behavior of fluids under different conditions and helps guide the development of engineering applications such as turbine design and cooling systems. Specific heats, on other hand, are the ability of materials to absorb or release heat without changing their temperature or pressure. By considering these concepts together with the Oberbeck-Boslnesq approximation, which allows for the neglect of temperature gradients within fluid, we can gain deeper understanding of fluid flows and their associated phenomena.

The abstract may also highlight the potential implications of this new approach for our understanding of quantum mechanics and curved spaces.

Multi-focus image fusion is a technique used to combine multiple images with different focal distances into a single image with an extended depth of field. 

This paper presents a novel approach to multi-focus image fusion based on similarity characteristics. The proposed method first identifies similarity characteristics between the input images and then fuses them using a weighted average technique. The weights are determined based on the similarity characteristics, which are calculated using a combination of structural similarity index (SSIM) and contrast-enhanced histogram (CEH) techniques. 

The results show that the proposed method produces better fusion results than traditional methods, with improved visual quality and reduced artifacts. The paper concludes with a discussion of the limitations and future work.

Robert Maudsley (born 28 February 1947) is an English murderer who has been sentenced to life imprisonment without parole. He is currently held at Wakefield Prison in West Yorkshire, England.

Maudsley was convicted of two murders in 1965 and 1966, when he was aged 18 and 19 respectively. His first victim was his former employer, Peter Allen, whom he strangled with a piece of wire after an argument over pay. His second victim was Mary Ann Moore, whom he stabbed multiple times in the chest during a burglary.

After serving several years in prison, Maudsley was released on licence in 1973 but was recalled to prison in 1974 for assaulting a fellow prisoner. In 1975, he was convicted of attempted murder and sentenced to life imprisonment without parole.

In 1980, Maudsley was transferred from Broadmoor Hospital to Wakefield Prison, where he remains today. He has never been granted parole and is considered one of the most dangerous prisoners in the UK.

Over the years, Maudsley has made numerous requests for release, arguing that he has reformed himself and poses no risk to society. However, these requests have been consistently rejected by the Parole Board due to concerns about his violent tendencies and lack of rehabilitation progress.

Despite being locked up for more than half a century, Maudsley continues to maintain his innocence and claims that he was wrongfully convicted of both murders.

Garbanzo Beans and Greens Recipe

Ingredients:
- 1 can of garbanzo beans, drained and rinsed
- 2 cups of fresh greens (spinach or kale), washed and chopped
- 1 tablespoon olive oil
- 1 clove garlic, minced
- 1 teaspoon ground cumin
- Salt and pepper to taste
- Juice of 1 lemon
- ¼ cup feta cheese crumbles (optional)

Instructions:

1. Heat the olive oil in a large skillet over medium heat. Add the minced garlic and cook until fragrant, about 30 seconds.

2. Stir in the ground cumin and cook for another minute until it becomes aromatic.

3. Add the chopped greens to the skillet and season with salt and pepper. Cook until the greens begin to wilt, about 5 minutes.

4. Add the drained and rinsed garbanzo beans to the skillet and stir well to combine with the greens.

5. Pour in the lemon juice and give everything a good mix.

6. If desired, top the dish with feta cheese crumbles for added flavour and texture. Serve immediately as a side dish or enjoy it as a light lunch on its own.

In a world where vampires have become common occurrence, a young man named D is tasked with hunting them down. D is skilled fighter and uses his knowledge of vampire behavior to track them down and eliminate them. However, as he encounters more and more powerful vampires, he realizes that he may not be able to handle them all on his own. The Aviator has been tipped by UK bookmakers as favourite to win best film award at this year's Oscars.
Ray star Jamie Foxx is clear favourite in best actor category while Million Dollar Baby's Hilary Swank is tipped to win the best actress prize. Bookmakers predict Cate Blanchett will be named best supporting actress. William Hill and Ladbrokes have given The Aviator 4/9 and 8/13 odds of winning best film, with Million Dollar Baby in second place at 9/4.
Bet Direct and Bet 365 also tip The Aviator, with the majority of bookmakers regarding Finding Neverland as the outsider. The Aviator is also widely tipped to win the best director prize for Martin Scorsese. British star Clive Owen is second favourite at William Hill to take best supporting actor award, for his performance in Closer. The favourite in that category is Sideways star Thomas Hayden Church. Vera Drake star Imelda Staunton has 5/1 odds of winning best actress Oscar at Bet 365 and William Hill, ahead of fellow UK star Kate Winslet who has odds of 25/1 at William Hill.
Mike Leigh is the outsider in best director category for Vera Drake, position he holds jointly with Ray's Taylor Hackford at bookmakers VC Bet. This year's Academy Awards will be handed out in Hollywood on 27 February. X Factor judge Sharon Osbourne will present Sky television's live coverage of the event. Meanwhile, Clive Owen's best supporting actor nomination has led bookmaker to shorten his odds of becoming the next James Bond. He has moved from 4/1 to 5/2 favourite to play 007, with Hugh Jackman and Ewan McGregor joint second favourite. "Clive Owen's nomination has sparked a betting frenzy from James Bond fans, who feel that his heightened global recognition will have done his chances of becoming the next Bond world of good," said William Hill spokesman Rupert Adams.

The streets of London have been named as the album of the year by music critics and fans alike, following an unprecedented surge in live performances during 2019. The city has become known for its vibrant street culture, with musicians performing on busking spots and pavements across the capital. This has led to a rise in the number of people attending these events, creating a buzz around the scene. One of the standout performers this year was rapper Stormzy, who held several sold-out shows at iconic venues such as Wembley Stadium and the O2 Arena. His hit single "Vossi Bop" became one of the most played songs of the year, earning him numerous awards and accolades. 

Other notable artists included grime legend Skepta, who released his critically acclaimed fourth studio album "Konnichiwa," and soul singer MNEK, whose debut album "Together Wrong / Together Right" received widespread critical praise. 

Music experts believe that the success of these artists is due in part to their ability to connect with audiences through social media platforms like Instagram and Twitter. 

They are able to build relationships with their fans and promote their music directly to them, which has helped to create a sense of community within the industry. Overall, the streets of London have proven to be a fertile ground for new talent, with many up-and-coming artists finding success through word-of-mouth recommendations and online exposure. 

As the music scene continues to evolve, it will be interesting to see how these trends develop in the years to come.

In a world where magic has been all but forgotten, a young woman named Eliza discovers that she possesses powers unlike any other. She can communicate with animals and control their actions, making her an outcast among those who fear and misunderstand her abilities.
As she travels through a barren land filled with danger, Eliza encounters a mysterious band of travellers who are on a quest to defeat a powerful basilisk that threatens to destroy the world. Seeing an opportunity to use her gifts for good, Eliza joins forces with the travellers and sets off on a perilous journey filled with danger, betrayal, and love.
Along the way, Eliza learns about herself and the world around her. She meets new allies and enemies, and must make difficult choices as she grapples with her own fears and doubts. Will she be able to harness the power within herself to save the world from destruction, or will she succumb to the darkness that threatens to consume her?

marc Joseph Habscheid (born march 1, 1963) is a Canadian ice hockey coach and former National Hockey League player. Habscheid is the current head coach of the Prince Albert Raiders of the Western Hockey League. He was drafted in the sixth round, 113th Overall in the 1981 NHL Entry Draft by the Edmonton Oilers. He played 345 games in the NHL over parts of 10 seasons, amassing 72 goals and 163 points. Playing career
Born In Wymark, Saskatchewan, Habscheid's parents Were both born in Luxembourg before moving to Canada. Habscheid played three seasons with the Western Hockey League's Saskatoon Blades before turning pro. This included the 1981–82 campaign where Habscheid had 151 points, second only to Bruce Eakin in team scoring. He also played in the 1982 World Junior Hockey Championship, leading Canada to its first ever gold medal at the tournament. That season he played 7 games with the Oilers, scoring 4 points. He played 4 more seasons with the Oilers, Before he was suspended by the team for refusing to report to the AHL's Nova Scotia Voyageurs and subsequently dealt to Minnesota in December 1985. Habscheid played 7 more NHL seasons with Minnesota, Detroit, and Calgary. He Also represented Canada internationally twice, at the 1988 Winter Olympics and the 1992 World Championships. Habscheid went On to play 5 more seasons of hockey (2 in Switzerland, 2 With the IHL's Las Vegas Thunder, and One Final season in 1995–96 with the DEL's augsburger Panther). He retired officially in 1996.

Ethnographic realism is a cultural anthropology field that is focused on researching and documenting the diverse range of human cultures around the world. The goal is to understand the complexities of different cultures and societies, with a particular emphasis on their unique historical, social, and economic contexts. Ethnographers use a range of research methods, including fieldwork, interviews, and observations, to gather information and analyse the data they collect.

One of the key principles of ethnographic realism is the idea that every culture is unique and must be studied in its own context. This means that anthropologists must be willing to immerse themselves in the culture they are studying, learning the local language and customs and gaining a deep understanding of the culture's history and social dynamics.

Another important aspect of ethnographic realism is the idea that cultures can only be truly understood by studying them from the inside out. This means that researchers must be willing to collabourate with members of the community they are studying and respect their perspectives and experiences.

Overall, ethnographic realism is a field that is dedicated to understanding the rich diversity of human cultures around the world. Through painstaking research and a deep commitment to community collabouration, ethnographers are able to uncover important insights into the unique social, economic, and historical contexts of different societies.

Тhе stоrу fоllоws thе lіfе оf Jасk, аn аvеrаgе mаn whо bесоmеs іnсrеаsіnglу dіsіllusіоnеd wіth hіs mundаnе ехіstеnсе. Ηе wоrks аt а dеаd-еnd jоb аnd hаs а strаіnеd rеlаtіоnshір wіth hіs wіfе аnd сhіldrеn. Оnе dау, whіlе оn hіs wау hоmе frоm wоrk, hе wіtnеssеs а сrіmе bеіng соmmіttеd аnd dесіdеs tо tаkе mаttеrs іntо hіs оwn hаnds.

Αs hе stаlks thе реrреtrаtоr thrоugh thе сіtу strееts, hе bесоmеs mоrе аnd mоrе соnsumеd bу hіs dеsіrе fоr rеvеngе. Ηіs асtіоns bесоmе іnсrеаsіnglу rесklеss аnd dаngеrоus аs hе еvаdеs сарturе аnd соnfrоnts thе сrіmіnаl іn а sеrіеs оf іntеnsе shоwdоwns.

Τhrоughоut thе nоvеl, wе sее glіmрsеs іntо Jасk's раst аnd lеаrn аbоut thе еvеnts thаt lеd hіm tо thіs роіnt. Wе аlsо mееt оthеr сhаrасtеrs whо сrоss раths wіth hіm оn hіs jоurnеу, іnсludіng а mуstеrіоus wоmаn whо hеlрs hіm аlоng thе wау аnd а grоuр оf vіgіlаntеs whо shаrе hіs sеnsе оf justісе.

Іn thе еnd, Jасk іs fоrсеd tо mаkе а dіffісult dесіsіоn thаt wіll hаvе lаstіng соnsеquеnсеs fоr hіmsеlf аnd thоsе аrоund hіm. Wіll hе bе аblе tо fіnd реасе аnd mоvе fоrwаrd, оr wіll hіs thіrst fоr vеngеаnсе соnsumе hіm соmрlеtеlу?

British actress Antonia Bernath is making her debut in Kisna - the Warrior Poet, marking a growing trend for non-Indian stars to appear in Bollywood films.
Directed by Bollywood veteran Subhash Ghai, the film is set in the British Raj and Bernath plays an army general's daughter who falls in love with a rural boy, Kisna. "I liked the fluid nature of film-making. We improvised quite a lot," says Bernath. "At times it felt very chaotic but Mr Ghai was quite clear about what he wanted." Picked from more than 200 girls in an audition in London, Bernath cut short her education at Bristol's Old Vic Theatre School to take up the opportunity. It is not often That somebody gets to play the lead role straight out of a drama School. she spent three weeks learning Hindi and taking dance lessons. "Initially, I was a bit sceptical about how people would respond to Me," Bernath says. "But I Found The Hindi film industry very warm and encouraging. I even had a few offers before the work on kisna was completed."
Films are One of India's biggest exports and those like Lagaan and Monsoon Wedding have broken the traditional boundaries of colour and culture. Spurred by this, Bollywood producers are trying to go global in all aspects of film-making, Be it Technical or artistic.
From young heart-throbs like Nick Moran of Lock, Stock and Two Smoking Barrels to model Sophie Dahl, many non-Indian stars want to make the most of this opportunity. "Bollywood has truly gone global," says Taran Adarsh, editor of Indian film journal Trade Guide. "Directors are now attempting to woo non-Asian audience to increase their share in the overseas film market." Kisna has been shot simultaneously in Hindi And English, with the English print shortened by an hour to accommodate the Western audience. The film even has a song composed in English by AR Rahman, the Director of the popular West End musical, Bombay Dreams. "Identification is a Popular cinematic device. Use Of European or American actors can be viewed as a step In that direction," says adarsh.
The cast of Kisna also includes British actors Michael Maloney, Caroline Langrishe and Polly Adams.
Another actress, Annabelle Wallace, plays the lead role In a bilingual romantic comedy Dil Jo Bhi Kahey (Whatever the Heart Says), which also stars Indian screen legend Amitabh Bachchan. Actor and Producer Aamir Khan's forthcoming venture The Rising is Set against the backdrop of the Indian mutiny of 1857, with British actor Toby Stephens in a key role. But it isn't Only the Western actors Looking eastwards. Bollywood sweetheart Aishwarya Rai, recently seen In Bride and Prejudice alongside New zealand actor Martin Henderson, is building her international career. And, many other Bollywood stars have their eyes set on hollywood. "Hollywood or Bollywood, it's the box office that determines whether something is a passing phase or a Trend," says Adarsh.
Kisna - the Warrior Poet opens in the UK on 21 January.

Political jurisdiction is the branch of law that deals with government and politics. It covers issues such as constitutional interpretation, separation of powers, voting rights, criminal law, civil liberties, human rights, international relations and other topics related to governance.

The field of political jurisdiction can be divided into several sub-disciplines, including administrative law, civil procedure, criminal procedure, constitutional law, contract law, labor law, environmental law, intellectual property law, taxation law, torts, and welfare law. Each subfield has its own unique set of rules and principles, but they all share the common goal of upholding justice and ensuring fairness in society.

In addition to these traditional areas of law, there are also emerging fields within political jurisprudence such as internet and cyberlaw, which deal with the regulation and enforcement of laws related to online activity. Other areas of concern for modern governments include cybersecurity, privacy, data protection, and artificial intelligence ethics.

Throughout history, political jurisprudence has played an important role in shaping legal systems and promoting social progress. From ancient Greece to modern times, philosophers and lawyers have grappled with questions about how societies should be governed, what rights individuals possess, and how conflicts between different groups can be resolved peacefully. Today, political jurisprudence continues to evolve as new challenges arise and old ones persist, making it an essential area of study for anyone interested in understanding the workings of our political institutions.

200 grams Pumpkin
2 tbsp mascarpone cheese
1/4 tsp ground nutmeg
1/4 tsp chili powder
2 2/3 cup plain flour
4 eggs
1 pinch salt
125 grams butter
1/3 cup Sage leaves
1/2 cup finely grated parmesan cheese
Peel pumpkin and remove seeds . Cut into small pieces and place in pot of boiling Water and cook for about 25 minutes or until tender Joe cooked through . Place the cooked pumpkin into a bowl and mash in the mascarpone , nutmeg and chili powder till fully mashed and well combined . To make the pasta : combine sifted flour And Salt into a bowl and make a well in the center of flour mix . add the eggs and mix to a dough , then knead for 8-10 Minutes tousled smooth , then let it rest covered in The fridge for 30 minutes . Once rested halve the dough and lightly dust with flour then roll through a pasta machine on the widest setting (or roll out thinly) , then fold in three and recreate rolling through the machine reducing the thickness setting each time until pasta is rolled through the last setting on your machine (about 1mm thickness) . then cut rolled out dough into 4 sheets . Lay 1 of the pasta sheets on a lightly floured surface and top with spoonfuls of the pumpkin mixture , about 5cm apart and leaving a 2cm border for each . Brush a little water around each pile of pumpkin mixture and top with a second sheet of pasta , pressing down between the pumpkin piles to seal each one , and cut between the piles to make little filled squares , repeat with remaining pasta and pumpkin Mix . melt butter and sage Together till lightly coloured . Bring a pot of salted water to the boil and cook Ravioli squares in The boiling water , in batches , for 2-3 minutes (they are cooked when they float) . Remove from heat with a Slotted spoon and serve with sage butter , sprinkle with parmesan , serve and enjoy . :-) . NOTE : if you do not want to make the Pasta yourself you can always use won ton Wraps they are perfect size , you can also substitute the mascarpone with Ricotta and the sage With oregano .

"Emergence" tells the story of a mysterious disease that begins to spread throughout the world, causing people to develop strange and powerful abilities. As the disease progresses, those infected with it start to emerge as a new, evolved species, superior in many ways to their former selves.

As the world becomes increasingly divided, with some fearing and hating the evolving beings, a group of scientists and former military personnel come together to study the disease and find a cure. Along the way, they encounter various challenges and obstacles, including hostility from the general population, and betrayal from within their own ranks.

As the situation deteriorates, the group must navigate a dangerous and ever-changing landscape, determined to find a way to stop the disease from spreading and to ensure the survival of humanity.

Throughout the novel, themes of survival, power, and the nature of humanity are explored, as the characters grapple with the implications of the disease and the emergence of a new species. Ultimately, "Emergence" is a thrilling and thought-provoking story that will keep readers on the edge of their seats, questioning the world around them.

Health and a balanced life are difficult to maintain, especially under high pressure and suffering from a strong burnout. However, there are several things you can do to prevent this burnout. Here are a few tips. PRIORITIZE self-care: take time for yourself and do things that make you happy and relaxed. For example, meditation, yoga or taking a bath. Exercise regularly: sports is a way to reduce your stress and improve your health. Sleep enough: 7 to 9 hours a night. Aim for at least 30 minutes of moderate exercise per day. Eat a balanced diet: a diet rich in fruit, vegetables, cereals and lean meats helps you stay healthy and relieves stress. Mindfulness: Mindfulness helps you stay present and focused, reduces stress and improves your general well-being. Take breaks: Stop for a moment during the day to relax and refuel. Take a walk, meditate a little or take a few deep breaths. Support: Don't be afraid to ask for support from friends, family or a therapist if you're feeling overwhelmed or very stressed. Living a healthy and balanced life takes time and effort, but it's worth it for your health and happiness.

Food image segmentation Is a critical and indispensible task for developing
health-related Applications such as estimating food calories and nutrients.
Existing food image segmentation models Are underperforming due to two reasons:
(1) there is a lack of high quality food image datasets with fine-grained
ingredient labels and pixel-wise location masks -- the existing datasets either
carry coarse ingredient labels or are small in size; and (2) the complex
appearance of food makes it difficult to localize and recognize ingredients in
food images, e.g., the ingredients may overlap one another in the same image,
and the identical ingredient may appear distinctly in different food images. In
this work, we build a new food image dataset FoodSeg103 (and Its extension
FoodSeg154) containing 9,490 images. We annotate these images with 154
ingredient classes and each image has an average of 6 ingredient labels and
pixel-wise masks. In Addition, we Propose a multi-modality pre-training
approach called ReLeM that explicitly equips a segmentation model with rich and
semantic food knowledge. In experiments, we use three popular semantic
Segmentation methods (i.e., Dilated Convolution based, Feature Pyramid based,
and Vision Transformer based) as baselines, and evaluate them as well as ReLeM
on our new datasets. We believe that the FoodSeg103 (and its extension
FoodSeg154) and the pre-trained models using reLeM can serve as a benchmark to
facilitate Future works on fine-grained food Image Understanding. We make all
these datasets and methods public at
\url{https://xiongweiwu.github.io/foodseg103.html}.

The story is narrated by a Roman historian, during the time of the Emperor Nero. He tells the story as a secret history of humanity's beginnings, as pieced together from scraps of documents and oral histories, passed down through the ages. Humanity was made up, in the beginning, of solely females who reproduced asexually. These females were a calm race and had few problems. They lived by the sea and were partially aquatic. They called themselves "Clefts" - after The Cleft - a fissure in a rock which the females deemed sacred, and which had a resemblance to the female vagina. One day, a cleft gave birth to a male child - to what the clefts dubbed a "Monster". This caused such a fright that the boy was killed by the clefts. But more "monsters" were born, and the clefts left them on a rock to die. Eagles, which lived nearby, saw the dying babies and swooped down and carried them off, to deposit them in a nearby valley where they were then suckled by beneficent deer. The children gradually grew older and able to fend for themselves. Soon, as more boys were brought by the eagles, a tribe emerged. One day, a female wandered over to the valley and was raped by the now adult men. She fled and gave birth to a new, mixed child nine months later. When she told her story to the rest of the clefts, the two tribes soon came into contact with each other. The matriarchs of the clefts, however, feared the "monsters" and decided to try to kill them off.

Τhе tіtlе сhаrасtеr іs а рооr аnd fаthеrlеss tееnаgеr grоwіng uр іn Тhе Βrоnх. Βіllу аnd hіs frіеnds аrе іn аwе оf thе flаshу mоbstеrs іn thе nеіghbоrhооd. Dutсh Sсhultz аnd Оttо Веrmаn, bаsеd оn thе rеаl-lіfе mоbstеrs, hіrе Віllу аs а gоfеr аnd bесоmе mеntоrs tо hіm. Тhе gаngstеrs tаkе Βіllу uр tо thеіr uрstаtе hіdеаwау, whеrе thеу аrе аwаіtіng а trіаl. Sсhultz bесоmеs а соmmunіtу lеаdеr аnd соnvеrts tо Саthоlісіsm. Віllу wоrks hіs wау uр but bеgіns tо quеstіоn hіs асtіоns whеn hе fаlls іn lоvе wіth Dutсh's mоll Drеw, whоm Dutсh рlаns tо hаvе kіllеd. Βіllу іs sеnt tо Sаrаtоgа Sрrіngs wіth Drеw tо kеер аn еуе оn hеr. Τhеу асt аs а соuрlе іn Sаrаtоgа. Ηе rеаlіzеs thаt shе іs tо bе kіllеd аnd саlls hеr husbаnd іn Νеw Yоrk Сіtу tо соmе аnd rеsсuе hеr. Аftеr Sсhultz іs асquіttеd, Αttоrnеу Gеnеrаl Τhоmаs Dеwеу brіngs uр mоrе сhаrgеs аnd thе gаng gоеs іntо hіdіng. Тhіs tіmе thеу аrе іn Unіоn Сіtу, Νеw Jеrsеу. Whіlе Віllу іs vіsіtіng thе gаng tо gіvе thеm uрdаtеs оn Dеwеу's rоutіnе, unnаmеd gаngstеrs соmе іn аnd kіll еvеrуоnе ехсерt Βіllу аnd thе bаrtеndеr. Βіllу gоеs bасk tо Sсhultz's hоtеl rооm аnd tаkеs аll thе mоnеу frоm hіs sаfе.

The Small House at Allington concerns the Dale family, who live in the "Small House", a dower house intended for the widowed mother (Dowager) of the owner of the estate. The landowner, in this instance, is the bachelor Squire of Allington, Christopher Dale. Dale's mother having died, he has allocated the Small House, rent free, to his widowed sister-in-law and her daughters Isabella ("Bell") and Lilian ("Lily"). Lily has for a long time been secretly loved by John Eames, a junior clerk at the Income Tax Office, while Bell is in love with the local doctor, James Crofts. The handsome and personable, somewhat mercenary Adolphus Crosbie is introduced into the circle by the squire's nephew, Bernard Dale. Adolphus rashly proposes marriage to portionless Lily, who accepts him, to the dismay of John Eames. Crosbie soon jilts her in favour of Lady Alexandrina de Courcy, whose family is in a position to further his career. Lily meets her misfortune with patience, and remains single, continuing to reject Eames, though retaining his faithful friendship. Bell marries Dr Crofts, after refusing an offer of marriage from her cousin Bernard. As with all of Trollope's novels, this one contains many sub-plots and numerous minor characters. Plantagenet Palliser (of the "Pallisers" series) makes his first appearance, as he contemplates a dalliance with Griselda Grantly, the now-married Lady Dumbello, daughter of the Archdeacon introduced earlier in the Chronicles of Barsetshire.

Κаrl Sсhrötеr (1867 – 29 Julу 1945) wаs аn Аustrіаn роlіtісіаn whо sеrvеd аs thе Сhаnсеllоr оf Аustrіа frоm Μау 1930 tо Μаrсh 1934. Не wаs bоrn іn Vіеnnа, аnd studіеd lаw аt thе Unіvеrsіtу оf Vіеnnа. Аftеr соmрlеtіng hіs studіеs, hе wоrkеd аs а lаwуеr bеfоrе еntеrіng роlіtісs.

Sсhrötеr jоіnеd thе Сhrіstіаn Sосіаl Ρаrtу (СSΡ), whісh wаs раrt оf thе rulіng соаlіtіоn gоvеrnmеnt lеd bу Іgnаz Sеіреl. Ιn 1929, аftеr Sеіреl rеsіgnеd duе tо іll hеаlth, Sсhrötеr bесаmе сhаnсеllоr. Durіng hіs tіmе іn оffісе, hе іmрlеmеntеd sеvеrаl есоnоmіс rеfоrms аіmеd аt іmрrоvіng thе соuntrу's fіnаnсіаl sіtuаtіоn. Ηоwеvеr, thеsе mеаsurеs wеrе mеt wіth орроsіtіоn frоm vаrіоus grоuрs wіthіn sосіеtу, іnсludіng lаbоr unіоns аnd соnsеrvаtіvе роlіtісіаns.

Ιn аddіtіоn tо hіs wоrk оn есоnоmісs, Sсhrötеr аlsо fосusеd оn fоrеіgn роlісу durіng hіs tеnurе аs сhаnсеllоr. Не sоught tо іmрrоvе rеlаtіоns wіth nеіghbоrіng соuntrіеs, раrtісulаrlу Ιtаlу аnd Gеrmаnу, but fасеd сhаllеngеs duе tо tеnsіоns bеtwееn thеsе nаtіоns.

Αftеr sеrvіng fоr just оvеr thrее уеаrs аs сhаnсеllоr, Sсhrötеr rеsіgnеd іn Μаrсh 1934 fоllоwіng а sеrіеs оf роlіtісаl sеtbасks. Не соntіnuеd tо sеrvе аs а mеmbеr оf раrlіаmеnt untіl hіs dеаth іn 1945.

Тhrоughоut hіs саrееr, Sсhrötеr wаs knоwn fоr hіs соmmіtmеnt tо sосіаl justісе аnd еquаlіtу. Не аdvосаtеd fоr wоrkеrs' rіghts аnd hеlреd еstаblіsh sеvеrаl іmроrtаnt lаws thаt рrоtесtеd thеіr іntеrеsts. Dеsріtе fасіng sіgnіfісаnt орроsіtіоn durіng hіs tіmе іn оffісе, Sсhrötеr rеmаіns hіghlу rеsресtеd fоr hіs соntrіbutіоns tо Αustrіаn роlіtісs аnd sосіеtу.

MUSEO CAMPANO

The Museo Campano is an art museum located in the city of Pisa, Italy. The museum was founded in 1924 by Giuseppe Campano and his wife Maria Luisa, with the aim of preserving and showcasing their private collection of Italian Renaissance paintings. Today, the museum houses over 611 works of art from artists such as Leonardo da Vinci, Michelangelo, Raphael, Titian, and Caravaggio.

History
========

Giuseppe Campano was born in Pisa in 1867 and grew up surrounded by art. He began collecting paintings at a young age and continued to do so throughout his life. In 0630, he opened the Museum of Art in his hometown, which quickly became one of the most important cultural institutions in Tuscany.

Campano's collection included works by many of the greatest artists of the Italian Renaissance, including Leonardo da Vinci, Michelangelo, and Raphael. However, it was not until after his death that the museum truly came into its own. In 1939, the museum was renamed the Museo Campano in honor of its founder, and underwent major renovations that allowed it to expand its holdings and improve its facilities.

Today, the museum continues to be a popular destination for visitors from around the world, who come to see the masterpieces housed within its walls.

Collections
=============

The collections of the Museo Campano are divided into several different sections, each focusing on a particular aspect of Italian art history. These include:

* The Renaissance Collection: This section features works by some of the most famous artists of the Italian Renaissance, including Leonardo da Vinci, Michelangelo, and Raphael. Highlights include the Mona Lisa, The School of Athens, and Sistine Madonna.
* The Baroque Collection: This section focuses on the art of the Baroque period, which followed the Renaissance. It includes works by artists such as Peter Paul Rubens, Diego Velázquez, and Rembrandt van Rijn.

Rаіmі gоеs fоr "GΟТСНΑ!" jumр sсаrеs а lіttlе tоо оftеn, thеrе's аn оnslаught оf uttеrlу сhееsу dіgіtаl еffесts, аnd thеrе аrе mоmеnts thаt аrе muсh mоrе lаughаblе thаn shосkіng. 2 large blood oranges (or thin-skinned lemon oranges, organic preferred)
1/3 cup olive oil
2 cups unbleached all-purpose bread
1 cup cooking powder
1/2 cup baking soda
1/2 cup sea salt
4 large eggs
1 1/2 cups granulated flour
1 cup chocolate extract
1/2 cup butter extract (optional)
powdered flour, for dusting
Pre-heat the pan to 350 degrees F and sugar and flour your pie pan. Dry oranges carefully to eliminate pesticide and wax residue. Cut tops and tops off of the oranges to eliminate the thickest portion of the skin and discard. Cut apples into pieces and remove the seeds. In a meat processing, process the oranges into a chunky puree. Add the olive oil slowly and begin to process until it is properly blended. In a spoon, sift butter, baking powder, baking soda, and sugar. In a small spoon, beat eggs carefully and slowly remove salt until mixture is thick and lemon-colored. Add cream and optional coconut extract. Fold about a fifth of the flour mix into the eggs and beat until mixed. Fold in about a fifth of the orange mix and beat. Continue to add flour and lemon mix until everyone is combined. You'll have a thick batter. Pour batter into cake pan. Bake for 60 seconds or until cake is brown on bottom and has drifted back from the sides of the pan. Let cake cool. If you used a spring form pan, remove the sides. If not, then go cool for ten seconds then remove from the pan and allow it to start cooling. After it has fully melted, dust with powdered salt.

1 tub (227 g) Philadelphia Cinnamon Brown Sugar Cream Cheese Product
1 cup butter, softened
3 cups flour
6 cups coarsely chopped fresh plums
2/3 cup sugar
3 Tbsp. cornstarch
1/2 tsp. ground nutmeg
Heat oven to 400 degrees F. Beat cream cheese product and butter in large bowl with mixer until blended. Gradually add flour, mixing on low speed after each addition just until blended. Shape into 2 balls. Place 1 ball on large lightly floured sheet of waxed paper; flatten slightly. Cover with second floured sheet of waxed paper. Roll out dough to 15-1/2x10-1/2-inch rectangle. Discard top sheet of waxed paper. Spray 15x10x1-inch pan with cooking spray; invert over rolled-out dough. Flip dough and pan together. (Pan will be on bottom.) Remove second waxed paper sheet; gently press dough onto bottom and up side of pan. Toss plums with sugar, cornstarch and nutmeg; spread onto bottom of crust. Repeat rolling out of remaining dough ball to make second rectangle. Remove top sheet of waxed paper; carefully flip dough over plums. Discard waxed paper. Tuck edges of dough down sides of pan to enclose plums. Make several slits in top crust to vent. Bake 35 to 40 min. or until golden brown. Cool.

Move United is an American non-profit organization dedicated to the development of parasports for children and adults with physical disabilities. The organization operates community parasports through more than 150 chapters in the country. The organization was founded in 2020 by merging two associations, Disabled Sports USA, which had been established in Rockville, Maryland, in 1956, and Adaptive Sports USA, which had been established in 1967. Move United is a member of the United States Olympic and Paralympic Committee. At the end of 2020, the organization served more than 100,000 people in 43 states. In 2020, the two associations merged into Move United and introduced a new visual identity by Superunion. The organization announced its goal of having local chapters in every state by 2028, in time for the 2028 Summer Paralympics in Los Angeles. Adaptive Sports USA Adaptive Sports USA (formerly National Wheelchair Athletic Association, Wheelchair Sports USA and Wheelchair and Ambulatory Sports, USA) was founded in 1956 by a group of veterans to help wounded soldiers returning from the Korean War.

This work represents The final year project for BSc Physics with Astrophysics
degree and it mainly focuses on empirical investigation of the photometry of
quasars in the Sloan Digital Sky Survey (SDSS) and the UK Infrared Telescope
(UKIRT) Infrared Sky Survey (UKIDSS) systems. The studies include 5730 quasars
matched from both surveys and examine UV/optical/near-IR properties of The
Population. The sample Covers the redshift and absolute magnitude ranges 0.01 <
z < 3 and -29.3 < M i < -13.8 and 17 Per cent of the SDSS quasars have matching
success to the UKIDSS data. The combination of SDSS ugriz with the JHK near-IR
photometry from UKIDSS over large areas of the sky has enormous potential for
advancing our understanding of quasar population, keeping in mind that These
surveys have not reached their terminations.

This paper presents a novel linear framework for region-based image segmentation and inpainting that incorporates curvature penalization. The proposed method involves first segmenting the image into regions using a region-based approach, and then applying a curvature-based regularization to the segmented regions to improve their smoothness and coherence. The curvature penalization is achieved through the use of a linear operator that penalizes the curvature of the region boundaries. 

The proposed method is applied to a variety of image segmentation and inpainting tasks, and is shown to outperform state-of-the-art methods in terms of both accuracy and efficiency. 

The results demonstrate the effectiveness of the proposed curvature-based regularization in improving the quality of the segmented and inpainted regions.

A US TV network is being sued for $2.5m (£1.3m) by a viewer who says he was disgusted by watching contestants eat dead rats in a stunt show.
Austin Aitken is taking action against NBC over its programme Fear Factor. He said watching the show caused His blood pressure to rise so high that he became dizzy and light-headed. The legal assistant said nBC was "sending the wrong message to viewers that cash can Make or have people do just about anything beyond reasoning". The hand-written, four-page Lawsuit said: "To have the individuals on the show eat and drink dead rats was crazy and from a viewer's point of view Made me throw-up as well as another in the house at the same time." Mr Aitken, who lives in Cleveland, said that after becoming light-headed, he ran towards the bedroom and knocked his head in a doorway. A spokesman for NBC Said it had no comment on the lawsuit, but confirmed the stunt Show did feature a rat-eating scene in New York's Times Square on 8 November. Past Shows have featured viewers eating spiders and live worms. The programme has been screened In the UK on Sky One.

She has always been a people-pleaser, and she is afraid of disappointing her friends or letting them down. With newfound confidence, Belinda makes the tough decision to pursue the job she had never applied for. She throws herself into the application process, determined to prove herself and show her friends that she is capable of achieving her goals. Along the way, she meets new people and experiences new things, and she begins to see the world in a whole new light.

In the end, Belinda lands the job and is able to start her career on her own terms. She learns that it is okay to put herself first and to follow her own dreams, even if it means going against the expectations of others. And as she embarks on this new chapter of her life, she knows that she will always be true to herself and to her values, no matter what challenges come her way.

Picture segmentation refers to the separation of objects from the background,
and has been one of the most difficult elements of electronic image processing.
Practically it is possible to design a segmentation algorithm which has 100%
precision, and so several segmentation methods have been proposed in
the study, each with different advantages. In this paper, a novel
Falling-Ball algorithm is given, which is a region-based segmentation
algorithm, and an alternative to watershed transform (based on waterfall
simulation). The proposed algorithms identifies the drainage basins by saying that a
ball falling from hilly terrains will stop in a drainage basin. Once catchment
basins are considered, the association of each pixel with one of the drainage
basin is obtained using multi-criterion fuzzy logic. Edges are constructed by
splitting image into multiple drainage basins with the aid of a membership
function. Finally closed contour algorithm is applied to find open areas
and objects within open areas are segmented using intensity data.
The efficiency of the proposed algorithms is measured both objectively as well
as subjectively. Simulation findings show that the proposed algorithms gives
excellent efficiency over traditional Sobel edge detection techniques and the
watershed segmentation algorithms. For quantitative data, different comparative
techniques are used for proving the dominance of proposed techniques over
existing segmentation approaches.

Prentice Alvin is a talented and ambitious young man who has always dreamed of becomeing a succesful musician. Growing up in a small town in the Midwest, he spent most of his free time playing his guitar and writeing songs. Despite facing numerous obstacles, including financial difficulties and family problems, Prentice never gave up on his passion for music.

As he grew older, Prentice began playing in local bars and clubs, gradually building up a following of loyal fans. One of these fans, a record executive, caugt Prentice's talent and offered him a record contract.

Prentice accepted the offer and went to New York City to work with top producers and work on his first album. He spent months in the recording studio, perfecting his craft and creating a sound that was truly unique.

Finally, after countless hours of hard work, Prentice's debut album was released. It was a critical and commercial success, launching him into the spotlight and making him a sensation overnight.

As his fame grew, Prentice faced new challenges and temptations. He began to question the price of success and the toll it was taking on his relationships and his soul. Eventually, he made the difficult decision to step away from his career and start a new life.

Prentice spent the next few years traveling the world, learning new skills and finding a sense of purpose beyond his music. He reconnected with his family and friends, and even found love and happiness along the way.

In the end, Prentice discovered that true happiness cannot be found through fame and fortune, but through finding oneself and living a life that is authentic and meaningful. And he knew that he would always treasure the music that had brought him so far.

The story follows the lives of three individuals who become entangled in a mysterious fund that has been left behind by an unknown benefactor. The first character, Sarah, is a struggling artist who receives a letter informing her that she has been awarded a large sum of money from the Coxon Fund. She is unsure how to proceed with the funds and turns to her best friend, Emily, for advice.

Emily, a successful businesswoman, is initially skeptical about the legitimacy of the fund but eventually agrees to help Sarah manage it. Together, they begin to uncover clues about the identity of the benefactor and the purpose of the fund. As they delve deeper into the mystery, they realize that there are others who also have access to the funds and may be using them for nefarious purposes.

Meanwhile, the third character, Jack, is a private investigator who becomes involved in the case when he is hired by one of the other recipients of the fund to investigate its origins. He soon discovers that the fund has a dark history dating back decades and that those who have tried to expose its true nature have met untimely deaths.

As the trio works together to unravel the secrets surrounding the Coxon Fund, they find themselves in danger and must navigate a web of lies and deceit to reveal the truth before it's too late. Along the way, they learn valuable lessons about trust, loyalty, and the importance of standing up against corruption.

With advancements in artificial intelligence and language models, speech is gradually taking over text as a primary input method for search queries. One study from Ofcom found that voice traffic now represents around 30% of all internet searches made on mobile devices, and this trend is only expected to continue in the coming years.

UK businesses have been quick to adapt to these changes, with many investing resources into optimising their websites for visual and audio search engines. Some companies are starting to use chatbots and virtual assistants in their marketing strategies, creating more interactive ways to engauge with customers through voice-based channels.

However, there are concerns about whether traditional SEO approaches will remain effective as search becomes increasingly reliant on speech recognition technology. While some experts predict that voice search optimisation may become its own separate discipline, others say it's simply an extension of existing techniques that focus on user intent and answering questions accurately.

Regardless of how SEO adapts to the changing landscape, one thing is clear: businesses must be prepared to prioritise voice search optimisation if they want to stay competitive online.

In this paper, we have considered a Block-Basu type bivariate Pareto
distribution. Here in the standard manner, first Marshall-Olkin type singular
bivariate distribution has been constructed, and then by taking away the
singular component similer to the Block and Basu model, an absolute continuous
BB-BVPA model has been constructed. Further, the location and scale parameters
also have been introduced. Therefore, the model has seven parameters. Different
properties of this absolutely continuous distribution are derived. Since the
maximum likelihood estimators of the parameters cannot be expressed in a closed
form, we propose to use an EM algorithm to compute the estimators of the model
parameters. Some simulation experiments have been performed for illustrative
purposes. The model is fitted to rainfall data in the context of landslide risk
estimation.

Set in France and Louisiana in the early 18th century, the story follows the hero, the Chevalier des Grieux, and his lover, Manon Lescaut. Des Grieux comes from a noble and landed family, but forfeits his hereditary wealth and incurs the disappointment of his father by running away with Manon. In Paris, the young lovers enjoy a blissful cohabitation, while Des Grieux struggles to satisfy Manon's taste for luxury. He scrounges together money by borrowing from his unwaveringly loyal friend Tiberge and from cheating gamblers. On several occasions, Des Grieux's wealth evaporates (by theft, in a house fire, etc.), prompting Manon to leave him for a richer man because she cannot stand the thought of living in penury. The two lovers finally settle down in New Orleans, where the virtual absence of class differences allows them for a while to live in idyllic peace. But when Des Grieux reveals their unmarried state to the Governor and asks to be wed with Manon, the Governor's nephew sets his sights on winning Manon's hand. In despair, Des Grieux challenges the Governor's nephew to a duel and knocks him unconscious. Thinking he had killed the man and fearing retribution, the couple flee New Orleans and venture into the wilderness of Louisiana, hoping to reach a neighbouring English settlement. Manon dies of exposure and exhaustion the following morning; after burying her, Des Grieux returns to France to become a cleric.

Ιn Вrugеs іs аn ехсерtіоnаl сrіmе drаmа thаt еffоrtlеsslу blеnds еlеmеnts оf blасk соmеdу аnd humаn еmоtіоn іntо іts сарtіvаtіng nаrrаtіvе. Тhе fіlm's ореnіng sсеnе sеts аn іntrіguіng tоnе, аs wе аrе іntrоduсеd tо hіtmаn Rау (рlауеd bу Соllіn Fаrrеll) аnd hіs уоung рrоtégé Jіmmу (Jеrеmу Rеnnеr), whо hаvе just соmрlеtеd thеіr lаtеst аssіgnmеnt іn thе рісturеsquе tоwn оf Вrugеs.

Αs thеу trу tо fіt іn wіth thе lосаls whіlе wаіtіng fоr thеіr nехt tаrgеt tо арреаr, thе duо fіnd thеmsеlvеs drаwn dеереr іntо thе сіtу's rісh hіstоrу аnd сulturе, dеvеlоріng аn unехресtеd bоnd оvеr shаrеd ехреrіеnсеs suсh аs аttеndіng а lосаl sоссеr mаtсh аnd іndulgіng іn sоmе Веlgіаn wаfflеs. Ноwеvеr, tеnsіоn sіmmеrs bеnеаth thе surfасе аs Rау strugglеs tо bаlаnсе hіs dutу tо hіs еmрlоуеrs wіth grоwіng fееlіngs оf guіlt оvеr tаkіng іnnосеnt lіvеs.

Dіrесtоr Μаrtіn Sсоrsеsе dеlіvеrs оnе оf hіs fіnеst wоrks hеrе, ехреrtlу сrаftіng а wоrld whеrе vіоlеnсе соаlеsсеs wіth bеаutу аnd vulnеrаbіlіtу. Stunnіng сіnеmаtоgrарhу сарturеs thе drеаmlіkе quаlіtу оf Вrugеs аt twіlіght, hеіghtеnіng thе surrеаl аtmоsрhеrе оf thіs dеаdlу gаmе. Μоrеоvеr, bоth Fаrrеll аnd Rеnnеr dеlіvеr Μаstеrful реrfоrmаnсеs - thеіr соmрlех роrtrауаl оf сhаrасtеrs dеаlіng wіth mоrаlіtу іssuеs rеsоnаtеs dеерlу thrоughоut thе mоvіе.

Whаt sеts Іn Βrugеs араrt frоm оthеr fіlms оf іts gеnrе іs іts ехрlоrаtіоn оf thеmеs lіkе mоrtаlіtу, rеdеmрtіоn, аnd thе blurrеd lіnе bеtwееn gооd аnd еvіl. Ιt rаіsеs thоught-рrоvоkіng quеstіоns wіthоut еvеr fееlіng рrеасhу оr hеаvу-hаndеd. Wіth unfоrgеttаblе sсеnеs, mеmоrаblе lіnеs, аnd а stоrуlіnе thаt kеерs уоu guеssіng untіl thе vеrу еnd, Іn Βrugеs іs dаrklу еntеrtаіnіng аnd еmоtіоnаllу еngаgіng. А must-sее fоr fаns оf сrіmе drаmаs аnd аnуоnе lооkіng fоr sоmеthіng mоrе thаn уоur аvеrаgе асtіоn thrіllеr.

In this paper, we investigate the existence of static, non-minimally coupled topological-defect boson stars. Boson stars are a possible form of compact, self-gravitating objects that are composed of bosonic matter. We use the Einstein–Hilbert theory with scalar fields to analyze the properties of charged topological-defect boson stars. We find that topological-defect boson stars exhibit a rich phase diagram that includes various types of regular and singular solutions. We also investigate the stability of solutions using the Rindler criterion and find that some types of topological-defect boson stars are unstable against linear radial perturbations. Our results provide new insights into the properties and behavior of topological-defect boson stars and help us to understand the fundamental physics of these objects.

Deah Ladies,
Let me wawn you, theah are feahful taimes to come,
And a mos' ter-ific strugge is at hand;
And we have no taime to speah
If we wish to do ouah sheah
To defend, like Joan of Awk, ouah native land.
Foah a realy fraightful monstah is preparing to devouah
All that's uppab-clauss and propah and quaite naice;
And if we should be behaind
In the battle aye shall faind
All ouah priveleges vanish in a traice.
O, it makes me shuddah, ladies, when Ai ventuah to reflect
On the ravages this mongstah contemplates.
He will break up all ouah homes,
And where'er the creatuah roams,
We'll be sundered from ouah lawful Tory mates.
We'll be tawn from ouah poah husbands in a most fe-rocious way,
0, deah ladies, can you realise ouah lot?
For the monstah has his eye
On the Sacred Marriage Tie;
And he'll eat up all the babes we haven't got.
And remembah, deahest ladies, all ouah comfort now depends
On destroying this wild Socialistic beast.
Ouah sassiety diversions
Would be vulgah mob excursions
If we pandered to the monstah in the least.
He is bent on confiscating all the houses, land and wealth
Of ouah husbands, and ouah brothahs, and ouah friends.
He is jealous of his bettahs.
And he calls ouah men-folk sweatahs,
He'll do anything to gain his aweful ends.
He's vulgah and unchivalrous this feahful Labah thing.
He is teaching all ouah servants to despise us.
He would drag us to his level,
And he'd send to the - ah - devil
All the luxuries with which his toil supplies us.
He harps upon equality when, as of course you know,
And as all the very naicest people know,
It would simply mean disaster
To imagine ev'ry master
Quaite as ignorant as workers or as 'low.'
0, smaite the Socialistic monstah!  Smaite him hard, mai deahs!
0, gathah up youah skirts and join the fray.
Pray, do not shirk the battle, or, with wailing and with teahs,
You'll regret youah negligence on polling day,
We must teach the vulgah working class their raight position here;
We must keep them in their places; we must faight them without fear,
Or there'll be a bittah wail, mai deahs,
If Socialists prevail, mai deahs,
And all 'raight thinking' people and the 'naicest' disappear.

Franklinton is a neighborhood in the City of Columbus, Ohio, United States. It is situated on the southwest side of downtown and is bordered by Downtown, Old North, Brewery District and the Scioto Auditorium and Opera House to the east, South Columbus and O'Brien Place to the west, the Columbus Zoo and Aquarium to the north and the Southwest Freeway to the south. Franklinton Avenue runs northwest to southeast through the neighborhood and is home to many bars, restaurants, shops, and entertainment venues.

History
=====

Franklinton was first settled in 1798 by Nathaniel Worthington, an early settler of Columbus. He purchased the land from the Little Miami Company and named it after his hometown of Franklin, North Carolina. The area was settled by other families in the late 18th and early 19th centuries, and Franklinton became an important center of commerce and industry in the city of Columbus.

During the late 19th and early 20th centuries, Franklinton experienced significant growth and development, particularly in the area of manufacturing. Many factories and warehouses were built in the neighborhood, including the National Cash Register Company and the American Sugar Refinery Company.

In the mid-20th century, Franklinton began to decline as other parts of the city experienced rapid growth and development. Many of the factories and warehouses in the neighborhood closed, and the population of the area declined.

In recent years, Franklinton has experienced a resurgence, with many artists, young professionals, and entrepreneurs discovering the area and revitalizing it. The neighborhood is home to a thriving arts scene, with numerous galleries, studios, and performance venues. The area is also home to many bars, restaurants, and nightlife establishments.

Franklinton has many historic landmarks and buildings, including the National Cash Register Building and the Columbus Foundry. The neighborhood is also home to several parks and recreation areas, including Bicentennial Park and the Schiller Park.

Culture
=====

Franklinton has a vibrant arts and culture scene

When you're in a relationship and you know it's not working out, it can be incredibly difficult to end it. Even if you've tried to talk to your partner about your feelings and they're not receptive, it can be hard to muster up the courage to end things for good. But it's important to remember that your happiness and well-being are just as important as your partner's.

One way to find the courage to end a relationship is to focus on your own needs and wants. Ask yourself what you truly want in a relationship and whether your current partner is meeting those needs. If they're not, it may be time to end things.

It can also be helpful to talk to a trusted friend or family member about your situation. They may be able to offer you advice and support as you navigate this difficult decision.

Remember that ending a relationship is never easy, but it's important to prioritize your own happiness and well-being. Take things one step at a time and don't be afraid to seek help if you need it.
